Will definitely come back to sample the rest of the items in the menu as I left wanting to try out more of their selections. 10/10 would recommend if you're in the mood for some good Asian food!

I have never given a review of a restaurant on Yelp before but I had to let Boston know about this wonderful place - it is truly amazing Asian food and brand new (3 weeks old).  Already coming back for the 2nd time.

This is a wonderful spot that serves up great coffee, sandwiches and pastries.  They also have a wonderful baking section, with books, flour, chocolate and everything else in between.  I usually atop in for a weekend coffee when I'm out and about within East Cambridge and would highly recommend this place for snacks or if your trying to impress someone with a pie.

I visit Elmendorf regularly, and it's my favorite bakery in all of Boston and Cambridge. The service is so personable and prompt, and everything here tastes fantastic. 

If you come for weekday lunch, the bread for the sandwiches has just the right amount of chew, and the fillings are fabulous (my favorite is the mozzarella, pesto, and tomato sandwich, but I've had all of them and enjoyed each one thoroughly). If you visit for breakfast or weekend brunch, their egg sandwich is fabulous: the egg is a scrambled-but-not-dry thick layer and the bun is, as with the bread for the sandwiches during lunch, certainly not an afterthought. You can get oatmeal here, too, which can help you switch up your breakfast game.

The biggest reason I come to Elmendorf is because the pastries are off-the-charts delicious. I like to switch up what I buy, but among my personal favorites are their ginger molasses cookies (which are legitimately the best ginger cookies I have ever, ever tasted), strawberry rhubarb pie (but it's only in season for a short time!), and apple pie. The sugar and chocolate chip cookie choices (regularly available) are also excellent. And although I'm not a connoisseur of coffee or tea, I always find that they have things I like on offer to drink. On top of all of their lovely offerings, they also have a stock of baking supplies that make me positively green with envy. If I could have a shopping spree anywhere in the city, I'd want it to be here. 
Keep up the great work, Elmendorf!
Go sit down alone and have a portion of their spicy dumplings and a Tsingtao (beer). You'll be feelin good!

Everything here is done thoughtfully & perfectly.
Wonderful charming setting and if you take the time to ask the unassuming owners about their products you'll always learn something. I saw them impress an older italian lady with their delicious cappuccino
and I love their baking. Wonderful

NOT FOR INDIANS!!! THE MOST horrible taste ever and certainly NOT Cheap! We made a mistake of going there twice. First experience they took order wrong. It was a simple order just 2 items. Second experience was today. We ordered onion uttapam.. no onion! cornsand peas in it!! AND IT WAS COLD with a weird dry chutney on it.... Masala dosa was very dry  tooo..and we saw another costumer's order mixed up besides us. WE ARE VERY MUCH DISAPPOINTED. NEVER EVER!

The food is always great here. Always fresh and hot out of the kitchen. The one time they had a question about my order they called me, which I appreciated instead of guessing. The staff is incredibly kind, especially the one guy that is always there (unfortunately I don't know his name!). Would highly recommend. 

Pro tip: Order directly from their website instead of a third party like GrubHub. If you order directly from their website it's cheaper by a couple of dollars on each dish!

So excited we finally have a good Indian restaurant serving authentic tasting Indian food. Their black dal makhani and naan especially are delicious! Great selection of vegan/vegetarian food. Plus staff is super courteous. Definitely my new fav spot for Indian cuisine.

Is there a no star option? Perhaps the worst food I've every had (and I've been to some real lousy places). Not only was the food bad but the service is worse. You ask for a refill on water and get half a glass of water 10min later. You might think these are isolated incidents, but I forgot how bad it was and went back after 1yr....still the same. yuck

I was very disappointed with the Boston desi food after moving from sf. This bengali place hands down is as authentic as it can get. Very happy with their menu which includes quite a few very traditional Bengali cuisines. In terms of service, they are still working through kinks. So, if you care about the food, not the service and ambiance, do stop by to experience authentic bengali food.
really mediocre burritos.  There are so many better places to go.  The rice and guacamole are particularly bland.

We happened to park right in front of Zoe's while on a particular mission to find a hangover breakfast and figured it was fate.  Zoe's was exactly what we wanted.  The place was full but there was no wait for us for a big booth to fit 5.  The menu is huge and also has Greek food.  I debated between a gyro wrap and an omelet but ended up with an omelet with smoked salmon and asparagus.  It was a stand up breakfast - just big enough and pretty tasty.  It isn't expensive and they keep your coffee cup full.  It's definitely a great place to stop when you just want a hearty chill meal.

this is the bar that all my high school peeps somehow gravitate to when we're home over the holidays.   somehow a call goes out and that night there are like 50 of us at the B-side, drinking to rehab from thanksgiving with wierd relatives... it's a solid, decent bar.  one time, Matt Damon showed up and he was wearing a really dorky fleece and no one made fun of him.  how's that for hometown love?

An average brunch place

The good: it's clean, convenient location, no fuss for reservation, friendly waiters, big portions, good for groups

Being someone without a car, this place isn't always the easiest to get to, but if you have a bike during the summer and spring, it's not a bad ride at all.

A good place for groups, I usually steer away from dinner as the food is good, not great, and can be a little pricey. Small plates of snackies aren't bad for the money either. Average selection of beer. Depending on who is behind the bar, you can hear some great music you won't hear anywhere else (last time I was there a summer ago they were playing a lesser known 80's punk band Sloppy Seconds which I've never heard in public.)

Came in on a saturday night; the place had just enough people to not be too crowded or eerily quiet. We had reserved a table online 10 min before, and there was already water waiting for us by the time we got there. Extra point. 

Service was great. The waitress and staff really knew their food and was able to explain exactly what went into their dishes. 

Started with the scallops. Not sure if we liked the salad or the scallops or their sauce more. Needless to say, we fought over the last bit of the salad (it was perfect balance of lemon, pear, radishes, and sliced lettuce) and was just about shy of licking the plate clean of their delicious sauce. 

Got their braised brisket sandwich. Brisket was definitely house made, and cooked pretty much to perfection. The bread was perfectly toasted, and had some great dressing and caramelized onions. We split the sandwich, and somewhere in there my bread was stolen. The plate was empty at the end, it was that good. 

Definitely recommend, want to come back and try their brunch.

Yummy, yummy, yummy!

Zoe's Kitchen is an odd blend of Greek and American style food. I was introduced to the place as a "Greek" restaurant and was surprised by the number of the non-Greek things I found on the menu. However, not having been to very many Greek restaurants, that's the type of fare I always try when I go.

The chicken and rice soup is fantastic. The souvlaki wrap sounded scary from the description, but turned out to be extremely tasty. The lamb dinner is very tender and full of flavor. And they have sweet potato fries! The portion sizes are huge. For optimal flavor sampling, go with a large group of folks, order different things, and pass them around and share. Don't forget to finish it up with dessert - try the baklava or the rice pudding, but they're all tasty. Best of all, the prices are reasonable (and lower than I expected.)

Be careful when you go hunting for it, though, as it's somewhat below street level and you might overlook it. And it's near Harvard Square, so unless you get lucky with street parking, you might have a hard time getting a spot anywhere near the place.

the name says it all!

i've given this place a few tries when crazing americanized chinese food.  living close to central square i'm still in search good chinese take out.  they deliver and the food arrives pretty quickly but the quality is not so much.  

i most recently got general tsao's chicken and beef and broccoli and was pretty underwhelmed.  there was way to much batter on the chicken which had me thinking "where's the beef?" but the beef and broccoli was no better.  lots of broccoli but not so much beef.  the beef that was there, was clearly tenderized with some type of artificial meat tenderizer so the consistency didn't resemble meat at all.  

steer clear.

After waiting in line for 15 minutes on opening day today I just finished eating a cobb salad. My co-worker had been raving about this place forever ever since the signs went up in the Galleria saying it was coming. I was surprised to see the line especially since the Galleria has been pretty quiet for a while. 

The salad was ok. The balsamic dressing lacked enough salt to bring out the flavor. I don't know if it was nerves or strict portion control, but the people behind the counter seemed to be a bit too precise on portions for my liking, but hey, it's a business so I guess that's how it goes.

I'll try it out a few more times to see how things progress over time given that I work right next to the Galleria.

I have been to Mainely Burgers twice now and was so excited on our last visit to see that they were serving beer.   I'd say this place is a solid 3.75.  The burgers are great but the buns don't really do it for me.  The buns are pretty forgettable so I went open-faced (if I'm going to eat a carb, it better be worth it!).  The fries and the brussels sprouts side dish are awesome - really delicious.  Did I mention that they are now serving beer?  I might have to add another star to this review if they add a wheat beer and wine. ;-)
Seems like a great place, but they forgot to build my sandwich. I am not sure if there are too many people behind the counter. It just put a slow spin on an otherwise awesome Friday.

In general, I just really like these gritty, cheap, hole-in-the-wall type places that deliver decent food for good prices. 

So for what it's supposed to be, Brookline Lunch is good. Breakfast choices are pretty basic, food is made QUICKLY, large portions, and best of all, it's CHEAP. The eggs benedict is okay, the omelettes are decent and hearty, and the potatoes and veggies are consistently greasy but good. I've only had breakfast foods here, not sure about their other stuff.

I took off some points for hair found in my food more than once (but the truth is, I actually find hair in my food anywhere I go--seriously--and I'm convinced it's because I tend to look more than others. Good thing I don't really care that much), it's cash-only, they waiters are so busy they don't waste time trying to be friendly but that's fine, it's a little cramped so if you sit in the middle you get knocked a lot by the wait staff's elbows, and it can take a long time to get seated. This actually really sucks if you come in the winter because there is no indoor space to wait so you have to wait outside. And it gets really cold if you're eating near the door because it's such a tiny place. 

But as long as I live so close, I'll definitely keep coming back here for a quick CHEAP brunch with friends.
I have to say I often forget to come here, I will have to work on that. The food has been consistenly good when I have visited. Nice selection with a stand out veggie lomein.

the staff is amazing.  i go here almost every morning before work for a tasty bagel with the best hummus and what I consider the best brewed coffee in boston/cambridge.  this is what a coffee shop should be - independent, friendly staff, good coffee!  

i also like coming here in the afternoon for another coffee and sometimes a yummy cookie.  

beantowne offers a coffee card where you buy 10 coffees and get the 11th one for free.  they also put your coffee card into a weekly drawing for a free sandwich.  

as others have noted, this place is cash only.  so make sure you bring some money with you.

I, like many an office worker in Cambridge, felt the need to stop by for the grand opening of Saladworks at the Galleria, to see just who were these people that had the audacity to charge $8-9 for a salad.  And surprisingly enough, the price was actually worth it.

If you're still balking at the price tag, be aware that you're getting a restaurant-quality salad here.  You get your choice of over 50 fresh ingredients, over a dozen dressings, and a fresh warm bread roll with every order.  Add in soups and sandwiches, and you have yourself a pretty decent addition to the food court.

Everyone keeps saying that this place is great and I finally made it here.  I got the romantic wrap and the ginger crush, I also tasted some of the rebel and the island alive, all were fantastic!  The only thing working against the place was the crowd (although that's also a sign of good things) which unfortunately made service a little slow.  Luckily I wasn't in a rush.  Anyway, go there if you want delicious, healthy vegan and vegetarian food!

The B-Side reminds me of "The Max" from Saved by the Bell where those 90s teens hung out for burgers and shakes after detention with Mr. Belding...but instead it's a bunch of Cambridge 20-somethings who hang out for hard boiled eggs (FREE) and beers after detention with their full time jobs.

I love this place - it resembles a diner on the inside but you are most definitely in a bar. There are some tasty bar eats that I've picked at whilst lurking over a friend's shoulder, "Oh excuse me, I don't know how my face landed in your plate of French fries w/ blue cheese..."

I remember the last time I was at the B-Side I ordered a Harpoon beer and I was pleasantly surprised with a gigantically tall glass of the liquid goodness. And the price was more than affordable - cut to me Tipsy McGee.

The bar area is usually crowded along the sides, so beware if you need to make it to the bathroom--if you hold it too long you may get an elbow to the abdomen and sprinkle a tinkle in your denim. If the crowd isn't your scene, make your way to one of the many tables or booths in back- a waitress will find ya!

And it seems like every time I'm at the B-Side, I bump into someone I know outside on the corner - it's definitely one of those neighborhood joints. Check it out.

Great shabu shabu. I went with a friend after a long day of classes and per my usual hot pot routine, having eaten little that day and wearing my eating pants. My friend and I ordered the surf and turf platter with udon an add on dish of mushrooms and a plate of shrimp stuffed fish balls. We got the chicken herbal broth.

The food arrived quickly and was fresh smelling and looking. The seafood plate had a decent selection of fish and shellfish: clams, mussels, scallops, white fish, octopus, and shrimp. The meat plate was plentiful. The veggies featured wood ear mushrooms, white button mushrooms, assorted greens, giant hunks of cabbage, squash and possibly a few other things I'm forgetting. The shrimp stuffed fish balls were awesome. They were more like a dumpling stuffed with shrimp paste and roe. Biting into the ball squirted out a sweet spray of roe into my mouth. Surprising and delicious. the broth was good but didn't blow my mind. It was as good as any other place and they topped us up when we got low.

The surf and turf platter with 2 add on plates was more than enough for the two of us. I had to be rolled to the T to get home. I may have unbuttoned my top button and loosened my belt when I sat down. But you'll never know.

We went all out but prices aren't too bad considering how plentifully they fill the veggies, seafood and meat plates. I suppose you could save a few coins by ordering a la cart. the Add on/a la cart items are HUGE plates of food.

It is a little dark at the table but we had no problems talking or seeing our food. I'm going to need to drag other friends here for the food.

The only bad thing was that the soy on the table had some serious issues with fruit flies, not an unusual thing, but could easily be solved by popping the container in the fridge overnight (several nights in a row.)

An unassuming little breakfast joint.  So unassuming that I've been biking past it every day for two years now, and didn't even realize it was there until last week.

Incredibly cheap (you can get a nice filling breakfast for 5 bucks no problem) and very good as well.  Now a few words about what makes Brookline Lunch unique:  

1) They serve grilled vegetables as a side with most orders, making you proud for at least giving a token nod to good health as you add another dollop of maple syrup to your bacon.

2) The toast comes with some kind of smoked jelly, the likes of which I have never seen anywhere else.

3) The tables and furniture have a nice dinged up charm.  Kind of like the picnic bench by the archery shed at summer camp, only you don't feel sketchy eating off it.  A nice compliment to the crayola doodlings of dogs on the ceiling.
These people are lovely, and the food is fantastic. We had pancakes, and the club sandwich. It was among the best club sandwiches I've had.

The food is generally okay, even if sometimes it's not well cooked. Staff is rude, keeps people waiting and is incredibly slow when preparing the food. Felipe's is hands down the best place for Mexican food in Harvard square, despite the longer wait.
I ordered some takeout food- 2 noodle dishes. It was delicious, perfectly cooked veggies, and just the right amount of sauce! Yum!

The service at this food truck is incredibly warm and kind- truly great!

I had the grass-fed beef sliders, which were served on toasty, butter baguette slices with Cabot cheese. They were just phenomenal! I also had the fries, which were hand-cut and tasted like they were freshly made, seasoned with just the right amount of salt and pepper.


I can't wait to visit Compliments again to try more of their menu options!

Everything is a struggle here.  Whether I'm ordering a salad or sandwich for myself or for my company (we occupy the same building) it's a huge inconvenience or a big challenge for the staff.  I recently stopped in early to order an assortment of sandwiches for lunch pick-up.  It felt like the whole operation came to a halt while they tried to figure out if they could do it.  I wasn't even picky or specific, just give me 8 random sandwiches, I want to come back and pick it up in 90 minutes.  It always feels like I'm asking too much, or I should be so lucky if it turns out.  There are some really nice people who work here, and they do have a delicious chipotle chicken melt, but it doesn't quite makeup for the lack of confidence or capability.  Also, really inconvenient.  They used to take AMEX for large orders, but apparently they no longer do.

Ok, so I guess for mall salad, this is ok.  But for the price, I expect a lot more.  Their own salad creations don't do it for me, but it's best to order one and tweek it to your liking rather than creating your own.  It works out cheaper.  I wasn't overly impressed with anything I ordered.  It's pretty generic.  I thought their chicken was kind of gross.  It definitely is not real chicken breast, but some processed chicken meat.  And while they have a large assortment of dressings, they have very  few that are low fat, and no non-fat dressings at all.  

I did love their bread!

I went here again for lunch today and found out that when making your salad you can have lettuce and pasta as your base, and the pasta doesn't count as one of your toppings! Saladworks just gained itself a half star!
Very poor service.  Not interested in customers or what is available on the menu.  Very unpleasant experience and when management contacted about same, defended the second/third waiter.

Food was delicious, I got blueberry pancakes and hubs got croissant french toast both with a side of sausage. As previous reviewers have said the service is not the greatest (hostess didn't walk us to our table she pointed to it and handed us menus), but good atmosphere and definitely a crowd favorite as there was a wait on a Saturday morning.

Wish I could give Beantowne 3.5 stars...

Their sandwiches are great and are high quality, but their prices are pretty high for what comes down to a sandwich...$7.50 for a full size sandwich.  Like I said, great food, but it comes at a price.  I'd knock them down a half-star because of that.

The problem is spotty QC.  I came here once and got a salad that included about 60% of the listed ingredients, and was quite small (usually their salads are pretty fair) and another time, the wife got a sandwich that included about 3/6 items listed for it, but didn't discover it until she got home.  Almost 8 bucks for a sandwich that doesn't have the apple/cheese/sauce/etc. listed is garbage, and now I feel inclined to check my lunch before I leave so I don't get home and get disappointed.

I go back from time to time and it's good food but you do pay for it.
Amazing food and great atmosphere! Just opened...try the Chicken Tikka Masala and the the Rack of Lamb.

Ya know, I have been here once, and I was with my ex-girlfriend and her 1yr old son. I mean, it's not the Ritz, it's not anything particularly special. I got a burger with some home fries, and you know what, the burger was kind of pre-fab, but the home fries were quite good, they mixed in grilled vegies, and the large portion left me full for the rest of the day. Like I said, nothing fancy, but the whole tab for the entire lunch for the two of us, tip included was $20, and that was with an excessive tip. All in all a very cozy experience. The waitress couldn't have been nicer, and she couldn't have been better with kids, she had a whole bunch of tricks up her sleeve to keep a 1yr old amused. If only they had a liquor license for the people like me who need that beer for breakfast to take the edge off.
My favorite place for Indian food was Shalimar in Central Sq until I came here. Food was awesome! The waiters and manager are nice. I try to make it back there once a week.

This was our first time at a shabu place and it was definitely a great experience. We ordered a couple of meat combo platters along with two kinds of soups, tonkatsu and kimchi. The former was much more delicious but the kimchi was good too. We ordered the prime rib with our platters and it was amazing! 

The service was exceptional too, the wait staff kept checking on us, making sure we didn't run out of soup

Came here cause the bf had a groupon since they're promoting their new restaurant. They have a great location in the center of porter square, the convenience of a parking garage, but unfortunately I found the food to be disappointing. Ordered the:
1. Pork cheek yakitori - of all the dishes this was the best but pricey, 3 scantily clad skewers for $6 or 7 
2. oyster mushroom yakitori -nothing sexy about soggy mushrooms
3. Tried to order the hamachi collarbone but apparently they only have one a day
4. Fried oyster -came out piping hot, nice fried exterior, didn't enjoy the oyster itself though
5.spicy salmon and tuna roll - just your standard roll but pricey
6. Lovers roll - tuna, salmon avocado, crunchies, and roe topped with a sweet spicy sauce. Sauce was good, rice was hard and stale. No bueno.

The restaurant is huge btw, but when it's empty it's not as impressive :/ the magnetic belt rotating in the center for people doing hot pot was though. Prob won't be back

Looking for a place that has no pretensions of providing "fine dining?"  Just don't feel like eating in but don't want to blow the paycheck on the latest farm to table experience?  Frank's the place - OK food, reasonable prices,  friendly staff, good service.  For us old coots,  it's a relief to be free of the new Boston restaurant scene and just be able to have a decent meal and hear yourself talk.  Far from gourmet, but that's OK once in awhile.  The menu has been jazzed up a bit this year, but I stick to the old stuff.  Think 99 without the zillion TVs all tuned to sports.

I'm so glad this place opened up in the neighborhood. I've been searching for good Indian food around Cambridge and am always frustrated by how far I have to travel for it. I came here for dinner and the service was excellent--the servers were friendly and welcoming, and the restaurant had a wonderful neighborhood feel to it. The food was delicious, flavorful, and reasonably priced, as were the drinks. I will definitely be coming back here as often as I can, and can't wait to try lunch!

A nice coffee house and sandwich shop during the lunchtime hours.  The sandwiches are prepared fresh while you wait and they usually have enough staff during the rush to keep up and get your food prepared quickly.  Only quibble is that the prices are a bit higher than I'd like (about $6), otherwise I'd come here more often.

I went to the New England Food Truck Festival at Shoppers World last weekend and had some great food...Compliments was my least favorite.  They had a terrible system for taking orders, wouldn't take orders if they had too many orders backed up, which they always did!  The food I got was great, but I ordered french fries and they took over 15 minutes.  If you are in a rush, go someplace else.  If you have time, it might be worth the wait.
So good. Worth the long line. Amazingly delicious and fresh. Friendly staff and warm seating area downstairs. Thumbs up!
Woah! I just dropped what I was doing to write this review. Everything is immensely flavorful, I LOVE the cauliflower, and better yet, everything on the truck but the bread is gluten free!

Everyone keeps on using the word artery-clogger...yep. I've had the scrambles here, and they were okay -- definitely a good quantity for Boston brunch, but grease on grease on grease. I can't say it was terribly good quantity. The grilled cheese, same levels of grease, but actually pretty good. The jam on the side is a nice touch (some acidic food is needed to counteract the grease!) and the sweet potato fries are also good but not as much as the hype might suggest. Drinks are well-priced, though -- get the grand marnier milkshake or the mimosas. 

I think there are better brunch alternatives in the area. If you're going to come here, it's for the ambience, and the food is not worth the wait -- but if you still plan to wait, order the cayenne-cheddar bread.

Great food and service! The Malai Kofta was delicious, very flavorful and fragrant with just a bit of spice. Would be great if they asked how spicy dishes should be explicitly - I like my dishes hot!

My girlfriend got the vegetable korma, which she said was delicious. The vegetables still had crunch and texture so often lost in a korma, while the spices all came through individually. This place has amazing vegetarian options!

Overall, I'm super excited to have such a great Indian restaurant on Mass Ave. Will surely be a regular in the years to come!

Why doesn't Yelp allow me to give Darul kebab 0/5?

The only thing good about this restaurant is the first four letters in its name.

On a serious note, the (20$/8 pieces) "kebabs" were too fatty, tasteless and dry (considering we received no chutney with our order). 

Even after telling aunty that I would make her restaurant popular among students who only eat Halal meat, all she did for us was replace a dry naan and a decent raita with two cylinders (they were so shitty that they don't deserve the stature that seekh kebabs command).

The problems didn't immediately after consumption of (I repeat) dry and tasteless cylinders. My friend felt the cylinders tease the inside linings of her stomach well into the next day.

Gist: Do NOT make the MISTAKE of eating Darul Kabab, even if it is after consuming a lot of Daru.
Great lunch buffet for a good price. Nice variety and very tasty. Nice atmosphere too; love the decorations and the modern feel.

First time in the MIT area, and decided to meet old friends. We ordered 2 pizzas, and a mac and cheese. All of which are very rich in flavor and good portion to share. 

The environment is perfect for meeting with friends or even for business. Service was great, although I would highly recommend rsvp in advance to ensure you can minimize wait time!
Awful service! The hostess didn't have control on the waitlist at all. Not even an apology.

I've always ordered ahead and haven't eaten in the restaurant (yet), but each time the service is speedy and the serving size is well worth the amount you pay.  

I'm only in Boston for one year and I definitely plan on eating here as often as I can.

This is good Thai food for a good value. No fuss, no mess, no fanciness. If you are hankering for a big plate of deliciousness in the form of carbohydrates, this is the place to go. I always go for the drunken noodle and I always find myself hankering for it at some odd hour. If it's good enough to dream about it's probably good enough to yelp about. Thanks for being there Curry Thai. 

Note: I recommend pick up for the best experience. I've dined there and really didn't like it as much as eating the food in my own dining room, just FYI.

This is our go-to Thai restaurant in Cambridge, hands down. We usually just do take out, and we're always 100% satisfied with whatever dishes we order. Such a great restaurant. Could be overlooked due to the look and feel of the place, but worth a visit if you're looking for quality food.
Good burger and fries. I'd eat there again, no arguments. It's a small, old building, so I don't ding them for this, but be aware that the unisex bathroom is downstairs and is not accessible.

When I visited my friend in Boston, we went here from brunch/breakfast.  I had the sweet potato pancakes.  They were definitely unique but somewhat bland and a little dry.

Service was also slow.  I understand that a place can get busy, but it shouldn't take 10 minutes to bring a small container of milk.

Overall, Zoe's is just another diner.  Okay food at cheap prices.  Nothing spectacular.

lunch recently, ate at the bar....bartendress was great, helpful and attentive, some interesting drink cocktail items which my wife tried and enjoyed,  i had the brisket sandwich, great flavor, maybe a little too much roll, wife had the irish benedict with smoked char which was great, not a real big place but very nice and very good food...recommended!!!

We came here for a late lunch early dinner. 

For appetizer we tried the four piece soy garlic wings and the sautéed beef tongue. The wings came in three sizes: 4, 6, or 8 pieces. It also came in three different flavors. We only tried the soy garlic and do recommend this flavor. The chicken was crispy and the flavor was very good. I was not impressed by the sautéed beef tongue however. First off, it's not a dish for everyone and secondly, it does not help serving it pink! Sorry if I grossed anyone out. I hope you survived to the next paragraph. 

We tried the spicy Tonkatsu ramen. I have had better ramen in Harvard Square and Porter Square so its very hard for this bowl of ramen to even receive a passing grade. The broth was not flavorful despite the claim that it took over 12 hours to cook. And at $14, an additional side of pork slices brought the ramen close to $20. 

For hot pot, we had the seafood platter with the spicy Szechuan broth. The seafood were very fresh and the portion was fair for a hefty price tag of $35 not including broth. The platter came with a small basket vegetables and mushrooms, and your choice of either rice or noodle. The broth was well not as flavorful as other hot pot restaurants I have tried. For one person, you can easily spend $40 for a set and for the broth. The one good thing that I have to come back and try is the restaurants meat selection. Again, hefty price tags that go over $50 easily. My biggest complaint though was that there was no ladle for the broth and nothing other than the chopstick and a small soup spoon to cook and retrieve the food with. 

The soup dumplings took 15 minutes to make and the waiter did notify us of this wait time. I wished they had timed it better by bringing this out before our main entrees arrived. And I must say that I have had better soup dumplings in Chinatown also. There were nothing particularly special about the ones served here. The dough was thicker and the dumplings miniature in comparison with what I am used to. Also there was not much soup on the inside. However, I do like the fact that the restaurant steamed the dumplings on top of cabbage leaves. And I also like how the restaurant very thinly sliced the ginger that was in the vinegar dipping sauce. 

Overall, very pricey, which I don't usually mind if the food blew me away but it did not. The food was lacking in flavors in almost all the items we tried. Unfortunately I will not be coming back anytime soon. And if I do return, I would order hot pot only and not ramen or the appetizers which we tried. Please see my pictures.

A bit pricy (about $11 for salad and drink) but the best option in this food court, if you don't want ABP.  Salad comes with a roll, and is very customizable.  The food looks very fresh, and I like that there are labels to everything.

Wow!  Easily the most friendly service I've ever gotten at a chain fast food restaurant!

I like Qdoba when I'm in the mood for Qdoba, just like I like taco bell when in the mood for that.  And authentic Mexican food when I'm in the mood for that.  Today was a Qdoba day and it was very tasty.  

I was so surprised by how friendly they were.  Minus a star because the boyfriend said he felt rushed through the line, not necessarily a sentiment I shared, but if they make him upset, I'm upset.

Update:

Thursday's lunch was good as always.  The first two counter people encountered were pleasant and courteous.  The cashier was a little awkward, but perhaps he was new.  At least I finally got up to the 1000 point limit to get a free lunch next time I go in.

We just moved a couple of blocks away and finally decided to walk in... I'm definitely becoming a regular! Great options on draft and really friendly service! What else can you ask for? Definite coming back
Really good guys, I've been following them for a while. Glad they're doing big things with the new brick-and-mortar spot.

My girlfriend and I went around 7 and we were the only ones there. The food was excellent and they serve breakfast all day. I highly recommend breakfast food and my girlfriend recommends the steak. The waitress was very quiet and a little slow to take our order but overall this is a must go if you are looking for a quiet place to eat with high quality food!

This is one of the healthier options in the food court. The Buffalo Blue salad is by far the best. Along with being one of the healthier options it is also one of the pricier options too. About $7.50 / salad. 

If you come here often I recommend signing up for their rewards system. On your birthday they give you a free salad -- you don't even have to come on your birthday to receive it!

The girl taking orders was SUPER nice...maybe too nice...maybe focusing a bit too much on taking the orders and being friendly than getting orders out. Or maybe it was that it was a newer food truck that made the food take a bit longer. I don't hold it against them, as I've seen enough TV shows/episodes about how hard working out of a food truck is.

I got the grilled cheese with tomato and it was delicious. There are three types of cheese in it including sharp cheddar and swiss (I forgot the other...) but maybe it needs a bit more as the tomato was sliding out. The bread and grill on that was perfect though.

Another person had his order given to someone else and the girl taking orders gave him a free coke and doughnut-muffin which he didn't want and I had. It was great--covered in sugar, so what's not to love. Tasted a bit like apple pie which is what they were going for. A bit dense, so not good for sharing (couldn't tear it apart).

Really awesome place in Cambridge, the owners Sonia and Bill are very charming and offer exceptional service. The sushi is excellent, and I highly recommend trying the seared scallops. Hands down the best Japanese sushi place in the Boston area!

B-Side reminds me of an edgier version of The Peach Pit.  They share the same retro diner charm but B-Side's cuisine is definitely a few notches above the burgers Nat was flipping on the grill.  Their home fries are some of the best in the city and my friend's egg tortilla with salsa was tasty as well.  If you could just get Dylan McKay to hang out here, I'd be a regular.

I've actually eaten here once before, and the food was pretty good.  We decided to order delivery from them for dinner one night.  Only one problem: the food never showed.

I called them after waiting an hour, and was assured that the food was "right around the corner."  I called a half hour later (90 min wait time), and again was assured that the food was close.  After another half hour (120 minutes) I called again, and was told that the delivery person was "stuck in Boston" and was still 15-20 minutes away.  This seemed slightly nonsensical, since I live less than 2 miles from the restaurant in Somerville, but we agreed to wait the addition 15-20 minutes for our food.  When the delivery person hadn't shown up 2.5 hours after we had placed our order, I called the restaurant again.  They told me they had canceled our order because of the delay.  Though we were promised a refund, my delivery.com account (which I placed the order through) does not reflect that.

It was pretty shitty of them to put us through that and then cancel our order.

We've been coming here every so often for the past 4 years and not once can I say we were disappointed with anything. 
And as far as the prices go for the area, you can't complain. Overall just very consistent good food.

I feel bad dumping on Darul but they really don't understand how to run a restaurant. Darul's food is decent. Not amazing but not terrible. They used to have an incredible takeout business before they redid their dining room and tried to make it more upscale with the whole white tablecloth thing. Turns out they really turned off their existing customers (us and many others) and as a result have only a few customers a night. They no longer carry the takeout samosas that we used to love. They filled a great spot for quick, affordable ethnic food in the neighborhood. They've really lost their niche with this new plan. They're now just one of many overpriced ethnic restaurants in the area. I really wish they understood this and their market because I hate walking by everyday and seeing 3 people in their dining room. They're now trying the buffet thing which is a good idea given there are no ethnic buffets in the area. The only problem is that they have become wrapped up in having elaborately expensive dishes like fish and lamb curries. As such the buffet is terribly over priced at $11 for lunch and $13 for dinner. Again, they've priced themselves out of the market. I really hope they figure their offering and market out. My husband called to ask the buffet price and the owner was defensive and explaining all of the expensive things on the buffet. The only problem is that the target market doesn't care about the lamb or fish curries! Know thy market and it's a chicken tikka masala and garlic nan one (even if it's incredibly inauthentic and a perverted version of indian cuisine). Here's to hoping they read this and figure their stuff out!

I'm not a coffee drinker myself, but I often hit this place up for lunch.  The sandwiches are good and just the right size.  The staff is friendly and patient and the atmosphere is a definite plus.  While I don't drink coffee, I can vouch for the astounding hot chocolate.

On the down side, they do not accept credit cards.  They state that this is to keep the lines moving and the prices low, but when done right plastic is faster than cash and their prices are hardly low.  Can't have it all, I suppose.

love the music, the atmosphere, but would love if there were more beers there. i'm somewhat a beer snob, i guess, so i can't be too upset with a place that has hard boiled eggs for peeps at the bar while they're waiting for their food....

I'm from New York, so my diner standards are high. Came here with a big party and the servers were so accommodating! Made sure we were well taken care of, had refills of coffee, etc. Came for breakfast and the home fries, French toast, and scrambled eggs did not disappoint.

The restaurant seems to have an identity crisis as they have a huge menu serving all types of Japanese food, none which was impressive.  The sushi served wasn't high quality nor was it cheap which explains the meh rating.  The service was good, but it's also wasn't crowded.  I'm going to continue to search the area until I find a better spot.

I don't know how to start writing this review without drooling all over my keyboard. I'm not sure its physically possible. 

Pretty eyes (fried pickles): fried crunchy goodness of a PERFECT pickle. And those who favor pickles over plain old cucumbers know, it is very very hard to find a perfect pickle. Some are too sweet. Some - too salty. Some have a consistency of a jelly fish, which personally I find the most disterbing. So koodos to you guys for finding the perfect pickle. But enough about pickles. 

I would like to chat about I'd Like To Sea You Later (tuna melt). The combination of bread, the yummy spead, cheeses and the tuna - my mind is spinning trying to come up with the perfect word(s) to describe this, but I am failing miserably. English language is simply not vast enough (or my vocabulary is limited, which could be the case). Its like the first really warm day in the spring - the kind of warm where you can take off your jacket because it is actually too hot to wear it, not because you are trying to prove you can handle anything. Magically delicious. 

Anyways. Just go to these guys. Follow them on Facebook, too - so you can keep track of their schedule, events and menu items.

I haven't tried any of their sandwiches, but my colleagues rave about them all the time. I got their fried cauliflower which was a good choice by me bc it was yum. It wasn't heavy, and they did a good job of frying it bc there wasn't much excess oil. The owners were so so nice, and I like how the truck is simply white without any unnecessary signage.

I have had so much better. Salads have old ingredients with sloppy dressing that smells and tastes nasty and staff are rude and obnoxious. There are so many better choices why would you over pay at a place like this?

Pretty solid American Diner and Greek-American fare. I walked in with no wait on a Thursday evening. The gyro was huge and delicious. The cole slaw wasn't great, although I'm pretty picky about cole slaw (and I love a good one, so I'm always willing to try a new one). They seem to have their own house root bear and my root bear float totally hit the spot. My friend thoroughly enjoyed her baby spinach salad and the waitress was friendly and very careful about my friend's nut allergy (the receipt/order ticket read: ALLERGIC to WALNUTS!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!). I love that they serve breakfast all day and will certainly be back to try it.

Minus one star for the sketchy bathrooms down a dark hall- the womens was closed for renovations (a plus that they are being fixed?) so you had to use the mens- cautiously making sure no one was in using the urinal, first!!

BROOKLINE LUNCH ROCKS BREAKFAST!! (been a few times now)

I finally decided to give this dinky dive a try for breakfast. My wife had mentioned it a few times, but we never took the time to go check it out. I wonder why they don't call it Brookline Breakfast, since they seem to be more known for their breakfast than lunch.

As it turns out, today was a rainy Friday morning at about 9:30am. Walked right in, picked a seat, nice young man came and took my order, and I kid you not in 7 minutes was back with my food.  It was delicious and CHEAP! 2 eggs and bacon, with home fries for $3.75???! Yup!  Home fries were good and greasy, bacon wasn't all crunchy (I like soft bacon), and the eggs were perfect.

The place was clean, and had kinda weird paint on the walls (designs), and the enormous a/c unit hanging above the entrance looked like it was a lawsuit getting ready to happen at any moment.

I don't know if I'd be so impressed if I had to wait outside in a line, or if I had experienced slow service, both of which other Yelpers have mentioned. I guess I'll only go on weekday mornings, and avoid weekends.

Oh, I did meet the owner lady and her baby who was providing us with screeching and yelling child-made background music. Owner was very nice to me.

Qdoba offers a totally chain-like experience in every way.  Genericized, bland and not authentic/special in the least.  Definitely not an Anna's Taqueria or Boca Grande replacement by far.  Just big, many locations and more available.

As an example, a much better by far in my personal opinion is:  http://www.annastaqueria.com/locations.htm

I've honestly only been here once, but on hearing that it's closing, I felt I needed to write a review while they're still open.

This place feels strangely like a 50s diner, except updated and with an impressive cocktail menu and much better patrons.  This isn't a typical Boston bar, which makes it better than almost any other bar I've been too in the area.  It has a gritty, retro feel and generally features some of the best crowds of Cambridge.  I would get drunk here any time.

This place was packed at 11 on a Saturday morning, we almost left because they couldn't accommodate a  table for two without a reservation. Luckily a spot at the bar opened up right before we were about to walk out the door. Overall I would say the idea is there.. cool decor, trendy "hipster" employees, a few very different and original items on the menu. But I wouldn't say I will go back next time I'm in town. It was pricey in my opinion, although you do get your moneys worth (Big portions) just wasn't my favorite, everything came out okay, I just thought my food was bland.

Visiting from out of town and met up with some friends here for a midweek breakfast. The tables were rather yucky. I couldn't tell if they'd been "cleaned" with something sticky or if they just had a lot of grease on them from who knows where. I could tell that they had been wiped, but I used up quite a few napkins to wipe off the yuck. This was not a good start to the experience.

The space was cold. I was wearing many layers and I was still feeling chilly. 

Now for the more positive aspects. The food was completely reasonable breakfast/bunch food. I had the french toast, eggs, bacon dish and it was all pretty good so no complaints there. They don't have fresh-squeezed orange juice. The final payment was extremely reasonable for everything we had consumed so that was a welcomed ending to the visit.

I enjoyed the art on the walls that they are selling for very reasonable prices.

Downright good salad and sammich place in a mall food court! Went with a sandwich and the bread and ingredients were fresh, crisp, and tasty! Price was a little high for a sandwich, chips, and a bottle drink though.

QDOBA should have received a higher review if it wasn't for the disrespectful customer service. 

This is a build your own Mexican food type concept. I got the burrito with steak meat, guacamole, salsa verde, cheese, white rice, and black beans. One plus is that they do not charge any extra for the guacamole which is rare to find. I got chips and salsa on the side which were very tasty along with a soft drink. The soft drinks are in the Coca-Cola machine which allows you to make your own concoction which is great. The overall quality of the meat and the burrito were great. When comparing it to Chipotle I actually find this place tastier. 

With regards to price for my meal it was about $13. Which is slightly above average for this type of concept but it is within reason. The free guacamole I believe makes up for the price. 

Now the customer service was horrible. If I was the owner of this business, I would have fired the entire staff on the spot. Most of the people they hire seem to not appreciate the job they are given. They all look bored to be there and none of them care to help you. The male workers kept give me bad stares as if they were looking for an issue. I just minded my own business and kept to myself. The one female employee was doing her make-up in the middle of the kitchen which I thought seemed pretty unsanitary. When they finally assist you they have major attitude. Two of the cooks were staring at me and laughing, there was nobody behind me, so it is obvious they were making fun of me for some odd reason. No wonder why it was the only place that wasn't crowded on a rainy day in Harvard Square. 

The restaurant appearance was fairly clean, however, the restroom needed some cleaning. 

Even though the food was great, I would unlikely come again as I felt pretty uncomfortable the first time and wouldn't want to endure that again. This is a great food concept but they need to step up their customer service if they want to last around here.

Love this place. Tried it for the first time today with a friend while wandering around Cambridge, really happy we did. Awesome food, easy going and cool staff. Our server, Jess I believe her name was, was super nice and eager to help. Will definitely be going back next time in Cambridge.

Curry Thai was a restaurant within walking distance of our hotel on our recent trip to Boston.  The food was tasty, though nothing spectacular, and relatively inexpensive.  It's the kind of fare you expect near a college campus.  I had the drunken noodles, a favorite Thai dish of mine, and the dish was done well.  My partner had the Tofu gaprow, which wasn't tofu at all, but a fake meat made with wheat gluten, and not a very good fake meat at that.  I probably should have shared my superior dish with him, but I was mean and didn't.  He's gotten over it.  The coconut juice drink was lovely.

10 dollars for a salad? Ugh, alright. Nothing healthy around. 10 dollars for a gross salad? No thanks. You're better off eating a burger from BK for lunch and eating a salad you prepare for yourself later. It will be unhealthy, but at least you'll save a lot of money.  Weird weird chicken that didn't taste like chicken, weird tasting lettuce.. just not good. Nice people working there though, and the bread was tasty.

Nice pIzza and even nicer garlic knots. Whoa. The fire roasted pizzas are cooked very well in a hot wood fired oven leaving some well done patches on the pie. If you like it that way it's perfectly done.

Hyperboles aside, real talk: I tried between 50-250 different kinds of food at the BLFF this year. No, I did not eat anything else for the following 27 hours. Yes, I fell into a slightly worrisome food coma on the T had to Thanksgiving-style waddle my way from the silver line to the blue line.

Now that you understand how disgustingly much I consumed in three hours, absorb the magnitude of this statement: Compliments served the best thing I had all day. Of potentially 250 things. Some of which were chocolate-covered. Compliments still topped them all.

Enter the Falling in Love sandwich: a localicious (it's a word now), sweet take on the classic grilled cheese. Compliments turned the concept snobby and exponentially more delicious in one swift motion. Toasted walnut buckwheat bread filled with goat cheese, honey and apples... I definitely pulled a "this is what an orgasm looks like" upon first bite. No shame.

I can say with confidence that this may be the best sandwich that has ever found its way into my mouth. After I track down Compliments again, something tells me i'll be "Falling in Love" i.e. - making inappropriate noises in public, on the regular. Seriously, if you can keep a straight face while eating this, you're dead inside.
After more than a year going to BeanTowne it continues to please in both product and service. Keep it up!

took kind of a long time to order and i think it's a tad more expensive than felipes. 
but, happy customer

I dunno, I didn't really like this place much. The food was alright, but nothing special. I ordered the crazy roll, mackerel roll, Alaska roll, chicken yakitori appetizer, and chicken wings. The sushi was alright, and the chicken overall was alright. I dunno, maybe it was the restaurant itself. It was totally empty and felt cold. I don't think I'd be back soon.

Simply love this place!!! But the Brussels sprouts are out of this world!!! They deserve a gold medal!!! Seriously?!!! How does one make Brussels sprouts taste that good!????  The chicken is perfect! The burgers are EXACTLY like their slogan says " how a burger should be"!! The girl at the register was an absolute sweetheart and so helpful! I love that you can get your burger on a bed of lettuce, as I can't eat the bread !!! You'll never find a better place!!! GET THE DANG BRUSSELS SPROUTS!!!

If I could eat here everyday, i would (and never get sick of it!) It gets super busy, so I like to call ahead and order and that makes the process so much better than waiting in line for 30 mins! They have live music somedays during the week, too, which is fun to eat and hang around and listen.

I visited this place for the first time. I knew exactly what i wanted. I ordered the Haleem and Chotpoti as the Appetizer. The Chotpoti was an absolute disaster. The Lamb Meat in the Haleem was very chewy and would take a couple of hundred years to get over each morsel.
Finally, i went ahead and ordered Chicken Tikka Masaala, Dal Makhani and Chicken Curry with Garlic Naan.
The Chicken Curry was cooked well and had all the spices right ! The Chicken Tikka did not turn me on ! 
The food overall was dripping with oodles of oil. So if you fancy good oily Bangladeshi food minus the ambiance ( which apparently did not exist !!!) then try this place.
Nothing too great but it is well worth a second try !
Vic

For such a healthy, "live foods" cafe they could at least make a green juice correctly. I got the Life Alive juice, which promises apple, beets, carrots, celery, kale, and wheatgrass. I understand that this means the portion of kale will be small, but I expected some sort of kale leaves in my juice. Instead, they only used kale stems. He juice tastes and looks like apple-carrot-beet juice. It's very boring. I'm glad I don't live here because this is a pathetic excuse for a juice. 

Other than that, the food dishes look good. The wait for juice was pretty long (10 mins) after a long wait in line (6 mins). People here think they're pretty special with their healthy food, but when they cut corners is it really that healthy?

We sat outside because it was absolutely freezing inside the restaurant. The outside eating area was teeming with cockroaches. There was a manhole next to our table on the sidewalk with cockroaches running in and out of the air vents and among the tables. The staff didn't seem to mind, they just stepped over them and sort of apologized when I screamed because a roach was crawling into my purse. I think a roach crawled onto a woman sitting at the table next to us because she screamed and jumped out of her seat too. The food was really good, but the cockroaches ruined the meal. To management, you really need to do something about the infestation.

I don't know why this place does not have a higher rating.  I've eaten there a few times and it has always been good.  Nice char on the steak and perfectly cooked.   The French onion soup is delicious, too.  Yes, it's old school.  But I'm not paying $52 for a steak.  That's just me.  For $20 you can get a satisfying steakhouse quality meal. Staff is always friendly.
Well, we're back! We haven't seen The Worst Waitress Ever in some time, so we're now visiting Zoe's again regularly. Breakfast all day, everything is delicious, and we're happy this all worked out!

5 stars for this place if it wasn't so pricey. I tend to eat my salads sans meat so I personally think they should have different prices depending on your toppings when you make your own. The salads do come with a roll though.

That being said...I work right next to the Galleria and this is definitely the best/healthiest option in the food court. They have a pretty wide selection of toppings (love the artichoke hearts and the fact that they have a dark salad mix, not just iceberg) and I really love any place that mixes the salad with the dressing for you. The servings are pretty big and come in cute white dishes. I'll definitely be frequenting Saladworks.
Beantowne coffee is a good spot to get coffee,pastries,sandwiches and baked goods. The staff is great friendly and they get you in & out very quickly. The prices are very reasonable.

I can definitely see this place becoming famous and a new hot spot! It is truly a hidden gem. It's located in technology square which I think is becoming more popular. I've lived in Cambridge for 2 months and have already been there several times. 

I love that everything is cooked in the brick oven, it's incredible! Of course the pizzas are to die for!! The menu changes almost everyday but the dishes are always incredible.

I recommend the chicken as well, it is the juciest, most flavorable chicken I have ever tasted in my life. The menu fits everyone's tastes, they have great options. 

The place has a modern, yet cozy atmosphere. It is not necessarily casual but not necessarily fancy, which is perfect! You can go with friends or on a date. The place is very eclectic which I think is perfect in these times.

The service was great! The servers were friendly, the cooks are always up for talking to the customers, and the manager, Mike, is super friendly. I've never seen a manager so involved in everything up front, he serves at the bar and talks to the customers. It's so great!

I recommend this to anyone and everyone. No doubt I will be back there very soon.

Gross. I went twice and felt sick both times afterwards. I know I know, fool me once, shame on you, fool me twice, shame on me. I can't understand how they stay in business aside from having massive funding from Jack in the Box.

This place is terrific! The staff is so friendly and quick, and they have a great selection selection. They call themselves a coffee shop but don't be fooled, on top of a consistently great cup of coffee you can also get a wonderful lunch there for a very reasonable price. I highly recommend the Pollo panini wrap, you can get a half or a whole of all the sandwiches, never disappointing. Working in Kendall square, they are my go to for coffee runs and quick lunches to go. Only downside is they are not open on weekends, Beantowne, we want you every day of the week!

My husband and I used to get takeout here a lot, but then didn't for a long time. The food was inconsistent at times, but always good. We only stopped going here because our habits changed. Then one day when I was home sick I came back to get lunch to go. The soup I got was good, but the entree was obviously leftover and reheated. I was very surprised and disappointed. After reading some of the other recent reviews it seems like Curry Thai has really gone downhill. I don't think we'll be back, sadly.

My DH and I went here with a Groupon recently and had no preconceived notions other than having read the mixed reviews on Yelp. I am happy to say we enjoyed our meal!

We decided ahead of time that hot pot could wait for another time and focused on apps and sushi. We started with a large hot sake which the server said to let cool for 3 minutes but was never very hot to begin with. We ordered fried oysters (because lots of people raved about them in their reviews) and yellowtail collar. Our oysters arrived relatively quickly and needed time to cool; they came with mayo and spicy mayo, and were tasty.

We put in an order for some maki rolls and they arrived before the collar, so be warned that they must need extra time to prepare that. Once it did arrive, o.m.g. was it delicious!  I love yellowtail sushi (my husband is less of a fan) but the broiled collar was smoky and moist and yum. It looked like "two dead birds" with the fins attached, according to DH, and it did maybe a little. :) Tasted a lot like swordfish.

Sushi was fresh and delicious, which was encouraging since some reviews dissed the sushi. No conveyor belt sushi on weekends which makes sense I guess. I think plates only have 2 rolls on them anyway so it all works out in the end. Service was good; only hiccup was when waiting for the credit card/Groupon to be processed but otherwise a good experience.
Been here a couple of times and tried both A la carte as well as their Lunch Buffet. Nothing special, their Buffet Spread was Ok-ish and wasn't really great.

Grilled cheese sandwich with the fries were good but their selection in beverages was poor....they only have Diet Pepsi and Mountain Dew...nothing else...they give water with no ice and the place was pretty hot. Just need to step it up a bit on beverages.

Obama is a fan so it must be good! I really like their coffee bar (iced soy lattes are phenomenal) and their white clam pizza with bacon is out of this world. They use high quality ingredients and you can tell. 

Be warned though that waits are ridiculous especially on weekends.

I go here almost every day with friends from work on our coffee break.  

BeanTowne is definitely my favorite coffee shop in the area.  The staff are really friendly.  The coffee is fresh and brewed well; interesting special varieties of coffee are rotated in weekly.  The hot cocoa is thick and satisfying.  As far as the food goes I appreciate that they use substantial French bread instead of fluffy stuff that's mostly air like many delis use.

Visited this place on a Saturday morning with a couple of my friends.
There were American breakfast items ,some of them with a Greek touch like a Greek omelet with feta,olives, which I have ordered.As far as the taste of my omelet is concerned,I have had better ones
The food is decent .There weren't any items that I recall were great nor any of that were bad.
I felt the place could have been a little more tidy .

This has been one of my favorite places for at least ten years. The food is absolutely delicious, the home fries and orange juice are the best, and the decor is so amazing. Don't forget to ask for crayons - they;ve got pin-up style drawings to color while you wait!

Gotten delivery a few times and it's not bad but not necessarily good either. Lomein uses these weird thick noodles, like udo, which I hate, so FAIL. 

Chicken dishes are passable, beef egg rolls are like a grease hand grenade. 

Decent scallion pancakes, fried dumplings, and crab rangoon, though. 

Basically go here for the AmeriChinese cuisine and quick delivery and expect exactly what you think you will get.

Very friendly staff, great music always playing in the background and good food. I have not tried enough things on the menu to give a quality review of this place overall, but I love it here for the familiar and friendly faces I always see behind the counter and the overall atmosphere. 

Note: They do not take credit cards.

First, I am grateful that they have a salad place at the Food Court.
Pros:
Salad greens are fresh
The line moves fast
They would make minor subsitutions
Nice takeot containers

Cons
The dressing (Vinaigrette) cloyingly sweet.
Chicken in the salad is of a mass-produced pale strange-textured variety

My search for the perfect cocktail has come to an end!  The A4 Electrical Fire is the most wonderful balance of smoky, savory, spicy, earthy, freshness I have ever experienced.  Inspired by the unfortunate electrical fire that burned down Area Four's sister restaurant, this drink is a true work of art.  Many thanks to Josh W. and Rairon for their creativity and skill.

My first experience at shabu & mein was amazing. The staffs/bartenders in particular was on point. They absolutely know how to treat their customers. 

By far the best hot pot and ramen broth in Cambridge area! You must try the ramen, it was worth every dollar! My most favorite is the lobster rangoons, soup dumplings, & pork buns. 

I'll definitely recommend this venue to all my friends!

Authentic Japanese ramen. I haven't had ramen this good since I lived in Japan. I ordered the vegetarian ramen with mushrooms, in mushroom broth. Service was exceptional; efficient and friendly. If I lived in this neighborhood I would go to this place once a week!

Pretty good breakfast place in Cambridge. I love all the old school decorations, lots of old street signs and advertisements almost like a route 66 place. Food is good quality, I had the pumpkin pancakes - really good. It is kinda hard to find in relation to where most apps show it to be, sort of around back.

When I was much younger, before I moved to Asia, I used to love places like Pu Pu Hotpot. You could get the same stuff wherever you went, with varying quality, you had to ask for chopsticks, and little American kids like me couldn't honestly tell the difference between what was authentic Chinese food and what wasn't. Heck, most of the "authentic" stuff looked pretty gross, anyhow.

Fast forward about 10 years, and I suppose I have to come out and admit it: I'm no gourmand, but I 'm pretty snobby about my Asian cuisine. In the intervening time between now and my younger years, I lived where you get the real thing, and I know how stuff should taste. So when I talk about how Americanized Chinese food sucks the balls, I'm not joking. 

I had the unfortunate duty of eating takeout from Pu Pu Hotpot one day during the MIT debate tournament. The selection criteria for the MIT debate team was "what can we get on our budget?" and in this I suppose the restaurant performed admirably. But barring the sudden implosion of all nerves in my brain devoted to culinary skill, I will not ever eat here voluntarily. 

Pu Pu Hotpot might appeal to you if you're just looking to wet your feet in the whole Asian cuisine thing. To me, though, it's an exercise in subliminal confusion - everything's so wrong. The rice isn't sticky enough, the food is impregnated with far too many sesame seeds and not enough flavor, and the menu selection mixes and matches random bits of the eight Chinese food families with badly rendered Americanized dishes invented by wealthy overseas Chinese chefs in the last eighty years. 

I'll give it 2 stars since it didn't exactly make me sick, but if you're looking for something that will stimulate your Asian side, I'm afraid you will be disappointed.

After a several year hiatus from Yelping (for many varied reasons) I just wanted to give props to McCabe's On Mass. In the interest of full disclosure I frequented Spirit quite a bit from the time it opened until the time it closed and loved the place (not sure why the hate for Spirit in many reviews). I followed the progress on McCabe's from inception through opening as it's 2 blocks from mi casa and having great faith in the owner and his partners that this would be something completely different. Boy was that an understatement. 

First on atmosphere. The place looks great and is very comfortable. I'm a bar patron and the bar is the center of the M.O.M. universe. Service is top notch with great, attentive bartenders always ready with suggestions, recommendations and good conversation. I rarely sit at the tables but the times I have the servers have been great. I know a lot of people have been kind of qualifying this place as a "hipster" kind of bar. Well as the least hip, least trendy human being alive I can tell you there are very few bars I feel more comfortable and more at home at then here. While my multiple nights at the bar days are sadly behind me I consider McCabe's my local

OK, once again in the interest of full disclosure the awesome beer selection is wasted on me as I'm a macro-beer drinker (and yes they have my beloved Miller Lite) but my beer nerd friends rave about the selection. The food is the star of the show. I tend to end up here between meals or late night so I've mostly had the shared plates versus entrees. Get the bacon appetizer. Just get it. I've called this the best $5 spent in the city of Cambridge. The sriracha wings are just plain awesome as are the duck confit sliders (a lot of people mention "salty". I think they're perfect). The short rib is luxurious. While I haven't had it yet the brisket sandwich is on my radar for sure

Bottom line is this is a comfortable, friendly, unpretentious food forward spot perfect for anything from date night to group gatherings to just sitting at the bar. Just go, you won't be disappointed

Came here for brunch w/ friends a few months ago and really loved the look & feel of this neighborhood hangout. I'm always on the hunt for good breakfast, lunch & dinner, and this is def a place I'll have to visit again to explore the rest of the menu. The service was friendly, the scene relaxed and a little hungover on a sunday, and the music was great. i have big plans to come back again to try some of their other menu items and hang out at the bar.

Caution! This restaurant does not manage their online reservations. If you make an online reservation (on Open Table for instance) and arrive to a busy restaurant, your reservation will not be honored. They do not remove times from online reservation sites when they do not have space and they do not integrate the online reservations with their hand-written paper list. After making a 7pm reservation online, we showed up right at 7 and were told it was going to be a long wait because there were several people ahead of us on the paper list and we'd be put at the bottom. Needless to say we walked out immediately and went elsewhere.

I went to Frank's last week for the first time after going by it hundreds of times.  The good news is we didn't have to wait to be seated - it was fairly empty when we got there but the place filled up fast.  The bad news is we waited a long time once we did get seated - the service was poor but I think we got the lousy waitress - the tables around us didn't seem to have the same problem - I'd say the food was average - both in terms of quality and price - I've got a buddy that raves about it - to each their own.

My Jr. High School sweetheart had the name Zoe. When I think of her and that time I think wholesome goodness (at least superficially). So, as I dodged traffic, biking my way down Mass Ave. to meet a friend for brunch, I had this vague impression of alfalfa sprouts poking out of a fat omelet. 

When I passed through the door, sweaty and breathing heavy, I realized that the place was operating on a leftover business model from the late-80s that imitates the mid-50s. There was cheezy 50s music coming out of flush mounted ceiling speakers and furnishings that vaguely resembled something old.

I think it's what happens when someone who has lost touch with the diner scene opens a diner. They must think all the real diners are gone so they imitate something they saw in Diner or American Graffiti. They're not all gone! 

Those old school diners with good home cooking and street-smart waitresses are half the reason I write breakfast reviews. The other half is split up between hippie havens, retro-diners done right, the occasional French bistro and a hundred other eggy variants. 

Now, a little cliche musicology never bothered a potato or an egg so much so I decided not to make like a tree and leaf. (That's on with the cliche theme y'all!)

After some diner standard coffee (the kind you pretty much enjoy drinking because of a long-held habit but wonder if it's really having an effect), and a heaping helping of good small talk, my Mediterranean Omelet arrived. 

A word about the menu: it has just about what you might expect. The omelet selection is exciting at first, but has typical combos like Italian, Mediterranean, Broccoli and Cheese. 

First, let me say, I did eat everything and I was pretty satisfied. But my nitpicky campaign for breakfast improvement side has got to put a few words in, especially at $8 an entre. 

1. An omelet is not just a folded scrambled egg/veggie pancake. 

There is a technique to making a French omelet. You cook it at high heat and keep lifting it with the spatula so the runny egg on top can slide to the bottom and cook. You don't just cook the hell out of it or bake it. 

Zoe's omelets have the hell cooked out of them. They come out all brown and burny on the edges. Sacrilege! Also, all the ingredients are cooked into the egg, instead of being folded inside, so you loose that lovely layered thing omelets were invented for. 

2. Fried potatoes can be delicious.

Unfortunately, potatoes lie on the front lines of breakfast industry between taste and efficiency. Every place has a different method for keeping a mound of them ready for action. 

I'm guessing, but I think Zoe's uses the keep-a-mound-on-the-grill-all-day method. Which is great for the cook but somewhat hazardous to the palate (and I think I heard somewhere that overcooked grease ain't so good for ya). With this method the grease really soaks into the potatoes and becomes one texture. 

I'm still guessing, but it could very well be that the busier Zoe's is, the fresher the potatoes get because they cycle through the grill faster. 

3. They didn't have squeezey plastic Heinz bottles in the 50s. 

Okay, now I'm just being cranky. 

Overall, Zoe's ain't bad, but it ain't what it used to be. Er, I mean what it pretends to used to be. 

 If you can stand Presley's "Hound Dog" five times in a row (for real!) check it out. Don't bother telling the waitress about that though, she's "immune to it."

This review is for the rebuilt Cafe after the fire. 

Coffee - First things first - Best Cold Brew in town. The main reason I keep coming back is for when I need the best pick me up I know I can count on the that Cold Brew. 

Breakfast - This is why its three stars. I am always on the search for a good breakfast place around Kendall. I have tried the breakfast sandwiches here along with the breakfast bowl. Both times were a disappointment. The eggs are all pre-cooked and you can taste it and getting something on a bagel is just all bread and they are not that soft. Today I went for the breakfast bowl thinking ok can't mess this up. The egg are just slices of the pre-cooked egg they put on the sandwich, the hash is your normal hash ect. Needless to say that was not finished and thankfully my office mate offered me his chobani. 

Lunch - The soups are normally very good, along with the sandwiches. I tend to go with half and half even the salads are pretty nice so good job here. 

Overall the place is good but man that breakfast needs to help, until then it's here for coffee and different location to grab breakfast.

These guys are the BEST! Their food is so fresh and delicious! They make everything from scratch and you can tell its make with love. I had the grilled cheese with tomato and It was so yummy...healthy comfort food at its best....
My favorite breakfast place. The Pantry in New Haven is also in the conversation. Both are in cities I love. Both are run by family's.

I'm vegan, and they have great vegetarian options.  Delicious, authentic Thai food in a great location!  Decor and atmosphere are very nice.  The service is excellent and the wait staff are very friendly!  One of my go-to dinner spots.

Traveled to Boston for a week long conference. Did my usual Yelp search for Vegan Restaurants and decided on Life Alive -based on menu, reviews and photos. Ordered the Jive Alive Juice, Lentil Soup and The Rebel Bowl.

OMG! Everything was fresh, delicious and pleasantly presented. I placed my order over the phone, and just as previous reviewers stated - the line to place an order was out of the door at 6pm.

Will definitely eat at Life Alive for the remainder of my stay in Boston!

Oh my steak!! I went to Franks with a group of friends for a birthday dinner. The service was pretty decent considering we arrived at 10:00pm. I ended up deciding to split the Tomahawk steak with the birthday boy since he wanted it. It was 3 pounds of steak. The food was great and the steak was cooked perfectly. All together we had a great night at Franks. The prices were pretty fair for a steak house.
This was one of the worst salads I had, stale and all wet. If you want a salad at the Galleria, go to D'Angelo's instead.

Came here for brunch. We got two orders of duck confit hash and a breakfast burger - all delish. Servers were friendly and the bar looked well stocked. Excited that there's a great brunch place so close to me in Porter. Will be back to try the rest of the menu!

I was happy to finally find a diner in Boston (who knew?) and The Friendly Toast wasn't disappointing.  I got the Almond Joy Pancakes which were HUGE.  I would recommend just chocolate chip pancakes- the coconut baked into the batter made the pancakes crumbly, but they were still really good.  I would also recommend the hot chocolate (butterscotch was amazing).

Cute diner with a fun atmosphere and a great menu.  However, minus one star for insanely slow service.  It took us an hour and forty five minutes just to get pancakes and hot chocolate.

Beware! New ownership, new menu, bad news. The famous brisket sandwich is gone. The new menu is pretty bad. We were a large party and tried numerous items, including four different kinds of wings. I'm sorry to say that none of it was good.

Enjoyable atmosphere and overall just really cool. Decent drink selection and nice presentation. Food was good too! We ordered fried oysters, gyoza, ramen and the unagi bento. My only qualm is the service wasn't great for the price of the meal.

It's not DiGiorno, but it is delivery!

I was at my friends' place and they wanted Thai for dinner, so I got introduced to their Thai delivery of choice - Curry Thai.  They tend to favor the rice plates, which turned out to be the Combination Plates - which, by the way, make no mention of coming with rice on the online menu, but they do!

These Combination Plates at dinner time come with your choice of three sides:

Siam Rolls (2 pieces)
Pork Dumplings (2 pieces)
Veggie Tom Yum Soup
Chicken Coconut Soup

And are priced based on the protein (or veg) of choice:

Chicken, Beef, Pork, Tofu, or Vegetable $10.95
Shrimp, Seafood, Duck, or Crispy Chicken $12.95

I was feeling veggie, so I ordered the #14 Eggplant Basil Combination with Tofu since that's the way the dish was described under the Vegetarian Options side of the menu: Sautéed eggplant with onion, peppers, yellow bean curd, garlic and Thai basil.
