My dish was tasty, the vegetables well cooked - I prefer my Chinese Eggplant on the soft and mushy side - and good amount of it!  The portion - sides and all - would surely have fully satisfied my appetite.  With the addition of homecooked brussels sprouts and mushrooms, however, I had enough Eggplant Basil and rice for a light lunch the next day.

Speaking of which, the Siam Rolls were nice and crisp, and my preferred side of the three I selected.  I'd probably do without the Chicken Coconut Soup next time since it just isn't to my taste, but it was fine for what it was with its tangy flavor, floating sliced mushrooms and tender pieces of chicken.  The Pork Dumplings were okay too, but nothing special.  I'd probably go with a double order of the Siam Rolls and a single order of the Pork Dumplings.

We ordered enough to redeem one of their online coupons for a free appetizer (an order of crab rangoon for a $30 purchase).  I think there were either 5 or 6 of the thin crisp triangles in the take out baggie.  It stood out in its presentation since I'm used to fatter forms of bundling, but it was a tasty version of the Chinese American food experiment.

Speaking of deliver, you have a choice - the restaurant can personally deliver, or you can order through Eat24, or Foodler.  We ordered direct.

(my 800th review - whoa!)

Great location, and reasonably product, but the coffee service sucks at rush hour.  All of the regulars will get their coffee and you will be served last, with a totally lame excuse if you bother to ask why.

I won't go back.
Egg drop soup is scary.  Food is far too oily, and doesn't taste that good.  The dish I had with chicken/peanut/celery was okay, as was broccoli/beef.

I ordered Curry Thai through Eat24, and with every delivery order I entered in my full address including my apartment number. I did not enter in my phone number, this must have automatically been sent with my email address. I waited over the allotted time, and noticed an email from Eat24 saying my food was here and I needed to meet the driver at the door. Though, I never received a buzz at my apartment from the driver. He was too lazy to get out of the car and actually deliver the food. Instead he called me, but at a number I never provided, so I never received the call. I called the restaurant and the woman tried fighting with me about my phone number, even though I never actually entered any phone number into the order screen instead of trying to contact the driver to bring the order back when he has a chance. I'm extremely disappointed, as the delivery man should know what his job entails - actually delivering the food and not just driving it to the curb. With the so so reviews I've read and this experience - I'll never attempt to try this restaurant again. And I recommend others considering this restaurant, to STEER CLEAR!!!!

Please send the staff to Barrista training.  It is clear they do not know how to make a good Latte. Especially when you are paying close to $5 bucks. Also the staff can be quite rude, makes you feel they are doing you a favor when you ask for something that you are paying for!!!! Not good service not good food and certainly not good Lattes

Okay - I have no real dislike for this place, but one complaint that's kept me away. The food is decent, the wait staff is usually friendly, the atmosphere is good, but this I DON'T like: I'm single. I like to eat out in diners and restaurants once in a while, and it never fails that I get treated like a second class citizen, seated near a stinking bathroom, or near a banging door to the kitchen. I didn't have this experience at Brookline Lunch, but I WAS asked to move. I came in, the place wasn't busy, I sat at a booth, placed my order, and before it arrived I was asked by a waitress to move to a small table in the center of the room so that three people could have the booth. Know what? The center of the room is lousy. People constantly walk by you on both sides and there's no sense of personal space at all. I felt like I was dining between two freaking escalators. The impression is, possibly wrong, that this place originally only had booths along the walls, and then crammed small tables and chairs into the center. Nowhere did it say, or was I told when ordering, that booths were reserved for parties of three or more, so it should be first come first serve. I don't care if I AM a party of one, I'm a paying customer. Having to give up a seat after ordering for someone who came in AFTER I did is wrong. The waitress could have pulled two small tables together in the enter of the room (they were empty) and had them sit THERE. I haven't been back in years.

Now THIS is the type of neighborhood place I want around the corner from me!! I never had an issue when the Spirit or Dubliner were there...but the last thing this area needs is another dive pub. McCabe's was the perfect place to take over.

- The atmosphere is very comfortable, not loud, very cool inside with the decor, and unlike every other place in the Boston area...does not have 500 TV's. They have a few, and for big events they have a number of screens that come down from the ceiling...but its not a typical Boston bar with blaring sound and TV's everywhere you turn...its VERY refreshing!!
- The beer list is great and rotates often, the cocktails are creative and always strong - Food is fantastic. Its clear the chef puts in a lot of thought and takes great care in both seasonal offerings and a creative approach to the standards, but the quality is top notch and everything I have had is delicious. A simple burger is always cooked perfectly, and higher end items like the short ribs and scallops are always on point. If you leave without trying the Duck Confit Sliders...you need to have your head checked.
- Also important to mention the staff is fantastic. Really friendly, knowledgeable, and the guest experience is priority #1 for them. I actually saw a patron sitting near disappointed that their favorite dish was no longer on the menu and the server asked the chef to come up with something similar for the guest...well after it came out  the manager, server, and chef all come out to make sure they were happy. I mean...who does that?????

Can't say enough nice things about this place.

If you're not an early riser the line for brunch is a pain, but the food is worth it if you can stomach the wait! 

The first time I came here a few years ago, I had the gingerbread waffles and the strange-sounding Mojito Milkshake (surprisingly yummy!). More recently I had the Ole Miss. It's a big sloppy mess of deliciousness, and the potatoes that come on the side are divine. 

I wish I possessed more willpower to get up early to beat the line, or more patience to wait in it more often.

Just had the sliders and the fried cauliflower.. for $11 bucks.. we have had alot of food trucks here and on average for $11 we got alot of food. The sliders were ok, cauliflower was good but ended up getting another lunch at the mall. 

The portions are way too small for the price

Excellent hot pot. Tonkatsu & miso broth with surf & turf combo + vegetable combo = two people waddling out of the door. Don't forget that you can get half and half for the hot pot broth, and there's no charge for refilling the pot with more broth!

I gotta say, they really live up to their name. Not only did I personally witness them giving someone a compliment but I am sure they can put a smile on your face even if you are having a bad day. The person working at the front was very nice and enthusiastic-- she made you feel more like a friend rather than just a stranger taking your order. Despite the handful of vegetarian options, they will be sure to have my business again!

Something really cool about working in the Kendall area restaurant scene is that everyone kind of gets to know each other, sorta.  My restaurant was pretty close to Bean Towne, and as we didn't serve coffee, we relied on Bean Towne to jolt us for the afternoon rush.  I was usually elected for coffee runs.

At first I felt like a jerk, interrupting the morning rush with a 10 drink order, but the staff always handled it with grace and aplomb.  They were always super nice.  Even when the line was about to hit the door, I never saw them break a sweat.  The music was always good, and the movie trivia questions posted on the board both kept me entertained and added to the sense of Kendall communal goodwill.

The best part?  Pomegranate tea!  How can you go wrong with pomme tea?  You can't.  The laws of quantum physics prevent it.

Bean Towne could easily be a mediocre establishment that happens to serve good coffee and tea.  But it's not.  The staff is personable enough to make any trip to Bean Towne memorable.  Whenever I'm in the area, I make sure to drop by and say hello.

how i love the B-Side...I only started going there about a year ago when my band mates and i were looking for somewhere to grab food late, after practice on a friday night.  and it's nice-- they serve a full, delicious menu until midnight on the weekend.  it's the best when you're staaarving and it's late and you want something yummy and high quality.

anyway, now i'm a big fan.  just went there again last night with my husband and the food was sooo good.  i got the grilled chicken club on fresh ciabatta bread with homemade chips and it rocked.  my husband got the 'pig tostada' and it was really yummy.  in the past i've had the roast chicken too, which was amazing, and the pear arugula salad is tops.

okay, and now a warning-- the smoked gouda skillet.  it is AMAZING.  but-- it is seriously way too much for just 2 people for an appetizer.  when it came to our table last night i was terrified because it was so huge and decadent, yet i wanted to eat the whole thing. it's to die for.

the classic cocktails are awesome, wine selection is okay, lots of beer choices...i've never come here just to hang out and drink, but lots of people do.  seems a little crowded at the bar, with no intuitive place to situate yourself, but hey, lots of bars are like that.  

looses the 5th star for being so damn out of the way...we can never remember how to get there by car...but maybe that's just us!

4.5 stars??? this place has better reviews than rendezvous and bergamot yet it's food couldn't hold the jock strap of those 2 restaurants.. 

really people? really? look. i'm all about living healthy and eating healthy but i'm sick and tired of damn hippie liberals in new england over-rating and over hyping all of this healthy food.

under raving suggestions and reviews i checked out life alive in lowell, the original.

i ordered two dishes and two smoothies.  the price was RIDICULOUS for a smoothie.  

this reminds me of the big fresh in natick.  great idea, healthy decent food at a premium price.  don't get me wrong, it's better than the average restaurant and healthier, but it's not amazing food.

you get the same food by purchasing these ingredients and throwing a sauce of choice over it.  if you can't cook worth your life then yes, this place is half decent.  but if you have ever made your own salad and was able to pour some dressing over it then you won't be impressed.

i don't even want to talk about the smoothies.. we're talking about 6+ dollars after tax for something i make myself every morning in 30 seconds.

look, i'll def eat at life alive when on the run and hungry, it's better (and healthier) than 90% of the choices out there.. but 4-5 star quality food? NO WAY JOSE.  for a meal here you can go to cragies on main and get one of their signature burgers, which blows this stuff out of the water all day everyday.

yelp standards are so low!

update: kristina B is my housemates gf.. i'll have to have a little chat with her about her review!!

I love burrito chains......Boloco, Chipotle...a few other ones that aren't as good...but Qdoba tops them all. I remember many years ago trying my first burrito and Qdoba...the thought of a burrito grossed me out because I really was not in to Mexican food....it. was. amazing. I have been hooked ever since. BIG burritos, great taste...can't beat it.

I like the atmosphere.  2-fer tables go down the middle of the restaurant so you're not forced to sit uncomfortably close to strangers.  Staff is exceptional - super friendly, welcoming, accomodating.  We had a special menu request due to dietary requirements and the chef modified a dish in an ingenious and particularly delicious way.

Having said that, there's just something missing in the food.  It is all excellent ingredients and you can tell they DO care.  But...   we had candied bacon.  Basically just bacon put under the broiler for a minute with brown sugar.  Nothing special.  We also had fried calamari with scallops.  Neither was crispy - seemed undercooked - and the scallops were still cold inside.  The burger was overcooked and served with a mountain of fries - enough to feed 3 people. It should have come with half the fries and some sort of vegetable - whether it be salad or cole slaw or something green. My partner did rave about his smoked duck dish.

And the $12 dessert - strawberry shortcake - was just mediocre.  It desperately needed sauce and whip cream.  Instead it was 2 slices of poundcake and with 2 scoops of too-frozen strawberry ice cream and some slices of fresh strawberries.  To do it right, the VANILLA ice cream needs to be soft so it can moisten the poundcake, and the strawberries need to marinate in sugar for an hour or so to create a syrup that is poured over the cake and ice cream, and then it needs whipped cream so it all melds together into a delicious delicacy. 

There's nothing wrong here.  It's just missing some finesse which you sort of expect at this price point.   It seems like it could be great, and I hope it gets there because it has all the elements to be a place I would visit regularly.

Above all the customer service here is superb. There's nothing better first thing in the morning than being warmly greeted and being served a great product efficiently. Great customer service seems to be a dying art in general but not here. And should anything go awry (which I've rarely seen in many visits) they will make it right with a smile. Lunch menu is good as well.

I probably eat here once a week for lunch. It's a pretty good spot to grab a lite lunch. 

They do salads and wraps. You can create or get one of their custom salads/warps.  

The toppings and selections are very good.  Their buffalo chicken is great. The dressings are also very good. Their Lite Italian is very zesty.  The veggies and toppings are all fresh. 

It'll probably cost you around $10 to create a salad w a roll and drink. The size of the salad is of good portion and often will have some left over and it comes in a to go carton. 

It's one of the more healthier options inside the Cambridge Side Galleria. Better option than Taco Bell and Burger King. 

So if you like to pick and choose your toppings for a salad then this is your spot.

Food is good, however, service can be very very clumsy and slow, and there is this funny confusion about serving take out food people or sit-on people first. They need to learn how to be passionate about their customers and how to serve them.

I don't know. It's not especially easy to get to. Although on a bold night I could walk there from my house. It seems popular. There is definitely an "in" gang who are with the bartenders and doormen and kitchen staff and the rest of the people. I feel a lot cooler when I'm with the kids in the know. Nothing is cheap. Not the beer and not the food. However, it's all delicious. So you pay for what you get. Top shelf fancy and right out of the water taste. And. Free hard boiled eggs. Just march up to the bar and grab it off the saucer. Salt up. Yum.


Also. Definitely not a place I would take kids 1 yes vote.

Bobby is the man. Have known him for years. In fact I met him when the Compliments truck was just a plain white truck waiting to be picked up in California. 

He and his crew have always had a passion for healthy food and they really brought it to their menu. Gluten free, organic, but with a comfort food edge. It has it all. I highly recommend trying everything on the menu. And if you wanna be really fresh to your body, get the grill cheese. It's worth the extra day you have to spend at the gym to work it off :)

The food was excellent. Theirs sliders are very well seasoned and they have gluten free options. The owner is gluten free and loves to talk about it. He is very cool, super friendly and great to talk to. Defintiley check it out. It's delicious and healthy
I marvel at how seemingly healthy food can be so delicious. I would wait in line for a tuna melt. And fresh cut fries? Yup, they have those, too. Just an amazing place to eat.
These guys have the best onion rings I have ever had in my life, seriously.  My only regret is that I didn't order a sandwich, too.

What are you grass-fed hippies smoking?  Just had the sliders here.  $8 for 3 tiny, plain, burgers on hard bread with like 2 strands of shredded cheese melted on them.  The burgers were well done and flavorless.  For $8 people expect a meal.  This was like something I'd whip up when I have no food in the house but some old beef in the freezer and a slice of stale bread.

The staff was nice, but that was honestly the worst meal I have ever purchased.  I had to go out and grab a bowl of soup after to make myself feel like i had an actual lunch.
Very excellent food. 100% authentic, high quality meat, not too greasy, and very friendly staff and owners! We will be coming back soon!

OMG!  When I 1st heard about Life Alive, I couldn't really get what could be so good about vegetables.  I mean, I'm not a vegaphobe, but I don't tend to get super excited by them either.  But then my BF dragged me to Life Alive and I was astounded!  Everything was fresh, crisp, tasty, and perfectly seasoned.  I keep thinking I'll branch out and try something new, but I've gotten stuck in a Goddess, Fool, and Lover rut and it's too tasty in there to climb out!  Also, the Ginger Crush?  YUM!

Part of my initial hesitance came from my dislike of beets.  But Life Alive makes beets not only edible, but tasty too!  And the sauces....  oh where to begin?!  I tried to replicate them at home with minimal success.  Yeah, I guess that's my only complaint - Life Alive, sell your sauces or better yet, give out recipes.  I'd still come back, but I'd be eating healthier at home too.

Come to b-side if you want to hang out in a funky dive that serves gourmet bar food. Walk in to find 1950s diner booths, a sleek rectangular bar, an antique tin ceiling, and eclectic tunes emitting from the sound system. The crowd is typically a good mix of hipsters and yuppies that live in the neighborhood. The bartenders and wait staff are friendly.

A couple of vegetarian options include Arugula & Baby Spinach Salad, Baked Gouda in a Skillet, and Latin 3 Way (homemade tortillas served with avocado puree, spicy roasted tomato relish, and a black bean & goat cheese spread). They have about half a dozen beers on tap, but their cocktail selection is prolific, featuring everything from martinis like the Stardust (light rum, Parfait Amour and lemon juice) to intriguing concoctions such as the Moscow Mule (vodka, lime juice and ginger beer).

A salad at lunch that doesn't suck would be nice. Unfortunately, this is not the place. Maybe I'm just pissed they put chicken on my turkey salad? Either way it would have been a subpar experience. 

Trust me, I don't expect much from a mall food court...but this place sucks.

I used to love Beantowne, but their service have seriously declined since they re-opened. I come here often enough but am close to eliminating them as an option for my lunch. A good 60% of the time, my orders are messed up to a point where the meal isn't enjoyable--they constantly forget to actually toast panini, they forget to add sauce to sandwiches, or forget certain ingredients, like cheese. They're very inattentive when you ask them to substitute an ingredient, etc., which gets very frustrating especially for someone who has allergies to certain foods. The half-sandwich sized are irritatingly inconsistent.

They do get really busy at lunch, so i understand it can get a little hectic, but the frequency with which there are ingredients/toasting/etc missing from the standard items on their menu is difficult for me to justify. 

My colleagues and I are all pretty disappointed with this place. It's one pro is that they're good seating options if you don't want to lug lunch back to the office, but for a long time half the space was always occupied by employees sitting around on laptops doing admin work, etc. while customers couldn't find a place to sit.

Meh indeed.

The drinks are amazing... old-school drink menu- tons of options. Free hard-boiled eggs at the bar. Friendly and nice waiters and bar-staff. Excellent food. Simple, basic, American-style food. Roasted chicken, the salad with candied walnuts, pears and blue cheese could be the only salad I eat for the rest of my life and I'd die happy. It's my favorite, for dinner or drinks- and I wish I could go more often!

This place actually surprised me with how tasty it was.  I wish there was more seating!  I had the cauliflower po-boy and it was delightfully heavy and filling.  Definitely needed a food-coma nap after!

I was in Boston last weekend to see a comedy show and decided to pop in McCabe's after reading the menu on the door.  I hate it when I get sucked into a restaurant based off the menu and then the food is mediocre at best.......fortunately that did not happen this time! The food was even better than expected! Based on the waitresses recommendation we ordered the mushroom flatbread that was jamming with garlic (which I LOVED....the people sitting near me at the comedy show, not as much!) and split the roasted vegetable sandwich. The sandwich was done well with great bread, perfectly roasted veggies and avocado.........but where this shined was with the French fries! Fries in my mind can make or break a meal and these were a highlight!

I should just let you know that my friend found a long hair in her food (this crap happens people -- not a big deal!) -- the waitress immediately apologized and offered to get another sandwich but my friend declined (we were getting full!). We told the waitress not to worry about it and not to take it off the check (we had eaten more than half of the sandwich and mmmm those fries!) but she still took it off which I think was unnecessary but a great business move.

The restaurant was clean, cozy, well decorated and had a fab selection of beers on tap. If I'm in the area again, I'll totally go! ohhhh... why not 5 stars.... we didn't get dessert. I can't do 5 stars without trying the sweets!

I've worked right next to Shabu & Mein for years, but just now tried it. I came for lunch, which was pretty busy, but the waiter made sure to let us know about how long we'd have to wait for our food, which was nice. I ordered the regular pork ramen, which was good but not as good as other ramen I've had in Boston. The thin noodles were a bit of a let down and I felt that there wasn't as much pork as I would have wanted. The prices are okay (not super expensive, but not cheap either), so I'd probably come back if I were craving ramen and didn't want to go out of my way

The friendly toast has a cool atmosphere and a lot of great options to accommodate people that are gluten free, vegan, and vegetarian. The one complaint I have is the service. Every time I go the service is extremely slow and the employees can be forgetful. Today I didn't get my side until my check came and that was because I had to remind my server to get it for me.
Atmosphere was great ! Food amazing and bartender was very cool . He took the time to give us recommendation about the menu and beers . You will never go wrong with specials

This a cute little family run Thai place in East Cambridge off of Cambridge Street.  

This is my favorite inexpensive Thai place in Boston.  The pad kea mow (drunken noodles) and the red and yellow curries are all fantastic dishes.  The only dishes that I would avoid are the pad Thai and yum pla muk salad.

I would highly recommend trying this place out!  It offers great value!

Came here on a Saturday afternoon for brunch. Duck confit hash = $$$$$$. Not literally (I think it was $14 literally) but figuratively. So good. Totally worth.

Only docked a star because I got the yogurt and fruit bowl and didn't actually get to eat the duck confit hash myself (I just watched my brunch buddy foodgasm the whole time while he was eating it), so I'm a bit rueful. But that's my own fault, not McCabe's.

Don't be fooled. You may think "I love bacon and burgers, I would NEVER eat at a vegetarian restaurant!" But you would be wrong.

Life Alive is like the best guilty pleasure that isn't actually guilty. Loaded with veggies, brown rice, quinoa, and other deliciousness, you will probably be surprised that the food is vegetarian if you're a former meat and potatoes girl like me. 

Be prepared to wait forever in line and order things with ridiculous names. It's also a little pricey, but its all fresh so no complaints! 

I can recommend the Adventurer and the Lover. And the Rebel. 

... ok, really everything. Be sure to add additional goodness. Avocado? Egg? Cashews? Sundried tomatoes? ADD IT ALL.

Ordered off the Yelp app for delivery to my hotel. Easy and fast! The delivery itself was quick and they included all utensils and napkins! The food was very fresh and hot! I had the Siam Twins and crab wontons, both were fantastic! Will eat from here again!
If you offered me the choice of some chinese food from Pu Pu Hot Pot OR a steaming hot bowl of wet cat food garnished with spider legs, I'd have to think about it for a little while.

I was looking for some brunch today, and decided to head to Zoes. I walk in, and theres a few couples in front of me waiting for tables, not a big deal. What was kind of annoying is that a four-top walked in about 7 minutes after I did. And they got shown a seat RIGHT AWAY. Even more annoying? A three top came in, and got seated RIGHT AWAY AGAIN. Not cool. 

Once I sat, I ordered the Athenian Omlet and a milkshake. Milkshake wasn't good and the server only gave me a straw with it and ta-da, it was too thick for the straw. The omlet was good. The hashbrowns were bland. 

I'll head to East Coast for brunch next time.

I've been here for dinner and brunch and both were amazing! The Margherita and Clam and Bacon pizza are awesome. As well as the biscuits and gravy and the Hot Mess. Service was great as well. No complaints...only praises

Here we go...

Plus:
+can sub tofu into all of the omelets=VEGAN breakfast options--woohoo! breakfast is probably the hardest meal to eat out as a vegan, so thank you!
+tea selection is fabulous, quality is amazing
+Soymilk available for all the coffee/tea/chocolate drinks
+fun decor
+obscenely large portions (or maybe this is a minus?)
+awesome home fries

Minus:
-in my dream world, there would be vegan pancakes
-wait on weekends is RIDICULOUS, but:
-no reservations allowed!
-restrooms are all the way downstairs... why?
-gets really LOUD
-variable service

It's a takeout place in a college town.  You make the call about expectations.  I would say that it's not an atrocity but it's not chinatown quality food.  The food is inexpensive esp. the lunch specials, but somewhat lacking in flavor.  The scallion pancakes taste like pieces of fried dough with hardly any scallions.  The last time I came here my friend ordered some steamed vegetable buns for takeout in Chinese while I spoke to the waiter in English.  He looked quite perplexed but was amiable.  Service here is decent, as far as takeout places go.    The vegetable buns were okay, but miles away from the ones you would get in Shanghai oh for about less than a quarter in USD.

I'm a Mexican food snob, I admit.  I'm Mexican what can you expect?

That said, I have a particular bias against these so called "fresh-mex", SoCal, yuppie takes on "Mexican"-- burritos aren't Mexican food,  people.  They are an American concoction, a yummy one, but stil a thing of its own, and as such must be reviewed accordingly.

Within the microcosm of Burrito Places, Qdoba is mediocre at best.  The food's okay, but it's certainly no Anna's or even Felipe's.  In a pinch I guess it would do.  There are only two reasons I go to this particular location frequently:

1. I dig the chips (crispy, warm, with a hint of lime) and guacamole (decent.)

2. They take crimson cash (for us Harvard folk.)

Wow. I am currently eating the brussels sprouts from Mainely Burgers because I wanted something healthy to go with my chocolate Frappe ;-) and Wow. They are amazing. Obviously, if you don't like brussels sprouts in the first place, you might not enjoy them- but if you just kind of like brussels sprouts, you need to absolutely try these. I will be ordering these again. and then again. They give you so many too! Go Mainley cooks! The frappe is always amazing.

The mac and cheese is outta this world, along with the chicken BLT. I've only been here a few times, but it's always packed and the food and drinks have always been great. I enjoy the laid back/old school vibe. The waitstaff was pleasant, helpful and fun on a weekend night, amidst a lot of tables to wait on!

Zoe is easy to miss because it is tucked away below street level. I had to drive by a couple of times to find it. I swear there must have been an early law mandating that streets cannot be perpendicular and that there must be more than four streets meeting at an intersection. :) Fortunately I eventually found it because my omelet was excellent - American cheese, spinach, asparagus, mushroom, and sausage was brimming, fresh and very tasty. Service was also prompt and friendly. I would definitely come back if I am looking for a hearty breakfast.

Really fresh and nourishing. I've had the goddess bowl for lunch and the green goddess with eggs and avocado for breakfast and the former has shockingly good flavor. Add ginger to every smoothie and you'll be happy!  I will note that the line is almost always ridiculous, they leave the door open as a form of climate control, and there are definite moments I wish they had baked goods.

So glad it finally reopened! Can't speak to the lunch menus, but the breakfast offerings are a solid 3.5-4 stars. Breakfast sandwiches with meat for under $5, breakfast burritos and bowls for around $7, and solid coffee options all under $5. I got a BEC on wheat bread, which really hit the spot. They also served it up super quickly.

It's nothing fancy (unless you look up at the lights!), but as someone who grew up being able to grab a BEC at any gas station (yes, I'm southern), I've never understood how Boston/Cambridge has so few coffee spots slinging traditional breakfast sandwiches. 

Definitely a neighborhood staple - looking forward to becoming a regular here! My only suggestion would be to mix some local goods in with the potato chips/snacks/drinks, etc.

okay. let's just leave all the "crap jokes" aside and get down to "business." ha. that was lame.

This place is hit or miss. You have to know what to order, but the only way to know what to order is to try different stuff...I guess a double edged sword here is their extensive menu. There's always something you haven't had and whether or not you want to try it...you're never too sure about that either.

I went to Boston College so the epitome of great cheap Chinese takeout is New Hong Kong, and I have to say that NHK is leaps and bounds above pupu in every way possible. Except the fortune cookies. They taste just about the same and their "fortunes" are just as lame.

The only thing I've gotten here which has satisfied me taste-wise is the cold noodle. everything else, although it's a good price and HUGE portions, hasn't exactly been a great experience. You get what you pay for!

I just moved to Porter Square and was trying the variety of cuisines on  Mass Ave when I came across Darul Kabab. It is a Pakistani/ Bangladeshi cuisine and you must try the moghlai parathas and the malai kabab.. it will melt in your mouth!!
Also the waitress was very kind and gave us complimentary jalebis. 
The only problem is there are 5 screens of TV and each keep showing some Pakistani/Indian TV serial, which is annoying.
I hope they change those and just play a soft instrumental music instead.
But surely a must try for Indian/Pakistani food lovers :)

This place is great. The Mainah was delish, almost unexpectedly so! Good sized burger, super juicy and well executed. The fries were big enough that my bf and I should have split one order! The truffle fries are pretty subtle on the truffle (which I really liked, bf not so much). I'd definitely come back here!

Great food! Healthy and lo cal. Service was slow though. A little disorganized and I didn't understand why they were being slow. I wasn't in a rush so it was ok, but service could be streamlined. Got the mandarin chicken salad with fried wonton chips, cranberries, mandarin oranges with sesame dressing. 290 calls without the dressing.

I last came here on a bitterly cold day in December. I came in the door and was immediately proud of myself for choosing this restaurant because it was warm and a bit humid inside, like a greenhouse. I had the Sufi Poet, which has cranberries, hummus, apples, and cashews, among other things. It was fantastic, and I felt twice as healthy when I left as I did when I arrived. I can't wait to go back and try something new, as I'm sure it will be just as good. The servers are helpful and friendly, and it looks nice on the inside, with colorful trippy paintings on the walls and books to read, if you'd like. It also has free wifi for an hour or two.

Came here to try their pizza, and it was delicious! We ordered a mushroom + fontina pizza and added chicken, and it did not disappoint. Very flavorful, and also quite rich so you don't need many slices even though it's thin-crust. And the crust is great! Not the bland, flavorless crust a lot of pizza places have, but rather an airy crust with just a slight char. Mmmmm. 

We also ordered the garlic knots, and while they were tasty, the portion was HUGE. (They were more like garlic rolls) It could easily feed a group of six, and was definitely too much for just 3 ppl. I wish they had a smaller portion available...

The hostess and waitress were also super friendly, and very attentive!

Good design, nice cocktails, good coffee, and baked goods that are tempting but not overly so. They have strong wifi and a lot of indoor seating. 

Outdoor cafe seating is unshaded and gets rather hot.

Overall, a good place to work and have a cup of coffee with convenient location in Kendall Square.

I came here for dinner with a bunch of friends on Saturday night. We were seated without much of a wait which was nice. In terms of food, we ordered garlic knots and a bunch of pizzas to share. The garlic knots were decent and taste exactly as you would expect. For the pizza, I mostly ate the mushroom pizza. Overall, it was okay, though for some of the slices the bottom was burnt quite a bit. Overall, everything was pretty alright though not much more than that hence the middling rating.

B-Side is a neighborhood landmark bar.  From Beatles to punk...the music is as meticulously selected as the classic drink menu.  The bartenders are elite and sharp-witted.

Go there to be social, or just to catch a game on TV.  I offten settle on an IPA to compliment the Pizza of the Day.  (Always a good choice)

The menu boasts  a wide variety...all well-prepared; ask for the specials.  The cook is fantastic.

On weekends, this place is bumpin' (litterally).

This place is huge and the staff is super friendly. It was empty when we went in so they were extra attentive. We were given noodles for free, just cause, and even were discounted $10 after my gym membership discount. The food is really good and not too expensive either. I'll be going back frequently.

The Wednesdays "all you can eat" deal is spectacular. The shabu (hot pot) broth was especially flavorful. I recommend the herbal chicken broth.

All the meats for the hot pot were amazingly tender and tasty. The lamb, beef, and shrimp were far from dry or tough; they were 5-star quality minimum. The waiters were always attentive and helpful, bringing over extra water for the broth and adjusting the heat for the hot pot. 

Lastly, I recommend the tuna sashimi, which was moist and fresh.

So torn on this review. The food is good but the delivery is EXCEPTIONALLY slow. 

2 hours slow. Twice the amount of time they tell you it will be there slow. Had my clothes been in style to begin with (they're not) they wouldn't be by the time dinner arrived slow. Slow. 

The food IS pretty good... but with dozens of similar options in the area we are going to consider switching our go to...

I always get the drunken noodle, the wife goes for the yellow curry. Can't really go wrong with either.
B-side is awesome. I heard that it below to the same group who owns Sonsei, Paradise and Avalon. I like the booth and food is good. Dim light but good food and drink.

In this bizarro city where good brunch places are about as hard to find as a bar open past midnight, nice people, and a warm day, Brookline Lunch was definitely a breath of fresh air.  Delicious BRUNCH food, good service, and cheap.  The only reason I didn't give it a 5 was that I don't think they serve bloody marys, which are essential for the perfect brunch. :)
Wish I could give 5 and a half stars! This place is the greatest little dive ever. They use tons of fresh veggies, and the staff is very sweet! Try the homemade blueberry jam or the Turkish coffee.

Yep, still solid when I went there again for another friend's (surprise) birthday celebration.

Though we went for some of the favorites from last time (Such as the Agedashi Tofu and Fruit Smoothies), the new sampling we had this time was also quite nice.  The Salmon essence that others have mentioned is *very* nice, and beautiful too to boot, so I'd definitely recommend getting it.  Also we got the Tom Yum, Kimchee Tomato, and Szechuan soup bases, and they're all very flavorful .  The Kimchee tomato was very hearty, and the szechuan extra spicy had a really good kick to it.

A nice plus for the combo platters was the addition of some mushrooms and some more greens to the platter from what I last remembered.  The lamb was same good ole' deliciousness (and the chicken was great too!).  All beautifully sliced and presented.  By the end of the meal, we totally made converts out of the three Shabu-newbies at the table.

Service was fantastic as usual.  Everyone was very attentive from start to finish and made sure stuff came out in reasonable time.  I feel bad for staying til closing time (since we came in a bit past 8:30pm) but they were very nice about it throughout.  Sonya and Patrick: you guys were amazing.  :)

**Also awesome news: they had their liquor license approved!  End of the month before they get it, but (hard) drinks + Shabu will soon be upon us!
Expensive.  Ramen (original tonkotsu) was good but not up to the price.  From what I've had in other places, I'd say it should be around $8 / bowl to make it a decent value.

I don't like this place. It only skirts getting one star because its cheap and in a good location. 

Entrees are greasy, the fried rice is dry, the scallion pancakes were overcooked. Its a small place so the service is okay and you get what you need. Went twice and had no desire to go back, although I lived down the street.

I will say this place is not for everyone, but if you're into healthy living, healthy tastes, and just health in general then you definitely will love this place!  The decor is very hippy-like, but cute, and this place can super, super busy during normal eating hours... it's even busy during off-hours.  So be prepared to wait a little for your food/to order.

I've tried a couple of their smoothies, and they're all pretty good, but my fav was the Spring Alive.  For their food, I don't particularly have one to recommend, but same with the smoothie, they're all pretty good.  I do need to iterate though, you will NOT like this place is you're not into natural, and healthy flavors, so don't bother if that's not your thing.

To borrow an analogy from Batman, Shabu & Mein is like Two-Face. 

On one hand, Shabu & Mein's ramen menu provides the diner with a great set of options. It's probably not the best ever, but the ramen is not the worst either.

The noodle itself has a healthy snap and the broth has a balanced flavor profile - not too salty but not muted either.  As echoed by other reviewers, go for the thicker noodle.

The appetizers need a little work (we got the sauteed beef tongue and it was very salty; the spring rolls are a disaster more suited for PF Changs). The bar is well-stocked.

The setting is contemporary and comfortable. Very well laid out.

Now to the bad... In the case of Shabu & Mein, the bad vastly outweighs the good.

Shabu & Mein gets a major thumbs down for it's uneven service. The service was top-notch on my first visit. The waiter was quick to offer suggestions and handled our party of 12 like a champ.

The service was abysmal on my second visit. The hostess turned us away at the door without an offer to place our names on a waiting list or an estimate on when a table would be available. 
This experience was unnecessary - we would have gladly waited for a table.

At its heart, Shabu & Mein is a casual dining experience; not the VIP room at the 40/40 Club.

All I can say is, buyer beware. There are plenty of other places to go that don't treat its customers like a nuisance.

Mixed review. Very friendly service. The place is close to sparkling clean. Big windows. But I have only done take out as it is near the M2-Harvard Shuttle stop and I have picked things up on the way home. 

The salsas are made fresh daily and they come in a variety of heat strengths-I prefer things on the hot side. 

The first meal I had was a taco salad with shredded beef-very good indeed. Next time, soft tacos with chicken-good. The Mexican soup-pretty good, but over-priced because the small take-out container was only filled just over half way. Last stop, nachos with pork. The chips did not make it home without becoming limp (and I don't live that far away) and while pretty tasty, they are served with soft ingredients to boot and lack the bite of individual peppers, onions, or tomatoes. So it was all rather gooey. 

From looking around the tables, burritos are clearly popular, but I don't care for burritos in general. The steam heat turns the tortilla into a floury sticky thing. (as opposed to wraps which are uncooked flour things)

I will go back for the taco salad though.

I had a free day in Cambridge and came in for brunch (10am?) and there was no line, so I was able to order immediately. THe menu was a bit overwhelming but seemed reasonably priced. I had the adventurer "demi" plate, which was delicious. The almonds add a nice crunch. Also, the food came out very promptly. I didn't get a drink because there wasn't a clear drink menu (aside from the smoothies/juices) and I had a feeling they might be overpriced.. I think a hot tea was 2+

I was surprised that the woman who was in the middle of taking my order picked up the phone while I was right there which i found a little rude, though she put the person on the other line on hold once she answered to finish the order. still, I would think another co-worker might help out. 

THe decor and vibe is very inviting and warm, I enjoyed the assortment of books they had out on the counter. didn't get to check out the downstairs area. I appreciate the goal of the restaurant but found the organic mission statements everywhere to be a little overwhelming/in your face.

I have visited this food truck multiple times and look forward to it every week!

The fried cauliflower is incredible and the spicy sauce that accompanies it is equally delicious. The tuna melt is also great and the portions are large. Service is excellent as well!
Awesome vibe and great food with local ingredients. Best burger in the Cambridge area!!!

I agree that this place is cool as is the decor, but the service is eh not so great, a little slow, people are definitely not in a rush. I had a sandwhich not bad, not the greatest. I would think that if you are in the mood for brunch or hungover this would be the place to hit. I personally would go back again to try the breakfast and that killer french toast. Also, I would say this is an attraction to the rather mundane Kendall area especially for the locals who actually live there.

Area Four is my second favorite place to get pizza in Cambridge. (Top honors go to Otto in Harvard Square, but they have no seating.)

It's all about the crust. Crispy, but not too crispy--it's got to have just the right amount of chew. I'm not that in love with the Margherita here; it was a little drippy from either the tomato sauce or the cheese, or a combination of the two. But I fancy the mushroom and fontina pie after our first rendezvous together, and I usually prefer red sauce over white on my pies. The mushroom sauce and the gremolata make the pizza pop, especially the freshness of the lemon and parsley.

Make sure to save room for dessert, and order the soft-serve sundae of the day. While the chocolate and vanilla twist I had seemed simple enough, it was delicious, with nice, subtle touches, like cinnamon in the chocolate ice cream. 

I haven't tried the brunch items here, but the sticky bun/cinnamon roll special I've spied is as big as a small cake. Tempting, but I think I'll always save room for the sundae.

Not sure why this was my first visit having grown up in nearby Brighton.  The sizzler lived up to its reputation and getting out of a steak place less than a hundred bucks with the wife ( drinks and tip included) is unheard of . 
Staff was friendly and very accommodating.  Even though the place was hopping, no delay in food coming out of their kitchen. 
Can't wait for the next visit. 

Frank & Diane
Billerica, MA

Who knew chicken sausage was a good idea? And the Crispy French Toast was fabu! 

great place to kill a hangover or bring your mom when she visits from out of town.

We were drawn to this establishment from the Phantom Gourmet write-up on the beef brisket sandwich and were sorely disappointed. The meat was super fatty, the bread didn't hold up and in general it was lacking in structure. The wings were lackluster--too fatty, the skin wasn't crispy at all, although the sriracha sauce had a good spicy flavor. We ate 3/10 wings and were done with them. The bacon bliss starter was so-so, not as candied as I expected, not a thick cut of meat (hard to compete with the bacon at Rosebud!). 

Cool bar, nice ambiance, super friendly servers. A place I'd go for drinks, but wouldn't recommend the food.

I stop in here a couple times a month on my way home from work. The food is phenomenal--so fresh, healthy, and nourishing. The Rebel is my favorite--I love the quinoa as a base, and the dark greens, broccoli, and tofu make it such a filling and nutritious meal. The lentil soup is one of my favorite soups in the entire world. I don't know what makes it so exceptional, but I have had a LOT of lentil soups and have never had one as satisfying and delicious as Life Alive's.

This new place may just displace Bluefin as my new go-to casual weeknight sushi.

The Good:
* Unagi is crispy and firm, doesn't seem to have been thawed from a pre-sauced package
* Spicy rolls use whole cuts of fish, not a mushy mayo salad
* Green tea served in the warm, nutty genmaicha style
* Rice has just the right acidic bite
* Nods to authenticity with appetizers called "izakaya specialties"
* Sleek, modern interior decoration with all non-bar seating out of sight of TVs
* Website has good deals - check it out for $10 off

The Disappointing:
* So happy to see conveyor-belt sushi, but bummed that it's all veggie-only.  More the Cambridge Department of Health's fault than the restaurant's though
* The chef either forgot or delayed part of our order, causing a long wait for the final two pieces
* Place seems to lack focus with the combination sushi and shabu concept.  Commit!
* Specialty rolls almost all have avocado
* No liquor license, no sake.  Who wants Kirin "0% beertaste beverage"?
* Ikura didn't have that pop-in-your-mouth texture I love so much

The Intriguing:
* There is Okonomiyaki on the menu, my favorite Japanese comfort food.  Will have to go back and investigate.

This review is for customer service. I have ordered here through GrubHub some times and the food was good. However, the estimated time GrubHub gives us is not accurate, at least for this restaurant. My order arrived 15-20 minutes earlier and I couldn't answer the phone when the delivery guy arrived. I called the restaurant and the owner was extremely rude, saying it would take another hour to deliver or he could cancel it. I was trying to explain what happened and he kept saying "I will cancel, I will cancel". From the other reviews here, it seems I was not the only one threatened with the cancelation of the delivery. Well, there are plenty of good Thai restaurants and if he doesn't need customers, good for him. Will never order in this place again.

Grabbed the "work well together" sirloin sliders and the "soo sweet" sweet potato fries, and man was everything good. Some of the best burgers I've had, let alone from a truck. When I checked out the fries, I was thinking, man, they totally burnt them.... not even close. These were the BEST sweet potato fries I've had. I will certainly be back.
Best Indian food I've had in Cambridge. The weekend buffet is amazing, lots of options for vegetarians. I've also ordered take-out from here and it arrived quickly. Can't wait to go back!

Reliable, but nothing to write home about. It's just standard mediocre diner food.

Not open late at all. I think they close around 10. Diners should be open 24/7 and if they aren't offering the convenience of 24 hour service, they should be super-duper delicious.

Major disappointment. These salads feature old ingredients with gloppy dressing that literally tastes vaguely of vomit and a staff that looks at your food disgusted as they mix it.

Go to a real restaurant, order a salad that was made this month.

I wasn't impressed with the quality of food here. This was a very standard Chinese restaurant with all your normal meals. I was sad to see my moo shu was pretty much burnt cabbage with some chicken. Like other people have been saying if you are looking for cheap Chinese this will be your place, but don't expect much.

A favorite in Kendall for yummy lunches, great cookies, (no coffee - I get that free in the office), and an awesome, friendly staff. I have to agree with some of the other reviewers on the sandwich bread - otherwise I would give 5 stars. Fresh bread rocks!

Wifi is not "official" but always works. wink wink.

Standard diner fare for brunch, but I keep coming back because it's the most efficient option in Harvard Square. 

The place gets packed on the weekends, but the staff manages to do a relatively good job of taking care of clients. Prices are reasonable, and much better than most other brunch options in the area.

Food here is unabashedly greasy, and ranges from mediocre to occasionally quite good - the wild mushroom omelette was *meh* at best, but  the CA Benedict (with avocado) and it was delicious, and their sweet potatoes fries are pure yummy goodness. 

The downside is that I always walk away feeling bloated and ashamed of my choices. Zoe is that ex who is impossible to leave for good because she's convenient and already knows just what you want, even if it makes you hate yourself in the morning.

Wow.  I went here on a whim to celebrate a pair of friend's birthdays, since it recently opened.  Plus, hotpot with a group is always good fun.  Needless to say I was quite impressed (enough to get me to write my first review in a long while!).

We started with their fruit smoothies, since they haven't gotten their liquor license yet.  I do not regret it one bit; it's been a while since I had a really good lychee smoothie!  For appetizers we got some good ole' edamame, agedashi tofu, and okonomiyaki, and all of which I was very fond of (as were all in our group).  I ordered a Black Pearl Maki that I absolutely loved and was well received by everyone.  The broths they had were very nice (I sampled the mushroom, szechuan, and chicken herbal).  Shabu ingredient quality was quite good, though the portion of the vegetable plate is somewhat small given the price.  The protein part was good though (lamb and sirloin deliciousness).  In general, the food presentation was great.  

However the wait-staff deserves a special mention.  They were definitely very attentive throughout our entire evening and welcoming throughout.  Our evening was definitely made that much brighter with their presence, particularly Sonia.  Birthday cakes went through without a hitch and they were very accommodating.  I was also quite happy with the good reception/treatment of my blind friend's guide-dog (we've had issues with other establishments in the past).

The place is very spacious and I'm quite fond of the decor (I can see why some may consider it a bit "dark" but I quite like it).  It's a very clean and sleek appearance, with quite the nice view of Porter Square (it's not much of a skyline but it's still nice).  The use of induction cookers for their Shabu is a nice plus.

Price-wise, it's definitely not "cheap" Shabu compared to alternatives, but this *is* Porter Square, and the location is a big plus for me.  It's still within a reasonable range too.  We splurged a tad since it was a birthday celebration but it was money well spent.

A very solid addition to the Porter Square scene.  I'm definitely coming back for more.

Mmmm...giant burritos.  I'm a newbie to to Qdoba, but they have more or less copied the Chipotle model, which is quite good in my personal opinion.  No complaints on the food, it is what it is.

Regarding this specific location, my compliment is that the restaurant is always clean.  However, the service is typically slow.  The lines are more a function of this being a high traffic area rather than any fault of the employees.  Avoid the noon hour and you'll be fine.
Perfect place for weekend brunch!! Every time I have a friend in town I take them to the friendly toast it is worth the wait!! Service and food are fantastic!!
The staff alone is worth going and now that Nick has returned...an all star staff that takes amazing care to deliver everything to your liking.  Great food!

So glad I finally  got my husband to want to order Thai food . . . we ordered some stuff for  take-out last night and it was great!
I recommend the Chicken Satay and the Tom Yum soup with shrimp. Delicous! My husband had some kind of spicy beef dish that he loved too. We will definitely become regulars here. Thank you, Yelp people for steering me here!

This is probably my favorite pizza  spot in the Boston area right now. You will not be disappointed as the toppings are plentiful and staff are very friendly. 

Looking for take out? Give them a 15-30 min heads up and your pie will be waiting for you hot & fresh right out of the oven!

I've been to both locations so far...I would recommend you try both of them!

We were meeting friends here for dinner and aside from the fact my phone kept trying to take us to Area Four the section of Cambridge rather than Area Four the restaurant, it was excellent.  The food really was fantastic.  The atmosphere was nice.  Would definitely go back for more.

Great atmosphere, great food and wonderful staff. I had the margarita with caramelized onions and it was cooked to perfection in their wood fired oven, light fluffy dough with a good crunch on the outside of the crust. Sat at the bar, the bartenders were very attentive and all around good at their craft. Tried a drink with mezcal,tequila,habanero and passion fruit and it did not disappoint. A very welcome surprise was the fact that they use Barrington rosters coffee and their baristas know how to pull a shot, needless to say I was pumped after my first sip. I was in town for my sisters graduation so thank you for making our family brunch that much better! I definite recommend this place

Nice location and decor. Family run is good. Vegetable Hakka Noodles is very good.

Lamb Dosa - good but Lamb was overcooked. Sambar contains too much water, too much tamarind and less dal. Coconut chutney did not come out well - you may want to soak coconut pulp in hot water for 5 minutes prior to making chutney

Ordered Tandoori Mixed Grill and Full Tandoori Chicken. They were over cooked and over colored and at room temperature. Should watch the grill to retain moisture and juices

Will try again in the next 4-5 months

Best oven pizza in Boston/cambridge. The place is cozy and the staff super friendly. It has a small bar in the waiting area. We absolutely loved the sausage and banana peppers. Also, the mushroom and fontina was fantastic. I'm a pizza lover so I'm very happy I found this place.

This is a hidden gem.  I've been looking for a great, value-Thai restaurant in Cambridge, and this is it.  

Excellent food -- I've not yet had anything that was mediocre.   Good prices.  The restaurant is small, so I like to come slightly later for dinner.  Great service.  

This is going to be one of my go-to restaurants for a value dinner.

We went on a Saturday night.  They sat us at a dirty table, and instantly removed the dirty dishes.  No one came back to wipe the table down or provide utensils, glasses, etc.  We waited 20 minutes and no waiter showed up either.   

Eventually, some angry asian woman came over, dropped a bowl of edamame on our table, said "on the house.  for waiting." And left.  As she walked away, I heard some staff member yell at her and she shouted back "Free because I'm nice!"

We waited another 10 minutes, still no waiter, nothing.  So we left.

The menu itself left a lot to be desired.  It says Japanese Sushi, Grill and Shabu.  No "Grill" items on the menu.  And the sushi had no standard items like a 'Sashimi Dinner" which you see everywhere for like $25 for chef's choice.  It makes ordering much easier.   Indeed, the few options available on the menu were all $1 - $3 more than any other Japanese restaurant I've been to, and all have been better than this place.

The Shabu menu was also $3 - $5 more per item than all the other great shabu restaurants around town.

The place was completely disorganized, staff members yelling at each other, tripping over each other, etc.  My guess is they are severely understaffed for the weekends.  

Doesn't matter though.  30 minutes waiting at a dirty table for a waiter to show up is more than fair before giving up on the place.

Went to this restaurant for the first time, actually i was there for the next door restaurant THIRD EYE but they closed, so i thought lets try this restaurant. They was serving buffet, i kinda liked it. I personally manage indian restaurant and been to so many restaurant. They dont have oily food, which is healthy. Mushroom curry, dal makhani and chicken curry with the bone was awesome i felt like home made. loved it.

Been there two times, hopefully never again. This is a bar that thinks it's  on Newbury Street and tries to act the part. First time I've ever gone to a bar with a group where people returned their drinks they were so badly made.  Second time I went was around 8pm and the bar was full, there was half a dozen (at a minimum) empty tables yet they wouldn't let us sit there because we weren't planning on buying dinner. Ridiculous mindset.

Whoever thought of this place is a genius. I love salads! The variation here is amazing! Oh, and the wheat bread is MOUTHWATERINGLY DELICIOUS. I really wish one would open up in Allston, so that I could have a healthy choice to go to. I ordered the Bently (eggs, tomatoes, cheese, chicken) and added tuna to my dish with light Caesar dressing. The serving was SO BIG, and I couldn't finish it, but it was good while it lasted.

Ate a lot of food. Had chicken yakitori, takoyaki, salmon essence, shabu with prime rib and pork, and caterpillar maki. A lot of food for two girls but worth it. Super fast service from Thuy the server. If you come in ask for Thuy, she's super friendly and fast server. Their shabu meat is of really high quality, you can tell the difference from the first bite. We also got mango smoothies and it's not often you get mango smoothies made of out real mangos. It was delicious.  Definitely coming back!

HOLY CRAP.

I have been wanting to come here for 13 years--when I lived up the street from Frank's. It took me until last week to get there and stupid me for keeping myself from Frank's all this time.

1. Steak. Lots of it. With sides that should come with steak. Like pilaf. Garlic bread. Vegetable medley.

2. Harvey Wallbanger. Never had this cocktail before, but it seemed to be right in line with what Frank's is all about. Keeping things exactly the way they were 50 years ago--simple and good.

3. Mushroom gravy.

4. Me: What's better, the mashed or the loaded baked potato? I feel like I should always take advantage of a loaded baked potato when I have the chance.
Server: That's a tough one. The mashed is AMAZING. But the loaded is, well, it's a loaded baked potato.
Me: [Long confused pause of indecision.]
Server: We can do loaded mashed, you know.
Me: HOLY CRAP. Done.

5. One pound of steak, rare as rare should be. Perfection.

6. Mushrooms IN the mushroom gravy.

7. Oreo ice cream pie.

8. Another Harvey Wallbanger.

This is not the place to go for itsy bites of exotic flavor explosions. For things that are chopped into little pieces and decoratively served in an Asian soup spoon. This is where you go when you want to sit at a booth with steak the size of your head and wish that you had a fedora and a secret knock.

There is fried chicken. And for the same reason that loaded mashed and steak being steak is king, I will be returning for that fried chicken very soon in hopes of making up for all this silly lost time.

I love you Frank's.

Love the B-Side.

Asked the bartender one time, "What do you recommend for bourbon?"

"What do you like?"

"Well, Maker's is fine, I prefer Knob Creek.  I like Blanton's a lot, Bulleit is good..."

"Do you want to have sex tonight?" he asked.

"Wha?"

"Were you planning on having sex tonight?"

"Not tonight, but maybe tomorrow...?"

"Okay, try this."  He poured me a taste of George T. Stagg, which was fantastic.  Very strong stuff, but tasty as hell.

"I'll take one of those!"  And now it's the bourbon I judge all bars by.

So yeah, the bartenders know their stuff.  But the food's very good as well.  The baked gouda appetizer?  Awesome.  The roast beef sandwich is great, the mussels are to die for, and everything I've had as an entree has been very satisfying.  

I love their brunch (follow it with the pecan pie FTW!), and though the service is slow sometimes, the waitress we always seem to get is cute as hell and just as sweet.

This review will be short and sweet, as I am crafting on my iPhone, which doesn't lend itself to poetic waxing (or even decent spelling). 

I came to Kendall on a recon mission to find the newly opened Friendly Toast. That's when I saw this place, right next door. As I'm hitting up the Toast with friends tomorrow, I decided to give the Bean a whirl. 

Wow. I tried the Provence, which was their daily sandwich special. Roast Turkey, roasted red peppers, and goat cheese on multi-grain. Simple, and simply amazing. The service was great, the other patrons were discussing their favorite John Updike novels, and the in house tunes were surprisingly good. 

In closing, I will be back, and I will bring others. Qwerty.

TERRIBLE. I ordered some delivery and tossed most of it. Foodler led me to believe that I could find some marginal/garden variety amer-chinese food here; instead I opened up a grease soaked bag with mystery meats and utter disappointment.

There's a tear in my beer (well, my cocktail) 'cause I'm cryin' for you dear...

Oh B, how I will miss you. Good times and bad, you were always there for me with a tasty cocktail and some delicious food. I will miss your blue cheese fries, gouda, and brussel sprouts most of all. Your periodista, lucha libre, caipirhinia, and mint julep will always hold a special place in my heart. You were the first place where I learned that the grasshopper is a perfect nightcap, and I love that your expert bar staff were always willing to make a creative cocktail for me. Know that you will never be truly replaced, but I will continuously search for a place that can rival your greatness.

Now, I need someone to show me the way to the next whiskey bar...

I liked the food, so the one star is for the deception.  I checked over their menu online.
According to their website, my order should have come to $22.  When I placed the order, I was told by the waiter at the bar I should pay when the order is ready.  When I got the check, the order total was $31.  I was going to give a small tip for my take out order since it is an upscale restaurant, but I decided not to since I was made to pay $9 more than their website claims the prices were.  My advice if you plan on dining there or getting take out is to find out in advance what the total will be to make sure you don't get ripped off.

I decided to try the new Government Center location today...big mistake. I just paid $11 for wilted lettuce, rubbery chicken, and brown avocado. Don't get me wrong, I don't mind paying extra for fresh, premium ingredients, but this was something out of a college dining hall. Do yourself a favor and avoid this place at all costs.

Four stars because hotpot, and I lahv hotpot. Came here with a Groupon which is a pretty good deal. Got two lunch special and some things on the side. Plentiful to eat ranging from meat, to seafood, to vegetables. Got the szehuan spicy broth per usual. Service was okay. Will come back, because hotpot.

Came here for hotpot, and regretted it. The soup was so bland and the meat quality and quantity was not worth the price. Didn't try the sushi but the fact that it was on a conveyor belt was not appealing to me whatsoever. Don't plan on coming back for hotpot, but the restaurant is beautiful so I'd probably go for drinks at the bar instead.

Nice unpretentious Greek diner, walking distance from Harvard square.  Very extensive menu, and they seem to serve everything all day long. I went there for lunch with good friends. I had the spinach and feta cheese omelette, with potatoes and bread on the side. Quite nicely done and very flavorful. Around the table, we tried the mousaka (my friend was so excited about it!!), the Greek salad, and Zoe's omelette. We ended our meal with baklava - they use honey and a mix of walnuts and almonds for a filling, which makes it sweet but not overwhelming. Overall, the food is very tasty, and the experience good. The place can be loud, though, and it attracts many students. 

Warning for those who insist on 'authenticity': the frappe in the menu is an ice cream smoothie and not the Greek cold coffee drink.

Stop hatin' on the Pu Pu, people.

Their lunch specials are practically free!  A big mac or a big serving of spicy beef with rice?  No brainer.

They also have these dumplings in peanut sauce that I looove and this is the only place in the area that makes 'em almost as well as my old love, Tai Ho, which went down in flames.

MSG TASTES GOOD!

Food was really good, but the hostess was extremely rude. I was told a 45 minute wait, which is understandable and fine for a Sunday. We ended up waiting over an hour with extremely rude responses coming from the hostess. Very disappointing.
Fast, cheap, and open late. I usually come here after a night on the town or for a late dinner. Delicious burgers and fries. Also, they serve beer!

Yay for healthy food in the mall. Minus one for being a bit pricey when the ingredients are clearly not that fresh. Wilty lettuce half the time, brown avocado sometimes. Meh ingredients with meh pricing would be fine. $10 for a salad? Really ought to get fresh ingredients.

Minus one more star for being very inconsistent with portion sizes. Sometimes I feel like I get three days worth of food in a salad. Sometimes it's barely covering the bottom of the salad bowl. Pick a size and stick with it, at least people will know what they're going to get.

I have to give this place credit for creating really cool atmosphere with 60s stuff everywhere.  This place is like hipster crack, so you're gonna see a lot of them there.  The girl next to us was loudly exclaiming that she knew some other kids that were into almost the same music as her, but not the EXACT same kind, so she had to put them in their place.  Extremely annoying.  Still, that's hardly the proprietor's fault.  The drinks were pricey but delicious.  Don't know if I'd order "the Matt" again.  Sandwich was kind of greasy.  Overall a good place to try.  I'd try to go during off-peak hours.

I have been to this Saladworks twice now and twice they have managed to mess up my order. Both times I went they were out of one of the incredients needed to make the salad of my choice.
Other than that the salads are mediocre. The lettuce is crisp and tasty and the cucumbers were fresh. However, I expected to get warm chicken w/my salad instead of cold chicken but that is just my preference.
If there were another Salad place in the foodcourt I would try them before I went to Saladworks again.

It was all right... I got the sizzler steak thing like everyone told me to. It was 16.95, which was cheap, but i definitely got what I paid for. 

I think next time I'd just get the bar food (I hear the onion right loaf was good, but did not partake): Buffalo wings were good, but hot damn was it a rifpoff: $7.00 for a pound (i.e. 6 wings). The potato skins hit the spot (my heart). 

I thought it was a decent steakhouse given it's cost. But you might as well dish out an extra $10 and go somewhere really good.

This is an example of that One and DONE type of places. I placed my very first online order that was smooth delivery time wise as promised. I approached cashier who mentioned they Don't have any honey mustard :/ what a bummer as I was loosing my appetite already with carelessness to details. The employees there are not very professional nor receptive. I mentioned something to cashier the other colleague was eves dropping to hear what I asked as I stepped away the 2 girls were having side convos, not professional to be taking about customers in public. Moving forward to the food. Highly disappointed, fries I ordered the truffle fries were simply tasteless. And had no evidence of truffle oil or seasoning. Very bland simple typical straight up hand cut fries is all I got but ordered the truffle version. The cauliflower app was DRENCHED in oil and had NO CAPERS as they initially advertise. Very inconsistent very misleading. All in all Worthless considering the money spent for small quality and quantity of food delivered. The only tasteful and appetizing food was the hot dog which had a unique taste and texture. Otherwise I will avoid this establishment. Not worthy of $26.00 the cauliflower will cause an upset stomach within 5 minutes, heavy duty seasoning and oil. The capers would help balance it out but they simply forgot about it... go figure since it's a lousy way to retain new or existing customers. Do your research before you come and I suggest order wisely!

Got the Shroom and Swiss burger and for the size of the burger and ingredients (pretty thick beef patty, plenty of mushrooms, lettuce, tomato, cheese), I would say $8 is a fair price. They ask you how you want the burger cooked- I asked for medium, but it came out closer to well done. The beef patty was fine, but nothing special. The burger as a whole was decent, as they don't skimp out on the ingredients. The fried cauliflower was pretty good and had nice seasoning.

Wouldn't come out of my way, but if I was already in Central Sq. and craving a burger would probably stop by.

Just stopped it to try a lamb kabab sandwich. It wasn't on the menu, but they made it for me anyway and charged me only for beef (around 6 bucks). The lamb was spiced nicely and the bread was great. 

So far so good with this new place. I expect to write a another review, after I try a few more things on the menu. It's definitely worth a try. I'm still trying to figure out whether it is supposed to be more of a sit down restaurant than takeout place.

Great hole in the wall in Cambridge. Awesome authentic Turkish coffee  the vegetables that come with the omelette are savory delicious Mediterranean style. The eggs are served hot and fresh. Pancakes are like cakes.

I visited Boston once a few years back and the friend i was with brought us to the B-Side.  To this day, the B-Side remains one of my favorite bars ever.  All I really remember is drinking way too many B-Side Cars and being super stoked they were playing The Exploding Hearts.  Big ups to Boston and the B-Side.

Life Alive Urban Oasis and Organic Cafe will make you turn vegetarian or vegan if you're not already. This place has a unique and hippy type of atmosphere, unlike any other restaurant I've been to in the Boston area. The first time I walked in, I automatically felt happy and full of life. You order food upstairs, then can either sit upstairs or downstairs where there are tables/benches against the wall. Downstairs, there is live music and the seating looks cool. I would advise to grab a table as soon as you order the food, it fills up quickly. An employee will bring your food right to you when it is ready, which is a plus.

Life Alive offers a wide variety of vegetarian and vegan foods. They have fresh veggies and make their own deliciously healthy smoothies. On any given night, expect a long line... sometimes almost to the front door. That's how you know this isn't just some regular ol' healthy vegetarian place. My favorite dish from here has to be The Adventurer, all the ingredients are perfectly blended!

Healthy food that is delicious for an affordable price... Life Alive.

BeanTowne is great for coffee and breakfast but that's about it. Their sandwiches are mediocre at best and there is hardly anything inside for the price you pay. The salads are $7 and are small and not worth the money. You can go to Kendall Kitchen and get great sandwiches and large salads and you get what you pay for!

Huge menu with great options! My favorite is the chicken caesar salad with their homemade balsamic vinaigrette dressing - I could almost drink this dressing, it's so good! Great service and a lot of place to sit.

Delicious, invigorating human food! The food here leaves you feeling satiated, healthy and balanced. Speed of service is very intermittent, and avoid going at rush hour (such as lunch time during the work week), unless you want to wait in a long line.

More like Poo Poo Hot Pot if you ask me.

I hated the food there. I didn't try it once, it wouldn't have been fair. I tried it three times with three different entrees each time and I found the entire orders to just be really greasy, and of very poor quality. I'd rather starve then to eat their food again, honestly.

I know a lot of you may think that you get what you pay for, but you honestly don't. Their chicken never tasted like chicken and their sauces are really sugary and stale. I'm pretty sure they still use MSG as well.

Bad. avoid. never go.

We live in the neighborhood and decided to give McCabe's a try and we were glad we did.  This location is now on the third establishment in two years, but I hope this one lasts.  The food was delicious, the staff were friendly and attentive, and the ambiance was nice.

It was about 10 degrees outside the night we went and when we walked in there was only one table  occupied at 6:30pm.  I didn't have much hope, but I was very pleasantly surprised.  We started with the duck sliders which were different and wonderful.  The duck was pleasantly salty and combined with the coleslaw the overall taste was just right.  I had the beet salad and the butternut squash fritters.  These were both small plates and together they made a nice meal.  My husband had the brisket sandwich which was terrific.  My son had the burger.  It looked good, too, but I didn't try it.  My Chardonnay was nice and they have many beers on tap.  

Their dessert menu is not large, but three of us split the Angel Food cake with maple gelato.  It was an ample portion so it was good that we split it.  The gelato was superb.  

On a busier night, McCabe's will probably get loud, but the night we went it was just pleasant.  

Our bill came to about $90 which was excellent for three people.  Nearly a third of the bill was from the bar, but the food was well worth the price.

My friend heard about this place so we tried it for the first time. The waiters were cute and super friendly.  I had the eel bento box which came with a delicious lobster Rangoon, salad and fried shumai and dumpling. The eel was delicious but a small portion! It also came with two California rolls. It was not bad for the price. Next time I wanna try the ramen. Also, FYI someone needs to clean the bathroom more frequently. There was a dirty toilet seat when I went in.  Other than that, the place has great decor and parking was easy to find across the street. It's near the Cambridgeside Galleria.

I used to like this place when it was the Spirit. Then, when it became McCabes I was dubious at first, but learned to *love* it! One of my faves in the area. But now it's some post-McCabes shadow of itself. Something in between the fun, accessible, affordable Spirit and the delicious Top-Shelf McCabes-that-was. 
Food is as expensive as it was when McCabes was in top-form but is only slightly better than the much cheaper Spirit. Basically, you're going to get food that's about on par with the other restaurants in the neighborhood but you're going to pay 20-40% more for it. 
Cocktails have gone SOOOOO downhill but the prices haven't budged. (And the main bartender with long curly hair is inattentive and, well, if he's working just do yourself a favor and stick to the beer.)
Atmosphere is simply bizarre. Gross "buck hunter"-style and boring golf video games dominate the entrance. Weird "2nd bar" to watch the TVs but good luck keeping a plate and beer on its narrow surface. 
DO NOT ORDER THE BRISKET SANDWICH!!! It's famous but it is SOOOOO inferior to what the "REAL" McCabes used to serve you'd be better off at Arby's. 
So, yeah, it's ok in a pinch but I won't be back until it gets redone again, hopefully with management/chef/bartender other than they have now!

I'm hoping they make a decision and either go back to a posh place or go downscale to something I actually want to visit, otherwise I'll stick to the other,better choices in the neighborhood.

I know it's trendy to hate on chain restaurants and for the most part, I participate on doing so myself.
