When it comes to burritos, however, I'm not afraid to go fast-food-chain-it-up because you really cant mess up a burrito (at least I thought so until I went to boloco), and a chain exists because it's initial business model is good and so is their food. 

I've been here a few times this month and the wait it never too long, and the staff is as nice as they need to be, and not overly chatty or fake. I've never gotten an attitude from any one working here and with the economy the way it is, expecting people working jobs like this to be all bubbly when upper management is on their ass for them to perform while they make a sub-sufficient wage is unfair to them and will ultimately leave you as the kind of disappointed customer who takes bad service personally and will leave a mean spirited and vey artificially inflated 1 star review later that day.

The most important part is the food. If you have them add extra fixings like guacamole, corn, couple kinds of salsa and lettuice, you will find yourself with a good 2 pounds of food that you wont mind paying 8.35 for. I go to places like this for their generosity (ahem, Felipes... Boloco) and to get a sufficiently satisfying and very tasty meal.

Lastly, people who commented before me have left complaints about the food not tasting as well as it should but I assure you, it's very good. Just don't be afraid to spend another couple bucks on add ons and you'll leave a happy and satisfied customer like myself. 

I've been to every burrito place in Harvard Square (been working here way too long), and this one is my favorite aside from Chipotle. 

Stop reading and start eating!!!
Delicious food, friendly attentive staff. We just enjoyed dinner here as an alternative to the usual Thanksgiving meal and it was a great decision.

Delicious, just wish the service was faster. The line is often out the door and once you finally order, you are guaranteed to wait a while for your meal to be made. I'm not sure how they could fix this problem - it's not the employees' fault. I usually call ahead and order my meal to go, that way I can skip the line and don't have to wait for it to be made. 

Despite the wait, Life Alive is one of my favorite spots and I go there often. I recommend the Seeker in a wrap.

I've only had a few experiences here, and I keep going back because I like the idea of getting coffee from a local shop instead of Starbucks or Dunkin. The coffee is good. It's not too strong or too weak for my tastes. The real problem I have is with their low fat muffins. The first time I bought one I thought that something was wrong with it, like they must have missed an ingredient. It was inedible, tasted like Crisco. I went back and tried another time assuming the first muffin was a fluke... but unfortunately that one was the same. The last time I went there I decided to forget about being healthy and went for the plain croissant, and it was greasy but very delicious. I will definitely go back but I don't ever plan on getting a muffin there again.
Amazing food and service. Dine-in or take take out this place never disappoint me. Chicken methi with garlic naan and tamrind margarita will just make you day.

What a hideously pretentious crowd. A prime gathering spot for weak males, the kind of guys you'd want to crush against the boards in a hockey game, only they would never play hockey. The pizza was good, though.

I wasn't expecting the decor to be quite so diner-ish, but it's cute, and the food was very good.  Between us we shared the potato leek soup, arugula salad with toasted pecans, pear & a vinegrette, the pork chops, the mac & cheese (w/goat cheese, broccoli & smoked apple bacon), the banana bread pudding & the pecan pie.  Delicious comfort food- we really enjoyed it all.  Apps were around $7-10, entrees were $15-$20....but I think they also have a bar menu.  The drink selection, as others have said is enormous!  We'll be back!

Certain circumstances have led me to visit North Cambridge a number of times recently, and I haven't been to this part of the area for about two decades.  When I was a youngin' in high school, I would've never thought of going to a steakhouse as my taste buds weren't quite matured yet for a great steak.  In fact, I didn't even know this place existed back then.  

But if this place has been in business for that long and then some (since 1938), they have to be doing *something* right!  

And I'm always a sucker for a good steak!  

I heard of Frank's when it was featured on an episode of the Phantom Gourmet and the episode in particular emphasized their prime rib.  There's the 10oz, the 16oz and the King Cut at over 22oz.  Yes, please!  

Boy, that medium rare prime rib was awfully pretty (and over 22oz big).  I almost didn't want to eat it.  

It was great!  That side of mashed potatoes that came out were nice, too.  Not too chunky (for those who don't like it that way), and real potatoes.  Yum!!!  I usually get a salad beforehand, and their salads are perfectly fine.  Just enough to take the edge off of your hunger (*do* come here hungry) before the main course comes out. 

Pretty much every time I've gone here, I've gotten the King Cut prime rib, because I'm a creature of habit.  However, last night, the hubby wanted to try their Tomahawk Delmonico Steak.  It's $49.95, over 3 pounds of meat, and Frank's recommends it should be shared.  

Motherf**er is HUGE!!!!!  And *beautiful*. It looks like something from The Flinstones!!

It's *thick*.  We ordered it medium rare, the best way to go, and it was a bit charred on the outside, which really only adds to the flavor of it.  A bit of marbly fat, as steaks like this usually are, seals the deal on taste. 

And it's quite a novelty...two lovely older ladies that were sitting near us were getting quite a kick out of our faces when we saw it come to the table and when we took that first bite.  

Just typing this out makes me want to go back there tonight to have another one. 
 
Additional notes...another reviewer mentioned this is the type of place where the waitress is going to call you "hon" and that's why she liked it.  I agree.  It's part of it's charm.  And service is friendly.  No pretentiousness.  Just good eats and relaxed atmosphere.  

Their parking lot in the back is a bit awkward to navigate, and small.  However, there is additional parking across the street at the bank after hours.   Otherwise, it's street parking. 

I'd like to see more of a diversity of draft beers, but I can deal with what's given if I'm hankering for a beer.  Otherwise, there are some nice cocktails offered.

Solid place.

I wish I had listened to the other reviews. This place may be good to eat-in but DO NOT attempt delivery. We ordered through foodler and after 2 hours just gave up and canceled it. And if you call all they do is lie to you. Do not order from here. DO NOT.

It's open again. I'm not a fan of the new incarnation. The two sandwiches I liked are gone from the menu, mainly the buffalo chicken, which was a staple. I tried the next closest thing but if I'm going to pay $9 for a sandwich it needs to be filling. I don't know if the sandwiches are smaller now but it wasn't enough for a meal like the sandwiches used to be.

Really cute and conveniently located spot. The omelette I got was quite hearty and I loved the jam that came on the side for toast. Only drawback is that it's cash only, but there is a bank across the street!

The folks working behind the counter are so loud -- and the music is so loud-- that it is almost impossible to digest the meal. In order to nourish, let's turn down the music and the volume of loud man voices.

Decided to give Nirvana another try and had a much better experience. The food was fresh and well prepared and the service was friendly. The tamarind margaritas are really good. Happy to see a positive change since our initial visits and we'll be back.
Best burger. Period.  Done rare, cheddar cheese melted to perfection, nice moist bun.  Really good fries.  Still warm when you get home.    Found a good parking place.  So what more do you want?

What a sleek spot and welcome addition to Porter! 

-High quality ingredients (the shabu rib eye and short ribs were beautiful)
-Solid standbys and some inventive dishes that really worked: Salmon Essence, tobiko-stuffed fish balls for shabu, and snow crab maki
-Friendly, attentive waitstaff
-Reasonable prices!! Nothing worse than overpriced shabu with watery broth.

Thinking of having a party here once they get their liquor license - def great spot for group celebrations.

Went here for a Sunday brunch a little earlier than typical.  The restaurant was nicely lit and very welcoming inside.  Had the duck hash, tried the bacon and had a decent bloody mary to  boot.  The duck hash alone makes it worth the trip.  Absolutely somewhere we will return with guests.
We ordered goat rezala, instead of rezala they sent goat curry. Whenever we call back they said it's rezala. Lame

This place was clean and friendly. Cheerful and busy, although we did not have to wait in line for a table on a Friday night. Unfortunately the Ramen was not remarkable and the noodles disappointing. The prices were also a little high. Not the best ramen experience in Cambridge.

Darul Kabab is impressive. Considering the fate of the various restaurants that have opened and closed in that location over the last four years, I hope we finally got ourselves a winner and by winning I mean a place with a little staying power. The food is all there. 

Tried the Aloo Paratha bread - thick and fluffy good. The DK chicken curry was standard but delicious, not too hot, just right. Thick cubes of chicken, tender and juicy. The DK Lamb Kebab was also excellent - served with a small salad - too many onions were the only real complaint. An oil vinaigrette on the salad. Includes a yogurt dip for the ground lamb and herbs. Basmati rice came with the curry. 

A glass of iced tea and your set. Prices are very reasonable. Clean setting, personable staff, the owner came over to make sure all's well.

This place gives very generous portions for entrees. I have tried the loaded potato skin boat, fried mozzarella, fried onion loaf, king cut prime rib with sauteed mushrooms, New England clam chowder, and the chicken piccata with mashed potatoes. The appetizers are good, but the steak is what this place is known for. It is delicious and huge!! If this is your first time here then definitely get the steak!! I wouldn't recommend getting vegetables as side because it is just a chunk of dry broccoli without seasoning. I tried the chicken piccata this time and I definitely will not be getting it again. The chicken was good, but it came with linguine (only option) and it definitely made a huge difference compared to angel hair. Angel hair with chicken piccata tastes much better. The cream puff dessert was very good and came with vanilla ice cream. Our waitress was amazing and very accommodating so I wish I remembered her name!!!
Lovely place. Amazing food at decent price. Would go back anytime. I had the Aphrodite salad and ocean blue latte -both delicious.

This is our main go-to local pizza shop. Over the last few month we feel that the quality may have taken a hit, but overall service is prompt, orders are complete and pricing is fair. Having grown up frequenting many New England sub/pizza shops, this doesn't rank at the top. But it's a decent neighborhood option.
The service was terribly slow and the pizza was a bland greasy mess with cheese sliding off each droopy slice
Completely over rated on the menu! Prime rib was very thin and over cooked, not cooked to medium raw as asked, French onion soup was beef broth and cold and service was not friendly. Will not return!

Not sure why yelp says it's closed because it's NOT! The food and family who owns it are just as amazing as they always have been. Amazing service and home cooked food, and CHEAP! Cash only though. Best to go earlier because they get packed since they are popular (as they should be). Casual atmosphere :)

I've gotten pizza here a few times - usually take out, but I tried delivery tonight as it was freezing.  Delivery was faster than quoted and the pizza was hot!  One of my go to neighborhood pizza joints.

It's pretty rare that a burger place opens and they nail it on the first try.  Mainely Burgers has done just that.

The burgers are awesome and they'll cook them however you ask.  Bread/buns are fresh/filling and not cheap like lots of places.

Fries are fresh/handcut.  Not cheap frozen fries.  Yum.

Frappes are solid and they use Gifford's Ice Cream. If you've had Gifford's, you'll know how amazing it can be in frappe format.  The only thing they're missing is a coffee frappe option!

The space is well-designed.  They clearlyyyy spent money here and it shows.  People in the restaurant seemed to agree.  Feels like HGTV did a restaurant space.

Finally - and most importantly - they understand customer service better than any other business I've been to in recent memory.  These guys are pleasant, fast, and open to all suggestions.

I was thrilled to have such a great first experience here. I will be back.  FYI, best burgers in the area now are UBurger, Mainely Burgers (both quick-serve places) and R.F. O'Sullivan (sit-down/table service).  And Five Guys is the best national-chain spot.
Terrific bowl burger. I had the Arriba. Amazing!  Brussels and Cauliflower are terrific if you are staying away from fries.

Good place for brunch. Good prices, good food, and fast service. It's got a good diner vibe and friendly service. If anyone wants the diner brunch experience this is the place to go. I'd rate it as a 3.5 but that options doesn't exist.

Amazing food ! Finally tried the Uyghur restaurant, Silk Road, in Cambridge - so good  the owners are Uyghurs, and food seem authentic ( to me). Kitchen is super clean. Noodles are actually pulled by hand and not pre-made or pressed by a machine .

I love Mainely--really good burgers with creative topping options. I like the Mainah which has bacon, apple, and maple mayo. My partner likes the Coop which comes topped with a fried egg and spicy Thai mayo among other things. The fries are really good too.

When they say spicy they mean spicy. I didn't mix all of the red stuff into my tonkatsu ramen and it still gave me a kick. Soup was very hot too. I got steamed buns as well and the dip smelled way too strongly of vinegar, which was a huge turnoff so I didn't even bother dipping into it. Waitress was a little MIA when I wanted a green tea refill. Prices are a bit on the higher side. I would say this place was good but I've been to better.
Outstanding the best steakhouse have been to absolutely outstanding all around. I got the chili and cheese and then I got the prime rib and it was absolutely delicious.

Came for brunch at 12:15 and the place was super packed, but my friend suggested we make a reservation ahead of time so make sure you do that so you don't have to wait a long time. But they also have a first come first serve bar area if there's only a little bit of people in your group. 

The ambiance was nice and very modern. Very cool tables that convert to fit your party size.

I got the OG Hot Mess, the sticky bun, a mimosa and a coffee. everything was super good. The hot mess was very tasty just a little smaller than what i had expected. What really sold me was the sticky bun. At first they had told me they were out but were currently making some so if i was okay getting it in the middle of my meal they can bring it out. I said sure and when i got it it was fresh out of the oven and super tasty.

A bit expensive here but overall a good place for brunch.

Wonderful food! Authentic, fresh, exciting and delicious. Set up is very simple. It is not a place for a date or romantic dinner, but if you are married to a cook, they should appreciate it. No alcohol and no hot drinks, but fabulous desserts that you can also buy separately to go.

They also offer a unique whole lamb leg or even whole lamb as a delivery option.

This is not a place for a vegetarian, but I am not and enjoyed every fish I tried.

I did not try the shabu, I went here for the sushi, or rather maki. 

If you are looking to eat shabu (hot pot) read other reviews... if you are looking for sushi, either from the menu or from the conveyor (kaiten) read this review, and then go elsewhere.

My first experience was on a Wednesday, I came here alone. $26 all you can eat (but you are charged for uneaten plates). There was sushi on the conveyor included and shabu included. The sushi was okay, and had some nice flavors, but was not over the top special, and did not feel fresh. There was a decent variety on the conveyor, but could have been more exciting. 

Since I only ate 6 plates (~2 small pieces each), and did not touch the shabu, this was a poor value. If you come for the Wednesday special, come with a group that is prepared to eat [sub-par food]. 

I decided to return on a different day in the hopes that there would be more variety. I had thought that the plates circling on Wednesday are the same $2 plates circling at lunch, and that dinner had various fancier $3 and $4 plates. 

Anyways, we sit near the conveyor, and there is stuff circling... sparing you the long winded explanation, there was only one kind of sushi circling, the rest was raw vegetables and other raw/frozen things to be added the shabu. -- Also, the sushi that was circling had shrimp with the tail still on (as opposed to sushi that can go straight into the mouth).

This unexpected disappointment led us to order from the menu, to satisfy our curiosity. we tried a plate of Alaska, California, and Philadelphia. Plates cost $6, and has 6 teeny pieces each. 

A buck for a piece of already excessive for teeny pieces. Add that they were terrible. Rice was of low quality and or undercooked,  nori was too thick, and fish inside was salty (why then soy sauce... and they did not feel fresh for stuff supposedly just made. Seriously, stuff at food courts and Market Basket is better. No lie!

Service was something else... the restaurant had probably two or three groups of people, at 5 PM, on a Saturday (my friends claim this is a sign to avoid a restaurant). It was horribly slow and inattentive. It took probably five minutes to get some utensils, also a long time for the order to be taken, and for water to be refilled. I will applaud them for not charging us for the plate we took off the conveyor and tried that was of frozen stuff (for shabu).

2 stars and not one for some decent pieces during Wednesday.

...either they need a major revamp, or, being empty at dinner-time on Saturday they will not stay in business for long.

Kicking it old school at a locales institution in Cambridge. I felt transported back in time, we were seated in one of the dark booths in the back, there is also brighter tables and bar seating, heck even Keno booth in the front. Seems like a casual, lively place with a diverse locals feel and folks celebrating daily life. Full bar w/ Tall drinks and 3 lbs of Tomahawk steak if ya want it! We had the famous "sizzler" NY strip on a sizzling tray which was done perfectly med rare for us with a slightly char on the ends, you can tell they have mastered steak here and the vibe is soo chill and neighborhoody. We even chatted with the owner and manager on the way out to hear great stories of their photo wall full of famous visitors, such as Steven Wright and his mom who would dine here, also noted  steak knows no partisan lines as Elizabeth Warren and Mike Weld both have exclaimed great meals here. If looking for authentic old school steak house, local vibe and nice service it's here and seems like it always has been right in front of ya. Stop in, Cheers!

I love this place, it is fast, cheap and good.  I have never had anything but there tofu options.  My favorite would be there garlic and broccoli tofu, or the spicy ginger tofu.

I live right down the street, and I could call as I leave my front door walk my dog up there, and by the time I go inside my order is done.

I was wary at first because I heard horrible things, but this place honestly has the best tofu options (i have tried all of them) in central square.
Nice place, tasty food, great service  - a combination you can't beat! My Caesar salad wrap was on point with plenty of chicken.

Yeh, so the food is semi-questionable here. I've definitely had better Chinese elsewhere (see my Great Wall in Bedford review). However, if you are in Central Square and want Chinese takeout, this is a very real option.

Ordering by phone is best, they really do arrive lickety-split. I have gone into their odd shop to get take out and the guy (owner?) taking the orders is all business. No smiles. No hugs. Nothing. He just takes your order and your money and then pretends you don't exist until your food is ready. I guess I wasn't looking for a best buddy, but...still. The "restaurant" itself kind of reminds me of a kitchen area in someones apartment. Strange. 

My man almost always gets the Pu Pu Platter and I always take his egg roll (good stuff). Chicken with broccoli is my constant pick. I'm not sure what sauce the chicken and broccoli actually come in... but the broccoli is always fresh, crispy and hot. 

As long as you don't think too deeply about what you're actually eating, you'll be just fine... besides isn't that the urban myth about Chinese food? I mean, no one REALLY knows what's in the egg rolls.

Best pizza in boston, maybe even the world. There was quite a wait when we arrived for brunch on Sunday, but it was totally worth it. The Hawaiian was a nice 9.2 (no one is perfect), but we had to pick off some of the pineapple because the distribution was uneven. The caramelized onion and gorgonzola  pizza is a delicious combination of sweet, rich and tart flavor that cannot be missed. The hot mess was just that. The idea of it was great, but once we tried it, it was mediocre. Stick with the pizza. Ambiance was edgy and we liked it.

This may be just one of many (and I mean many) neighborhood pizza places...but they are seriously great at what they do. Super, super friendly guys working there when we went. We split a chicken parm sub (they were out of eggplant but so apologetic about it there's no way we could be at all put off). We also split a pepperoni pizza. The food was great, perfect to satisfy our late-night craving. And, they let us stay and finish even after they had officially closed for the night.

I ordered the sizzler, it was very dry and more well done that I requested. I also tried the prime rib and it was flavorless. Really expected something more for the price I paid. The loaded baked potato wasnt good either.

Not sure why this place gets so good reviews. Ordered twice. First time, they had the lights on and appeared closed when I went to pick up the food, and had not confirmed the order. Eventually got an angry phone call demanding me to pick it up. The dry fried noodles with beef was tasty, but barely had beef. The chicken plate was as if they just chopped up pieces of chicken, so there were pieces of bone throughout the dish.

Decided to give them a second try. Dry fried noodles still didn't have much beef, but it included a piece of a metal dish scrubber. When I brought it back, the guy was not surprised (seems to happen a lot? do they not rinse out their pans?) but at least offered to replace the dish. Can't find the picture, but glad I didn't swallow a sharp piece of stainless steel. No thanks, won't be back.
Great music and even better food! The Santa's Shakshouka (chorizo sausage, onion, salsa, 2 poached eggs and naan) was freaking delicious. Very big portions and reasonably priced!

Aaron is right, this is quite close to being an official municipal cafeteria.  Never have I been here without seeing at least a cop, postal worker, road crew or firefigher.

Good chicken parm, the spinach pie is nice but huge and the gyros is fair.
We got kicked out of this restaurant because our baby was too loud. We had already ordered and would have spent close to $200. DON'T GO HERE. The way they treated us is despicable.
Incredible. The service. The food. The atmosphere. The wait was worth it. Carrie Ann was the best!

Excellent Brussels sprouts ($4) - fried and dried, with a faux roasted exterior without the puddle of grease that's too common with these little cabbagey oil sponges. Toss the bulbs in tamari, garlic, brown sugar and sesame seeds, and serve enough of them to share. I'm a big fan. 

Bummed to report they totally stole the show from an only okay shroom and Swiss burger ($9). Plated for the 'gram and cooked to a requested medium rare, it checked the boxes early on, but unwrapping yielded a soggy, sloppily assembled sandwich and woefully under seasoned meat. And sauce. Even the mushrooms and Swiss were abnormally mild. It wasn't bad - the burger simply didn't taste like much of anything at all. Which, when compared to the super flavorful sprouts, poses a real head scratcher. 

And hey, I know it's a fast casual spot smack between MIT and Harvard meaning mainely (!) student staffers, but yo, don't ask for my order then proceed to loudly whine to your colleagues about how time is creeping and you can't wait to get out of there. It's tacky.

Still, I'd go back for a different burger, the killer sprouts and one of those dope looking milkshakes.
Excellent food.  This is a gem in our local neighborhood.  I had the scallion lamb and the beef/onion samosa and both were delicious.

I love the unpretentious, old school atmosphere.  Great drinks and steaks.  Can't comment on the fish or other food items. Always busy when I have been there, and my only complaint is the wait at the bar.  They could probably use another bartender because the last time I was there, one bartender had to handle everything including making drinks for the waiters, etc.

I work right around the corner and Dimitrios never fails! I love the food and I love the portions. The people are always friendly and when I walk in they typically already know what I want. 

I love this place.
I've eaten at this place at least 5 times since its opened up and only one time have I ever been disappointed and that's only because I tried a random dessert.

Delicious pizza with a great atmosphere and service. This place typically gets quite crowded around lunchtime on weekdays (we were told the wait would be around an hour), so we instead came another time for an early dinner. If you find yourself asking "should I/we get the supreme knots?" then my advice is yes. Worth it.

Baklava Pancakes were insanely incredible. Family-owned, real deal gem. you can tell the owner takes great pride and care in the art of his food and makes his customers feel good.  My friends got Benedict's and they were very very happy with those aswell. Truly a Must try spot.

This place is so amazing!!!  We were actually planning on going somewhere else and saw the food through the window and had to go in and try it.  We were NOT disappointed 
Such a cool mix of flavors. So many wonderful sounding things the only issue was we could y try them all
We will DEFINITELY be back!!!
Out of all the "Fast Food" places in Cambridge I have to say this one has the best seafood.  I always get the Fishermans Platter.  One of the only fast food spots that also serve whole Clams.

I recently stopped by the food truck on Beacon. The people in the truck were nice and cheery. I had a truly outstanding grilled cheese sandwich. They had a lot of great stuff on the menu and I hope to stop by there again soon.

Awful service and bad food.  Really going for the bohemian look, but it's only good to see, not to eat.  Portions were small, eggs over cooked, dirty glasses, bad coffee, and flavorless food.

Edit:  I had a cook from the restaurant contact me through Yelp about this review.  He was a bit angry sounding.  I stand by what I said.  

The place is cozy and I really used to like the food when I lived nearby around 2 years ago.  I've been back twice in the last two months and both times it has been awful.  

The first time my dish came out okay, the meat-filled omelette was bland and just tasted like scambled eggs mixed with warmed up deli ham.  My GF ordered eggs Benedict and the poached eggs were WAY overcooked (like solid hard lumps).  To add to all this my coffee glass was really dirty and had a bunch of dried-up coffee crusted on the outside and some sticky syrup on the lip.

The second time we went it took nearly 15 minutes to get drinks at the table.  When we did both glasses were smudged with oily residue around the outside.  We asked for new glasses and the waitress (who seemed rushed, but very nice prior to this) looked at us like we were demanding her wallet.  We did get new glasses but she was annoyed and decided not to take our order for nearly twenty minutes more (it's easy to tell how long all this takes as they have a large clock over the entrance on the inside).  My GF ordered french toast  and I got huevos rancheros.

The French toast was okay, soggy but sweet.  That was definitely not a bad choice.  The huevos rancheros were a joke.  They are scrambled eggs with a slice of american cheese melted on them with a spoonful of room temperature black beans alongside a mediocre pile of potato medley.

The mixed potato medley has a weird assortment of vegetables and just doesn't have a coherent flavor.  It's like something I would throw together in the morning hungover with stuff lying around in my fridge; however, they serve it on every plate!

I do not intend to give this place a third try.
Great high quality food. It's really unique and authentic.  A bit expensive but otherwise excellent.
The sambusak is must! Literally everything we ordered was fantastic. Service was quick and super friendly and accommodating

This place was for the most part rly good what I liked was that there was a big variety for vegetarians. Also this place has amazing water. All the stuff was good except the chai. It tasted rly watery and had no milk. The service was fine but I would rly recommend the paneer tikka masala and goat biryani

Frank's is such a wonderful treat. In a world of chain restaurants, Frank's has survived to be one of the few old-fashioned steak houses. The atmosphere is cozy and relaxing, the menu is what you would expect from a steakhouse.
They have great daily specials, and their food is delicious. Try the onion ring loaf, Frank's is famous for it.
There is parking out back as well as street front, we've never waited more than 10 minutes for a table. Steaks are unsurpassed, service is friendly.

Awesome neighborhood sandwich shop! Try the italian sub, hot pastrami, or gyro. Also makes a great fish and chips. Good prices, delicious food, and outstanding service! 

And if you're deciding between Dimitrios and certain other options across Brookline ave, there's no contest, Dimitrios is FAR better.

By far and away the best Indian restaurant in Boston. Anything you get here is top notch. When I order delivery it comes HOT. The spices in the food are fresh and so, so good. If you want authentic, go here. The prices are comparable to other Indian restaurants in the area, but the quality is superior. Also take away includes a reusable bag, not paper or plastic. Kudos. 

Other Indian restaurants in the area that are inferior: Shan E Punjab, Spice Delight, Punjabi Dhaba, India Quality, Desi Dhaba and Masala Square. The only other place that is equally good is all the way in the South Shore - Taj Modern Indian.

Ordered a delivery for dinner tonight at my sisters. Pizza menu was hard to find. The prices on the two menus I found did not reflect the price I was charged.
I expected approximately a $20-22 total, it actually was $33.61 (included a $1.00 delivery fee). So I'm not sure where the accurate prices are listed.
Ordered a barbecue chicken pizza, a large pile of the toppings had slid off to the corner of the box. -May have been dropped?-
I'm from the Midwest, the pizza was very good quality, toppings were decent, and very good flavor.
Delivery time was quoted at 45 minutes and it was actually just a little more than an hour for delivery.

This was my first time here. I saw the reviews on yelp and decided too visit. The food is great. We ordered chicken chettinad that could have been more flavourful. Dal makhni was great. Chicken tikka in appetizers is excellent. The portions are a good size. The staff is courteous. Must try: mirchitini cocktail. :)
Food so fresh and  good here! Love this place! Goji berry brooth was sooo good, service is awesome! One of my favorite hot pot place

Came here with friends on a cold winter night, Shabu & Mein really hit the spot!

The Hell's Ramen dish was absolutely divine, the bright red soup is very Szechuan style, with lots and LOTS of peppers. The flavors were amazing, one of the best bowls of ramen I've had in Boston. However the angel hair like ramen noodles were unexpected and disappointing, I wish they used the thicker type that they have available when you make a custom bowl. Their ramen doesn't seem too traditional in broth, but I enjoy their take on Japanese.

As with franchises, I think it's a love-or-hate review system on the food on some large scale.  I will instead focus on the service and attitude of employees.  I don't think it's useful to negatively review an individual location on the basis that you hate all Qdobas.

My friend and I went with coupons after receiving them in the mail.  BOGO they said.  It'll be free the said.

I ordered one bowl for each of us with a BOGO coupon.  I added meat to both of them.  Little did I know, I was purchasing an "extra product" x2.

In fact, extra meat is not an add-on to the menu item (bowl) as with Chipotle's bogo.  It is actually an individual item you can order.  After (I kid you not) 10 minutes of talking (read: being reviled) with the 'manager', I will take my business elsewhere.  This system was NOT noted on the coupon.  When questioned, I got a "take it or leave it, we don't need you as a customer" tone.  I want to see the training slide where the manager is told to tell the customer "if you don't like this fact that is stated literally nowhere to the customer, please never come back". 

On the bright side, you can buy "extra meat" without anything else for $2.  Please just buy the meat and rice for $3 and save yourself $6 if you do go here.

Four points to note about Area Four:

1. Lattes are good
2. Baristas are nice
3. Buy 10 get 1 free
4. Pay with your phone (LevelUp)

I don't know what you call this place. A short-order grill, a modern diner, a pizza/burger/philly kind of joint.  It doesn't really matter -- Dimitrios has knocked it out of the park with everything we've tried.  First, it shares a parking lot with the incredible Metropolitan Laundry (if you've never been, I'm not even joking that it will change the way you think about laundry. It's a revelation). So you'll have an excuse to go every week. 

The real winner at Dimitrios is, surprisingly, the subs. The steak and cheese is a chopped steak, gooey cheese, on a toasted bun, with fresh veggies. I get it with BBQ sauce -- theirs is very different than any kind I've tasted, with a tangy flavor that cuts through the sweetness of the cheese and is a great fit. They don't technically have a chicken philly on the menu, but will make one if you ask -- it is even better than the steak. Their steak tips sub was also good (though the tips are a bit bigger than I'd like, so it eats more like a meatball sub) with tender, well cooked steak.  

The cheeseburgers are definitely passable, though honestly there are better places in Cambridge to go for burgers.  They also have a wide array of greek dishes, a seafood menu (scallop plates, fisherman's platters, etc.), standard italian fare, and assorted other sandwiches that don't appear on the menu (spicy chicken on a roll, lamb dishes, cubans, etc.)

There's always a game on the TV (and the location seems to make it a popular hang out for police, firefighters, and EMTs); and the staff are very friendly (if you are a Spanish speaker, you might try ordering in Spanish, as the three main guys seem to speak it).

Love them... Everything... So So Good.... 
Burger, fries and chicken fingers Yummy
Love the salad and buffalo cauliflower 
Just WOW
Yummy yummy! Love me some mainely burgers. The only thing yummier than the burgers is the Barber bros. Tasty!

I am Bangladeshi American and my mom is a great cook so I am very picky with South Asian Cuisines. I haven't been able to visit my family due to COVID travel restrictions so I thought why not order food that reminds me of home? And boy it did not disappoint! The Kachi biriyani was melt in your mouth good. More oily than my mom makes but hey I was treating myself! The shik kebab and moglai paratha were also great. Most of the bad reviews of this place are people ordering soups/salads.... like bruh go to a regular cafe for that stuff. If you want great biriyani and kebabs then go to Darul!

Heard about this place from a friend. Went to check it out. Not going to lie, it's the cutest sub shop I've ever been to in regards to the decor. It is SUPER cute inside. Very clean. Service was friendly & food came out quickly. 

I tried the onion rings & mozzarella sticks, I really like the mozzarella sticks. I also liked that neither one was super oily, typically fried eats like this can be heavy, but it was lIghtly battered.

Also tried the steak  & cheese & it was yummy! Will definitely be back when I am in the area!!
It's a salad. It looks and tastes like I made it from a day-old bunch of lettuce and veggies. Totally acceptable, somewhat tasty, nothing special.

Every Qdoba seems to have the same exact rating.  3 stars.  I tend to agree with that, particularly this Qdoba.  Unlike Anna's, there's only one size (oversized).  And unlike Chipotle, there really isn't much of a taste to the whole package, even when you add in the more provocative-sounding salsas like chili corn (Chipotle also has a corn-based salsa, but not as well-named).  Overall the burrito is quite boring.

On the plus side, they're quick, filling, and unlike some places (Chipotle), the burritos stay together throughout the entire process of eating it.  So no burrito asplosion.  Also, the bathrooms at this one are clean, and the service is generally good.    Basically a quick in and out when you're on the run in Hahvahd Square.

Nirvana The Taste of India... Where do I start? I've never had a bad experience with them, never tried something I didn't love. Team was extremely sweet and helpful in every way possible. Everything we had here was good. Nirvana The Taste of India - Certainly a new spot of mine.

I ordered a samosa, and the stir fried beef. The beef was seasoned nicely. It had a lot of cumin, which I like. However, the beef was tough, and rubbery. It has a lot of gristle as well, to the point where half the plate was inedible. I liked the flavor of the seasoning, but I won't order from here again.

The food is delicious and the portions are huge! But maybe even more importantly -- the people there are so kind. They have an entire pandemic-friendly set-up so that you can social distance from the staff, and everybody is wearing masks and gloves. I've been here so many times and always have a wonderful experience. I highly recommend the dried noodles, but everything is incredible!

Incredible. We were stuck in Boston because of a cancelled flight, and decided to check out the area near our friend's place.

We've had plenty of uygur food back home in LA (we're from the 626), and this place is phenomenal. Family run from top to bottom, and the food was awesome. Give it a shot, with an open mind! We're honestly so happy our flight was cancelled now.

The laghman and the samosa are the safest things to order if you're not sure about the cuisine, but if you want to explore a little more, add in the mung bean for sure. The quail soup is phenomenal but maybe a little salty if you're not used to herbal soups.

That's all I've got, two thumbs way up.

Delicious!  The malai kofta was so wonderfully creamy and had just the right mix of amazing spices.  I haven't had food that tasty since India.  My husband prefers spicy food as well, and there was a nice chili layered into the sauce.  I also ordered dosa and the chicken vindaloo.  The chicken vindaloo had a vinegar spice to it, my husband enjoyed it very much.  For the dosa, I know it's best eaten fresh, but we had to do takeout given government orders!  I toasted it up though once it arrived, and it was nice and crispy.  

Will definitely order again from here, and hope to visit in-person soon!

These guys are great (very nice people & superb service) - the chix ceasar, turkey club and cheeseburger club are phenomenal (also the chicken cutlet sand w/out sauce) is excellent.  If you keep w/in these boundaries, you'll do great.  

PS  One comedic side note to all the observations of Cambridge Muni workers - I have often noticed a CFD fire truck double parked on the sidewalk & there is 8-10 CPDers inside, hysterical... I wonder if they will ticket the fire truck?

I had been excited to try this place, since I roll past it every day on the bus, but it was kind of disappointing.

The place is shiny and new and clean, and the folks who took my order were pleasant and told me that the dish was mild and asked how spicy I wanted it (important!).

I got my food pretty quickly, within 5-10 minutes, and took it home. They threw in free rice (I think normally it's a few bucks extra?) which was nice. But the dish was just okay. The sauce was super oily and not particularly flavorful. It was also pretty evident that most of the "9 selected vegetables" came from a bag of frozen veggies (you know, the ones that come with peas, carrots, corn, lima beans, string beans?). There was cauliflower and potatoes, too, but really? I can make korma with frozen veggies myself. 

Also, there was supposed to be cheese in my dish and there wasn't any. Maybe I was accidentally served the Mixed Vegetable Korma instead of the Navrattan Korma, but it was still only okay. And kind of overpriced at $10! (And the Mixed Vegetable Korma is only 5.99, so I hope that's not what I got instead.)
Authentic Uyghur cuisine I ate large plate of chicken, fried barbecue, lamb skewers, grilled buns. My favorite food I will eat here in the future.

I got the Big Plate Chicken and it's just like how my mom used to make it, plus the noodles and the extra spiciness. The potatoes and peppers are soft and well flavored, and the noodles complemented the spice very well. Would definitely order again.
I love this neighborhood pizza and sub shop! I've been a customer since they opened and I'm never disappointed! Delicious!

Perhaps I've been spoiled by the amount of Uyghur restaurants available in NYC (Cafe Kashkar! Empire Kebab! Erqual!) but I found the food at Silk Road to be average. Frankly, it wasn't as amazing as I hoped it to be, but you can tell the family-run restaurant puts a lot of love and pride in their food so I'll bump it up a star. 

The cucumber in garlic sauce and pilaf were pretty standard and the naan was dry, crumbly, and disappointing. The hand-pulled Laghman noodles (considered a national dish) were thankfully much better with a great bouncy, chewy texture that could do with more vegetables. The star of the show would probably be the lamb skewers, sprinkled with cumin and other spices.

September 7, 2018
Several houses on Peters Street are experiencing a significant increase in rats in and around the neighborhood, on the walks and street, and running through the small children's park. David Power, a code inspector for the City, came out to inspect our properties.  He found evidence of infestation and has traced the main food source to the restaurant on the corner of Brookline & Putnam.  A citation was issued but the issue persists.  He canvassed the block where the restaurant is located and left orange door posters with instructions. You can contact him at 617-349-6119.

Great coffee shop. This place reminds me of what life was like before Starbucks. The guys behind the counter are a little edgy looking and the clientel have their noses buried in their books. Starbucks why do you exist?

A great little Japanese place in Porter Exchange. portions are a good size tha it's cheap. There is always a line during the lunch and dinner rush, but people move quickly. I green tea milk shake is a hit on a hot day.

One of my favorite coffee shops in Cambridge.  Even though I live along the river and am closer to Beacon Hill than I am to Inman Square, and despite the freezing January temperatures, I've been heading over to 1369 for coffee and a little breakfast more often than is probably sensible.

As many reviews mention it certainly gets busy, but that's the price you pay for being a fantastic spot in the middle of the neighborhood its in.  I certainly prefer this original location to that in Central Square, perhaps just because it feels a bit more quaint.

For many they'll find the lack of free wifi and the "slow" service (which truly hasn't been a problem for me) to be a pain, but if you aren't in a rush and you'd like to read or chat with a friend then 1369 is a great choice.

Rebecca's is one of the lesser evils of the overpriced Kendall Square lunch options (-the food trucks). I work at MIT about a block from Rebecca's and come here often for lunch. It's nothing out of control, but it's a sandwich place people.

They have solid, yummy bread, you can design your own sandwich, their ingredients are fresh and there's one little lady that's always there that smiles HUGE when you're nice to her. I'm a sucker for nice people and I've had great service at Rebecca's. 

They also have salads and daily rotating entrees which are nice options.

Sure, their sandwiches are about a dollar overpriced (they'll usually run you around 8 bucks) but if you're in Kendall Square and are considering the neighboring Cosi or Au Bon Pain, choose Rebecca's instead. Au Bon Pain smells like poopy and Cosi is gross and overpriced. Rebecca's is decent and you'll get a smile-hug + homemade bread.
A fantastic place to sit back and relax with a cuppa joe.  Small, but homey.  I haven't been in a while, so I wonder if they have free wifi now...

hey it's cheap and fast....and they have tamales.  the tamales used to be smaller and you'd get three of them....now it's the size of a small burrito!  but still delicious with fresh salsa and their watered down sour cream sauce.   tacos al pastor can be hit or miss depending on when you get there.

One of the best tea place in Cambridge. Although they don't have as lot of tea choice. They have much better price and service is friendly , cookie and cake  are fresh. Very nice wall color and lights,friendly and relaxed environment. They also have local artist's art work for show and sell on the wall. Great service!
Funky and inviting, and the lentil soup was very good on a cold day. It reminds me of what coffee houses were like before Starbucks.

Vegetarian's Haven! Lot of options and good portions that will fill your deepest hunger. 

The entree's are prices just perfect that I consider reasonable in a location like Cambridge. 

Their hours of operation make it convenient to grab food when leaving home after a late evening movie.

Tacos Al Pastor. YUM. They are my favorite thing at this place, they say they are wheat free (I didn't have a problem) and they are CHEAP! 

The tamales and the black bean soup are good too.

I'm sounding like a broken record here, but, yeah-- go when it's empty. Got a weekday off for some reason? Want to play some pool in a pretty cool, newer pool hal? This is your place. Drinks are reasonable, they have good beer (courtesy of Cambridge Brewing Company right next door-- stop by some time and take home a jug of one of their famous beers). Also, a decent jukebox, which is always a plus.
The Gluten free Pad Thai was good. The menu was a bit limited for GF options but I was happy to see a handful. Tables are sticky ... My only complaint.

I love deep dish pizza and had many in Chicago. This UNO is nothing like there. 

Pizza was ok (the vegetarian looked more like Pizza Hut), but the service, was the worst I had in US so far!!! 

First, the waitress took a long time to come to our table. After ordering the drinks, another great while without seeing her to order the food.
When the pizza arrived, while we were eating the first bites, she asks if we want dessert. "Well, we are still eating pizza". She passed by our table and asked 2 times more while we were eating. 

Then when she was taking the plates out of the table, she asks if she can bring the check. We say we want dessert. After ordering the dessert she asks to pay the bill as soon as possible because it was almost her time to leave. Well, nobody asked her that! 

We had still one hour until the kitchen closed, so why keep hurrying us? She could let the table to other waitresses. It was totally rude of her part. 

I wouldn't come back to this UNO.

I walk by this place everyday on my lunch break but the line is always super long so I never go in...
I finally went on Friday and prepared to wait forever but the line moved super fast!

I ordered the red vine pizza and they had no problem substituting onions for tomatoes & the staff was outgoing, courteous, and full of energy.

It was exciting to watch the line cooks work... Everyone had a specific job and station... I would actually love to bring my kids here for lunch because they would enjoy watching the cooks. 

The pizza was amazingly delicious and certainly worth the $8.00 I paid.
I will definitely be a regular!!!!!

Love it.

Came here a lot when I lived nearby. The tatooed blonde at the bar (hope she's still there) made a nice Canadian Club Manhattan. The food was all  right. I loved coming here with friends, especially on the dead nights or at night before it became too lively.

A happy mix of divey and Harvard. It's nice to see some place get that right.

Hands down one of the worst venues ive ever partied at. Yeah the events can be cool but the venue itself is disgraceful. Staff are soooooo rude!! I was trying to be friendly with everyone but all i got in return were bad-mannered A-holes of a staff team!!

Other than that the music is good, but the venue is so grim, dull and dirty. Toilets are disgraceful (unless walking in puddles of urine is your thing). The smell inside the venue is horrendous, but i guess thats what you expect in a venue full of sweaty people when there is no air conditioners, just furnace style heat lol

The mid-east seems to be trying way too hard to be something its not, it reminds me of those old, wash-up bars you would find your college professors hanging out at.

There are much cooler places in Boston you can find good music and people at.
15% discount for mit students!   a good lunch place (i always got one of their many different quiches).

This is both my favorite sushi place to eat at in the area (near Inman Square), and to order delivery from (I use Foodler). Either way, you get a good amount/quality of Japanese food for the money.

It's a good location for a date--pretty relaxed, with polite and enthusiastic waitstaff. In addition to wine and beer, there's an interesting selection of cocktails. (If you want something fruity, I recommend the "Ecto-Cooler.")

i only went once, for a double espresso that was so overextracted that it tasted like vomit.

but, the homeless people outside who rile up my dog on neighborhood walks are enough to keep me away forever.

came with a large group. terrible and slow customer service. the waitress was so slow like damn try reading a book for gods sake. my burger was burnt, the side I requested was incorrect, several other orders had been messed up as well (pizza was soggy, pasta tasted spoiled, vegetarian sandwich had meat in it), and we weren't given enough cups nor pitchers of water. 
waited over an hour for the food and a half hour for the check. food was bad, service was slow, would not recommend anyone to eat here. what a waste of money

This is written as a review of the bar and not so much the restaurant itself.  I must say that the service there has always been stellar, the draft beer selection is excellent, and it's one of the few places around Harvard Square that isn't absolutely mobbed during peak hours.  It's nice and cool downstairs and I usually end up dropping in on almost every trip I make to Boston.

So, I used to love the BG.

Used to.

Their grilled veggie burritos were something I'd actually crave. (pintos, no cheese, please.)

I'd get a side of chips and guac. Since I first started coming here I asked the people I was with, like they knew, if there was sour cream in the guacamole. They assured me there was not. I was satisfied. My tummy was not.

Then when I started coming solo I'd ask the person who took my order if the guacamole had sour cream in it and he'd tell me no. I'd get some. When I paid for my order I'd double check with the cashier, she'd tell me there is no sour cream in the guacamole.

Lately I have been becoming more and more suspicious. I have been trying to notice if I would specifically get sick from eating just the guacamole and I swear I would. But why would I?! There's no dairy in it!! So I just started going there less and less, but it's so difficult when one works in such close proximity. Sigh. So, I think the big tip off is when I got guac with a smear of whiteness. If that's not sour cream, then I don't even want to know what it is.

So I went there on Wednesday. I got chips and guac. When I went to pay I questioned the cashier making sure there was no dairy because I told her it makes me sick. Then she said, "oh, yeah". "Wait, there IS sour cream IN the guacamole?" "Yes." Every time I come here I ask that and everyone tells me there isn't."  "Well, I don't know what you want me to do, I already close the register."

Are you fucking kidding me? I want more than my money back. I want the years of my life and the trust that you stole away from me, you lying bitches.

But seriously, who does that? Why would you lie to your customers repeatedly about the contents of your food? OK, besides being vegan and realizing I should never trust ANYONE and the whole lactose intolerant thing, I'm not hurt that badly. What if someone was extremely allergic to dairy, or, something else they are hiding, or any restaurant is lying about. That just pisses me off.

So, just for the record, their refried beans are made with lard (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lard) and their guacamole has sour cream in it. 

Have a nice lunch.

The sandwiches at this place are amazing, especially the turkey and avocado on grain bread. The Italian sodas are great too, I love the raspberry. The place is so welcoming for a cup of coffee with a friend. Check out the hand drawn maps on the walls done by a client, they are really good.
Good place to eat. Good place to drink. Good place to hear some live jams, upstairs or down.

There is a heavy smell inside, not ventilated. They're using low quality oil and butter in their products. I got a short rib cheese sandwich today, and they made me wait for 15 mins. Most people ordering meat products behind me got their order earlier than me. Staff waiting behind the bakery section overwhelm customers by asking over and over again if they want a bakery item. They use a lot of wrong references in their food menu. Shakshuka is done with eggplant, not with egg. The one with egg has multiple names excluding "shakshuka;" one of them is "menemen." Sesame bagels are not Israeli. The owner either does not know those, or exploits people's lack of knowledge about the middle eastern food culture for her marketing concerns. I will not visit any Tatte anymore. It was a huge disappointment when Cafe Vanille on Charles Street was closed and replaced by another Tatte branch with 3rd class, low quality desserts.
Love the fresh mozzarella sandwich SO GOOD!!! just didn't like the muesli at all!! The yogurt that used for it was too sour.
Nothing competes with Boca for me.  I was there when the first shop opened in Kendall and have coveted the carnitas burrito ever since.  Wish they had more further outside the city.

I took my mom to a concert to the Downstairs- it was an Russian Metal band. The concert itself wasn't that great, but the crowd and the atmosphere of the place made it great!
When it became too loud and crazy downstairs, we came up to grab some food at the restaurant and enjoyed some lovely mellow music by a local band. The food was fantastic- having recently travelled to Israel, I approve of the authenticity and the flavors. 
Over-all, great place for all kinds of people to hang out. 
Diverse and Unique.

We love the blaze concept, pick your toppings, price is the same regardless of what you pick, vegan cheese available and ready in about 5m.  Only wish that this was in a location with free parking.  Drink options were all delicious as well (blood orange lemonade).

I wouldn't even bother coming here. There are so many better places around Kendall Square. I ordered a smokey turkey club and it was mediocre. I could've made a better sandwich myself. I ordered a latte and let me tell you, it was NOT a latte. And the staff kept rushing me. It was my first time there and wanted to explore the menu. They kept looking at me and waiting for me to finally pick something. Relax woman! There wasn't even a line. Yeah, just don't come here. There's nothing special about this café.
This is a small place where you'll also find Ben and Jerry's. This is almost like the Indian version of chipotle. Quantity is great and food is tasty. Although messy, I like the naanini!

Customer service was not great. I asked for a BURRITO and ended up with some sort of chicken platter. I decided that wasn't that big of a deal and took it even though I was slightly annoyed. 

The food is kind of bland. The salsa is good and fresh but that's really the only good thing I can say about this place.  The chicken doesn't seem to be good quality and isn't cooked well. I won't be returning.
Love this place! Coffee and pastries are great and the staff is really friendly and helpful.

Raw chicken

I had eaten Boca Grande on for years, not because it's such a great place or such a wonderful experience.  The place was next to where I worked at the time and was a source of quick and convenient lunches.   Pasty, flavorless burritos?  Yes.  Nasty, unfriendly staff?  Indubitably.   Clean and spotless?  Nah...

One day I ordered their grilled chicken and ended up with a box of raw chicken pieces, browned just enough on the surface to make them appear cooked.  I took a bite, saw the bloody uncooked protoplasm, spat it all out, threw the mess into the dumpster, ran over to Rite Aid, bought some antibacterial mouthwash and spent the next ten minutes rinsing and gargling, attempting to get the taste of dead chicken out of my mouth.

Raw chicken.

Are you kidding me? 

Gross.

This is an institution among students in Boston....well at least among my circle of friends.

I think I ate here almost once every week over my four years in Boston.  Somehow I can't get sick of their food.  My favorites are the boneless fried chicken, fried chicken curry, miso-marinated mackerel, hamburg steak on rice and spicy beef bowl.  All super duper yummy.

You can always request for an extra fried egg or cheese to be added to anything.  I love those extras with my curry (sounds weird with cheese but it's really yummy).

Service is always quick and they've gotten their operations here down to an art.  It's tiny but you can fit a group of 5 or so if you don't mind waiting a little.  The lines get long during peak lunch/dinner hours but I say it's totally worth the wait.  You can place your order while in line so that your dish will be ready when you're table is ready.  It's located right beside the Porter Exchange T-stop and there's also a parking lot that's free.
the food is disgusting  n tasteless. drinks r high  priced n the place smells like mildew when u walk in. they need a new cook, remodeling  n menu.

OH CAFE MAMI.
if I could sing, I would sing you a praise, however, with my lack of talent, it'd come out to be more torture than delightful. 

After living in Boston for 4 years, all the way at BC, which, is at the end of the green line, I've made countless treks to eat at your tiny tiny establishment. Rain or snow, I'll be there to experience the tastes of true culinary goodness. Personally, I love the chicken that is lightly breaded and fried. The tenderness and flavorful chicken never ceases to amaze me. Then there's your famous hamburg with the tomato sauce. I'm salivating even as I type this. 
To top off all that heavy meat, the side salad is light and refreshing, a perfect compliment to any dish. 

A meal at cafe mami always leaves me satisfied and happy. and most importantly, always wanting more.

Toni L. is right, it does seem like less of a pool hall and more of a bar that happens to have pool tables -- but that's sort of to be expected when you have that many corporate captives being unleashed back into the wild at the exact same time every friday. It's bound to become a bar with pool tables instead of a pool hall with a bar.

The food is OK and the waitresses and bartenders are really nice -- which is suprising based on the amount of crap they take from customers. I'm always embarrassed on behalf of the human race everytime I go in there and witness the way people treat employees. I find myself constantly apologizing for other people's behavior. But then, that's just cambridge I guess.

Grafton Group restaurants are like Wes Anderson films (not the décor per say, but the restaurants themselves) - you'll know one when you see it. Park is no different (dark shades and revival furniture).  Love the faux living room corner. 

Though more playful (and perhaps more casual?) than Russell House Tavern, Park shares the same approach to the menu.  Well executed American. Granted, it isn't that innovative, nor is it a value choice, but Park isn't trying to be either.  

Still a winner (particularly the bar).  No shame in a whimsical play on an old standby.

Bottom line up front: this burrito is alright but it's not great even compared to New England standards. I ended up paying $11.00 for a burrito with chips and salsa. Bad price to taste ratio. On the plus side the service was friendly and they do have a wide selection of meats to choose from.

The concept of custom made pizzas is neat, there is a large variety of toppings and the process is quick. However, there is only one size for pizzas and it is relatively small. Our pizza was slightly burnt on the bottom and incredibly greasy. It tasted okay but I have definitely had better.

Finally a nice and cosy place to hang out with your friends in Harvard square! The decor is sooooo cozy and friendly that you can be pretty sure you'll find me there in the coming months grabbing a beer with friends or just hanging out! I would recommend it anytime! Awesome playlist on top of this

Don't be fooled! It seems that given the location and the atmosphere you will be eating sub-par food but that is not the case. This is a great place to grab something when you are on the go, but you will want to sit down for this!! 

This place serves really good food and fast. Ask for the spiciest if you love spicy food. :)

I used to come here a lot on my way to work to get my usual bagel with cream cheese and one of the specialized tea lattes. I always miss out on the special coffee thing they have only in the afternoon time! $5 limit for credit card use! 

The tea lattes that I always get:  Mate (definitely caffeinated, watch out!),  Rooibos (a bit on the sweet side), and Chai...now that I think about, there are varieties for that one, so be sure to describe the type carefully? Sometimes I feel like I keep getting the same taste with most Chai flavors...or maybe it's just me. 

The owner (Simon) is really nice. I can see the love and care he puts into his shop (local also btw!), especially when he comes out every now and then to mop up the floor or clear out things after he finishes making an order.
They have fountain Cherry Coke and are a good place for dining alone. That's really all I can sell you on. The burritos aren't bad, but eating them isn't like talking to god.

Finally finding some pinball.  FTJs currently has two pinball machines- Simpson's Pinball Party and Twilight Zone.  Twilight Zone and I don't get along, but I found it to be in good working condition.  Simpson's Pinball Party was in slightly rough shape, but it was fun to play.

  There isn't a change machine, but the bartender had plenty of quarters on hand.  There was also a Golden Tee machine for those interested.

Three charms of The Cellar:

1: shared sink outside of bathrooms

2: bar and patio tables for easy group seating

3: Quentin.  As if Faulkner himself dropped the best bartender in Boston behind the massive bar.

This place earned 5 stars back when you could only order bags of chips for food, and the stoop was used to share a smoke.

I'll preface my review by saying that when I go to a coffee shop, I usually get a hot coffee and dump in a lot of cream. Not a fancy-drink kinda girl.

Simon's is a solid place, presumably they are authentically local-hipster-artsy-etc.

Consistently, their drip coffee is very good. Nice roast, flavor, strength. Staff are generally friendly. Line in the morning tends to be on the longer side.

Minimum $5 purchase in order to use a credit card is enough to dissuade me from going there, especially if I just want a coffee drink. I carry cash about 3x per year. There are a ton of other payment methods out there which are friendlier to small businesses! If the pastries were more delicious, I might mind this less, but I have unfortunately been underwhelmed a number of the pastries - dry, not super flavorful.

I'm a little bummed out that there's no free wifi (even like 15 minutes), but I understand that might increase costs considerably for a locally-owned, small business.

Bottom line: I'd go to Simon's if it were convenient and I had cash, but I might also hit up the Starbucks down the street.

Came in for dinner tonight. We met a welcoming hostess at the door whom later we found out was the owner. She sat us at one of the last tables and asked if we had been before, I said no. She asked if we were "groupies". After a second I got it and said yes, we bought the groupon! She made us comfortable and suggested some popular stuff on the menu like some special makimonos. 

Our server, didn't get his name, though he was awesome the whole night. He mentioned, oh I saw you met the owner. I didn't even know she was the owner til then but it was cool to see her overseeing her place. 

We decided to go with the sushi sashimi platter for $30 and a pad Thai for $11. To start we got shrimp spring rolls $6. 

The rolls arrived looking small. I immediately noticed there not to be much shrimp. There were 2 tiny shrimp in each, which were cut in half down the side, so it was technically 1 shrimp per roll. It was like eating 2 rolls of vermicelli and iceberg lettuce. The sauce on the side was good however. 

The sushi platter came out first looking good (10 sashimi, 6 sushi, 1 roll). I thought it had a decent variety of fish. Everything from your typical tuna and salmon, then some white tuna, fatty tuna, yellow tail, octopus and eel. The pieces were thick. For a sushi and sashimi platter I wouldn't consider octapus to be a good choice on the plate as it very rubbery and doesn't appeal to all tastes, I would of liked to see another eel or ikura. Everything was very fresh though and the white tuna was incredible. They subsituted the roll on the side from a tuna cucumber to spicy tuna at no charge. The spicy tuna was one of the best I had. It had the big chunky tuna in it with just right sauce and rolled in poppy and sesame seeds. 

The pad Thai arrived delayed and was bland. It tasted very pasta like. It could of used more sauce to soak in. The pieces of chicken were tender. The shrimps were small and not many. Overall I can say it needed some flavor. 

I had asked for tea in the beginning and a small pot was brought over, then I was shocked to find out they charged $6 for it on the bill! Had I known that I would of gotten an actual drink. I mentioned this to our waiter who was very understanding. You could tell it wasn't the first time he's heard a complaint about it. It wasn't really great tea either. I like the natural kind with the earthy stuff in it. This one was powder based and bright green. Your forewarned of the tea! 

The miso soup was never brought out with dinner. When mentioning it to the server he apologized and gave it to us to go instead. On the way back with our change he mentioned he spoke to the owner about the tea and they may consider the charge in the future for it. 

I found the sushi/noodles to be a bit pricey for the area. I was pleased to have the groupon to get to try it out before paying the full price. The Groupon was definitely a good thing to bring new business in. Parking in the area is a bitch so I probably won't go out of my way to seek it out again. I did like the atmosphere though. I may give it another shot in a few months to look for improvements after they get through their "being new" transition.

Man, I feel like an alcoholic, making a bar review for a perfectly viable restaurant...

My deal with Park is simple enough. After ordering two amaretto sours, Park ran out of amaretto. Instead of apologizing and then awaiting a different order, they made me mock-up of an amaretto sour, asked me if I liked it, and offered to replace it if not. Plus, when the bill came around, the drink was discounted.

That's some pretty fantastic customer service and went a very long way to making me a very happy customer.
I absolutely adore their Oxford Fog (English Breakfast and Lavender syrup) tea! I cannot wait to try their other tea blends!

Good lord, I love this place. Unassuming and unpretentious, and the dishes are out of this world. My favorites are the oxtail and stewed goat, both perfectly seasoned and tender. Every order comes with rice, beans, and side salad. The Jamaican patties are flavorful and spicy, and highly addictive. The staff is super nice. They will set aside a quickly sold-out dish of you call ahead.

Fresh, looks good, tastes good, their pizza is thin and not overloaded with dough or sauce, not greasy and more European than US style. 

I loved it, put my own together with the regular sauce pepperoni Italian sausage and banana peppers, and it was delicious.

Price quality satisfaction ratio is top!

This is the bar you want to become a regular at. The stone walls remind me of a cozy den.  This is not a fancy-pants affair, but a warmer, nicer, cuter version of say, Cantab's basement.  It has a loyal "usual" crowd where you'll  recognize enough people to comfortably enjoy a beer even if you are solo. Quentin (the bartender) is the nicest, smiliest man alive.  The other bartenders are so hip you wish you were them - but remain friendly while exuding cool. That's a rare and complex combo. The owner is a hoot.  The crowd is always a mix too - everyone sortof fits.  Finally, cheap beer. So good. The wine is fine, but the glasses are tiny. Does anyone else hate drinking wine out of tiny glasses? Otherwise, it's the cellar. Always the cellar.

I've been a few times for take-out lunch with a large group.

The service has always been good (they even have free bread/rolls to munch on while you wait). My only complaint is that paying by credit takes a minute; there's one machine in the back shared by all of the cashiers.

For the area, the price is fine. The food is good (although I've gotten one of the specials every time I've gone).

I had my doubts about another Asian fusion place in Inman Square but after many visits over several months it's high time to give Ginger Exchange its due. Pretty darned good sushi (and by Boston /Cambridge standards, actually, quite good sushi). Grilled eggplant dish is quite tasty. Great dumplings. Nice selection of beers and wines. Personable, competent bartenders. Bar you can eat at. Hey! ...

... My only quibble is the slightly inflated entree prices. But they're providing good "East Asian" in Inman ... so I won't hold it against them TOO much.

Here's what the menu left off: Bon-chon style chicken wings or what they call "Wow! Wings."  Wow, indeed. You get 8 for $6.95. That's 8 jumbo size wings perfectly breaded and juicy that come in Thai BBQ, Soy garlic, and Spicy.  The flavors aren't overpowering, and the spiciness is easy to handle.  I couldn't find these on the online menu, or the standard restaurant menu, but only on a small flip-menu at the bar.  I don't understand why they are so hidden, because these need to be prominently featured on the menu.

The bento box combo was an insane amount of food and a good deal for 18 bucks, enough to split for two.  My main concern was the korean BBQ beef, but it was tender and edible, not chewy.  The gigantic box included house salad, fruit salad, rice, spicy salmon roll, potstickers, and summer roll.  If I had to do it again, I would not get the summer roll.  Although the dipping sauce is delicious, the noodles of the summer roll were obviously cold and refrigerated for too long, as they were hard and inedible.  

Delicious dessert made up for the mediocre entrees and consisted of green tea ice cream, and ice-cream filled mochi bites.  My only qualm is the priciness of the $2 bite sized mochi.  

Otherwise, Ginger Exchange is your typical asian-fusion bar/restaurant with hip decor and a mess of a menu. Unfortunately, they have a pretty limited sushi menu.  It's redeeming quality is the great bon-chon style wings, because otherwise this would drop to 2 stars.   My suggestion?  Grab a buddy and hit the bar to order a dozen bonchon wings while watching the game. 

Food (6/10)
Plating (3/5)
Service (5/5) - Even kind enough to print our BuyWithMe
Decor (4/5)

I shouldn't say nice things about this place because then it will become even more popular and harder to get a seat. But, since I have spent a billion hours here in the past 4 years of living nearby, I wanted to contribute. 

First, I was amazed at how so many reviews said the staff was rude or had bad attitudes. I think they are so great! Over the years, sure there has been a lemon or two in the bunch, but on the whole, I have found people to be cheerful and patient (even though I rarely know what I want when I approach the counter...). In fact, the positive vibe from the staff is one of the big reasons this place has a nice relaxed energy.

They are no longer cash only. They accept cards!! Woot!

They serve Curtis street bakery amazingly delicious gluten free baked goods, although they run out fairly often. 

They also offer 45 minutes of free wifi.

And the drinks are great. The mango ceylon iced tea is delish. Their iced coffee and regular coffee is great. I don't get cappuccinos much, but they are made with much TLC.

Warning: If you come here on a Saturday or Sunday, you will have a hard time finding a seat. It is a small place. But they can't make the building bigger and did the next best thing by opening a second 1369 down the road (where seats are much easier to come by). During the week, especially during the day, I am able to find a seat 95% of the time.

A quick follow up - obviously the Fuji staff is keeping up with Yelp.  My last order I received gratis, which I really appreciated.  Thank you for caring about my experience with your restaurant.  I plan to order again this week, so hopefully all goes well :)

I thought it couldn't get any better!  Went to Fuji again, this time for dinner...The roll creations are incredible and because I am all about presentation, the food looked as good  as it tasted. Once again I was greeted by and attended to with outstanding service and hospitality. I am looking forward to the next time!

I haven't seen an owner that rude, irritated, disrespectful to his customers !!!! I went there tp get some food to go, and he made me feel like I should get out of the restaurant asap. I was trying to be friendly and start a conversation, but he answered me in a really rude way and insulted me with his answers. He didn't insult only me but also his employee. I really feel sorry for his employees. Obviously he's not happy with his job, and he makes his employees and customers uncomfortable. I don't even want to talk about the food. It was literally disgusting!!
Great lunch box! Serve in various delicious type of food. Enjoyed a lovely lunch with my love again ;)

I was a big fan of Tatte, had a few great eats before, last visit was disappointing: my table had leftover food from the last visitors, nobody cleaned it up, dirty tables, shashukas were soupy and mild in flavor (we had three, all average but not great), got attitude while picking deserts...felt ripped off since we gave tip yet did not have all the normal basic services
Great service, huge menu, beautiful and tasty sushi! There are also wok dinners, udon noodle bowls, etc. So yummy!

This is definitely one of my all-time favorite restaurants here in the Boston area.  They are opened up all the time - even until 3!  I love their chicken falafel wrap with hot sauce, and maybe a little hummus if I have extra napkins. 

The ground beef and steak wraps have a little too much connective tissue, which is why I avoid them. 

The chicken plate is excellent as well.  

Their pastries don't seem to be home made.  I would avoid that.

I used to be a die-hard Boca Grande fan. Now I know it's because I just didn't know any better. I really enjoy the California burrito (bean sprouts and sliced avocados). No other burrito places have this option. They also have a few different fillings to choose from- two kinds of chicken, some veg options, steak, and a few more.

Burritos are a seriously cheap meal to produce (beans, rice, a little bit of meat and a tortilla), and I don't think you get what you pay for here. You have to pay extra for the beans and rice if you get a California, and they charge a ridiculous amount for guacamole and sour cream, separately. Refills are not free. All of the burritos are the same price, so if you are getting steak then you are getting a great deal-  but if you don't want steak then why the hell are you paying so much?

My biggest complaint of all with this location is that the staff doesn't speak very good English and they really resent you if you can't understand what they are saying. I am always over-the-top polite, but that never stops them from talking about me in Spanish while I'm standing right there. Obviously I don't speak Spanish, but it's not that hard to figure out what is going on- they don't bother with subtlety.

CHEAP.

In price.
In content.
In experience.

A soggy, sticky tortilla, blahh rice, day-old-tasting chicken, 1/100 of a teaspoon of guacamole, a sliver of cheese and WHERE THE HELL IS THE SOUR CREAM OR TOMATOES?! How do you run out of these things, being a tex-mex establishment?  Or are you just too lazy to go back to your fridge and replenish your stock?

Better than Anna's. NO where near Chipotle.

Very cool atmosphere and fun place and of course perfect location! However experience was not as great in terms of the food.
