They were out of chai and chocolate croissants so I got a regular croissant which was okay but a little dry. They also didn't have a toaster upstairs to heat up the croissant which was a little disappointing. The thing that got me the most was their insanely high prices! Everything seemed over priced to me. However, I will probably go back and give it another try.

There's really not much that can be said about Uno's - you've all been there (if not to this one, than to another), and the food is decent, if not better than most chain restaurants.

This location gets an extra star for really really nice servers.  They're just sweet, and kind, and super fast.

I almost took that star back off for the ridiculously narrow bathrooms, but I guess they somehow add authentic "flavor" to the place.  God forbid you're obese or handicapped though...
Really great setting. You got to try it at least once. Grab a book, fight for a seat and just enjoy it
I ordered Unagi bento lunch box. It was not too bad. Unagi tasted a bit bitter but miso soup tasted good. I do like the atmosphere inside the restaurant. Decorations are nice.

I'm over the moon about Tatte opening in Harvard Square! Beautiful ambiance and quality food (per usual)! 

Came in at 9 am on their first day and was, as always, pleased with the atmosphere and decor. Staff were very friendly, even with being a little stressed figuring things out on their first day serving customers. 

I got a delicious pastry basket ($9.50). The pastries were fresh and sweet, and the complimentary coffee was very much to my liking. Thick and strong, but not too watery. 

I will definitely be back. I'm so happy to have a Tatte within walking distance of me.
Absolutely horrible service. The servers rush you, don't really listen at all. Won't go back.

Cocktails, bar snacks, beers... hell I've even had Thanksgiving dinner at Park. Looking Glass might be my favorite Mezcal cocktail in the area and the chickpea veggie burger is the absolute best. I was led astray only once, tempted into trying the nearby Grendel's Den. Never again. My allegiance is to Park, and yours should be too.

SUCH a cute little falafel place!  And the A3mu (Uncle) that runs the place is this adorable little Syrian man who will let you sample his amazing lentil soup if you converse with him in arabic (hehe, if you know but only a few words, I'm sure this will also score you some free treat points :)  The falafel is MAGIC!  I've eaten my share of arabic cuisine, but his falafel is easily the best falafel I've had...ever!!  
This place is literally down the street from the Central Square T stop, so if you're looking for a quick bite to eat, check it out :)

Bon apetite!

This place is really solid. I live down the street, it opened 2 months ago, I wanted to check it out.... and we really liked it. I have been to Duozo and many other Sushi places around Boston and this place is near the top of the list. Most importantly, the menu is extensive, my GF does not like Sushi at all and she was very happy.  I can't believe people on here gave it 2 stars, that is a complete joke. I don't think you can ever give a Japanese Restaurant 5 stars, so consider this my highest rating....  great food, delicious drinks, and good people. Sit at the bar and have a tasty meal.

I work around Central Square and this is my favorite place to pick up a relatively cheap, healthy, and delicious meal.  I'm a fan of the grape leaves and baba ganoush wrap in particular.  I'm glad it's open late as my work shifts have been going later too.  If it had some seating that would make it even better but otherwise I think it's pretty great for what it is!
Excellent almond milk lattes--always good bakery options for vegans and gluten free folks. Highly recommend the blueberry muffins!

Walking by this place yesterday brought back my utter feelings of disappointment and dissatisfaction with this falafel experience...I can't believe that I never wrote this place up!!! Thank goodness I walked by it yesterday to remind me to tell all of you true yelpers out there, that this place has to be one of the most overrated places I have been to in Boston so far....

It is tiny...which sure shouldn't be a deterrent, but since it is a late night place, I am not one who after my night of drinking, wants to have to worry about some weird guy standing all up in my business at 2 or 3 am in the morning, let alone trying to make sure you don't spill your food in such cramped quarters! It is cold outside, so of course everyone crams in this place like little sardines to try and eat their late night munchies!!

And the fact that I had had a couple of drinks before I got here, makes it even worse that I was so sad about the quality of food I got here. I literally should have gone to some alley in Central Square and found a dry cardboard box, and taken a bite or two of that, because honestly, their falafel wraps here taste about the same as I think a cardboard box would. Absolutely flavorless, hard, bland, and stale...People must have to be seriously wasted to like this place. The sauce even had no flavor and the lamb wrap we got was exactly the same only a little chewier!

They do have fast service here, especially for the late night crowds that is usually a good thing, but in their case, I would say that means that the food has been sitting out a while...which is maybe why it tasted so bad. Why eat food if it isn't satisfying in the least bit? My question exactly, and if I came here again, I just wouldn't eat anything. Problem solved.

Today I visited Tatte for the first time ever to get lunch with my friend. I ordered the mint lemonade and the BLAT sandwich but was disappointed with what I got (I mean I paid $15!). 

The mint lemonade was only 3/4 of the way full and I bought the size large (which did not look like a large at all)... Additionally, when I received my BLAT sandwich, I was disappointed to find that the bread was super soggy and mooshy and there was hardly any avocado in it (I couldn't find where it was in the sandwich.. that's how little there was!) For a $15 meal I expected a whole lot more quality if not quantity...

Overall, the experience and atmosphere of Tatte is cute, but I wouldn't recommend coming here for a meal. You'll spend a lot and still be left hungry. The bathrooms are clean and there's adequate seating available at this Harvard Square location but the tables often have leftover food and plates and haven't been wiped, so expect to clean up after the people at the table before you. 

I'll be coming back to try the other items like the almond croissant, quiche, and other baked goods. Hopefully I'll have a better experience next time.

I've come here a few times and am very pleased with the quality and service that they provide. I get the create your own and what I love about it is, for less than $10 you can add as much and whatever you want to the pizza. The service is professional and high energy. The manager is very observant and you can tell runs a ship where quality and customer service are the primary focus. The pizza comes out quick and you can take to go or stay. The pizza is enough for 1 hungry person or enough for lunch the next day if youre not starving.

Not the best place to play, but it is the closest to where I live unfortunately.  More casual shooters here than competitive players.  There's twelve 9-ft tables.  The balls and cues aren't great though.  I think they run a league on Mon nights and there used to be free pool after 11pm on Wed.  Since they're only open until 1am, that gives you two hours, unless they want to kick you out early like they did to me since there weren't too many other people there.

Izzy's is one of the reasons I miss living in Central Square.  I still make a point of going by, for my PR food fix.

I always get a warm greeting, great food, and a happy memory, when I leave.  

Their Ginger Beer is the BOMB!

I have been shooting pool at Flat Top Johnny's since the mid-nineties.  The beer selection is always good, and the staff is always friendly.  However, my sole complaint is kind of a major one:  

YOU ARE A POOL HALL.  PURCHASE MORE POOL STICKS.

We almost had to leave mid-game last night because someone grabbed our pool stick while one of us used the bathroom & the other put in a drink order.  The funny thing is, there was still one vacant table & the other customers did not seem to be hogging the cues. 

There just aren't enough pool sticks.  Good thing we aren't shy about approaching other folks to ask if they 'accidentally took our stick' and the person who did was kind enough to give it back rather than argue about it.  He actually gave my husband a 'bro-hug' when we gave him our stick when we left.

Good beer.  Good crowd.  But for the love of gawd - get more pool cues, please

The food is not amazing but the choices are at least different for Kendall! You can get something more homey for lunch, instead of the usual sandwich. And there tend to be a lot of choices, some more healthy than others. Things can be greasy but some days you get rice or mashed potatoes and some chicken and it makes you feel like someone cooked for you :)

I have been to Fuji quite a lot because it is my friend's favorite and, apart from the first time I went there, I haven't had any mind blowing experience. 

Wok dishes are missing something... and I don't recommend them. 

Maki rolls are OK. The tempura flakes in the spicy tuna/salmon lately is not crunchy.. maybe it is because it has been sitting there for a while.

On a different note, ehe hosts are very pleasant and polite and care a lot about the customers and the fish is fresh.

I am a sushi lover and have had way better...

Update 4/21/13

I had chirashi today and the dish had almost no fish. For $24 I expected more. Definitely not going to Fuji any longer.
coffee is good, the place is pretty nice and warm BUT the cash only thing is just a pain.

It is amazing what a difference one year can make.  This time last year, I was living across the river in Boston, unmarried, and wouldn't touch coffee.  Now, I am living with my wife in Cambridge and have become a regular coffee drinker.  Simon's Coffee Shop was integral to this personal transformation.  It is the consistently outstanding coffee (especially when made by Simon himself), friendly service, and overall inviting environment that makes Simon's easily our top choice coffee shop in the area.  In addition to the coffee, my wife especially loves their warm scones and is one of the reasons we like to arrive first thing in the morning on weekends (the other reason is to secure a coveted table in somewhat limited seating).  Overall, I am glad that this coffee shop exists and am happy that it has become a part of my regular routine.

So I just had to write a review since my best friend wrote one, and I disagree with him this time:)

If you've been to an UNOs before, you know that the food is OK. Maybe you love it, maybe you don't. But it's a chain restaurant, so you know what you're getting across the board. Could you get better burgers elsewhere? Yeah. Could you get better pasta elsewhere? Yeah. But you should know that walking in. 

Their specialty is their pizza, and I personally like their pizza a lot. Recently I had to switch to gluten free, and both times I ordered it at this UNOs it was delicious... just a half notch below north end quality and decently priced. The menu is pretty large, and the desserts hit the spot.

The best part about this UNOs is the service. Unlike the Braintree UNO location, the servers here are competent and friendly, the management is visible, and I've always been seated and served quickly. If you like UNOs food, this is the UNOs to visit.

We came by one night on a whim, having always dismissed Uno's as just another chain restaurant. The food turned out to be hearty. They have one of those pick two for a set price promotions sometimes - and the portions for that are very well thought out. The salad stands out in my memory the most because it was more sophisticated than I had expected, with a mix of strawberries and goat cheese. The pizza/pasta servings were not very large, which I appreciated, though might be something to look out for if you're looking for large eats. I thought they were very accommodating for our many needs and capricious decision making. I'd recommend Uno's if you're at a loss for where to go in Harvard Square and just looking for predictable comfort food, for a reasonable price, at a comfortable sit-down environment.

There is very little to fault PARK on. After a few visits, I think it is my new go to in Harvard Square. 

First, the cheese spread is amazing. It comes out with these great crackers that are light and airy. So much better than plain old bread and butter. My favorite app was the crispy mussels with preserved lemon were phenomenal and came in the cutest presentation. The brisket, the steak, the salmon, the scallops, all were amazing. Everything is always cooked perfectly. The cocktails are inventive and delicious. The servers are knowledgeable and timely. Park is also really comfortable. No loud music (although the music is good!), comfy seating, servers that don't rush you out. 

The only place to fault PARK is the dessert. The pie filling was great, the crust was rock hard and flavorless. Amp up the pie and you are a 5 star joint!

their garden veggie thin crust is great. so is their deep dish. 
i went two years ago and the service was extremely terrible (don't even ask) but i returned upon my friend insisting. service was acceptable. dessert was great.
so, yeah. maybe i'll come back in a few months. maybe not. we'll see.

Good, but not great food. Nice room off at the side where six of us could actually have a conversation over dinner. Nothing really to object to, but nothing to enthuse over either. The braised pork shank was very good (its a hard dish to screw up), the fried mussels were an interesting experiment that really didn't quite work. Service was prompt and attentive. Probably a really nice place to go for drinks, the draft beer choices were very good. I'd be happy to go back, but wouldn't go out of my way.

I have been a GIANT fan of Moody's Falafel Palace for years and years but I only recently discovered (stumbling out of the Field drunk at 2:09am) they are open until 3:00am!

I'll make this short and sweet!  If you're a McDonalds/Burger King dude/Chick ... they are open late and have drive through.  If you like good food cheap  - eat here!  Lunch, Dinner, or late night you'll always be happy.  It's a cheap take away place with some of the best damn falafel (and other stuff too) around.   What more can I say?

I love falafel but this was a let down. It's a lot of fun for a late night snack. I found their falafel a little too try and the service slow. 

The price was right though, so I guess you get what you pay for. It wasn't the worst experience I've only been once, so I might have to go again but as of now there are better places to get falafel.

The people who work there are so dumb and so slow
They always drop stuff
They don't put enough paneer in (4 pieces)
I've been at least a dozen times and it's always bad
Not going back

I didn't really know how many stars to give Fuji.  I'd give them 5 stars for customer service and appearance of the restaurant, but 1 star for sushi quality and value.  I came here since I am a sushi fanatic and always searching for the Holy Grail of perfect sushi in Boston.  I was very impressed by the service I got from Tony, one of the partners, and his willingness to valet park my car for free.  Upon entering the restaurant, I was blown away by the chic decore, the sold white stone countertop, mirrored glass behind the wall of the bar, the perfectly flat screen TV displaying ESPN, glass door/windows everywhere, everything super modern and sleek.  I cracked open the menu and realized I'd be paying top dollar tonight for sushi, and having been disappointed so many times by the sushi quality in Boston, I decided to order just a few sample items to see what Fuji had to offer before shelling out the big bucks.

We ordered the crunchy avocado salad, salmon avocado roll, and rainbow roll, very standard dishes found in any Americanized sushi restaurant.

Crunchy avocado salad: It tasted fine, but it wasn't very crunchy at all.  The crunchy flakes were too small and barely noticeable.  I wish the portion was a little bit larger.

Salmon avocado roll: This was the smallest salmon avocado roll I've ever had.  They skimped on the salmon, avocado, and rice!  I really hope it was a fluke, because it was a real disappointment.  If a restaurant is going to skimp on the salmon or avocado, at least give me some extra rice filler, so I feel full at least, especially for $6.50!  This was my biggest gripe.  If they had charged $3.50, I may not have felt as ripped off.

Rainbow roll: On the petite side, but not as bad as the salmon avocado roll.  I thought it was acceptable for the price.

I don't think I'll be coming back unless there are dramatic improvements in the sushi.  The price just isn't worth it if you are looking for good quality sushi.  I think it'd be a great place to catch a Celtics game over drinks, and bar bites, but to have a quality sushi meal without feeling ripped off, I don't know if that's going to happen here.

Excellent Indian fast food. I got a chicken tikka nanini. You customize it like a burrito with options for a bowl, burrito, or gyro style with naan bread. Momos are solid for sure. Definitely worth getting if u like indian food

They had me at Jamaican Beef patties.  

I don't know what else you could want.  BUT, in case, you wanna be a jerk and want something else, they got PLENTY!  

Get the beef turnover, try the fish cake, go for the jamaican beef patty with cheese and if you're feeling extra special - order that with some coco bread.

Ya not feelin' any of these, order a sub or dinner plate - can't lose.  

Ambience-wise - this place is good for a take out or a quick meal.  It's not anything la-di-da, just simple stuff really.  

The servers and staff - great people.  They greet you with a smile and ask how've you been and be friendly.  

Good people, good (cheap) food, great times.  

I'll always be back

I'm picky but I was impressed.  I would describe the food as Normal American food but classed up.  I had the bacon three ways. It was light but shareable.  The roast beet salad was to die for.  Def would recommend.

Dropped in after a movie at Kendall Square Cinema. What a nice surprise! I am always looking for places that have vegetarian options, and this had quite a few. Inexpensive, nice decor, pleasant waitstaff, and the food was really good. I ordered the vegetarian delight (asparagus, broccolini, carrots, rice) which was delicious. We also had mango-avocado maki - very unusual and delicious. (The only - very slight - disappointment for me was that the miso was not vegetarian and they didn't have brown rice.)

I would definitely go back.

If it wasn't for my best friend's obsession with Uno's, I don't think I would ever come.

I mean, honestly, their food is decent. And by decent, I mean it's nothing special. You're better off going to a mom and pop burger joint (specifically, if you're in the Harvard Square area, stop by Bartley's Burgers) or fast food (as it will be cheaper and will get it quicker).

Whatever, though. I've been here maybe 5 times in the past few years and I've always been seated on the main level. I actually enjoy the layout, there are booths, stand alone tables, large tables for gatherings. I dig it. The last time I went, though (this weekend for lunch) we were awkwardly seated downstairs in, what I imagined were, cigar lounge chairs. They weren't too conducive for eating (but, I wouldn't  mind smoking a cigar, drinking a beer, and acting pretentious). 

The bathroom is in the basement, too, which is a little strange. I would imagine the bathroom would be on the main level (especially for those older folk who have trouble getting up and down the stairs). Again, low expectations. 

The food is, as stated earlier, sub par. I've gotten the rattle snake pasta before (which, I hate saying because I have a huge fear of snakes) and it's something I can make at home. Whenever I go out for lunch or supper, I really try to order things that I either cannot make or refuse to make. But the taste of the pasta feels like it was just warmed up or made in bulk earlier and just microwaved. I'm not impressed. 

The burgers don't suck, which is good. I had a veggie burger the other day and it was pleasant. I was a little shocked they didn't have bleu cheese, but what can you do. I ordered french fries for the side and, let me tell you, this was the only impressive thing about Unos. The fries are pretty good.

I feel bad writing such negative review,  but such is life. I wouldn't recommend going here, ESPECIALLY because there are SO MANY BETTER PLACES you can go. Give Tommy Doyle's a shot. How about Bartley's. Have you tried Cambridge, 1? Go somewhere else.

I think they make drinks using beans from George Howell's Terrior Coffee, but they do sell them in bags. Maybe I got lucky but the drip coffee was actually fresh and quite delicious on its own. Their menu features separate entries for espresso and ristretto- my ristretto had a curiously thin body, but was topped with nice golden brown crema (no redish hues to speak of) and tasted rich, decently complex, and completely defect free.

Went Saturday 4/28/18, and ordered chicken breast with ripe plantains (the yellow ones) and salad;  everithing was delicious.  Invited a friend that has never been there before and she ordered "mondongo" and fell in live with the place.  Thank you!

This is a great place for after work drinks near Harvard. It's a cozy, comfortable basement with decent beer, a friendly bartender, and good bar food that's not too expensive. It's casual and an easy place for a large group to hang out.

This is a solid place, good food, interesting atmosphere and pretty good service.  The upstairs has a completely different feel from the downstairs though so it's a little odd- the upstairs seems more put together but is super brightly lit so we always end up sitting downstairs, which has more of a dive bar feel.

Solid place.  Why doesn't this State have a happy hour?  All I could think was, best Happy Hour spot ever, but that was the Happy Hour fanatic in me.  But the drinks and food are worth the full price!  The decor and ambiance was fantastic,  very lounge like, and I liked it.

Starting the meal, instead of having bread and butter, they brought out a lavash bread and handmade cheese.  Seriously, was a nice surprise and way better than the regular offering on bread and butter.  

We had a gilt coupon which allowed us to try a decent amount of appetizers and entrees.   The only fail in any of the food for the night was the Bacon three ways.  I love bacon, read some of my other reviews, and I love meat, ham being my favorite.  This dish definitely underwelmed.  The prosciutto overpowered to melon in one of the three ways.  I couldn't really taste the cheese on either of the other two bites, and they were gruyere and brie, so they're strong cheeses, I just think the dish missed the mark, but could have been epic.

On to the good stuff,  starters of the Roasted Beets was amazing.  It had lots of different varieties of beets.  Then the lobster & crab dip.  Gooey, lobstery, crabby, cheesy deliciousness.

Entrees, Cheeseburger. Came on Sandwich bread and a mountain of fries.  It's a good burger and who doesn't like a ton of fries with anything.  The Salmon dish was pretty awesome, fish cooked perfectly and the salad of greens with feta it came with was really refreshing with great flavors working together well.  The Steak dish was pretty awesome, steak cooked exactly as ordered, with creamed spinach and potato wedges.  Everything worked well and tasty.  There's a ton of other entrees that should be tried as well, lots of great offererings.

Dessert, got the triple tartine, was chocolate heaven.  If you like any kind of chocolate you'lll like this.  Also, got the seasonal fruit pie, it was a peach & raspberry pie, served with a scoop of vanilla.  It was also really tasty.

I usually will talk about drinks first, but I felt the food was amazing and I should give it major props.  But now we're moving on to my favorite, well crafted alcoholic drinks.  FYI for those who don't drink, they are willing to make some of their concoctions into mocktails.  I had the Giant Killer, great name, great booze, happy with my choice for sure.  It was Bourbon, Limoncello, house made lavender rose honey syrup, and some lemon juice.  It was so refreshing for the summer and if you like bourbon really gives you that bourbon flavor with some sweetness.  I want another one right now.  I also tried the Lavender Moon, gin based, with elderflower liquor and the house-made lavender rose honey syrup.  Also a solid drink with great flavors.

All in all, great place, come here, bring friends.  Next time I'm in Boston, hope to be back!

My first visit to the Cellar was for a friend's birthday. Ashley R. on yelp, to be honest. You should check out her reviews. I think normally I would have passed over the Cellar for the rest of my life had she not picked it for her birthday get-together. She has a lot of friends. They all showed up. So, the Cellar was pretty packed. And even so, it was a great time. We ordered fries. We talked. The bartender was "jovial", which is probably the only time I've used that word in a sentence. Despite it being a smaller little hangout, it was a great time, and it opened our eyes to the Cellar. 

Fast forward to last night, where I met up with a few friends (including Emma D., whose reviews you oughta check out) and had Thursday night drinks and dinner. Excited to get some Hoegaarden on tap, we entered to find it was not nearly as packed as it was for Ashley's birthday. 

The same bartender was working. We suspected that he might be the owner. My friends food had already arrived, and damn if it didn't look really tasty. Which was sort of surprising. I hadn't eaten the last time, (except for the amazing rosemary fries), so I didn't have any idea what to expect. 

I got the chicken croquettes and a cuban sandwich. I also ordered Hoegaardens. The bartender was supremely friendly, minding me to "watch for seeds" in my beer. How funny. My food arrived, and the chicken croquettes were soo good. They were like crispy little balls of chicken pot pie. The cuban was also very good, but also sort of felt like a reuben? A cuban reuben? Didn't matter, it was still heavenly. A+++ would buy again. 

My friend Sam also had a cuban sandwich, and she asked for a box. The bartender brought over a chinese food box and asked if "he could bend it". She's like sure, and he bent the sandwich into the chinese box. It was actually really really funny, and only added to the charm of the place. 

Interestingly, I told the bartender that I was going to yelp him. He hadn't heard about yelp. So if you're reading this, hi, I gave you five stars, because I love the Cellar. It seems other people here agree with me. congratulations.

Things have gotten much better since I know now to stay away from the mixed drinks. I mistakenly ordered a Mai Tai and it had blue liquor in it. I had a sip and soon asked for a regular Crown and Coke to replace the bad Mai Tai! Other than that - some good points:
1. They have $2 PBR - the hubby loves it!
2. $1 oysters on Thursday
3. Their cooked food is actually pretty good. Ex: Singapore noodles, Unaju, and House Dunkard's noodles

Their sashimi sometimes leaves more to be desired but I regularly get the salmon sashimi and haven't had any problems. They also don't have mimosas but they can do a sake mimosas which tastes better than a regular mimosa.

I am what some may call a "regular" at this UNOs location ie, I probably go here way too much.  My mayorship on foursquare is a testimony to this!  That being said, I have been seeing some pretty mixed reviews about this place on Yelp and contrary to what some of the negative reviews have been saying, I have honestly never had a problem during any of my visits.  The management consists of friendly people who always want to help a customer if they are having a problem, and the serving staff (bartenders included) have always been very friendly to me from the beginning.  For all of you sports fans out there, note that their bar is absolutely loaded to the brim with HDTVs! You can be watching all of your favorite teams simultaneously!

The food on the other hand is generally good, especially if you come in for their snack hour at night (half price apps).  I definitely recommend getting the pizza though! 

Thank you for being in Harvard Square! I look forward to many more visits in the future!

This place is really cute and the staff is really friendly.  I was a movie extra for a film that used Simon's Coffee shop for a scene and they were really friendly.  The coffee and treats are really delicious.  I strongly recommend the mint-chocolate hot coco, it is delectable.

Paid for a ticket to see RJD2 with 3 openers...got to the Downstairs at 8pm on a Thursday night to find a sign that there were 5 openers and RJD2 would not be playing until 11:30pm. Decided to try and stick it out. Upon walking downstairs I had my bag wordlessly ripped away from me by security and my water bottle tossed out before I had a chance to say anything. 

We stayed until shortly before midnight and never saw the band we came to see. I'm not sure if he ever showed up...had to leave to catch the last bus of the night. 

The venue is definitely awesome and the drinks are affordable but I can't believe I paid for a ticket and didn't get to see the headliner. Definitely feel taken advantage of.

Comfortable, inexpensive, and delightful. We were amazed that even though great music was playing, we could still hold a conversation with each other. The Cellar wasn't too packed, and we were able to find a nice little table with elbow room to spare. The other bar-goers weren't older or younger, and hubby said he felt like he was just about the average age in this bar. No frat boy yelling nonsense, no hootchie mammas dancing up on my man.... Perfect. 

The bartender was working this rockabilly outfit / look / haircut, and was very cute and friendly. He seemed truly excited that our two drinks only cost $8, and exclaimed "The price is right, folks!" He even gave us a tasty teeny bag of Fritos for free! Yum!

So, I heart The Cellar. Three rounds of drinks only cost us $30 (that's tip included), and it's within stumbling distance of my apartment.

I rarely post positive reviews for chain places especially when they can be a hit or a miss. For the most part, I have nothing but great things to say about this place. 

Occasionally they mess up when they get really busy and my experience had been more or so great except for one time where they totally got my pizza wrong and gave me someone else's. The management was nice enough to take care of me and a friend the next time we visited which I thought was wonderful. 

The toppings are fresh, the staff are friendly and the choices are unlimited for the price you pay. The only issue I see is that they need to get more help at the oven station where most of the things can go wrong occasionally otherwise everything is fantastic.

LITERALLY the ONLY place to go to for authentic Spanish food. My fiance and I absolutely LOVE their beans. I'm a v3getarian now but before every Wednesday we would get the chicken and cheese empanada and the Jamaican beef patties. It is a small little place so often their is not a lot of seating. But honestly once you taste their food you forget about all that. 
Mmmmmmm.

Good stop in for dinner.  Near Harvard Square and passed right by it the first time before realizing I missed it and needed to loop back.  

Had a reservation and table was ready and waiting when I arrived.  I did think it was nice that they sat me as I waited for my dinner date (my daughter) as I know many places want everyone there first.  I would have been fine with it, but having just come directly from the airport after a long flight, I appreciated the hospitality and being able to relax and order a much needed drink.  

Warm and inviting inside with a very nice drink list and food menu.  We split the duck confit poutine and the lobster and crab dip to start, both which were very good.  Generous potion overall including very nice hunks of duck meat on the poutine.  Flavorful and good comfort food for a cold night.   

For mains I had the patty melt and Jillian the short rib.  Both were very good.  I generally skip the bread on my burgers since so many of the large buns overtake the taste, but this was served on a delicious rye bread which really enhanced the flavor.  I tasted the short rib as well which was very good.  Service was very good throughout and this was a very nice stop and on the return to list.

We stopped in for a weekday lunch at the Ginger Exchange and were pleasantly surprised. The restaurant is nicely decorated, modern, and very clean. The service was attentive. The place was surprisingly empty....guess it's not a big lunch spot. 
On to the food:
We had the Naked Narutos, Drunkard's Noodles, Singapore Rice Noodles, and General X's Chicken-- Everything was good, not great but tasty. The lunch portions were generous and the prices were very reasonable.
The wine prices were a bit high but not ridiculous. 
Overall a solid 3.5 stars experience and I would come back for lunch again.
I don't know why this place doesn't have more stars. It's authentic, it's affordable, and it's easy.

Watching a lot of beefy construction workers doing their thing, day in, day out, all through the fall and the early winter, you get kind of curious about opening day in the spring. So along we go, opening day jitters not withstanding. And since then, we have returned half a dozen times or more. This spot is making a solid splash in the neighborhood. 

The space is very bright and open, with an assortment of tables in one fairly roomy area and some awkward looking booths in a narrow hallway that also leads down to the ladies room. Fairly loud music from a radio station permeates the restaurant (lots of techno and Adele) and ESPN is lovingly displayed on the TV above the bar. There are always solo diners eating here which is nice. Keep that in mind if you are on your own. 

The menu is totally enormous but we always order sushi and a glass of wine or sake, which seems like the right and proper thing to do. There has been no sushi in this area and the idea of having sushi in Kendall Square made me renew my lease early when I heard they were going to be a neighbor. The sushi is beautifully prepared and is not bad at all. But I must admit that I was surprised to see a very large lemon slice adorning one of my sushi orders. 

There are options for vegans, vegetarians and even BLT lovers, as there is a BLT sushi roll. Creative concept, but I don't eat bacon so can't comment there. Bacon sushi? Hmmm. 

Service can be very hyperactive, informal, confused and uber-friendly. No, I really do not want to hear about your private life. They also spend a lot of time yapping and fooling around with one another in an unprofessional manner. The exceptions are the sushi chefs. They are quiet and respectful. 

This location sees a lot of lunch traffic from people who work nearby and is clearly a happy vision for many of my neighbors who are reveling in all of the new restaurants coming to the Kendall Square area. 

The prices here are on the expensive side, I am finding. It's not easy to get out of here for under $20 and feel satisfied.

Welcome to Kendall, Fuji.

I eat sushi a lot so I end up trying about everything, but I really need to stop ordering here. I just don't think they do sushi very well. It doesn't taste authentic. Tastes like a chain sushi joint with below-average food. And it's not cheap. Find something else if you can.

Food: 3 Stars.
Valet: 0 Stars. DO NOT TRUST THE VALET!
I gave 2 stars, because the food and waitstaff were nice. The manager and bus boy/valet ARE HORRIBLE.

H and I came in on a relatively busy Saturday night. We were celebrating our babymoon (last hoo-rah before baby comes)... and decided to venture out of our hotel. We found Fuji at Kendall was less than 2 miles from our hotel, and with a Groupon for $40 worth of food for $20, we decided to take advantage. Off we went in our car... and happily found out the restaurant offers complementary valet for patrons. More on that bit later.

Not thinking of calling ahead (although we did call several times) for reservations, we had a short 10 minute wait while the bus boy (who also valeted our car) cleaned our table. When we were seated by the host and perused through the menu, nothing caught my attention. Things mentioned are often found in other Japanese/Chinese restaurants (H and I have made our way through over 10 in the past month alone)... So we asked the bus boy (who was busy bussing tables) for any recs on good dishes. He happily suggested the pineapple fried rice, general gao... and that's all I can remember. The pineapple fried rice did picque H's interest enough for him to order it. H also ordered a Philly maki. I ordered the tonkatsu with soup and salad and added an avocado-mango maki. H's pineapple fried rice was incredibly presented in half a pineapple and was nutty, but fruity. (It's almost as good as the pineapple fried rice I had in San Diego!) My tonkatsu was dry (it's easy to overcook pork after all, no biggie)... but the portions were pretty generous. The "bulldog" sauce was also sweeter than I normally take it- that was a nice surprise. The noodles were greasy... but I don't like noodles in general, so I didn't mind the grease. Both makis were average, but I like that the sushi chef didn't bombard the rolls with rice.

About the valet: H and I had a great night and a nice meal together as we prepared for the impending birth of our daughter. After paying, we asked for our car and it was pulled in front of the restaurant on the main road. The valet/bus boy came back in commenting, "It's so cold!" and that was it. Never told us anything about any issues he might've encountered. The high beams were on and the radio was turned off, but we didn't think anything of it. We drove off... and when I turned on the radio, I noticed I had to input a code. WTF? You only need to input a code if the car battery is completely drained. (H had a full tune-up and regularly maintains our car. Plus, even with the 111" we've had in Merrimack Valley, the car has always started, never stuttered- NEVER had a problem.) LIVID at the lack of communication from this employee, I expressed my anger and disdain. The manager was less than enthused and basically brushed me (and H) off, saying that it was definitely MY CAR WITH THE PROBLEM. H explained that he wanted to speak with the bus boy/valet to see WTF happened, so the manager explained that he'd speak with said employee. 10 o'clock rolls around and I get a call from the same manager. Blah, blah, blah. It's the car. Not the employee. I didn't even get an apology.

The other reviews weren't kidding when they mentioned a horrible managerial staff. The managers don't care about the concerns of their patrons.

I've eaten countless burritos here, and I can safely say that if you're looking for your standard burrito fare you won't be disappointed. There's a fairly wide selection and the size of the burritos is roughly equal to a 5 pound brick.

There's a few important details however that prevent me from giving a 4 or 5 star rating. First, their topping selection is pretty minimal - no cheese, veggies, etc. Just the standard beans, salsa, and hot sauce. Secondly, the sour cream and guacamole cost extra. These really are standards for a burrito so it's kind of annoying that they're not included in the price. These really aren't reasons that prevent me from eating here repeatedly but i comparison to the many other awesome places around the city that don't charge for such things, it's a bit disappointing.

The Inman Square 1369 has a cozy atmosphere, good for meeting up with friends.  It tends to be closely packed with smaller tables, and it may be impossible to find seating at some times of the night.  Music is louder than I'd appreciate for good studying; tables are small, except for the back area.  If you're looking to get a bit of work done, go straight to the back.  Fee-based wireless actually helps me focus on studies but inhibits nabbing materials from the internet.  Mixed coffee drinks are OK; I like mine sweeter with a tad more coffee flavor.  Basic gist is that the 1369 is a nice alternative and good for chatting, reasonable for studying.

One of my biggest beefs with 1369 is their early closing time, 10 PM on most weeknights.  I'd like to stay later, and I'm definitely not the only patron to be there at closing multiple times.

Bike parking is available at a few limited locking posts around the corner or some select street signs.

I have been boycotting Tatte since my third coffee experience there. Didn't warn my boyfriend, and he paid $4.50 for a mocha with gritty coffee grounds all over the bottom of it. 

Every time I see news about the newest Tatte opening (what is it now, 9?) I just shake my head. This is becoming a depressing money-grab and the quality is awful. Stop being an embarrassment to local chains! Boston deserves better!

I love Izzys w/ all my heart! Located just a stone throws away from me, I have gone to this diamond in the rough over and over and over again. Everytime I need that Puerto Rican flavor to satisfy my hunger I know that Izzy's is the place to be. My go to items are always the specials as my usual staples of arroz amarillo, platano maduro (or salad if im feeling healthy) and choices of stewed chicken, stewed beef, goat, oxtail, etc. have all hit the spot consistently. 
I love the Puerto Rican Pop of memorabilia all over the walls and the tables. The Puerto Rican icons (from baseball players, government officials, etc) decorated all over the restaurant also adds power to the punch and gives this place instant street cred...
I love how IZZY and family make me feel like one of their own, as I am always greeted with a "Hola Primo Como Estas?" 
The food comes out lightnight fast (honestly w/in 1 minute one time) and always filling. My only knock on the specials is that sometimes they may run out of the popular meat items like oxtail, etc. early on the evening leaving me crying back home after a long days work. 
Other than that Izzy's is always there to make my day and I will always continue to come back over and over and over...

Izzy's is a small shop that is worth going in to. I've been a couple times with a friend and we weren't disappointed. The prices are reasonable and even better because the portions are large. Go with an appetite. There's always a table to sit at and enjoy your meal.

The meat in sauces have been tender and the soup is tasty though a bit salty for me since I don't really cook with much salt. Jamaican patties are good, especially if you like spicy. I've enjoyed the rice and beans and plantains as well. There's definitely more I have to try so I will be back.

The Middle East may not be the flashiest, largest, nor nicest club in Boston,but nevertheless it is the premier venue to see small to moderately large acts that come through Beantown. Pretty much every night has a phenomenal booking, well worth the trip to Cambridge Central no matter what end of Boston you are coming from. I go to school at Boston College in Brighton and the bike ride is a clean 15-20 minute shot down Comm. Ave mostly, but the T runs right there and cabs are always available.

Definitely see buzzed about acts at the Mid East because despite the capacity and prestige of who performs, it is always an intimate experience. You can be right up next to the stage and reach out and touch the artists if you wanted. I saw Black Lips perform at the Downstairs twice this past year and they have recently stepped it up to opening for the Raconteurs at the BOA Pavilion. The Hives recently came here to do an intimate show while they were on their tour with Maroon 5. You are getting the best acts in the country (nay, the world!) and you can see the perform in what feels like basically a step up from a community center hall. But in a good way of course. :)

TIP: Leave your bags and coats between you and the stage monitor speakers. It sure beats paying for the coat check and having to wait on the long lines that form after the shows.

Been living in Cambridge for over 5 years now and this USE TO be my weekly spot, I refered everyone here, my gf/nephew/etc.  Loved the food and the owner.  This place recently has been renovated and the owner is no longer present so the INMATES are running the place.  

FIRST.  The woman at the cashier is always on the phone so she won't interact with you at all.  Unless she is Yelling at you saying your wrong and she is right regarding a sandwich?!

SECOND.  If you want something on your rollup like pickles/no onions you better not ask because its not BURGER KING where you can HAVE IT YOUR WAY.  Especially if there is 1-2 people behind you.  

DO YOURSELF A FAVOR AND SKIP THIS PLACE.  SAD SAYING THIS BECAUSE THIS PLACE WAS AWESOME!  USE TO BE....NOW IT IS JUST ANOTHER SNOBBY TRENDY EATERY!

I WILL NEVER EVER GO BACK...........................

Simon's Coffee Shop has the most professional baristas, and they use superior coffee beans. Their iced mocha latte is to die for! You'll find a lot of writers, law students, and locals taking up most of the space. Believe me, it's always packed with nowhere to sit most of the time. They play a wide range of music...from Nirvana, Arcade Fire, to Serge Gainsbourg. Yes, I love Serge, and this coffee shop plays his music. How rare. That's why Simon's Coffee Shop is my number one favorite in Cambridge. Excellent people, coffee, and music!

Ordered delivery on Monday at 6:30 from foodler.com and it arrived in 30 min.  

Middle of the road sushi in terms of quality, taste, and price.  Got a negi hama maki, philly maki, green monster maki, dumplings, and bok choy.  All good, nothing that blew me out of the water.

Fine just fine.

As authentic as it gets in Cambridge. This puertorican restaurant will have you believing you're back at the island. Highly recommend any meat (pollo guizado is my favorite) with white rice and beans, accompanied with some tostones. Pernil is also delicious, as is the mondongo and asopao soups. Service is outstanding; the family that runs the restaurant is very welcoming.
Great food and a decent beer selection. The burgers are fantastic, as is the spicy lamb gnocchi. Their chef is clearly talented. Relaxed atmosphere with quality bistro food.

Split an Oyakodon and Yakidon. Both were really good. The Oyakodon (essentially chicken and egg), was really flavorful. The chicken was really moist and the egg had a really nice texture and flavor to it. The beef in the Yakidon was nice and thin sliced, but not dry. The rice in both bowls were really sweet and cooked just right: not too sticky, not too raw. 

They also give you a miso soup at the beginning of the meal, which is nice because while Cafe Mami is a "fast food" place, you still need to wait for the chef to cook the food.

Just be aware that there are tons of students there around lunch time so you might not get a seat and you may not want to bring all your buddies at the same time.

Overall, it's a good place to get good food for a good price. 

Try out their lunch specials!

Honestly, I've never seen a cafe as beautiful as this one. The interior is stunning. I love how the sunlight comes in through the big windows and the white interior. A downside to the set-up would be the teeny tables. And it was a bit cluttered because it was so busy! (People tend to leave their trash and dirty dishes behind too, but that's more so something the customers should work on!) Fortunately though, my sisters and I grabbed seats by the long table beside the window. (There's upstairs seating as well!)

The baked good honestly look amazing! And they look fresh too. They are evidently made with care. I was tempted but too full to try any of them. Definitely regret not having tried any though.

The food was more pleasing to the eye than the tastebuds, but still very good! I got the breakfast dish with avocado(the name is slipping me) but it was made with sourdough bread, an avocado spread, radish, greens and poached eggs. I was full off of half the dish! It's worth the price but a bit heavier on the avocado (not complaining though) and it could have used more salt & pepper (but easy fix).

My sisters enjoyed their dishes too. My one sister was especially pleased by the croissant sandwich (though she also said she wished it had more salt & pepper). She gave a thumbs up to the orange juice too, though it wasn't as chilled as she had hoped (no biggie though).

The service was super friendly, nice and efficient for a place as busy as this.

Definitely recommend!!

Got a quiche (5.68$ with student discount). It was a little salty, but hot and filling; it came with a mediocre fruit salad. Unfortunately, I found a hair in the fruit salad, which was kind of sad. I'd go back though -- it's cheap and filling, without being really heavy (as Anna's, my other go-to in the area when I'm hungry, tends to be). Also, it's one of the few places in Kendall that's not super swamped at lunch time -- there were a few people around, but there wasn't much of a line.

Love the vegan cheese option. The prices can't be beat either. Basically build your own pizza for $9. They don't buckle and siSign up for the app too. Great location too. 

Update: the staff at this location is super nice. Thanks for smiling while you're working guys. You make the good food even better!
Great falafel & shawarma! Very cute & tiny hole in wall with really friendly service. Its super cheap and very tasty! We will be back!

Came here for coffee after Latin brunch at the East Coast Grill and was very pleasantly surprised to see Mexican hot chocolate (chocolate, cinnamon and chipotle) on the menu and ordered a large one.  Very rich and chocolaty, but not sweet.  You also got a subtle hint of the pepper at the end.  

I wish this was on the menu year round instead of just for the winter.

So glad to have this place back! If you liked the old Flat Tops, you'll love what they've done with the renovation. It still has the same vibe and the same great people with a ton of welcome improvements: 

1) New equipment. There are fewer tables, but the revamped layout makes playing on them much more comfortable.
2) An awesome new menu. In my opinion, one of the best improvements. The menu has been given a total overhaul: more options, more variety, and everything I've tried so far has been delicious.
3) A new layout and design facelift. The bar area is more spacious, and is perfectly positioned for spectators.
4) A much improved FLATIO. One of my favorite things about Flat Tops is the patio in the summer time. It's always been the perfect place to have a beer outside (whether you're waiting for a game or not), but it's even better now with the planters and the new patio furniture.

There really is no place in the city quite like it. Go check it out!

I tried their fried mussels. The first few were good because I was soo hungry, but overall it was way too greasy and I felt sick afterwards. 

Their drinks are good. I had the lemondrop, mojito, and this customized spicy grapefruit drink that the bartender made for me - all pretty good. However the prices for customized cocktails are pretty steep, I was charged $20+ for mine. 

I'm not a fan of the atmosphere here. It's usually pretty empty and there's an awkward spacing between groups of couches and standing areas. There's also a separate seating area partitioned by a half-wall, which just makes it harder to mingle and navigate in the venue. 

If you are looking for a dive bar or something similar, this is not the place to go. It's more of a quiet, after dinner drinks place. For some reason I always get a slightly pretentious vibe from this place when I go, but that might just be me.

I've only gone once so far and I will definitely go back and probably update this review...

I totally agree that having darts (and not with tables in front of the boards... cough cough, Spirit) is a huge plus and needs to be copied by more bars.

So I went on a Wednesday in February at 11pm for the free pool. It was free pool, so I can't really complain. Very nice tables, too. And there aren't many pool hall options on this side of the river.

But I do have a couple nit picky points... first, they were playing a Rage Against the Machine album and it started to grate after a while. I like them, but not easy to listen to song after song. Also, I thought the tables were a little close together. Seemed closer than Boston Billiards or Big City in Brighton, for example. I was bumping into or waiting for my table neighbors a lot. But anyway, these are minor issues.

It's a cool space and I definitely plan to go back on a more crowded night. I also plan to continue taking advantage of their Wednesday night free pool special.

EDIT:

I've been back a few more times for Wednesday free pool and my friend and I are starting to make it a weekly thing. The service is very good and one of the servers remembered my friend's drink choice after he had only been once before, two weeks earlier. Very impressive!

On a Saturday night recently, it was surprisingly not packed. All the tables were taken, but the bar area was still pretty sparse, though it was continuing to get more crowded when we left at 11pm. There was a pretty young crowd there, as well. I think it was due to no bouncer at the door and the fact that they serve pitchers, if you know what I'm getting at.

My experience at Park was fantastic!  My boyfriend and I were equally impressed by the food, the drinks, and the service.  The atmosphere is casual and cozy.

The appetizers are quite large.  I ordered the crispy mussels.  They were delicious, but definitely enough for 3-4 people to share as an appetizer.  The Caesar salad is unique because it has a "toad in the hole" instead of traditional croutons.  

For entrees we had the seared scallops and the bar steak.  I've been thinking about those scallops and the lovely veggies they came with all week.  

We will definitely be back to PARK and you should try it too.

Still in love with the place, but a bit let down as of late. I used to prize this place for its cold-brewed iced coffee. I can only drink iced-coffee, and cold-brewed is so much better. I always order the same thing (oh, how I long to be a regular) and it used to be perfect. Iced coffee with soy, and I don't add anything in. The sweetened soy milk had just enough sugar that the coffee was fine, especially since the cold-brewing makes a less acidic flavor that doesn't need as much sweetener to cut the sharp taste. 

The past few times I've ordered my usual drink though, its tasted a bit different. I wouldn't say its no longer cold-brewed, but it doesn't taste like it anymore. I get just a tiny bit of an upset stomach drinking the recent blends, similar to when you drink a large coffee on an empty stomach - not a stomach ache but a slight bit upset. Maybe they switched the base for this coffee, maybe it just sits out longer these days since winter doesn't bring much high demand for iced coffee, I don't know. I'll keep coming, but will start branching into more palatable beverages if the coffee doesn't go back to being the star attraction it once was. 

I still love you, 1369!

This is a tiny little restaurant in porter square....very tiny, like 2 tables and a counter for 2 people tiny.  They have basic japanese food, like curry, donburis, and the basics.  No sushi or anything fancy or any noodle dishes.  I went for the pork katsu curry, which was decent and tasted pretty good as a japanese curry, however the curry wasn't spicy at all.  They give pretty good sized portions, a whole katsu and curry and you can get free rice refills.  I also got the green tea milk shake, which was delicious and seems to be the thing to get at this place.

If you want to eat here, go early as the line forms fast and slow considering there are only 2 tables to sit down.  Take out might be a good option as well!  Service was decent, slow with water, but ok.

This place has EXCELLENT food and mediocre staff.  Their Sake 2 Me roll is to die for.  Warm, fresh cooked salmon wrapped with cream cheese and avocado in a roll covered with that delicious eel sauce.  All of their fish is fresh, their pieces are large, their drinks are delicious and you can be sure to be satisfied every time you go.  Thursdays ate $1.00 oysters, and you really can't beat that.  The only downside about this place are the prices.  I'm always shocked by how expensive my bill is.  Happy hour has amazing prices, but I'd rather they just balanced out the prices all around.  I can't get a filling dinner for under $40.00 and that's a ridiculous amount for an average night.

Didn't want to rate 5 stars because then y'all think I am a plant. 
Arrived at FTJ around 3pm,  had a good beer, a great burger, and some wonderful games on the perfectly upkept, and creatively modified bank of pinball machines. Had to leave by 4, but will most definitely return. 
All Hail the Funkatron!

Terrible!  Just left there.

We waited for 15 minutes at the bar an received no service whatsoever.  They seemed busy, but there were plenty of open tables and seats at the bar.  Several hosts, managers, servers and bartenders aknowledged us with eye contact, but no one offered a menu or drink or anything.  Several people around us had to flag someone down for their checks so they could leave.  

It seems like takeout is their primary business.

I came here a few times before because it was convenient. The first time was about 3 years ago and I really enjoyed it. When I went the second time my food was not warm and I think the portions got smaller and it wasn't as satisfying as it once was before

I never had a scone before last Thursday. But now I am constantly thinking about the oatmeal and blackberry scone I had from 1369 the other day. The coffee is good, the staff are friendly. I pop in for a coffee on my way to work a few days a week and I wish I had a little more time to go there and hang out in the evening.

Consistent with the other Unos outside of Chicago. They have a new buy 2+ dishes for $6 each menu, I had the buffalo chicken salad and Uno Classic pizza. I think these are all smaller sizes than the single sizes in the regular menu. I quite liked the buffalo chicken salad but the Uno Classic Pizza just isn't deep enough for a deep dish pizza. Overall I'd be a lot happier if they dropped the price to $4 or $5 a dish with this menu.

The pizza is great and the service is top notch. I ordered a pizza online and went to pick it up but I had messed up my order so it didn't get through. They prioritized my order and made sure I got it asap even though I was the one who messed up. The manager is on top of his game even when things are crazy busy. Love this place.

I get upset every time I walk past this place but I'm not sure I can put it into words.  Maybe its how bland the food is, maybe it's that the staff is trying to be nice, but actually isn't, maybe it's that they put stupid shows on tv while you're trying to enjoy a meal, maybe it's the HORENDOUS MUSIC that they pump onto the sidewalk.  I think it's mainly the bad music.

The ginger exchange is the epitome of mediocrity.  Parents in the neighborhood for dinner?  Don't want to splurge on the East Coast Grill, and not sure you want to slum it at Bukowski's?  Ginger exchange is a perfect safe bet.  

Have an awesome time.

Add my review to the list of Yelpers who love the Middle East for shows. 

The Upstairs is cool because it seems to showcase more local acts. The Downstairs manages to be both spacious and cozy, and unless you are standing behind 10 rows of people over 6' tall (ahem, Irving Plaza),  you'll be able to see. I like the bar area, where you can stand off to the side with an elevated view of the stage and crowd.  They also serve Magners. Cider and good music? Sign me up.

Don't just think of it as a sushi bar!   This is a great choice for a healthy, vegetarian or vegan lunch in Inman Square--um, practically the only choice.  Many of the appetizers and noodle dishes are offered with tofu and vegetables instead of meat.   At lunch at the bar today,  I ordered the Singapore Rice Noodles with tofu and vegetables.  A few minutes later, the waitress came back to ask me if I wanted eggs included in the dish, which is how the chef usually makes it (I hadn't noticed this on the menu;  verdict:  no eggs!).   Note that this happened *without* me having previously thrown a fit about being vegan and discussing at length what a vegan is (" You don't eat meat? Well, how about some nice chicken!").  I can't remember this happening before in Cambridge.   A nice surprise...

The bar was pleasant, the Singapore Rice Noodles made a filling, tasty lunch and I'm looking forward to eating my way through the menu at the Ginger Exchange in the future.   My only criticism is that the wines by the glass were few and pricey ($9 for Clos du Val Chardonnay!) and the pour was scant.  Perhaps this was not always the case, because somebody (presumably in management) had set up a sample wine glass in the back corner of the bar with the "correct" pour, which the poor (new?) bartender had to use to measure her own pours against.

Went to the downstairs for a hip-hop show. It was cramped, but luckily I wasn't a person who was in the crowd. The bathrooms need serious updating and cleaning. It's typical for a nightclub, but I wish it were cleaner for health's sake. They had a good tap selection for a club. The staff was nice (well, they didn't really acknowledge anybody- good in my book!). There was a ton of incense burning which covered up any of the stink other people are talking about. It actually smelled ok.

It's hard to find a Japanese curry joint with vegetarian options! I had been searching for one in NY to no avail, and for once Boston has delivered where the Big Apple falls short.

I didn't actually end up ordering the tofu curry on my last visit, as I was in the mood for something milder. The fried tofu don was quite good - the texture of the tofu was the right balance of crispy and tender on the inside. The yaki sauce was just okay.

On a future visit, I look forward to sampling the many other vegetarian options on the menu.

I love Blaze! I work right up the street, and I go on pizza runs nearly every shift for my lunch break, and almost every time my coworkers ask me to pick them up something as well. It's a hit over here because not only are the pizzas good quality, but inexpensive as well. It's about $8 for a pizza with whatever your heart desires on it, and I'm vegan so wasn't I pleased to see that the Daiya mozzarella they use isn't extra! What is extra: high rise crust and gluten free crust are another $2. But it's worth it, since you can load it with unlimited toppings for the same base price. Another plus is that they have outdoor seating, rendering this a dog-friendly restaurant. My Boston terrier loves coming here and I can't resist her little eyes begging for a piece of my crust. The atmosphere is friendly and efficient, so even when they are a little busy they keep the line moving at an impressive pace while still having time to greet me by name. (Does them remembering me mean I have a pizza addiction?) Make sure to get their app, they have a rewards system which I call "basically a virtual punch card" because with every pizza you order via the app, you get a flame and with ten flames you get your next pizza free! And when you download the app, they send a code for a free fountain drink as well. The only suggestion I would like to make is the addition of a vegan protein, such as grilled tofu, tempeh, or maybe some veggie beef crumbles. I know a lot of us plant eaters would be grateful. :-)

UPDATE: They added tofu! It's reallyyyy good. I'm sure my fellow vegans will be as excited as I am to see the new addition. (It's next to the meat, though, so I suggested in person that they put a cover over it so none of the meat accidentally falls in.)

An amazing little find in Central square. Fresh everything, can be served as a sandwich or plate. Very courteous personnel. I had the falafel sandwich which was quite a large portion and very filling. They have very limited setting though (only 2 tables with 2 chairs each) so I see how this place can become crowded quickly. Regardless, I can't wait to go back!

I've only eaten at the downstairs bar so this review is for that.  It's cash-only, which you don't find out until you read the menu.  It's small and cozy, with a random assortment of decorations that oddly seems to work well (wooden beams, Christmas lights, rock covered walls, and pub-like tables).  They have Stella, Bellhaven, and Peroni on tap, among others.  The house red wine is fairly decent.

The four stars come from the tater tots.  And it's only four because I have to average with other foods.  These delicious balls of potatoey goodness are creamy on the inside and lightly crispy on the outside...they are what I imagine arancini should taste like.  I want to buy a ton of potatoes and experiment to figure out how they managed to achieve the perfect balance of creamy and lightly crispy along with all that flavor.  They are going in my "last meal on earth."

I have also made a tater tot song (to the tune of Planet Unicorn.  I will sing this song in the shower tonight.

A Yelper wished for a planet full of tater tots
Planet Tater Tot 
Tater Tot Planet
Give it up for truffles
ooOOoo balls of starch
and ketchup
Planet Tater Tot heyyyyy

Also good:
-Cellar burger: buttery bun, good meat, delightful rosemary-truffle fries
-Smoky chicken soup: makes me want to start making chicken stock from scratch.
-chicken flatbread
Good service. Fun staff. Amazing bartenders and tasty drinks. The drinks are on the sweeter side but I personally like that. Plus the new thin crust Chicago style pizzas are absolutely heavenly.

Sometimes you need that restaurant that simply is just on the border of "ok", yet open up later than most restaurants in the area so you still go.  With that said, it is not the most consistent place for food.  Depending on what you order, it can be ok or not.  I have eaten here a handful of times, and surprisingly the safest thing to get here is their sushi.  Keep it safe and you should be ok.  Whatever you do, do not get Korean food at this place.  If you've had the real thing before, you will be utterly disappointed.  Let the dice roll and good luck!

Stopped in when I was working in the neighborhood. I wish I had checked the Yelp! reviews before eating here. As I was pressed for time, I opted for take out. The hostess was great, she took my order and noted on the order my food allergies and my request to add carrots. When my order arrived I double checked it and not only had the kitchen added an item I was allergic to but they also omitted the carrots. The hostess apologized went back to the kitchen and told them. I heard her explicitly say to them that I was allergic to corn. They remade the dish and when I double checked it, there were no carrots. She offered to have them remake it again but after waiting for 25 mins, I just wanted to leave. The food was mediocre at best and the portions were small. Unless you are in a bind, I'd skip this place.

If all you want is to grab some pitchers of beer with friends and play some pool, this is your place. If you want food, I'd go somewhere else first and then come here. We ordered a bunch of appetizers (all standard, greasy bar fare) and it was all okay (not great, but standard). The chicken tenders were kind of dry, but that's no big deal. The only thing that really lost them a star here is my burger was burnt (as was everyone else's). And by burnt I mean pretty much inedible even after our pitchers of beer were gone!

That being said, if I ignored the burger fiasco and rated it on atmosphere and prices alone it would get 3 stars. Definitely a lot of pool tables, a decently fun atmosphere, and reasonably priced pitchers of good beers. I'll be back - just not for food!

This place is one of my favorite restaurants in Cambridge.  Sure the service could be better but what do you want for these prices?  The key to enjoying your meal is checking the schwarma before you buy.  I always look to see which looks the least dry (lots of time both are fine) and then choose lamb or chicken accordingly.  I'd disagree with the poster who recommended going with the chicken kabob....these guys hate to make it (it takes a long time and slows everything down) and it usually comes out dry.  I love the humous, baba ganoush, tabouli...pretty much everything.  This place just feels so much more authentic than the falafel king at downtown crossing

Rebeccas!  I go here for lunch about twice a week and love their sandwiches.  I sometimes get my turkey grilled like a panini or do the amazing marble rye turkey.  Not only do they have sandwiches but they also do salads, pastas, muffins, cookies and much more.  It is a bit pricey charging 7 bucks per sandwich but the pickle that comes with it is worth it!  I am a big pickle lover and will usually ask for one on the side.  
The people that work there are sooo friendly and nice.  They now know me so greet me everytime I go in.  
I did take one star off because it is busy in there over the lunch time and the wait for a sandwich is sometimes ridiculous.  However, one of the women working gave me a free cookie because it took about 10 minutes for my sandwich.

I used to come here a LOT when I worked right across the street.  My favorite Mexican fast food place because i'm not a burrito lover, and Boca Grande has great non-burrito choices that aren't just boring old rice plates.  

I LOVE the 1/2 chicken meal.  With loads of salsa and veggies it's actually a healthy meal, and you can eat the little tortillas if you want to make it carbtastic.

Excellent enchiladas.  I like the red sauce for flavor, but it's not spicy so I have them squirt tons of hot sauce on top.  Mm mm good.  Plus, their guac is good.

I remember their tamales being decent, too.  

The people working there were really nice and efficient but the lady at the register glared at me.  I won't blame the restaurant for that, she seemed to have a lot of 'tude.

Okay, we've been a little slow here at Boston Burger Blog in 2010, yes there was The Burger Bash, which was amazing, but we haven't done a review since December. Meanwhile, my burger list grows every day, and it's not always easy to coordinate a burger time for two people (yes, Nick is real) even if I'd love to go once a week. In any case, we headed to The Cellar on Saturday night, not our first go around as we were there for the Burger Crawl, but that day was all about gluttony, not exactly a day spend on enjoying, tasting or analyzing the burger. One thing I did have in mind was the bun, as is always the case the bun is a major factor in my burger experience, and the wrong choice can really mess things up. I love brioche on its own, but rarely does it work in a burger for me, because there's just too much of it and it can be too rich in taste. The Cellar does its best attempt to make the most pretentious burger it can, using brioche, arugula, grass-fed beef, aioli, and truffle, in an otherwise non-pretentious atmosphere. If you haven't been, when they say The Cellar, they really mean it, but it's a great space regardless, perfect to meet friends for a drink or two and enjoy classic pub food done with a twist.
   I do have to say, the truffle-rosemary fries are some of the best we've had, maybe the best, perfectly crispy, and infused with the wonderful aroma of rosemary and truffle. How they stay pale blond and still deliver the crunch is anyone's guess. The burger is very good, I have to admit, but that damn brioche just doesn't make any sense, would love an explanation from someone who prefers it because it doesn't work for me. The beef delivers, full of flavor but is not quite juicy enough. Not dry, just not juicy, although it is cooked just right.  The Vermont cheddar, while melted for once, gets lost, as does the aioli, because the bun just soaks everything right up, the sandwich doesn't come into sync, it's either a big bite of all bread or all beef, but rarely everything together. And it's too bad, despite everything, individually, there is a ton of flavor, just not as a complete package. I guess part of me almost wants that classic, ketchup and pickle flavor, I don't know that I prefer aioli and arugula on a burger, but you never know, I'm open to change.  The fries here really do steal the show, and for $10, you get a good burger, upper echelon for sure and I would certainly recommend this place, just not quite good enough for elite status.

I've reached meh level with Rebecca's.  Yes, the food is good and fresh, and filling on top of all of that.  But it doesn't really excite me.   Rather, it fills me up, which in theory doesn't sound too bad.  However, I'd like to be occasionally excited by food that is actually supposed to be exciting.  

Take today for example:  I get an island jerk chicken fajita.  It's okay, and unlike places like Chipotle, it's not going to fall apart on me immediately upon its opening.  But, while it's tolerable, it doens't have that ooph one would expect ffrom jerk chicken, or peppers for  that matter.  At least with a place like, say, Chipotle, you're getting a taste.   At Rebecca's, it's food, and it's edible, but it doesn't have the kick.

The service is good though, but overall, the average food makes for an average review.  Of course, in a place with such a dearth of food places as Kendall/MIT, it does well.

This place is supposedly renowned for its Chicago style pizza.  The pizza was very mediocre.  The crust was dry and had the consistency of a cracker on the bottom.  Honestly, it tasted like one of those pizzas you buy from the frozen section of the supermarket.
I've had a couple burritos here, and they haven't been very good. They didn't have much flavor. Pretty uninteresting.

Excellent. It's very busy but the service was still relatively fast. I got the turkey sandwich and it was superb. Excellent amount of turkey and bacon and it was warm and crisp. The apple butter was a very nice touch.

Horrendous in every way.

Service was non-existent. Food was by far the worst middle eastern food I'd ever had in my life--eggplant hummus that looked that dog vomit, slapped on a plate like dog vomit, and tasted akin to sour cream of wheat. 

Pita bread was cold and came in a plastic baggy. I examined mine for mold it was so bad.

Burger tasted like cafeteria food; beef shwarma sandwich was tough and lacking.

For some reason I used to hate this place, but then the last few times I have been there I have had a pretty good experience. I like the burritos a lot, and their prices are really good and cheap. Their tacos are good to, but sometimes I get mostly rice so then it's a bummer. When they are right on they are really good, but when they are off, they are not good. Lack of consistency brings it down a star.
I also really like corn in my burritos, and they don't offer it, so it comes down another star.
Because i drive everywhere and can't find parking it gets another star taken off. 
If none of the above things bother you like they bother me then take everyone else's glowing review, as I sad, when they are good, they are really good!

So I have been to quite a few coffee shops around town, it doesn't make me an expert, but you don't have to be one to know when you drink an amazing latte, or are treated like a human being, and not a money dispenser. 

Not only are the latte's and baked goods the best in town, the service is truly genuine, and incredible. The cashiers always are patient, and provide service with NO PRETENTIOUS bullshit. Sandwiches are made quickly and to amazing quality, and still are an affordable price for about anyone who has the money to be spending at a coffee shop. On top of all of this, you will see the owner, Simon, working often, he made my latte the last time I went, that's just neat....

I work in the area and it is one of the more reasonably prices options for lunch.  I've always enjoyed my meal here and never wished i went somewhere else.  Great sandwiches, love the rotation of specials and you can check online before you go.

Never had a bad anything here. Discount to MIT employees and free lunch after 4 purchases. They also use level up so geeks unite and leave the wallet at home. 

Grilled veggie wrap with hummus is now my favorite.

This hidden place is located in Harvard Square, and I was captivated the moment I walked in. The atmosphere is quite dark and cozy, and the couches provide an easy place to have nice conversation. I ordered the Aloe Love cocktail, duck confit poutine, and the toast sampler. Let's start with the drink. It was strong! The cinnamon syrup really came through, and some of the blackberry and aloe flavor that I had been looking forward to was lost, but overall a great drink. The poutine could have used more cheese. In fact, it was missing the signature cheese curds that I was used to (being from the Midwest and all). But the duck was so tender and the fries were a mix of sweet potato and yellow. So good. Please order the poutine, I'm begging you. The toast sampler was the last dish I ordered. There are three kinds, a pear walnut spicy honey and ricotta combo, prosciutto fig onion jam and brie combo, and a cranberry butternut squash gorgonzola combo. My favorite was the pear one! I loved the crunch of walnut with the sweetness of pear and kick from the honey. The gorgonzola one made the bread quite wet, which I did not enjoy. The fig onion jam was delicious and sweet which paired well with the saltiness of the meat. Overall, awesome experience and would come back again.

I just tried this place for the first time a few weeks ago.  A friend of mine's friend from home is a chef there.  It was FANTASTIC.  I had a green salad to start that was perfectly dressed, and for a main course I had pan seared halibut that came with a vegetable medely that was to die for.  It was so flavorful and the portion was just right.  I hate when places give a heaping portion and I sit there picking at it when I'm long past feeling full.  

The service was good, and the space in there is not the best, but it isn't awful either.  The food upstages the decor.  It does get super crowded, so get there early.  I got there around 6 and got right in, and about a half hour later it was packed.  The waitstaff is also lovely, and they didn't rush us even though we stayed quite a while after our meal was finished to drink the rest of our never ending bottle of wine.  

The pricing is fair for the quality of the food, but it is by no means cheap.   The entrees start pretty much in the low $20's and go up.
