They have it set up somewhat like an italian resturant, with small plate options (none of which I tried but many of them sounded good) that are all $10+.  The wine selection is good, with a variety of price ranges.  Everything on the menu sounded very creative and well thought out.  This place avoids the common trap of trendy upscale dining, places where the food sounds and even looks interesting but you're not sure if you want to eat it. At the Cellar the food is simple and delicious, but just has a different twist.  

Definatley go here if you get a chance.  It's rather loud so I wouldn't go for a first date or a long lost relative reunion, but for all other small group occasions this place is a great option.

I've only been here twice; once with my close friend and another time with my boyfriend. And both times were great. The food was consistently delicious. I ordered the same both times which was the hamburger rice plate (don't remember the actually name of it), it's one of their signature plates. It is to try first time you go and to get everytime you go. It is a must try. The service is also good.  

The only thing that I would have to pick at is how small the place is. It is located inside the Porter Square food court mall. So they share space with other competitions, thus, they only have about three tables (really small). So expect a wait when you go.  I would recommend going when it's not rush hour. And I would also recommend going with a small group (2-4 people). Cause if you go with more than 4, you probably won't get in or get hate stares the whole time you are there for hogging the whole restaurant to yourselves. Just saying.
Rebecca's Cafe is closed in Kendall Sq. but Rebecca's still offers catering delivery to MIT, Harvard, Kendall Sq and Cambridge area

i saw a band at middle east last friday - they did some music for my friend's short film, so we went to check it out.  it was fun but i dont really get the set-up... its like part restaurant, part club, and then other parts that are next door.  anyway, its a good spot to see music.

No, this isn't the best coffee in the world, let's put that out there right now. But this is nonetheless one of the best places in the world.

Are you a bunch of women in your mid-30s getting together each weekend to laugh it up? Are you a crew of mid-30s dudes telling each other, "Yeah, Sonic Youth was great, but Jimi was the *essence* of cool, man." ? Are you a (likely crazy) old man looking for a barista to talk to? Are you an old couple who just wants to read the paper, or a young couple who just wants to cuddle and sip lattes? Are you a lowly grad student who needs to work on their dissertation? Are you a middle-aged bearded man in a raccoon hat who needs to cut up some cardboard, and a coffee table would be a perfect workbench?

If you're any of these people or anyone in between, 1369 is for you. This coffeeshop is a community cornerstone in one of the corners of Inman Square, with all walks of life walking in and out. The music is always great and extremely eclectic: instrumental hip-hop, old-time country, wanna-dance electronica, smokin'-a-blunt ROCK. The baristas are super friendly: always a smile and a genuine "How's it goin'?"Varied food/drinks: great pastries (omg those lemon bars), good cafe au laits, plenty of teas, killer breakfast sandwiches. And those chalkboard drawings on the outside sign are always on point.

A quirky place wouldn't be without some quirky tips:

(1) Wifi is free, and there's a password you have to enter that's always "community" plus three random letters each day; there's a sign at the front showing the letters.

(2) Restrooms are in the back, but if you go back there you see two doors, each with a sign of a random tool-like objects. All the time I see people going back there and staring at the signs, wondering if that screw is supposed to mean male genitalia. There are keys attached to said random tool-like objects by the counter.

I came here for a night out and had a blast! The drinks were good and the food was better (I'm a foodie, what can I say). It was a typical bar scene - the bar food was a great addition to the environment; which is what really took the cake. Being surrounded by pool tables and an arcade set up made the typical night out drinking a bit more diversified. The staff wasn't intrusive but was always there to clean up and make sure we were doing well. I'll definitely be back here

My first time to Park. Awesome food and Awesome drinks. One of the best bartenders in Boston there. Will Tomlinson. The bar manager Chris was a great guy and the GM Micky wanted to make sure everything was perfect. Check it out you won't be disappointed. Had the Crispy Duck leg and lentils. (love that dish) then onto the Scallops. Can't wait to go back and Park myself there again.

Cheers. Bill

Dear Chutney's, 

While the concept is good and the price is right, and the food is sometimes delicious, customer service and consistency are a problem. 

Burritos are often served cold or they run out of ingredients during PEAK lunch hour and make no effort to replace them. Step up your game, Chutneys. Regina is watching.

Thanks, Regina

I'm sticking to my guns here and giving Moody's 2 stars.  I wanted to move up to 3 or even 4 after finishing my Chicken Shawarma roll-up with Falafel 'cause it was so good, but I'm forcing myself to go with my initial instinct.  Why?

The service is just awful.  Every time I go there, I wait an inordinate amount of time for my food.  That is, when they remember what I ordered.  Or that I ordered anything at all.  Tonight, I went with my roommate.  They took my order, and left him standing there in front of the register, money in hand, for at least 5 minutes.  Then they take his order, and we sort of fade back into the crowd to wait.  He got his roll-up and finished it well before the guy making the sandwiches looked at me confusedly and asked "did you order something?"

I don't know how one could organize a restaurant more poorly.  Here are some easy suggestions for improvement in case anyone from Moody's cares about their poor service, though from how pissy they seem with the customers, I doubt they do.  How about a system in which orders are assigned numbers, which are then called out when the order is ready?  One time I got a block away from Moody's when I was chased down by an employee to be told that they had given me the wrong roll-up.  For god's sake, how does that happen?!?  Second, how about some sort of line system, so people aren't standing around going "did you order?  did you order?"

Etc, etc.  But man that's such a good roll-up.

Maybe the best value lunch option in Kendall.

I have become a semi regular for lunch at Rebecca's. The food is generally good. The wraps and sandwiches use fresh ingredients and are tasty. The plate lunches are not bad. The plate lunches are one of the best ways to get a balanced full meal for lunch in the area. They change daily, so they are a good way to get some variety in your diet. I wish they didn't use parboiled rice, and the chicken enchiladas were not really enchiladas, but it is alright. 

They are also efficient and fast, so you will be in and out in less than 10 minutes. Finally, the food at Rebecca's is pretty affordable. The base price is low, but they also offer a 15% MIT employee and student discount and also have a buy 4 get one free card. It basically comes up to around $5 for a pretty good lunch. 

Not bad at all!

Blaze is a blessing to those with gluten (and other) allergies. After I discovered my gluten allergy a few years ago, I became deprived of pizza and bread in general. It was pretty emotional. Enter blaze- the ONLY place I get my pizza fix. It's a huge plus that their gluten free crust is also soy free and egg free (very rare combo). Oath craft and Otto's have egg and/or soy in their gluten free crusts.

I am leaving my review for this Blaze specifically because they have the best service in comparison to the other branches and usually make sure to follow appropriate protocol when I mention my allergy, which is greatly appreciated. They are also keen to make my pizza extra crispy.

I wish I liked Simon's better, since it's so convenient to where I live.

I wish they'd add some better food selections.  Right now they have nothing but dense pastries that aren't terribly enjoyable.  They also ought to switch up their layout - the tables are all squished against the wall in line with the bar, making it difficult to have a private conversation.

They do make a mean rooibos latte - but sometimes the staff makes mistakes.  I asked for an iced green tea latte, which is on the menu, and a staff member said he couldn't make it.  I came back on a later date, and a man who looked like an owner apologized and asked which employee it was so he could make sure it didn't happen again.  I applaud any indie coffee shop that makes that much of an effort to fix problems for customers.  For now, I'm just ordering basic things off the drink menu.

Don't get me wrong, the staff and location are top notch, however, the food is just mediocre pub food.  I had the deep dish pizza and the crust is really good, but it lacked the flavour of the sauce.  I made the mistake of getting hamburg on the pizza as a topping.. As a Canadian, we eat our hamburg completely cooked and unfortunately, the hamburg was quite pink.

The grilled cheese sticks were also just mediocre.

I love the soy latte (the best), herbal chai latte, and I love the artsy quirky and friendly staff there. and Simon is pretty awesome himself!

I don't deem myself to be a regular at many places, but I certainly do here :)

Yay

Having only recently started working in the Kendall Sq. area again after a 12y absence, I'm overjoyed to be able to return to Izzy's, and even happier to see that they haven't changed.
Yummy, large portions, reasonable price. Many others have discussed the details so I won't reiterate. I've been 2x in a week and expect to be returning about weekly, when the weather's reasonable for the 10m walk from work.

Hot. Iced.  Tea or coffee.   It's all good
The staff is oh so friendly.  
It's great to have them so close to where I work

GREAT MENU, lots of choices, but the food is so tremendously disappointing!

I went once a few years ago and didn't remember it at all -- after going back yesterday, I know why I forgot it!  The food at Boca Grande is almost bizarrely bland. My husband ordered a burrito grande w/ Chicken Colorado ("it's spicy") and "hot sauce" (among other things) and even said HIS was bland.  I don't like spicy food, but I thought mine was really bland too.  Despite having (extra) salsa, lemon grilled chicken, cheese, etc -- it still tasted like I was eating a giant tortilla full of unseasoned rice. Bummer. We also had guac and chips and even the guac was overly bland. I'm amazed by the reviews here.  I think 2 stars is generous.

I wouldn't go back unless i was in pretty dire straits.

Yawn...another $8 Kendall Square sandwich.

Don't get me wrong, my grilled chicken with bacon (+$1.00) and honey mustard on focaccia (+$.50 - not posted on sign) was pretty delicious, but $8?! C'mon, it is, after all, just a sandwich.

Four stars for taste, two for value.

I liked this place a lot. Clean, friendly people (both working there and hanging out there), wonderful location. I considered docking it two stars because of the paid wifi, but that's not really fair (though I ain't paying $3.95 for an HOUR of wifi). The pots of tea are cheap ($2.05) and mine was delicious. This would be a fairly regular spot for me if I lived in the neighborhood.

OK, so it's Uno's. It's a chain, complete with the laminated, professionally printed chainey menu and the chainey registered trademark drinks and dishes. But for what it is, Uno's gets 4 stars from me. The food is decent to quite delicious and decently priced for what you get, which is a full meal and usually leftovers for the next day's lunch. They've got the pizza recipes down- both deep dish and thin crust- and as for everything else, well, why would you order anything else? It's a pizzeria. Just get something cheesy with marinara and you'll be all set.

A few tips: I've found that the service is best downstairs (maybe that just has to do with the times I've been? who knows), and there are comfy chairs near the bar. Yay comfy chairs after a long day at work and getting pulled over on the way home for speeding.....but that's another story. The service has been great and prompt every time I've been, and you can't beat the convenient location, right on the redline and off the #1 bus. Want gourmet pizza? Want a classy vibe and a suave lounge? Go to Cambridge 1, which I also really like. Want something cozy and quick, with tried and true recipes and friendly service? Maybe you should consider Uno's.

wow, was gonna try Fugakyu but found out about this place.  Their curry rice is the truth.  this is how authentic japanese cuisine should taste like.  Fugakyu costs 2x as much and cant be as good.  ill still try them later so i can review them 2.  but as far as authentic, economically priced japanese food, can't go wrong with Mami.
Really good coffee. Id give them 5 stars if they had some sort of non gluten pastry.
Amazing breakfast sandwich- Avocado with poach egg, arugula, radishes on sourdough bread. The pastries were very fresh- almond and pear muffin was awesome! Great view from second floor!

My love for this place started a few months after I moved here from Puerto Rico. I was extremely homesick, and needed something familiar. Well as soon as I walk in, it hits me.The smells, the sounds, the decor...It akk screams small Puerto Rico town cafeteria, I loved it. As soon as the owner learns im Puertorican, he pulls out the bottle of coquito and serves me a drink.

Yup. I love this place. The food is amazing, very genuine, and the service is great. I would really recommend this place.

I was in Harvard Square the other day looking for a quick lunch and this place hit the spot! I decided to get one of the wraps. The preparation reminded me of a fast food burrito place, where the wrap is made for you in front of your eyes as you choose what to put in it. I got the paratha bread with tandoori chicken, rice, lettuce, tomato, and the tamarind sauce. It was very tasty and I could tell the ingredients were very fresh. To top it all off, the employees behind the counter were very nice and polite. The only problem was that the menu was a little hard to navigate so I was not quite sure what the thing I had ordered was going to be like until I saw it.  I will definitely be going back!

I went only to support a friend of mines' band. They did awesome! 

I also met the owners, Nabile and Joseph! They are great guys. Even though the kitchen was closed they had the chef prepare  a maza plate.
I Will definite return! and my next review won't be at 330a!

Ah, Simon's! The first few times I came here - I LOVED it. They have great coffee, yum Hot Chocolate and loads of teas and rooibos to choose from. Their scones are the best in Cambridge (yep, better than petsi's and biscuit's) and their sandwiches and soups are always tasty. All positives, BUT.....

The staff are unfriendly and sometimes even rude. They barely look at you when taking your order, and you can forget about getting a smile. You better know exactly what you want when you get to the counter, because the last time I was deciding (there was no line behind me), I heard a long sigh from the girl behind the counter. All of the staff act like they're too cool to work there or something - full of notions, and I don't get it. Such a pity, because with such great food and drink, I would be here every day. A few lessons in customer service perhaps?

OMG, the pastries are superb, the coffee is above average, and the bread is true French. I like the vibe, a bit pretentious, but chill enough, and the subway tiles are a clean look. The breakfast sandwich pictured has awesome ingredients and flavor. If you have a sensitive pallet then ask for the bread untoasted because it gets pretty crispy.

While I liked the interior with the pool tables and bar and high seating, the service was just confusing and slow. Or rather: there was no service. The bar tender was the only staff available to talk to really, and putting in our food orders took forever. Either they were short staffed tonight (Memorial day evening), or it is a structural problem. The food was decent. Sweet and normal fries were tasty, pulled pork sandwich as well. The veggie burger was a little bland according to my wife. Not sure we'd come back. Pool tables look tempting though...

Went to the Middle East a couple years ago with friends for a drink. It was just okay. Nothing that impressed me. I recently went back to see a show and grab dinner beforehand. I had the most delicious Lentil Soup, a Falafel Sandwich, and Baklava. No complaints about the food. Talked about it for days afterwards. The show was good; up close and personal with performers because it is a small venue. Bathrooms are dingy and they kind of scare me but the food makes up for it. I returned to The Middle East again for an early dinner when a neighboring establishment was closed. Once again, the food was delicious and cheaper than expected because I was unexpectedly charged the lower lunch prices. Only $3.50 for a large Falafel Sandwich! I probably won't jump at the chance to catch another show at The Middle East but I will return for their generous vegetarian/vegan menu options.

My first experience here was great, but then after eating here 5-6 times, I found them very inconsistent the way they do business here. Things can go wrong so easily when they get too crowded.

One thing I notice is that they do not train their staff correctly. Whenever I ordered the past 2 times, they always messed up my pizza. For instance, at yesterday's order, the girl squeezed and tore spinach as if she was angry at them before the put them on top of my pizza. Why did she do that to my spinach is a mystery to me.

I said "goat cheese" and she put shredded mozzarella on it and then by the time she heard my word "goat cheese", she said "you would have to pay extra if you want both"....duh, I did not want BOTH.

They do not seem to like it when I used reward, coupon or even their digital gift card. They do not seem to know how to address issue when a problem comes up. They gave me a guilt that I was holding up the line. I had a $5 off coupon. The cashier could not get it to work and he said all he could do, was to give me 50% off....that offer was not $5 off!

Mobile order (via their Android app on my Galaxy S8 phone) never worked for me during their crowded hours because they were overloaded with orders. The moment I paid, I got error. If overload was the case, they should prevent me from creating the order at the first place....they wasted so much of my time made selection and customized my order.


I have a $25 gift card recently. I am only half way through it. I don't know if I would get a chance to use the remaining balance as I start to hate coming back here.

Great for dinner!

After walking around Harvard Square, I got super hungry and wanted to treat myself to a nice dinner (I was on a solo trip to Boston for a few days).

I stumbled upon Park's menu and wanted to try their dinner special (I forgot the name of the special, but I got a Shepard's Pie with peas) and it was delicious! I also had a glass of cider, which was crispy & light.

The server was a doll - she checked up on me several times to see if everything was okay.

There's a good-sized bar to your left when you walk in, so it seemed like an excellent place for Happy Hour.
gone down hill since the japanese owner left and sold it to non-japanese owners.  just not the same.  servers are non japanese also.  no longer authentic!  stopped going.

I had an order of 10 sandwiches for an office lunch and they were so helpful and friendly.  I had a few sandwiches that were specially ordered and every sandwich was made right.  When I picked up the food everything was ready on time and the sandwiches that were supossed to be cold were cold and the sandwiches that were supossed to be hot were hot.  Everyone in the office said they were deliscous!  Will go back again if we have another office luncheon.

This is a great place to hang out, grab some beers, and play pool/darts. The bar staff are excellent -- quick to grab your order, get it right the first time and are friendly

I second RyanH's comment on the food -- it's very cheap to eat here. My favorite are the soft pretzels and mustard, buffalo chicken wrap and the hummus plate. We typically sit at the bar, so I can't comment on the service here

I've never had to wait for a pool table and I've been here probably once a month for the past 5 years. It's a great open space that allows you to have a large crowd or a small gathering. The darts are off to the side and there is typically one board open (there's two in the place) so it's nice to get in a game while you're there. 

We've recently moved to Somerville, but I plan on still visiting this place pretty often

I've never had food here, and I've always hung out downstairs, so this will be a departure from the rest of the review.

The Cellar's basement is always empty for me.  Always.  Even when I'm joining a group of people here, I always feel like me and my friends are the only people in the bar.  For some reason, I feel like I'm at a bad hotel bar.  I can't quite put my finger on it.  Service is okay, though.
First time coming here and happy to say I would totally come back! The chicken Katsu curry was delicious and flavorful, especially with the spicy mayonnaise sauce

Coffee is good. Place is nice. Once I asked for an everything bagel w/cream cheese, owner gave me an everything toast [messed up the order, he said]..I took it sat down and saw green creeping on the edges of the toast. Didn't know what to think about that. Today I went in with a blank slate - 3/4's through my egg sandwich I found a long brown hair. I brought it back in and showed them..owner said 'Thanks' with a smile. ..So, that's it for me ..2 strikes.

I'm so confused... I don't know why so many people love the place. It's small, crowded, and yes, I did get the rosemary truffle fries, and they were pretty good I guess, but soooo incredibly oily. I also got a flatbread which was dripping oil all over the place. My friend got grilled cheese, and she said it was really greasy, and I could tell just by looking at it.

It seemed an all right place to go to, but really, not a destination spot. I felt like I was in someone's basement, and I just wanted to get out of there quickly.

Also, cash only, be warned.

After eating here a few more times, I feel much more "meh." about the place.

I've been eating here less frequently, but as a other reviewers have mentioned, the food is simple and the pasta dishes (which I don't believe I had tried when I wrote my original review) are bland.

Just a quick note on the best quick-mex place in the area: everything is fresh and the service is great. Try the Colorado - ask for less rice and black beans (who needs to waste the calories of that stuff). Oh, make sure you get guacamole and hot sauce. If you like real lemonade which does not taste like sugar water, make sure you try that as well... really, ignore any bad review of Boca you may read - those reviewers do not know what they are talking about.
The place looks dirty but in the way that your favorite dive bar looks dirty in a good way.  They make good food at a fair price.

For Boston, this falafel place is pretty good - not the best, but it will do. It gives me that NYC feeling of having a hole-in-the-wall restaurant open late.. but it isn't exactly like the NYC falafel places I'd visit back in NYC.

To date, I've only had the falafel and had a little bit of the lamb shawarma roll-up that my boyfriend ordered. The falafel is not bad, but if you're going to be eating it with friends, make sure not to talk too much or else it will get cold fast. And if you want some spicyness added to it, make sure you ask for it spicy, and not ask for it on the side. The side spicy sauce is thick and pasty, not like the easily pour-able sauces that I've had. Maybe it's just my experience, but I like the more watered down, diluted version of the spicy sauce.

The lamb shawarma that I tasted had A LOT of onions. It made my boyfriend's breath smell wayyyyyy bad. And he knows this. It was pretty tasty for what it is, but then again -- I've had better.

Very bad service at the bar. I arrived and ordered a beer. I was never asked if I wanted food (and I did).  I guess the first bartender didn't care because he was ending his shift. Next bartender was more concerned about setting up his music and setting up glasses and gossiping with waitress. The bartenders need a class on providing customer service. They're lucky I left a dollar tip.

First off Very good server named Paul 

My favorite food is Macaroni and Cheese (Tip for the food: DONT PUT BREAD CRUMBS ON TOP OF IT, THATS GROSS)

However the place was clean, and I order the $12 for 2. Nice and cozy. Very nice hostess and overall nice staff. I would come back again.

Was disappointed by this place.  Was in on a slow Fri night and got the veggie combo plate.  The $7 dish has hummus, baba ganoush, 2 pieces of falafel, taboulleh, and pita.  The taboulleh was very watery.  The pita too thin and cold.  The falafel not crispy, rather mushy actually.  And the hummus had a weird unpleasant tanginess to it.  Not the middle-eastern food I was hoping for and I did expect more out of this place.  Cheap but you get cheap food.

A wonderful reminder how wonderful Japanese food is beyond ramen and sushi.

Since they only seat around 10 people (a couple of small tables and some counter space), the line will get pretty long by 6pm. The line is usually shorter than it's neighbor Sapporo. It is also cash only, so make sure you pick up cash at the BofA ATM in the mall or outside of the mall.

We got the pork cutlet curry, which was *enormous*. The pork is crisp on the outside and moist. And fresh! Since we got seated at the counter, we got to see everybody's order go from the stovetop/fryer straight to the plate. Make sure to add a fried egg to the top. This curry was born to be mixed with some runny yolk.

We also got the Tatsuta Don, which is a bowl full of boneless fried chicken cutlet, their special "mami" sauce, and egg. I'm a sucker for complete meals that come in one bowl. The egg is opened up into the bowl, mixed with the sauce, and steamed in with the rice. 

Overall, a wonderful homey meal for two for under $25 after tip. A ton of people got the green tea milkshake, which I'll be sure to get next time. The only thing to be worried about is the wait, so come early!

I've only been to Park once, so this may be the first of several reviews. 

The good: 
- Ambiance and decor is nice.  Relaxed environment and comfy seats.  
- Drinks: good selection and tasty cocktails.  

The bad: 
- Food was only OK.  I had the slow roasted beef brisket.  It was tasty, but very heavy and too salty for my taste.  My friend had the roasted chicken and also came away thinking that it was just OK.

I'm planning to go back again - it was good enough to give a second shot. It's a new place and probably has a few things to work through before it gets everything right.

Flat Top Johnny's was an overall very odd but not terrible experience.

To start we went on a Friday evening around 8 with a group of 6. It was packed, fair, it's a Friday night and you have pool of course everyone wants to be there. Upon arrival you check in at the front desk to put your name in at a table. Since it's first come first serve for the tables there is no set time estimate for when a table will become available. Also fair, I understand that you can't really kick people off tables once they start playing. Cool we'll get drinks and wait. 

To the bar....

The poor bartenders here. They had one bartender and one barback/food runner behind the bar. The wait for a drink was a while.... I wasn't timing it or anything, but to put it in perspective in the time it took my friend to get a round I had time to go the bathroom and take a phone call.

And while we are on it, let's talk about the bathrooms...

Since the bar is in an office building they share the lobby/bathrooms with the building. This means you exit the bar and walk down the white tile florescent lighting hallway to the bathrooms while the office security guards stare you down. Positive side, the bathrooms are clean, but this is an overall a delightfully sobering experience. 

Anyways, back to the bar.....

The bartenders were hustling and friendly so no fault with them, and we did eventually get our drinks. Also we took a chance on ordering poutine, and despite no discernible way for the food runner to find tables we did get our food. And it was as good as cheese fries can be, but a warning to any poutine snobs it is more of a cheese fry nacho dish than actual poutine.

And in this time we get called for a table, horay! 

Wait no, they called our number and passed over us... even though we were sitting literally right in front of the pool host's area. But it's ok, there is another table, whoo! That was close. But we get to the table and they had no pool cues left... I went to the front desk to ask if there was a secret stash only to be informed they were on back order and I should ask other tables. But it's ok, I asked a kindly table to share and they gave me a cue, victory?

Let the games have commenced! Once at the tables with a cue secured, it was all the fun one can expect with a game of pool. The game area also had a waitress to come around for drink orders which was cool and convenient AND she had a touch screen ordering system that let you split the bill nicely when it was time to pay up, super convenient for a group. 

Although fun was had by all at the end of the night, I would not come running back. And given the fact that the place was packed, I'm pretty sure they really care if I do or not. I would overall recommend this place if you are in your early twenties, don't mind a crowd, and don't want anything fancy. Otherwise, proceed with caution on the weekends, and maybe more reckless abandon on the weekdays.

1369 Coffee has an incredibly good chai tea latte and I have enjoyed each of their scones which are big and filling and not overly sweet. This is not a good location for a party bigger than 2. Even then, good luck finding a spot as it is filled with students whose laptops and papers take up much of the table space. Be ready to get your order to go. 

Major plus: They are open late (~10pm).

I previously posted a 2 star review complaining the service was unnecessarily slow, but it seems steps have been taken to to speed up their service in response to my review. It was much faster service, everyone was friendly, and I was helped quickly. The pizzas are great as always. Thank you for making a big improvement!

Went here with a group of children with disabilities. We were expecting to practice skills such as ordering off a restaurant menu, but got so much more. In addition to being so polite and attentive the waitstaff and managers went above and beyond to also be both patient and accommodating with my group. The manager on duty showed the children how pizzas were made and how they stores things in the walk in freezer. They even got to make their own pizzas!! We all left feeling satisfied with their delicious food, but even more satisfied with their amazing service!
A+ place!!!

The food here was extremely below average and was definitely a disappointment. I could not even eat the kibby that we ordered, it was so strange. The atmosphere and music choice was not conducive to a nice meal. I would not recommend this restaurant.
Coffee is great here and I can put up with snooty staff but $4/hr WiFi! It's insulting, at least it was for me. Never coming back. Sorry Simon but there are lots of alternatives nearby.

i have been here twice since moving to Cambridge about 10 days ago. They have a gluten-free menu which appeals to me as I have Celiac's disease, but both my mother and my boyfriend (non-Celiac) also loved it! The service was nice, the meals were very reasonably priced, and they had a good drinks menu (including PBR for $2/glass). The miso eggplant/salmon was especially good. They also have $2 edemame after 10pm. I've never had the sushi here, so I cannot comment on that, but the other appetizers and entrees have been very good, and the prices are quite reasonable. Would highly recommend.

Eeeeeeeh, it seems to get a little worse every time I go. The music, that was once such a fun eclectic mix, has turned into a running joke that has included such wonderful moments as playing an entire Beastie Boys album straight through. And not even a "good" Beastie Boys album, mind you, but one of their recent abominations.

The food, which I would previously have described as above average (okay, average to above average) bar food, is now merely palatable (at best). The staff is hit or miss; there are some great people there and some lazy and sloppy people. It always seems to cost a little more than it should. And what's worst of all, the once fantastic tap list is getting worse and worse. There's barely any really delicious beer left on the list; I have to resort to buying the same beer multiple times throughout the night.

FTJ's, what happened to you? I will only come back because I have yet to find a better alternative, but there must be one out there.

Working late in the office ordered some sushi from Fuji At Kendall for delivery:
D-E-L-I-C-I-O-U-S!!!!!  This is the way I am describing my experience.
I ordered the Volcano Makomono, Maguro sushi, Tobiko sushi and Hamachi sushi.
Great combination of flavors.
Highly recommend the restaurant especially if you can get away from your desk and eat it in the atmosphere of Kenji At Kendall.
Great authentic Puerto Rican food, will definitely come back with my family. Just stopped in for lunch, food was on the table hot and fresh within 10 minutes.

Hmmm....well let me just say I'm cheap...and after arriving in Boston from Nebraska I went into anaphylaxis at the prices in this town...and as one who used to play pool regularly, and I mean almost every night, I was finding it hard to find places that were affordable and where you could get a table without waiting for hours on end.  I guess I settled on Flat Top Johnny's...the prices are steep (I was used to laying down 50 cents at my local watering-hole)...but when split 2 or 3 ways it's not too bad.  The tables are nice...the atmosphere is pretty laid-back.  Get there early to get a table though.  I can't recall how good the drinks are...I guess they're pretty good...food is regular bar food.  Oh and like one poster already mentioned, they do play good tunes here which is always a plus!

After reading some informed reviews, I was well aware of the history of Middle East. A Cambridge resident for the better part of the past year,  I had yet to venture into this establishment, I suppose, in attempts to fend off any disappointments due to seediness, a youngish crowd or apparent heat stroke that may come with dancing your ass off in a basement with little to no ventilation.

So what finally forced me to put my socio-medical concerns aside? 

Booka Shade.

I had some cool things here and there about this duo and was really interested to see how they throw it down. In a (hyphenated) word: bad-ass.  They got that place so full of energy, everyone dancing and having a good time...no one passing out from the stifling heat. A win-win for everyone.

As others have pointed out, Middle East does a stellar job of featuring bands/artists/acts that go on to become really, really BIG. And always packing the house full of people!

One major concern is the lack of bar staff and said bar staff's lack of being able to serve these huge crowds in any sort of efficient manner. Last night, I had one bartender glance in my direction from halfway across the bar, apparently looking to take my drink order. After motioning to the girl next to me (who had been there way longer than I had) he shouted "I don't have time for that" and moved on to another customer who just rolled up to bar. We had been waiting a while and I made it a point of saying so ti him, and rudely told me that I could, "go to other bar."

So I did and got my drinks in less than a few minutes. I understand that this is not some Michelin-rated fine dining establishment, but there is an order to taking bar customers that should generally be followed. And since the profession is based on tips, being rude never really helps pad your pockets.

Aside from that, which can easily be fixed with proper training or just more staff members, Middle East gets the thumbs up from me, in terms of opportunities to see cool musical acts in a large venue.

I usually don't leave reviews, but I felt like I had to for this particular place. It was such a beautiful place and had a good vibe. It was pretty lowkey, so it's perfect for a night out with close friends. Plus, the bartender, Greg, was great. I can't even explain how excellent the customer service is here. He would check in every now and then to ask us if we needed anything. Overall, it was such a nice experience and we are definitely coming back to this place.
This place used to be great and now it is a burning trash can. Avoid at all costs.

Rebecca's has some of the best sandwiches in the Kendall area for the price.  They have good bread and fresh ingredients.  The staff is friendly and will do a sandwich to order, no problem.  They also have meal specials which look good, but I have not tried any yet.

Owner comes around to ask if you liked the food and makes recommendations for future orders. V. crowded but good for people who want to eat late at night. stools (high chairs) were not easy to sit in but the food was great. Must order the OMG, pyro salmon, spider rolls
Get the iced honey latte. The service wasn't the friendliest and it was kind of small but it's a good alternative if you want to quick bite to eat.

By no means is this the place to go if you want to sit and relax at lunch/dinner. However, going here for a quick lunch is always a treat and  nothing I've had here feels really heavy. The food is energizing, which is just what you need midday to keep you going through the rest of the day! Not a greasy (albeit, delicious) burger that will have you passed out on your desk an hour later. 

One of my favorite things here has to be the chicken momos! They're just so good - come closer to noon to get them as fresh as possible, as they will be cooked early and left in a steamer until ordered. Still delicious any time of day though!

Their Happy hour (which is only food, no drinks) is pretty darn awesome. 
On top of that, I came with a friend who had a groupon and my gosh that groupon was amazing. 
It took $40 dollars off our entire meal. Making the meal only $7 
We ordered a miso soup, Green monster roll, Sweet potato roll, Hoppin' jalapeno roll and a Spicy scallop roll. 
Split between the 3 of us, $47 dollars was a pretty a good deal for sushi. 
The service was really great and I also liked the fact that the restaurant is extremely upbeat and aesthetically pleasing. 

I am definitely coming back for their happy hour rolls ($3.95 each, normally $4.75-$5.50), but other than that I would stick with their specialty rolls for full-priced eating. The happy hour rolls at regular price don't really seem worth it.
This sushi is a great deal, and I'm so happy that Ginger Exchange has filled the sushi void of this part of Cambridge. Look for the spicy trio and the summer rolls. All yummy!!

Add to the list of great brunches in the area! Solid value, with innovative choices all around - their pancakes are unique and kickass.  The fried chicken sandwich, served on two buttermilk biscuits, will fall apart on you, but you won't mind. Their bloody mary is everything it should be, and even the big item - the hanger steak - only runs 22.  It's a chatty, boisterous atmosphere and the daylight is mitigated by the lower-level situation, so headaches will be eased until you can get that sweet nectar of Sunday morning life back up in you.
We got a meat pie and steak. The steak was good but the meat pie and butternut squash pudding were not amazing. The atmosphere was dark, pub style. I recommend sticking to wine or beer here.

Love this place! Cute little place & cheap! I have only gotten the beef patties and fried plantains... so really that's what I'm reviewing. If you are looking for a really spicy beef patty this is the only place I've found.

Went to Chutney's at the Garage in Harvard Square. I got their chicken Masala wrap on some tasty bread with some sweet sauce and mixed veggies. The prices here are cheap and the food, while not outstanding, will give you a quick cheap fix for indian food that you were looking for.

Also they have a good selection of ethnic drinks. I tried the lychee juice, it was delicious. I will return just for that.
It takes 2 minutes to get a burrito.  The burrito you get is huge.  It tastes pretty good, and it doesn't make you want to kill yourself 5 minutes later like fast food or Chinese.

thank you izzy's! finally, a girl who moved from washington heights to cambridge has found a place that has yucca, tostones, and chicharron that are almost as good as the stuff that i'm used to on dyckman street in in manhattan. it's taken me awhile, but it was worth it. the best part is u are only 5 blocks from my apartment.

The Middle East restaurant has actually hit rock bottom and started to dig.  My server (I call her that for lack of a better term... there certainly was no service involved) could not have been more disinterested in my table of three.  My soda was never refilled... she showed up every 20 minutes at most, and when we got the check... guess what?
Preadded tip.  For a table of three.  No wonder she didn't give a rat's ass about us.
Service here sucks. No free refills on soda if your server doesn't come back, and you can't go to the bar if you're seated.  They must just call themselves a restaurant for tax reasons... since they clearly don't care about serving food.

Never again, Middle East.  Your food isn't that great anyway... and if I really want it that badly, I'll go to Zuzu's where they actually treat you like a human, and ask them to sneak it out of your kitchen.

My first attempt at Mexican food in Cambridge = Awful. 

Beans = undercooked and completely without spices, including salt.

Pico de Gallo = all tomato

Al Pastor = chewy and with flavor of a Bolivian Tinku helmet.

"Hot Sauce" (salsa verde) = totally flavorless. 

Queso = a slice of government cheese - I didn't ask for it, but the added it anyway. 

Service = quite bad. The fellow put rice in my burrito then asked if I wanted any...I didn't. I was charged for a Grande, but ordered and ate a regular.  

Stomach = extremely displeased with my decision to eat here. 

Yuck!

Consistently tasty, a local favorite that outshines any national mexican chain. Whether you're craving a classic steak burrito, a spicy tomatillos chicken tamale, or something new like their sweet/spicy tofu or pineapple chicken, Boca Grande's guaranteed to make something you like. 

Reasonably priced, although as the prices of fresh ingredients fluctuate, so do the prices of meals. My family has calculated that homemade enchiladas(never as successful as you might hope) cost about 30% more than the ones at Boca.

I've only been here a couple times but some of my favorite bands play here. The shows are pretty cheap but are really small and not the best for sound. The food is overpriced (I got a veggie wrap for like $10) and the drinks are on the pricier side. I'm not much of a beer drinker but it seemed like the selection was kinda shitty (most people were drinking pbr..gross). It's definitely a cool atmosphere and even if you dont know the band playing, it's a good time.

I've been living in Boston for the past 4 years and I finally got a chance to go to Porter Square and to try this place out. Honestly, the SO and I walked around to see our options but I ended up choosing this place because so many of my friends rave about their curry. 

First off, we came at a perfect time (around 5PM on Sunday) and beat the rush of people. Granted, we sat at the booth tables because the other 2 tables were full but no biggie. This was probably the quickest we've both ever made a decision on what to order - it's a relatively small menu, especially for the curry dishes. I went with the pork cutlet curry, the SO with the tatsuta curry, and we shared a yakko (tofu) and green tea milkshake.

For the goods, in order of how they came out:
- Green tea milkshake: This actually wasn't what I expected at all. Tasted more like a smoothie/slush than a milkshake but it still tasted great! Kind of a ripoff at $3.50 though for such a small cup
- Yakko: Not my cup of tea tbh. I don't think I'd prefer this texture for my tofu, and I didn't like how the sauce was sweet. The SO liked it though!
- Miso soup and salad: Soup was pretty standard. I thought the dressing on the salad was REALLY good though. I'd come back for the salad dressing alone LOL. I actually didn't even know this came complimentary with the curry so +1 for such a cheap deal.
- Pork cutlet curry: fairly big dish (I had to take half of mine to go, but probably because I got stuffed with the other food that came out earlier). The curry was perfectly savory and spicy. The cutlet was crispy and cooked to perfection as well (wasn't dry at all). Delicious!
-Tatsuta curry: I don't have much to say about this since it wasn't my dish but I did try some of the chicken and it was juicy and not dry at all, as well! I think I prefer my dish a little more but to each their own.

I'd say my first time here was pretty darn good. I'd come back and recommend the spot!

You would think that Inman Square, of all places, would have more than one hipster coffee shop. You would think wrong. This is that one.

It's not bad -- the coffee's excellent if you're in a to-go kind of mood; maybe the filter coffee could be stronger but I almost always say that. The steamed milk drinks are much better than you'd find at a typical Starbucks.

It's when you start thinking about doing other coffee-shop activities that 1369 starts to falter. This place is way too crowded. I haven't had great luck finding a table here because of all the working-or-reading types. I'm an enormous hypocrite for complaining about that, since that's why I go there too, but it's really inconvenient when you go to a coffee shop to work and you have to bug out because 20 other people had the exact same idea. Especially when there aren't any other options around. 

I don't think I've ever gotten anything to eat here. I'll confirm other reviewers' comments about the place being eerily quiet for how busy it is, at least during the day, but I've never found it unpleasant. They charge for internet here but I go to coffee shops to escape the internet anyway. The staff are friendly as baristi go.

I want to love this place, but even if I can't I'm going to keep patronizing it. If there were half-stars on Yelp I'd give it 3.5. I'm rounding up because all the reviews in the right-hand column are three stars and I want to be different.

Best Arnold Palmer in town. It is the perfect blend of tea and lemonade, not too sweet, not too sour, just greatness in a cup. There is not a better AP in the city of Boston, and the staff puts hard work into everyone, fresh squeezing the lemons and blending everything right then and there.
I go to Fuji a lot. Food is good. Price is on par with most other sushi places around. The best part of the meal is getting one of their smoothies, sooooo delicious.

I was really impressed with Rebecca's Cafe's customer service. I ordered a vegetarian sandwich which was tasty, but unfortunately missing the fresh mozzarella! The manager was very nice and really made it right by sending an employee to my office. I'm really glad we have a Rebecca's Cafe here in Kendall Square. The service is speedy and efficient.

Their staff is friendly and will recognize repeat customers... It's a very small chain with another four locations (I think) so it's still good but they are a bit on the expensive side considering that their stuff has nothing special.. I mean forking over $8 for a sandwich is a bit much.

Don't get me wrong, the food is good, and they are quick, have a great selection available including daily specials which change every day. Their food sometimes, once in a while, won't have too much flavor depending on what you order and the portions aren't too big which has caused me to get a bit hungry shortly after visiting the place but overall I like it. Their drink selection is quite impressive as well.

good eats. try to coconut aqua fresca, its like crack. Not that i know what crack is like. I dont. I dont do crack. I dont do any drugs ESPECIALLY crack. Ive never done any drugs.. well, ok thats a lie, I tried weed once... ok thats a lie, I tried it 3 times. The first time was weird, the second time made me flip out, the 3rd time I thought the 2nd was a fluke but nope I FREAKED OUT EVEN MORE.. but it WASNT CRACK. I SWEAR. .

try a high rise

Stick to playing pool and beers for this place.  If you're just looking for some place to hang out with friends after work, this is fantastic.  But as soon as you start craving a bite to eat, its all down hill from there.  We grabbed a table to kill some time before a pool table opened up, and we started eyeing what we presumed to be yummy bar food.  How can anyone mess up nachos, quesadillas or chicken fingers?  Well apparently its possible.  

Needless to say I left there as hungry as I came, but I had a great time with my friends and my pool game drastically improved.  Which isn't saying much because I was having trouble making contact with the ball to begin with.  

added bonus: the kitchen staff was making cookies for themselves at the end of the night.  we had the table closest to the kitchen so we were sniffing around so much that they brought us a plate of the rich buttery goodness- on the house :-)

Tucked away in a strip mall style area this restaurant is a diamond in the rough. Limited seating and usual wait times but the food is fantastic at a very affordable price (for Boston). For some reason, the water here tastes real good, hard to explain. Street parking nearby but pay by meter and ends metering at 8pm. A bit hard to find if it's your first time. Would come here again.

Why do the bartenders here all have such terrible personalities! We pay for drinks here, we don't have them for free! They really take the volume of customers they get from business around the area for granted. You guys are lucky to have some many people frequent this spot due to convenience and location... try to do better.

This place was okay. Stopped in for a coffee to go. My complaint was the barista who rang us up hardly said two words to us and basically just continued her social conversation with another staff member. Coffee was fine, I wish they better advertised the apple cider specials; I would have gone with that had I seen the sign first.

The restaurant is a popular spot.  I went there with a few friends as walk in.  The place was completely packed.  We got lucky and got a table for 4.  My friends ordered the luxury bento with salmon.  I ordered the spicy maki lunch special. We also shared the pop corn shrimps as well.   The place nice decor and the foods were good.  The spicy makis have tempura raw fish.

Beautiful inside with lovely looking pastries but....I had the chocolate tart and it was quite dry. I would try again to see if it was a one time thing, but the crust was dry as well as the filling which I thought would be creamy. My friend enjoyed his cheesecake but also found the crust to be overdone.

This place is super cute and yummy! They have lots of great food, bread, and pastry selections. You MUST try the cheesecake and tiramisu because they are to die for. This is a great place to stop by for a quick bite or if you want to get some work done!

A perfect three. 

We used to come here for the grilled cheese sandwich. And the rosemary fries. The cheap beer didn't hurt. It was always a good place to hang with friends.

I came back on a Tuesday afternoon for the rosemary fries. And the cheap beer. The fries were better in memory, although they weren't bad. It's just that I won't be coming back again for the fries unless I'm with a crowd. The $7 portion is too big for one person. The $3 PBR was just right.

It's still a clean little dive. It's so small and often empty that it takes on the character of the patrons. Bartenders have always been chill, and that remains true.

Service was terrible. They screwed up my pizza twice, each time the remade it, my order would go to the back of the line. I was stuck there for 25mins waiting for them to make my pizza. Never got an apology, or compensation for my time.

The "make your own pizza" concept is just perfect. I picked everything I wanted from base of the pizza to all the ingredients. I also had a chocolate brownie. The taste is just THE best.
The staff was helpful too. They were friendly and explained it to me how choosing a base and the toppings would matter. An absolute perfect hangout, ambience and food when you need a break. I recommend this place for sure.

I had an extremely unpleasant encounter with the owner/manager. His rude and smug attitude was unacceptable and, unbeknownst to him, we were looking for an establishment to organize a networking event for ~100 people. Not only will I take the business elsewhere, but I will never set foot in FTJ and will spread the word about my terrible experience.

There's a Spanish menu hidden behind the counter. Only order from that. I'll give you a hint: if you are fine with pork (which is a staple of the cuisine), get the Pernil. This is the best seasoned and cooked pork in all of Boston. Also, add some tostones. This is not a place to go to if you are vegeterian.
Pretty average sandwiches but at least they are decently priced...  The service is quick which is a plus for people on the go...

My friend had been trying to get us to Blaze for months. recently, we gave in and boy am i glad we did. Me and some coworkers stopped in for a quick lunch, and it was amazing! The service was amazing, and the lady working asked each of us if the amount of toppings she put on was good, how we were doing, etc. Extremely friendly atmosphere. The service was fast and efficient, considering how we had come as a group of five, each one ordering our own pizza. The pizzas are personal, so one person can have their own pizza (I finished mine eagerly), so its really customizable. The range of toppings were impressive, and fresh. They fire your pizza in front of you, and if you get a cookie (like each of us did),  they warm them up right on the edge of their big pizza stove, so that it's warm and gooey on the inside. The pizza was under $10, even with three toppings! I didn't get a drink, but I saw many different options of lemonade, and the next time I go (because there will definitely be a next time!), I'm definitely trying some!

Ok let me just say I'm from NYC and raised by
My Puerto Rican mom who cooks authentic Spanish food. 
If your expecting something like this you will not be disappointed. Had the pernil and chicharrons with yellow rice and beans and I can truthfully say I was pleased with meal. Not only that put the food Came out hot and fresh after you place order with counter person as there is no waiter service. A Latino cuisine that's not watered down highly recommended. I give it 4 out of 5 Coques.

Usually, sushi places are cheaper for lunch and worth the visit. Fuji is still expensive for lunch and does not deliver outstanding quality food.

SERVICE:
While the restaurant was very empty for a Saturday afternoon, it took awhile for our server to say hello, longer for him to take our order, and then even longer for our food to come out. The runner and server both seemed kind of confused throughout our meal.

FOOD:
+ Golden Gate Maki: A solid roll that integrates salmon both on the inside and outside of the maki topped with roe. The price is representative of other sushi restaurant prices around the area at $14.
- Eel Bento Box ($16): I usually do not order bento boxes at sushi restaurants, but we thought this would be the best deal given the price of everything else here. The eel was cooked well, but was tiny. The bed of rice under the eel was not sticking together and kept falling out of our chopsticks. The salad did not come with dressing. The fried vegetables were covered in batter so thick I could barely taste that I was actually eating broccoli and sweet potato.

Given the prices and quality of this restaurant, I will probably not return. I think the establishment would do much better in an area like KSQ, dominated by biotech/pharma companies, to have weekend deals for lunch or dinner that would bring in visitors that aren't coming in for a business meal. My 2 cents.

So underwhelmed! The only reason to go here is to play pool (or to go to Dead Pool w/ Chris Ewen). There is a fair selection on draft as well. That's all the positives I can list. Now on to the fails. 
Service is slow and unpleasant. The menu is sparse with poor options for vegetarians. The food quality is abysmal. I had nachos which were pretty soggy and a tuna sandwich that was not at all as anticipated (was pinkish like cat food on toasted french bread). So unappetizing that I couldn't bear to eat it. The Groupon that enticed me to eat there didn't make the deal any better in the end.

This place is crazy busy! It's quite the study spot for students, and often, almost all the seats are taken. Definitely go early if you can, or go with a friend who can find a seat as you're waiting in the long line.

My friend and I both got the shakshuka. This came with a glowing recommendation from my friend, who's been a student here for 3 years.

There's a poached egg, a hearty tomato soup, and warm fluffy bread. It's a wonderful breakfast or brunch item, and the bread goes perfectly with the soup.

My only concern is that the soup is rather salty, so if you're worried about your sodium intake, perhaps avoid the shakshuka.
Everything on the menu is good. And cheap! I definitely will be back. A low-key, hole-in-the-wall place I will bring new friends to.

The lunch specials here are great. I came in on Sunday, place was quiet. I ordered the three roll lunch special, a green tea, and it came with miso soup. All of that for $15 (before tip.)
I had the Philadelphia maki, Spicy Salmon maki, & Spicy Tuna maki. All of them were excellent, & good sized rolls, too.

Such a great deal! service was great. A quick, easy lunch spot. 5 stars for sure.

The problem with this place is that it suppose to be quick but I end up spending more time in line then I would if I ordered a pizza online or just walked into a pizza shop and ordered. I spent over 35 min in line on my lunch break and the workers are extremely SLOW!!!!! The purpose of this place no longer exist! It's Just like any pizza spot but smaller pizza

I rarely post on here, but this experience was spectacularly bad! 

The service was not good. There were more waitstaff than tables, and yet we felt like everything we did annoyed our waitress. It took twenty minutes between our appetizer and main (again, it wasn't busy in there).

Now to the food, I've never felt like I was purposefully bait & switched at a restaurant...until today. I ordered their veggie burger which comes with tomato, avocado, lettuce, onion and bbq sauce...when I recieved my burger it had the veggie patty, onion, lettuce and what can only be described as a drizzle of marinara sauce. It took 5+ minutes for our server to look our way long enough for us to call her over (which seemed to only annoy her more). We told her that the burger was supposed to come with avocado, tomato and bbq sauce - she said that the marinara is bbq sauce (it just isn't) and then stared at us as if wondering why we bothered to call her over. We requested to please add avocado and tomato and only then did she offer to take it back and get it fixed. 

My gf's burger was missing the Russian sauce it was supposed to come with, but she made do without! 

We rarely, if ever, complain about food or service, but this experience was something else. Harvard Square has so many amazing food options...this place really needs to step it up!

Also...MARINARA SAUCE IS NOT BBQ SAUCE!
Great place in Cambridge for pool! Had an excellent waitress, decently priced drinks, good food, and a handful of arcade games by the bar
Decent spot but gets  pretty crowded. Pool tables were occupied and most of the chicks were standoffish. Didn't slow me down.
Went there after 3pm and the food was a bit stale. Crowded with lost of high school students, hungry and loud!

Tatte (which I recently learned is pronounced like "latte"), despite owned by Panera, is nothing like Panera. It's trendy, pricey, and distinctively not middle America. Indeed, your average Tatte patron is a 21-year-old trilingual college student named Francesco with an $80 haircut and a Canada Goose jacket. That's to say, if you're willing to spend $17 on a salad and a coffee, there's no better place to be (my favorite is the Halloumi Salad followed by the Green & Nutty; the pastries/baked goods are as tasty as they look, and the coffee is as strong as my love for the bougie chain).
Service is prompt. The concept of Indian food served mexican style is good and lives upto expectation. Better than most Indian food served around in Boston.

Tatte is one of my favorite cafes, the downsides are that it is always overcrowded and so hard to eat in peace. Ordered the avocado toast, muesli, and French toast. The muesli was amazing, will for sure be ordering this again. Don't underestimate the power of creamy Greek yogurt, amazing delicious powerful granola, honey and fruit. 
The French toast was interesting, probably will not order it again but worth the try. Some suggestions, it has goat cheese and raspberry jam on top of it which was intriguing for the taste buds, not entirely bad though. The mint on it however was out of place. Some bites have mint and others don't, I personally didn't like the bites with mint. However the bread of the French toast was delicious. 
The avocado toast on the other hand was disappointing to me, but my friends enjoyed it. Overall not the greatest avocado toast I've ever had, but still a solid savory breakfast if that's what you're into. Disclaimer it's so hard to eat and so messy so be prepared. 
The matcha latte and the vanilla latte was so delicious. The cappuccino was pretty good too. 

All in all, I still love tatte!
Only a 4 star because the overcrowdedness of the restaurant made it hard to enjoy the meal.

Here's a well-guarded secret: pool is half off on Sunday nights. I discovered this by accident in June and I was just in there last weekend, the deal is still on! Even better, I have never had to wait for a table here.

Too bad the kitchen was closed by 10:40pm on Sunday but with the Patriots creaming the Steelers, a nice group of pool players, and music to boot, I couldn't think of a better way to spend a Sunday night.

Tried twice now given some of the hype... both times underwhelming... under cooked pizza.  Got a plain pie one time, and hot link second.  Too much in toppings, but both were undercooked on top.  

 Will not be eating there any more.

Really delicious, perfectly roasted coffee. Friendly staff, and a wonderful bustling location. I wish I could have sat and enjoyed the cozy atmosphere all afternoon--and I will definitely return once Covid restrictions can be lifted.

Goods: Food was good, better than I expected since we just went inside on a fly because we were hungry and walking around the town. Spacious place to sit, I cannot imagine ever waiting for a table. 
Not Goods: Place was clean but food didn't taste very fresh. The servers were not very eager to serve. We had to keep calling the people from their inside rooms for getting condiments or to order more food. They didn't even bring the food to the table after it took 15+ minutes to cook. They just yelled at us :-(

Park Restaurant is an American restaurant that serves many meat and vegetable options located in Harvard Square. The lights were set to a dim lighting. With it be a hip fully loaded bar, it was very loud and hard to have a conversation without saying "What?". It was cool that this restaurant had an industrial feel to it. The restaurant had a red theme to it as all the booth seats were red colored. The tables were all wood and the chairs were Carlisle metal dining chair. I liked how the walls were covered with framed black and white photos from historic and industrial Boston.

In summary, the atmosphere was nice but the food was okay. For drinks, most of us stuck with water, which was served in a Italian water bottle and kept on the table. I don't know if I was extremely thirsty or that Arnold Palmer was phenomenal - there was a nice blend of iced tea and lemonade and a hint of sugar. 

We ordered the following:
-Warm Corn Bread with Vermont Maple Butter 
-Roasted Beets Salad
-Caesar Salad
-Bar Steak
-Plancha Roasted Salmon
-Oven Roasted Redfish
-Farro Stuffed Squash
-Roasted Fall Squash (side)
-Roasted Carrots and Charred Onions (side)

Here are my thoughts of some of these items:
The Warm Corn Bread with Vermont Maple Butter was so delicious, it was gone in 60 seconds. I liked how it was prepared in a skillet and some of the maple butter was melted on top of the corn bread. 

The Roasted Beets Salad was also a hit. The multi-colored beets were tossed with segmented orange and grapefruit and toasted hazelnut in a light citrus vinaigrette, served on top of a sweet, simple lemon vinaigrette, and garnished with a bed of arugula. The lemon vinaigrette consisted of sugar, lemon zest, white balsamic vinaigrette.

The Caesar Salad consisted of chopped romaine hearts, lots of parmesan cheese, and a toad in the hole tossed in a creamy Caesar salad. I felt that the salad was too cheesy and the parmesan stole the show. Their toad in the hole is different than most because it didn't have any meat or vegetables. Their toad in the hole consisted of a square toast brushed with salted butter on each side and toasted on a pan with a poached egg in the middle. 

The Bar Steak consisted of a steak topped with herb shallot butter and served with potato skins filled with gruyere and topped with bacon bites and arugula and frisée salad. The steak was a bit on the dry side, a bit overdone, and wasn't very tender but rather a chewy texture. Did someone taste test the arugula and frisée salad before it left the kitchen? Cause the salad with an olive oil vinaigrette was very sour and thus, hard to ingest.

The Oven-Roasted Redfish was served on topped of a mashed sweet potato or a sweet potato puree, confit cauliflower, and topped with a cranberry pear chutney, We were a bit disappointed because we specifically asked our server whether this particular dish was spicy since three of us has sensitivity to pepper and spices and our server responded saying not at all. When we received this dish, the fish was brushed with a spicy rub so we couldn't really eat the fish and only the items around it. I did enjoy the sweet potato and confit cauliflower.

The Roasted Fall Squash consisted of roasted acorn squash wedges. It was lightly seasoned with salt and pepper. I didn't like occasion bites with a lot of crushed pepper or spices. 

The Roasted Carrots and Charred Onions consisted of a medley of carrots and onions. I felt that this side dish was on point. I loved how the carrots and parsnips had a crunch.

Unfortunately, our party was too full to have dessert.

The food was fine, but the service was awful.  We had a 6:45pm reservation on a Saturday night, so I understand it's a busy time, but they didn't seat us for 45 mins.  The worst part was that they kept telling us the table was 'almost ready' and then would just ignore us until we prodded them about it again.

Then after we were seated, the service continued to be terrible.  We didn't see a server for another 30 minutes.  We ordered drinks and food and had to keep asking about our beers and kept hearing that "sorry, the bar is really busy!"  This didn't make sense as we saw people at the bar getting ordering/receiving drinks.  We ended up getting our food and eating half of it before finally getting our drinks - I'd estimate a full 30 minutes after ordering them.   Even paying was a hassle just to get our server to take the bill - we ended up having to ask another server to take it.  

You come to a restaurant to share time with friends and family and having to constantly stay on top of the staff just to get served was really distracting the entire evening.  I understand that Saturday nights are busy, but if a business is going to take in that many people, they have a responsibility to staff up appropriately.

the fries here are amazing!! Definitely should order this!

There is a cheap $3 beer.

beets sliders doesn't have much taste - so I wouldn't order it again.

Would like to go back and try other items on the menu - they have some interesting options.

THIS IS A DISCRIMINATORY ESTABLISHMENT. 

"So that you'll stop staring at me, I guess I'll take your order" - The Owner of The Cellar

This was by far the worst restaurant experience I have had in living in Cambridge for 2.5 years. The owner of The Cellar "served" us... but we ended up walking out, insulted, before we could even order drinks. I have never, in my two and half years as a graduate student at Harvard and MIT and living in Cambridge felt SO discriminated against, for my race (I am an Asian-American woman) or for my age (late 20s). I cannot tell which it was, but I cannot understand otherwise why we were treated so poorly. 

Looking for a good, quick dinner, I suggested to my boyfriend that we try The Cellar, a place I'd been many times before and enjoyed. My boyfriend and I walked in ahead of another couple, and were seated. Oddly, I noticed that the woman, the owner as she told us, proceeded to first take the order of the older, white couple who came in after us and were seated after us. I thought that was odd, but decided to ignore it. We waited another ten minutes without being helped. The owner finally walked over then and told us she was short staffed, but not in a professional or apologetic way. Fine, I thought, that is no big deal! But she went back to drinking (she was drinking pretty heavily) and muttered something under her breath about us. 

After waiting for a few minutes, my boyfriend and I had decided what we wanted to order. I was trying to make eye contact with the owner to take our order, but she was completely inattentive. Her friend then came over to her and muttered under her breath that we were looking at her, so the owner came over, with a judging look on her face, oozing disrespect, and said - LITERALLY VERBATIM - "So that you'll stop staring at me, I guess I'll take your order."

I could not believe what she had just said - it was so rude and completely uncalled for. I responded, "ma'am, there is no need to talk like that to a patron, and yes I was trying to catch your eye because we were ready to order." I cannot explain if her discriminatory behavior was race-based or age-based, but I have never EVER felt so insulted in my life in a restaurant. I will never go back to The Cellar and urge anyone who lives in Cambridge or visits to respect the racial and age diversity of this beautiful town and to not support this horrible management with your business. Never again!!!!!!

There are so many unique treats here-- I wish I lived nearby so I could come back and try them all! The Stumptown coffee was excellent and I really enjoyed the peach ricotta tartine. I felt safe ordering takeout during Covid.

A great cafe spot in the heart of Harvard University. A very large space filled with window seating and table seating. The pastries looked appetizing and fresh. But I ordered the croqued madame and it was beyond large! It's like Panera bread style of serving and receiving food. It was good overall but could not finish it because it was so large!

I ordered four personal pizzas for a little over $50. 1 was burnt/inedible... and I NEVER throw away pizza. 2 were just wrong (for example, they put garlic cloves for no reason.. a lot of them). The only correct and not cardboard pizza was the plain cheese. I wouldn't be writing this review, but they don't seem to answer the phone, clear their voice messages or review any calls from customer service. I've worked at 3 pizza places.. it's not that hard guys.

This place is excellent! Great pizza, friendly staff, quick service. 5 star experience.

 Bonus, if you're on a keto diet, they have a keto friendly crust, and it is really tasty. I strongly recommend watching the Thomas DeLauer video about Blaze keto pizza and paying attention to his cautions regarding which cheeses, toppings and sauces are keto friendly, before ordering.

The only negative is the price of the keto pizza (their regular pizzas are far more reasonably priced);13.99 for a 10 inch pizza....granted, that's with unlimited toppings, but, if you're someone who just wants 1 or 2 toppings, or plain cheese, that seems a bit excessive. Not enough to take a star away, but, worth mentioning.

*****CASH ONLY*****
Japanese comfort food in a small food court, located in Porter Square. Super casual, super good. There is a BOA atm right outside the building, yay! 
If you go during prime lunch hours, be prepared to wait 20+ min, usually a 10 min wait. 

Portions are humongo, can be shared between people, but who wants to do that when the food is so good. 

*Tokyo Hamburg set: one of my favs, there are a few different types of hamburg sauces, but this is my go-to because it is not too tomatoey or teriyaki sauce heavy. easy-over egg is a must ($1 extra) to add a bit of moisture to the patty.

*Curry Katsu: I would visit Boston just for this. Katsu is thick and crispy, super moist and flavorful. Curry is Japanese style, a bit more watery. Again, portions are hefty! 

Service is prompt and well organized, despite there being only one waitress. 
Limited seating area, good for parties of 1-3, take-outs are good as well.

falafel wrap was delicious and really cheap!  possibly my new favorite spot in central.  on the other hand, my bf's chicken shwarma was not as great and he was wishing he went with falafel too. the place is tiny so definitely be prepared to grab and go.

they are also open until 3am, which i have yet to cash in on but definitely plan to.

Most of the reviews are not helpful logistics-wise so here is everything you need to know before attending a concert. 

1. DO NOT MAIL YOUR TICKETS TO YOURSELF. You have the option when you purchase tickets to either pick them up at will-call or mail them to your home address. Thinking I would be able to skip the crazy will-call line, I paid the additional $10 to have them shipped to me. Well, guess what. There is ONLY ONE LINE. Whether you pay to ship them or not, you will all wait together in a long line outside to get inside. Then a bouncer will let you inside ten at a time to get in another line where there will be one line person checking I.D.s and tickets. The system is ridiculous so either get there extremely early or expect to miss the opening act. Either way, the line is unavoidable so save the money and pick up at will-call. I was scammed out of $10.

2. The entrance to the Middle East Downstairs is on the right-hand side of the building. There are no signs indicating this, so a lot of people (myself included) were going into the front restaurant entrance looking for the concert entrance. The entrance to the restaurant is on Massachusetts Avenue and the entrance to the concert venue is on Brookline Street. 

3. The security guards suck big time. I attended a Peter Murphy concert on 12/9/16 and the guards were constantly walking back and forth with their eye-wateringly bright flashlights distracting from an intimate show. At one point, security went to the side of the stage and started to play on their phones and eat dinner while on the clock. Peter Murphy got into a heated (and colorful) exchange that ended with one of the guards kicking his equipment and causing thousands of dollars worth of damage. How unprofessional is that? Peter Murphy walked off stage for 10 minutes and I was almost out $80. He did eventually come back, but it's hard to enjoy a concert when everyone is yelling "f**k you" and the people behind you start loudly demanding refunds.

4. There were clear signs stating that concert attendees could only take pictures during the first three songs and videos during the first two songs. Well, nobody was listening. One idiot was FACETIMING THE CONCERT TO HER FRIEND. The venue is standing room; everyone is at one level so the people in the back couldn't see anything besides a sea of phones and bright screens. Security did nothing about this. 

5. The sound quality was horrendous, on par with a garage band.

I was really disappointed with the Middle East. Peter Murphy was amazing, but I will never return to this venue again. Call it what it is: a dingy, urine-scented basement being "guarded" by a handful of volatile children.
We get breakfast from here all the time. Real eggs!!! Everything I've had was great. Never had anything else. It's pretty cheep too, and they deliver!!

This restaurant was always fine for me, quick and easy. Tonight I had a terrible experience that is worth sharing - I ordered online and it said it would take 45 minutes. Usually that is overestimated and the food comes much more quickly. Tonight however, over an hour passed so I called and they couldn't explain what was going on. They called ten minutes later and said the food was on the way (the restaurant is five minutes from my house), they were talking to the driver. Ten minutes later I called back - they said they had a lot of orders today and the driver must have gotten stuck in traffic in the North End. As that is completely the other direction from my house, I can only imagine the state my food was in! I requested a cancellation, which they said they would do (hopefully they will, since I paid by credit card). So disappointed - don't order from here if you want something quick and good.

Boloco makes what I can only describe as fusion burritos.  It is not a mexican restaurant.  I enjoyed my buffalo chicken burrito and it filled me up for an afternoon of work.  Definitely a good, quick stop for students and young professionals on the go.

Ordered an omelette for pick-up.  Wasn't impressed by the size of it- suuuper small.  The homefries where okay and the toast was passable.  The cheese in my omelette tasted like the fake plastic cheese that kraft makes in singles slices.  Probably won't eat here again.  Mass Ave diner is exponentially better.

I went here for a lunch buffet today (Saturday).
