Some of the dishes were great (the fish masala and the yellow lentils), while others were not so good (the fried stuff and naan did not taste fresh - specifically, the naan was not fluffy).  The rice pudding tasted good, while the other dessert, some type of white sugar dough ball, tasted like it came straight from a can.  My friend who is Indian told me that the white sugar dough balls should have had better texture and flavor (she had them in India).
Decently sized convenience store near harvard on Cambridge St.  Great selection of refridgerated beverages for those hot summer days.  The store contains an ATM.

I come here for lunch occasionally and usually get the pizza. Best part about this place is actually that they take credit card. They do sandwiches, salads, pizza by the slice, a daily hot special, and breakfast. They are usually pretty fast too so its great for lunch.

If you think that Bengali food just means mindless, stomach burning hot spice, then this restaurant is on the dot. However, being a Bengali myself, I know that our cuisine is more about the flavor of the spices rather than the nose-dripping mixture this restaurant claims to be Bengali food. I have most certainly have had much better..oh heck, I can even cook better than this myself and it does not even cost me as much.

The negative reviews here truly surprise me ...I have come here again and again and run into other random foodies here-- we all agree --yummy cauliflower curry, 2 different fish dishes ( the one with bones is a bengali standard, and one with few or no bones) and delicious goat curry. The onion chutney is fresh, tastes like it was made in the last couple days if not day of...and if you get there around noon when the lunch buffett on the weekend opens, all the pakoras, samosas, and flat bread are just made and still warm...

and real chai , unlimited, comes with your food....

I made a point of eating once at every Indian lunch buffet on Mass Ave between Central and MIT during the course of a month. Royal Bengal isn't the best, but it's the closest to my office. I've eaten there twice, the food is inconsistent and so is the service. They ran out of naan the second time and didn't replace it while I was there. I don't leave there feeling really happy like some other Indian buffets, but I leave satisfied.

We held the PJA Advertising holiday party at Kennedy's last night and they outdid themselves on the food, the hospitality, and the overall experience. As background, for the past ten years we have held our party at Upstairs on the Square, a place we loved, and found it hard to believe we could find an alternative that would rise to that standard. 

I stumbled into Kennedy's for lunch one day and was completely taken by two things: the friendliness of the place, and the lobster roll. I drilled the server on the lobster and learned that they had their own tanks and cooked the lobster fresh every morning. It's also a great space, anchored by a large bar. With a little trepidation, we decided to give it a go for our party.

Mike Lanno, the general manager, was fantastic and went all out to accommodate us in terms of room set up, wine tastings, you name it. We also worked directly with Yankel, the executive chef who designed a beautiful menu. For passed appetizers he made vegetable maki, fried oysters, crab cakes, little lamb chops, and a handful of other delicious things. There were also mini-lobster rolls and latkes served as small plates. The main course was tenderloin, roasted vegetables, butternut squash ravioli, mashed potatoes, and a beautiful salad. Desert was chocolate covered strawberries, and homemade donuts. Yankel personally oversaw all the cooking and was in the dining room carving and serving people, which we really enjoyed. 

There are so many intangibles that define a restaurant. All the servers and bartenders were genuine and accommodating without being patronizing or phony. It just has a good vibe. This is a fantastic addition to Harvard Square and I suspect will become a go-to destination for a lot of us. My advice is to enjoy Kennedy's before the secret is out.

This is a decent North Indian restaurant, though it doesn't go out of its way to cater to vegans, sadly (I had heard they used to have a vegan buffet, though that might be at their other branch).

I normally hate Indian buffets, but they had some decent vegan dishes in their lunch lineup (~$13). I enjoyed the rajmah (red beans) which is a traditional dish that you don't see often. The aloo channa was also solid, as was the mixed peas with corn and carrots. All of the dishes were hearty and unabashedly spicy, which I respected.

They gave me naan instead of roti, which is a shame, as naan is not vegan, and it's also made with pathetic white flour. You can order roti separately, but they charge you extra for it, which is ridiculous in my opinion. They should allow customers to choose between roti and naan.

They also don't offer brown rice, which is also a shame, though the odds of finding an Indian restaurant with brown rice is slim to begin with.

Lastly, there are no vegan desserts, which is also not surprising, given that this is an Indian restaurant. But couldn't they experiment and come up with some newer, interesting dishes to cater to the smarter, edgier demographics of Cambridge?

This is my favorite Indian buffet lunch in Harvard Square because it has a good vegetarian selection, serves pompadum, and is $1 cheaper than the others (hey, it adds up!)

Everyone I bring there talks about how they'd never find it on their own, but now that they know it's there, they'll be back.

Maybe because the place is small, conversations are easy to eavesdrop on. And for some reason the people I end up sitting next to are always overachievers who make me feel inadequate for not applying to jobs halfways around the world.

I don't think they change the dishes around very much, so you pretty much know what you're going to get. Consequently, I can't go there too often because it's all the same. Service has been unspectacular but fine. Also, the last time I went was for a very late lunch, and they never refilled the pampadom! The whole reason I go is for a pompadum fix! Also, they've served vermicelli kheer the last few times, and I just prefer rice kheer.

Pro tip: since they bring the naan to your table, they're just going to throw it away if you don't eat it. So fill up on rice (and pompadum, of course), and ask them to wrap the naan up. It freezes well and can be brought back to life in your humble toaster.

Yes for cheap/good/big sandwiches and salad.  The place next door has the best sandwiches, but they cost more. Not the greatest prices for the items on the shelves.  It is convenient so its hard to complain. Coffee's good.  Employees are nice too.

Royal India is now called Royal Bengal I think.  vegetarian friendly Indian restaurant located between Central square and MIT.  

the lunch buffet is good for MIT people and cheap.
delicious breakfasts (they deliver!!!) but seated service for egg breakfasts took over an hour so we walked out! strange!...

The food here is good, and for a long time my husband and I ordered from here a lot for delivery and all was swell. At some point, however, it really started to lack- wrong items being sent, orders missing items altogether- it got to a point where the husband called them one day angry after they'd neglected to send part of the order, and they gave him attitude in return. 

Zoe's is a 5 minute walk from me. I will be ordering breakfast from there from now on. After what we dealt with from these people, I can't recommend them, which it sucks to say because they used to be one of our favorite order-in restaurants.

This place is our standard safety "yep, its Chinese night". 

We order from this place probably every other week. They deliver fast and are friendly. They share the same delivery staff that does Passage to India. 

The Wonton Soup, while very simple, is quite delicious and enjoyable! Clean, hot and yummy!

Nearly everything we've gotten from this place has been great! Soups, rice, lo mein, you name it. Its standard chinese food take out fare... and its done well. 

My personal favorites are:
-Wonton Soup
-General Gau's Chicken
-Chicken Lo Mein
-Sesame Chicken (though a little syrupy)
-Peking Ravioli
-Moo Shi

You can order on foodler.com which makes this place another gem! Online ordering and I can use a credit card! Fantastic! 

One time we did pick up, not delivery and saw them making the Crab Rangoon right there on one of the tables towards the back (complete with a drape making sure it was sanitary). This made me smile since they MAKE stuff there, not just fry up pre-made order bulk stuff.

The only thing I wish they did, was also give you bread with your orders. My old chinese food place from college gave you small rolls with every order.... totally unnecessary, but appreciated!

Out on a Friday night, two hot young ladies stepped out on the town - and into Temple Bar. We nabbed one of the two remaining outdoor seating, providing for endless enjoyment of the gorgeous non-humid weather and the silly people watching. Our waiter was friendly, if a bit nervous, but he was cute enough to overlook that.

I liked how the bread basket had baguette, focaccia, and corn bread. As a girl who can't ever make decisions, being able to sample all three was a win.  Emily had the mussels appetizer for her main and I ordered the herbed gnocchi with mushrooms and peas. The mussels turned out to be just as big as my entree so it was a good choice. I unfortunately cannot eat bivalves but she nommed with deliciousness on the spicy sausage and crusty bread and mussel innards. My gnocchi were melt in your mouth amazing, very soft and fluffy. The herbs and mushrooms and peas were very complementary, and they got along just fine with me.

It's a little pricey, but I think I'd like to go back and try more things, especially their cocktails.

Tasty food, very tasty drinks, friendly and accommodating staff...what more could you want in a bar? Seems that a lot of people like this place as it was fairly crowded on a Tuesday even.

I, personally, wouldn't go out of my way to come here since its on the other side of town...but if I was still in the Cambridge area, I would totally stop by. Lots of booths, a few TVs scattered appropriately here and there, and a good sized staff make this a solid place to go get a drink after work or before a date.

Look like anything but not omelet . fake eggs and non fresh ingredients .over priced and the Pastries very old . the coffe very light .I wish I can give more than one star but I can't find any reason .

Dilara is not closed. It's just open at odd hours - which could work out great in some circumstances. When you call at 3am, they ask delivery and you say pickup but they hang up before you can even say you're literally across the street, there isn't much more to say. I've unfortunately had bad experiences dealing with them before; I like to order chicken tenders and they always skimp on the sauces (but throw in 1 extra blue cheese to make up) and one time they way overcharged and tried to talk me out of it. Sadly, I'm going to avoid them at all costs going forward.

Got delivery here recently for me and 3 others.  Delivery was SLOW but food was still fairly hot and still delcious.

Prices are fair.... example: $8.50 for chicken tender meal with fries and salad.

Food was pretty good, fries were awesome and so was the frozen yogurt I got!

I'm a a huge fan
Way better than the other Mexican places including qdoba chipotle and Felipe's
The ingredients are ORGANIC and taste fresh 
Felipe has weird vegetables 
This place is very friendly to all types of healthy diets

Well, the other day, I was running around, and realized I needed to eat lunch before work started at 11am.  Few lunch places were open, but Boloco was, and they were serving lunch at 10:30!  I was very happy to get a filling and tasty lunch, at an hour where it would have been tough to find food that wasn't egg- or sausage-based.  Thanks, Boloco!

I've been to every Indian restaurant in the Square (Bombay Club, Cafe India, Tanjore, etc.) and this one is by far the best. I usually go here for lunch because I like Indian buffets -- Indian is one of those types of food where I like a variety of different things because I never know what the hell is in the food, so I like trying different things.

The food here is amazing. I don't know the name of anything, but the chicken is always tender, the fried foods aren't soggy, the rice is fluffy and not hard or sticky, and the naan is always warm and yummy. 

The atmosphere is not great -- it's located in a basement and the stairs you go down to get there are a lawsuit waiting to happen, but once inside, you forget the darkness and the cloth curtains covering up the brick walls and you just eat.... and eat and eat....

My friends and I really like coming here for trivia nights on Thursdays, and while the food can be a bit pricey, they do offer a nice happy hour for appetizers and often have a good cheap beer on special. Not a health food location by any means, but if you're looking for a massive burger with a variety of interesting things on it (from mac and cheese to fried pickles to Thanksgiving stuffing), this is your place. MAJOR love to our favorite waitress, Tanya, who is super kind, accommodating, and generally a wonderful person!

I was ordering regularly from this place, I've always found the food to be  good and I like the fact that they have more on their menu then just subs/pizza like other shops.

 I've had some problems with the service. My order is never ready when I get there. I learned to give them at least 30 more minutes then their quoted pick up time and even then it's a crap-shoot whether or not your order is ready.  Sometimes my order was never put in at all !  I also started to feel nickel and dimed when it came to the bill. The prices aren't cheap and little x-tra fees is just frustrating. 

Again, the food is good, once you get your hands on it. Also the people are nice, but they need to learn a little something about keeping customers happy.

This place is great!! Right across the street from work, I can get fresh salads, pre-made sandwiches, or a hot meal everyday. Small scale, but simple and always clean, and fresh. The owner is the nicest guy ever and will happily do what he can to accommodate requests. Breakfast is fantastic too... bacon egg and cheese sandwiches are freshly made and a million times better than D&D or Starbucks!

I had doubts about a place called Wok n Roll, but the two times I've gotten delivery here have been very good. Kung Pao tofu, General Gau's tofu and veggie fried rice.

Other General Gau's tofu I've had was either too soft or just had air inside. This was both substantial and crispy. I do wish the sauce weren't as sweet though. The fried rice isn't at all greasy, and has water chestnuts, peppers, sprouts and a bit of broccoli. 

It arrived 20 minutes after I ordered and was still so hot I had to wait to eat it. I'm happy!

I came here once and was terribly underwhelmed. I have not returned and do not plan to. The place is subterranean and there were fruit flies all around (not a swarm, but still annoying). Expensive for subpar food. The portions were so small. The seasoning is spot on though, if that is any consolation. Service was slow.
Had the spicy green beans with chicken. Delicious! They offer brown rice, which is a nice change from the usual fried rice or white rice.

In a little shopping center with lots of Asian food options, I settled at this small place with three friends. The menu does offer a lot of options, but in the end, we felt the food to be quite mediocre. The rice bowls were not particularly big (for almost $9) and really not special tasting. It wasn't "bad," per se, but it was a lot of disappointment both for the price and the expectation of something that would taste at least a full step up from the make-at-home freezer bag Asian meals. None of us got the noodles (so maybe that's a better "specialty?"), but with the other options around, I probably would not return.

Royal Bengal is a sadly overlooked Indian restaurant. It's highly vegetarian friendly and my only real complaint about the place is that it's so small everybody notices the cold when the door opens on a winter's night. Specializing in Bengali and North Indian dishes, I recommend making an adventurous choice - it'll be worth your while at this place. A good choice for dinner with friends, but not a romantic night out, as it is small and crowded!

They derive their rating from me for the deli, breads, and interesting chocolates and sweets only...I stop here for sandwich fixings (cheese, turkey and ham) from the deli. They are reasonably priced and known brands. They also have good pre-made sanwiches and frozen food selections.

I would not recommend stopping here for produce, even if you are desparate b/c it usually is compost-worthy only...oy...the stuff i have seen rotting and brown in here...

Service has been slow a few times but if you are a tried and true cheap-o like me, you will wait until they get what you need.

I eat lunch here a lot.

Fast and good

Their daily specials are usually very tasty.
Honestly I have not explored the menu too much.
The rice side dish it typically very good

They had a teriyaki meatball sub with basil cucumber and carrots that I found amazing.
Their grilled chicken is really good

I find the staff friendly and efficient.
The falafel wrap is something I get a lot.
Really great food and very reasonable prices. Not as heavy and salty as many other restaurants.  Only negative was that the delivery was a little slow.
This looks like a cute place with authentic Japanese food! The Kara age chicken is delicious! And the curry is really good with Pork circle. Will definitely return to here again.

I didn't like First Printer but Kennedy's was better.  The service was generally great, unlike FP. And the food was very good.  The menu isn't large, but there was something for everyone.  I had the small plates fish tacos which were excellent and not so "small."  My side of vegetables were not great.  The asparagus and Brussels sprouts were nearly raw". The peas were the best part of this dish and there were not enough of them.  Two guys at our table had the corned beef sandwich with fries.  They loved both.  Another diner at our table had the pasta with vegetables, but didn't like it.  It was supposed to have a Parmesan sauce, but it just had a few shavings of parm and the pasta was supposed  to be angel hair and wasn't.  Wines and hard ciders were all good.  Noise level was loud, but we left before it got too bad.

If you are in the mood for a decent bite in the shop or delivered to your house, KEEP looking. Cafe Delaria has an okay menu, but can't live up to their current rating or price. After placing an order for a pizza, a sandwich, and a salad I had to wait about an hour and a half for the delivery which is less than a mile away. Only to receive a sloppily put together meal worth half of what I paid, not to mention the body hair that was found on the pizza. So spend the extra 2 mins of searching, and don't have the same experience I did. 


-Cheers

Harvard market has been my go to convenient store. They have amazing sandwiches, their pre-made salads are delicious, and their prices are very competitive. I highly recommend their sandwiches as they have wraps, panini's and subs which have all been delicious.

I would give this place 2.5 stars. Very small booth amongst several of small sit down shops. My friends and I came here after a long drive and the food was just so so. 

We started off with octopus balls which came with a spicy mayo and brown sauce. The ball was mushy on the inside and crispy on the outside. I've never had it before but I wasn't a fan of the texture and taste. The sauces were strange. The brown was vinegary. The spicy mayo was not like the typical one found on sushi. More creamy? 

For my entree, I got the pork tampopo Don on rice with brown sauce. The pork was made well. Crispy outside and tender on the inside. However, over rice, it felt strange to eat. Too dry? Sauce was thick. Oh, I chose the salad to start with and it was just lettuce with some ginger dressing. Nothing fancy.

The portions here are great and it is cheap but my entree wasn't that great and service was okay. I had ordered tea and had to ask twice before I received it. The server had no tables at the time.

I would give this place another go. They have a huge menu and this cuisine is not something that I am familiar with so I would like to see what else there is to offer.

Not sure where this name came from - I know this place as Royal Bengal.

It's been a long while since I last ate here, but this was the clear winner for me for Indian food in college.  And there are a *lot* of little Indian restaurants in this area.  Looks wise this place is just like all the others.  It might offer the standard buffet too, I don't remember.  But I do remember liking their lamb dish a lot (can't be sure which one).  I know very little about Indian cuisine, so I can't comment on the authenticity, but the food here is definitely tasty.

Kind of unexpectedly pricey, but still good.  I preferred this place to fancier options in Harvard Sq and Boston.

edit:  back to try it twice recently - and it's okay.  3.5 tops.

Picture it.  A rainy day in Harvard Square.  A little hungry, a good bit wet, and terribly behind on my Psych reading, I decided to duck into anywhere close to me that served food and was somewhat quiet.  I was happy to find a cozy atmoshere with brick walls and friendly smiles behind the counter.  Also, the noise level in this place wasn't bordering on sonic boom so it seemed like a winner.  I was right.  Thank you Harvard Square Boloco for providing sanctuary from the elements, healthy choices at wallet friendly prices, peace and quiet and that killer chocolate chip cookie that I can't seem to resist most visits.  

I did mention that I sort of became a regular after my first visit, didn't I?

I have very mixed feelings about this place...

On one hand, they are very quick and the people are nice when you order in person. On the other hand, the delivery time, cleanliness and customer service after they mess up orders is downright TERRIBLE.

I still can't get over the fact that I ordered a grilled chicken sandwich with mayo and dijon and they delivered a grilled chicken sandwich with nothing but lettuce. No onions.. no tomatoes...pickles... Just dry grilled chicken, lettuce AND NOTHING ELSE. I called back and was like, "where's the mayo and dijon mustard? I asked for both. Doesn't this come with tomatoes and stuff too?" to which the employee responded "we don't put that on grilled chicken sandwiches." After pleading with him that I asked for the mayo and dijon to be put on there they told me to come in and return the sandwich.

Now, I don't live very far from them, but I asked for delivery because it was raining, so I insisted on them coming back to me. They refused. It would be one thing if I lived on the other side of Cambridge, but I live off of Cardinal Medeiros... A 3 minute drive away!
This really pissed me off because there was no way I was walking there in the rain.

The food isn't special anyway. And the floor on the inside of their establishment behind the counter is gross. It really wouldn't surprise me if they have "unwanted guests" that live there.

I can't give this place much higher than 2.5 stars....if only this neighborhood joint were a little better...

Went in for takeout, barely acknowledged at the bar, had to grab a menu for myself and sat there for a bit.  I put in my order and after that the bartender didn't speak to me, at all. Customer service is lacking as there was minimal interaction. Burger was good.
This was surprisingly good Chinese food. The dumplings were a little too doughy, but the scallion pancakes were great, and the chicken with cashews was a huge, delicious portion.

Great new restaurant in Harvard Square. I enjoyed First Printer as well. I knew it was going to be a good experience when we got a parking space right out front! 

They had a nice selection of beers on draft. We ordered the veggie burger and the mac and cheese. The veggie burger was a delicious homemade patty with chickpeas on a big yummy brioche bun. The mac and cheese was super rich and tasty, one of the best I have had in awhile with a ritz cracker crust. The prices are very reasonable for the neighborhood. Our server was great as well. I will be back!
The atmosphere was great and I loved the wide range of cocktails offered! The staff was also very courteous and helpful. Full of JFK pictures and themed menu without being overwhelming.

Stay away. I ordered a large cheese pizza and a Sierra Mist, which I figured would be pretty simple to figure out. I was wrong. It took over an hour and a half to get my pizza, which showed up cold and did not look appetizing at all. When I called to check on my order after an hour of waiting, whoever answered the phone seemed extremely annoying that I was asking about my pizza, and told me "it's probably on the way." Great service. They also gave me a Ginger Ale. Wish I could give zero.

My friends in Porter love this place for take-out. I had a good experience as well, although I think it is way overpriced.
For $12.85 I got the sweet and sour chicken and crab rangoon and brown rice - combo (forgo the soup for the extra rangoon!) It was exactly what the doctor ordered - the sweet & sour sauce was light and not that disgusting pink food court stuff. Despite previous reviews, I think the chicken was fresh and definitely real! And no food poisoning here :)
I'd say that it's not a bad place to eat in if you have that hankering for grub. But it's not super cheap... typically $15 gets me much more chinese. :)

Boloco's burritos and smoothies taste great, and I love that they have three different sizes. (The "small" burrito is usually more than enough for me!) Their huevos rancheros breakfast burrito is the best thing ever and a great deal ($3.50 for a small burrito, I think, which is actually huge). Most of all, everyone who works here is really nice and tries their best to make sure they're giving great customer service. As someone who first came to Boloco because I was sick of the perpetual rudeness of the people working at Felipe's down the street, this goes a long way. (Plus their burritos are so much fresher tasting and less greasy!) For example, I ordered a burrito with guacamole once, and the cashier forgot to add it to my order. When I realized the burrito I got didn't have guacamole and brought it up, they gave me a large container of guacamole on the side (more expensive than just in the burrito) for free. Not a big deal, but little gestures like this one (not the only time something like this has happened when I was at Boloco) go a long way.

I tried this place two weeks ago and I won't try it again . I orderd an egg white omelet with steak and veggies- no cheese, with wheat toast-no butter.  I received a greasy omelet with old meatballs instead of the shaved steak, veggies and cheese. My toast came drenched in butter... seriously? Did the guy taking my order not communicate with the back kitchen or are the cooks that incompetent? 

I ended up paying $8 for mediocre coffee and a terrible, greasy breakfast that I didn't even finish. If you find yourself looking for breakfast in East Cambridge, go somewhere else.

Think of it as a more expensive, lesser quality Anna's.  I go here (rarely) as it is walking distance to my apartment but I'd never choose this over Anna's if they were both an option.

The meats and rice are often not done correctly (either over or under done) and the sauces get slathered on.  The burrito ends up being a mess and not in the good way.  They have a lot of combinations for the burrito, though.

There are much better options. Don't settle. Their fried rice is bizarre, with chunks of undercooked carrots and peas. I don't get it.

If you have to eat from here, their house Lo Mein (shrimp, beef, chicken) is not terrible. 

However, the General Gau/Tsao/Goo/whatever IS terrible. 

Don't even get me started on the name...

Listen here Mr. Neglectful Snotface... You're awfully damn lucky I'm not letting your miserable attitude and lack of attention to me and my party absolutely RUIN this review.  In fact, everything else about the experience was so superb, in fact, that I am only going to deduct one star for the flatulance that exists in place of your brain... Actually, with your miserable freaking attitude, you probably won't survive as a waiter for long, so I'll just let my grudge end at that.

::Drumroll::

On to the good stuff.

AWESOME... i mean friggin AWE-SOME burgers.  I've been searching for an absolutely blow-my-tastebuds-away burger around here, and I have found it.  Temple must be in reference to a Temple of the Lord God of all Gods, because I was in complete ecstacy while consuming this hunk of juicy meat.  An experience that could only be described as spiritually enlightening.

As if that wasn't enough... there are the fries.  I adore french fries, er, i mean, freedom fries... yeah... and this place has something I've not been able to find for a very long time:

Very.
Thin.
Curly.
Fries.

Hells yeah! Just a perfect pairing for the Gates of Heaven burger.

So, as you gathered, the service sucked... but the manager fixed it with a gift card for our next meal... and trust me, I'll definately be back many times.

As soon as you can, you should enter the Temple.

Had a couple of cheese slices and a soda while out walking. Excellent crust. Great Nee York style pizza. Hey, NY gets one thing right. 

Price was right as we'll, 5 bucks.

Just had the pork kara-age bowl for lunch and it was everything I wanted. Juicy meat, good portion, very flavorful,  it came out very fast and it was under $7. I'll definitely be back... Maybe tomorrow, lol.

Even though this place hasn't gotten amazing reviews, I'll give anything a chance. 

My roommate and I ordered from here one night, she got a steak bomb and I got the chicken kabob plate. The delivery was quicker than I expected, but the food was--to use an original term--meh. 

The steak "bomb" was tiny. About the size of a 6-inch Subway deal, and hardly any meat. She said it tasted ok though. 

The chicken was a little dry, and the rice pilaf was pretty standard, but the salad was really fresh and crisp. 

I might go back, but probably for breakfast.

As far as convenience stores go, this place is the Trump tower of convenience stores. I've always been an eetsy bit scared of walking in, due to the slightly sun-faded photos on the outside windows... But today I was walking to a friend's house, and I was thirsty... And I needed bleach to kill the fruit flies that are in the kitchen drain. 

Wouldn't you know it? I found BOTH at Harvard Market. The store was clean, fresh, and shiny, with slices of home made cake by the front. Also, their selection of Tic Tacs and other breath preservers is, well, let's just say it's a buffet of freshness. There were some sweet looking sandwiches and wraps right by the front door (in case you don't want to wait in the lunch line at Darwins, I suppose). There were so many cold beverages lined up, I instantly felt hydrated and cooler JUST BY LOOKING AT THEM. Okay, I held the door to the fridge open for a while when I was making up my mind. The register lady didn't seem to mind. She was busy on the phone, which she did set down when ringing up my purchases (A++ for customer service). Lovely. 

Oh, there's a five dollar minimum on credit card purchases, but I didn't have a problem with that, being I had a bottle of water, a bottle of bleach, and about 15 packs of tic tacs. At two calories a pop, I figure I can eat about 10 of those packs a day.

Highly recommended, high quality with less money. Love their pastrami sandwich.
I will come back again Moe :) Thank you

Was back in town, lived in Boston for seven years, for a short weekend with friends and we needed a place with availability on open table for six folks. Voila! KotS was one of the only ones in Cambridge area that met the requirement, maybe a bad sign?, so we booked it and met up. Here's the highs and lows. 
Highs
Service, both bar and table, was cordial and responsive. Drinks were filled with the quickness, as were cocktail orders. Our bartender and waitress were both great and understanding as we took a bit of time coming up with food orders (although were heavy on the alcohol, so she was racking up a good bill). If you're in the 'hood and just need a drink or some bar apps, I'd recommend this place as a warn weigh statin for weary H-Square travelers that would like to get away from tourists, or just anyone at all, since this place was mostly empty on a Saturday night.

Lows
The food. The volume of the food was really inconsistent, and as that relates to price, it simply made no sense. "Small plates" that were ordered (at marginally lower price points than entrees) were actually quite large, while entress (priced higher) were smaller than "small plates". Of course volume does not a price make, and I'm not correlating the two, just calling out that "small plates" are actually quite large, relative to the entrees- so it's a misnomer. 
Aside from quantity/volume, the normalizing factor was really taste, and everything tasted like air, across the board. Therefore, knowing that everything will taste like you're dining at a flavorless oxygen bar, I would recommend ordering yourself a small plate and saving a few bucks. 

I remember a few different establishments in this location since I left Boston, hoping this place can get some taste injected into food, near term, and survive the location's curse.

This place has a salad bar. But, rather than using tongs or a scoop to get the toppings and put it on the salad, they use their HANDS. Yes, they wear gloves. But they also use those gloves to open microwaves, open refrigerators, etc. I've asked them before to use tongs (like all other salad places), and they haven't done it. Seems very gross to me to stick your fingers into a bunch of tomatoes, etc.

Order this and you will love: small classic mexican with fajita vegetables with brown rice in bowl. Also, their new guac (from scratch) is delicious.

Boloco is a company with a nice personality. There's a lot of transparency when they change the menu and prices. Their promotions are creative and they appear to genuinely welcome feedback.

This is the best market I've ever been to.  They have an incredible variety of food and merchandise, the service is always friendly and fast.  I go nearly every day to pick up something I need and just to say hello to the owner.  A true pleasure!

The dinner is fantastic. Really unique takes on a variety of Indian dishes. That's the only way I can describe it right now - unique flavors. It's the kind of food you want to eat slowly and almost contemplate. Worth the extra expense if you make it a special event.

The Lunch buffet is a waste of time and money, though. Expensive, but nothing special at all. And some of the food was cold. Better to go somewhere else for Indian buffet. (Tantric in Boston has a good upscale lunch buffet, and Ghandi in Central square has a nice, relatively cheep buffet).

Very good pizza, but the burgers and other sandwiches are more 2/3 star material.  Still, they're always on time or early with delivery and are pretty generous with the side dish portions (fries, mozzarella sticks), which are very good.

Definitely recommend for pizza delivery.

So its Sunday. Like a real man, Im watching the Pats. Brady is god. But thats another story.

Anyways. I was stuck with my 2 girls. I get the kids every other weekend. Whatever. 

They wanted chinese food and under normal circumstances, Id make it. You see I lived in China for 3 years during the 80s opening factories for various clothing manufacturers. I learned to cook real good. You want fried rice - come to my house. On this particular occasion though, like I said, football was on. It was Brady time.

So i tell the girls - you pick the place and order what you want. That is the first thing I did wrong. The second thing I did wrong was to call the place. I called the wrong number. Ended up chatting with this guy for 45 minutes about occupy wallstreet. Interesting topic. 

I ordered Mongolian beef. It was supposed to come with a spring roll - but in its place I received crab rangoon. Great Im allergic to cream cheese. I fed these to my cats - they seemed to love em. But onto the main course - the mongolian beef. Like I said, I lived in China - I know mongolian food. My buddy over there - ming - he was monglian. Ming says that mongolian beef is served dry - and very spicy. NOT WITH FREAKIN CORN. I had to put ketchup all over it to make it even decent. And even then it wasn't very good. 

I also got soup. On this occasion I opted for Wonton Soup. I love this soup. When my wife left me, I literally lived on it. I was a wreck at the time and it was one of the only things that would make me happy. So needless to say, I was a little upset when I put the dang soup in my mouth. It was freaking burning hot. WTF. You got to put a warning on something that hot! It wasn't spicy hot, but the type of hot you get when you boil water. So my mouth was burned. Now I don't even know what the stuff tastes like. However, I could still tell based on the consistency of the wontons - everything was wrong. These were NOT hand-rolled wontons like traditional Chinese wontons I grew up with. They were meaty - and very little scallion or paprika.

My daughters loved the chicken nugets they got. i wish I could say that would mean good things - but these girls ONLY EAT CHICKEN NUGETS! I guess you get what you pay for when you are the 99%

We stopped in for a late Saturday lunch. We were greeted by a very cheerful hostess and promptly waited on by a friendly and helpful waitress. The bison burger was delicious, and large (we were  hungry, it was delicious, I ate every morsel).
We'll be back!

I have loved Boloco until recently, but I am not going ever again.

I reported an employee at Boloco Harvard who was apparently lazy and inattentive a while ago, and the company promptly responded to that. However, when I visited Boloco again, that employee recognized me and made a joke out of me. How could he know my identity? I am now worried if he put something weird into my burritos. I am not going to Boloco ever again. Period.

I recently ordered breakfast sandwiches and homefries for my friends and I on a Sunday morning, via Foodler.  We placed our order at 9am, were given a 10am delivery estimate..and nothing showed up until 10:45am.  I called at 10:20 to check on it, and the woman said that the deliver estimate was wrong, because they don't start delivering until 9am.  Umm, what?  Anyways, once I did get my order, they got it wrong.  I ordered four sandwiches, and received three.  I ordered two of the received sandwiches to be on Rye, and they were all on White.  I called and after expressing my irritation and explaining that we were missing a sandwich, the guy on the other line argues with me, and tells me that no, I got four sandwiches.  Dude, I'm looking at three sandwiches.  Ugh.  Never, ever again.
$10 (weekend) for lunch buffet is totally worth it. i'm surprised no one has mentioned the honeydew pudding thing for dessert, that was so addicting

I don't like chinese food in particular, but pretty good value on the lunch menu for Porter Sq.  Personally, I'd go to Shang-Sho because they have green and yellow jello.

Aptly named because you Wok in, and have to Roll yourself down the street after getting MSGWTFPWND.

I am very upset with this place right now. I ordered a pizza that was half cheese and half ham and mushroom. they delivered it and it was made as half ham and half mushroom. i called to ask for the correct order to be delivered and they told me they were sorry but they were "really busy" so they could maybe "give me a credit or something next time" and he hung up on me. so i called back and he said it would take 2 hrs for another pizza to get delivered. so i told him instead I wanted to buy a whole new pizza and how long would that take and he said 45 minutes. I said I would like to order a new pizza as if I were a whole different customer, and he hung up on me. I hate them and will never get a pizza here again.

By far the worst service I have ever experienced! 

Somewhere between the waitress being okay with potentially murdering us and lying to us I realized something was terribly wrong.

We asked for no cilantro in one of the dishes. She assured us that would be no problem. When the dish arrived there was clearly cilantro. When we told her this she acted like it was our fault and then proceeded to tell us that the cilantro is cooked into the dish ahead of time (so she clearly lied when she said she could do it without cilantro). She then tried to convince me that I should like cilantro. What if I had an allergy?!?!!?!? I asked her to please just bring us another piece of Na'an bread as we couldn't eat the cilantro. 

She was incredible offensive and when the bill arrived she still charged us for the uneaten dish which she removed from the table. When asked why she didn't remove is she made it seem like it was our fault for ordering and that we never asked to have it taken off the bill.

We insisted that the item be removed, so she went back, removed the item, and then added a 20% gratuity charge instead!!!! 

When we asked her why it was added after we asked to have the dish removed she said the manager did it, even though she was the only one there using the computer system.


So offensive. I warn you not to ever step foot in this restaurant.

Don't get me wrong, this place is pretty good... but given all the raves I've seen (Best of Boston, reviews in the Globe etc) I was expecting much more. It's not mind-blowing, innovative cuisine ... it's somewhat above-average Indian food. I could have given it 3 or 4 stars, but decided on 3 because this place needs to be taken down a peg.

The food's decent. Some dishes are awesome, some are just OK. On my most recent trip, we started out with saag aloo tikki--spinach and potato cakes served with tamarind sauce. The sauce was delicious. The cakes were pretty bland. For my entree I got Khumb Muttar Rizala--mushrooms and peas in curry. No complaints about that, it was delicious. My dining companion got the tandoori cauliflower--a huge bunch of cauliflower with yellow sauce, it was interesting to look at. Unfortunately the sauce had a weird taste. Garlic nan was decent, nothing special.

The service was competent, nothing more.

As you've probably read, it's in a basement. It's not like you're eating in a dungeon or anything, but they could have done more to brighten up the place and make you forget you're underground.

You'll get a perfectly acceptable Indian meal here, just don't expect amazing.
Great location, not bad food, good vibe, but a bad winter had to close.

Not as good as it was. The "koi" looked like trout and was smelly. We had to return it. The "luchi" was crisp instead of soft and fluffy, the lamb biryani was over cooked and the rice tastes mushy.

The "mishi doi"  (sweet yogurt) was great. The service was friendly and they replaced the "koi" with carp. Though it still did not taste that fresh..

The tauker dal (lentil with raw mango) was good and covered up the poor taste of the other dishes.

Really 3.5 stars, because of a less than impressive soup--too buttery and sweet for me. I especially liked the bread and side of grit cakes. The late-night service was great. Hopefully, if I skip the soup, I'll like this place even more on a return visit.

I went in for an early dinner. I happened to be the only customer, not a good sign I know, but I was in a hurry. Ordered a lamb saag and samosa. I heard them put the Samosa in the microwave. Get it a few minutes later, outside is hot, inside is ice cold. I tell them it's cold, they put it back in the microwave. I get it back, outside is MOLTEN hot, inside is lukewarm. 

I get the lamb saag, and the lamb is chewy and stringy. Ugh. Never again.

In my opinion, simply one of the best Indian restaurants in the Cambridge area. This small restaurant is the perfect place to taste some really great curries and tandoories in a very quiet and cozy setting. The classic chicken Tikka Massala and any Lamb curry are excellent choices. The service is really good and friendly. Even without a reservation, you don't usually need to wait for a long time, unless you go with a big group. Finally, the price is pretty reasonable, given the quality of the food. You can expect the check to be around $30-40 per person.
As said, if you happen to be around Harvard Square and want to try some Indian food, drop by this place! For me, the best Indian food around (maybe together with India Castle)

I know that I may be in the minority about Boloco. But I much prefer to them Anna's Taqueria. They have more flavor.

I usually avoid chains, but I like the fact that I can go to Boloco just about anywhere really. I love the Bangkok burrito and I get it with Tofu.

YUMMY!

This place is right down the street from where I used to work so I have tried it dozens of times for lunch and have never been impressed.

Order in, take out, doesn't matter, it always takes forever. More than once I witnessed the server/host arguing with the cook about something. I've never had an experience where my order was messed up, but there's no reason a place that promotes it's lunch (when people want to be in and out in an hour less) should take this long.

The food is nothing special. It's very cheap, but doesn't taste like anything you couldn't get at the supermarket pre-made.
The food is great, especially the chicken katsu. The only downside is that the water tastes like dish soap. Their other drinks are fine though.

I'd pre-ordered a roast beef sub and salad. The roast beef was mushy and lacked any substance. I had to thow out the sandwich. The salad was also not good. This market is only not getting 1 star because the market is fine but I will not get food at Harvard Market again.

What a hidden gem! This place makes you feel like you're in an actual, authentic Japanese restaurant. Being from Japan, I had high hopes. And they we're definitely met. I got the pork katusdon and shrimp tempura. 

Pork katsudon: Very yummy, could have had a little bit moire sauce but still really tasty and moist. 

Shrimp tempura: Pretty good!! But it's honestly just like any other tempura really so if you don't get it you're not missing out on much.

Our burgers were delicious!! We ordered Mac & Cheese balls and they came late and cold... however the waitress gave us our money back and free ice cream. Though the s'mores shake wasn't good the gesture was appreciated!! Great waitress and good service.

Boo-hoo Wok N Roll! I wanted cheap takeout when I didn't feel like cooking and thought you would be perfect. I walked into your restaurant and you asked if I wanted takeout. When I said yes, you walked into another room, leaving me to wonder if you were still open. 30 dollars and ten minutes later, I picked up my food and brought it home where I thought I would be able to enjoy it. But, you forgot the rice that I paid for and when I called, you asked "did you order it?" I did order it though and had to drive all the way back out to get it. You could have thrown in an egg roll or something but instead you gave me a small (I ordered a large) dry white rice. I was too tired to complain again. 

The food was okay, but not for the 30 dollars I paid for two entrees (one all vegetable) and one appetizer. The vegetable dumplings were good, but not unlike what you get at most other places. Changsho is much better if you are looking to spend more and Shanghai in Arlington is better for cheaper food.

Okay--I never made it to the table here, so I won't write a review of the food. Yesterday I went to the Royal Bangal to meet two friends for lunch. All three of us were coming from different locations and Central Square was convenient. On their website it said they opened at 11:30 for lunch. It said the same thing on the door when I arrived at 11:40 to see it closed and dark inside. A few minutes later someone came to the door and put a sign out that said they were opening at 12:30. When I inquired, the woman said "just today". While I waited for my friends to show, three other people approached the place for lunch, they also scratched their heads. Not good business. Especially since there are several other Indian places in Central Square. We went to one of them, and none of us will return to the Royal Bengal.

I just moved to Cambridge and I needed a Chinese takeout fix. Wok N Roll was nearby, had decent reviews, and delivered, so I placed an order:

House special chow foon with an order of wonton soup.

Maybe I've been let down by all the takeout Chinese I've had in the past. Maybe I underestimated the quality of food in Boston. Either way, I wasn't prepared for how good this food was. The chicken, beef, and shrimp in my chow foon were (gasp) REAL meat, not the spongey meal-like substance often found in takeout Chinese. The noodles, were fat, piping hot, cooked perfectly, and adequately sauced. Hell, even the $3 bowl of soup was good.

From what I can tell, Wok N Roll puts a little TLC into all the food they make, which is more than can be said about a lot of similar restaurants. I'll definitely eat again.

Will never order from here again. Breakfast was estimated to be delivered an hour later (which is ridiculous in itself), it took an hour and a half, and the food was stone cold when it finally showed up. I shouldn't have to re-toast my toast!

For comforting, familiar Indian food, I stick to my neighborhood joints whose reliable lunch buffets have fed hungry students for years.  Tamarind Bay is neither familiar nor reliable.  There are some familiar flavors, sure, but they are combined and presented in thoroughly surprising ways.  The seafood curries are particularly bold and tasty.  The creative dishes are served up in a small but gorgeously decorated basement-level dining room.

Unfortunately, the quality of service does not match the food.  My first visit to Tamarind Bay ended on a sour note when our waiter watched me sign the credit card slip (I know!), then berated me for being cheap.  I had left him 20%, generous for his incongruously mediocre service.  "Maybe he sucks at math," my friend shrugged as we left.  I like Tamarind Bay, but because that guy still works there, I can't give them four stars.
better and significantly cheaper sandwiches and accompaniments than Darwin's (next door) ... friendlier too ... recommend ...

If you want the usual, lite fare, you can get it...but I asked what was good that day and said that I liked authentic and hot. I got  a deliciously spicy kasha gosh. The goat was tender, perfectly seasoned, and generously portioned. Yum. I'd recommend it and will visit again.

Like Shrada A. (read her review), I wish there was a 0-star option. My experience was almost as horrible as hers. 

* The Service / Experience *

We walk into the restaurant, and we stand at the front for a full 3 minutes waiting to be seated, even though the restaurant is empty. The two employees at the register kind of glance over and continue their conversation.

The waitress comes up to us, and right of the bat asks, for here or to go? We say for here, and sit down. She then comes to us in a few minutes and says in an annoyed tone, "I thought you said to go." She rudely props two glasses on the table and gives a exasperated sigh.

The whole time the waitress has a look on her face like she just defaulted on her house loan, was hit by a pie on the way to work, and slapped by every customer that ate there today.

Every few minutes she'd come by the table...not to fill up water, but to eavesdrop!!! Can you believe this @#$@ ? She had a look like we had just murdered her children and she was trying to find out where we had hid the bodies so then she could kill us once she got that info.

** The Food **

Terrible. If the food has been even average, I may have given 1 star. It wasn't. 

Some of the most bland, uninspired trash I have ever eaten. I think I can get a better meal out of a barn trough. The potatoes were basically swimming in a curry that was water, and the cholay were served uncooked right out of the can, lukewarm and bland.

** Parting Ways **

The waitress literally throws the check on the table, without us even asking for it. I kind of wanted to get up right there and give her a piece of my mind, but I didn't want to ruin my mood for the bar we were going to next door...which by the way had been playing earsplitting music the whole time so loud the water in the glass was vibrating.

This is absolutely the worst treatment I have gotten in my life, at a restaurant or otherwise. This happened about a month ago but I didnt have the time to write. And I agree with Shradha, the free market needs to send this rathole a message.

Okay, so I went almost a year ago (December 2009) to Boston and was remembering the good ole times a few days ago. Then I remember the most horrible restaurant experience I've ever had...at Royal Bengal. 

Pretty much identical to Shradha A's review. I spent way too long getting mad at them for the TERRIBLE/RUDE/TOTALLY UNPROFESSIONAL service as well as the mediocre not-properly-cooked not-great-tasting food, but I forgot to write a review. Suffice it to say, I was shocked a restaurant that has that kind of service is in business. I would have gone into more detail but decided not to as recalling the experience makes my blood boil.

I hope the girl serving us was fired, though I have my doubts that she was the owner's daughter or something.

From the variety of higher-rated reviews on here since, it seems perhaps they have improved, but I would say if you're willing to try this, be prepared for a pretty crappy experience. If they've improved, good for them, and good for their customers. But from my experience, I wish I could give them 0 stars.

We were looking for takoyaki (squid balls) but didn't want to go deep into Boston.  We managed to find them at this small shop hidden in Porter Square.  The squid balls were nice and similar to the ones we had at other good Japanese places.  The service was friendly too.

TWO HOURS FOR DELIVERY IS NOT OKAY. Get your shit together and hire more drivers or lose your customers.

Specifically ignoring my number when I call a third time to check on my food is also not okay. The only reason I'm even giving this place one star is for all day delivery breakfast and fantastic smoothies. The delivery time used to consistently be an hour, still obnoxious but acceptable. Lately it's been stretching into an hour and a half or two hours and the clueless employees just lie again and again that the driver us "just ten minutes away." 

Whoever owns this place needs to pay attention to these reviews before they lose their business.

God--SETTLE THE FUCK DOWN. This place is a serious buzzkill. Nothing but tuts and moans from the PETIT BOURGEOISIE clientele (that's French...BUT OF COURSE I DON'T NEED TO TELL YOU CUMMERBUND FUCKS). I gave this place about five minutes until I let the gust of wind from everyone's sighs blow me out the joint. 

FUCK IT.

I wish my memory was better.  Every time I make the horrendous mistake of purchasing food from Dilara, I promise myself that I will never return, but somehow I eventually forget that promise.  I joined Yelp specifically to write this negative review so I will remember to not order food from this establishment.

Bottom line, the food here is not good.  The pizza is poorly executed greek style.  If you desperately desire the standard pizza/sub/sandwich/fried fare, you can get it at numerous better nearby locations like The Snack Bar or Scampa. 

Another major problem: anytime there is a major sporting event or a nearby festival, they have no capability to keep up with the food demand and the wait time grows into the hours.  This has happened to me three times over the past two years!

Never again.  Please, Yelp, help me to remember this!

I happen to love this burrito place because it's very easy to build your own healthy burrito. Unlike most burrito places in Boston, Boloco has whole-wheat tortillas, brown rice, tofu, and an assortment of vegetable options. You can request their pre-made recipe (my favorite is the Teriyaki chicken--and its presence on the menu should tip you off that this place is hardly Mexican), or they're very accommodating if you want to build your own (for this I highly, highly recommend the online-ordering option, though, because sometimes people in line irritated when you make a chain of off-menu commands). 

After a year of living in Harvard Square, Boloco became a staple because I knew I could get a relatively affordable dinner with a lot of relatively healthy things in it (as opposed to the 10,000 calorie burritos as Chipotle). I never had a bad experience with the service and I'm happy to know everything is clean.

By the way, my roommate made a contention last night that they shouldn't call them burritos since most of the pre-made options are simply wraps.  ("Why would you call a Caesar salad wrap a burrito?") She's probably right.

On Foodler, spring rolls say (1pc) but comes with two. 

My deliver came 10 minutes from the time I ordered. The food was piping hot and delicious

Definitely recommend

If I were to just rate the food, I would give Kennedy's a 4, or even a 4.5. Kennedy's has a great feel to it -- it's very classy and certainly an ideal Friday or Saturday night destination. The tables are all elevated and so you will either be seated on a booth-style wall bench or a raised stool chair. However, service matters a lot, no matter how good the food and the ambiance.

I visited Kennedy's on a Saturday evening in a group of 10. We had made a reservation but there were no spots when we arrived, perhaps attributable to this being the Head of the Charles weekend. When we were finally seated after a 45 minute wait, we ordered quickly with a waitress who seemed a bit impatient. When I asked about certain items on the menu, her explanations were curt and a bit matter-of-fact. 

The food did arrive quite quickly, and the staff did check in with us to see if there was anything we needed, though I did sense that they were also trying to serve us as quickly as possible to vacate our table. The food itself was great. I tried the mac and cheese, which was rich with a sharp cheese flavor, but not too heavy. (The crumb topping really helped balance out the texture and flavor.) I also had the beet salad, which was served with arugula and candied walnuts, and a piece of crispy goat cheese. The cheese was perhaps the most special -- it was battered and lightly fried, leaving it with the perfect balance of crispy and creamy. I tried a bit of the duck confit, which was moister than I expected. It wasn't quite as flavorful as I would have liked. The truffle fries were distinctly truffle-y in taste though the fries themselves were not particularly outstanding.

All in all, I would come back again to give the service a second try. I might not come back in a big group though.

I haven't ordered Chinese food in the Boston area since 2008.  There's a reason for that: it's all crap, and I visit home frequently enough (where my favorite Chinese spot is) to get a fix.

And yet, one night, after much hemming and hawing - Thai or Indian?  Sushi or pasta? - we settled on finding a good Chinese spot on Foodler.  It was down between Wok n' Roll and one other - Wok n' Roll won out on the sheer basis that it was closer to home (and thus, delivery would be faster).

Let me tell you, this folks do a nice General Gau.  Big, juicy pieces of chicken, the sauce is a bit oddly sticky but it's spicy so I'm over it.  The food tastes freshly made.  The hot & sour soup was also yummy, but skip the fried rice...it was just, well, sad.

I've not given up on my hometown favorite just yet, but Wok n' Roll is my new local pinch hitter.

Excellent menu that changes every few months. Of all the good food I have had here, the french fries that come with the burger or chicken sandwich is what always comes to mind. Calamari is really good. Nice lounge feel. Food presentation and service is outstanding and the bread is awesome!

Fries have changed! Unfortunately not that good anymore..... to bad.

The burger was alright but not memorable. (I don't even remember which one I ordered.) The shakes are cute, but the one I got (peanut butter) was very watery. I recommend Mr. Bartley's if you're looking for burgers in Harvard Square :)

This place is terrible, I went there with two friends, ordered a pizza and shared it with them and we were all completely sick all the night and the next day, it definitely might have had something terrible, probably rotten, in the ingredients, and was badly cooked. Besides this, it is very expensive: each had to pay 20 $ for a Cesar's salad, chicken wings, a pizza and a shared desert. Totally disproportionate for the quality and the ambience! 
   At one point of the meal they began promoting some game and put a person on the microphone at a very loud and annoying volume all over the place, even though there was a lot of people just eating and not interested on that. Overall, it was a terrible experience with the worst consequences. I definitely do not recommend this place and will never come back!

What a disappointment! I have ordered from there couple of years ago and their service and food wasn't as horrible a it was last night! Guess they've made some changes. 

I convinced my friend to order from this place and we ordered from foodler. We chose chicken teriyaki dinner which came with rice and a chicken stir fry dinner which was supposed to come with French fries. Both dinners were suppose to have hot sauce on them. We orders at about 10:25ish pm. It was already past 11:45pm and we had not received our order yet! So I call the store and the man who answered politely said they not had one driver so it was taking some time, which I was ok with but I just didn't want to receive cold food. 

The diver finally pulled up and I'm looking out my window watching him sit there and take his sweet time to call me. He finally calls and I said I'll be right there (I live on the first floor). I go downstairs (it's freezing) and this driver was still in his car!!!!!!! Did I mention his car was parked right in front of my door?! To have made me wait this whole time then make me come outside and stand in the cold while you are sitting cozy in your car? Unacceptable!!!!!! He finally gets out of the car and struggles to get my food out his car and into a plastic bag..a plastic bag? I was confused as well! I bring the food home and see that the brown paper bag the dinners were in was soaked with OIL along with the plastic bag he placed them in!!!!! They didn't even give utensils!!!!!!!!!! I was soooo disappointed !!! And only one dinner had the spicy sauce on it!!!!!!! 

I will never order from there again and I'm letting all my friends know in Cambridge about their bad service!

Surprise! This sketchy looking Chinese food joint with the bad name is actually really good and veggie friendly! Whodathunk?

They recently added a bunch of really yummy tofu options to the menu, which now include entrees like sesame tofu, General Gau's tofu, and spicy tofu with vegetables. Plus, their fried rice is vegetarian!! Amen.

came here for brunch with a large group, and they were able to accomodate us pretty well. they had an interesting brunch menu--regular items with a little twist, but they were a little too different for our tastes. The french toast was very good though and was huge--looked like almost half a loaf of bread. the home fries were also very good
Cut little place right in porter square. I came here a tad early and they still let me sit and serve me. Very nice staff and it's great a salad comes with the lunch!

I'd love to give them 5 stars for this location but I just cannot do it. The now defunct Davis Square location was just that much better.

Heaping on praise for the new addition to the menu, the Yucatan burrito.

So good with the pickled onions.

Try it.

I asked the owner if they put cocaine in their chicken-fried rice? He said no, otherwise, he would have to charge more than $5.89. I work right by Porter Square and I can't get enough of their rice especially with the hot pepper sauce on the side, yummy. And my supervisor is addicted to the vegatable-fried rice. Friendly and best rice in Porter!
I LOVE this place. WAY better than Felipe's/Anna's.   The Summer and Mediterranean Burritos are amazing.  Whoever invented  Mango Salsa is a god.

I LOVE this place! Their pizza is excellent and the pasta dishes are fresh and homemade. On nights when I don't have time to cook, Cafe Dilara is my go-to place. Staff are friendly, which is a desirable bonus to this little neighborhood cafe. I always opt for pickup, and my order is usually ready when expected. Highly recommended!

What will it take to get really good food in this location? First First Printer and now this place - it's a decent looking place but food is lackluster. Sat down on Valentines' Day, and we were greeted with a friendly "Please leave within an hour and a half." from the host. LOL. Way to kick off an evening.

Ordered the turkey burger which was dry and tasteless. Could have ordered a burger of rubber and it would have tasted just as good. The fries were yummy, and the waitress was friendly. Now please "up" the food!

I really love Indian food.  I came here a couple months ago to meet some friends for our "Try a new restaraunt every month" project.  They were running late, but the host was happy to seat me, and check in every once and a while.  My friends finally arrived, and we ordered Lemon Rice and lots of Naan while we searched the menu.

I had the Dalcha Murg (chicken with onions and yellow lentils) and it was amazing.  I can't find it anywhere else!

For a while I had this mixed up in my mind with the Chipotle** in Harvard Square and was like "huh, Boloco is really inconsistent, why is this location so bad" Then I heard great things about their Nutella shake and decided to give it another try, and this was the closest one, so tentatively, I headed to Harvard Square...

And realized that the mysteriously bad Boloco I'd been going to was a Chipotle. 

The actual Harvard Square Boloco is delicious. As it should be. I wish they'd sell breakfast burritos after 11, though, or at least make it obvious on their website or somewhere that this was the case -- ok, I've just realized that I'm seemingly destined to make a lot of burrito-related errors. I need a minute. 

Had the carnitas "summer" burrito. Flavors were excellent. Didn't realize the mango salsa wasn't going to be gloopy (which is a good thing!), or I'd have added guac or something to make it less dry. Nutella shake was good but I couldn't finish it -- the flavor is just too rich and nutty to get through a whole regular size. Next time I think I'm going to add strawberries and honey, which apparently you can do. Decadent.

All in all, a pretty great burrito place! Somehow they feel like less of a good deal than Anna's, though, even though the prices are only within like a dollar or two of each other. Maybe that's because Anna's also has cheaper stuff (tacos etc.) and because I'm not tempted into buying expensive delicious smoothies there. But both are great! They're usually not right across from each other a la Starbucks and Peets or CVS and Walgreens, so, no difficult choices! I can have it all!

**Note: I may be unfairly judging Chipotle too. The Harvard Square one is a little off though. It made me sick once. Don't go there, even if you think it's a Boloco.
A little piece of modest Japan cuisine tucked away in Boston. It reminds me of a small eatery that you'd find in a train station in Tokyo. Lovely staff, delicious food, and simply comforting.

Technically, if Yelp allowed half stars, 3.5 would be a more appropriate rating for me!

We came on the premise of having gift cards from the Yelp Elite event in January. Because my boyfriend and I had such a great time there, we definitely wanted to come back!  

We came on a Sunday afternoon where Kennedy's served up their brunch menu.  Their menu isn't exceptionally big, but rather offers specialty items that should appeal to general palettes.  We noted that specifically during brunch, all sandwiches come with a choice of a side, including: Hand cut fries, Truffle Fries, Sweet Potato fries, Mixed greens, or seasonal vegetable.  These sides are normally $6 on other days or if you want to purchase them a la carte.

We both ended up picking the Lobster Roll (Fresh Maine Lobster, Romaine Lettuce, Chipotle Orange Dressing, $20) and picked the obvious side of Truffle fries.  

The price point of $20 is pretty decent, especially when you can find other places charging upwards between $25-30 for a lobster roll.  The roll came out stuffed with knuckle and claw meat, had little bits of celery and small pieces of raw onion, and was dressed lightly.  I didn't particularly notice the chipotle or orange flavors peaking through, but it all tasted good regardless. I'm also a sucker for any type of truffle fries and these were pretty darn good.  I almost think, however, I liked the fries more than the lobster roll itself!

That being said, we had no problems with service, our glasses were never empty, and food was decent.  Parking in the area is already difficult on regular days, but add on layers upon layers of snow and it makes things even worse.  A parking lot is available nearby - we noticed that on this Sunday, they charged $5 per half hour with a $15 maximum.  

Kennedy's is a bit out of the way for us, therefore, although the food was pretty decent, it doesn't prompt me as a place that I'm dying to come back to, hence my true rating of 3.5 stars.

I've been wanting to order from this place for a while.  I like the menu and they are on GrubHub.  I finally ordered today and I was really disappointed.  The food was fine but the delivery showed up with pasta sauce all over everything in the bag.  The delivery driver didn't even make so much as an apology.  If you are going to offer delivery, then put the food in packaging that is appropriate for delivery.   

Luckily, I have lots of other choices because I won't be ordering from here again.

**Harvard Market Evangelist**

I cannot recommend Harvard Market highly enough. Seriously, I love this place.

I've lived nearby for the last three years, and go there three to four times a week. They have the BEST SANDWICHES in Harvard Square, PERIOD. They are definitely better than at Darwin's next door... the meat is better quality and they give you more of it, they heap on more vegetables... and if you get the Lunch Combo, they cost $5 with chips and a drink, (or yoghurt and a piece of fruit, etc), instead of $7-$8 for just the sandwich at Darwin's. As a poor graduate student, I've been to every last place in the Square, and Harvard Market has the best food for less than $7.

The owner of the place, Moe, really takes care of me... usually I just tell him to surprise me with any sandwich, and they are delicious every time. 

I cannot recommend this place highly enough.

Only one person working in the entire place who takes ten minutes to make a single sandwich. I tried to use a Living Social on my phone but the woman wouldn't even turn on the computer to scan it and pleaded the ignorance card. Horrible service and a waste of my time - will never return.

This place is literally the best katsu don place you can ever find in boston. I have visited Japan numerous times and I feel like this place is even better than those Ive tried in Tokyo. Must-try restaurant in Boston.
Nice little sandwich and lunch shop. Paid $7.50 for a sandwich though. Sadly, around Kendall Square during lunch, that is about right, if not slightly below average. Will go back again.

One of my friends owed an arcade and serived japanese curry there, sadly his place closed down, but I still get a craving for curry now and againg. Thanks to Tampopo, I can satify the is craving! One of my favorite spots to go to for chicken cutlet curry. The service is always friendly, and the food is always delicious. Also, try out the crocketts and the octopus balls. Great for a quick meal and small groups, and remember it's cash only!

Side note: they have updated their menus and added more delicious items to the mix. Look for the deal of the day! A different dish is on special every day, just find the arrow on the photos (a nerf arrow!)

Good Convenience Store.   Well priced and stocked. Deli and ATM.  

Only downside. If you pay with a Bank of America credit card, it takes about 2-3 minutes for it to clear.

If you're vacillating between this place and any other Indian joint in Harvard, just stop and enter this dungeon of goodness. Let me summarize the reviews for you: fantastic Indian food (might be best in the city) but questionable buffet. In doing so you'll encounter some unfamiliar, authentic dishes leaving you in the dangerous hands of decision. I myself am paralyzed by this and usually trust myself with a new menu about as much as an angry Eastern European with a grenade (being Czech I can say that). 

I am happy to say that out of the four of us at dinner, three having never been, choosing dishes to share family style turned out to be a wonderful, mutually beneficial idea as everything was fantastic and unique in its sauce which is often rare in Indian food. We had the fish with coconut curry, okra, lameez chicken tikka and chicken curry (that was two words and started with an M). The garlic naan was cooked damn fine and covered with a blanket of diced cloves.

Ambiance was modern Indian, though the Buddha head in the entrance confused me. (Cast off from Om down the way? Isn't India like less than point one Buddhist?) It was warm and intimate. I only dock a star because the service was slow (saying five minutes and ending up to be 20 and to take orders) and the waiter kept making weird comments pointedly at me. I think next time the man will have to come so we can PDA the whole meal through. Or hey, what are you doing next week?

The burgers are stupid delicious but when you get to dessert, get the damn freak frappe. If you're lucky, you'll be sitting at the bar when you do and the best bartender in the world, Alyssa, will be making it for you.

0% hunger is just after you completed a large meal.
100% is when you feel like you could eat right out of a dumpster.
~30-40% is dangerous for me because I'll skip or delay a meal and then completely stuff my face once I reach 70-80% hunger.  Boloco helps me work around this problem.

Boloco sells tasty burritos and fruit shakes in a variety of sizes including small and mini.  This means, I can grab some food when I'm at at that lower range of hunger (~30-40%) and not have to crawl out of the store in pain.  This is a good thing because I've limped out of Qdoba before and regretted the experience.

In addition to all this, the food is relatively healthy, the fruit shakes provide essential nutrients, and they have impressive green policies, all of which don't make you feel like you're ruining yourself or the world, just because you're hungry.
I don't leave Cambridge without visiting Harvard Market. I love, love, love the sandwiches (esp the harvard market special), the guys that work there are really sweet too.

This place is surprisingly good. I get takeout from here every 1-2 weeks. Their Shanghai pan fried noodles are delightful..  A great mix of slightly salty and savory. I owe thanks to the Yelper who recommended that dish!  Also worth trying: Moo Goo Gai Pan (straight up, classic), House Special Lo Mein, and the House Special Soup. 

Wok & Roll is a solid choice for basic Chinese food in Cambridge.

I've been putting off this review for a few weeks because I wanted to try to find a nice way to say this...

I've given up. Here is my (hopefully successful) attempt at civility. 

wish me luck...

I was home sick from work on a Monday and wanted to have some breakfast delivered. I hoped on the interwebs sauntered on over to foodler.com and placed an order with cambridge coffee shop at 10:00 am. Delivery time was estimated at 10:40. That's fine. 
At 10:10 I get an email from someone at foodler saying that they can't get in touch with CCS, should they keep trying? i reply that i'm not going anywhere, so sure! why not? 

WRONG.

They never got in touch with CCS. (foodler guy was super apologetic and awesome) I asked him to cancel my order, checked the CCS website to see if there were any problems, there was no indication that they were closed (actually, it said they open at 9am!) so I counted it as a loss and went about my foodless sick day. 

on Tuesday at 10:30 I received a phone call from an unknown number. I answered it and was informed that there was a technical issue and that my food would be late, but was on its way. 
At this point i'm understandably confused. I didn't order anything from anywhere on Tuesday... who could this be??? Oh! How delightful! CCS was calling me to tell me that my order would be 24 hours and 30 minutes late! 
I politely informed them that i placed no order on tuesday, i had placed one on monday that was ignored, and was no longer interested. He couldn't figure out what the problem was, then remembered that they had been CLOSED on monday. 
There was no notice! they were even accepting online delivery orders on their website!!! what a scam! 

I will never be taking my business here again. If you are going to be closed, don't try to scam sick girls out of their money and breakfast. be responsible and let people know.

I have to say, i like their pizza. I've ordered it 3 or 4 times in the last couple months and its really cheesy with a nice thick crust. Especially good with feta... mmm. I had many people over each time I ordered it and everyone seemed to like it.
I've had the Snack Shop pizza and it is thinner. I didn't like it as much.
Snack Shop is cheaper though. But unlike Dilara, Snack Shop doesn't  take credit/debit cards. A deciding factor for me.

Plus one star because they changed something about their pizza in the last 5 years and it's better now. Though they tend to over-reheat it and the bottom gets kind of charred and tastes bad. Minus one star for that terrible new tablet based checkout thing, it's so awkward and takes longer and I don't want to sign that thing with my finger after 50 other people.

The delivery service from Cambridge Coffee Shop is abysmal. I've ordered from here multiple times with friends (usually orders of more than $75) and never once has the food shown up on time. We've had experiences that range from "just" half an hour late to more than two hours late. Calling CCS to inquire on the status of your order is hopeless. It's always "already on its way". This isn't to say that they're rude--quite the opposite. The staff and delivery people are always courteous, they're just hopeless at getting you your food on time.
