When the food finally arrives it's a mixed bag. Their pizza is pretty good, if a touch salty, and their smoothies and froyos are always satisfying. I've been disappointed by their cold sandwiches, though.

If you must get food from Cambridge Coffee Shop do it in person. At least this way you can walk out when your food doesn't arrive and you don't have to be held hostage by their endlessly "about-to-arrive" delivery service.

Terrific burger, pleasant seating and staff.   Frappe/Shakes to die for.  Portions large.  Easy seating in bar stool raised tables.   Extensive burger choices, can try non-beef as well if that is your thing. Would go back. Much better than some of those chains that have become popular in the Boston area.

I wasn't all that impressed by the paper bowl of bland scrambled eggs, tomatoes, and fried potatoes I got here. I guess I was expecting more based on how nicely decorated and spacious the cafe was and how cute the chalkboard menu was written. The food and presentation was nothing exciting at all and probably not some place I would visit again especially since there are so many other great cafes around that area. Their espresso machine also wasn't working that day which probably didn't help my mood since it was early and raining.

It's a good place if you're looking for a spacious and clean place to sit for awhile since that's hard to come by when you're in Cambridge. It's just not that good of a place for food.

The first time I had their food I was mooching off of my roommate's bbq chicken pizza at 4am. I was impressed and devoured three slices. The next week I convinced my boyfriend that it was phenomenal, so we decided to give it a try ourselves. 

Not even close. Here's why...

- I live a couple of blocks away so after being told that pickup was not an option, we were forced to order delivery. It took over an hour to deliver 1/4 mile away.

-We received a flat, burnt, pizza that was made with old, dense dough. I say this because I used to work at a pizza place and I know the difference between fresh dough and old dough. This was OLD.

-The worst part of the experience was the "buffalo wings". They forgot the buffalo sauce so we had plain wings. Frustrating, but no big deal. The wings were okay at first, but the taste seemed a little off. We then realized a foul stench coming from the chicken. I inspected it to ensure that it was fully cooked, which it was, but the MEAT WAS ROTTEN! The best word to describe it: RANCID. We stopped eating it immediately and the only reason we kept it in our fridge was to hold it as evidence in case we woke up vomiting in the middle of the night. We called back to complain, but no answer. I didn't even care about getting the $24 back, I just wanted to make sure they knew what they were serving people so that no one else would get sick.

-The following day my boyfriend checked his bank statement and they charged him double! The funds were replaced quickly, but it still added to the overall frustration since we shouldn't have paid a dime in the first place.

NEVER AGAIN.

Friendly staff. Reliable, basic sandwiches. Minus a star because they are SLOW. I call in a tuna sandwich with pickles and they say "15 minutes" and mean it. are they catching the tuna? I go to the shop to pick up my lunch and there's no one dining, just two people slowly working behind the counter. 15 minutes??

But the old guy always gives me coffee without me asking, if he's just brewed a fresh pot. That's nice.

I FINALLY tried this place, which is just a hop skip & a jump away . . . I admit I only came 'cause I desperately needed coffee, and was too lazy to walk all the way to the 1369 Coffee House.

I was not disappointed! I got a good cup of strong industrial coffee, and it was CHEAP! And the person behind the counter was NICE! I ordered a chocolate muffin to take home to my hubby, and the woman asked me if I wanted it grilled with butter! Grilled with butter ~~ three of the loveliest words in the English language. :-) I just got the muffin as it was, however, and it was eaten with glee.

I would love to come back here sometime so I can sit at the counter and try something grilled with butter.
Spicy Indian food. Just as it should be. Good service. A little pricy for the quality of the place. Hard to find though, it is below ground.

They have evening parking available for patrons, but we didn't find it on our first drive by, so we ended up parking in a spot just a little ways down Mass Ave.  Given the choices of sitting outdoors or in, we elected to sit inside at one of their lower tables rather than at the tall tables by the bar area.

We were started off with a wire basket of 3 types of bread and some softened butter.  The first was an olive oil based roll that had hung out a touch too long in the oven and arrived black crusted, which I decided to break and brush off.  The second was a nut bread.  The third a brown bread sweetened with molasses.  All were warm. the brown bread the best of the bunch, but more dessert than bread.

We shared two appetizers:
. P.E.I. Mussels $10 - Slow-Roasted Fennel, Garlic, Shallots, Saffron-Orange Broth, Grilled Baguette
. Verrill Farms Corn Chowder $8 - Smoked Shiitake Mushroom, House-Cured Guanciale

The chowder was not the smooth sort, but was indeed naturally sweet.  The bacony pieces added smoke and texture.  The mussels were plump and tasty, and the baguette slices nicely crisp.

For entrees, we tried the:
. Grilled Atlantic Salmon* $21 - White Sweet Potato Mashed, Garlicky Broccolini, Sweet-n-Sour Rhubarb Ginger Glaze
. Grilled 14oz Porterhouse Cut Pork Chop $23 - Vermont Cheddar Stone Ground Grit Cake, Braised Greens, Grilled Peach Tasso Piperade

As always, the 4 legged protein is a giant beside the smaller piece of fish.  I didn't care for the glaze on the just cooked salmon, but I enjoyed the two sides that came with it.  The pork dish was nice though.

For desserts, we had the:
. Roasted Peaches & Cream Napoleon $9 - Crispy Hazelnut, Raspberry
. Pineapple Upside Down Cake $8 - Roasted Pineapple-Caramel Sauce, Vanilla Ice Cream

The cake was nice and moist, while the napoleon seemed to be thin oatmeal cookies with cream and cookies.  Both were nice, but neither anything special.

New silverware was provided for each course, but I really wish they would have wiped down the table inbetween courses.  Instead, my blackened bun crumbs and spilt mussel broth languished on the table the entire evening.  Also, I'm not a coffee drinker, but my friend is, so it would have been nice if they had asked us if we wanted anything when they brought the desserts over, if not earlier.

I just tried the lunch buffet here for the first time on the recommendation of some friends -- my office is right around the corner but I'd avoided this place because of some of the reviews here. I'm happy to say that at least as far as the lunch buffet is concerned, I like what they have to offer. They have everything you commonly see at Indian buffet (naan, yogurt raita, mint sauce, tamarind sauce, chutney, rice, chicken tikka, tandoori chicken, aloo gobi) but also some more interesting stuff that apparently gets rotated in and out. They had a very nice curry with hardboiled eggs, which is a dish I always want to try when I see them in recipe books but are kind of rare at restaurants.

Plus there's free masala tea.

I'm not sure I'd particularly recommend this over other buffets in the area but it is certainly good in its own right and a nice change of pace if you find yourself going to the same Indian buffet over and over.

Some of the best food I ever had. I had the vegetable sampler and it was so lovely to find a vegetarian three course meal already put together intead of having to search the menu for the one vegetarian dish or something one could possible take away the meat from. The dishes were superb, so many great flavors mixed together. My only complaint (if you could even call it that) is that the dessert portion was very big; a smaller portion would have better completed the meal instead of leaving me full to the brim (and yet I left about half of it). All in all I definately recommend this place. The service was good and fast and the food was excellent!

I won't be coming here anymore. Place is gross inside, the prices somehow became sky high, and the service is sooooo sloooooow.

I will walk all the way to Inman for coffee from now on.

This place was PACKED. It's only open during lunch time and it has a big following. The line is usually out the door but don't worry it moves fast. The have a large selection of soups, sandwiches, salads and wraps. Be sure to ask what the special is because it rotates daily. And it's usually the best thing on the menu for the day. I ordered the special and today it was spicey chicken with rice and beans and a salad. The food comes mass prepared so the scoop it out from under the heat lamp and into a to go container. This place is built for speed. The chicken was very good. It was cooked and juicy. The rice and beans were also very yummy as well. You'll also have to get lucky if you want to be able to sit here, don't worry as tables turn over fast tho. Give this place a shot if in the area.

For some reason everyone I knew hated this place, so I avoided it. I finally went and really enjoyed it. 

They do get kind of funky with their burritos, nothing wrong with that. You can keep it basic, traditional and they are delish. I like it here because I like peppers and onions in my burrito and they've got 'em! My only complaint really, is that they ran out of what I wanted several times, though it was later in the day. Still frustrating, especially since they didn't have a sign up or anything like that and I proceeded to order.

They have pretty great shakes tooooooo.

While searching yelp for a decent indian restaurant that didn't involve a trek out to Allston, I came upon Tamarind Bay in Harvard Square.  We arrived to dinner on a saturday night to find a warm, inviting and very full dining room.  Despite this, we were seated almost immediately.   As the space is small, the tables were close together- this didn't matter so much as we were starving and happy to be out of the cold.  My boyfriend and I eat Indian food often, and were surprised to find that the menu did not contain any of the vegetarian 'standards' (Chana Masala, Saag Paneer, Aloo Muttar...). We welcomed the opportunity to try something different and ordered the PANEER E SHOLA. (Indian cheese with green pepper and onion in red chilly & tomato sauce) and SHAAHI BAIGAN (Eggplant cooked in rich cream & cashew gravy.)  The paneer dish we ordered spicy and the eggplant dish was served mild which created a nice contrast - along with some nan.  The food was delicious and really stood out from the standard indian fare that is served at so many other (indian) restaurants.  Music would have added to our experience-  but other than that, we really enjoyed dinner here.
Interesting burger combinations. Fries were good. They have a nacho and pork appetizer that's yummy. Wish we had this chain back home. Go!

I love this location and want this place to succeed. Finally stopped by for lunch to see how the new place is. Nice but I can see why it's so empty. Was looking forward to tucking into a delicious salad and expected a meal, given the $19 price point. I got a fistful of leaves, a thimble of dressing, and a small piece of meat. The food was fine, not great. I'm not a big eater, but I will leave this expensive meal hungry today. I'm in the food biz myself so I know how important food cost controls are, but this is beyond acceptable.

I'm giving this place one more star than my instinct is telling me to, on the off chance I just had a really bad first experience and that I might still be feeling the heat from WAITING 2 HOURS FOR MY DELIVERY. After almost an hour and a half I called and asked what was going on. "We're doing some stuff and then we'll get it over to you". Huh? No problem... fit me in whenever you get a chance, I guess.
Once my food arrived, right before I ate my own arm, I saw that the extra side of dressing I asked (and paid) for wasn't in there. Maybe they just put extra dressing on the sandwich, it's hard to say because 90% of the dressing had absorbed into the crutons in the 2 hours the sandwich had been sitting there waiting to be delivered.
Though I'm not pleased I spent 25 hard earned dollars here, it at least paid for a good amount of food, so it's got cheapness going for it.

Real Japanese kitchen! The people working there is lovely, works fast and they received us earlier than official open time. What can I say about the food? I have had so delicious takoyakis only in Osaka. The korokke are crispy in the outside and pure cream inside. Everything is amazing and great prices! Try also the veggie tempura and the potato salad.

Great local sandwich shop!  While visiting for a conference, I convinced a handful of co-workers to go here instead of one of the many mediocre chain sandwich shops in Kendall. Let's just say they were all very happy with their decision to walk a little further. Fresh ingredients, wide variety of choices, good portions for the price. My chicken salad sandwich was excellent. And the only reason I'm giving 4 stars instead of 5 is because the guy making my sandwich just assumed that I only wanted lettuce and tomato,  no dressing or other toppings and I had to interrupt him to ask for honey mustard and sprouts.

It's very possible that I went on an off evening, when the regular chefs were away.  I had dinner there Thursday early evening July 31, 2008.  When I placed my order, I heard a lot of chatter in the kitchen; I suspect the cooks didn't have the ingredients or knowledge of how to cook the entree.  I ordered Calamari Kostimbir, calamiri cooked with carrot, spring onions, tomato with light coriander & lemon dressing.  Well, the calamari was on the plate. But no light dressing, only a thick, salty and spicy sauce.  No coriander, no lemon, no carrot, no onion, maybe a sliver of tomato.  I also ordered lemon rice, which was OK.  I've tasted worse and tasted much better.  Not much lemon flavor.  The Shahi nan I ordered, which is supposed to have cashews and raisins, was edible, but I sure couldn't taste any cashews or raisins, just sweetness.

A lot of people give this place excellent reviews, so I really may have gone on a day when the regular staff were away.  I don't think I'll go there again, tho.  The server came by at least twice to ask if I liked the food, with a look of shock on her face, when I nodded my head each time.

I ate at Boloco Harvard this evening and the guys working there were so nice!  The menu looked like it had a good variety of food genres represented.  I had the Mexican Burrito with fajita vegetables and it was amazing.  Place was clean and atmosphere was pleasant.  Great location right around the corner from the Harvard T stop.  I'm crazy for burritos so I'm looking forward to next time!

This place is clean and pretty efficient. Ultimately you have to like the taste of what you get--they make a good classic mexican with carnitas and guac. 

I had the tropi-kale smoothie here. Good concept but tastes a bit too green/healthy for me. Had the milkshake (make your own) with mango and strawberry at a sister boloco--that was excellent!

Rule of thumb--you never want a mini (unless you're forcing yourself to drink tropi-kales to be healthy) but small in burrito or drink is more than enough

Good location--in the sense that it's quiet/slightly off the beaten path, but close to everything.

Would go back
They get 4 stars for the awesome hummus and veggie wrap, and the very friendly staff.  Unfortunately that's the only vegan option on the menu, some other options would be nice.

Wow is there even a way to give a 0-star!? We went here last night for dinner, during my visit to Boston. I'm from Bengal and was so excited to have Bengali food. 

I had been here a few years ago and my brother and I both had upset stomachs for 2 days. But I believe in second chances. No one will believe what happened last night though. I wish I had a video camera!

We ordered alu dam, chholar daal, luchi and paneer tikka - very typical Bengali fare. The luchi (bread) dough seemed stale because it had already soured. We thought well it seems most people here order Naan (another bread) so maybe that's safer. We requested that our luchi's didn't taste very fresh, could we please have naan instead. 

The waiter says - it's not my problem if you don't like the food. VERBATIM!

So we say well could we have some naan. He said he doesn't know. The guests at the table next to us leaned over and said, what does that mean!? 

The waiter just walked away. My brother asked him if the manager or owner were around. The waiter says he doesn't know and if he did, he doesn't care to tell. 

We were finally able to speak with the owner, who came out boiling with anger, and shouts: IF YOU DON'T LIKE MY FOOD, JUST PAY AND GET OUT OF MY RESTAURANT. 

every single person in the restaurant was dumb-struck. Our jaws dropped to the floor. We even tried speaking in Bengali to try and understand what the issue was. He seemed adamant that we either eat what's already been served or leave - even though we offered to pay for the old order and the new order, to make sure he didn't think we were trying to get free food. 

We actually just did pay for all our orders, the ones untouched on the table, the ones that hadn't even arrived at the table - the owner then threatened to call the cops on my brother saying he would claim my brother was drunk - we're Hindu and don't even ever drink!

I know this seems unbelievable right? This happened just last night!!! And I can't believe a restaurant like this exists. It redefines what the "hospitality industry" means. The capitalist system needs to have this place shut down! The owner thinks he's doing us a favor by serving us food, rather than respecting his customers and being thankful that they come to eat at his restaurant. 

I've never ever been this disrespected in my life - and I feel helpless because I can't take any action, but then, the world has much bigger problems to solve and my time is better spent solving the diabetes epidemic in this country than thinking about an uneducated hot-headed restaurateur.

Great lunch every I've been there.  I have sampled across the selection of sandwiches, soups, salads, entrees, and pizza. There is always a line for food around lunch but they staff sufficiently to move it along quickly. The seating is limited but many take their food to go. Great job, I'm sure I will be back.
Cool atmosphere, nice crowd.  The mixed drinks are pretty good, if a bit expensive.  The food is pretty killer as well if you're in it for late-night snacks, but I haven't yet tried the full menu.

This place is a decent convenience store as far as selection goes.  The one lady who is ALWAYS working is terrible though.  I've come in at 9:30 (they close at 10) and she refused to give me anything from the deli.  She's always talking on her cellphone, never even says hi, and is usually in the back until you literally make eye contact with her and stare her down, making sure you know she is hiding from you.  Eye-rolling is constant, lines are always long, and the whole thing is terrible when she is working.  The other workers seem nice enough, but going to Harvard Market is like playing Russian Roulette with five bullets in the chamber.  One time out of six, you get something that isnt absolutely terrible.

Solid Japanese fare. This is the kind of thing where if you're craving some decent curry or soba or maybe a sukiyaki plate, you won't be let down. Nothing was particulary amazing to me but definitely a great place to grab a bite off the beaten path.

3.5 stars!  Good place to grab a bite (breakfast or lunch) if you work or live in the area. I most enjoy the BLMT (BLT with fresh mozzarella). I wouldn't go out of my way to go here, however its because of the location, not because of the food.  There  is such a variety of other places in technology square,  even just on the same block; if this restaurant was located in a more remote place or in the burbs ... I would go out of my way to go here.  Like the other review say, it's very clean, fresh food with lots of options.  Very consistent.
My roommate got  rare fast acting form of food poisoning from this place...NO GOOD

This place creeps me out. I live a block from this "coffee shop" and when I first moved here I was so psyched for an all-day breakfast place! Yesss!! Nothing better than all day breakfast!! And they deliver breakfast on those mornings I'm too hung over to leave the house!! Yessss!!

Ugh. I've had consistently terrible service when eating in here. 20+ minute waits for eggs over easy and homefries. I used to stop in and buy a coffee occasionally on my way to the bus stop until the counter dude started shaking the tip basket at me. And 2 delivery mishaps in which they forgot to bring something I ordered. 

The last time I ordered delivery from this place, it was delivered by an older man. After I grabbed my food from him and closed the front door, I walked up the stairs and looked behind me, and the dude had his face pressed to the window of the door, grinning. I was wearing a semi-short skirt. He immediately left after I saw him, but the dude was totally looking at my ass !!! So fucking creepy. I can't walk by this place now without feeling completely grossed out.
My favorite restaurant ever the food is so good and the workers are so nice it is cash only but the delicious food makes up for it

Still in discovery mode of the place but I would say a little gem in Cambridge.
Food is excellent ( 3 visits) so far and service perfect.
My only advice for owner, some portions size are little too small

Okay this place is a GEM! The flavors that come out of the dishes that Kendall Kitchen makes are to die for. I've gotten everything from sandwiches to salads to soups and never been disappointed. Soups conquer all though! I've had the Black Bean, Spicy Chicken Tortilla, Chili, Lemon Chicken, Kielbasa, and Tomato soup and each one is the best version of that soup I've ever had! Even if the soups sound a little adventurous, I still go for it because I know the flavor combinations were carefully chosen to provide the best flavors.

The prices are insanely affordable! You can get a large soup for $5, sandwiches for ~$8 which is well under the average meal in the Kendall Square area. This place gets slammed around lunch time but do not be intimidated, the line moves fast! They are a well oiled machine and split the sandwich line as one and everything else in another, that way if all you need is a soup (can you tell I like their soup?) you don't get stuck behind sandwich people. Oh and did I mention they rotate their menu pretty much every day?! 

Highly recommend for an affordable, quick, and delicious meal!
Great, family run ambience with the best tasting, fresh ingredients! Absolutely floored by this place!

Sesame tofu. Really? Yay.
I tried this place for the first time today and was pretty happy with it. They have a few tofu/veg options so I'm excited to try more.
$7 for lunch with one appetizer & rice. Sounds good.

Finished bartending and came home exhausted, starving and sleepy. Jumped on foodler to order delivery, expecting it to arrive after I cleaned up and changed. Well that was OVER TWO HOURS AGO. It is now 5:30AM, I haven't gone to sleep because when I called 40 minutes ago, someone told me that the order was with the driver and should arrive soon. Thanks but ..it hasn't!!! Tried to call back- no answer and a full voicemail box (shocker). Now- even more exhausted and starving, not to mention pissed the **** off, and calling it quits! I wish I could give zero stars and use profanity, because seriously, EFF you Cafe Diarrhea. I used to live nearby in Cambridge and have gone here plenty of times, but I will NEVER order from you again. profanityprofanityprofanity *$**@#%% YOU guys.

Went with a friend who knew the chef, so we explored every part of the menu! From the appetizers to the ramen to the desserts, everything was so good! Felt like I was in someone's home kitchen. Will definitely be going back next time I'm in the mood for comfort food.

We tried Base Crave for the first time this week. It was fantastic!  It was (and still is) COVID-19 season, so we did "Curbside Pickup", as the dining room was closed. 

We had vegetable dumplings, the grilled chicken entree, and something else ... I forget its name now but it was noodles with shrimp. 

I'm not a "foodie", but I love tasty food.  This hit the mark. It was great first experience.

I've gone here twice now, as it is right down the street from my house. The service is great, and it is in a convenient location. 
I went with some of my family and all of the people working there were so nice! A family owned restaurant, and has a great vibe. They were efficient and kind. We started off with the Chicken and Paneer & Spinach Mo Mos and the Sweet Potato Chhoila. So good!!! The Mango Lassis there are to die for! They are amazing! I've gotten the Stir Fry Noodles and the Cashew Haddock Curry and both were absolutely delicious. I definitely recommend. It is such a great place!
Small venue inside a college campus mall area (?) We ordered the curry and it was okay! I would say it's good to try once!

I absolutely loved Base Crave. The drive was a little over an hour, after I previously sent my daughter here when she was traveling to meet a client. Worth noting, it was a scenic drive and Huron Avenue has some great shops. First, the restaurant took all COVID precautions, and the meal did not disappoint. We had vegetarian Mo Mo,  Gundruk soup, and Eggplant Basil Paneer. To say that the paneer is the best I have ever had is an understatement. The soup, unique and delicious - I want to learn how to make it, or version of it. If I lived in the area, this place would be my go to at least twice a week. Can't wait until next trip to Cambridge to visit this gem.

Monday, Jan 6, we had a delightful dinner at The Melting Pot, the new restaurant in the former Full Moon space.  The interior has been tastefully redecorated, and there are new tables and (very comfortable!) chairs,  The menu is Nepali, which is similar to Indian -- we had Dhal Soup, Spinach & Cheese Mou-Mous (dumplings), and Chicken and Mushroom Curry.  Everything was delicious.

They are in the midst of their "soft opening" -- they are currently unable to take credit cards, so bring cash (or a check).

For all the hype about this place I thought it was pretty underwhelming. It also gets expensive pretty fast if you want to try a burger, milkshake and fries as all are only sold separately and are each such an ordeal that they are practically a meal within themselves.

The milkshakes were delicious but had so many toppings and other chunks of dessert popping out of them that you could easily be stuffed from a milkshake alone. They cost around $10 and there are no smaller versions available.

The burgers themselves were average, cooked well but not super flavorful and nothing to rave about. I tried the king burger and was surprised at how much I liked peanut butter on a burger and the caramelized banana was delicious but there was very little peanut butter and bananas on top. In comparison some of the burgers that others in my party got (namely the 420 and the killer bee) were practically drowning in toppings.

Finally the fries....they have these big servings of decked out fries with super creative toppings like the bruschetta fries with tomatoes and pesto or pizza fries with pepperoni and cheese. I was super excited for the BBC sweets fries I ordered which are basically sweet potato fries doused in caramel and cinnamon. I could tell they would have been phenomenal but the execution was a huge disappointment. The fries were very undercooked and lacked a proper dipping sauce to go with them (obviously not the kind of thing that would go with ketchup). They left me craving something better.

In conclusion I thought this place was creative with bold ideas but sadly such poor execution that I'm not looking to go back anytime soon, sorry Boston Burger Company!

The place has a cheesy name and looks like a dive, but when I ducked in for dinner on the evening of Christmas Day, the service was excellent and fast and the food was quite good.  Then again, the place was almost empty, so perhaps the quality degrades when it's more busy.  Still, I'd certainly be willing to go again.

We used to come here when it was The First Printer.  We went last night and it is so nice!!!  The atmosphere was amazing, the Kennedy family pictures just fascinating.  I had a "Fox Rose", which was delicious and we go the Buffalo Chicken Wontons.  Karen was our bartender and she is fantastic!  I really like the additional appetizers and small plates being offered, and the new 70" TV added to the bar?  Vast improvement.  We are fans for sure and will be back.  Thanks!

Excellent new spot on Huron ave.

Slightly less family friendly than full moon and a totally different kind of food - Nepali vs standard American - but the food is excellent.

The interior will be recognizeable in layout but different colors - darker - and missing a play area.

The momo - called steamy dumplings - range in price from $10-$12 and come 8 to an order. They are excellent - delicately folded wrappers, well seasoned filling. The lamb, wild boar and chicken all contain juicy pockets of shredded meat and are hard to stop eating.

An order of bread is puffy, buttery and has a sauce similar to aji verde, only slightly sweeter.

Chicken choila ($13) is a mixture of chopped chicken mixed with vegetables in a curry like sauce highlighted by cumin and chili flavors - it is not spicy. It comes with puffed rice to mix in.

Gundruck soup is a deep red, soy based soup that is fiery and thick.

Dal  lentil soup is rich, also toothy.

The lamb and mushroom curry entree ($25) is ordered at spice level 10. This will be too much for the casual rusticator who likes to dabble in levels of heat. What's amazing about the dish is that the mouth never is so hot that food can't be tasted. However, The heat builds and soon sweat is dripping. The lamb is plentiful, the curry is a combination of nogh jolopia peppers, cumin and other spices sizzling around carrots, zucchini, jalapeno peppers, onions

Wild boar ribs are piled on rice and ordered at spice level 4. Sweet meat pulls easily from the bone. The meat is mild in flavor but moist. Served with an almost sticky curry, they are excellent.

Chicken noodle stir fry comes mixed with kale, green peppers and a pungent aroma of cumin and light pepper. Theee are not greasy noodles, they are cooked well and the seasoning sticks to the noodles well.

An excellent place. Delicious food. Friendly service.

I usually have a good experience here but must be off day. I had the spicy chicken kage and the old guy cooked. It was definitely undercooked as it was way too tender. I looked and sure enough, saw some pinkness in center. I only ate the outer shell of it, paid and left. I'm willing to give them another chance but was very suprised today.

We came here for lunch/ brunch with friends. We shared dressed up fries to start, which were delicious. 

My experience was not a very good one, however. They have 2 all gender bathrooms but no changing table. I asked the staff about a nearest changing table . . . no luck. 
By the time I found one in a different business, changed the baby, and came back, food had arrived and my burger was cold and soggy. 
For a restaurant of this size and newness that serves brunch, I was surprised by the lack of a changing table.

My husband and I came here to watch the game a few weeks ago. We grabbed seats at the bar and ordered a few beers and the Inferno fries. They were so good! They weren't really 'in your face' spicy but had a kick to them.

The sandwiches here are very good! A very good quick stop to grab and go, however, make sure you pay attention as they are cashing you out! 
First they didn't give me enough change back until I corrected them, and looking at the receipt they charged me for 6 sandwiches at $6.50 when I only ordered 5 sandwiches at $6. So, just pay attention!

Hi,
I rarely go to the Temple Bar because I usually eat in the Square or other areas in Cambridge.

Anyhow I've been there for brunch and it's been really good!

Recently though, I had the Bronzini! And it was delicious! I'm hoping to go back again to have it before the menu changes up again. Check it out!

New place with nice decor. Decent sized dining area with scattering of 2 to 4 seats with some larger tables. Fits around 40 I guess. Street parking easy but we went around 2 so not sure if it would be more difficult during lunch time. 

Food time:
- Calamari Grilled Small Plate has this delicious blend of spices that really perks up the taste buds. They let you choose spiciness level from 1-10 so  just mention which level your comfortable at. The calamari is sliced into smaller chunks but heavily marinated so its an explosion of flavor in every bite. The vegetable are crisp and gives a good crunch. The rice puffs are crunchy and gives the dish a good texture. Its not a big plate so really just small snack for sharing. 

- Haddock Curry Lunch Entree comes with basmati rice. The rice wasn't as aromatic as I expected but the curry was actually very good. The curry sauce is very condensed so expect the flavors to be intense. I picked spice level 6 and it was just right for me. There was respectable amount of haddock chunks in there but I was more impressed with the crispness of the veggies and how well everything came together. Needs more mint sauce though.

- Bread was extra $3 but was worth it. It isn't heavily flavored but came out nice and toasty and dipped in the mint sauce is incredible. 

Overall great lunch spot and definitely will come back to try other dishes. FYI, outside no signage yet as they're still new so you might miss it.

I wish I took pictures of everything, but my lunch dates dug into their meals too quickly. 
I got the Southwest salad and I added chicken. The salad was huge!! Incredibly fresh ingredients and loaded with flavorful shredded chicken. It had a minimal amount of salad dressing- my personal preference- so if you like a lot of dressing ask for for more. 
My aunt got a Greek salad with chicken added. Generous amount of feta cheese and kalamata olives, grape tomatoes (5), green peppers, and banana peppers. More salad dressing than mine had which she enjoyed.
My husband got a chicken pesto wrap- generous chicken but not much pesto flavor. 
We also got a hot chicken cordon bleu sandwich- plenty of ham & cheese in it.
The chicken is the same in all the sandwiches and salads we had- that's a good thing because it was delicious! 
The coffee was very good too.
Solid move, can't go wrong with these guys.  A quick in and out that leaves you satisfied :D

I would recommend this place to anyone looking for an excellent Chinese food experience. Big portions of high quality hot food. Found it with a Google search. So glad we took a chance on it. Impressed my wife, who is a very picky Chinese food fan. Will definitely go back whenever we can.

I hadn't been in here for a few years, and it is much better than I remembered. They have sandwiches that are better than Darwin's for less money. They use high quality bread now (which I don't eat, because I can't eat gluten, but they were fine with letting me get a bread-less reuben.) They have lots of great take away eats like a chick pea and cranberry salad, macaroni salad, etc. It's the best place to get a cheap lunch in the area.
They are open even after lunch hours. Service was very nice and they were very patient. Price is reasonable and there are many authentic options! Worthwhile coming

A Hidden Jewel in Kendall Square...

Coming from a foodie, that statement meas a lot.  Kendall Kitchen's food elevates homestyle-cooking to a new level.  Hratch's (the owner) dedication to customer satisfaction, far exceeds most in the restaurant business.  The variety of fresh food items, made on the premises, is enough to please anyone's palette - no matter how discriminating it may be...  From homemade soups, like mom used to make, to the best meatball subs in town, you just can't miss.  If you don't see something you like, just ask.  Hratch and his staff are always friendly and willing to step outside the box in order to make sure your experience is a pleasant one.  I hope that you take the time to experience this gem of a place.  You will surely be rewarded!

DIlara is still up and running, unfortunately.   I ordered a pizza, the delivery was quick which was a plus.  However, when I opened the pizza box, the chicken was burnt/black and was not in fact the pizza I ordered.  Upon calling them to rectify the situation I was told they would "call their HR on Monday and have it investigated" and then I was promptly hung up on. Though I understand it is late night pizza, I think an integral part of running a restaurant/food delivery business is to be able to provide what the customer actually orders.  If you want to be hung up on after paying money for something you didn't order that is also sub par, order here I suppose...

I really wanted to like First Printer, because I liked the space and the drink menu, but it always seemed to come up short. From a lackluster valentine's day prix fixe experience, to being randomly thrown in the vault sitting adjacent to your friend joining you for dinner, First Printer seemed to always let me down somewhere. But now, Kennedy's on the Square is in town, and it is a HUGE improvement. Everything has been changed from the spartan tables and menu of First Printer to warm, comfy red diner-like booths and an expanded (and honestly much better value) menu at Kennedy's. Everything about our visit was excellent, including the friendly waitstaff and the food. I ordered the arugula and goat cheese salad, and the mac n' cheese with ritz cracker crust. Both were fantastic, and a great duo for a meal. I was also impressed by the well-priced sandwiches available for dinner, as well as the extensive cocktail and beer menu. I will definitely be coming back, but I just hope the secret doesn't get out, as I imagine it will soon be difficult to get reservations at Kennedy's on a Friday night.

Omg .. love their veggie burger..

even the chicken burger is delicious too ..

A must try

By far one of the WORST Indian restaurant experiences of my life and Indian is one of my favorite foods so I've tried plenty. The atmosphere seemed nice but that is where it ended. First this place is outrageously expensive!!! And for a small portion and rice is not even complimentary. To make it worse, when the waiter asked if we wanted rice and we mentioned we assumed that it would already come with it, he let out the most pompous laugh. The Naan was dry and flat not buttery and flaky, absolutely disappointing. Our tikka masala chicken, that we had requested spicy, was watery and bland and un-fulfilling. This place is way over priced and the food is no where near tasty or worth the money and the service was rude. Sad we stumbled upon this disgrace in Harvard square after an amazing meal at Ambassador Indian Restaurant in Woburn just last week.

A new restaurant, just opened this week and what a great addition to the neighborhood! Delicious, healthy Nepalese food. Great portions and something for everyone. Best mango lassi I've ever had. Family run, great vibe, beautiful and comfortable setting. Can't wait to go back and try other dishes. Highly recommend the Momos (steamed dumplings) and sweet potato chiolla. We all tried different entrees and were pleased with our selections. Highly recommend!!
I was pleased with everything. Service and Staff was very welcoming. Food & Drinks was Delicious & the kids loved their Shake

Service was lacking. Server had to fix order twice after bring the wrong food to the table. Not enforcing masks on customers. The burgers were okay nothing special for the price. We ordered the fried chicken pesto and the burger bomb. Would not go back.
Amazing momos and curries - everything tastes fresh, made to order, and delicious! Honestly, felt like I could've gotten this at a restaurant in Kathmandu or Pokhara - authentic, amazing Nepali food!

It is inconceivable to me that this Boston Burger Company is rated less than 5 stars. I had lost the "dinner debate", (I wanted sushi) but boyyyyyy am I glad I did.
To start with, our waitress Cassie was a star, she was helpful, had perfect timing and was sweet to boot! Seemed like the other waitstaff serving tables around us were equally awesome! Now for the meat of it: THE FOOD

What we ordered:
-cannoli freak frappe
-bbc salad (hold the roasted peppers)
-garlic parm "wings"
-bruschetta fries

Portions so abundant that I was too stuffed to even get to the wings!  Everything was incredible. For me the highlight was the Bruschetta fries, but honestly every bite on my plate was better than the last! My 2 friends agreed with me, as our dinner conversation became mostly just "omgggg yummms" 

I loved the fresh, beads of garlic over the fries and wings- reminded me of warriors/giants games growing up in San Francisco- GARLIC FTW!!!!!

This was my third time going here and the food is good but the service is TERRIBLE. The waitresses have no manners and can barely do the check correctly. The staff is rude and never brings the right utensils and drinks. The kitchen was visibly dirty and for that reason I will never come back! The staff doesn't help other tables but their own (not even to alert our waitress) and act like they don't see you when you need something.

The grilled goat special is phenomenal, along with the perfectly textured and juicy momo (got the lamb and wild boar). Will need to get an extra side of rice next time for the grilled goat to soak up all the extra sauce

1 star for the food itself. Came in last night w my friend that has a gluten and nut allergy (legit)-and asked over the phone if they could accommodate prior to arrival. The girl said yes, so we came in. We notified the server and manager of the allergy and ended up ordering a jumbo burger adding pineapple to it, w gluten free bun. The burger came, totally made incorrectly, with the wrong sides  and missing bacon on it (um hello?) so I asked the server if it was suppose to have bacon on it or maybe they mixed it in to the meat itself? I don't eat pork, so wanted to be sure. What was her response ? Ummm I don't know if there was suppose to be bacon it would be there. She stood there dumbfounded as if I had 10 heads. I asked her again and she still gave me the same answer-whatever. 5 minutes later she came back to tell me that the cook told her he forgot to put the bacon on it..so when I asked if they can add it, she said oh I thought u didn't want it anymore? Like what the actual what???? Then she brings out a plate of chips(still the wrong side) and the missing pineapple and tells my friend that  she can't have them they aren't gluten free and walks away.

What a total waste, and shame-should've gone to Davis sq and not deal with such stupidity! They didn't even bother apologizing or removing the up charged items we didn't get.

All the hipster yuppies on yelp are down about this place. But it's a great place to get some greasy Italian food. I went on a dining 50% off certificate and glad that I did. The Linguine with vegetables hit the sweet spot, as did the lasagna with all its warm gooey cheese-ness. I know, not a word. I look forward to trying the pizza!

Had an absolutely delicious meal at Melting Pot last night.  And it is also a gluten-free person's dream.  95% of the menu is gluten free. The menu items are clearly marked with a GF, and the kitchen takes special care.  

Our table had the lamb and mushroom curry, the goat special, and the chicken & mushroom curry.  And the non-gluten-free eaters had the Mo Mo's - beautifully created homemade dumplings - in chicken and paneer & spinach.  They were amazing !

The staff and owners couldn't be more friendly and warm.  This is a wonderful addition to the Huron Village/Cambridge, Mass. neighborhood.

I used to LOVE Cambridge Coffee and Pizza shop but something has gone wrong - they screw something up (without fail) every single time we order something. It's nice to order breakfast and have it delivered but there have GOT to be other places out there (or we'll just cook ourselves). That's it - no more of this darned place.

WOW!  The food here is fantastic, so is the service and the wine and beer options.  This is in the space that used to be Full Moon in Huron Village.  There seems to have been some confusion about the name but there is no confusion about the quality of the food.  It is as good as it gets.  We will be back, frequently I am sure.

While the food is pretty good, I was not impressed by the fact that they don't take reservations. I went here on a Monday night for a birthday and we had to wait 40 minutes for a table for 5. You can't wait anywhere inside and it was freezing cold out, plus it can be hard to find parking nearby, so we had to walk all the way back to the car and sit there until our table was ready. If we didn't have a car to wait in it would have been an even worse experience. I'm not sure why there are still any sit down restaurants that don't take reservations at all, especially during COVID when reservations and appointments are useful and required by many businesses. I enjoyed the food and shakes we got, but I don't really think it's worth it for the extravagant price. Several members of our party had burgers that were undercooked to their specifications, they looked the same as the one person's that ordered theirs less cooked. Other than that, the staff was nice and safety protocols were being followed.

Finally something a little different from usual menus. This little gem that we just discovered yesterday, is simply amazing. It had been a while since last time I really came out of a restaurant thinking "this was superlative". Amazing flavours, nice presentations and the owner is super nice. If you are in the area, you should stop by, also to help local business that otherwise will be wiped out by the current situation.
If they allowed more stars, I would totally give it to them!

Kendall Kitchen! Our number one favorite go to place for lunch and breakfast (on the weekends! and especially when we want a fun meal out!)  We have been so lucky to find Kendall Kitchen in our 3rd year living in Cambridge. And especially during this pandemic, what a life saver it's been. It's true that good, savory food helps with the soul! I especially want to give a warm hug (since it's really not allowed during Covid) to Isabel who treats us like family. She's so friendly, warm, and kind, as well as her co-workers, Carolina and Felipe. We also had the honor to meet the owner and he is as wonderful as the food, his employees and entire restaurant. They will continue to provide smiles and a happy belly for my husband, myself, and our 3-year old daughter. Every day when we pass Kendall Kitchen on the way to school, our daughter says "Mama, I have a great idea. Let's have Kendall Kitchen for dinner tonight!" ;-) Thank you so much Kendall Kitchen. we're so lucky we found you!  (p.s we would recommend the Quesadilla, Breakfast items and Chicken Salad! YUM)

Hot summer day needed a cold drink. Went in to find a rude unkempt disheveled looking girl who threw money at me because I asked her for $1 in quarters as change. Very rude and terrible treatment. Stay away!

My wife and I went looking for the reasonably reliable yet quirky Full Moon and stumbled upon the successor in the Huron Ave space.  Base Crave brought together the very best Nepali food this side of Katmandou by a family of kind and welcoming hosts.   

We chose a selection of dishes including the savory and layered flavors of the Gundruk soup followed by the vegetarian noodles, vegetable momos and lamb momo curry - enough rib sticking food for two meals.  

Beer and wine was available but the list could be better.  

Definitely the best new restaurant I've encountered in a long while.

This is a great addition to Harvard Square. Cheaper than Russell House, with much more fun theme! 

I had a Moscow Mule to drink, which was great. But the real excitement were the deviled eggs and the Duck Panini. Both were absolutely delicious!

Service was fast and very friendly. Great date spot with the pictures!

Came here during lunch break, ordered basil shrimp and tofu noodles. This food has absolutely spectacular along with the service! Even after we received they called 10 minutes later to see how everything was! 10/10 absolutely coming back here again!!

This place is perfect!! We got the chicken curry and chili chicken with a side of lamb momo. The meats tasted so fresh. This is honestly one of the best restaurants I've been to in the area. I'm so excited they opened up so close by! They also have a cute outdoor seating area on the street. Highly recommend!

Decided to delve into some thin crust pizza before a concert at The Sinclair. Lo-and-behold this place across the street. A small but fairly interesting beer selection. Overall the place was very packed but didn't necessitate any wait times. We ended up getting a salad each and we split a pizza. My grilled chicken salad was fresh with just the right amount of dressing - subtle and tasty. My compadre had the arugula salad which was unfortunately somewhat dry and bland - not recommended. The pizza was delicious. Perfect crust (I love thin crust) and just the right balance of spices and a generous heap of grilled chicken on top. A nice pizza place to sit down at and take a load off.

Rode my bike 15 miles and wanted to treat myself to my beloved onion bagel from Bruegger's.  Of all the local bagel places, Bruegger's is the best.  So, why the two stars?  Wellll......

I entered this establishment through their perpetually heavy and very dirty door and stepped inside.

Their AC wasn't working when I went in yesterday, which made this bagel place quite toasty.  I ordered an onion bagel.  They said they did not have any onion bagels.  At a place that makes BAGELS.  They had like 2 sad looking poppy bagels and then I smelled smoke...looked at BAGELS ON FIRE in their little toaster oven.  The girl who told me they had no onion bagels was oblivious to the BAGELS ON FIRE.  I nicely said, "Um, the bagels are on fire.  I see flames.."  and pointed to the BAGELS ON FIRE behind her.  She shrugged and pulled them out with tongs and they were extra crispy lemme tell you.  Black as night.  I think she was going to still give them to the people who ordered but I decided at that point not to wait around to order my bagel (which I did hear in the kitchen would be ready in 6 minutes) it was HOT AS HELL in there and I decided to cut my losses and go to Shaw's and get a banana.  It's healthier and well, I was going to go to Panera but two hobos were standing outside of Panera swiling a HUGE bottle of vodka.  

Yeah.  I still think Bruegger's has great bagels.  When they actually HAVE them.  Of course, I'll be back because I do like their bagels. I just want you to know what it takes to get one.  

PS - I went to this location a few days ago and noticed that yet again they did not put the bagel that is TO GO in a bag.  If people want their bagels and cream cheese TO GO, please for the love of GOD put the bagel, cream cheese, napkin and knife in a @$^%#@^^ bag!   I do not understand this practice of not putting it in a mutha@%^^&%# bag.

Tupelo has been one of our favorite restaurants since moving to Cambridge. In the past, we would have given 5 stars easily. This latest trip however, was somewhat disappointing. 

My parents were in town and I thought this would be the perfect place to take them since they love Diners Drive-Ins and Dives. We called to make a reservation well in advance, but we reduced the reservation from 6 to 4 at the last minute. However, they didn't move the additional two-seat table very far away from the four-seater, so we had to uncomfortably squeeze between the tables (where people were already seated at the other table) to get into our seats. My dad's seat was also pushed against the side of the steps to the bathroom, so he was quite uncomfortable throughout the meal.

I'd been raving to them about the pork belly corndog - which is apparently no longer on the menu! The server seemed to be surprised that we didn't know it had been taken off in the spring. It was on the menu every time we went previously. I don't know why they would remove such a popular item after having it for so long (and Guy eating it on the show).

The water we were served was room temperature and we had to ask for ice. They also don't serve any kind of diet soda, which seems odd. The jerk chicken wing appetizer (which we ordered in place of the missing corndog) was ok, but not nearly spicy or flavorful enough to earn the name "jerk." The fried chicken was incredible as usual and we enjoyed the rainbow trout. However, the pork loin was pretty bland and nothing special. 

We were very disappointed in our visit this time. I can't say we won't be back (that fried chicken just calls my name sometimes), but I doubt this will be a place we take visitors in the future.
Fresh bagels. Quick service. Lots of cream cheese flavors. Bagels are reasonably crisp on outside while nicely doughy on the inside. Fresh coffee and very convenient.

You will like the Cantab if: 
-dive bars are your thing
-you enjoy bluegrass/folky/soul types of music and/or poetry slams
-a true diversity of clientele makes you happy
-beers and whiskey are pretty much enough for you
-you like a really, really casual atmosphere

You will not like the Cantab if: 
-you need your bars to have any of the following things: 50+ beers on tap, dress codes, swanky decor, squeaky-clean surfaces, a wine selection, 22-year old drunk girls in high heels and tube tops 
-you are more comfortable hanging out with people just like you
-you find anyone over the age of 35 in a bar to be creepy

The Cantab is amazing. With great music, completely chill atmosphere, incredible range of people who frequent it, and nice staff members...I freakin LOVE this place!

Last night I attended the singer-songwriter open mic for the first time and it inspired me to finally write a review for this place which is one of the most interesting spots in the Boston area. This is a singer-songwriter night that isn't full of angsty, emo ballads sung by local undergrads...it's a mecca for older, more seasoned musicians who are guitar-playin masters and are clearly a big happy family. It was a great experience and I can't wait to go back.

My other experiences of the Cantab include late-night drunken dancing, mid-day chill drinking, and early evening pre-partying. It's always a great spot. It's not for everyone, as you can see from some of the reviews, but it's absolutely for me.

Best meal in my 3 days in Boston. Well seasoned, fresh, bright and energizing after long days of walking around town. I love that I got a balanced amount of vegetables and grains. Wish they had a branch in California... :)

I never knew what a dive bar was until I came into this place. Everyone is friendly, laid back, they only accept cash, it sometimes smells of pee, there's always live music, and the patrons are in general above 40 years old. Save for the usual small group of young folks that show up for fun.

On Mondays is amateur night and you get to hear a mixture of a bunch of bad musicians sprinkled with some amazing ones that appear rarely and seemingly out of the blue. 

The staff is very friendly and there are a lot of characters working there. Sort of like a sitcom. The nights when the professional bands are playing it's possible to dance and have fun and the crowd is usually dancing. 

Good times!

One of the best places to go see a concert EVER.  They always have a great line up and some impressive names.  I've seen the Hot Hot Heat play here like 3 times.  They also encourage local band to come play, my friends play there every once in a while downstairs and they put on a really good show.  I've never been for food, only for rocking.

Worst staff in all of Cambridge. Be careful of the bartenders particular the red head. He is a racist and prejudice against people  in different social-economic classes. He made us uncomfortable for the lifestyle we chose. Which is surprising in Cambridge.

C3 is a decent spot to grab a good coffee and a bite if you are around Central. The space is a bit lacking in character, which can serve you well if you really do just want to sit down for a bit to eat/drink without the usual log jam of coffee shop artistes, unpublished authors, literary critics and other academic ne'er do wells.

So it is a bit dull (well, relatively... This is Central Sq still), but it isn't completely soulless (i.e. Charbucks). If it didn't have overly tall ceilings and inhabit a corridor heading into the Co-op it would probably end up with another star. 

So if you are solo or just want to grab a drink and a bite it is perfectly fine. If you are looking for a cozy spot to sit and have a coffee with someone, you would probably want to walk a couple of blocks in either direction along Mass Ave instead.

You know what I realized the other day? There are no good juice bars between Harvard and downtown. Maybe I am not looking hard enough, but I mean juice bars - not coffee and juice, or muffin and juice. Just juice. What happened to the guy in the Harvard Sq T Dan Grabauskas??

So when I ventured in to CCC with the idea that they might have some sort of magic potion to clear up my brush with swine flu (that thankfully never evolved), I found a short list of smoothies that looked like that might fit the bill. My friend tried the Strawberry Banana Mint and I tried the Pineapple Carrot Ginger. All yummy and nutrition packed and definitely felt better. We also split a vegetarian sandwich which was small, but tasty.

My small blip is with the service (as it seems to be with everyone else). I got another smoothie this morning (I swear it's NOT swine flu) and the woman, while smiling, definitely smiled with attitude - the kind that says thanks for coming but we know you secretly nosh on greasy bacon every other day of the week (which couldn't be further from the truth - I don't even eat it), but you get the idea. The space is nice but the service needs a little oomph and less hummph.

Lou, Lou, Lou Best Cheese Fries and Chicken Sand which ever!! My fiance took me to Angelo's on one of our first dates, this could be the reason I fell in love with him. You have to get the cheese fries and the chicken sandwich- don't forget the bottled cream soda!!!

This is a great place, especially for lunch, since the service is very quick. Their menu consists of basic salas and a selection of a dozen or so flatbreads/ very thin pizza that come in half or whole portions (half is good for a person) and they also serve alcohol. Try the black forest ham and spicey peppers or the portabella scallion one. The only thing I dont get is why everyone eats these thigns with a steak knife and fork. Its a flatbread..use your hands!

Not bad for chain pizza, but no where near as good as nearby Pinnochio's.  Slices cost a little more than one would expect from a quick mall-based store.  The guys who work there at night sometimes play Ozzy videos on the flat-screen tv.

I absolutely love this place! I've only been there Thursdays and Fridays, but since my first visit there hasn't been a Friday in Cambridge that I haven't at least thought about this place. There are two words only: Genuine Fun! Yes, it is cash only! Yes, it is dark! Yes, the staff is not going to lick you a..! Yes, it has a cover! Yes, it gets crowded sometimes! And these are just some of the things that make this place one of the few places around that is as authentic as it gets... If you're looking for a fancy, clean, neat, well-mannered place - that's not it! If you're looking for some real fun, good live music and real people - don't miss the Cantab! :)

Love going to bgood for lunch in between classes or during exam weeks. There are so many healthy choices. The veggie burgers are also really good! If you havent tried their dark chocolate coconut smoothie, i highly recommend it!

This place is awesome.  They have a great lineup of more unusual music than you will find at most other establishments.  They used to have a great open mike night too, real talent would come out...its been a while though since i went, but if they still have it you should check it out.

Great choice for a sleazy night out.  I must admit, my recent excursion here was only after my initial resistance.  I preferred to stay somewhere nicer with the girls and dance to better music.   But I do not regret it at all! For a night out where you just don't care, this is the place to go.  No need to dress up, you can show up in your jeans and tshirt and dance your little butt off to covers of some of the greatest soul and R&B songs known.  Just don't look too closely at the band, you'll realize they are 60 if they are a day and come equipped with their very own Stevie Wonder impersonator (I am not making this up).  

An added bonus: if you get sick of dancing, take a break and watch the colorful cast of characters roaming this place.  It starts at the door where the two semi-retired bouncers will confuse you with their differing perspectives of upstairs versus donwstairs pricing and entertainment.

Sure, it's crazy.  But it's the annoying Dr. Demento brand of crazy, not the cool Dennis Hopper kind.

The crust are just downright repugnant, particularly the sicilian style slices which have the consistency of fried dough that got left under the couch and discovered five months later.  The toppings, however, do offer a shot at redemption.  Bacon and potato, or buffalo chicken are some intriguing options, and are quite tasty even if they have the consistency of a teething ring.

I'd say walk the extra block and get a slice at Pinnochio's.  The flavors may be more traditional, but the pizza itself is just head and shoulders above this stuff.

There's a lot of hype here, but it's a definite hipster pizza joint. What else can you really call it. Perfecto for a date, but would you really go here with some friends for a night out drinking? I can't see it. It's expensive...and it's just pizza. Let's ease up here. The atmosphere is nice, the waitstaff is fine, the food is pretty good, but it's not incredible in any way, but your date will be impressed that you're a hipster.

What a waste of a good location! I really liked the design of the place for a pizza joint, and the Sicialian style pizza was ok, considering the creep behind the counter heated it for all of 20 seconds.
The worst part was the God awful, blasting weirdo music, designed to keep anyone from wanting to sit up there while Mr. Wonderful was busy with his little prep work.
It boggles my mind that an owner will shell out big bucks to rent here, and then have an anti-social person behind the counter. 
I ate and the one other person did and got the hell out of there.

This is my first yelp review but I felt compelled. I was excited by the prospect of good pizza that delivers. Our pizza came cold; the box was actually damp. We could tell that the ingredients they used were good but, perhaps because it had been sitting around a while, they looked very unappetizing. I was actually planning to call to complain but before I got a chance, the person who took my order called me and told me my pizza was ready. He got me confused with another patron by the same name. When the confusion was cleared up, I told him that we weren't happy with the pizza. He responded in a barely understandable way...something about putting a note in the file. I expected something more...a sincere apology would have been a good start and perhaps something to make it up, a discount or free pizza. We will not be returning to Fiorella's.

great price and good food!

great pizzas, wings, onion rings, fries!  soo reasonably priced.  nice staff.

mainly caters to the students there, closed when school is out etc.  it's full of kids at 2:30pm sharp.  avoid going at that time!

I usually stop in to grab a sandwich and coffee.  The iced coffee is great and the sandwiches are fresh and delicious!  My favorite is the grilled PBH (peanut butter, honey, banana - yum!).

My only qualm about the place is the service.  They get backed up easily, and maybe someone was new, but changing the type of bread for my sandwich was a big deal on one occasion.  I still give them 4 stars because I love the cafe vibe and the diversity of the customers.

So I said to my friend, "I have a random German guy visiting me for a week and tonight I'd like to take him out to pizza.  But not just pizza, you know, I want pizza, plus...plus something...something extra."

And my friend declared, "Mein freund, CAMBRIDGE 1!"

It was wonderful.  Great, modern atmosphere which was packed to the gills with people.  You'll definitely have to wait, but they manage crowds well.  On a Friday night at 7:30 PM, our group of three had to wait only 20 minutes and even snagged seats at the bar for beers for the wait.  Nestled in Harvard Square, it's a great place to take people from out of town and easily has something on the menu for everyone.

When we reached our seats, placed our orders, and quickly got our pizzas, we were in heaven.  Charcoal grilled, thin crust pizzas: portobello, roasted onion and asiago for me (killer); italian sausage, ricotta, tomato and fontina for my friend (wowwy, she said); and hot cherry peppers, black forest ham and manchego for our German acquaintance (he seriously dug it).  Our waitress was great.  The house red was great.  All around, great.

The moral of the story is I'm pretty sure any one can enjoy Cambridge 1, which is a something I previously thought was only possible to say about smiles, rainbows, and kittens.  So go enjoy!  Danke!

Great atmosphere and service, though I'm surprised and sad to say that the lobster pizza (the most expensive on the menu) was sopping wet with oil, barely had scraps of lobster and was generally tasteless and overall, a big old Meh.

This location has CLOSED!

I have been going to this location for 10+ years, and I have not had very many bad experiences. I will miss popping in for an iced coffee and bagel, and I will certainly  miss a few of the staff members I have come to know.  So very sorry to hear this is no longer operating at the Porter location.

SETTING: me and my date, a Friday around 9pm - we were seated right away but the restaurant was full except for the bar.  Ambiance was cozy (warm tones) and immediately welcoming.    

WHAT TO GET: 
APP: turkey meatball, French bread app.  
MAIN: try the amazing etouffee (my Southern date said one of the best he'd ever had) and if there's the Fried Chicken special with cheddar grits and greens - get it.  We took home leftovers, portions are generous.
SWEET: pecan pie a la mode (Bourbon ice cream anyone?).  
DRINK: If you like spicy get the Devil in Disguise - bloody mary spin and so good I had two.  Good selection of Southern beers (Abita).

WHEN TO GO: Anytime you want to impress someone from the South; first date; any date; anyone who likes unpretentious quality food.

ALL IN ALL: a great night out with fantastic food.  The only thing that could have made it better - if the waiter had a Southern accent

I've eaten here a few times before and thought it was pretty good.  I love the layout of the place and the feeling like I'm in a diner on The Jetsons.  Every staff member I've ever encountered has been solicitous and appeared genuinely warm.  In the past, I enjoyed a breakfast at night, and the tempeh sandwich.  I loved that actually.

But tonight, ugh.  I urge you, if you are tempted to try the open faced seitan sandwich, pick ANYTHING else.  It looks like the proverbial poop on a shingle.  And it doesn't taste sp good.  Several hours later, I don't feel so good.  I ate less than half.  When the server asked me how it was, all I could muster up was, "It's different."   Literally.  They way they served it was not as described on the menu.  The mustard baked beans which are listed as a side were splashed across the piles of unappetizing brown, making it unappetizinger browner.  

I still feel bloated and like I ate super unhealthily prepared food.  There's another place in Central Square that steams veggies in interesting combos and their menu urges you to notice how you feel after eating a meal there.  I always leave there feeling energized and refueled.

The way I am right now, I feel like I won't be back to Veggie Galaxy.

Tupelo is an excellent "comfort-food" location in Inman Square.  What I love:

1)  The cornbread!
2)  The hushpuppies!
3)  Brunch has a great menu
4)  Catfish Po-Boy
5)  Jalapeno Mac&Cheese

The service is usually pretty good and they do their best to get you a table as soon as possible.  The hours here are pretty skimpy, so when I go I always plan ahead.

I almost always over-order here.  The food just fills you up so much.
I love Clover so, so much! I never feel guilty eating their food-- all fresh ingredients! Happy to see more locations! And great hours!

OMG!!! My mouth was instantly watering as I was reading the menu. I came on a Friday night around 10pm, it looked busy but since I was by myself I sat at the bar pretty quickly. Watching the cooks make food, and everything looked amazing. The server was nice, I asked her what she recommended she offered a few popular choices. I went with the chick'n & waffles and I'm in LOVE! I also got a Big Ass Mimosa (its alright). Massachusetts did not disappoint on their variety of vegan options! I can't wait to be back & try something new.

Healthy option in the area surrounding Harvard. Order up front, pay, and they call you once your food is ready. Low key joint overall. 
Really enjoyed my meal here at B.Good. Got the spicy avocado and lime bowl. The serving is really generous; lots of grains, lots of avo, and yummy beans and corn! The topping sauce was so delicious. It's definitely not overly spicy but it does have a ton of flavor and a good kick. It was really not busy when I visited so I was served promptly! I'd highly recommend for anyone! Especially if you're a little more health conscious.

Dive bar, yes, but it's the best. The bathroom stinks, the celing, well, kinda looks like my cellar, but when I walk in the door i get the feeling, Wow, this is the kind of place where legends are born. Great music, and a great bitter old staff that wil not take any sh**! Bored on a week night, it's more than worth a trip, you will not be dissapointed!

I like Crazy Doughs concept. The pizza looks like it could be good. The problem is that it's been sitting out for hours. It's crusty and the cheese is way past it's prime. I work in Harvard Square and want to love this place, but the last few times have been inedible.

Special place to eat on an everyday basis. Food is super high-qual and fresh. Workers are friendly and create a happy environment. This is my favorite place to eat in Harvard Square. The bowls are so healthy, filling, and flavorful. 10 out of 10 recommend.

This place is a disaster both in terms of food preparation and customer service. I work in HS, I go with my colleagues to B.Good for lunch. Cashier that takes orders is in the world of her own. She doesn't listen, can't repeat or place an order. In fact, the service is so bad, that we now have to bet if the food arrives as ordered. Hamburgers are cold and not grilled to order. I once waited for a smoothie for over 10 min while there was no one in the space. Employees seem distracted and unorganized. Oh, and B.Good customer service is unresponsive. I sent messages several times, and I only heard from them once, promising to forward me inquiry to a manager. General advice is: avoid at all costs.

Literally I have no words to describe my experience dining at this place.
To begin with it's a very very very very very dirty place.
I doubt the quality of food. Had grape leaves and still don't know what to expect from this meal in a couple of hours. Btw, it's written that it goes with lamb and beef, but it's actually some ground meat that it is barely noticeable among rice( the ground meat is  far from lamb for sure).
And of course the music.. ok, it's fine for some time, but guys, playing almost same shit for entire evening is annoying.

I really like this place a lot! Its so much fun to go with a group of girls and just hang out and dance together.  The bar staff is adorable and super sweet but I wish the lady who seems to be in charge would smile more, she always seems really grumpy.  I wish I could remember more about why I like it but my check in my purse said I had 7 drinks so I'm assuming I should give them 5 stars.. Also not horribly over expensive.

After being rejected from another restaurant down the street because they were "closing early for cleaning" or whatever, Fiorella's took us in and fed us. And we weren't disappointed. We came in maybe 25 minutes before closing and we felt so bad. But we are from out of town, without a car, and this was within walking distance of our place so we tried it and we loved it. 

The food was just overall really good. The calamari is just as expected, crisp and flavorful. I also got the shrimp scampi and it was so yummy and filling. It's a pretty big portion in my opinion. Definitely bang for your buck.

I've only been here once (last summer) and I cannot assure you'll have as much fun as I did. 
It's really not a fancy bar; as a matter of fact, I would describe as a dive bar. On the top floor (street level) they had a blues band playing, which I have to admit, was beyond fantastic. After drooling over how great they were, we decided we wanted to check out what was going on in the basement. The basement looked abandoned, but I guess it was just really supposed to be a dance floor. The DJ was this 80 year old man wearing a tie dye shirt and a trucker cap, spinning under a banner that read "Turntable-Bill's". Yup. I shit you not... Surprisingly, he was playing all the radio hits! I even requested "Sexy Back" by JT thinking he wouldn't have it (this is when it first came out) and to my surprise, not only did he have it, but he jumped down the stage (one step) to the dance floor and danced with my friend and I. 
On a scale from 1 to 10, I gave the night an 8.5!

Started coming here about 2 months ago and keep coming back. This is one of those rare places where you can taste the energy and care the workers put into the food...almost like a home cooked meal. The food comes fast, but they take their time with it. Its help that you get to interact with workers more with them than some comparable places. The franchise runs a good local operation in boston and you leave feeling like they care about their customers.

Prices seem steep until you see portion sizes, and also they have alot of giveaways if you use their app. I often get a bowl and save half for dinner.

I love diners, but as a vegetarian, my menu choices are usually limited. So for me, Veggie Galaxy is a dream come true. Not only is everything meatless, but the menu offers tons of options that are both tasty and interesting. I'm especially fond of the omelettes. There are five off-the-rack models (you can, of course, build a custom omelette to your specifications), incorporating unusual ingredients like apple salsa, tempeh chorizo, and brussels sprouts. And somehow, those exotic choices actually work!

Plus, it's a super-friendly place, and the waits for weekend brunch are much shorter than they are down the street at Zoe's. Even if you aren't vegetarian, give Veggie Galaxy a try. It really is something else.
I love the chickpea platter with pita bread! I always get it because it never fails to impress! Great food! Highly recommend!

Heh. So apparently they have music there ...

I went on a Thursday night when it was just a restaurant. If I'm being polite, I'd call it cozy in there. I'm not quite sure how to describe the cuisine, a mix of mideastern and latin influences. The tapas were abundant -- 2 per person was a bit too much food, even.

The caipirinhas are nice, they go down like water (though of course they hit harder -- if not careful, you could easily drink 'til you pass out in your falafel). The mojito was just okay, I'd definitely stick to caipirinhas if I returned.

Cambridge 1 specializes in super thin crust pizza. It's almost like half a flat bread sandwich with the toppings on it.

Drinks are a little pricey, but the food is reasonable. They also have some delicious spicy bread sticks that they serve you at the bar.

This is such an amazing place. I wish I had discovered it earlier! It's conveniently opposite the Cambridge Public Library. I stroll in after my long work sessions at the Library. This place is highly under-rated. Usually not  crowded and the person at the counter (I don't know his name) is very friendly.

Brought my girlfriend from Louisiana here for her birthday dinner.  We were not disappointed.  Bring your fat pants!!!
Tried many things on the menu, ribs, brisket, blackened catfish, jalapeño mac and cheese, and cheesy grits.  All good.  The grits and brisket were stand outs.  Oh, and they start you with some sweet corn bread (cue expanding waistline....)
Only beer and wine....but a good selection that pairs well.  Recommend just getting a bottle of wine.  You'll need it to wash down the ample amounts of delicious food.
The staff is super attentive and the food comes out quickly.
The restaurant is intimate and cute.  Can't wait to come back and try more of the menu.  (Although I'm going to need to hit the gym for days after this place.)
Great quick service. We buy there twice a month. We prefer the pizza from Fiorella, the lasagne was good but not special.

Last night was my second night going to ZuZu! for their Saturday night "Soul-le-lujah" dance party, and like the first time I wasn't that impressed. 


Pluses:
- the crowd- diverse mix of people
-the bar tenders were very attentive
- only $5 cover

Minuses:
-sooooooo crowded- i could barely move around, let alone dance especially with a drink in my hand
-music was meh, a lot of songs were hard to bust a move to
-waiting in line outside... i hate lines! 

Overall, I wouldnt rush to come back.

Was craving chicken marsala tonight and got Fiorella's delivered. Was extremely disappointed. Though juicy, the chicken was totally bland, and there was barely any marsala flavor. Noodles were extremely oily with equally little flavor.  One star for the late, but kind delivery man.

I hadn't been to Tupelo in a couple months, but the other day we were planning on going to Tupelo since our friend from New Orleans was in town.  I decided to look through the menu while at work and literally exclaimed "nom nom nom" while reading the descriptions.  (granted, not many people were there that day but still).  Those noms were not to be mistaken as everything was so savory, with heat and delicious that I wished I had a larger stomach.  
Turkey meatballs for an appetizer, plenty of delicious cornbread and the catfish with cheddar grits and tabasco aoli.
