For what you get everything is reasonably priced and makes dining out something that can me a more regular experience.  Their brunch is also amazing with the right amount of breakfast foods and more savory options. 

I only wish they had some liquor options but hey, this is boston and there are crazy alcohol rules that i don't even care to understand.

The Cantab is a the diviest of dive bars. It's dirty, filled with drunk old men with sores on their faces, and it smells terrible. The bathrooms are disgusting, and I wouldn't even consider trying whatever they serve for food.

HOWEVER, It's also kinda awesome.

The basement of The Cantab is called Club Bohemia, and it's run by character named Mickey Bliss. Because this place is such a dive, Mickey is willing to give pretty much any band a chance to perform there. My band played more gigs here than anywhere else, and Mickey was always easy to plan with and very accommodating. Many of my friends' bands also got their start at The Cantab/Club Bohemia, and for that it has a special place in my heart.

The sound system downstairs works well for quieter acts, but for a loud punk band like myself, it was pretty awful. It's run by a single small mixing console atop a podium that Mickey operates while standing on an old plastic milk crate. The lighting consists of regular track lights that Mickey has rubber-banded colored gels around, and a single strobe light that Mickey turns on manually when he feels the spirit via a switch attached to the podium. You can't make this stuff up. If you have a loud drummer (we did), the vocals can't be turned up loud enough to compete without massive feedback. This meant that our vocals were always too quiet, and nobody could hear me (I sang). Bummer.

I have many fond memories of playing at The Cantab and going to see shows there. Some of these include wading through pee pee in the bathroom when the toilet overflowed, and some of them involve watching the unsavory bartender prepare drinks with very-likely-dirty hands, but it's all part of the experience.

Somewhere has to occupy the bottom of the totem pole, and The Cantab fills this role admirably.

I'm sorry to say I wasn't wowed by Tupelo. The stars all seemed aligned in their favor: I had my $50 groupon in tow, a Sunday off from work to enjoy brunch, and a sunny day to make for a pleasant walk down to the restaurant.

We almost considered visiting another Inman Square establishment, since Tupelo boasted an hour wait. Determined not to spend any money, though, I politely requested we wait and give the new kid on the block a shot.

The restaurant is tiny, but we were ushered to a back room type thing to wait in..too bad the restrooms couldn't have been hidden better to allow for more seating there. I did appreciate the owner, Rene, apologizing to us for the wait--- but unlike other reviewers here who claim their hunger was abated by a free appetizer, I can't say we were extended that courtesy.

I loved that our water was served to us in large ball jars, but I wasn't too impressed with the meal. I ordered a meaty hash type thing, but the pieces of vegetable and sausage were so diced that I barely noticed them. Good thing I didn't want to try the chicken and waffles, since I overheard they'd run out about 20 minutes before. 

Eating here is a pricey endeavor, and while I'm curious to try dinner, I won't venture back until the hype dies down a bit-- or the restaurant doubles in size.

I think that the cheesy fries are something to write home about, even if you are mostly writing about your declining health as it relates to the food you got at Angelo's or not. 

As a former CRLS student, it is really the only place worth going to, unless you have the money to go to Broadway Market, or well... Darwin's wasn't there when I was in school... Anyways. 

The pizza slices are cheap and totally edible. The cheesy fries will probably kill you eventually, but everything in moderation. Just never go around 10-12 on a weekday it's totally mobbed.

A simple take on flatbread. That's all it is. Throw some ingredients on a flatbread in a charcoal oven for a few minutes and bam. That's how simple Cambridge, 1. Makes it feel. It's more than just a simple flatbread though, excellent flavor pairing with the ingredients on the flatbread and a wide selection. We have the #2 and #6 while sipping a delicious peach iced tea. 

Highly recommended, a hidden gem in this busy neighborhood.

If it weren't for the one hour time limit on the wireless internet, I might live here. It's a nice ambiance for reading or typing away on my laptop, plus they have fair trade coffee and works by local artists on the walls. It can be tough to find a seat (esp. one near a plug) during peak times, but on weekday mornings or afternoons, it's not such an issue. My typical Sunday morning ritual is to order an everything bagel with tofu cream cheese and read Boston Globe Magazine online.

Not the most visually appealing but the food is great and it's cheap! The staff is friendly and extremely patient considering all the high school kids they deal with. 
I don't know of anywhere else to get 2 big slices and a soda for five bucks.

My wife's chickpea fritter platter was good, and my trumpet mushroom sandwich was tasty (though quite light in schrooms). Pineapple soda was undrinkable due to tasting purely of sugar (not a hint of pineapple), but I enjoyed the fizzy water on tap. 

Ordering was a pretty easy process (though I wish they'd clearly label whats vegan!). The guy who took our order was sweet and efficient. 

The four walls are nice, but I don't know why the one seater bathrooms are defined by gender...

Despite kind of terrible business hours (basically only when school is in session) Angelo's remains my favorite place for subs.

Anytime I get a chicken cutlet sub from anywhere else it's value is determined by its likeness to a sub from here.

Lou is the greatest and somehow remembers my friends and me from high school.  Whatta guy.  He signed my year book "buena suerte!"

Pizza isn't great, but cheese fries, burgers, and subs are out of this world.

Ordered takeout and was not disappointed! From order placement to pickup was under 20 minutes and our food was hot, fresh, and amazing. Chicken Parmesan was out of this world; the chicken was crisp, not soggy, and full of flavor. Pasta was cooked to perfection and the sauce was perfect. Chicken Caesar wrap was insanely good; not too much dressing, the chicken packed a punch of flavor that really made the dish. Good portion sizes and attention to detail really sets this place apart from the rest.

This place is really good.  Everything I've had has been excellent.  BQ Seitan sandwich, chickpea fritter, rosemary fries, even their iced tea, all really good.  The veges on everything seem really fresh, crisp, just delicious.  The pita bread they use for the sandwiches might be the best pita I've ever had too.  Lately I've been excited when I'm in central to go here.

The place seemed clean inside, service was very friendly, there is a guy just standing by the menu who talks to you about the menu and takes your order on his mobile phone.  Overall its got a cool vibe and I'm hungry to go back again for more!

I dread the mornings when I miss my breakfast and have to come here. Here's a typical Clear Conscience Experience, one that I happen to have had today. Arrive at the counter and you're second in line. Nobody's behind you. The person in front of you orders a bagel. You order a bagel. The counter staff begins to loudly plan its weekend. Five minutes elapse. Two bagels go into the toaster. No new customers arrive. The three members of the counter staff continue chatting while two bagels pop out of the toaster. Five minutes elapse. Conversation continues. One counter staff asks what you are doing at the counter. You are waiting for ten minutes for a goddamn bagel. Does a lightbulb go off? No. More conversation. One bagel goes out. You are hanging your head, wishing that you could just get your money back. A slap of butter goes onto your bagel. Will you get it soon? No. A new topic of conversation emerges. How will they meet once they get to JP this weekend? Why don't they have each other's phone number? A second slap of butter goes onto your bagel. It is wrapped. You somehow restrain yourself from wishing ill on the parents of the person who hands it to you.

This is confidential to the Clear Conscience staff, since the concept has clearly eluded you: People ask for bagels to be toasted because they want to eat them hot.

have you seen the size of the slices here?  place is off the hook.  best pizza in the square and possibly best pizza in the boston area.  lots of great schoices, cheap as balls and where else can you get a pbr pitcher for under $5?  what else would you want in an eatery?  this place rocks.

Can a restaurant be too friendly? Staff is excellent, fast and super nice. I got two poppyseed bagels to take home because the DD across the street doesn't make them. Got smoked salmon on a bagel. Best I've had in a few years. Love the salmon and capers !!

This place claims to have won pizza competitions?  Whatever.  I did not care for it.  I had a slice of cheese & a slice of margherita.  I loved the crust - it was crispy, but not overly crispy.  This pizza was just cheesy, oily, and no flavor, along with no whole wheat crust options.  Pass, especially as there are better places to eat in Harvard Square.  Staff was not at all friendly or helpful.

In response to Maria F comment .I don't know of one bar, restaurant or any place of business where you can go to and enjoy live entertainment free of  charge and not expect to make a purchase in this case a beverage because it is a bar if your looking for free go to a library

Oh Fiorella's, where have you been all my life? This new fixture was a welcome change after Singabella unfortunately closed, and the menu does not disappoint. We ordered the chicken parm, cranberry walnut mesclun salad, fried ravioli, and garlic breadsticks, and they were all amazing. Their signature marinara is not to be missed!

At first I was ready to write this place off because of the price of their smoothies: something like 6 bucks for a small cup!  But I'll give them a break because I know the bananas, strawberries, and mint in my smoothie are organic.  And because the 2 flavor combos are pretty inspired.

The wi-fi is free for an hour, which is better than none at all.  The teas are pleasant and plentiful (though scalding, watch out!).  The music volume has been pretty loud in the past, (especially by the speaker near the couches in the front window) which makes it a little hard for my students to chat, but the couches and chairs are comfy and the drinks are really pretty cheap.

I've yet to try the food stuffs, but I love the location of this cafe.  I can do my grocery/farmer's market shopping and sip on a lovely ginger tea at the same time.  I'll take it.

I kinda dig this place. I didn't  love it, but I definitely want to go back and try a few more of their signature pizzas
Although it is not the best pizza I have ever had, I am still intrigued....
Each pizza seems pretty hit or miss; it doesn't seem like there is too much consistency going on.
But I did take a good long look at their menu and I agree they have some really cool and creative ingredient combinations.
I have been really craving a calzone as of late and might try one here soon so expect an update...
It's three stars for now because I wasn't too blown away by the quality or the texture, but I did enjoy the flavors of their balsamic and tomato basil pizza.

The last hold-out in Central Square.

Go there for open Mic night.  Just do it.  Go for Bluegrass night, if that's your thing.  But, go there.

I've got a Magic Memory from this joint.  We used to go to Open Mic Night, almost every week, and "James Brown Jr", was always there.  He did the hits, and worked the moves, perfectly.  One night, he had some competition, and the upstarts fans started a fight with James Jr's crowd.

The bartender was a lady I always called Large Marge (she looked like the trucker Large Marge from "Pee-Wee's Big Adventure"), and she VAULTED the bar, and broke up the fight in 20 seconds.

I was Gob-smacked.  Sure, it's not a genteel story, but it's SO Central Square.

Awful. Depressing.  Wanted a bagel not toasted with honey walnut cream cheese on side.  I got salmon cream cheese on a toasted bagel.  Couldn't eat it wasn't hungry after. This place has gone downhill.  Head down the street to bagelsaurus instead.  Thank me later!!

Delicious, flavorful food, and a pleasant and sunny new location.  Although the prices are a bit steep, the staff truly sets b.good apart from other fast-causal spots.  Everyone who works here is upbeat, enthusiastic, and just plain good at their jobs.

I'm a vegan so maybe my opinion is skewed, but to be able to get inexpensive yet delicious vegan French toast in a great location is just awesome!  The atmosphere is funky & fun, and the place is often packed.  And the desserts are amazing too!

The chickpea fritter is basically a falafel sandwich. Get it with their hot sauce sauce. Delicious !
The other things on their menu are so so. They're also finicky about orders and won't modify them. Service is pretty slow too. Grin and bear it if you like a good falafel sandwich though.

The Cantab lounge has 2 levels, one at street level and another below. If you want to relax and have some drinks, you're probably better upstairs where the music is softer and where it isn't so dark. Bands play downstairs, so if you're into watching local bands demonstrate their musical prowess, head on downstairs to find a stage to the left, tables and chairs in the middle, and a small bar to the back (right). Overall it's an OK place to catch local bands. With $4 per bottle of light beer, it's moderately priced. If you're looking for parking, there's a small lot in the back that allows up to 2 hours of parking time. There's also a Bank of America ATM right across this street for cash if you need it.

The pizza is pretty good here. I wouldn't say it's the best, but it's good. It's thin crust and doesn't make you feel fat after you eat it. It's pretty light but filling but I suggest more of a variety of different types of pizza.

Clover is a vegan restaurant. This would generally have me running for the hills. However, I gave them a try because they had a solid rating. 

The BBQ Seitan was very good. It had a surprisingly meaty mouthfeel, but very little flavor on its own. I enjoyed the dish probably primarily for novelty's sake. 

The Rosemary fries were nice a crispy and oily. The had crunchy with a soft starchy finish to the bite. The fried Rosemary sprinkled in is a very nice touch and shockingly good for straight rosemary. 

I'm not sure this would be 4 stars if I kept coming back. But for this first time, I enjoyed it a lot.

We went on a Monday night. I waited 45 minutes for a smoothie and ended up asking about it. They couldn't find my order (very misspelled name) and when they did, they told me that they could not make the smoothie because they were out of almond milk. I switched to the kale smoothie (fairly ordinary) and seconds after they made my smoothie, an employee came in with a carton of almond milk. Overall, a terrible experience. 2 stars because the food was palatable and they did great catering for a work event once (which is why I had high expectations).

We are big fans of Fiorella's! The food is delicious. We've tried lots of different things off various parts of the menu and everything has been great from the pizza to traditional dishes like chicken parm to the less traditional dishes like pepperoni far diavlo. The ingredients are fresh and the portion sizes are large enough you can take half the dish home for another day depending on your appetite level. 

The service is very good. You order at the counter whether you are picking up or eating in. If you are eating there, the staff will bring you the dishes and clear the table when you are done. This helps makes it a family friendly location. The manager Julie is often there with a warm greeting and everyone there is helpful. We are there often enough that they remember our favorites and are so kind to our daughters who consider it one of their favorite places. 

We have also ordered catering from there. They are helpful with the order and we have never had any issues with the delivery. We've ordered trays of pasta and sandwich trays and regardless of the option we go with, everyone who has had their catering always raves about the food!

Tupelo is nothing short of scrumptious!

When Hungry Mother came along (1 Kendall), the prior est. Magnolia fell off the wayside.  But Petsi Pies came to the rescue to buy out this perfect gem of a location to resurrect southern cuisine in Inman Square with Tupelo.

I love the down-home chillaxing aura, low lights, antique-cushioned chairs, copper-ish tabletops, and cool music.  When deciding whether to go to Tupelo or Hungry Mother, the choice should be obvious, but please note that both are equally good.  Tupelo tends to offer a more casual feel and fair prices.  Restaurant size and flavors are similar.

We app'd on the special: crispy deep fried cheddar grits (crunchy outside, creamy center) and sipped on White Watermelon Sangria.  We gorged on pork shoulder, brined pork chop (juicy), pan-fried catfish (delightful!), and all the fixings (beans, phenomenal collards, pickle, ...cheesy grits).  It was actually heavenly.

Of course I would recommend this restaurant to anyone.  *applause*

Don't come here for the pizza. I was fooled by the high reviews which I gather are for the cheese fries and the nostalgia of youth. The pizza was decent - good sauce - but a bit too doughy and the cheese not of the best quality. Much better pizza to be had in Harvard square I think. I did love seeing the neighborhood kids flood in during a downpour to ask the guy behind the counter for plastic bags for their books, which he readily gave. A very cool neighborhoody experience in that way.

I always get a bad tummy ache after eating their pizza which sucks! It smells and looks so good, but doesn't agree with me.  My best friend who works in Harvard square comes here for their salads in a bread bowl, which look awesome, but I will not risk feeling sick anymore.  Service is terrible FYI.

Yummy pizzas and great casual ambience.

During dinner, this place is full of 30 somethings on dates.  The understated exterior of the place makes it seem like a secret hideaway, which adds to the date appeal.  Solid wine list.  Dark, Manhattanesque type of ambience.  Sometimes, the table wait is daunting, so have a backup plan in case you decide you don't want to wait.

The pizzas are amazing.  The lobster pizza isn't really worth it, though.  Kind of pricey, and the pizza flavor overpowers the lobster, so it's kind of a waste.  Every other pizza is pretty great.

Good for a quick lunch, too.

Pizza is almost always good. This is just a rule. It's almost never great, but screwing it up badly is the kind of thing that puts decent folk out of business. 

Crazy Dough is pretty good pizza. Apparently in 1907 it was voted best pizza in the country or world, but that's like saying Starbucks is the best coffee because not everyone has access to the one shop in Wisconsin (or wherever) that actually DOES have the best. 

I bought the $5 two-slice-and-a-soda-deal. You get to choose from Pepperoni, Mushroom and Sausage, which I think is pretty generous. She took my slices (pep/mush) and threw them in the oven to re-heat. Grrr. That's ok though, unless the crust is going to be all hard and pretzel-like. 

BIG THUMBS DOWN on only having Pepsi products. I mean, this is the 21st century we're talking about here. 

The slices were generous, although completely different sizes. The crust on the pepperoni slice was a good foot across, and hard and pretzel-like. *le sigh* The sauce was sweet and tomato-y, not a bad thing, if that is your thing. It's not my thing, but I don't really mind. Good pizza, but the re-heat was after too long and too dry. 

All this would have led to a 2* review if it weren't for the fact that they have PBR on tap, $1 draughts (plastic Dixie cup size) and $4 PITCHERS!! REAL SIZE!! I didn't know this, and would go back there and eat their cardboard just for this one reason.

This is a vegetarian diner with all kinds of desserts!

I came here with my cousin and she is not a vegetarian. She got grilled cheese and liked it while I got the Ruben. I went out of my comfort zone with this dish as I never had a Ruben sandwich before. I thought it was great although they could have added more of that veggie Ruben meat in there.

I requested a side of jalapeños of which they provided. I will say that the jalapeños added a ton of flavor. I also believe they actually make their own pickled jalapeños. So good!

We also got the poutine fries with cheese on top. There was a ton of cheese which is great but I think I would have preferred it without. They ask you if you want it vegan or not. Next time vegan it is. Nothing wrong with the cheese, but there was just too much. The gravy was great.

For dessert we got the peanut butter chocolate cake. I liked it okay. I think we would have liked the Oreo cheesecake more. Next time I am in Boston, I will try that!
This brueggers is special. I travel for work all over the country and this brueggers has better bagels than any other brueggers I've tried. I don't know what they do but the bagels are so good.

Fantastic margherita pizza. Really flavorful sauce, and sliced mozzarella cheese instead of shredded.  I've tried the chicken caesar wrap and was disappointed, but the pizzas are some of the best I've had near Davis sq. Warning: delivery can take up to an hour or more on weekends.

Dear Clover.  I love you.  I love your innovative sandwiches, your friendly faces, your Friday night whoopie pies!  I was eating clover at least weekly - sometimes more.  BUT - tonight, after an exhausting day at work where I didn't have time to stop and eat, I came by this shop around 7.  A sign was out saying "free goblin sticks (fries) for those in costume'.  I thought - cool.  I wore a costume to work today, I would love free fries with my Sweet potato sandwich.  I walk in and the guy says "cool costume".  I say "thanks - cool about the fries".  Another guy behind the counter says "those are just for kids".  Um. Seriously?  Why have a sign that doesn't mention that they are only for kids in an area where there are not many kids.  Disappointed I look for my favorite seasonal sandwich.  Crossed out.  Other seasonal sandwich.  Crossed out.
Now - I get that it is popular, and that this was evening.  But this is the THIRD time in a week that I have gone - one day "oops, we didn't get sweet potatoes in today", another early day "oh, we can make it, but we just don't have Shoyu mayo.  No big deal, right", and then tonight "yah, our line cook just didn't prep enough".
I am kind of cranky about this because I was legitimately ravenous.  
I have seen the quality of service go down at all but the Inman locations.  At Inman, the manager is great, but when he is away on friday nights, it is a disaster.  Last week, someone made my food with bare hands, made inedible whoopie pies, and gave me a ridiculously hard time about asking for a to-go container.  She actually smashed it in front of my face to prove a point and then packed up the whoopie pies in the crushed to go container so I could bike home with them.
I LOVE your food clover.  PLEASE focus on quality and consistency.  And Friday night whoopie pies.  
Thanks - one of your biggest fans, but tonight, also one of the crankiest.

We got the menu in the mail and are so happy we did.. we have ordered almost everyday since with our goal being to try one of everything. Everything we have ordered has been soooo good, especially the Italian and the Steak Milano they are my favorites so far.  Do yourself a favor and try them I promise you will be happy you did. 

To Fiorella's, thank you for your kind service, right down to the very pleasant delivery person. We are in the customer service business and it is nice to get great service back.

Yours truly,
Carol @ 22 Mill

Pros: 

Crazy variety of pizza! (if the name didn't tip you off)
Dollar Drafts! (i didn't even think this was legal in mass)
Drafts!  (hell any kind of beer with pizza is a win-win)

Cons:

Its in the garage (wading through teeniboppers)
Pizza can be a little too crispy (personal taste)
Drafts are served in dixie cups (any port in a storm right?)

So there's alot of pros and cons to account for but overall I dig this place.  I wouldn't make it an evening?  But its definitely a good start to an early evening of descending on Harvard square

Didn't care for the food at all, but the bar & bartender were both great. Wide selection of new & interesting beers and friendly service. Probably will come back for drinks but not to eat.

We also waited a long time to get seated, which ended up being at the bar (it was a Saturday night, but also was much longer than the wait time quoted). But that part turned out to be the best part of the experience at Tupelo!

I really like this place! I just ate here for the first time today... The food is pretty cheap, and it's really good! It's located right across from the library too! I ordered a slice of pizza, side of meatballs, and a drink... The slices are so cheap here! The meatballs are great too! I'll be back for sure!!

I just had a bummer of a delivery experience. I used Foodler to order one small cheese pizza and a grilled chicken caesar salad. I got a phone call half an hour later with an apology because it was really busy at the time of my order so they couldn't call, but they needed to warn me that it will be 30 minutes late. That was fine! 
Then delivery came and the pizza was cold and the delivery person had no idea where the salad was. She left and came back - it was in the bag all along! So I opened it up and there was my warm, soggy, already dressed salad. I guess at least the chicken was still warm? But the lettuce was also warm and kind of wilted. I didn't eat the salad.

Stinks because it's so close and convenient, but this service isn't worth my trying again.

10 STARS WOULD FIT BETTER!!!!

OMG, this place is sooooooo great! 
We are so sad, we've just been three days in Boston and we don't live there!

We went to Veggie Galaxy twice because it was incredibly delicious the first time we've been there.
Altogether, we had the Southwestern Salad (Romaine Lettuce, Black Bean, Shallot & Corn Salsa, Cherry Tomatoes, Fresh Basil & Creamy Chipotle Lime Dressing),
a Shepherd's Pie (Casserole of Quinoa, Button Mushrooms, Peas, Carrots, Corn and Fresh Herbs, topped with Garlic Mashed Potatoes, served with a side of dressed Field Greens),
a Kendall Square Burger (Roasted Red Pepper Puree, Beer Battered Onion Rings, Roasted Garlic Mayo, Baby Arugula), a Davis Square Burger (Green Apples, Grilled Onion Rings, Sweet Potato Mash, Baby Arugula, Mustard), a
Sweet Potato Carrot Cake, and a New York Style Cheesecake with Fresh Berries.
Having a burger you can choose to have a Chipotle Black Bean Patty or a Mushroom Chickpea Patty. You can make it a Cheeseburger as well and they even have vegan cheese! You can build your own burger as well!

Everything was incredibly delicious and we have to say, it's way the best diner we've been in the US! It's a great place I would recommend everybody visiting Boston/Cambridge!!
Thank you so much, Veggie Galaxy - we already miss you!

Pros: Best food ever, many vegan options (all desserts are vegan!! Whooop, whoop!) , gluten-free food as well

The thick-foamy-ist cappucino ever. 
As i walked up to the counter I caught someone eating a work meal. He tenderly described each perfectly arranged ingredient in the gourmet grilled cheese. 
Herb-boursin spread and (added) honey mustard, made this cheesy sensation a crunchy craving indulgence. 

panini-ed up and spoon-able cappucino in front of my lounging tired feet, FREE WI-FI (for an hour), I can finally do a brain-drain after work before heading home.

Great pasta delivered to your home. I have yet to have a bad meal from here but the chicken parm sandwich was not my favorite. The pasta dishes have all been good as well as the fried raviolis and garlic bread. Check them out.

Like every other kid at CRLS, I learned quickly of the greatness that is Angelo's Pizza. The food is greasy, tasty and cheap. You will be hard-pressed to find more bang for your buck than Angelo's. Go for the chicken sandwich with bacon and an order of cheesefries (if you feel the blood flow to your heart begin to noticeably slow halfway through the meal, don't worry. That's normal). 
Pizza slices aren't the best, but they are enormous and certainly get the job done. If you're going to get pizza, go after the mad rush of high school kids so you can get it hot.
Overall, if you're looking for cheap eats around Cambridge, you won't do much better than Angelo's. A true diamond in the rough.

I had a sausage, egg, and cheese on an everything bagel - it was delicious! Service wasn't great, but it wasn't terrible either. I will probably visit them again, and hope that I still get a good bagel and not the shitty service everyone else seems to talk about on here.
Unique location - very Cambridge feel. The pizza is simple and delicious. It's consistently good. I wish they would change the menu up - I don't know if the pizzas have changed since it opened.

Terrible customer service! I took a group of kids here and we were treated like dirt. Even the kids noticed and questioned it. As far as the pizza goes....eh....I can do without it. Nothing to write home about and certainly not worth the horrid service.  The people at the counter should learn to pretend to like their jobs. NEVER AGAIN!

It's suppose to be the adult version of Middle East cafe. The wait staff was wonderful, the decor lovely. But we were all pretty underwhelmed by the food. Nothing particularly wrong with it it was just like eh that was just okay.
Oh. My. God. Best fried chicken I've ever had. Incredible mac & cheese, flavorful collard greens, and a great beer selection. I'm in love.

I love B.Good.  The veggie bowls are amazing--really fresh, perfectly cooked, generous in portion size, well flavored.  

And it's very meaningful to me to frequent an eatery that is so engaged and generous in giving back to the community.
My boyfriend and I came here and ordered a delicious omelette, eggs, and a pancake. Everything was delicious and so fresh! Highly recommend their hash browns and apple butter!

I really wanted to like this place. It's only a few bucks for a small one person pizza and they have creative toppings. 

But the bottom of my pizza was crusted with black burnt pieces. Not like my pizza was burnt itself, but more like all the burnt  scraps inside the pizza oven got stuck to my pizza. Imagine not cleaning the pan in your oven for weeks then throwing a frozen pizza on it and letting it cook slow
ly letting those lttle pieces of charcole embedding themselves into the crust. 

I will not be back.

Vegetarian oasis but recently they've made some menu changes that as a regular I'm not fond of. I'm going to keep going because delicious vegetarian options are scarce around these parts and their food is still comforting, but I'm weary of the management...

Total dive bar, and totally awesome. 

There is a band who plays here- I do not know their name, but they are wonderful! 

Beers are cheap and the patrons are really entertaining. 

My only complaint is the bathroom- but I did say it was a dive bar... this is only to be expected. I'll just say it's gross and leave it at that.

The restaurant is counter service so you save some cash without a server. The prices are also pretty low for comparable food in this area. The food tasted fresh and portions were average. Flavor/deliciousness fell somewhat short, maybe you get what you pay for on that score. It's OK, not bad, just docked one star for tastiness.

the bartenders here suck. well, let me back up--they can pour beers and keep the counter moving, which is good. however, they don't know how to make...pretty much anything. if you want whiskey on the rocks, great. beer, awesome. vodka cranberry, sure. but don't ever order shots or any drink that is usually standard. they cannot make anything with more than 2 ingredients. why have a full bar if you cannot use it.

the dance nights are really hit or miss.
When you go here you have to try the garlic bagel with low-fat cream cheese I don't think I've ever had a bagel with this much garlic and it is awesome

I am happy that there is a 24-hour restaurant in Central Square -- the limited hours of Boston businesses are a rude awakening after Vegas and Miami.  I wish this place was better, though.  

The ordering process is a mess: there's a menu on TV's on the wall, and a guy standing there with a phone to take your order.  No podium, no counter, just a guy with a phone with a Square credit-card reader attached to it in his hand, and a carnival-style change apron around his wait for those paying cash.  

So you muddle through the ordering process and pay, and he takes your name.  Then you try to find someplace out of the way to stand or sit while they make your food.  And the cooks are bellowing out names:  "Chris!  Chris!  Eggplant platter!  Chris"  But some names sound like other names, the way they say it: "Jen!"  or was it John?  Or Jim?  Or Jean?  Or Gene?  Who f-ing knows?  You might want to pick a name that is unusual, easy to pronounce, and vocally distinctive.  I've never wanted so much to be a number, rather than a name.  

Stuff comes out as they make it, so your name may get called many times, depending on what you ordered.  Good luck keeping track of what's still coming, because you have no printed receipt to compare against.  I wasn't even sure what I paid for: I asked for a soda, a sandwich, and a side, which on the menu was going to be around $15.  The guy mumbled something about it being my first time there, so they only charged me $10.  When my side didn't come out, I assumed he had missed the order, so I sat down and started eating the sandwich (I wasn't about to try and sort out the details in that confused system), but a few minutes later a cook started bellowing my name with my side, so I got it after all.  Yeah, it's a mess. 

The interior is clean and well-lit, though they made an odd furnishing choice to go with a single huge table shaped like the profile of the tree trunk it once was, with about 20 stools around it.  I guess the concept is that you can socialize with your fellow patrons in one big happy love-fest, but in practice it just feels like you're intruding on one another, and is awkward.  Small counters with stools are at some of the perimeter walls for smaller parties and a more private experience.  

The menu is all vegetarian (though only a few items are Vegan).  This is not a plus for me, as I enjoy the taste of dead animals.  My chick pea fritter sandwich was fine, though messy.  It seems like Central Square has a markedly higher-than-normal concentration of vegetarian restaurants. which would be swell if they could produce something that rivals the taste of a cheeseburger or buffalo wings, but so far no luck.  

Anyway, Clover is good because it's clean, the staff are friendly, and it's open continuously.  It's drawbacks are its lousy ordering and delivery system, its limited menu, the poor use of space for seating, and the fact that it seems confused about what kind of dining establishment it wants to be: is it a Veggie McDonald's?  A Starbucks where people hang out and socialize?  An arcade where people play pinball?  We'll see what the operation looks like in 6 months or a year.  For now, 3 stars.

ZuZu has an incredible menu and very welcoming staff. Even the things you wouldn't expect to be delicious at a place like this are fantastic. I recommend starting with the chicken brick and finishing with Middle Eastern coffee.

Went to the Sunday night blues jam..it was incredible. A guy named Jay Henderson..on lead guitar and vocals..along with his brother on bass and the drummer..blew me away! Could have been Jimi Hendrix at his finest hour blowing the place away with blues to the core. Never saw anything like it..except for one time when I was about ten feet away from Albert Collins in Chicago and I could see his eyes...and hear his guitar..on fire...about 1981.

I enjoy the bagels but service here has fallen off steeply during the weekend rush times. Crew had trouble getting my order together, bagels were falling on the floor, and there was confusion over ringing me up.

C1---you are undeniably one my favorites--a true Harvard Square institution and one of my unequivocal  go-to spots in Boston/Cambridge. it all began when I started going nearly 6 years ago as a young whipper snapper to pre-game before a big night out on the town. I have yet to get a meal that stops short of 5 stars. But oh how times have changed--C1 is now a destination, a means to an end because I'm old and a meal out somewhere on a Friday night is a night in and of itself.  Service is always fast and unassuming. Beer and wine lists are small but sophisticated. Great minimalist decor and good for small groups, a date or a mid-week meal. The true test was when I finally brought some out-of-town friends who are pizza aficionados and although C1 is more of a flatbread place than pizzeria (by NYC or New Haven standards) we housed about 9 half pies and two weeks later my peeps are still talking about how impressed they were.

I go to this Bruegger's frequently, since I live around the corner and have their endless coffee mg club deal. They're about to get some major competition because Panera is opening across the street, and despite my deal (I have another couple of months to go before I've made back my money on it) I'll probably start going to Panera more often--it definitely has better food.

Bruegger's is a franchise, so they do vary from place to place, but the fare is the same. The coffee is OK, not Starbuck's quality. Forget things like hot chocolate, which is like chocolate flavored water. The iced coffee is weak, too. Iggy's bagels are my yardstick and B's are just alright. They're small, not very crusty on the outside. I haven't much liked the soup I've gotten on occasion, and a few sandwiches are pretty good. 

Food aside, the big issue I have with this Bruegger's is the attitude of one of the staff. There's one woman there who is surly, makes mistakes constantly, never apologizes, and gets angry about almost anything. I've seen her get into pissing contests with other staff, so it's not just customers. All the other staff are very courteous and friendly. The other staff I see on a regular basis always remember that I am in the coffee club, and that I like my bagel done a certain way. This one woman acts like she doesn't know me or remember anything about me most of the time, and this is after I've been coming in 5 times a week for over 5 months.

On an occasional basis, Bruggher's is fine, but I won't be buying the endless coffee mug club deal next year, I can tell you that. With Panera's great baked goods, better coffee, and wi-fi across the street soon, it won't be worth it. Also, I've never had an unfriendly staff encounter at any Panera I've been to.

The delivery person was extremely unprepared. My meal was 25 + 2.11 delivery fee plus she didn't bring adequate change and took extra money. The delivery woman ended up with $8.67 tip (including delivery fee) for a pizza and garden salad. When I asked for her to return with change she refused to come back. The woman on the phone was very nice but the delivery woman refused to even listen to her.
Absurdly delicious every time! Their burgers are great but the salads I feel are also worth a consideration
They have really good pizza hands down!!! The ambience and the food make me wanna return. Very nice, trendy, casual, artsy place - Harvard Sq typical. LOVE LOVE LOVE it!!!

I spent a lot of time researching places in Cambridge that could potentially be suitable for a casual, yet mildly romantic first date and after hours upon hours of painstaking web-based investigation I decided to seek refuge within the superior knowledge "supposedly" held by my fellow Yelpers and boy did you let me down!!!! 4 stars? No way in hellz!

Maybe my expectations where too high because it took me two attempts to actually make it there. Here's a little background on the build up that lead to their demise. My first attempt to woo the pants off a potential lady friend was sideswiped by an evening of drinks at Charlies Kitchen (a favorite hangout of mine, review coming soon). Needless to say, that relationship fizzled faster than .50 cent sparkler in the wind on the 4th of July. One failed relationship is often another chance at love, so I was excited that I had another opportunity to utilize my research and to stop by this establishment. Finally, here are the long overdue details of my experience. 

Cambridge 1 - (My rating system will always be based primarily on the food).

Pizzas are served on what appeared to be store bought loaves of pita bread which offered nothing but a boring tasteless place to slop down some decent ingredients. I had a half serving of the tomato, fontina, romano, garlic and basil pizza while my date sampled a half spinach, artichoke heart, chevre and slow roasted tomato pizza. Like I said, nothing special about this pizza. Boring store bought pitas with mediocre ingredients slopped on the top, not awful but definitely not worth 4 stars. 

Drinks - Although my beer was cold and tasty I was a little disappointed that the selection was limited to only five options. I enjoyed a Brooklyn Lager and my date had a seasonal Winter Ale. Their wine selection wasn't as bad but it was a bit pricey at $10 per glass. I had a Malbec and she had a pinot grigio. It's difficult to mess up a good drink so they'll get a few points for that.

Service - I had no idea who our waiter/waitress was because we were asked "how's everything" by almost every person in the place but when it was time for a refill the 13 people that had stopped by before were nowhere to be found. 

Atmosphere - This is the most redeeming aspect to Cambridge 1. The soft lighting and somewhat fancy brick oven pizza decor gives you the impression that they actually serve decent food but after your done with dinner you'll feel less than satisfied. 

Overall, I think Cambridge 1 offers sub-par food which is masked by a comfortable yet casual decor which is good for a first date but trust me, there is nothing "gourmet" about their pizza. Taste wise, in my opinion, you'd be better off eating at Bertuccis.

No words can completely do justice to the wonderment that is the Cantab Lounge. In a city with no shortage of generic Irish pubs, college-kid meat markets and overpriced, underwhelming dance clubs, the Cantab is a breath of fresh air (even if it sometimes doesn't smell quite as nice as that  metaphor might imply - part of the charm, right?).

The Cantab is a live-music venue for all those people like myself who prefer to drop $5 on a well-honed bar band than $30 on a over-hyped HOB show (Thanks, TicketBastard!). Its music runs the gamut from blues and country to funk and disco, but it's (almost always) live, and (absolute always) amazing.

There are two undisputed stars of the show here. Free Tuesday bluegrass is a Happening - yes, capital H. Come in, cozy up to the bar with the throngs, throw a couple bucks into Geoff's donations hat, and tap your toes away to the best fiddlers and banjo-pickers offered by the city of Cambridge (well, let's be real: Berklee College of Music). 

But for my money, there is undoubtedly no better night of dancing in Boston then the Chicken Slacks on Thursday. This funk-soul outfit brings together twentysomething hipsters, soccer moms, and a plethora of goofily grizzled barflies. Singer Diamond D is an endearing mish-mash of James Brown, Al Green, and that sketchy homeless guy that cat-calls weekend partygoers on the corner of Mass Ave and Prospect. Ladies: don't stand too close to him if you know what's good for you. (Where "What's good for you" = not acquiring syphilis).

I hear good things about the poetry slams and blues jams, as well, but I'm afraid to go, since spending four nights a week there will severely empty my bank-account and leave my social life unacceptably non-diverse. But I'll keep coming back to the Cantab as long as it offers feel-good music and an intriguingly varied clientele.

I've had several 3-4 star experiences here and one 1-star experience which will probably keep me from coming back ever. When I'm ordering drinks, one thing i value is consistency. Also, it should go without saying but the food here is awful. C'mon, it's a bar. Don't order food at a bar. Especially a dance bar.

Sunday brunch review only: 
We stopped in around 11:15 when they first opened and were seated right away. The ambiance is warm and inviting with funky wall art, cozy feel, and big windows. The staff was friendly, coffee came out quickly, and water was constantly refilled. 
I had the Oyster Po Boy ($14) which was quite good. The oysters were huge and perfectly fired, the salad and pickles were great match for the huge baguette. 
I had bite of my bf's chicken and waffles ($14) which was also tasty though the waffle was nothing special and the ckn portion was very small but the butter was delicious. 

Why only 3 stars? I wasn't blown away by my meal and I think the brunch prices were a bit inflated but the food was tasty, the service was good, and the atmosphere was nice. 
We were craving beer with our brunch but unfortunately they don't serve beer till noon which was no fault of theirs--stupid liquor license laws! =( 
Note: The place was packed by the time we left so get there early if you don't want to wait. 

Overall a good experience and we will be back for dinner!

I've only been here once but I don't plan on returning.  I don't really think their pizza is that great.  Mostly I think they overdo the whole "thin crust" thing and it comes out too dry and crunchy.  I also don't think the toppings are that creative.  Plus, the wait is extremely long.  There are better places to go for pizza in Harvard Square that don't require you to wait outside in the cold.

Disappointing.  I was actually looking forward to trying this place cuz it had some trendy digs and I was really jonesing for some middle eastern food.  I will say that I liked the vibe that was in here.  A bit too hipster but still fun with not pretentious.  Service was good, friendly and present when needed.  That, of course, is where my optimism ended.  My girl and I ordered the veggie and non-veggie pu pu platters respectively ($20 and $23 respectively as well).  It was (just as the server mentioned) a massive amount of food.  But we were up to the task and did indeed finish our platters.  

Here's a breakdown of my thoughts of where the food broke down.  The grapeleaves were pretty good.  A tiny bit sour and good flavor of the rice and ground beef.  Spinach dumplings  .  Empanadas very disappointing actually the worst I've had - I never thought empanadas could be a meh type of food until now.  The brik was too doughy and if all it was supposed to be was tasteless fried dough then mission accomplished.  The kibbeh was also lacking in taste.  It had a great texture and the outside had that wonderful flaky fried crunch but the fried meat inside was surprisingly lacking that savory flavor I would've expected.  The lamb and  beef kebabs ok.  Certainly not impressive and a little undercooked for my taste (I requested medium but got medium rare).  Texture was great, being soft and tender.  But the meats, too, lacked flavor.  I did think, during my meal, whether I was being too critical either by hunger or quashed optimism but no, I just didn't care for the food.  Amongst, middleastern restaurant standards this was easily among the worst I've had.

Their hummus and harissa were just awful.  I was wondering if all hummus was just pureed chickpeas.  Now I know they're not cuz whatever other restaurants do to add taste, zuzu did not.  Both lacked all discernable traces of any taste whatsoever.  That's not to say the texture was bad but I had to question whether or not I was actually had any matter in my mouth.

Do not go here if you're looking for great middleastern food.  Maybe the drinks are awesome but the food ... notsomuch.

I've been here a couple of times, and the food is just okay.  The menu is a little confusing - mostly Middle Eastern, but with some random American dishes thrown in.  I guess variety is good, especially if not everyone is in the mood for Middle Eastern, but it makes me question the authenticity...

As for the food...the Pupu platter is great in theory, with a little something for everyone, but nothing was very good and it ended up going half uneaten.  My boyfriend got the burger, and it was subpar, but really, you shouldn't be ordering a burger here anyway!

The service was fine - quick and friendly.  A heads up that this turns into a dance scene later in the evening, so don't make it a late dinner or you'll have people bumping up against you while trying to finish off your plate.

Fun vegan retro diner in Central Square - this place is a blast! 

BEST coffee milkshake ever. Made with coconut milk, perfect consistency - not too thick, obsessed with the strong coffee note. Yum. Shakes are large enough to split for 2. 

Split the Kendall black bean burger with a friend - tasty. The black bean burger was tasty itself, dressed with roasted red pepper puree, beer-battered onion rings, roasted garlic mayo, baby arugula. Well-constructed, wasn't falling apart. Would order again.

Grilled kale salad (special) was fresh, flavorful and perfectly dressed.

Wish I could've tried the famous meringue pie, but was already in 7th heaven...

So, I understand what people are saying about the staff not always being that friendly.  I have gone in and had them wait for me to talk before they say anything but then once engaged in conversation, they will usually chat if it's not too busy.  Honestly, I appreciate not feeling overly rushed to order but that's just me.  

They have really good Chai.  I've never had the food, so I can't judge that.

I definitely feel that when a business is doing customer service the right way, they should get some props.  I come here about every other week for a coffee and a bagel (which is much, MUCH more tasty than the Dunkin Donuts cardborad alternative).

I came in this morning having trudged through some gross snow and things were kind of in chaos at the register.  Their computers were down, the register wasn't working, cats and dogs living together, mass hysteria!  I placed my normal order and was given my coffee straight away and then stood to the side waiting for my bagel to emerge.  Waiting, waiting.... still waiting.... seeing people behind me get tasty breakfast foods.... three Raconteurs songs later... ok, I'm going to ask.

The computer foul up was definitely the root cause which is totally understandable.  Not only did the manager apologize really graciously, he started digging into the register to refund my money and give me the bagel for free.  I refused the refund, but REALLY appreciated the gesture.  What a great example of customer service!  

This place is pretty cool and I love supporting the locally owned places around here that clearly give a crap about treating people right.  Way to go CCC!

Good god.  So sorry to have to give this place is such a bad reviews but the truth be known, if I did not have  previous good experience at Middle East restaurants clubs etc over past twenty years or so , Id probably give it ZUZU one star . service was average, the waitress  is very attentive and tried her  best. It seem like she might've been on the job not for a long time. Very apologetic and  easy on the eyes was Fatima.I assured her many times she was not responsible for the quality of the  meals. The beer was okay. Cooker than the water but no problem. Two appetizers and a salad later, I wished I going to mary Chung which is just a couple 
of doors down. Sigh. They crispy salmon appetizer was crispy on the outside, not sure what was inside. Caesar salad was by definition a Caesar salad, The green leafy stuff was Age  to imperfection. Croutons were sl chewy, anchovies okay.. they added lamb kebab was, tasty and chewy chewy, paired well with the croutons. I am sorry to be so negative, but that was my reality at that time. It was a slow and hot evening 
Despite needing a reservation at 5:15 there was only one couple by time I left round 6:30..... well maybe things will get better by weeks end and heat waves end..

A chill spot to grab a bite to eat & sit and enjoy the wi-fi if you can snag a hard-to-get seat. Nothing about Clear Conscience Cafe has wowed me in particular, but they do have tasty sandwiches, soups, and other treats when I dont want to hike the rest of the way to Luna cafe. People seem to really gravitate towards spending lots of time holed up in this place, so maybe I need to start taking my work from home here to see how I like it...

Clear Conscience Cafe, also known as the other half of the Harvest Coop Market, is your standard half-yuppie, half-hipster Cambridge cafe. The food and drink here is decent, but fairly nondescript and slightly overpriced. I tend to get the alfalfa-peppermint tea when I visit, which is great, but a little expensive at $2.00 for barely 8 ounces.

The ambiance at CCC is wonderful, and makes up for the somewhat-mediocre food and drink offerings. There's warm lighting, lots of plush, comfy-looking chairs, and enormous tables that can seat 6-8 people (or 1-2 students and a ton of textbooks). There is also free wi-fi, which is never a bad thing.

The staff here has always been extremely friendly and personable on my visits--but I am disheartened to hear that a member of the upper management has been sending disparaging messages to Yelpers who have given CCC poor reviews. That's not cool at all, and such behavior will only make a business's rating go down and turn off potential and current customers.

I'm inherently skeptical of anything labelled "best __ in ___", and often discover that what they are best at is self-promotion and little more. So to walk in to Crazy Dough's and see them touting the "Best Pizza in America" (2 years!), well...

I ordered the BBQ Chicken personal pizza, which I'll confess wasn't BAD. It certainly wasn't the best - the BBQ Chicken at Canestaro's is FAR superior, and I can name half a dozen pizza joints I like better than Crazy Dough's, including a few right here in Boston - but it wasn't BAD. If you're in the Square though and you want a slice, just go to Pinocchio's. I prefer the latter, anyway, but maybe that's just me.

So what have we learned today kiddies? Taste is subjective, and one's best may be another's bust. And just because my mother tells me, "yes Hugh, I'm certain you ARE the best reviewer on Yelp," doesn't mean I actually am...

(Thanks Mom! Love you!)

I have never been here to eat, but I have been here to drink....and drink....and then dance...a lot. So much so that my calves hurt. Maybe I should have drank a little more.  Anyway, when I take my office hours at the bar I find that it is easy to get a drink on a non-"night," meaning getting there before the night's theme gets into full swing.  When I go to dance it is on their soul night, Soul-leh-lu-jah, on Sat. nights.  The DJs are very impressive in their selection and song flow and everyone comes out and dances and it's magic.  But, back to the drink...I do find that when it is crowded (i.e. the soul night) it can take for-ev-er and sometimes a 2 or 3 person strategic plan has to be sprung to timely order and receive your drinks.  But everyone is nice and sexy so you don't mind that much. But if you do sense the bar filling up and you know you're not an aggressive type, order a second drink right away just in case you never get to lock eyes with the bartender for the rest of the night.

Love this place, ive been going here for about 15 years now and I still love it, theres nothing special about angelos but its a great neighborhood pizza place, not to expensive (cash only) good pizza good cheese fries and really good employees

All hail Veggie Galaxy!!  This place freakin rules. I had to very unpleasant experiences right before entering this restaurant with idiots who make fun of vegans, but stepping into this restaurant I just felt happy and at home.  I'm pretty sure everything on the menu can be made vegan. I had a make your own vegan omelette. Oh My God. Pinch me. I cannot believe how freakin amazing this was! I wish i wasn't so full after so I could have had dessert or their weekend only donuts. Hopefully I'll be back in Boston soon and can come back here and try everything in the menu.

We love this place for simple and tasty food with reasonable prices and friendly service.Trout is really good and shrimp and grits special was just divine with 5 jumbo shrimp very juicy and extremely fresh.Very limited selection of deserts but rhubarb pie was yummy.

Bacon and potato pizza really sits heavy in your stomach...I think Crazy Dough's deserves at least some recognition for producing tasty pizza that isn't generic or a carbon copy of similar nearby options.

First time visiting this place and wish I had more time and stomach space to try more items on the menu! We went at 2:30 on a Sunday and it was crowded - people waiting for a table. Luckily with just 2 of us, we were able to get a seat right away. 
Husband & I ordered the vegan mozzarella sticks, french fries, vegan reuben, and the steak & cheese sub. Everything was great - their house-made seitan (in the reuben and steak sub) is excellent. The vegan mozz sticks had a different texture (softer and not stringy) but it did the job for me in terms of satiating that mozz sticks fix....haven't had them since going vegan 2+ years ago. 
I follow their Facebook and every post makes me so jealous of everyone who can eat here whenever they want! Wish I would've got dessert! Next time we are in Cambridge/Boston we will definitely stop by again!

A wonderful find - fun atmosphere and a limited menu that is limited to great stuff! First we had alligator, then came the braised pork for her and the gumbo for me - and it was hard to know which we liked better. Peppery but not hot for its own sake - really quite flavorful.  Now, though, I pine for 'Nawlins...

Wednesday, April 2

Interesting pizza toppings.  Oppressively hot and stale air.  People crowd the area where the warmed pizza slices are brought to and take other people's slices.  

Potato and Bacon Sicillian = 4 stars (would be 5 if the crust wasn't over done from sitting)
Chicken Cordon Bleu Sicillian = 2 stars (would be four stars if they didn't accidentally (?) put buffalo sauce on it)

Overall an average experience.  Just average.  Can I give 2.5 stars?

Never had a problem, been coming to this location since it opened.  Good pizza.

But they've since moved.  I recommend the one in the Prudential, though I don't think they serve beers.

The coffee and bagels are good, and I have not ordered anything else. A few nice people on staff.  The main issue is the lines and the way it is set up.  There are two cash registers, so why not use them during peak times?  Better coffee than DD, but if there was something else close by, I would go there instead.

It smells like pee.  Not joking, it really does.  Shabby even by dive bar standards, most of the people that fill the old, dingy basement seem to have some personal connection to whatever band happens to be playing.

After months of walking through C-cubed to get to the co-op, I think I finally realized why people come here.  Three reasons:  1. Dairy-free smoothies  2. Strange soylent green-colored liquid shots and 3. Iggy's bread.

The dairy free smoothie made with strawberries, bananas, and mint was okay but lacked in mintiness.  It was, however, the perfect post-Prana yoga pick-me-up ever.

The strange soylent green-colored liquid shot was in fact made from wheatgrass, which you know isn't actually soylent green, because you watch them pull a mat of grass out of the fridge and onto the counter.  Then, blades of the sweet grass are plucked from the dirt and blended to liquid form.  The shot at $2.25 is very small, but since they put so much effort into making the shot and warn that the flavor can be a personal preference, small is good.  But I actually liked it.  I'd do it again.  What does it do for you?  No clue.

Iggy's bread.  The best around.  Bagels, bread... head to Central Bottle Wine + Provisions for more of the good stuff.

So why the fair rating?  I just can't get over the juxtaposition of the cool organic sustainable feature-filled cafe dotted with dirty seat covers often occupied by Central Square "local flavor."  I'm happy to order a drink or a snack, but once that transaction's complete, get me the hell out of there!!
Loud, crappy, small, and overly expensive   ($8 cover for no-name band). We left promptly, never to return again. There are much better dive bars out there.
Brunch at Zuzu used to be pretty good. Egg dishes from current menu were gross and flavorless, and gave me a stomach ache. WIll not return.
Good food in a cool atmosphere. They have nice salads. The pizza isn't the best I've ever had, but it is nice and they have some good topping combos.

What is a Moly?  And what makes it Holy?  I ask because when I was here, I kept saying over and over the words "holy moly."  Holy moly there is a whole restaurant dedicated to vegan, gluten free food that doesn't taste like cardboard?  Holy moly the servers actually know the ins and outs of gluten intolerance / celiac disease, etc? Holy moly it's not super expensive?  

Great time.
Love the fresh vegetables.  Great hummus on both the salad and veggie panini.  The turkey panini is also a good choice.  There is a good selection of vegetarian options.

This is a really cool space.  Is it creepy to say that I like sitting in the back, with a view of the graveyard?  

The potato pizza really is as good as everyone else says it is.  I also like that you can do half-and-half on a pizza--that way you can go for a tried and true topping and also try something a little different and new.

But the service here leaves me wondering if I should just try making my own potato pizza at home.  Not too attentive table service on a busy night didn't bother me too much.  I was more annoyed when my boyfriend and I stopped in here one late afternoon pre-dinner and decided to get a pizza to go.  We waited, and we noticed that the kitchen put out a to-go pizza box--but we didn't jump up and claim it, because we figured it could be someone else's, and if it was ours, the bartender who took our order would bring it to us.  The bartender was too busy chatting with another employee and ten minutes or so passed before we were, after all, handed that same box we'd been eyeing.  If the restaurant had been busy, I wouldn't have minded (or probably noticed), but we were the only customers hanging around.

I love this place - the salads are great, the beer selection is good, and the beer tastes good with their awesome pizzas.  This is not your normal Papa John style thick crust pizza -- it's more like a really thin dough that's wood fired and comes out super crispy (almost like a naan + cracker hybrid) and then they put toppings on it.  Some normal pizza toppings, but mostly twists on pizza toppings.  I like almost all their pizzas (minus the lobster corn which doesn't do it for me).    The half size pizza is good for a hungry girl (me!) and the whole pizza is pretty big.  Great place if you want something relaxed, good value (especially for Cambridge), slightly different (but familiar) and not too heavy.  This place has never given me food coma.  Note - they do not have much dessert (only ice cream i think) so go to Burrdick's for that)  Only good for groups 6 or under.  There's not much seating here.

What a gorgeous place to have a coffee! As a remote worker, I used to suffer through my writing sessions at the old Harvest Cafe (free WiFi but lots of crazies who put Central Scare on the map). Now the cafe is a destination, with lovely lighting and very comfy couches and seats. I was also happy that their coffee was as good as the old Harvest Cafe's. Haven't tried the food yet!

Got in right away on a Wednesday night around 7.  Servers were friendly and fast.  Got the plain tomato, basil, and cheese pizza, and it was very good but poorly cut.  My girlfriend and I had to cut it up ourselves to slice off a piece.  Otherwise, everything was great.
This restaurant had a very small selection of food. To make things worse, the greeter and the waitress was not very welcoming. My friend and I decided to leave shortly after being seated.

Three-stars for being able to get a slice and a beer for under $5.  The pizza isn't fantastic - while the toppings and sauces are pretty good, the the dough itself is "crazy" greasy and the oil tends to congeal and turn into a crispy, artery-blocking biscuit after being re-heated.  Still - a good place to get your cheap drunk on with $4 PBR pitchers.

I consider Clover a well established business. Their food is consistently good and prices are also very fair. Beer selection is varied and nicely curated. 

But recently I've noticed some major disappointments. The bathrooms in this establishment are grotesque. They definitely violate health standards and there was never a time that all bathrooms were usable. I don't have high standards, or any really, for public bathrooms as they are prone to get run down from use. But the extent to which Clover's bathrooms are unbreathable and unusable is unacceptable considering and compared to their commendable business, service, and products.
I like this place, everyone who works there seems really nice, and the food it always great.  I recommend getting a bunch of bagels and cream cheese to go too, great stuff!

The quality of this place has seemed to go down since I last visited 4 years ago. The chicken was great but the salad was straight up bad! 

Tomatoes tasted old, peas were bitter and frozen. I had to throw the salad part away. The chicken portion was very small and even though I paid $13, I'm really hungry right now! :(

Too bad. I loved the mission of this place and that it's local. Hope they can improve in the future.

I first heard about Tupelo while watching food channel on a TV at the gym (I workout to eat so this makes total sense to me). They were on Diners, Drive-in, and Dives, a show I normally hate because I trust Guy Fieri on food as much as I trust Donald Trump on constitutional law (though I love it when the NY Times takes down his restaurants). But the food looked so good on that tiny television that I was determined to come. So naturally, it took me a year to drag my a** to Inman.

I'm so happy I did. Fried chicken and grits are two of my favorite comfort foods in the world and they were both out of this world here. The fried chicken was tender and juicy, and the seasoning was slightly spicy. It had a great crunch and the chicken breast was deboned, which I love. The grits, oh my god. I could live on those cheesy grits, though it would require many more hours on the treadmill. They were creamy and delicious. I'm also not a huge biscuits person, but these I could eat all day.

The service was great too, not overly attentive but enough to refill your water or coffee when needed. The food also came out quickly, which is good because I was starving that day.
First time coming here and This place was great! Very affordable and the food was a great lunch. I recommend it!

Didn't have a pleasant experience here.  A usual fan of Bruegger's Bagels this place lacks the execution of others.  Plain bagels were hard, not stale hard, but over baked hard.  Staff was a little all over the place.  Made me a sloppy sandwich and their chicken salad was below average.  No clear line for customers to stand and people were in the wrong line multiple times.  Superb location, and this must be where all their money goes.  My money won't be going here again though.

Came here for breakfast.  Had the impossible sausage egg sandwich.  Wonderful.  The flavor just snapped.  The folks behind the counter were working hard as the line was a bit long.  However, they were all very pleasant and polite.  I like the bright clean decor too.

Ok, so you're walking around in Harvard Square and you see the sign outside Crazy Dough's happily proclaiming that they have $5 lunch specials and $4 pitchers of beer. "Hot damn," you might think to yourself, "this is such a deal!"

Well, maybe. The dirty little secret here is that you have to buy food in order to get beer. Suppose you're walking around on a winter afternoon with a couple friends and decide you want have some next-to-free beer in the hideously cafeteria-lit environment of The Garage. No such luck unless you all get slices of mediocre pizza also!

Bleh. Want cheap pizza? Try Pinocchio's down JFK. Want cheap beer? Be sure you want cheap pizza too. Want both? Hit Newtowne Grill's pizza-and-beer special in Porter and enjoy a more comfortable space without high schoolers and fluorescent lighting.

Five stars.  I have a great time whenever I come to this place.  The atmosphere is intimate; good lighting, nice barstools, interesting artwork (which also makes a good subject for conversation with strangers).  The food is good, although it's mostly the music I come for.  Awesome drinks (without forgetting the good-old-standards: only $3 for miller high life!) and quick service - I never have to wait!  It's great for a chill drink on a weeknight, but it's best to come on Saturday night for Soul-le-lujia - the best dance party in Boston. (In fact, my profile picture was shot here!)

I love their smoothies. Yes, they're a bit pricey, but they're a nice treat when I find myself nearby. As for food, I've only had bagels so I can't really say what I think about that part of the menu.
pizza's creative, but middle of the road. main reason to come here is for the $4 PBR pitchers. yes, you read that correctly, FOUR DOLLAR PBR PITCHERS. awesome.

The food is very good. 

They mark some things as vegan even though they are not. When I mentioned this, instead of them saying "oops, you're right, we'll fix that" they said "well, if you don't want the cheese we can make it again for you without the cheese". Huh?

One of the go-to bars in Cambridge for live performances, sometimes 2 or 3 simultaneously.  The atmosphere is a bit grungy but if you like the band it doesn't matter.  Open mic poetry nights are cool to check out here if you're in the mood for something different.  Quality ranges from somewhat awkward to absolutely amazing.

The real downside is the cover charge on some nights.  My friends and I have frequently skipped the Cantab at the last minute because we  weren't sure the music was worth the cover.

It's finals week and I have been to b.good four times in as many days. The food is fantastic and healthy, the portions are generous for the price, and the restaurant is spacious (and equipped with wi-fi). 

Note: I left this review because they gave me a free entree in exchange for doing so.

I used to love B. Good because they had a dish that combined three of my favorite things: chipotle sauce, kale, and avocado. Sure it was slightly overpriced, but the grain bowls were huge and I could sometimes eat two meals from one bowl.

Not anymore, now it's like $13 for a bowl with chicken and a tiny amount of kale and grain. They've literally halved the size of the bowls. I never thought I'd say this, but Sweetgreen is cheaper if you want a healthy rice, grain and chicken bowl.

Everything else, the burger, fries, drinks, shakes, etc. is good but very  overpriced and so not worth it.
Delicious dinners delivered by Uber Eats from the Cambridge location. We enjoyed the Chicken Neapolitan and the Chicken, Ziti, and Broccoli. We will definitely order again!
I was thinking about the best way to describe my experience at Cambridge 1. All I can say is that is was the best thin crust pizza I have ever had.

If you don't want to stand in line for brunch at Cafe Luna, Middle East/Zuzu offers a great menu with interesting spins on traditional foods, I had the "Go Green!" scramble with basil marinated portabello, fried green tomatoes,
fresh mozzarella & basil cashew pesto. The scramble was excellent with solid flavor variance and perfect texture and the side potatoes were so good that I wanted more. Other dishes ordered and given a big thumbs up were the Cheeseburger & Corned Beef Hash which was a nice blend of eggs, peppers, potatoes and onions. An unsuspecting treat!

I had the spicy avocado and lime bowl made vegan and gluten free. It was awesome! The avocado was amazing and very generous for the price. I love that the ingredients are sourced locally! There is ample seating at this location. I wish the portion of chips was larger in the chips and salsa side. There is way more yummy salsa and not enough chips to eat it all.

Hello!
Zuzu is a great place if you like dancing to some good old soul music which I love!!! on Saturday nights  I recommend going right before 10:00 PM because the line gets long and the place isn't too big.

Since I've been there a few times for dancing I decided last week to take friend for dinner. To be honest I was dissapointed and it was my first time. Some of it was good and some of it was just bad. The good: The Empanada and the Falafel appetizer. The Bad: the Pita bread which was stale and some of them were too spicy to even eat ! but the worst was the Pork Chop dish! please do not order this plate, the pork was so tough that not even the big knife I was given to cut it with even worked!!!!!!  I say stick with the dancing on Saturday night ( that is why I gave it three stars) and skip the food.

I hate to wait and I especially don't like it when I've been told it's going to take 20 minutes to get a table and I end up having to wait 35+ minutes. Even so, the sausage and ribs, cornbread and green salad (liked the dressing and cornbread croutons) make a worthwhile dinner for 2. I stuffed myself silly and still had leftovers. The service is also phenomenal here (and they're willing to split an entree onto two plates--without making a fuss). I would say that the cornbread seemed to be made with powdered milk and that the potato hominy was not my favorite mashed potato recipe but I still really enjoyed the food (and looked forward to eating it again). 

Bottom-line: The food here is memorable (I still think about the chicken and waffles--from the brunch menu). Try Tupelo. (Or Hungry Mother...much more $$$ though).

this is topping off my list of places in boston that remind me of northern california, where they are alllll about clear conscience eating and political awareness in dietary choices. i am too, but i didn't necessarily notice anything glaringly conscientiousness about their menu other than fair trade coffee. my chicken sandwich was good (and toasted! which was a pleasant surprise), and came with a small scoop of mixed bean salad. they allowed my friend to sample different soups before she made her selection which was nice. 

however, there was absolutely no seating as it had just started to rain and swarms were finding refuge inside. our group of three pulled a seat up to a two seater table and we were gruffly told by someone behind the counter that we were absolutely in the way and would have to move immediately. oooook, we gettt itttt, my deepest apologies for ruining your flow of pedestrian traffic. the service continued accordingly like this which was pretty off-putting, but otherwise it was a nice spot to stop in for a [somewhat overpriced] lunch after grocery shopping next door.

What year is it? What year is it that somebody is actually plating mushy dog food veggie burgers. How do you open a restaurant and have no clue that planet earth actually came up with a veggie burger that didn't suck, or come on a stale bun with a single sliver of iceburg lettuce or actually tasted good years ago?

How does it happen?

Cheap, fast, honest. Walked in. Asked for pizza, he suggested slices instead. Saved me $2.
You'll get what you'd expect from the place. So if that's what you're looking for you won't be disappointed.
So many unique and healthy options here! My favorite has to be the rosemary fries - if I can get a giant basket of fries for 400 calories, I'm a fan of the place. ;)

I've been here twice, and enjoyed it both times.  The menu isn't huge, but they usually have decent specials, and I have never been disappointed in the food. I thought the coconut cream pie would have been better without the coconut shavings, but the other 50% eating it disagreed.  :)

Tried to go here for lunch today and the place was a disaster. It seemed like they just experienced a rush so I waited about 5 minutes to be served. The gentleman who stated my order was very stressed and three women behind the counter ignored his request for help (or any request at all). Halfway though my sandwich he threw his gloves on the counter, said "I Quit" and left. I stood there waiting, the girls chuckled, and continue to ignore myself and every customer on the store. Those girls were absolutely rude and unappreciative is what I assume was their overworked manager. What an awful experience!!

So excited that they opened a b.good so close to my work place. My weekday lunches got healthier and tastier than ever before. Very quick service. Its gets much easier when you order online and pickup at the store.

I don't go here often but usually its fine. Today one person making lunches (saw at least 2 other workers) and though she was nice she was very slow. One person before me got a salad which took 10 minutes. Finally got my order and then someone comes to help. Got back the office and she had put an unwanted item on. If I ever return I'll pay attention to the making of my lunch. It was not good!

This place is SO AMAZING!! My boyfriend and I love it here. It's like vegan heaven!!

We pretty much get the same thing so many times (him: galaxy burger; me: reuben) but we can't help it because it's SO GOOD LOL. Especially the reuben. I had never tried an actual reuben before I stopped eating meat, but my boyfriend had and he said it tastes better than the real one!! A+++ from me as well (-:

The BLT is also so good too. AND THE CHEESE FRIES. MY GOD. Idk how they make their housemade cheese sauce but it is delicious. I wish I could ask them for the recipe. 

AND their milkshakes!! They use Fomu ice cream which tastes really good. I love the classic vanilla flavor. 

This place has such an extensive menu, the whole family or group will definitely find something to eat!!

I love the Cantab Lounge!

As a former resident of Central Square I will fully admit the joy I have from seeing Little Joe Cook and the Thrillers! The top 10 classics sang over and over and over again.

The hits:
Peanut (featured in a Sopranos episode)
Sexy Lady from the Beauty Shop
Ramblings by Little Joe
