For an 82 year old pushing his cd's, Little Joe sure rocks the house Friday, Saturday, and Sunday night. Through out the rest of the week there are blues jams and open mic nights.

The drinks are reasonably priced --- CASH ONLY! --- Which is fine by me...
The food is decent. It surely has a dive bar feel. The bathrooms are tiny and reek of urine older than Little Joe himself.

The DJ if  there at all is located in the downstairs section. It reminds me of being at a junior high school dance if  I had ever gone to one.

Over all good times at the Cantab. Bring a date or try to hook up with a bar fly.
Pizza from heaven, as good as they made twenty years ago, and now they put eggplant on the slices if you want it. Simple, perfect, inexpensive. No wonder they survive.

Wandering in here at 2 a.m. after a night rocking out at the Middle East, this place can seem like a beautiful mirage. The space itself is shimmeringly gorgeous. The food is mind-blowingly delicious, even for a decided non-vegetarian like me. AND THIS AMAZINGNESS IS OPEN TWENTY-FOUR HOURS A DAY. Magic is real.

Came here for the first time despite going to school in Cambridge for six years and living in Central for two. Was originally going to go to Brookline Lunch, but it was closed, so came here. Glad we did! The inside is like a traditional diner and smells a little funky, but the menu has an abundance of options, many of which seem FODMAP free. I got the seitan BLT ($8.95) with fries ($1.50 extra), and there was enough to pack away for a second meal. The sandwich was pretty good, but the fries were a bit salty. I would return again to have this sandwich or give something else a try.

I'm afraid that my previously-favorite Harvard area burger shop is showing signs of entering a death spiral.

Their prices have now reached $8-$10 for a quarter pound burger, which is a huge jump in price for a relatively small serving. They have also dropped a lot of their classic burger types in order to offer a broader menu, which makes the restaurant feel less unique. Lastly, the quality of the food has suffered as well---on my mushroon and swiss burger, somehow both the mushrooms and swiss were cold.

I'm afraid that rent increases and other Harvard square pressures finally caught up to this place. I will miss it.

We do our best to make it to this Clover when we are in Cambridge.  The food is always excellent and innovative.   Today I tried their Pushpir sandwich for the first time and have to say it is simply one of the best I've ever had.  It is so good that eating one aspires to the level of a "life event" where when it's all gone you are haunted by the music and memories of the flavors and experience    I don't like writing Yelp reviews but I had to share this one for no other reason but to persuade them to continue making the sandwich and hope they consider opening a location here in Exeter, NH so I can get my fix more than once every month or so.

Gotta respect an establishment that commits to a theme, and executes to perfection. And with a name like Veggie Galaxy, it's also really easy to guess what that theme may be.

I love the diner atmosphere, it's great being able to see the chefs out in the open, and service was fantastic. Food was delicious, with heaping diner-sized portions and lots of flavor.

Is it the same as meat? No. Is it equally delicious in a different way. 100% yes. (I am definitely not a vegetarian, for those of you omnivores who are hesitant to try this place.)

Definitely a great restaurant for relatively quick sit-down service and an excellent vegetarian/vegan menu.

The Summary:

Atmosphere - 5/5
Neighborhood - 4/5
Food - 4/5
Drinks - 5/5
Inclusivity - 5/5
Experience - 4/5

The Story:
Came here for a solo brunch and was delightfully surprised! The restaurant has a great atmosphere, the service was charming, and my omelette/potatoes got the job done. Also enjoyed a boozy smoothie - honestly been thinking about it since I left!

Will definitely return - would love to see what they offer for other meals (and to check out the nightclub!!)

Came here in a whim for Sunday brunch and it was one of the best decisions I made! The food is really good and it is definitely one of the better southern brunch options in Boston!  My friends and I ordered the Cajun Gumbo, Fried Chicken & Waffles, and the Tupelo Sandwich, all of which were delicious. It's also really helpful that this place lets you make reservations or you can call ahead of time to put your name down.

OMG!! This place makes me want to take the next plane to Louisiana!!! My girlfriend and I ALWAYS have our weekly date  (2x a week sometimes) here! The people are so friendly & I love the environment! Small, homey, cozy, and warm! I've never had gumbo & I usually don't like "slimy" food but my dear that's my favorite dish from Tupelo! I love the fried oysters! I have recently tried steamed mussels with the cut fries! AMAZING! Prices are great also!

What time do Astronauts eat? Launch time, here at Veggie Galaxy! I'm a big space nerd so what brought me here was the catchy title and of course vegan options! I had started with the chocolate milk shake, it was sooo delicious! I had the Kendall Square, make sure to request it vegan. The Kendall sandwich has onion rings! It's very rare for me to find vegan onion rings so I knew I had to give these a try. The burger was overall delicious. However, my favorite was the chocolate milkshake I had. It did not bring all the boys to the yard, but It will be bringing me back next time i'm in Boston!

Clover is a pretty solid place for their Chickpea fritter sandwich, and their drinks are usually pretty neat. Each ingredient tastes fresh, including the prepped chopped veggies, hummus, tahini sauce, and the pita bread itself.

I appreciate that they also serve the drinks without lids or straws, so you can consciously choose to your liking if you need it. Environmentally conscious composting/waste disposal as well!

Ordered the chicken Mediterranean bowl and there was no chicken. Good thing I checked, just had a feeling it was gonna be wrong Bc the 2 girls behind the counter kept on switching back and forth. 

Not great customer service, just said "I don't know why there's no chicken." Put it in and said "ok all set". 

As Bgood expanded, customer service is no longer their top priority. Sad to see a good "local" company becoming more commercial & lose its human touches.

Their website, Google, Apple Maps etc, all say they're open from 8am-8pm on a Sunday, serving breakfast until 11am, but I showed up at 9:45 and they clearly weren't. The weird thing is that their breakfast menu was on the board, they were just obviously not open. Not impressed.

Used to buy  sandwiches from this place but not hygiene at all  from this  employee ,not enough all the business working hard to survive on the  middle of these  pandemic  and people like that doesn't care about costumer safety..it's a shame .Where are you gloves sir ?

shared the steak sandwich with my friend. It was pretty good, i love cheese, mushroom, and steak combination. Somehow, this mix of filling makes the sandwich so flavorful and moist (I swear its the mushroom). 
The place is small and looks like its been around forever. Service is ehh, can't expect too much. pretty well priced. 
did not get to try the pizza, but it smelled really good!
This is your typical Subway restaurant. The seating is shared with other restaurants in The Garage.

I have never been treated so rudely in a restaurant before. We were aggressively-nastily refused service because we didn't want to order two entrees, and they are pricey, especially for lunch. I just wanted a salad and dessert. It was late--past the rush and there were only a couple of tables occupied so why the nastiness?
Went here for an infrequent parent lunch out. Had come for work-lunch occasionally before. So bad the mean proprietess ruined the mood.
A place like this is why Yelp is such a service. Save yourself aggravation

Raucous, noisy, jam packed and one of the best times I have had in a bar in a long time. great Irish band, friendly staff and patrons, generous drinks and reasonable prices.
We started with some nice wines and the calamari appetizer. 
heading to sit at the bar we enjoyed $5 shots of Tully whiskey and a great snifter of Redbreast 21 year old whiskey.

Yes, there are plenty of TV screens above the bar; they were playing the Celtics and soccer on adjacent screens.  The food was generally American with an Irish-pub vibe.  

As far as "Irish" pubs go, my GF and I sat at the bar with an Irish bartender & Irish co-owner sitting next to us.  I did start with 'The 1916' cocktail -- basically a twist on Boulevardier.  There were about 20 beer choices along with 4 wines & Fernet on draught.

I had two appetizers: mussels and poutine.  The GF had a burger, and she really liked that it was cooked to medium-rare as requested.  The mussels were as plump and tasty, but the poutine is really just decent fries with gravy & cheese curds.  (I went to Montreal last year, this is fine but not Canada-level poutine.) 

Good for them on going local on the mussels (from Maine), poutine (curds from Vermont), and offering chicken from Mayflower down the street.  I wouldn't be surprised if the burger came from a Western MA cow...  

Service was not lacking in any way.  The lights were better when they turned them down; otherwise it's a casual restaurant in an area where other new places are trying to be more upscale.

If you love deep dish pizza this is the place for you. A great selection with a tremendous feel inside. Certainly the best late night pizza and perhaps food in Harvard Square and a staple amongst all of the students.

We had a dinner reservation nearby and decided to drop in here for exactly one drink. So I order a beer and my SO orders a bourbon maple cocktail. The bar counter is made of scrabble pieces so we obviously start finding longest words. Longest we found was EXECUTIVE PRODUCER DICK WOLF. We peeked into the plates of what others were having and seriously contemplated cancelling our dinner reservation and eat here instead. Three drinks later we decided to honor our reservation and come here again to try their food. All in all, its a great place to hang out.

Sometimes, I just want a Subway. Why? It's just a sandwich, the architecture of one is not all that difficult to mimic. A monkey could make one.

Yet there is something distinctly Subway-y about a Subway sandwich that pleases me so.

Don't know why so many Yelpers seem to be down on Changsho.  I like it a lot -- largely because they simply do a good, normal job on Chinese food.

Granted, the prices are a bit expensive -- but a couple of things make up for it.

First, I really like the decor inside.  The restaurant is very well decorated and feels very peaceful to me.

Second, they simply get Chinese food right.  Nothing fancy or special -- just right.  I haven't found that every place in the area (see my Happy Garden review) has been able to do this.

Bring down the prices a bit, and you'd get 5 stars.

Tried both their boneless and bone-in wings with their secret sauce, both were really good. Service was only okay though. I met up with friends here who already got their food, but after ordering, I never got silverware or a napkin. Also, I waited over an hour after getting my food for a waitress to stop by our table so that I could ask for a refill of my water. The wings were kind of spicy, so waiting so long was a little ridiculous. To be fair, we were eating during the Patriots playoff game, so it was busy, but still... the restaurant isn't that big, and the waiters/waitresses passed by our table a million times but just never stopped.

Food would probably be a 4, I'd give service a 2. It's kind of on the pricey side so not sure if it's worth coming back if I'm going to be sitting without silverware and an empty drink throughout the whole meal.

Came here for brunch this Sunday with 4 other people. My son lives nearby, and had been here before.  On this particular day, there was a staffing problem. Our food took especially long to come out. Our server was very apologetic. The cook even came out to apologize. When our meals came out, they were all hot and tasty. We were compensated by not being charged for any of the food! So, even though it took nearly 45 minutes to get our food, it was well worth the wait. I will not hesitate to return and recommend The Rising with no hesitation.
Just opened recently. Tasty food in a nice bar atmosphere. Had some good cocktails. Nice to have another option at Inman Square.

Been there five times already in the past two weeks and can admit that this subway has some of the best and fastest service I've seen! They usually have three people at the counter so they get your sandwich up and ready in about 1 minute which is terrific, especially when you only have a 30 minute lunch break.

The only thing keeping it from five stars is the fact that they didn't microwave my chicken breast before putting it in the sub and toasting it which is something that I hadn't seen before. But they did when I asked for it the next time around.

Subway, I love you!

Amazing amazing place! Some of the best pizza in Boston and a huge difference from my last visit. My favorites are the zucchini and artichoke Sicilian slices. Definitely a top pick in the Harvard area for a quick bite.

Not in Kendall Square!

OK, so I'm the new-to-Boston guy who just assumed that a place that is in Kendall Square would be near the T stop just like so many other places I frequent in Davis or Porter Square.

Not the case.  Anise is at One Kendall Square which is actually a bit of a walk (and I can't exactly tell you which way) from the Kendall T.

Nevertheless we made it and were pretty happy.  Considering the reviews here seem to be either STELLAR or POOR, I was pleased with everything.

The food was all tasty, not too pricey, and came out really really fast.  OK, there was only one other table on a Friday night , which did bother me a bit but perhaps everyone else also got lost.

Try the Dan Dan Noodles.  I was excited to see these on the menu and they were garlicky and great!  Too bad they were just an appetizer portion.   Yum.

Just had late lunch. Quite, clean and kind service. Server named Roy did awesome and great service today. Food was great and delicious. Price is little bit hight but its totally worth it. Definitely recommend!!

The bubble tea here is delicious, but they are very stingy with their tapioca as opposed to Boston Tea Stop in Harvard Square. It is also more expensive, too. 

The bubble cakes are the reason I go here, not the bubble tea. The cinnamon bubble cake tastes delicious, and it's a good deal.

Came here Sunday morning, and totally see why it was rated best brunch! I had a VEGAN "hollandaise" -- I'm not even vegetarian, and I wished I had more, after finishing it in record time. I also ordered cheesy grits as a side, which were the perfect amount of cheesy (Gouda, if I remember correctly) and even though I was positive I wouldn't, I finished the whole bowl. 

Out with my mother and my soon-to-be brother-in-law, the conversation never even slowed, mostly due to the fun trivia and the "famous movie wall", a collection of very well-painted icons from well-known movies; you can spend a few hours just guessing which movie is which, and that's before you even get to the other games available. 

Service was impeccable; manager Jay was welcoming and clearly excited about the menu, which he helped to create, as well as just a fun interesting guy to talk to. Server Julia was attentive, sweet and knowledgeable, and was there when we needed her like she was psychic. 

Can't recommend this place more! We're from Connecticut, and really look forward to coming back on our next visit to Boston. Thanks Wit's End!
A tasty treat near MIT/Kendall! The boba teas (mmm jasmine) are really tasty! As are the mango smoothies. =) Will be coming back for more this summer.

Being adjacent to MIT, this place gets quite crowded for lunch, as you might imagine.

The food is not bad--I ordered a chicken salad with goat cheese and a lemon vinaigrette.  The dressing really brightened the flavors, but the salad was not really anything creative, or better than what I could put together myself.

Wow. This was the worst chinese meal I've ever had. I was lured in by the magazine reviews I'd seen, but came away not only disappointed, but borderline sick.  Something clearly is wrong here; I sense that a key chef has quit or something of that order. The only thing decent was the chicken lettuce wrap appetizer. The Salmon in bean sauce was so sloppily done that they might as well have used canned salmon. The beef with tangerine strips was inedible; as opposed to slices/strips of beef, they served chunks of low quality meat covered with a horrendous sweet/sour sauce. Truly one of the worst meals I've EVER had.  Stay away!

So, I am not an expert on Chinese food by any means, but this place was solid. Food was good, servers were attentive, and the ambiance was really nice. It's a spacious place, which I don't see a lot in Cambridge or Somerville and, while I didn't use it, they apparently had a parking lot. 

I got a tofu lunch special and learned one important thing: the food really isn't very spicy. The menu said it would be spicy, and while it was flavorful, there was almost no spiciness to the dish. I ended up asking for hot sauce, which had a smoky flavor to it, but was still good!

I'd say definitely visit if you're in the area and looking for a solid experience.
Three cheese pizza is my favorite! It's very thin pizza so it's not as filling as normal pizza. Wild recommend take out from this place instead of sitting there and eating.

Anise is a great place to satisfy your adrenaline-rush need for spice - straightforward chili heat and the hard to find numbing pleasure of sichuan peppercorns. And it's perfect pre-movie din-din before going to the Kendall theater a few steps away.

As for the food - Vinegary scallop soup featured many of the flavors of a good hot and sour soup. Loved the salt and peppercorn crusted beef tenderloin (my mouth drools as I write this), double-cooked pork belly and both the wontons (sauce more vinegary) and other dumplings (sauce more numbing). 


Despite fantastic food (on one visit, mind you), I feel this place won't make it. Kendall is a little bit of a ghost town at night. Apparently Anise has a bigger lunch crowd. But we were one of just three tables in a pretty large and well appointed space. So visit now, while you can. Hopefully these guys can stay in business long enough to build up a following.

OMG! I had been looking for what I call "egg crates" in the Boston area and was sure nobody sold them.  Then some nice girl on yelp told me Leisure Station had them, and I almost died of happiness. However, Leisure Station calls them "bubble cakes".  It's sorta like a waffle looking thing that also looks like an egg crate to me.  But it's SOFT, warm and delicious! Smells so good.  Oh man...I was SO happy when I found this place and realized they had them.  Their location is a little hard to find, but if you find it, it's well worth it! GET THE BUBBLE CAKES! It was kinda pricey- $5 for a large one that took me 2 minutes to inhale, but I loved it.

I got a lychee bubble tea and the boba was TRULY awesome! Had great softness to them! Perfectly cooked.  This place also has a HUGE assortment of flavors- hot, cold, smoothies, teas, you name it! I clearly have to go back and try them all.

I'd say the only downside is it's a bit pricer than what I'm use to in Chinatown- I paid almost $10 for the bubble cake and boba.  However, the quality of the boba is definitely there and I'd probably pay anything for bubble cakes since I'm in love with them.  

It's a very small place but they have electrical outlets if you want to go with your laptop and hang out.  

Totally recommend and will go back!!!!

3.5, I frequent Darwin's. I noticed a continuous staff change this year. The counter staff are pleasant mostly. When its busy you can wait anywhere from 10 to 20 minutes for a sandwich, easy. I will go to the coffee counter to get most of my items now --as it is less busy there.

Summary: Decent food and drinks, fun atmosphere/games, TERRIBLE SERVICE. 

I came here for a friend's birthday party and we were a group of 12 on a Sunday night. None of us were particularly loud or obnoxious (I am sensitive to things like this and observed other patrons to make sure we weren't attracting their attention), we ordered both drinks and apps so I have no idea why our older male waiter decided to give us abundant attitude. From the moment we sat down he acted like he was doing us a FAVOR by taking our orders, while at the same time kind of hovering over us and glaring at us like we were naughty schoolchildren. Every 5 minutes he would walk over and demand our drink orders, yet refilled our water jug ONCE in the 2.5 hours we were hanging out there, after my fiancee asked twice and was told at one point to "fill it himself" (???) 

At the end of the night he did run our cards separately, which was nice and not something that most Boston restaurants do, but while HE OFFERED to do this, he also acted like it was a huge inconvenience, which ??? 

This waiter's bad attitude was so obvious that the entire group noticed and were talking about it after we left the restaurant. I've seen no other reviews complaining about him so I don't know whether he was new or whether he had some mysterious personal vendetta against us. Either way, it's a shame because Wit's End is exactly the kind of bar that I would recommend to my circle of nerdy friends but now intend to avoid like the plague.

Who knew my uncle was a jetsetter who would bring my reunited extended family and I to the Kendall Hotel's quaint living room-style bar, rumored to have the best Boston Creme Pie in the city?

I'm glad I got to drink with my relatives this weekend, and also that they brought me here. We sat near a cozy fireplace at mismatched quilted couches and armchairs around an antique metal table type-thing, and soon learned our coveted dessert was out of stock-- but the pecan turtle cheesecake and black and white chocolate mousse made up for it.

The drink menu is pretty basic: wines, full bar, and some specialty mixed drinks. As soon as my pretty blue eyes saw this place served champagne, I tried to order a French 75, which evidently stumped both servers and the bartender. 
**
I've also been back a couple of times to find the waitstaff to be somewhat confused when I order classic drinks (i.e. Tom Collins). One atrocious evening, we were ignored by our waitress for 40 minutes, and had to flag down another one to get service.

On some nights, this hotel lobby bar/restaurant is delightfully refreshing and fun, and on others, the customer service and beverage quality is so awful I want to cringe. 2.5 stars for inconsistency.

Ordered pizza close to the place. After 50 min called the pizzeria. They said it'd be a few minutes. After another 25 we called and they said 5 more min. So took almost 1.5 hours to deliver a pizza less than a mile. Pizza was barely warm when it arrived.

Eh, I can't get excited about Darwin's. They have weird sandwiches, with things that in my world don't belong on sandwiches.  But my spouse loves them - it's her favorite sandwich place in fact, so perhaps I just haven't given them enough of a chance.

I like the food here....however, chinese friends have told me that this is not at all authentic. Luckily for me, I am white. I guess if you are looking for authentic chinese food this is not the place. But if your a random white guy who has never been to china, the food is great, the service is very good, and the atmosphere, well, I'd much rather park in their private lot on mass ave in Cambridge than look for a spot in chinatown. I really do like the food.....could be a little spicier though.

It has always amazed me that Harvard Sq lacks a decent Chinese restaurant. This is a good addition to the neighborhood. Food was fresh and flavorful. Portions very generous. Always looking to match Mary Chung's saun la chow  sho, we had the spicy dumplings. May even like them better - the dumpling dough was more think lo mein noodles rather than chewy Peking ravioli.

I had never tried Bubble Tea before but was curious what the big phenomenon was all about so I decided to give it a go. This place is very small and although it appeared that they serve food, I couldn't see a food menu anywhere. The Tea on the other hand! They have a really large variety of teas to choose from. I told the girl I had never tried bubble tea before and she told me a little bit about the types of teas you could get, I settled in on a honey/green tea. it was DELICIOUS! (I guess its a popular kind) The only thing, I would have liked more tea and less bubbles. They are chewy tapioca balls that taste like gummy bears but there were just too many in my tea! It would have been great to just have a few less bubbles and more tea. Other than that it was so YUMMY! I will be back again to try the coconut one next!

I am always on the look out for good Chinese food, because it serves as wonderful comfort food for me. Cilantro did not look like much from the outside, and it is tucked somewhat away, but on a hungry Saturday night, we decided to try it, and we were pleasantly surprised. 

Parking - street meter parking along Mass Ave, or it is also reachable by the Red Line or the bus.

Decor - The restaurant itself is not very large, and decor may not be as nice as some places nearby (such as Dumpling House), but still clean and bright. 

Food - We ordered Fish Fillet with Chili Sauce (水煮魚）, which was flounder and mixed vegetables steeped in chili oil sauce with herbs, and Sauteed Diced Chicken With Chili & Pepper Sichuan Style （辣子雞）, and a cold milk tea (they ran out of bubbles). Both dishes were fantastic, though they were both spicy. I would highly recommend them if you like spicy. The milk tea was also delicious and served as a great quencher to the fiery spiciness. The portions were quite large - between the two of us, we finished the fish fillet but had to take home some of the chicken. 

Service - Staff were friendly and efficient. No complaints at all.

If you want some good Chinese food and do not want to wait in the long lines at the nearby Dumpling House, Cilantro is a solid choice. In fact, Cilantro might even be a better choice.

My boyfriend is rarely impressed by Korean food because his mom's is the best, but this place impressed him. We were thrilled to it! And I was thrilled that they not only have the typical meaty bibimbap- Seoul food serves it up with raw tuna, snapper, tofu or all veggies.

I had the veggie pancakes and the stone pot tuna bibimbap. It was the best Korean food I've ever had in a restaurant! I didn't think I would finish the whole pot- but once I started there was no stopping me. The service was very attentive- they never let our water glasses get empty.

The place is very casual and plain. No alcohol. And it's tiny. So expect to wait during peak hours- especially if you are with a group.

I've only been to for lunch because they have a buffet which is usually pretty good because they have a variety of dishes that you can try and some fresh fruit to finish off your meal with. The buffet costs $9.99 or something like that and you get a choice of soup to go with it and all the water and tea you can drink, which is usually good enough for me because the drinks they sell are very small and somewhat expensive with no refills if I remember correctly. A friend of mine once got the Green Tea, instead of the normal tea they serve and it was $9 and not all that good, so stick with the normal tea.

Such a lovely spot in Inman square. Cozy neighborhood bar feel, with classy cocktails and down to earth vibes. The owner characterizes the place as a "mule bar" but the selection of the other drinks and food are extensive and quality. I loved how every drink was named after a person, added as some fun to choosing one. As a Moscow mule lover, I was excited to see several to choose among!  As a Texan, I was surprised to see the prevalence of Texas liquors on the menu. Shout out to Tito's and Deep Eddy!

They have food specials on Monday and Tuesday night - tacos and wings I believe - and there are some board games scattered throughout the bar. Things can get pretty packed so I'd arrive earlier to guarantee a table for your group.

We got to the Kendall hotel late so decided to eat at their restaurant since it was so convenient even though we knew it had averaged 3 stars on yelp. We had a 3 star experience as well. The waitress was very nice and helpful. I ordered a drink with Campari in it, and she warned me that I might not like it. Even brought over a sample. Thank goodness! I do NOT like Campari. I ended up getting a pomegranate martini which was average- but drinkable. The free bread basket had an assortment of bread which was okay but not amazing. I ordered a salad with pears and gorgonzola cheese-- it was again just okay.  I ordered the scallops off the menu. The scallops were delicious and the portion was very generous. The roasted vegetables weren't cooked enough-- although seasoned well. My boyfriend ordered off the prix fixe menu-- their salmon entree, basic salad, a glass of wine and dessert for $34. Salmon portion was small and somewhat bland. The Boston cream pie was pretty darn tasty.
I really, really, really want to like this place. The layout is awkward. The staff is awkward. The food is okay. The wine list is pretty decent.

If you order the right thing and you're not in a rush, it's quite a good place to have a leisurely lunch.  If you're in a rush, go somewhere else because it's small, has only a few tables, and a few servers to go with it.

On the upside,  the tomato basil soup is great, the steak tip plate is great (has a huge plate of greens with tasty steak tips on top - it's more of a hearty steak salad than just a plate of tips), the chicken pot pie is good on a cold day.  On the sandwich side, the roast beef sandwich is probably one of the best sandwiches there.

The menu doesn't change often - so if you like it, great, if not, it gets boring fast.

Pricey, but one of the few nice places to get a bite to eat right in the Kendall Square area.  So if you're on someone else's dime, and have time to spare, it's a good place to eat lunch.  Otherwise, try the food court across the street.

I'm very fond of Darwin's - they have an awesome selection of sandwiches and their craft beer selection is really fun and quirky. This Darwin's on Mt. Auburn is almost a small combo convenience store sandwich shop. The staff are always very nice and frighteningly efficient - they manage to really churn out those sandwiches during rush hour with amazing speed. 

Darwin's gets extremely crowded during the lunch rush, but as mentioned previously the staff is efficient enough that it isn't much of an issue most of the time. They have a pretty dizzying array of sandwich options, but my favourite are definitely the roast beef (with that delicious horseradish mayo) or the smoked turkey sandwich. 

Sandwiches come piled high with meats; they're a good size where one sandwich could probably fit 1.5 people - it's not enough if you split with someone else but eating the whole thing makes you feel kind of sick. 

I would try to eat Darwins sandwiches as soon as you buy them - I've found that they really don't keep well past an hour or so, and tend to get extremely soggy.

This is my special lunch place that I don't share with too many people... This place offers hearty comfort food. Lunch has fabulous options like the Waldor Salad or the Crab Cakes. Portions are generous and the quality of ingrediants is of the highest caliber. This restaurant is absolutely charming. The colors are provencial, the furniture resembles american antiques.  No it's not romantic, its simple and adorable. The Black Sheep will forever remain one of my "happy places".

This review is for the restaurant. The hotel its self seems cutesy and nice enough. 

I wish i could say the same for the food. We had slow rude service, lots of confusions over the appetizer we actually ordered and as if that wasn't enough, the food was bland and oily and it made both me and my sister sick. 

if your going to stay here, go out to eat.

I just discovered this place and love it.  I've only had the taro boba but it is fresh and tasty.  The seating area is clean and comfortable with a large screen television in the background and free wi-fi.  Besides the drink I was really impressed with the bathroom.  Large and clean.  A great new hangout.  Can't wait to try all the other drinks and the food too.

This place is pretty great!
+perfectly cooked chewy but not raw and not gluey tapioca bubbles
+SOY MILK!!
+a million flavors 
+friendly staff
+cute decor
+comfy seating
+free wireless internet
+good frequent buyer card (buy 5 get one free)
+they will adjust the sugar content to order, so it doesn't have to be overwhelmingly sweet

...thank you!  :D

Went to lunch at Changsho with a group of 11 other people.  We got there and the restaurant was fairly empty, but by noon it was crowded.  I was impressed by the variety of food offered and how often they replaced quantity as soon as it was low.  They had Chinese variety that were good as well as sushi that was also surprisingly good. Highly recommend for lunch.

We stayed in the hotel on a bitter cold and snowy night and decided to "eat in" rather than be adventurous. That was definitely the right decision.

Our choice of a light meal turned out to be quite hearty. We shared the delicious 6 shrimp skewer and enjoyed the bread basket which was half mixed whole grain and half slightly sweet white bread with a hint of orange. The Engine 7 salads with blue cheese and nuts were incredible. Our bowls of soup, firehouse chili and butternut squash/apple, were very good. We passed on dessert but the four choices looked incredible.

The rather small wine list had good variety. A merlot blend from the low end was luscious, even though I'm not a great fan of merlot.

The wait staff was very polite, chipper, neatly dressed, extremely efficient. If there is a problem with the speed or quality of service, we saw no hint of it.

The breakfast, which is included in the room rate, is great.

So, a nice atmosphere complete with fireplace, really good food, and fine service - we really are fans and recommend the Black Sheep to anyone.

My husband and I went to Changsho with some of my work colleagues as a group dinner. The crab puffs were really great (not too much cream cheese, not too oily). He ordered the chicken & shrimp fried rice and I ordered the pepper shrimp which were amazing -- lots of shrimp! So we ended up not even touching the rice that came with my shrimp and just sharing our meals.... amazing.

They also have these chocolate fortune cookies that are quite interesting! Overall, a very delicious Chinese restaurant.
Massive lunch time lines..... maybe new staff? prices are ok, sandwhiches good. Coffee decent. Staff are friendly. I hope the lines change soon.

Good for a relaxing lunch. 

The food here is not bad, it is just pricey. The roast beef sandwich was enjoyable, packed with real roast beef, and very substantial. The tomato soup was spot on. The crab cake (singular, in the menu as crab cakes) over greens was good, but for $20 not sure it was worth it. 

Service is professional, but it's not going to be fast enough for a quick in and out lunch in less than 30 min. 

Come if you want a slow, relaxing lunch in the summer, especially if you can seat in the outdoor patio.

Great little hole in the wall place.  Authentic Korean food.  I had chapchae which was really tasty, but truly on the pricey side.  Nonetheless, they seem to have a steady following of regular diners.  Most of the people showing up for dinner didn't seem like first timers.  I couldn't believe that I was charged for tea!  Guess I take that for granted in San Francisco.

I went to Changsho last week for lunch with people from work. The lunch buffet was $11.99 which, while not the cheapest lunch in my history, is really not that expensive for a buffet in a sit down restaurant. 

They have their own parking lot, which is across the street (not next to them where you would expect) which is a great feature in Cambridge as you don't have to find and pay for street parking.

The tables where clean, the atmosphere was nice, and the lighting was perfect. They had round glass rotating surface tops on the table, which allowed the waiter to drop all the drinks and soup off in one spot and then simply rotate it to us, rather than putting their arms in between every person. I thought this was a nice touch. 

The Food: The buffet was not the largest buffet I've ever seen but the food that was there was delicious. This is not authentic Chinese food, but American Chinese food. Personally that's exactly what I want. 

The chicken was white meat, always a plus, the vegetables where available sauteed, fried, or steamed. The crab rangoon were fresh, even if they were lacking any crab. The pot stickers were delicious. The only complaint I had was the noodles seemed cold, even when they were just delivered to the line. 

For parties of 6+ a tip of 18% is automatically added to the bill, and while I saw no problem with this there were other people at the table who were unhappy about this. Personally I would have tipped 20%. The only reason I mention this is because it took us a few minutes to realize why the bill was higher than we had expected. 

All in all a good experience, I'll eat at Changsho's again.

Horrible!

I live in the neighborhood and one day I forgot my nice sunglasses that had been given to me as a gift by my partner. I came back the next day to get them, and I was told they had kept my sunglasses in an office but the door was locked and they didn't have the keys. I came back the following day and a different person and the manager checked and did not find my glasses. I told them their co-worker had told me they had the glasses and the manager aggressively yelled at me asking if I was insinuating they had stoled my glasses! Unbelievable. I have never been back since and recommend you do the same.

Their regular slices are meh.

The sicilian slices are PURE HEAVEN.

Their spinach one is like CRACK!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
Great coffee, charming atmosphere, passionate and friendly staff, and delicious waffles. What more could I ask for?

We've eaten in several times and do weekly take-out. The folks are friendly and efficient. The food is fresh and delicious! I highly recommend pea pod shoots, seafood with pan-fried noodles and cumin pork. It is so good.

This place was impossible to find and was dead on a Saturday night. And when I looked at the menu I knew why. This restaurant was really BIG with really dumbed down American decor. The menu is primarily traditional Schezuan cuisine - which I love. But I can see where it isn't for everyone. And I can't imagine that they fill the dining room with such an authentic menu. Our meal was great - very spicy and everything was very fresh. Traditional pork and cabbage was great and the dan dan noodles were to die for. If you're in the mood for a "real" Asian meal - don't be scared off by the weird ambiance. Give Anise a try..

My boss just brought sandwiches for lunch. He got 2 "veggie specials" and 2 turkey... Not sure about the turkey, but the veggie sandwich was absolutely amazing. garden burgers, tomatoes, romaine lettuce... and best of all: the wildly tasty (mango?) chutney, oozing from the crest of that delicious bread, dripping down the sides of my mouth.  I couldn't help but slowly lift my finger to my chin and collect the sauce to my tongue similar to a filthy sweat pouring down my temple after a close game of foozball...
A must stop anytime I am in the Boston area. Tastes like college, lasting friendships, and love. Best hots for sandwiches anywhere.

I thought I hated Chinese food, until I tried Anise. I went here a couple of weeks ago with a friend, who suggested we grab Chinese. Begrudgingly I went along, assuming we'd walk out with some wire-handled carry-out boxes filled with chicken fingers and friend rice. Instead, what we got, was pretty much a killer meal.

Sadly, the names of our meals escape me, since both were good enough to recommend. My friend ordered the fried chicken and chili dish that had the disclaimer "good choice," which we took to be a good enough recommendation. Mine was a beef with chili and cabbage, which was dubbed a "traditional" choice on the menu---just my style. 

My friend's chicken was great. The texture was amazing and chicken was cooked perfectly. The beef, however, was out. Of. This. Friggin. World. It was so good that both of our eyes bugged out when we took a bite. It's been almost two weeks since we had dinner there; I'm still dreaming about it.

The beef absolutely melted in your mouth. I continued eating long after I was full because I've never experienced meat that was so well-cooked in my entire life. 

Both meals were short on the vegetables, but well-seasoned. They were spicy enough to need the white rice that came with them, but hardly lip-burners.

Sadly, we walked into Anise so famished that we went right for the entrees---so no reviews of the appetizers. To make matters worse, we were in a bit of a rush, so we didn't have time for dessert and drinks. We definitely didn't give Anise the time it deserved; we'll definitely be back some time soon.

Overall, Anise has remedied everything that usually makes me cringe when I walk into a Chinese restaurant: they have shorter, more directed menus; made clear what the chef's specialties really are; and offered the authentic dishes on the English-language menu. 

My only complaint: They had a Bonnie Tyler and a Michael Bolton CD on alternate shuffle. Fix that small detail Anise and you might just see your tables fill up on a Friday night.

Connoisseur of bubble tea I am not, but I like this place. Their bubble tea product is good, although a bit sweet compared to others I have had. I have also sampled the smoothies--they use real fruit in some of them (but maybe not all) which makes me feel like I am having something a bit more healthy. Conveniently located within walking distance of my workplace, this place is addictive. They offer lunch specials, which are reasonable, but a bit pricey. ~$11 for lunch and a milk tea (bubbles/boba/pearls are extra) which is passable asian food. I like it because they put an egg on top (never underestimate the power of the egg, which comes close to that of bacon) and it is the closest place that can satisfy my almost constant asian food craving.

So, why only three stars? These guys have some kinks to work out. Efficiency is certainly not their forte and I have witnessed this result in a few less-than-satisfactory customer interactions. They need to work out how to deal with more than two customers in the store at a time and how to effectively handle unexpected bumps in the road. I have high hopes for this place if they can get their ducks in row.

The staff is always really nice and they have cute, modern decor that has largely been purchased from Ikea, which comforts me. Definitely worth checking out, just be wary if they are busy; you may want to consider coming back when you are the only customer.

Little sandwich and coffee shop- the place is right by my school and is a regular spot for everyone in the building. Sometimes the staff can be a bit short, but I assume it's because they're so busy. Avoid it around 3pm as there is a school that lets out nearby and it swells up with loud teenagers. I especially appreciate the tiny 8 oz. cups for when you just need a lil' boost of caffeine in the afternoon, and the assortment of tiny perfect cookies they sell every day.

Cambridge is a tough spot to find lunch. Most places are either pricey or crappy, and usually both.

Darwin's is neither. Soups and sandwiches are super fresh, the staff is rad, cookies are rocking, and they've got an awesome wine selection just in case you've got a hot date later or your nearby job has a really lax booze policy.

Just get ready to hear your name yelled loudly if you order something to go. And finding a seat can be a competitive experience.

Great new pub in Inman Square. Their food is great. Get the Mac and Cheese, it is the best I've had in Boston. The rotisserie chicken with mashed potatoes and gravy is a great option if you are looking for comfort food. The buffalo wings are also a great snack. Good food. Friendly Irish staff. Awesome beer selection. What is not to love?

Went here and got "non traditional" American Chinese and it was great! We got the Pork Trotter and Soy Sauce Duck. Then we went back and I had shrimp and veggies and he got Lo mein. Both were boring and had little flavor. The shrimp were plentiful, but tasted bad so I was afraid to eat them. I didn't get sick though...
The food is always delicious and the service is prompt and courteous. I recommend thiis restaurant.

A little spendy for what it is, but fine food.  And yes, it's true, the owner is a real character.  She is not an accomodating how-may-I-serve you waitress.  Expect demure service, upscale decor, and patience with your stroller and baggage?  You'll be disappointed.  Want a pot of something hot, have a few extra dollars, an adaptable attitude, and no toddlers in tow?  You'll be fine.

Maybe I'm biased because I *really* liked the Mexican place that used to be here, but honestly Cilantro was pretty meh. 

Don't get me wrong, some of the food is pretty good, and props to them for having Peking Duck. The prices are very reasonable, and the staff is quite nice. Portions are good, service is speedy, food is not bad, and they have cute little teapots. 

They have dim sum on the weekends - the menu kind, not the cart kind. Selection is pretty good and covers all the basics - har gao, shao mai, the little spareribs, and pork buns. Their soup dumplings came oddly dry, and the scallion pancakes came without sauce, but overall it's pretty good. 

I think Cilantro's biggest flaw is that it's redundant without bringing much new to the table, especially with the already passable Chinese options in the area. If I want scallion pancakes or greasy Asian fare, I'll go to the Kong. If I want great soup dumplings, Dumpling House does it better. If I want something a little more homey, I'll go to Yenching. There's nothing inherently wrong or bad with the food or service here, but I don't really see how Cilantro adds anything new to the Harvard Square Chinese food scene.

This is my new favorite bar in Cambridge. Amazing food, great decor, and the service was phenomenal. I went in for Sunday brunch and had the two eggs with bacon. The brunch at The Rising rivals proper brunch places as my dish was superb. Will definitely be stopping by again.

My boyfriend absolutely loves the "Uncommon Pizza" and looks forward to getting it whenever he comes to visit me in Cambridge. The pizza is delicious, fresh and you can tell the ingredients are quality. However, the only way to get it is to physically go get it yourself. 

We have ordered delivery from them twice, and both times have been unfortunate experiences. The first time the delivery guy came an hour and 15 minutes after we ordered, with a cold pizza and then forgot the 2-liter of diet coke that was part of our order. My boyfriend paid the apologetic and polite delivery guy, who then told us he would be right back to bring the soda. He never showed up. 

The second time, we told ourselves we should probably go pick it up, but we were busy packing for a vacation and decided to give the delivery service another chance. This time they showed up 15 minutes later with the pizza which was great, but then forgot my salad. The again apologetic delivery guy said he would be right back, and an hour and 10 minutes later, still no salad. We then got a call apologizing for the salad, and the manager said they would take it off the bill. We appreciated the offer, but the dinner was already ruined. My boyfriend ate his pizza cold, trying to wait for my salad, and then i ended up eating my salad alone an hour and 40 minutes later. 

Unfortunately, we will not be ordering from Upper Crust again unless they can work on their service issues. I'm not sure if it is management or the drivers, but there is a disconnect somewhere and it is very obvious. We hope they do, because it is really good pizza!

So. Amazing. 

I had mocha made with Taza chocolate and their plain Belgium waffle. Never had better mocha and waffle. I am now considering moving to Boston. 'Nough said.
Great food.  Good value.  Nice they are open on Sunday when the rest of the neighborhood is pretty much dead.  Great almond bubble tea.

Love it.

I really want to give this three and a half, since I do crave it from time to time but it also is a little pricey for what you get.  

The house special fried rice is awesome, if a little heavy.  The salt and pepper pork, or salt and pepper calamari are really good too.  

I've been to the lunch buffet once, too (a while back so they might have changed it.).  What is it about those things that make you think you can eat five people's worth of lunch in one sitting?  And I'm a little put off by sushi at a buffet at a Chinese place, but hey, the rest of it is pretty good.

Great local pizza joint. We ordered a large Fenway (sausage, onion, peppers) and it was a good serving size for two people with plenty of leftovers. We really enjoyed the thin, crunchy crust and fresh flavors. Would definitely return!

I have been to Pinocchios before but had to come back on my pizza hopping adventure while back in town because- nothing beats East Coast pizza! This was my first stop and, as usual, the pizza was phenomenal! They charge about 3.75/slice for this cheesy goodness. After trying a few other spots in the area along my journey (and during my time living in Boston) this place tops my list. Its small inside, but there are a few tables. On a nice day you can grab food to go and sit out in the yard or other appropriate people watching spot
Like the place but it is too cold. When you ask them to increase temp you get unhelpful responses. So, I recommend avoiding the place in winter!

I had great vegetarian dumplings and a coconut milk tea for lunch, and it was so good I went back for an after lunch smoothie. Fast, friendly, incredibly tasty- AND 5 minutes from my office. I couldn't ask for more.

I am Chinese and have lived in the U.S. for 10 years. My boyfriend is caucasian. We just moved to a house a block away from Anise. 

I heard Anise is one of the best Chinese restaurant in Boston, but never tried it myself. My boyfriend went there once with his colleagues for lunch and said it was horrible, which pretty much stopped me from trying. One day, I was really craving for Chinese food and didn't want to go far, so I convinced myself and my boyfriend to have dinner there.

It was by far one of the best Sichuan (Chinese) restaurant I've ever been to in the U.S. (I lived quite some time in NYC and SF also). All the ingredients are very authentic. I was extremely happy aftter the meal.

I think here are the reasons why it wan't very popular
1) It's strength is authentic Chinese food, but  it's much more upscale than most other Chinese restaurant which is not good as most Chinese around are students who are price sensitive
2) The other strength is it's nice ambience and a lounge/bar area that opens till late which could attract many non-Chinese professionals, but the food is too "authentic" (i.e., spicy) for non-Chinese to adapt

Anyways, their appetizers are quite reasonably priced $5-7 and have some of the best dishes

Had lunch there recently with my partner. The prices for lunch are very reasonable and the food was ok, though mine was pretty bland and boring. I did like the brown rice, though, and my partner's dish was much more tasteful/spicy. The waiter seemed a bit absent-minded, but was nice enough. It's certainly a good option for a quick and cheap lunch.
Had lunch there again and was pretty disappointed. We explicitly asked for "very spicy" and the food was still bland. But it's nice to be able to sit outside. Still, the food is just boring.

Cozy bar with decent beer and food. I stop by on occasion for the happy hour oysters, $1 each and always tasty. 
Staff is friendly, easy to flag down, and on slow nights fun to chat with.
Plenty of TVs with sports playing if that's your thing and has trivia and other events depending on the day of the week. 
The only downside is that it can get pretty noisy even even when if it's a quiet night. 
I live fairly close and I see myself enjoying many a Friday evening here to close out the work week.

Have I never reviewed Changsho?  Seriously?

I have been to Changsho repeatedly over the past few years and have never had a bad experience.  My boyfriend and I tried it out on a whim a while back and the food was reasonably priced and delicious.  It's Americanized Chinese food that is better than take out, but by no means authentic.  Exactly what I like!

I've come here with a large group the last two New Year's Eves.  The service has always been spectacular.  We got a few apps and several dishes and paid about 20 bucks each.  Not bad!  Family style's the way to go, even though the portions are not all that big.

The ambiance is beautiful and I will be back often.

Had dinner here last night after drooling over the menu all day. The first thing you notice when you walk in is the various board and tabletop games all around the restaurant-- my husband and I had fun posing trivia questions to each other while we waited for our order.

The menu itself is set up in a slightly confusing way-- the first page shows duelling versions of a dish: two mac n' cheese options, two taco options, etc. The next page shows regular dishes, and then there's a section for things like "Taco Tuesday" and "Slider Sunday" that lists some great-looking dishes that are only available on those particular days. Kind of sad, since I saw some options that I would've been interested in if they'd been available. Without those extra options the menu selection is slightly smaller than I'd expect, but everything on it looked good.

After asking our server how many dishes we should order (he said that one dish was usually enough for him, but I'm glad we didn't take his advice because I'd say that two per person was probably a better idea, or at least 1.5), we ordered four.

The bacon and gouda mac n' cheese was excellent-- nice and creamy, with a smoky flavor and just enough greenery on top to cut the heaviness. The potato skins with BBQ brisket were a touch disappointing-- they didn't have much cheese on them and the ratio of potato to topping was a bit off, making them seem dry. The crab cakes were decent-- nothing special, a little breadier than I like-- and the corn salsa that came with them was tasty. The real disappointment was the ahi tuna tacos, which I had not realized were going to contain well-done tuna. I do not blame the menu at all-- it never said either way-- but I'd been expecting seared tuna, which I always think tastes better.

We also ordered a tasting flight of 4 beers, which were good with the exception of the hard cider. I'll give our server credit-- he did warn us that the cider's description of "spicy" was due to jalapeno flavor rather than more usual cinnamon/ginger flavors-- but we ordered it anyway and regretted it. Do not get the cider unless you're a real fan of jalapeno-- not just the kick but the specific flavor of jalapeno peppers. But other than that, we enjoyed our drinks.

All in all, this is a good restaurant. Not amazingly fantastic, but good. It's likely that we'll be back at some point, probably with a group. The food was relatively reasonably priced, and the tacos were definitely better than those at Lolita, which is the last place I had fancy tacos. I'd recommend it!

Didn't have a chance to eat here when it was newer, but the food at my recent lunch here was certainly nothing special. Really just take-out quality, which isn't what you come to a place like this for, is it?

Rude service can NEVER be excused by good food.  Never.  Did we learn nothing from the soup nazi?

I went last Thursday night around 7pm with 3 other people.  The place was completely empty except for us.  2 of my friends wanted to split a bebimbap, another wanted just an appetizer, and I wanted a meal.  The owner waited on us, and said effectively, we are too small a place to let you split meals, or only get an appetizer!  Then she rudely snatched our menus away. We all felt horribly shocked and left in a daze.  I thought we were in a Seinfeld episode.  I really wanted Kramer to egg the building.

If there was a disclaimer about minimum orders on the menu, then we would have ordered a minimum amount, OR just not have entered the restaurant.  No one should have to be treated so rudely!   What a NASTY woman.   I would not be surprised if her place went out of business.    Maybe if she had let us order a small amount, I would have fallen in love with the food, come back for takeout, or recommended it to my friends.   Now I won't.  In fact, I will do the opposite and encourage all I know to boycott them.  The reason they are a small shop is because they are NOT interested in selling food or making money!  Just being rude.  They lost out on our order, and any future orders, and who knows, we may have ended up ordering EVEN more, once we had a few tastes.  I will never forget how I was treated at this place, and will never forget the name of it.  I will tell all who I meet about how horrible it was.  Plus, who wants to walk through the kitchen to use the bathroom?  Think about it.  How unhygenic.  

And yes, this is a real review from a real person. I joined yelp specifically to review this place, but will do others later I'm sure.  And I bet you that the other reviews are NOT fake spam, like others suggest.

I hate that my first review is a negative one, but this wasn't the first time this happened with this specific location. This would be a decent review but my order never arrived. Estimated delivery time was 40 minutes. I called after an hour - "they're on their way", called again 30 minutes later (I live 15 min away) a "they're on their way, they had to come back to pick up the Pizza"...another 30 minutes go by. For ONE pizza! Called back because it got too late. So annoying, and their only suggestion the last time we spoke was to take off 30%. Terrible customer service. Sorry.

I had absolutely no idea that I liked bubble tea, but I do. The jelly's at the bottom of the cup really make the drink for me - maybe it's a texture thing. It's definitely a bit pricey, but well worth it. I work in the area, so I am sure I'll be back to try another tea. They had a very extensive selection. Maybe a frequent tea drinkers card?

I've gone many, many times and never had a bad experience. I think I've ordered every veggie and fish dish on the menu. It's all good, though the soups are the best. 

Personally, I like the lady who runs the place. The first time I went there, she "helped" me stirred the bi bim bap, but I didn't mind. She's always friendly, even if she has a bossy streak.

The atmosphere is a little like someone's kitchen-- too-bright lights, awkward table arrangements, plus I think the same classical music has been playing on a boom box in the corner there for five years-- but I prefer that style to the TGIFs/Chili's feel that plagues most Boston area restaurants.

P izza late at night or
I   n the afternoon. 
N ice for a group of friends or a slice
O n the go. I love that it is tucked away and you 
C an only find it if you know where to look.
C an't go wrong with a
H ot Sicilian square or something
I   n the shape of a triangle. Lots
O f sandwiches, drinks and tables as well. Beware of
S tudents though. If you are not in the mood - they can be obnoxious.

This place probably has the best iced coffee I've ever had - super smooth and not bitter/acidic at all. The iced tea is delicious too!

There are a couple serious downsides to this place: There's very limited table space and hours (seriously, what other coffeeshop closes at 2-3pm??) This makes the current Curio Coffee only a four-star place, but I'm giving five and hoping that'll encourage Curio to stay open later!

Had dinner on a Thursday night. We are vegetarian - and there are lots of veggie options here, all clearly marked with a "V" on the menu (that's really nice to have from a Chinese place). The eggplant garlic dish was just awesome. Tender and a slight bit sweet.

The decor and dishware are really nice. Its true that the restaurant was fairly empty. Looks like they survive from all the lunch business as its within a high density office area. Will definately go again. Great place for large groups or families with kids since there's no wait.

Amazing waffles. The dough is buttery and there are big chunks of sugar that add the right amount of sweetness.

Coffee takes great, they use counter culture beans. Very friendly staff, and cool decor. The atmosphere is urban, chic with some western vibes.

As much as I don't love this place, I end up frequenting it fairly often (once a week, sometimes more). I work nearby, and it really is (usually) one of the fastest and cheapest places to get a filling sandwich.

These people see me all the time. They're never extremely friendly and a few of them mumble and I can barely hear what they're saying when they ask about condiments or how much I owe them at the register.  I mean, I can't imagine being super eager to make sandwiches all day, but I do really value customer service and making the customer at least THINK you wouldn't rather be somewhere else.

Food quality is similar to any other Subway I've been to. Mediocre, bread sometimes a little stale. Eh. It is convenient, and unless the line is really long I can get in and out pretty quickly.

Last night was horrible! Waited 40 mins for a pizza that was never made!!!!! No one had made the pizza at all! Demanded my money back and was told they didn't even need my credit card to refund me! If I find a charge on my credit card I am going to be angry! And I read about this happening before-- while reading yelp reviews. Erika if your reading you need to learn how to fire people!

Today at 3pm, I ordered a wheat foot long with some meatballs, but they were out of provolone cheese. Subway is a huge company and they were out of provolone...how could this happen? That's very bad. Yes I could have other cheese, but I don't like them. It's their problem that they're behind. Very important for their costumers to get they want and expect it to be available because it's a large company.

I've been waiting for a bubble tea station in the area for as long as I've been here and thought that I would have to give up bubble tea for good. Your only other options right now are starbucks and that place in the kendall food court. This place won't disappoint with those as comparison points. I was totally happy with the quality and comprehensive menu they had. I ordered myself a jasmine milk tea + egg pudding for $4.60. Other plus points: (a) they're open in the early morning so that I can use it as a coffee replacement (b) the menu is a little pricey but they make deliveries in a smart car so I'm OK with it, just get yourself a loyalty card (c) free wifi

They also have ethnic treats and sushi snacks. It was really tempting but I'll have to save that for the next time I go.

The waffles! These are the best, crispiest, tastiest waffles I've ever had the pleasure of trying. I got a cortado and my partner got a cafe au lait. Both drinks were fantastic. The interior is so adorable. I wish they had a location in Detroit so I could have that waffle every day.

This pizza used to be great and now it's mediocre at best. I ordered the Spinach Leaf, which was flimsy, cold and barely had any toppings. I wish I had know it was going to be this bad, so I could have ordered from Emma's or Armando's. On the bright side, the shift manager Zach was very nice and offered a few options of recourse. Sadly, it sounded like he's had some practice apologizing for their food.

I walked by this abandoned fire station on Main Street for years, I'm so glad it was remodeled not demolished. The sandwiches and salads here are superb - I love going there for lunch. They are pricey - ~12 for a sandwich, so go on an expense account if you can. Probably the best lunch in Kendall Square.

service 5
General Ghaos chicken 5
egg drop soup 4
fried rice 3.5 
crab Rangoon 4.5

staff 5

Surprise - I used to hate this place for high prices, bad service and "american" food. 

Tonight we went for an event with about 50 people. I went reluctantly and was stunned. Service was excellent. Food was very tasty and authentic, refined Chinese. Something that you would find in Shanghai, Beijing or Shenzhen (okay those are not culinary centers of the world, but they are professional). 

The menu was preset (and I had no part in ordering). Everything was covered - appetizers (some chicken on a salad shell), delicious soup (no idea what it was, but it contained some meat and grain and wasn't the canned kind one could have expected), main course variety: very tasty beef tips, phenomenal steamed fish, chicken, pork, shrimp, duck, mushrooms with some green vegetables, outstanding fried rice (that wasn't over soyed) and finally a delicious cake. 

Special surprise - A vegetarian on our table. That's a problem at most Chinese Restaurants. Not here!!! They automatically substituted the first chicken appetizer with pancakes. Soup then came automatically in a vegetarian version. Individual servings of tofu and vegetables appeared along side all the meat dishes. All delivered with a smile. 

Along the way I ordered a couple of bottles of wine (which was not part of the preset dinner). The 2010 Catena Malbec was good, albeit over priced at close to $40 and I still don't appreciate the automatic addition of 18% tip to the liquor bill which made it almost $100. I just hope that the money really made it to the servers. They were excellent.
So happy to have this place in my neighborhood. Awesome coffee, fresh waffles, friendly staff, and a great atmosphere.
Very nice cafe shop! Forget my wallet there and owner keep it well. Great food and nice experience !

Curio's the best coffee shop in Cambridge. The interior's beautifully designed and cozy, full of the scrumptious smell of their liege waffles, and just the right amount of busy: lively, but I've never had trouble finding a table. I come here for the $2.50 pots of mem tea, big windows, and friendly staff who won't bat an eyelid if I work here for a couple hours. Well worth the walk even if you aren't an East Cambridger!

More like 3.5. Lots of love for the cobb salad. No love for the waitress (or perhaps a vagrant who was understandably confused by our expectation of service). Somewhat pricey, but the food is pretty good.

You might not want to eat here, because you may discover you really don't like Chinese food, that is real Chinese food. Unless you have had home cooked Sichuan/Szechuan or been to China, the taste sensations will challenge your palate in ways that it may not have been before. The menu here is unapologetically authentic. I went on a Tuesday for lunch and the place was quite full. I'm guessing the lunch crowd from Amgen et. al. keeps this place going, because I have seen reports that it is always empty. For lunch on a Tuesday it was packed. I wanted to have the Rabbit but they were out so I started with Spicy Beef Tendon, Pig Ears and Cucumbers, and Bamboo Shoots with Wild Fern. The tendons was very spicy and shaved wafer thin, it did have the mouth numbing heat. The pig ear was shaved slightly thicker but not too thick, it less spicy but quite delicious. We have been cooking bamboo shoots at home more and I'm reall a fan of the fresh stuff sliced quasi julienne, not like a can of overcooked LaChoy vegetables. The texture of a more al' dente shoot is wonderful. The real star of the dish however was the wild fern which was heavily pickled. This is the type of taste sensation I was eluding to before, completely foreign to most western diners. Cold and tangy yet crisp, perhaps even a tad slimey. Alternating bites of fern and bamboo made for a a real yin and yang counterpoint. Since it was lunch I did get a special the Double Cooked Pork bellies. These were served in a dry-ish sauce that could have been more spicy with rough chopped long green peppers and leeks. I devoured the entire plate in 3 minutes.

* The menu has English and Chinese, you don't have to hunt for the gems the Frog, Rabbit etc are right out there.
* they do have the spicy beef with napa cabbage, that will be my next choice.
* the service was slow, only 1 waitress and  hostess had the entire floor. I did not hold it against the staff.
* I did request brown rice but received jasmine rice instead.
* food came out relatively quickly
* Excellent bar with large Single Malt selection, I had a  Tsing Tao to chill the spicy heat. Fair wine list.
* Two TV's at the bar, SciFi channel and ESPN playing.
* NIce layout with a small but cozy waiting area and hip furnishings.

My favorite pizza in Boston/Cambridge.  Period.  It's thick and perfectly seasoned.  Don't leave without trying the tomato basil--it's 10 times better than their plain cheese and probably their best.  Spinach and artichoke are both really good as well.  Stick to one of these three and you'll be safe.  
Not a date place (trust me, you will not impress), seating is cramped, limited and lacks any sort of ambiance.

I wanted to like this place.  It's fast, reasonably priced, and as convenient as can be (given where I work and where I live).

But on my visit today, they refused to serve a cup of water.  "And a cup of water, please." "We don't have water." "Just a cup, and I can get it myself?"  "Nope.  You have to buy it."  He pointed to a cooler full of $1-$2 bottles.

That sucks.  Water in a bottle is a rip-off not to mention an environmental blunder.  (All that energy going into plastic and fuel, when we have perfectly pipes built for exactly this task.)

As best I can tell, Subway has no corporate policy prohibiting location from charging for water.  OK.  But I'll never go back to this place -- never -- so long as they keep charging for water.

You know, people who review Seoul Food poorly are one of two things: 
1. stupid white people who don't know Korean food
2. rude customers who do something stupid

Because hands down, this is my favorite restaurant in the whole Boston area. We drive across town to come here now, and we come here for special occasions.  I would never, ever trust the culinary opinions of anyone who spoke poorly of this restaurant, because it would mean that they either don't know food or are the kind of people I wouldn't want to go out to eat with. Or both.
My husband and I headed out of the suburbs for a night out in Cambridge. We enjoyed playing trivia games and puzzles together over drinks and delicious food.  I highly suggest this bar/restaurant.

my dad and i hit up this place before a movie one weeknight last winter. the place was empty so service was fast and friendly. we told them we were in a bit of a hurry and they were accomdating. 

the food was good but nothing out of the ordinary. the food was a bit greasy, but that was to be expected. my dad's soup was spicy as requested and there were smiles all around. 

as far as a quick meal before a movie or after work, i would say go for it. it is not a cheap chinese place, but will not break the bank either.

Laid back, chill vibe, good sandwiches & coffee, lots of outlets to plug your computer in, huge tea selection, cool art, sells the New York Times and located a little off of the beaten path.  I couldn't find anything not to like about this place if I tried.

Very fast service, decent chinese food and decent atmosphere.  The spring rolls were hot, fresh and tasty.  Shu mai was also good.  Beef and broccoli, general gau chicken and shrimp with macadamia nuts were all good.  Price was a little steep ($10-12 for each entree which seemed to serve 2 people) but its a nicer Chinese restaurant so i guess you pay a bit more.  I would come back.

Not a bad option for lunch. I came here on a school function so I did not see how much it cost, but the food selection wasn't too bad. 
Some hilights include chicken skewers, curry fish, shrimp, veggies, tempura veggies, California rolls, and some fruits and cakes for dessert. Not a to-die-for kind of place, but not bad when you want Chinese food and variety. 
My favorite is that they have chocolate fortune cookies! =] 

Side note: if you drive, the parking lot is ACROSS the street from the actual restaurant.

Not bad for a white-ified Chinese restaurant! The food quality is good and the ambiance is inviting to your non-Asian friends. I'd recommend the fried rices, noodles, and an assortment of the appetizers. I haven't had a wrong dish yet.

I recently changed my rating from five to four stars, because I went back and had some different menu items that were not quite as good.  I think my five-star rating might also have been a result of my server's totally hot ass.  Nonetheless, my original review below is still appropriate:

My roommate and I wandered into this place when Cambridge Brewing was overflowing with people and we had a movie to catch in an hour.  What an experience!

Completely refreshing menu (sorry, no Gen Tso's Chicken here), serene atmosphere, attentive staff...  My roommate lived in China for several years and this was the best Chinese meal he's had in the US.  I completely agree, this may be the finest Chinese I've ever sampled.

I think Allix T. was expecting something like Avalon--I can understand her disappointment.  I will be returning to Anise very soon.

Came here for the $1 oyster specials. They were the Wellfleet, East Coast oysters are on the briny side, which I love. Service was quick and polite. The happy hour portions are the same as the regular house appetizers so we got a good amount of apps and then ordered some steak tips. Overall all the food was good. Fried calamari was a bit overfried but I think it's their batter. 

They have a long list of different draft beers and a nice atmosphere to just enjoy a quiet meal.
If you love 7 to 10 dollar 13 ounce drafts and a cutesie-poo theme, this is the place for you.
I stopped by recently with my wife and baby to check out this new Chinese restaurant.  I was not disappointed.  The food was tasty and the service was prompt.  I would definitely go back.

The bubbling fish could be the best dish I've ever had. Period. Like flavorful butter. My wife loved the salt and pepper shrimp. Wow! Great place. Cabbage with dried shrimp fabulous. Peking ravioli great. Can't believe anyone could say anything negative about this place.

This is not meant to be a swinging bar but rather a really fantastic place to eat - If you want to go to a really trendy, hot spot - then go to a crowded bar - don't go to a restaurant and get pissed that it is - well - a restaurant.  I have thoroughly enjoyed every meal I have had here - and also appreciate that I can get in without a fuss and can hear my friends when we dine here as a group.

I find the food absolutely wonderful - the last time I was there was in May.  I also especially love their tea, which is served in a glass tea pot and opens as a flower...it is artistic as well as wonderfully yummy.  I agree that the food is not typical or Americanized - but that is one of the things I liked about it...also, they have a sister restaurant in Medford, and I think this restaurant beats Medford out - which was previously a favorite.

I also like that I can park at the garage and get a validation - so, it is easy to get here and get out...

I do understand why some people would be freaked out that there are few people there...I can say from my experiences eating there that it is not in any way indicative of bad food - just perhaps bad marketing.  So, I hope it picks up - because I love to eat here, and I want them to stay around for a while.

I feel ambivalent about this.  I give 4 stars for the tea and 0 for the "Fusion Rolls."   The tea is good - if you've never had bubble tea before you'll probably rave about it, it's pretty fun to drink/eat.  Their version can be a bit of a sugary slurry for my taste, but on the whole, not bad.  I get it regularly as I work nearby.

The "Fusion Rolls", at least the one I have tried, was terrible.  There were crunchy bits of rice in it.  The roll was too big to eat in one bite, but so sloppily put together thant it disintegrated after the first nibble.  To top it off, the "Tuna" in the roll tasted like it came from a can.  Truly disgusting.  I know they have a sign up that says "Like sushi but without the raw fish" (note: to me this is like saying "like a day without sunshine."  why bother?), but since I saw "tuna", I assumed they were making an exception and making sushi today.  Clearly not the case.  It was hard to choke down, and I'm sticking to the tea from now on.

A very nice and relaxing environment. Very tasty spicy szechuan which was plated beautifully. Pork dumplings w. rice vinagar were delicious! Food was very clean and not greasy. Loved the spicy chicken with cucumbers. An very attentive cute waiter. It was a holiday and was mid afternoon and no other customers. Not sure as of why.
Poor bathroom which is in some scary basement. 
Boo for charging $10 for a pot of oolong tea.

My husband and I were in town from Baltimore (a self proclaimed foodie city) visiting family and stumbled upon this place while looking for a cozy spot to grab a bite and some drinks. Sometimes things just happen for the right reason! We were seated immediately (even though they were quite busy, it was a Saturday night) and our drinks were out in seconds! The staff was super attentive and made sure everything was to our liking, the drinks were delicious and we never went without, and the food!!!! Wow!!! The small dishes were perfect for my husband and I to share and get a little bite of everything!! And the games at the table were the perfect way for us to actually put our phones away and spend time with each other!!!! Amazing place to reconnect with friends in a casual atmosphere that serves up some bangin food at a reasonable price!!! Highly recommend!

It's like they did some focus groups on Millennials and figured out exactly what they wanted.  For the most part they nailed it.  Games/movies/scrabble theme throughout. Great food and drink menu.  Fish tacos with Chipotle slaw were flavorful, although tortilla was like cardboard.  Sliders were great.  Beer flight rocked.  My GFs cocktails all hit the spot.  Friendly service. Intellectual crowd.

This place just opened but is already in midseason form. We got small pizza - half North End (meatballs, italian sausage, mozz) and half veggie with summer squash, zucchini, and artichoke added - and it was phenomenal.
