You might consider offing yourself right after consuming one of these pizzas because your life will only be downhill from there. Phenomenal.

No alcohol yet - liquor license pending.

best pizza in Cambridge bar none

delicious and healthy thin crust made with fresh dough

I like the Swellesley

Extremely mediocre Korean food.   

I worked a block from this place for a while, so I decided to stop in.  The one time I went here, I ordered beef okdol bi bim bab, my Korean staple.  I *love* bi bim bab.  Beef + egg + veggies + rice + hot sauce + soy sauce = spicy salty sexiness.  Well, this stuff just wasn't very good.  It wasn't served hot enough.  The quality of the ingredients just wasn't up to par and left me feeling very underwhelmed.  One of the foods of my dreams should never leave me underwhelmed.

The atmosphere is crappy.  I actually wasn't lucky enough to experience the server's beotchitude, I think she may have even liked me.  But she did sit there staring at me, which I wasn't lovin'  You want to take a picture or something.  The only time she really looked away is when I needed a refill of my dirty plastic water.  Nothing like some lukewarm water graciously devoid of ice to enjoy spicy food.  And by enjoy, I mean push underwhelming food over the edge to moderately nauseating.  Adding to the nausea is the general grubbiness of the place.

Honestly, I left this place feeling like a sucker.  Little hole in the wall Korean places shouldn't cost this much!  They should be priced like the much tastier Korean food in the Porter exchange just a block away.  And call me inauthentic or whatever for liking Korean food at a Thai restaurant, but I prefer the bi bim bab at Spice and Rice.  Tastier, cheaper, and MUCH better service.  Glasses with ice refilled promptly.  No weird staring.  

I would give this place 2 stars based on food, but the lack of cleanliness and too-high prices knock it down to 1 star.
Sandwich is excellent!  Recommend Mt Auburn. You get a lot if meat.  It's a little pricey $9, but your are full at the end.  Interior is cozy and has music and cafe section.

We all know what Subway is supposed to serve and how it's supposed to taste.

This location SUCKS! The employees were sitting on the floor in the back room when we walked up, I should have walked away right then. 

The bread was dry and crumbly, the employees barely spoke to us and mumbled any questions they had and the place was just dirty, trash and dirty floors, would not go back. Ever.

I read the bad reviews and I gave this place the benefit of the doubt. The other cases were two people sharing appetizers, etc. 

But I planned to go with a group of 8 people who were all hungry and were all interested in trying Korean food. First of all, it was really expensive korean food, not that  much too it, and the lady kicked us out saying that we took up three tables which equals SIX spots and we still weren't allowed to order six entrees. Each person had to get a $20 entree. Koreans SHARE food, there's a lot of meat or food in one entree. She could've easily suggested to us to order six entrees, but no, she was "losing business" just by seating us because we didn't want to order EIGHT entrees. 

Horrible horrible customer service. I also saw her yell at another group telling them to go to hell because there were no more tables left. It was just so awful. It wasn't even busy. She was so rude and slammed our menus and took them out of our hands and told us to basically f*ck off. I felt so embarrassed to be korean and to bring my american friends over. It was very degrading and pathetic.

I love the use of puns on everything on the menu, but my experience has been sub par the few times I've been here. The cocktails are hit or miss, so I'd stick to beer or wine. Location is great but it's not the first place I'd go for food (and definitely not drinks).

I really hate to give a scathing review based on one experience, but I doubt I'm ever going back here again, and the story must be told.

I had just finished dinner at home when a friend called me up and wanted to talk and grab a bite. It was a quiet weekday night, around 8pm, and neither of us had been to Seoul Food, so we figured we'd give it a try - I wasn't hungry, but tea and a cup of soup wouldn't hurt. The place was half-empty, so we took a table for two by the door and poured over the menu. The menu had lots of options, but none of them appetizer-sized. No big deal, I thought, I'll get some tea, and maybe a dessert. My friend said he'd get the larger sized soup, and ask for an extra bowl so I could try some. Deal.

It took a long time to get waited on, but we had catching up to do, so it didn't bother us too much. When the man came over to take our order, my friend ordered the large soup, and asked for an extra bowl. He said, "no, we don't do that." Pause. 
Me: "That's fine, don't worry about it." 
Friend: "So, I'll just have the soup, then." 
Man: "So, that's two soups?" 
Me: "No, it's okay, I'll just have some tea." 
Man: "No, you can't do that" **What?** "You can't do that. Two people, two meals." 
Me: "But I'm going to have some tea, and maybe later get some dessert." 
Man: "No, sorry." 
Friend: "You mean you aren't going to serve us?" 
Man: "Sorry."

At which point, we're flabbergasted (mind you, the place still has at least 3 empty tables) and collect our things and leave. We went to the Thai place across the street, where my friend had an awesome curry, and I got my $3 appetizer soup. Sheesh.

Maybe I should applaud this place for keeping true to its standards and not buying into the American model for a service economy, but I still can't believe I got kicked out of a restaurant for only wanting tea!

This place is the best...why; because they have the best sicilian pizza in boston. AND they are open till 2AM. I have spent a lot of late nights here. The workers are funny to when you see them mess with first timers.

This place is amazing. Great beer list, awesome cocktail menu, delicious food. AND the menu is full of puns, which hold a special place in my heart. Try the truffle fries, the buffalo cauliflower, and the tacos. And say hi to the owner, Pete, who is the man.

Five. Five dollar. Five dollar foot loooooongs! You all know the jingle by now, right?

What they probably don't tell you is that not ALL the foot-longs are five dollars. Only a few of them are (the basic cold cut ones), and the rest are specialty subs and cost a few bucks more. No biggie though. 

I like this Subway. Even though they are almost always packed, and they are super tiny, they are as efficient as a Chinese factory pumping out designer handbags. The assembly line here doesn't slow down regardless of what time of day it is. When you approach the counter, be ready to order -- what kind of sub do you want (check the menu)? What size (six inch? foot long?)? What kind of cheese? Do you want it toasted? What kind of veggies? What kind of dressing? Do you want to make it a meal and add a soda and chips/cookies/apples? Pay and then boom -- you're good to go. 

I had to dock them a star, because for the longest time, their soda machine was broken, so you had to get the cup from them, then walk across the way to Chutney's and fill up your soda from there. Um... AWKWARD. But, the soda machine is now fixed. No worries people, it only took two months. A little chink in the efficiency, but eh, we'll let that slide (pun intended - I'm allowed to joke though. I'm Chinese.).

I cannot speak for the food, but I can comment on the service.  We were told that the two of us could not share an appetizer and an entree, despite the fact that the entree was a large pot for around $15.00 and the restaurant was almost completely empty.  This restaurant is run as if it's in a different universe, one I don't belong in.
Waffle Waffle Waffle must try. Coffee is absolutely fresh. Coffee + Waffle became my Sat/Sun routine. Staffs are very friendly. Love this place.

Maybe it's just me, but I think Darwin's is good, but not great. Sure, fresh ingredients are good...and they do have yummy bread...but unfortunately, I think I could have gotten behind the line and both effectively and efficiently not only made my sandwich, but the four other people waiting in line with me before the girl made ONE. Now, I will concede that she might have been new, she could have been having a bad day...but it was my one and only experience here and I left feeling 1) jipped (it's flipping expensive) and 2) rushed (because I waited 15 minutes for a sandwich and had to get my butt to the train station ASAP). 

Over-priced sandwiches leave a bad taste in my mouth. Sorry Darwin's.
I always make time for Pinocchi's when I'm in harvard sq.  My favorite is their tomato and basil sicilian pizza..drooool.  The service is quick,  the prices rock and they are open late.  nuff said.

This is such a random spot in Kendall Square. I mean, if you come here on a Saturday night like I did. I'm pretty sure during the weekdays it's a normal food spot, but if you go at the time that I did, it's weird because it's in a desolate spot.

So the tea list is extensive, and since I always know what I want when I come to these kinds of establishments, I got my normal taro bubble tea. Hot. It was delicious because they totally used whole milk. Definitely a good place for bubble tea if you're in the Kendall Square area, but keep in mind it's on the creamy side in terms of milk teas.

After living in Porter Sq for over a year, I finally visited Changsho this past month. Parking is easy with a lot across the street (super rare), and the restaurant itself has a large capacity. 

We got hot and sour soup, some apps (didn't see prototypical chicken fingers on the menu), dumplings, and a main chicken dish. Overall, the flavors and portions were solid, so I would recommend. Also, pricing is fair, so if you are in the area I think it's worth getting a table.

This one time I saw a cool sign outside Upper Crust. It was a reference to some meme drawn with chalk and pizza puns.

I was hungry, but you know, the kind of hungry where I'll have a slice of pizza right now, especially if it's some funky topping thing, like cauliflowers and such. So I go in and say, 'Hey, I'll have a slice of that cauliflower and such, it appears to be the special.' And they go, alright it'll be 5 minutes or so.

So 15 minutes go by and I see some dude order the same pizza, and then get the slice, eat it and leave. And I'm thinking, ok that guy looked chill, but where's my slice?

I go up and ask and they say 'Oh, sorry but we're all out of that pizza and here's a refund.' And then I walked out and pretended nothing ever happened.

2 stars because I got a refund without asking for it.
Nice atmosphere. Tasty food. Friendly service. But can't figure out why they had the doors wide open and no AC when it was 90+ outside. What were they thinking?!

Who knew you could get amazing liege waffles in Cambridge? The waffles here are incredibly soft and chewy with a nice crunch from the pearl sugar embedded into it. The iced coffee wasn't bad either, but really the waffle is the main star here.
Worst buffet ever. $18.00 including gratuity for poor selection and nothing but grease ridden faire of shopping mall food court quality

I spent a weekend in Boston, visiting my sister who goes to college there (go terriers!). On my last day in Boston, before my flight we made a mad dash to Pinocchio's. And it was so worth the trouble of rushing through Cambridge and dealing with the T on a holiday weekend. This might be one of my favorite pizza places ever. But that list changes every time I find a new pizza place.

You don't need me to tell you about Subway.  You already know everything you need to know about Subway.  This particular establishment shares seating with a few other restaurants in The Garage, including Felipe's and Chutney's.  That's pretty much all there is to know.

Oh, and (not that this will affect more than 1% of you) for some reason they run out of chocolate chip cookies partway through the day and there are never any left in the evening.  I mean, sure, nobody eats Subway for dinner anyway, but would it really hurt to bake fewer cookies of other types (of which there are inevitably many left over at the end of the day) and a few more chocolate chips?? /end rant

Joseph M and Katie r hit the nail on the head with this place. I am not sure what their problem is but when myself, my sister and her husband went to order, they basically told us the kitchen was closed 5 minutes after seating us. I haven't been back since but was so shocked i started writing on my own food blog because i had to get my experience out there. Here's my original review (this was a third visit too) from over one year ago:
"Having been a recently pleasured customer, I returned to enjoy another wonderful meal at Seoul Food Restaurant on Massachusetts Avenue in Cambridge MA at the intersection of Forest St and Mass Ave. Seoul Food is a tiny home style cooking Korean restaurant with seating for 20-22 patrons. I arrived at approximately 845pm (Seoul Food closes at 940pm according to owner) to eat dinner by myself; I was told I would have to wait as most of the tables were occupied (except for a six top, i.e. a six seat table, close to the kitchen). After having enjoyed my last meal, I agreed to the wait.

Fast forward fifteen minutes (900) and my roomates call - they inquire if they can join me for dinner. Of course, I ask the proprietor if he can serve three instead of 1 - no problem. A four top finishes their meal and the owner seats me at the table - It is approximately 905pm. I order some Kimchi and a soda (no alcohol served at this establishment). I am served my appetizers at 911pm (I know the time because I have a text message from my roommates that arrived at precisely that moment which informs me that they are parking the car on the corner and will be in Seoul Food in a minute). 

917pm. My roommates arrive. I leave the table to greet them at the door. We return to sit and the owner tells us the kitchen is CLOSED!!!!!! I had not even taken a bite of my Kimchi or sipped my soda (which, as I mention, had essentially arrived simultaneously to my roomates)." oh well.

Same ol subway food with a bland, no nonsense staff here.  Not that they're rude, but no smiles while they are making your sandwich here.  Other than that, decent, clean and fairly fast at this location.

Got coffee and pastry here.

The pastry was blah and the coffee okay. A little overrated but if you don't want chain coffee a nice thing to try.
Side notes- they sell groceries as well but they were very expensive but if you need a pepper in a pinch stop here. 

Will go back to try sandwich as I hear that is what is best and update.

There's one woman there who speaks English, maybe the owner, who is
not doing her business a favor, which is perhaps why there's nobody
there at lunchtime.  After we were served, she came over and told us
we had to mix our bibimbap really well so it would taste good.  One of
us was a bit slow, so she grabbed his flatware and did it for him.
After eating, one of us accidentally wrote down a tip of about 8%.
She saw that, and shouted "Fix this!  Tip is 15%", handing back the
slip  Then while going to the cash register she's cursing and mumbling
"Terrible. Nasty."  I had the nerve to say, "Well, tip is 15% if
you're happy with the service." and she waved her finger threateningly
at me, saying "Don;t you say anything.  Leave now."  Whacko.  Plus,
though I love Korean food, theirs is mediocre at best.

Great pizza! I love trying a new pizza every time I order from them. My absolutely favorites are a chicken pizza with a pesto splash as well as their BBQ Chicken Pizza. The staff are very friendly and helpful. UC is one of my favorite go to spots for pizza!

The chang sho is expensive, americanized chinese food, but daaamn it's good.  I used to work across the street and hit the lunch buffet a little too often but it was getting to the point I was going to have to roll myself back to the office so no longer.

BEST HOT AND SOUR SOUP IN TOWN.  You heard it here.  So good.  My bf surprises me with it all of the time because he is smart, and knows that my love is purchased with hot and sour soup.

I like the beef terriyaki, hunan spicy beef and this spicy beef with greenbean dish whose name I forget.  It's definitely less greasy than the standard fare.  The gau's is yummy but very sweet, it's the diabetic special.

Basically, screw solid food, I am hooked on the hot and sour.

Stumbled upon this place after trying to find a place in the city...but not being able to find parking so we headed to Cambridge to find something! We walked by Wit's End and took a look at the menu, everything on the menu sounded amazing, and had reasonable prices so we decided to try it out! We got seated right away! I went with the chicken and waffles which were amazing, I do tend to love chicken and waffles wherever and these did not disappoint super large portion too, we also split the brisket fries, but the portion of the chicken and waffles would have been totally fine by theme selves! We left and when I got back to my hotel I realized I had left my purse their the workers were super nice about it and left it for me to pick up the next day, and everything was still in it:) the o my problem I would have is the noise was a little loud but I suppose it is a pub, so that's understandable...and the device went above and beyond hence the stars:)

Good bibimbap and the Mom & Pop figures are nice enough but somehow I just didn't really get into the place.

Maybe it was the sky high prices disproportionate to the 4 strips of eel within said bibimbap or the fact that they never refilled the little small dishes (side note: I'm not sure if it's decorum to do so, but other Korean restaurants in the area do make a point to keep us well stocked in kimchi), overall I left with a less than full belly and a weird feeling. I can't really qualify what that feeling was, but I do know that I probably won't be going back any time soon. 

And I agree on the ambiance. I get the "homey" friend feeling, but if they were my friends I'd tell them to change their creepy lightbulbs.

This is among the most disgusting food I've ever had.  I work close by and when I saw the Best of Boston sticker I was very excited.  I tried it a few months ago for takeout, tried the beef and broccoli after it took like 10 minutes to get the hostess to notice me.  

It was gross.  I went back to ask for hot sauce, but they apparently don't stock hot sauce.  I asked for chinese mustard, and they had no idea what I was talking about.  I appealed to another service girl, who said "you mean yellow sauce?"  I felt if I spoke I would say something extremely rude, so I just nodded.  

Unfortunately, the mustard they stock is the disgusting bright yellow fake packaged chinese mustard like they use at a mall food court.  Since I needed the a good hot sauce or mustard to cover up the foul taste of the beef and the slimy gunk of a sauce that everything was in, I just threw it all out.  

I thought maybe I'd just gone on a bad day, and vowed to try it again someday.  

That day was today.  I ordered the spinach and tofu soup and the minced pork w/ spinach noodles.  I ordered it via phone, in hopes that I wouldn't have to wait as long this time.  No such luck, when I went to pick it up, I had to wait 10 minutes for someone to come to the front desk.  There were like 4 people eating.

The online menu has this soup listed at $3.50, but they charged me  $5.95.  I didn't noticed this until after I got back to my desk, and I quickly forgot about the price differential when I touched the soup to my tongue.  It was vile; it looked and tasted like dirty bathwater.  It also had no spinach.  Not a single piece.  

I went back to return the soup.  They took the soup, brought it to the kitchen, put some spinach in it, and gave it back to me.  I asked for my money back, which they gave me, and they told me to keep the soup, which was nice.  But the soup was still gross so I dumped it out, and added the vegetables and tofu to the pork & noodles.

This dish also turned out to be pretty gross.  They layered the pork atop the noodles atop a peanut sauce in a quart-sized plastic soup container, so there was no way to mix them together without making a huge mess.  The noodles were overcooked and slimy.  The pork tasted like little packets of rusty dirt.  The menu didn't say that it came in a peanut sauce (which I hate) and had I been allergic to peanuts, I surely would have died, since there was no indication of said sauce's existence, since it laid secretively on the very bottom of the container.

Maybe I've just had bad luck but this place is gross.

Very reasonably priced drinks and very cute and friendly bartenders! The atmosphere is relaxed and the decorations (wallpaper, tiles, etc) have a very coordinated, tasteful look, so we had the entertainment of the many TV screens without the usual kitschy feel of a sport bar. I prefer cider, and they had a good selection, including the local favorites Bantam and Downeast.

It really is the best Pizza.

This is a basic pizzeria, not somewhere to go on a fancy date, but the pizza really is out-standing. Myself and friends ordered a large regular and a Sicilian pizza. Both were just outstanding, the regular had a great crisp crust, and we got it loaded with all the meats on the menu (it was a heart-attack Sunday) and this thing was so un-greasy and tasty. The Scilian was this big square pizza that was just light, tasty and delicious. I really can't say enough about how good this pizza was. 

The staff weren't particularly friendly, but with pizza this good who gives a flying f**k! :)

Overpriced and nothing that special/creative.

Had coffee and a pastry but was told to go back and try the sandwiches.

Went back and had the Mt. Auburn (turkey ) and a chicken salad. They were okay but definitely not worth the $25 for both. Nothing memorable and rather bland. 

Flour has better sandwich options nearby.
This is one of the best bars in Inman Square!!! Their Moscow Mules are on point, and the wings are  unique as well Will definitely come back when I'm in the area.
Nice place for sandwiches and coffee. A little noisy though. The crowd there is definitely artsy, so it's fun to hang out there...

Great gourmet deli sandwiches with fresh ingredients. Despite the other reviews, I've never had a problem with the staff-  they are unique but friendly. I only took off a star because it's a tad expensive at $9 for a sandwich, though the sandwiches can be quite large.

The pizza was okay-- I must admit I'm not a huge fan of thin crust pizza in general though. We also had a caesar salad which was nothing special. In general, I'd likely not go again but wouldn't say it was terrible. It was just "okay".

Anise is a very awkward restaurant. On one side of the story, there's an amazing decoration, from the tables and chairs to the silverware and glassware and even the walls, floors and atmosphere. On the other, there's the food. That was the problem for me. Way too spicy, not savory or meaty enough and overpriced, I left disappointed but charmed by the fact that there is hope. If only they were to tweak their menu a bit and were to reduce the heat from the spices, they could be a hit! You can't replace flavor with heat.

Leisure station is a nice bubble tea hang out when you can't make your way to Chinatown.  The tea is good, albeit sometimes a bit sweet.  I usually get the almond milk tea and 
ask them to make it a little less sweet and it's perfect.

The bubbles are always cooked right, not too chewy or too soft.  However, to my surprise, they charge you 50 cents to add bubbles.  This caught me off guard since usually bubbles are included in the base price.  

The pricing here is not cheap, but they don't really have any competition nearby and I assume the rent isn't cheap in Kendall square.  

Overall a good hang out with yummy bubble tea.

Yes, yes and yes again.
In love with the simple yet so delicious waffles. Great service and super cool design all together.

I really like this spot. The actual space is really nice and rather large. It gets pretty busy during the evenings / late evenings but because it's spacious it doesn't feel crammed. As in all bars, it can get a bit loud but nothing too crazy and again the large space makes it appear less loud. I love sitting at the tables next to the windows which let a lot of light in at these tables. 

The theme of the bar is pretty much games and their menus is really fun to go through as it is themed and pretty punny. I also love that every table has different games that you can play - really neat and just a fun idea. The service is really good, the staff is super kind and overall very patient with indecisive customers (like me). It's also pretty quick, i didn't wait long at all for the food or the drinks ordered. 

The drinks are good though what I got was a bit too sweet for me. However, I also tried the Tom Cruise and it was pretty good very well balanced not too sweet or strong at all. I got a brisket taco which I honestly didn't like very much and wouldn't get again. The meat and the toppings could use a lot more flavor. However, their lord of the wings were amazing!!! Si tasty and a pretty good portion. On tuesdays they have taco tuesdays where they have more types of taco at a discount and Wednesday's they have more type of wings at a discount as well. I'll definitely be coming back on a Wednesday to try their wings that weren't on the menu. Also, both of their fries looked really good.

Overall, a neat place to have some food and drinks and enjoy a few board games!

This place is a great place for some late night pizza in the Havard Square area. They make great Sicilian pizza. The dough is this and delicious, great sauce and a great choice of toppings. I also like that they sell Sicilian by the slice very cheap.

This is my favorite bubble tea place in the Boston/Cambridge area. The bobas are JUST RIGHT. Don't bother going to Chinatown because I've tried a lot of places there before finding this gem so close to MIT. Also the girl working there is super nice, and will always accommodate me even when I have boba cravings at 11pm when they are about to close!

Tthe price is slightly expensive too ($4.50 for a jasmine milk tea with boba). But it is the best we've got here.

A year later, I gave this location another chance.  This time, they provided water upon request.  

Food is consistent with Subway standards.  Location is convenient.  Now meets expectations.

Darwin's recently raised prices on their squarely mediocre offerings. You'd be better off going to crema for pastries, cardullos for sandwiches, and somewhere else (although good coffee is difficult to find in hsquare) for coffee.

I took out some food from Anise today.  After a long St Patty's day outing I was still feeling a little undernourished, so today I really needed some noodle soup goodness.  Wisteria is a little far away for pick up and I think Mulan is closed on Mondays.  So, this was my first visit to Anise even though I've wanted to go many other times.  In addition to the noodle soup, I ordered the Sichuan beef dumplings in sweet hot sauce.  

Yum.  

For take out, it was excellent.  A tupperware full of soft wheat noodles in spicy savory broth with chunks of beef (the beef wasn't great) was exactly what I needed.  Next time I might ask them to leave the beef out, though.  Unlike some takeout, the soup was combined with the noodles, etc. in one tupperware.  After some time, it would probably sog up, but I was able to enjoy it quickly.  The dumplings were superb.  Simple and delicious with a hint of spice and the sauce: garlicky.  I liked it.  

Anyway, it hit the spot for an $8 lunch.  I'd get it again.  But their lunch menu appeared limited.  And their dinner menu is a little expensive.

I'm babbling.  It's Monday after St Patty's day.  I think I'm allowed to babble.

Side note: $20 minimum for deliveries + $3 charge.

I'm of the opinion that they are a bit stingy with ingredients here, and I've come by for the broccoli cheddar soup a couple of times but find it consistently feeling like it's watered down - the soup is usually thicker and has more broccoli and cheese bits in other locations.

Would suggest avoiding until the franchise becomes a bit better managed.

Yikes!  Changsho was quite bad.  Calamari and spring rolls came out quickly and were what could have expected from any Chinese restaurant.  It took a very long time for spinach, duck and rice to come out.  The duck lacked flavor and had a poor texture from not being cooked properly.  The pancakes for the duck were cold.  I won't go back even though its walking distance from my house.

Such a beautiful hidden place, while known for the delicious sicilian pizza, espicialy the tomato basil, what alot of people overlook is the amazing steak and cheese here.Its not like your run of the mill d'angelos steak and cheese, no sir this is deliciousness at its finest.  Pinocchio's i love you.  Go in before 12:45 on the weekends so you dont have to wait and have a better selection.

I'd like to give this 3.5 stars, actually, but I'll settle for 3. This restaurant in the Kendall hotel is pretty cute. The atmosphere is relaxed and casual (jeans are appropriate), and the service is good. The entrance and lobby spill over to the dining area, which can be a bit cramped, and though low-lit, doesn't quite approach "romantic". The food was hearty and good, though I wouldn't describe it as inventive. I'd go back, but I certainly don't think the prices were consistent with the quality of food, although Kendall does have a dearth of nice restaurants, so there is some value in that, I guess. I started with the fried goat cheese, which came with crackers, toast points, and some fruit. It was fine - nothing original. For the main course, I went with the sea bass, which was flavorful and good. Again, the food IS good - just don't go expecting it to be imaginative. 

Note: The wine list is nothing to write home about.

The five minute walk from Harvard Square is definitely worth it, as the coffee at Darwin's is so much better. I love sipping a latte in the cozy seating area -- if only they had a little more space, I would hang out here more often.

Like a recent reviewer said, I hadn't been here in near 20 years but went when my girlfriend, "M" said she had an odd hankering.  She won't play THAT card anytime soon.

The place used to seem slightly fancy, if you can believe it.  Now it is just a large restaurant with badly stained chairs... some so badly stained you're unsure the stain has "set" and that you might take on the pattern when you get up.

As appetizers, I had a fairly "ok" plate of cold sesame noodles, and my girl had a hot and sour soup she'd rate 5/10.  But then things went south when the entrees arrived.  I had the Chicken with Lotus Seeds, which the menu said was one of their house specialities and a "lighter General Gau's chicken."  M had the "Mongolian Beef" special. 

Both were generally fried fare with no charm.  The "beef" seemed as though it might not be beef at all.  I don't want to consider that any further, as we each had some of both plates.  The chicken was essentially just a poorly offered General Gau's chicken.

What were the plus points?  Two.

Our server, "Joe", was attentive and seemed to want to make up for the bad foundation his employer offered diners.  He observed me trying to pick what might have simply been a tea leaf out of my tea, and he came dashing over to offer me a different cup (I think we both suspected it was a fruit fly).  I was embarrassed by his diligence and waved him off.

The portions were frigging huge.  We'd have been fully fed had we shared either entree.  Alas, the quality was so marginal that we ate about 40% of each and left.

Just not a place I'd ever recommend.  As other have said, there may be worse places, and perhaps there are better times to dine or different menu selections.

Although the teas are good (they are pre-sweetened though, so it's hard to cut down on sugar).

Their delivery is TERRIBLE. They would call us ~every hour to postpone delivery. After three hours, we called to cancel. And they were upset at us!!! Even though the driver hasn't even picked up our order yet for delivery.

Terrible service, much better in-person.

my search continues for good pizza in cambridge......
 Me and the gf saw pinnochio reviews on yelp. After experincing   oggis  the disaster we tried it.
 we arrived inthe pouring rain..... order a slice of cheese first. vs  a large pie( we wanted the option to cut our losses after 1 pie)
 
 result is... a

 You people out here dont know good pizza... really. 
you know chowder, you know boston accents, you know bad driving ... but you  dont know pizza!!!!
Crust was crunchy , like dry crunchy as in no flavor and kind of sort of burnt.
Sauce.. no taste needs more oregano and sugar...
Cheese was ehh.  The entire thing had no "soul"

 it was meh....  I am honestly thinking that bertuccis might be the best pizza out there. .. next stop is armando's and   village kitchen..... let s see

(Second) I'm naive and I returned even after posting the review below. The entire restaurant was pretty much empty, as we expected.

But empty can be good! Maybe you want a quiet place to relax. 

My friend and I went at 4 PM because we wanted a place to talk and unwind in peace. We ordered food with low expectations. We decided to linger and enjoy the meal, and didn't feel bad because we knew we weren't taking up an in-demand table. 

The waiter (middle-age, male) kept coming back to ask us what we will order, even after we asked for more time. By the end of the meal, he actually said "you should finish now, you take long time." Are you kidding me? We weren't even there a full hour after getting our dinner when he demanded this rudely...

Very dissapointing. It would have been great to have a decent Asian place in harvard square, because Hong Kong food is not meeting the standard. But between this servicce and the $15 steamed vegetables that tasted like manure, I'm advising you not to go!

**
(first)  Change my mind yet again. I took out a tofu pad thai this time and it clearly tasted like straightup ketchup (did they run out of ingredients?) and smelled like poo. Furthermore, it had a hair in it. Furthermore, the entire restaurant was empty. Furthermore, it was way too expensive for how disgusting it was.

Also, I ordered a side of plain steamed vegetables and it costed almost $15 after tax. What??? 

 I won't be returning.

Loved this place. Very chill and hip vibe. Not a lot of seating however. I ordered a cortado, which turned out to be one of the better cortados I've had. Very neat espresso shot. Also got the liege waffle, which was amazing. Definitely would come back.
Good atmosphere, very nice waitstaff/bartenders, decent pub style food. Overall was a solid place to get some late dinner and a few adult bevs on a cold night. Would give 3.5 stars so rounded up.
Solid, BIG portion beef noodle soup like the kind I had in China! They are still starting out so staff seemed a bit frazzled but food was solid. Barely finished all that good fat broth

If you're looking to have a quick dinner, this is the place to go. Their wings are great, the burgers are pretty good, and service is fast. It's not fancy, but it's also not expensive.

If it wasn't so loud all the time, it would be awesome.

This is a coolest coffee shop! Fun vibe with interesting funky things on the walls. The cups are stamped with (snake) tattoos and so are the employees. I found myself wishing the line were longer for my coffee so I could look at all their cool sleeve tattoos. Very friendly staff and delicious topped waffles too!

Super solid authentic Chinese food in the heart of Central Square! Service was ok but could be improved and is the main reason I took off one star.

The first time I went here I got take out of Sichuan braised beef noodle soup. It tasted delicious! Meat was tender, noodles had good texture, and there was plenty of bok choy as well. The food was ready within 15 minutes of when I ordered.

I came here again for dinner with my boyfriend. There was no wait, although the restaurant stayed busy during our time there. Most of our food came out pretty fast, but the waiters were sometimes a little confused about who ordered what. One dish took a little longer, and just as we were about to ask about it, a waiter told us it's not done but will be out soon. Overall I didn't mind the inconsistent service too much as we didn't have to wait too long and it was easy to say that we did or didn't order a dish, but service could definitely be improved for customers to have more peace of mind.

The delicious food really makes up for the service! 

Water spinach with garlic was very flavorful; the garlic somehow added a subtle sweetness that really made the vegetable enjoyable. I'm not usually a huge fan of water spinach but I devoured the water spinach here.

Minced pork with black bean sauce over noodles passed my noodle test. Noodles have good texture and sauce is just right -- not too sweet, not too salty, and good ratio of sauce to noodle. Sauce came with plenty of pork and bean curd.

I also really enjoyed the Sichuan diced chicken with chili peppers. It's actually not very spicy so if you like spicy you probably need to ask them to make it more spicy.

One thing to note: rice doesn't come with any dishes and costs $2 a bowl. Most Chinese restaurants my boyfriend and I have been to have complimentary rice so this was unexpected for us. I personally don't mind since I'm not a huge fan of rice but my boyfriend likes rice and thought the price is a bit steep. Other than that, prices and portion sizes for the dishes were pretty reasonable.

I plan to come back to try some other dishes next time I'm craving authentic Chinese food!

The dry fried eggplant is one of the most delicious things I have eaten in a while. Paired with other dishes of mushrooms and bok choy, wontons in chili oil, Dan Dan noodles, and dry pot beef. All were delicious, but the eggplant is truly something that is worth the visit. Fried batons of Chinese eggplant tossed in Chinese peppercorn, cumin, salt, dry red chili's, and cilantro stems. Fantastic. I'm a Dumpling House fan and they now have some worthy competition.

The food isn't terrible, but it's completely forgettable, with lots of small errors. I had the filet MR, but it came out medium, with no sear to speak of, and under seasoned. Asparagus was the "seasonal" vegetable, though I'm pretty sure those aren't in season in Boston. The special house salad was over dressed and presented in an out of fashion way.

Service was very disappointing for a place at this price point. No water refill until I asked, and left me to sit a long time after service ended, even though I was one of just a few tables here. More interested in side work than in taking care of me.

Currently writing this review as I'm eating in this just-opened location.

A few things ... first of all, great food! For real, Mapo Tofu with real numbing peppers. Good portions and yummy selections. Also, I enjoy that the menu has all the things I grew up eating at regular Chinese restaurants. It makes me really happy to find authentic Chinese food so close to Cambridge! Now I dont need to drive all the way to Boston for this stuff. The food came out really fast!

Service is spotty - my waiter only wanted to talk to me in Mandarin ... water didn't come until after we ordered food. 

Overall, pretty pleased to rate this a solid 4! Would have been a 5 if it was a friendlier service team.

Nothing was good. It takes me a lot to slam this restaurant. I hope the owners take this seriously and improve the quality . Thick gooie sauces - flavorless from start to finish. Enter at your own risk. I will go back in a year to see there have been any improvement. Salt and Pepper shrimp is void of salt or pepper - General Gou chicken was sitting in a thick flavorless sauce.
First stop in Boston conveniently located by our Airbnb! Must try the liege waffle + cappuccino

This place is def my favorite coffee shop in the entire Boston area. The baristas are so kind and welcoming, and the coffee is the best I've ever had. I'll never get sick of their waffles either. Luv u Curio

Location (5/5): close to Harvard Square. Some street parking nearby. 

Service (5/5): the waitresses are always super nice and helpful. The dishes are served pretty fast is you never have to wait long for some delicious Sichuan cuisine!

Food (5/5): probably the best Chinese/Sichuan food in the area. Super authentic (I'm from Sichuan, so trust me). I love the spicy boiled fish (水煮鱼) and spicy chicken (辣子鸡). The dishes are perfectly seasoned and sooooo spicy, just the way I like it. They also have Amercanized Chinese food, but I can't speak to the quality of those dishes since I've never gotten them there. 

TL;DR: not for the feint of heart, but the spiciness will blow your mind/tastebuds.

DECOR:
WAIT, IS THAT A... Wasps nest? Old globe? Cow's head? Picture of a snake's skeleton? Baracuda? My friend said the nic-nacs look like "random shit she would find in her dad's office who lives in Kentucky." I could not agree more (in the best possible way). Decor is minimal and hip. Greenery is lovely. Lots of instagram opps so you can show people how ~cool~ you are. 

COFFEE:
I ordered a cappuccino and it was very good- smooth and perfectly foamy.

WAFFLES:
KANYE'S BEST PRODIGY (sorry Chance The Rapper). 11/10. Quite possibly the best waffle I have ever had. I am still dreaming about this warm, dense, ever-so-slightly sugared piece of heaven to this day. Well I came here this morning. But still.

CUSTOMER SERVICE: 
Friendly and attentive. I did not feel judged for taking photos either. Always a plus.

PLAYLIST:
V good.

Menu is minimal, but what they do, they do well.

I will be returning.

The Rising is a perfect local spot in Inman Square. The manager, Ian, and the bartender, Steve, really work hard to deliver great customer service. They remember everyone's name and are always checking in to see if you need anything. They work hard to market themselves, produce fun events and introduce great food and drink specials (like their beer of the week at 3.50 and their food happy hour specials every day). Inman Square (and Cambridge in general) need more local-friendly spots like this, so I really hope they continue to get good business and stick around. Go there to watch a game, for trivia, live music, or even just for dinner (beer and burger for $10 every day between 5-7!). My friends and I live in Inman Square and go here at least twice a week, so I can promise you its a great spot where they really care about their service, their food and their environment.

Came here for a work lunch, it's a pleasant spot inside the Kendall Hotel. I got the curry chicken salad (pictured). The curry had a lot of flavor, and the other ingredients (lettuce, avocado) tasted really fresh. Service was solid, and the space is small but comfortable. It's a good work lunch option if you're in the area!

I have been to this place a few times. Love the IPAs on tap. Bartender and manager have curated an excellent beer collection. I enjoy the food too. It's not just a great place for a beer! The food, including the fish n chips, gnocchi (haven't seen it on the menu in a little while), and salads have impressed me! Honestly, the fish n chips are the best I've ever had in New England! Definitely worth a visit.

Sandwiches are delicious and come highly recommended, but on two occasions now we have had somewhat abrupt service from cashiers. Expect grumpiness behind the counter, and you'll probably be fine. But perhaps this is why Darwin's doesn't pop the four-star mark? A shame, because it's entirely possible to find stellar service elsewhere in Cambridge and Boston.

Pretty delicious! Came here for lunch around 1pm on a weekday. Lots of space to sit. Also a lot of people eating alone and we were probably the only table making conversation - was a bit weird when the music cut out (which was modern US pop, also felt like a strange fit but no complaints!).

We got two apps to share - cold jelly and spicy edamame. Cold jelly was delicious, never had it before. It has a bit of a sour tang which was nice for the flavor balance. The spicy edamame was pretty addictive spicy. Not sure it was worth the cost and might get something other than spicy edamame next time, but loved the cold jelly dish.

For the main we shared a customized stir fry, which you walk up to a counter and order from. For veggies or meat, you must select up to 3 items which then are paid for by weight. We got woodear mushrooms, enoki mushrooms, and bokchoy for veggies, and tripe, lamb, and squid for the meat. Excellent taste on the stir fry and the ingredients were really nicely cooked. We ate everything with two bowls of rice and a large bottle of tap water. 

Now about the spice. Came here because wanted to be blown away by spice. We asked for the spiciest level on the stir fry. Didn't really feel it was spicy, but I could actually taste the spice (which sadly is saying something bc I was recently at a restaurant that served me something w undetectable hotness when advertised otherwise...). Still tasted really good though. Left very full and had leftovers :)

Just one complaint. Service was kinda spotty - weird considering the ratio of staff to customers. We got way too much attention before we were ready to order and then basically no attention when we were trying to get the check. Also if I'm gonna nitpick I wish there was complementary tea

Overall though - wonderful Szechuan place. I recommend it.

The Rising Bar is a hidden gem. It is the perfect place for after work apps and has an extensive drink selection to try while enjoying their frequent live music.

But it's the breakfast that earns it one of my favorite places in Cambridge. At this point I have tried almost every breakfast option and have yet to be disappointed. In fact I find myself saying this is my new favorite everytime. Today I had the avocado mash and I am now set on having this every Sunday. It was everything I  needed and the idea compliment to watching Brady crush Houston. 
My mouth honestly is still watering from it. My table also shared the French toast between us as a little treat. Perfect end to brunch. 

 No Sunday scaries at the Rising I'll tell you that.

I ordered a single large Pizza for delivery from here during a recent Sunday afternoon football game (Pats won!). Delivery was reasonably fast and the thin crust pizza was tasty with no issues.   Delivery person was 100% professional. In all, I would reorder from here.

Unassuming, instantly likable and highly addictive.

There is something very comforting about a place that makes consistently good coffee - whether milky or not, hot or cold - without too many distracting frills and flavors. The coffee is the centerpiece, and the menu is next to the bust of a cow. You don't have to look hard or long to make your choice: barista is right there at the ready to get you on your way, or what you need to settle in for a while.

The food menu is just one item: the liege waffle. Better than another restaurant in Cambridge that specializes in this pastry, they come off the griddle fresh and nicely caramelized on the outside. Beats most pastries by a landslide because it couldn't be any fresher, and it is filling too. I don't want to know if it is good or bad for me, and by how much: I just know I want one daily. And that coming here, with its simplified yet quality choices, provides a consistency that life often doesn't allow. 

Staff is interesting, human, talented, and customer focused without being phony.

Stopped by for lunch and had a very good experience here! My partner has a gluten and dairy sensitivity. We ordered a gluten-free, vegan veggie pizza and it was delicious! The gluten-free crust is maybe the best we've ever had in a restaurant, and the cheese was SO GOOD and actually melted and had a similar consistency to classic mozzarella pizza cheese. The crust was thin and crispy, could not even tell it was gluten-free! We would come here all the time if we lived in town. Friendly staff and clean space. They also serve Stubborn Soda. Yum! 5/5!

Pizza was very good however for the price ($12.99 for a small plain very thin crusted 14 inch pizza),  there are much better pizzas out there with real crusts not paper thin.  For example, LaRonga in Somerville where you can get a huge plain cheese pizza for only $9.00 with a nice thicker chewy crust.

I spent $8 at subway for a meal that took me nearly 2 hours to finish (phewww....exhausting!I) $5 for a footlong????really..... I say $8 well spent this week! Convenient location and healthy food, why not?
Real deal Belgian waffles and great coffee too! I like the staff and the place but it can be very warm in summer :")

Rolling out of bed at 10:30am on a Sunday morning (after crashing at 2:30 earlier that morning), we were looking for a place to grab a bite to eat so we could start our day of adventures. My cousin's sister-in-law recommended this place for good sandwiches. After walking down Mt. Auburn, we found this place in an quick mart-style building. Honestly, I was a little hesitant, but we went inside anyways. This place reminded me of a much tinier Trader Joe's with local fare and fresh deli foods. It smelled really good inside, so I opted to order some breakfast.

I made my own omlette with egg whites, smoked turkey, green peppers, baby spinach, and salsa on a spinach tortilla. The guy in the kitchen was finishing up another girl's sandwich, so I waited around and browsed through the rest of the tiny little shop. They have wines displayed on their wall, as well as some fresh produce, specialty chocolates, baked goods, etc. My friend ordered "The Gerry" sandwich and his was done way before mine (and he ordered after me!). He also got a side of their pasta salad. A good 10 minutes later, the guy in the kitchen handed me my breakfast burrito. It may have been done sooner, but he didn't take the time to call out that my order was done or to look for me as I was around on the other side of the wall looking at the rest of their fare.

When I took a hold of it, it was piping hot! The guy handed it over with a smile and told me to enjoy (it was actually, a sincere "enjoy", too!). Next door, they have seating and a coffee shop, but it was so crowded, we ended up sitting on a bench outside. I unwrapped my burrito and dove right into it. The first bite was amazing! I don't know what they did (I wish I watched him make it), but it was the best breakfast burrito I had ever eaten. So flavorful and moist, I gobbled it up, trying to savor each bite, but always wanting more! Even though I asked for salsa, I didn't really see any in it, or taste any spice, so I'm not sure what happened there. There was also not an abundance of fillings, but I am willing to overlook it because of just how darn good it tasted! My friend also seemed pretty pleased with his sandwich that was oozing with creamy goodness. His pasta salad, on the other hand, wasn't so good. He threw out half of it, and I agree, it lacked flavor and the flavor that was there, wasn't good.

The service, although for me, was slow, but definitely local and friendly. They take great pride in their food and by the students, young families, and that one old guy who sat next to us on a nearby bench, who visited this place during the 30 minutes we were there, this place is quite popular. The food was to die for -- I would definitely go back for another breakfast burrito, or even to try one of their infamous sandwiches. However, I would stay away from their pasta salad. I didn't get to try any of their sweets, but a family did buy them for dessert for their kids, and they seemed to like them! Definitely try this place if you're looking for a good, hot, hearty breakfast or lunch. It might be really cute to grab a picnic lunch from here and take it down by the water or the Public Gardens in Boston. Definitely one of my favorites!

​The pizzas here are tasty but the service is awful. Last night was the third time that I've tried to order a pizza for delivery that resulted in a messed up order. And now, it will be the last time that I order with them. I was in the car and decided to order a pizza using my phone so that it would arrive not long after I arrived home. I went to their website and tried to order online, through the browser. It was a terrible experience (you couldn't read the description of the pizzas until you had already added them to your cart, among other issues). 

I noticed that they had a blog post announcing a new mobile app so I downloaded that to place the order there. I placed the order and the last screen told me that it would be delivered in an hour. Great. After 1 hour and 15 minutes passed, there was no pizza so I called to ​check in on the order. The man that answered wasn't familiar with the app I mentioned and said that no order had been received, that something was roaming. 

Eventually, he offered to send a free pizza but said that it would be another 45 minutes. I wasn't willing to wait 2 hours (in total) for a pizza. I live a 3 minute drive from the Cambridge location. When I declined that offer and asked for a refund for the original order, he said there was no order to refund (yet the charge was already showing up on my account). When I pushed, he said that I could call back the next day to speak with the manager. I did that, the same man answered, and told me the manager wasn't in. He updated me and let me know that the order came in today which unfortunately isn't useful to me. He told me to call back the next day to speak with the manager. 

So now I'm going to have three days of calls trying to get a refund for my pizza. I've already had multiple issues with receiving orders from this location and I find it unacceptable for a pizza shop to repeatedly fail at providing the most essential pizza shop service (order a pizza, get a pizza delivered).I will no longer be giving them my business, sadly.

Changsho, what happened to you after your renovation?? I'd give you two stars after last night's experience, but because of your prices, you get just one.

Changsho was always on the expensive side for a Chinese restaurant, but it was worth paying a bit of a premium for the consistently good food. And isn't Changsho the place that charges customers for hot tea?? I THINK that was Changsho. But hey, in the past, the beef dishes were especially good (great quality meat), and everything else was really well-seasoned and tasty, so overall it was worth it.

So last night, a friend and I went there for the first time after Changsho reopened. We were greeted by what I THINK were far fewer menu choices. Okay, whatever -- fewer menu items should mean even better food, right?

Wrong! We both got hot & sour soup to start. I had gotten hot & sour at Changsho a million times before, and it was always good. Last night it sucked. There was ZERO vinegar -- no "sour" at all. At $3.50 for a small bowl. Okay, fine -- so they missed on the soup. At least the entrees were gonna be outstanding, right?

Wrong! We split two dishes: sizzling chicken with black bean sauce ($15), and sizzling beef au poivre with mushrooms & onions ($16.50). Both were totally underwhelming -- so/so seasoning. The beef was still very good quality meat, but that was really the only redeeming feature.

We didn't talk about the food quality until after the meal, so our individual impressions didn't bias each other. After we ate, my friend remarked that the meal was unusually mediocre, and I had silently been thinking the same thing.

At least the chocolate fortune cookies were still good!

Not sure if the usual chef was away that night, but something was definitely amiss. I'll return one more time to see if this was just a fluke -- hopefully it was -- but if my next visit is anything like yesterday's, then Changsho will become a distant memory for me.

This place is definitely up and coming. I loved the decor, the board games on the table and the general atmosphere. 3 star review is because my friends and I thought the service was kind of weird. We came on a Wednesday and did the wings special which was a great deal (the wings were not the best I've ever had but still pretty good). We also all got drinks given the raving reviews for the cocktails (the tom cruise and the Lindsay Lohan). Personally I thought the Lindsay Lohan was a lot better than the tom cruise which was kind of a strong gin and tonic. My biggest complaint of any would be that our waiter didn't know how to give us space which was a bit annoying. All in all I'd definitely come back and give this place another shot and would definitely recommend if you're looking for a new dining experience near the central/ Inman square area!
Cool atmosphere with the old firehouse but the food is a bit pricey and doesn't stand out.

TL;DR: Mediocre Chinese food, good service. 

Randomly walked into this place without any expectations. Staff was very nice. I waited 2 minutes to get seated on a busy day. Portions were large for price which was nice, but quality and taste was underwhelming. If there is one thing I would recommend it would be the pork buns.

Ok, it 2:35. We walk in on a Saturday afternoon, and ordered Turkey Sandwich. The waitress says, " Sorry, it's brunch. It's over at 3pm. We don't have the Turkey Sandwich at brunch.  You need to wait a half an hour to order the sandwich" So we left. Really? You just couldn't make the f****** sandwich because of 30 minutes. You'd rather loose busines because you have "rules"? The world would have ended if God forbid you made the sandwich.  Not a good way to do business. Needless to say my wife and I won't return.

I wanted delicious authentic Chinese food in Chinatown Boston and I found it here. My experiences taught me that you won't find them in trendy restaurants with a great drink list and bar. That's the trade off. 

I walked in to the restaurant filled with local Chinese which is a strong confirmation that I've found authentic Chinese.

I ordered Hot & Sour Soup , which is my litmus taste for their level of quality cooking. The soup looked superb. Every component was distinguishable and it wasn't blended as what you'd see in a bad hot sour soup. It was delicious and the texture was right on with its viscosity. Couldn't take a photo at the time, phone was out of power.

I also had  spicy  cucumber as appetizer. Once again the food looked great and the cucumbers were fresh. 

I didn't know what to expect from the  Lamb Cumin dish which is listed as a Chef Specialty and . It looked amazing!!! Keep in mind that good Chinese food takes pride in the appearance with its colors and how it's presented. It's important to Chinese cooking. As you can see from the pictures, the meat and vegetables aren't heavily drenched with its sauce. The sauce is to compliment and bring out the ingredients flavors especially the meat. I don't mind some gamey taste in lamb meat but this wasn't gamey at all. The meat nor the vegetables were burnt at all or overcooked. The meat was tender and delicious. The cumin spice was subtle not overwhelming in the food. They definitely have mastered the use of spices in their cooking. 

I would definitely come back here again. I also ordered hot tea and a bottle of beer for a total of less than $37.

This review is for my son, who went to this particular location on a school trip. He asked that I give 1 star and 1 star only. He said the customer service here was terrible compared to other subways he's been to. This is definitely a place to avoid because they were stingy with the portions.
Very fast service.  Friendly staff and my favorite meatball sandwich.  Awesome. Simply five star.

They are really rude.  The sign outside says their hours are until 10 pm. I arrived at 9:25, and as I entered with my girlfriend, a woman who looked like the owner was in back behind the counter.  There were 2 or 3 tables occupied in the restaurant.  She scowled and waved us away. "We're closed!" she snapped.  I was confused, so I rechecked my watch and the window outside and saw I had 35 more minutes.  I came in again and  said  "The sign says you're open until 10. Can't you seat us? There's only two of us and we came a long way from home just to try your place."  Then she retorted "What? When am *I* supposed to go home? Huh?" and she snorted, threw her hand at me, turned her back and stalked away.  If I hadn't gone out of my way to come I would have laughed at the absurdity, but I was really offended that she could be so rude when all I was doing was taking her own sign at it's word.  I was speechless. As we made our way out, I turned back to her and said "You really should change the sign so it reflects when you are willing to serve people." I was trying to remain polite because my girlfriend really wanted to try the place some other time.   At that point she literally snarled at me and shouted "Everyone knows what the sign means!! NOW GO AWAY".  I was shocked.   This was like something out of a comedy sketch.   After that, I'll never go to this place, and I would highly recommend that everyone take her at her words:  "GO AWAY".

Big fan of this place--nice to have authentic Chinese restaurant without having to go into Chinatown.  My favorites are spicy tofu, spicy fish and baby choy.  The crispy rice with seafood was great as well.

The location is a bit out of the way for most things, but once you find it you won't regret it.

Small cute cafe that does not have many frills - they serve good coffee and delicious freshly made belgian waffles. 

Their hot lattes and iced drinks are highly recommended- and their waffles are a MUST get. So good and perfectly sweet, they come out warm and pair amazingly with coffee. 

I do dislike how some people do "laptop club" on some tables with only one coffee ordered and stay for hours when many customers come in and have to take orders to-go because the seating is limited. Still highly recommend coming here. One of the must visit cafes if visiting Boston.

I've been here on two occasions and have tried the chili wontons, fried pork buns, cumin lamb, and the dry pot deal with both meat and vegetables. The dry pot deal is an offer to choose an assortment of traditional hot pot meats (like the thinly cut fatty beef and lamb or fish tofu) for $14 a pound and vegetables for $10 a pound. You can opt for dry cooked in spicy sauce or to have as hot pot.  I had it as dry cooked in spicy (ma la). Everything was tasty and tasted very authentic! I really enjoyed the chili wontons and found the dry pot deal to be very cost efficient (lots of food)!

Prices here do not need to be so high for what you get. I ordered delivery via GrubHub, and the only saving grace was that I signed up for a free month trial of GH+, knocking the price down $10 and giving me free delivery. Before that, 2 pizzas were going to cost me almost $50 . I'm gluten free, so my pizza is more money for less taste (as it is almost everywhere), so I'm doubly reticent to spend that kind of cash on a meal. I tried my boyfriend's non-GF pizza and we both agreed it was good but not $20 good. There are plenty of other places to get pizza and even specialty pizza that won't break the bank as hard. I don't have a problem paying for quality but this $20+ pizza was $10-15 quality. From my experience the pizza is also salty enough to make you crave more, but not more of the same bland pizza (in my GF pizza case).

I think of Wit's End as a chill pub/cafe with a side of gaming.  I don't think their inventory has a lot of the "epic" strategy games that might take a while to play, though I'm sure if you bought food and drink they wouldn't mind you playing one. But they've got a nice assortment of popular and social games that go well with conversation, and that food and drink. The cocktails are interesting in name and make.  

We came in for dessert and after-dinner drinks, having dined earlier. The desserts are very pricey, but the meals, from what I saw at other tables, were better deals and looked very appealing. Service was great.  

I would feel equally good bringing a party crowd here or a gaming crowd.
The food is good, and the price is fair. The service is ok. There is a variety of decent Szechuan dish.

Wonderful!

Service was quick and polite. Food was of generous portions, but not too much. Exotic cocktails and adult beverages. Considering the lines at places along the street, the Black Sheep is an unknown steal.

I've been a customer for years.  But no more.  Ordered a calzone today and this is what I got.  $19 for this mess.  Not an Upper Crust calzone!  
No garlic on top.  Triangular shape.  Tasted totally different that what I should have gotten.   I deserve a full refund for this.  I will not be back unless refunded for this.

I had a great meal here once, so I was excited to see that they were on DoorDash.  I ordered the same things I had eaten in the restaurant, but unfortunately, it didn't work out quite as well... actually, it was a complete ripoff.  Allow me to describe my $77 meal.

The first thing I noticed was that the fries were completely soaked through.  I get it, fries are difficult to get right with delivery, but these may as well have been boiled potatoes.  They were so wet that I ended up trying to eat them with a fork before giving up entirely.  Disappointing, but an unfortunate reality of the food delivery experience.  

The real problem, however, was that the food itself was prepared poorly. The buffalo cauliflower was apparently thrown in the deep fryer without having been battered.  Check out my picture and compare it to the other pictures on here and you'll see what I mean.  No crunch, just cauliflower.  It also had barely any buffalo sauce on it, so I had to douse it in Frank's to give it some flavor.  I could've thrown some frozen cauliflower in a frying pan and gotten the same effect.  They have this app at *every* restaurant now, so there's really no excuse to not get it right.

The most disappointing part of the meal was the veggie sliders.  When I had them in the restaurant, I loved the creamy coleslaw, caramelized onion, asiago cheese, and chipotle mayo on these burgers.  The flavor combination was perfect, and the ratios were just right.  This time, however, I literally just got a big mash of beans on a tiny bun.  The picture I posted is completely untouched... that's what they actually looked like.  No discernible coleslaw at all, about four tiny pieces of onion, and what appears to be a tiny pinch of cheese.  Certainly there was no chipotle mayo to be found.  Needless to say, these were dry and flavorless.

I finished the meal with a $12 slice of chocolate cake.  Now, I'm no snob.  I've paid $25 for perfect cake in Michelin-starred restaurants, but I've also enjoyed dozens and dozens of $3 "house of pizza" chocolate cake slices in my day.  It doesn't take a lot to make a cake that's pleasant to eat.  This $12 slice, however, was just that: a hassle to get down.  I don't think I've had a dryer cake. It was a chore to eat.  That's a problem.

The only thing that was passable was the mac and cheese, but only after I added a bunch of salt and pepper.  At first it just tasted like someone had poured cream over some pasta with maybe a pinch of cheese, but it was fine with some seasoning.  That's the best I can say about this meal, which is a shame because I remember the food being so much better last time.
The food was good but WE FOUND A BUG IN OUR FOOD. Yuckkkk! We told the management and they didn't even apologize about it. Gross and bad customer service.
Very simple coffee shop with really good coffee! I got an iced mocha latte and it was very tasty and made very well! Not too much mocha and perfect coffee strength! Definitely check it out!
Good Classic comfort food, cooked by some of the best chefs in town. The salmon with rice, and veggies is yummy, and the wine list is perfect. Homemade deserts and fresh options for all tastes.

This is the most delicious sichuan style chinese restaurant i've ever been to. The prices might be pretty high for what you expect at a chinese restaurant but it's really really yummy. It's a perfect choice if people want to just do a fun dinner party with a small group of friends. They have really good mala soup and all the noodles / pork / beef related dishes are all mouthwatering. Every time I have a get together with groups of friends I always suggest five spices.

Wit's End is a solid option for cocktails, beers, and elevated bar food that doesn't take a backseat to the drinks. There's lots of space, both at the bar and in different parts of the restaurant, so it's a good venue for one-on-one time and group outings alike. There are lots of great options in the Inman Square neighborhood, and Wit's End is part of that list!

What a hidden gem! The size, flavor, texture, toppings, everything was absolutely delicious. Note that the Sicilian style is pretty thick and it is cut into big squares so I was full after eating 2-3 slices. Will be back to try more!

i always get the pork dumplings, i love them! They are sooooo good. I also get an Apple juice, I recommend it for kids. My parents got them black mushroom and bok Choy dish, and they thought is was good. They got the Szechuan fish, and they really liked it. But it is very spicy. It is good though! I recommend this place!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

This is a great place to hang out with a group of friends to play card games and board games. There are lots of nerdy interesting quizzes on the walls that you can solve.
I highly recommend drinking a mule here. Skip the boring Russian mule, and get something different. There are 5 different mules. I had Dr Zhivago which was spicy, it is made with sweet chilli vodka. It tasted like a normal mule but your throat burned a little after. You can make any mule a big ass too!
The food is just ok and overpriced. The names of the dishes are extremely creative though. We had the the Lord of the Wings, which was ok. The nachos with texas brisket, and it was not good. The brisket was very dry. Don't order the nachos. The mac n cheese good though. Sausage of the lambs was just ok. 
I recommend coming here for games and drinks, not so much the food. Hint hint: Christina's homemade ice cream is right across the street, you should go there for dessert after!
The staff ignored a mentioned food allergy and got my girlfriend sick.  Pizza wasnt great either.  Avoid.

I went there last weekend to grab a sandwich. It's been a while since I visited a Subway (at least a year). The person who prepared the Spicy Italian for me did a great job in preparing the sandwich (so 5 stars for him). Why then 2 stars only? It seems that the size of the sandwich vs. last year was much smaller - perhaps 20% shrunk. The price seemed to climb as well with over 7 dollars.

So jealous of my friend who lives within a 4-block radius to this wonderful coffee shop. It's a pretty small hipster-ish  place with two dudes in tats and another girl taking orders and making stuff rather efficiently. My friend said this place is super packed on weekends but luckily we got a table that day(there were hardly 3,4 tables).

 There's not much breakfast items on the menu except authentic Belgium waffles 2 fancy types and one regular classic waffle. Got the margarine waffles with ham and fig spread which was oh-so-delish. My friend also got this and the classic, which was also equally great! Our lattes were perfect too!!

Come in early or take your waffles and coffee to go but do not NOT visit this place if you are in the neighborhood!!
Great vibe and food. Employees are consistently amazing, and just  look at the tea selection!

In the event that y'all think that I may have given way to dementia, nope, haven't yet lost myself to the air. BUT, isn't part of ordering to a $25 worth (8) chicken wings and a small (14") cheese pizza, delivering from a quarter of a mile to my apartment, SHOULD BE AT SLIGHTLY A BIT ABOVE, the air temperature outside, and, maybe the pizza dough should have a hint of beige? Ya know, try to make me think it's cooked?!
I ask you, shouldn't my order come to me hot and cooked? Or, am I just overreacting? Silly me!?

I am utterly shocked by the low reviews here. In fact, I've steered away from Cilantro because of low Yelp reviews, up until now during coronavirus quarantine, when I'm desperate for Szechuan food after failing to cook up dishes on my own. I see people have called out Cilantro for being unoriginal (with other Chinese options around the area) if not because they don't make good Americanized dishes or dim sum. I would totally recommend ordering the Szechuan stuff, because this must be their bread and butter from my recent order.

I ordered some signature Szechuan dishes - dan dan noodles and beef noodle soup with chili on top of some scallion pancake and panfried noodles. Oh my goodness.. everything is just delicious. I am shocked my peppercorn-obsessed self didn't try this place earlier, considering how many disappointing dan dan noodles and beef noodle soups I've had to date. 

The beef noodle soup with chili is rich in beef flavor, surprisingly very spicy with nice bits of pepper that you might get one of your spoonfuls, and salty. I've had to add hot sauce and/or soy sauce in most bowls I've had (from Zoe's, Mala, Taiwan Cafe, Dolphin Bay, etc.), but this one is just perfect. No condiments needed, which is great during this quarantine as I am running low on my precious Koon Chun double soy sauce, black vinegar, and other condiments I can't get a hold of right now. The beef is tender, not too fatty, but not too lean - you get the right chew. I asked for wonton noodles in the comments section of my GrubHub order, and they actually read it and delivered that too.

Onto the dan dan, wow. The noodles don't stick together, and the sauce like the beef noodle soup, is just enough salty and spicy. There's a bit of sui mi ya cai with the minced pork - this stuff is legit. I can mop this down in one go.

My Cantonese self was also craving pan-fried noodles and I found these to be traditional and tasty. I was worried they'd be those really crispy type of noodles - but they provided the real Hong Kong pan-fried egg noodles that I know and love.

Scallion pancake was fresh and tasty, though I've only had a bite since I ordered all of this for myself and I'm stuffed having tried a bit of each of the three dishes.

I am definitely coming back (and ordering more during quarantine).

Food poison! We order dishes online and apparently they were so bad and so awful. The vegetables have gone bad and they ignored that and still cooked them. After I taste them, I immediately spitted them out. The meat is not fresh either and very greasy. Everything tastes like they have been in the freezer for a year then they hit it up in microwave. Gross!!!! Save your own life and don't go to this place.

Overall a solid Chinese joint, came for the Mala Xiang Guo, but found it a tad bit too oily here. Also got a liver dish that was very well done, along with some steamed pork and cabbage dumplings which were honestly my favorite. The skins were made in house or at the very least not from a grocery store. Also grabbed a lo mein to indulge my craving for "shitty" Chinese food, but since this place is more authentic it came out more like chow mein (thinner noodles not much flavor).

A note on the service here, if you are native Chinese you're pretty much right at home. Very curt, not extremely amicable need-to-get-shit-done style. But in American restaurant standards, the service would have been described as "rude"

I also found that the portion sizes here are smaller than usual Chinese restaurant portions, especially for the price point. Nonetheless a solid Chinese place and worth a visit :)

While traveling in Seattle I stopped at several coffee shops and had good coffee, unlike the Starbucks brand which took the fame.  After a few sips this reminded me of that trip, but the coffee was slightly under par.

The pumpkin waffle was a bit sweet (American style), but good.

This place is a good restaurant. Good service for the usual 5-6 dollar price. They do it quickly, but they make sure to get everything you want on the sub. The person behind the counter is very friendly. Altogether, nice place for a hungry college student to grab a meal every now and then.

This review is only for the dry pot/mala xiangguo.

I keep coming back to 5 Spices because it's the closest restaurant that can satisfy my mala dry pot cravings. While the dry pot hits the spot, unfortunately it can get pretty expensive because it's priced by weight. It usually comes to around $15-20 for a to-go dry pot for me.

Cambridge, count me a fan! I was in town on business and, from my colleagues' descriptions, expected more of a sports bar than craft cocktail scene.  But after a grueling few days of meetings, I wanted nothing more than a chill place to unwind over a bourbon, have some friendly non-work-related conversation, and may be grab a bite to eat. 

Enter Wit's End.  Far from crusty sports bar, Wit's End hits the perfect sweet spot between ball cap and buttoned-up in a cute little area called Inman Square.  I felt right at home in the industrial-chic meets library interior - think exposed ceiling, wrought iron, leather chairs, warm wood floors, and broad tables with chess boards built right into the top.  Grabbing a seat at the bar, my inner dork smiled seeing the SCRABBLE letters inlaid in the bartop and the quirky movie reference mural behind the bar.  But what really caught my eye were the rows upon rows of spirits - everything from Cynar to Campari to craft bourbon.  Someone here clearly knows their cocktails.  I decided to play it safe to start and settled in with a Manhattan for some people-watching.  The crowd leaned professional, my age-ish (21 more than a few times over...haha), and the whole place had cozy Cheers kind of "we're all friends here" vibe.  

Service was quick and friendly.  The bartender, Maraina(?), seemed to have a good crowd of regulars, and after sampling her cocktails, I understand why.  The bar filled up fairly quickly as well.  After draining my cocktail coupe (in my defense, it WAS Friday), I decided to try one of the tempting-looking concoctions that kept going by in frosty copper mugs.  My Dr. Zhivago Moscow mule was spicy, refreshing, and appropriately limey.  And I noticed they use the good stuff - Fever Tree ginger beer. Yum.

Time to think about food.  Originally I'd expected pub grub and planned to head elsewhere for dinner. But the menu at Wit's End was surprisingly sophisticated and substantial.  The tuna tartare stack and mussels sounded great, but the gouda mac 'n cheese was calling out to my inner fatty.  I impulsively decided to blow the rest of my per diem on ordering way more food than needed in the hopes of not forgetting all my leftovers in the hotel mini fridge.  (Does that happen to anyone else?! Every. Trip.)

The food was awesome.  The tuna was definitely fresh, beautifully presented, and a good portion.
