I asked for a spoon to slurp up the rest of the buttery "Americal Mussel" broth, which was perfect for fry dunking.  Great fries, by the way.  And the gouda mac 'n cheese? Martha Stewart, high priestess of butter and cheese, would definitely approve. Just the right mix of creamy and crisp in all its savory, nutty-cheesed spectacularity.  By this point I'd switched to beer and was glad to find lots of great local options on draft.

Unfortunately this was the last day of my trip, or I'd definitely have returned to sample more of the menu.  The bartender was sweet enough to give me a short list of other places nearby worth checking out, but honestly, next time I'm here, I'll probably just head straight for Wit's End.

p.s. don't miss the epic wall of TV Guides. Too funny.
p.p.s. Taco Tuesday! I hear the brisket tacos rock.

Got takeout from here on a Friday night and the food was very yummy. Ordered on the phone and food was ready for pickup within 15 min! Really easy and fast. Their dry pot and cumin lamb were awesome. Wish it was a bit bigger portions for the dry pot price.

I love their orange chicken! It is just the right amount of spicy and sweet. They have a very solid menu. Everything I have had here has been good. The service is friendly and efficient. The prices are very reasonable and the portions are quite large. This has become my neighborhood "go to" for Chinese takeout.

My friends and I went here because we heard it's one of the best bubble teas in Boston. We had milk tea, taro milk tea, and green tea, all with pearls. We all thought it was good. The bubbles were just the right amount of chewy. The only issue I really had with it was that since we came on a Sunday the area was deserted. We almost passed it.

Tucked away a quarter of a mile out from Harvard Square, this is a cute cafe and sandwich spot. There's a wide selection of sandwiches, most of which you can choose the type of bread you'd prefer. The size of the sandwiches are right in the middle of the fairway -- not too big or small -- and will run you ~$8-$10 a pop.  We came right around the critical junction between breakfast and lunch, so fittingly, we split our order between the two. 

Oxford - breakfast burrito with scrambled eggs, chourico (some form of chorizo), onion, cheddar cheese & Tabasco wrapped in a red tortilla (maybe it had some kind of flavor, but it wasn't noticeable). This was a hefty burrito that you can hold one handed! Soft scrambled eggs with just the right amount of hot sauce kick. 

Prescott on sour dough - hot tender roast beef, cheddar, lightly sweetened bbq sauce, lettuce and tomato. The meat was flavorful and not overly slathered in bbq or juices, so the sandwich stayed in tact for the most part.

Next time I'd pass on the iced coffee -- decent but nothing special, and dare I say even a bit watery with limited coffee-robustness.

As a Chinese American, I'm always in search of places where I can get my Chinatown favorites.  I've only ordered a couple of Cantonese dishes off the menu, yang chow fried rice and beef chow foon.  Both were decent, but the thing about this place is that the food is very heavily seasoned with either salt or MSG.  I can tell because after I eat, I feel a little sick afterwards.  It's not too bad, so I will go back if I'm ever in a pinch and need something quick.  I've experienced much worse post meal symptoms eating at other establishments.  The fish cooked in chili broth looks delicious.  I would love to try that here in the future.

What's in your wallet? A free pass to the chillest 24 hour VIP lounge in Cambridge? Access to a machine that will spit out whatever bills you want? Half off prices at a better than Starbucks cafe? Probably not. That is, unless you bank with Capital One.

Let me explain. 

The Space:
The Capital One 360 cafe consists of a modern space with spread out tables, couches, and sofa chairs. The combination of spread out tables and partial glass partitioning in several seating areas allows for a much more enjoyable experience than you would have at the cramped Starbucks or Harvard Coop down the street (that is, if you can snag a table. I've been successful in securing one 2 of the 3 times I've come here). The whole cafe is open to the public during the day, but requires a Capital One card to come at night.  

The Coffee:
The cafe in the cafe is Peet's coffee, which in my opinion is better than Starbucks. If you have a Capital One debit or credit card, all drinks are 50% off!

The Banking/Customer Service:
The place has two amazing Capital One ATMs. I've never seen this before, but on withdrawals, you can actually choose how much of each kind of dollar bill you want. Pretty nifty considering that every other ATM I've used only gives 20s.

The customer service is also excellent. In addition to the Peet's employees, there is always a security guard and a couple of Capital One agents who can help you with any banking questions. I idiotically forgot the PIN number on my debit card, and the agent who helped me walked me through how to reset it. There was no hint of "how the heck did you forget your PIN number" judgment from him and he gave me a free drink card after getting my PIN number reset!    
   
I've always been a fan of Capital One ever since I started using them, but this cafe has made me an active promoter. The whole concept of the cafe is still somewhat of conundrum to me as I can't imagine how they are making money off of this unless it is just to build up their brand. If that is their intention, I guess it's working pretty well!

Good fresh squeezed OJ and very nice grilled cheese (with mustard) but a bit pricy for what it is...

I really like the OJ and the grilled cheese was nice- however the price was a bit high- almost $10... which seems like a lot given that I wasn't totally full when I finished.  The upstairs areas are nice and the tables leave lots of room to sit with friends and chat for a bit.  The staff was friendly and polite so all of that was fine... I just think that the $/ value (especially for a student) isn't really there!

Thank you, Taco Bell, for contributing to the overall downfall of my health.  For real, why are you so damn tasty?

Okay, the same general wonderfulness that you'll find South of the Border.... and a would-be 4 star... but  their credit card machine has been down for like 2 weeks now... come on Bell!  Get that shiznit fixed... you're in a mall food court for jesus sake!

And, now, I'd like to end with inappropriate Taco Bell references.

Don't forget, when you go South of the Border... eat tacos and think outside the buns.

fin.

I normally frequent the location downtown, but I occasionally swing by this one and appreciate the longer hours (open til 9! that's pretty unusual for places of caffeination). I can get a lot done here: I can do my banking, or get a coffee while I wait for the ATM to be free, or ask the approachable and helpful Capital One gurus a question or two (like international transaction fee questions before going on an international trip [yay! no transaction fees on debit or credit!], iPhone pay app, etc), enjoy AC, charge my gadgets, and use the free wi-fi.  There's way more seating here than the downtown location and it's super clean.  Ambience isn't nearly as quaint as say Cafe Nerro or Jaho in Chinatown, but at least there's space to sit.

It's extra nice that drinks are 50% off when using a Capital One card, and Peet's Coffee is pretty good stuff. Not the best I've ever had (some other local coffee shops make a better roast and brew and have way better ambience), but it's more than decent and very good value. The service here is also top notch. They always remember who I am and greet me and are quick to get me my drink when it's something easy like a cold brew. Say hi to Kelly, the glittery fairy barista! She and the other baristas are pretty awesome.
It is a good deli, the caser salad is great. But if you are not fancy a big mac, DO NOT try their panini.

I wish I could give no stars.  I love LOVE Mexican food (not that it's to be had in Boston), but this tastes absolutely like the McDonald's of burritos... which is not surprising, I suppose.  Just don't bother.  I felt literally ill after lunch.  Every once in a while I'm tempted by the convenience and cheap price, but it's really nauseating.

I like at least one of the other reviewers don't understand how anybody could rate this restaurant in any way other than highly. It has a funky decor that, if you're expecting Dali, no - it's not romantic -- but it is refreshing and modern.
The waitstaff was courteous, and prompt, without being invasive; a big plus in my book.
Onto the food . . .
My lunch mate and I ordered 5 tapas. The servings were generous enough to have fed three (for lunch).  I'm having a hard time deciding which was my favorite, the goat cheese stuffed eggplant and its delicious sauce served on the side, or the grilled shrimp, which was served with a slightly tangy, slightly sweet side sauce.  Or maybe it was the spinach and pine-nuts, or the spanish sausage . . . oh well - you get the picture. 4 stars up! And the sangria is to die for, although I don't know if you should have it if you  have to head back to the office. Fortunately for me, I did not!

River Gods was such a great addition to this neighborhood.  Solid bar food, good drinks.  They really support local DJ culture with a variety in their lineup.  Plus, IIRC, this may be the only bar in the Boston area independently owned by a woman.  You go!

Seriously, why come here when you can go to Anna's across the street and Boca Grande nearby?

I had a card for a free burrito and probably got what I "paid for". The steak burrito was awful. Not enough steak, though it was fairly tasteless dried steak anyway. Too much bland white rice with peas, along with too much beans...was very unappealing. There weren't enough vegetables either. 

The salsa (medium) was mild, and the mild tasted like water. The tortilla chips were okay, since they tasted fried...how can anyone screw anything up fried? Well, the chips were too salty and they overpowered the salsa. 

Much overpriced, don't settle for less with this joint. Pass, pass, pass!

This place is delicious! Ive been here for dinner before but this is the first time I tried brunch. Both meals have amazing options. I got a monte Cristo which is sandwiched in between two pieces of French toast. My friend got a Il meglio which also did not disappoint. You can get your side of potatoes replaced with something else on the menu for half the price if that side. We switched out one order of potatoes for a side of biscuits and gravy. The bread was so soft and warm. Next time I'm there, I would consider getting the biscuits as a full meal.

So I'm still relatively new to the Davis/Porter sq. area but I think we have finally found our pizza place.  For the last 6 months or so we have been getting pizza delivery at random from wherever would deliver but now that I've found Zing! I'm in pizza heaven! 

They're pizza's are huge, ingredients are super fresh (and made using all natural / local / organic ingredients!), and slices are delicious.  On top of that they have wonderful and innovative creations.  Two of my favorites that I've had recently: 1) You say potato: Sundried Tomato base, Rosemary, A bit of olive oil, Ricotta cheese, Rosemary potatoes (delicious!) and 2) The Blue October: Butternut squash sauce, Mozzarella cheese, Spinach, Caramelized onions, Bleu cheese (quite fantastic as well).

I have loved all the pizzas I've tried from here and will be coming back for more!  I have not tried the Roti yet but I need to do that some time soon.

I've eaten here many times over the past 2 years. The food is OK (not bad, not great).

The service can be OK, or it can be horrible. On my latest (and final) visit, we waited 25 min for our food before my husband went up to ask where it was, and they said "we might have lost your order". No apologies. The post it note system they have for food making is not a good one.

Sam Adams Winter Ale for $3.75.. burritos for $5-6 bucks.. sure why not. 

I don't go to Qdoba often considering it's in Porter Square and there are a number of other options in that area. Although, I do like their ancho chile BBQ chicken burrito. The burritos are brick sized and they stand straight up when you set them vertical. The general contents are pretty good, although since it's so thick you won't get any perfect or near perfect bites (you know.. those bites that have a little bit of everything in them). On occasion, the wraps can be a bit doughy which gets worse when you leave them in the aluminum for awhile. However, I do appreciate the aluminum wrapping because it makes for cleaner eating for someone like me who usually needs her Yelp bib. I don't really like anything else. The nachos are way salty and I only get taco salads from Herrera's (it's like an unspoken pact I have with them).  

They also have Corona, Coronitas, Bud Light, Mike's Hard Lemonade, and Mike-arita's (yeah I don't know) which may be a plus for some. I'm generally an Izze or Nantucket Half and Half person with my burrito.. but that's because it's not really Mexican. I'd call it more.. Mexican inspired American wraps.
Pronto is one of must visit Cambridge restaurants. The food is delicious and yummy as homemade.

Not bad for a national chain restaurant, but better options are close by.  Qdoba features regular burritos with beef, chicken, and veggie fillings, as well as a few specialty burritos such as, mole chicken and a poblano pesto (!?).  Like most chains, the meat is a little too salty and hides the flavor of the specialty sauces.  With Anna's Taqueria literally accros the parking lot and Picante a 5-minute walk to Davis, I find it hard to justify going here over the other two similar and superior restaurants.
Perfect spot to sip a creamy latte, sit on the patio, and watch the local scene. Lovely.

I love going here to grab lunch! Quick take out service (I typically call and order ahead - however there are 15-ish tables to sit down and eat). I love the middle eastern veggie wrap, but everything on the menu looks delicious. The staff is SO friendly. One side of the place is a small restaurant and the other a small convenience store. I would definitely recommend going here for lunch and I am looking forward to trying breakfast here some day!

"How do you like them apples?"

A scene from Good Will Hunting was filmed here...

The market may be small, and the atmosphere may be less than perfect, but the food is extremely good for the price.  Their selection of pastas is unqiue and they have a large variety of other foods to choose from.  I also strongly suggest their baked goods.

Taco Bell is really nice place to get good and fast meal. At least for me and my friends. What i notice that this is very clean and organized place. The staff is extra friendly and they act like you are their best customer or part of their family. seems like they really happy if you like their food and if u satisfied. And it's make you feel special. 
The food come out really fast.
All stuff wear gloves and they change them when start work on other order. I know that how it suppose to be but be honest, not a lot of places does it. And it's very important for my friends because they eat only kosher food. so when we order taco without meat just with vegetables they always ask if we would love some extra beans and i just love them.
So this is really good place to go. I didn't give them one more star because i notice that sometimes their cheese are not as fresh as it suppose to be. I told in beginning that my reviews are honest.

Totally disappointed! Party of 3, we tried all the stuff we heard on Yelp was good... but it wasn't good. The food did not have sharp distinct flavors....and it was luke warm, not cold not hot. I wonder if it was just sitting out there on the counter for a while? 

We tried the petit mignon -- fatty. Rabbit -- tough! Veggie crepe -- rudimentary, blah. Scallops -- fair/black rice was okay. Lamb - only liked the fig compote, but who can mess up fig compote? the lamb itself was a small portion and fatty. 

I can't remember what else we ordered....the sangria was too sugary and cinnamony.... I guess it wasn't memorable, huh?

For dessert, we tried the pear... the pomegranate ice cream was delicious, but the crust of the tart was yucky. 

Such a cute place!! I wish it was better!!! I really love tapas, and we need a great tapas place!!! Come on, Boston!!!

Visited Kika for the first time last night. For a Thursday night the place was empty. Thought it was because it is January and everyone is dieting, but to our surprise, the other three restaurants located in the same  building (EVOO, Za, and Commonwealth were hopping. 
They gave us a puny bread basket. We had to have it refilled 3X. It was like pulling teeth to get them to bring us more bread.
 The red Sangria was topped with one tiny cube of an orange. To me, any Sangria worth its salt is loaded with fruit. I had to ask for a zippy drink straw. Waitress plunked the glass down with out a cocktail napkin.
The waitress was robotic. Not rude, just like a machine. No personality at all.
The lamb chop app was so raw we were afraid were were going to sick. We pointed this out to the manager who agreed the lamb was indeed raw. 
The braised shortrubs were outstanding. The garlic shrimp was good. But they should step up by using larger shrimp. I'd pay more. Those mini shrimps are substandard. Does anyone actually enjoy those mini shrimps?
The paella bowl for 2 with fresh seafood was just okay.  On the bland side. Wasn't seasoned nearly enough or properly, in my opinion. 
Our dining experience wasn't terrible, but adding all the little things together equals a not so great dining experience. We will go elsewhere for tapas in the future.

5/5 stars for service. I had received an email this morning from ABP with a free hot coffee and any bakery item as an anniversary gift for signing up to their mailing list. When I went to this store, however, the email with the code wouldn't open (it said that it had been opened too many times, even though I hadn't opened it at all). The cashier was nice enough to let me pick out a bakery item and substitute the hot coffee for a soda even though the code would no longer open, which was so nice of him! I ended up getting a chocolate croissant (normally$2.69), which was sweet and flaky and chocolate-y, and a glass of lemonade. Thank you for the great service!

Yikes! Went for the first (and last) time on Saturday night. 

We started with one of their specials, a $14 trio combination. It was awful. The food was tiny, tasteless, and poorly put together.

The paella was little better: the snails and mussels were mushy.

Scallops with black sticky rice? The seafood element was tasteless, and the rice was like gravel.

Service was slow too.

The best thing about this place is the outdoor seating. A lovely way to spend a good weather day. The second best thing about the place are the baked goods. By Square standards, possibly the best cookies around. So then why only three stars? 

Attitude. I feel like I am dealing with New York models on low-carb diets forced to work in a bakery.  Gee, I am so sorry to bother you. I really have to want one of their oatmeal pecan coconut cookies to drop by. 

Nose-bleed prices. I can deal with this if I can sit outside, but otherwise there are many better eateries. 

Sandwich to Sandwich. Darwin's wins. Ice tea, bring it from elsewhere (Tealuxe rules for me).
Best bagel sandwich ever! Be prepared for a line but don't worry it moves quickly. Just go here.

I've been to Mexico.  I've eaten authentic Mexican food.  I'm not quite sure if Taco Bell ever stepped foot in the country before though.

Never-the-less, bravo.  Just do yourself a favor Taco Bell, perhaps visit the country one time, because as hard as I looked while over the border, I did not find one instance of Dorito taco shells.

Doesn't strike me as a place I would go out of my way for. I mean they have a decent specialty coffee selection, but who doesn't nowadays. I'm not particularly impressed with their drip coffee, which is really what I look for in a coffee house. If you're looking for a place to chill, but don't expect ti be blown away by their coffee or pastry selection for that matter.

Really sub-par. The baristas are incredibly slow and clueless. On multiple visits they couldn't make basic coffee drinks like lattes to save their lives. They took roughly 7-9 minutes to get one together. And once coffee is made it tastes worse than equivalent drinks at Duncan Donuts (I mean big drip grind sized coffee grounds in espresso-based drinks? wtf?!). On one visit they also didn't know what food was available until after it was paid for - and then tried to make an impossible substitution rather than give a $4 refund. Pastries are very bad, no clue who their vendor is, but they really need another one. The place looks ok, but don't see how they can afford so much space slinging bad coffee and some iffy snacks. Recommend that all staff be sent to barista re-edcuation camp. Or at least get sent over to Casablanca in Harvard Square to learn what cappuccinos and such are supposed to taste like, and the average Starbucks to learn how to crank them out efficiently. I mean, come on, they run a coffee shop and they don't know how to make coffee?! And let's not even discuss their sad attempt at making hot chocolate. It's like I had asked for a Brandy Alexander with real cream, Napoleon XIII brandy and hand-ground nutmeg - given the ham-fisted ingredient juggling on display through their vain 10 minute (failed) attempt to construct one of their basic menu items. Not going back again until I hear they're getting their act together from people I trust. Or until they crash and burn and we get our neighborhood record shop back ... grumble, grumble.

My roommates and I were really excited to see that a pizza place was opening up across the street.  It has not disappointed.

Zing Pizza, which is run by the same people who own Porter Square Books & Cafe Zing down the street, has a quirky style.  They have no set menu, instead making 4-5 different types of pizza a day.  The pizzas are wildly creative and uniformly delicious, made with locally grown/organic ingredients, and, just in case that isn't quirky enough for you, the pies are rectangular.  Oh yeah.  

My favorite pizza is their Smokin' Pumpkin, which is smoked mozzarella with maple-glazed pumpkin pieces.  Until I tasted it for the first time, I didn't realize what pizza could *be*.  Other favorites include beet & goat cheese pizza, and pomegranate & molasses pizza.  I can't wait for the spring and summer to see what other seasonally inspired masterpieces they dream up.

Some more info:
~ They always have several vegetarian options, as well as a "plain" version.  
~ As other reviewers have mentioned, they also have roti, which I have never tried, because how can you not have the pizza?  
~ The drink selection is the only downer.  I'd suggest grabbing a soda from the CVS down the street.
~ Prices usually run between $2-4 for a slice, depending on how fancy it is, and around $14 for the pie, which I would say feeds three.

The decor is creative and cool, and they change it up pretty regularly. The chairs are so grandiose, you feel like you're sitting in a castle.

And they make one hell of a burger.

I'm a fan of the place in general but just know that if you get a sandwich you are mostly paying $9 for bread. They make their own bread, which is awesome, but they slice it so thick that all you can taste of the sandwich is bread, and they don't balance it by putting more fixings between. I got a BLT and it was one leaf of lettuce, a couple thin slices of tomato and a smattering of bacon. I paid $1 extra to add "avocado" but there were no avocado slices, just some brownish avocado smeared on the bread. What a bummer. The smoothies are good though and the staff generally nice.

In the spring and summer, I want to be outdoors as much as possible.  I try to think of new places to go, but I always end up coming back to Hi-Rise because they have so much outdoor seating and the food is so good -- on the pricey side, yes, but so far ahead of any competitors in Harvard Square on the taste dimension.   

Plus side, get one of their fantastic coffees and enjoy the warm weather.

Downside, your coffee will be gone by the time the food comes.  But this is fair -- would much rather have them take the time to make a great sandwich (or for me the grilled chicken salad) then pick up a pre-wrapped something elsewhere.

Despite what another poster said I have got to disagree: old weirdos playing chess outdoors is the BEST THING EVER.  Don't buy anything from Au Bon Pain but spend some quality time in the gorgeous outdoor seating space watching chess masters seriously school some newbies.

If you want to play, drop some money to play a master.  Some of my friends play here every few days and they have learned a lot because of it.

Staff is warm, sociable and efficient, easy to strike up conversations with Capital One reps as well. 

Location is excellent and tends to be less crowded than Peet's across the street and Starbucks due to the shop leaving more empty space between tables and chairs and to (possibly) fewer people being familiar with the Capital One Cafe concept. 

Coffees and teas are good, food selection can be limited as shop has tried to do prevent wasting unused inventory. Recommended for quiet studying, the usual patrons and the environment are conducive to focus.

This place makes we want to eat sushi more often. We had the white tuna and without getting to graphic I believe my mouth climaxed. Service was great, when a more seasoned sushi eater at our table made a complaint about the freshness of the salmon roe, they immediately apologized and removed it from the bill.

This place is great! I got the Viet Bowl with kale and quinoa. It was super tasty but also felt light and healthy. It's exactly the kind of meal I'd try to prepare for myself: fresh ingredients, not too much sauce or dressing, and lots of delicious greens. Then the crunchy peanut topping and fresh basil take it to the next level. I'm definitely coming back soon!

The only reason I'm even writing a review is that we were charged $1 for bread that we did not order. Seeing that on the check made me feel like I walked in, ate, and got kicked in the balls before I walked out. Now for the review:

Unfortunately, to be fair you have to compare this place to all other Spanish tapas bars. And when you compare it with the likes of Barcelona, Toro, Tasca, Tapeo, and Dali, this place is a couple steps below the rest. Pretty cool, modern atmosphere, but the food is mediocre. Tried 8 tapas, and the only one that jumped out was the Bunuelos (Broccoli & Cauliflower Fritters). Everything else from the calamari to the patatas bravas, very basic.

Server was great. If it weren't for the bread thing, I might give this place 3 stars. Unfortunately, when you spend $120 in a place, that extra buck is unforgivable.

I ate lunch here with the folks from my office for the Holidays. The interior is beautiful and modern. We had a group of 12 and the accommodated us nicely. The waitress was attentive and friendly. We had the red Sangria which was good. We ordered several things and all of it was great. The pork with mushrooms and blue cheese were fantastic as well as the Salmon with spinach with Dijon sauce. I also really enjoyed the garlic shrimp and the roasted mushrooms.  I would absolutely go back!

LOVE it. So far I've tried the Blushing Billy (Goat Cheese, baby spinach, Shredded red beets, toasted pine nuts, Mozzarella cheese), the Greener Pastures (sauce, pesto, Mozz. cheese, chicken, onion, mushroom) and the Blue October (squash, Mozz cheese, onion, forget what else). So, as you can see they come up with some very interesting varieties of pizza. And they all work so well! They also have a plain cheese pizza, which I haven't had, but must be good. The Blushing Billy one is so amazing - definitely my favorite! 
The slices are very big. Last week I ordered a whole pizza (they'll do half one flavor, half another) and I fed myself one night, me and my friend another night, my boyfriend and me another night. I'm telling you, it's a lot of food. Oh and the service is great too - part of my pizza was apparently a little too "crispy" - which i didn't even notice, and so they threw in a peanut butter cookie. great, now I'm also addicted to their cookies! sooo good. 
lastly, I have had one salad there, and it was awesome. The main awesome part was the dressing, but i forget what it was called right now - something lemon something, sorry, but it was soo good. 
I still haven't tried the Roti but I'm really dying to. Apparently they sell out fairly quickly during the day. 

So - to sum up, the food is just so tasty and the service has always been lovely. To add to that, they use fresh and organic ingredients when possible. HIT IT UP!

It's not terrible. Sometimes when I get sick of Anna's and don't want to make the walk over to Tacos Lupita I will stop in and have a few tacos. This place will probably be closed by Christmas.

UPDATE: maybe they'll be closed by next Christmas. I don't know how they stay in business. Down one star after a bad visit where this place turned into a kiddie playland. Plenty of empty space to run around in, I guess. The walk to Tacos Lupita is such a  better idea.

UPDATE 2: Ha, so I was only off by a year!
This au bon pain is particularly good because of the location.  Grab a safe and good sandwich from the bakery and then enjoy a game of chess, or just people watch.
Great Funky pub in cambridge. DJ spins techo on most nights. Good food---try the steak sandwich and the garlic fries. Friendly Irish barkeeps too.

the harvard sq abp is a landmark (and the best abp, i might add).  it's a great place to meet up with people, particularly out-of-towners, and always a good spot to grab some lunch/coffee and take in the harvard scene (prepster students, drugged out drop-outs, and of course the chess-players).  i used to spend hours here freshman year when i was depressed by mit and kept wishing i'd applied to harvard (of course, i got over that quickly :P).

Listen, Small Plates, I really wanted to love you...I really did.  Maybe that's why I was just so...um, unimpressed by what you gave me.  

Hey, hey...it's not you...it's ME.  I'm just so used to OTHER tapas places that actually have charming character and decor and exciting food.  It's not like you were terrible, Small Plates, but it just kind of paled in comparison.  Your food _was_ tasty, but I don't know.  I expected something...more.

You only could offer me and my date about 10 bottles of wine to choose from...  I know, I know...you didn't want to overwhelm me.  Unfortunately, you sort of underwhelmed me.  

Listen - I'll try not to make it awkward if we happen to run into each other in Harvard Square...I'll walk by and say "hi", but I don't think I'll sit down again....just try not to make it weird for me and my girl Tasca, OK?

I definitely will go back on a regular basis, especially for the coffee. 

Hi-Rise is definitely one of those places that if you are not looking for it, you'll walk right by it.

I went with my friend Andrew this past weekend for the first time. The outdoor patio was really nice and I liked the quiet atmosphere. Perfect for people watching and enjoying a quiet meal in Harvard Square.

I tried the iced coffee because of a recommendation. They have two, I ordered the 24 hour brew and was very impressed. The flavor was not too intrusive but it was definitely present and it was smooth. Definitely a nice change from the burnt iced coffee that I usually get at a place like Starbucks. I drink my coffee black, so if you like good smooth black coffee, I recommend it.

The service was a little slow, so I wouldn't recommend this place if you are in a rush, but the food was excellent. I ended up getting a sandwich that I can't remember the name of. It was pate, ham, cucumber, cilantro, etc. all on a baguette. Very tasty. I was impressed. I wasn't sure what to expect. I topped my meal off with an oatmeal raisin cherry cookie. The size of the cookie was HUGE and it was nice and soft. However there really wasn't much flavor to it and I was a little disappointed.  Despite this, I would definitely recommend Hi-Rise and its coffee.

I come here often because I live locally but I'm never disappointed. I got the chicken entree and the salmon the last two visits and I highly recommend both of those choices. Drinks are also fantastic and a lively environment.

The food is really good, so is the Sangeria. Been there a couple of times and both times the servers were very pleasant. It is a little pricey though. I would Definitely go back. My favorite dishs are the garlic shrimp, the lamb chops and the goat cheese stuffed eggplant. I'm very picky when it comes to food and I think this place is great would highly recommend.

Finally... my search for a good sushi place that will not make my wallet anorexic has ended with Hana Sushi. The best part is that it so close to my apt so it has quickly become my new fishing hole. 

It's a cute restaurant on Mass Ave. that could easily be missed. The decor inside is nice but nothing too amazing, except the ceiling. For some reason, I really like the decor on the ceiling. The staff is also very friendly. There's this one waiter working everytime I come in and he has the most adorable and welcoming smile. 

I'm not a big fan of sashimi even though I know that's how you REALLY taste the fish. My absolute favorite roll here is the Butterfly Roll, which they put really small apple pieces on it to make it interesting. All their other specialty rolls are also very delicious. I haven't hit one I did not like yet.

All in all, the fish is fresh, the service is friendly and quick, and the prices are reasonable. I'd say it's true love. Although I have to warn that some of the rolls might have peanutty things in them. My friend who is deathly allergic to peanuts found this out the hard way with a nice trip to the ER. So be careful if you are allergic to peanuts.

I have a Capital One account and thought there were no real C1 banks in the Boston area, so when I stumbled across this one not far from my neighborhood I was pleasantly surprised! ATM, bank tellers, free wifi and HALF OFF all café drinks! So I got a large chai latte and did some work there for the afternoon. It was mostly empty and the staff was super pleasant. What a great deal!

I ordered the cowboy burger and bacon cheddar cheese fries. The burger was okay, but very dry and while I ordered medium rare, it was much closer to rare than I usually go for. There was basically no BBQ sauce to be found. But the most disappointing thing was definitely the fries. There was very little cheese, the "bacon" was basically just chopped ham, and the biggest tragedy was that the fries themselves tasted like McDonalds. Granted, they were tasty, but if I wanted McDonalds, I would have gone to McDonalds, and I certainly wouldn't have paid 6.95 for them. All totaled, while the meal arrived on time and hot enough, it wasn't worth it. I should have gone to McDonalds.

I have tried to come here for lunch a few times.  Both times prior to today, the kitchen was closed (once with a huge sign that said "Kitchen Closes at 4" across the sandwich bar, but it was a little after 3)

Perhaps they cater better to Newfies  Which might explain the size of their sandwiches (those Newfies can eat!)

So the weather is finally fantastico, and I was hankering for a little outdoors dining, and decided to give it a whirl - and it worked, for the most part.

The ordering area is tight and fast paced, and I can see where other yelpers have found the 'tude to be sub par, but I was more distracted by the fact that they try to get you ordered and paid so quickly, and when my sandwich choice was sold out, I got flustered with trying to pick something else - and I ordered randomly.

I then sat thinking at how I had probably made a bad second-choice - the fracking sandwich was on cornbread, which assaulted my brain as I thought I was going to get this weird crumbly greasy concoction that would just ruin my experience.

Fortunately the 'corn bread' wasn't nearly the thing I had in mind - and the sandwich was quite good.  I am not sure it was $9.50 good, but then again, I pay $11 for a chicken sandwich at some pubs, and it definitely was several steps up from that.

the joint is cash only, and light on non-coffee drinks.  The outdoor patio is nice, and they apparently will deliver your order to you so you don't have to wait in the sardine factory of a store front.

the sandwich did take 10+ minutes to make, but it was warm, and the place was hopping, so I guess I wasn't expecting Subway.

I think Darwin's around the corner on Mt Auburn does a better sandwich, for less.  But I like to mix it up, so this won't be my last lunch here.

Came here to catch up with a friend who just moved back into the city. We got a half-dozen oysters (love how they have Island Creek), salmon on a bed of spinach, the daily special which was pasta with clams, and flourless chocolate cake. The service was great, the food was delish, & the ambiance was nice, spacious and a bit upscale. It was a bit pricey, so I would recommend this for a special occasion. I also wish they had more gluten-free entree items, though the cake was perfect!

I worked across the street from Sarah's for years so the convenience store and the café were my go-to places.

The convenience store at Sarah's is a bit more upscale than most corner stores.  Yes you've got lottery tickets and the typical conveniences but there's also higher end treats, etc.  Tucked beside the register is a rack of greeting cards and I've had much better luck with those than the Hallmark cards at CVS.  

The sweet spot at Sarah's is the café.  I've made my way through much of the menu and had many of the meals dozens of times, sometimes eating there twice in a day. As others have mentioned, Raj and the cooks are super friendly.  Your food is cooked right behind the counter and I am always impressed by the cooks' good nature even as they take orders, fill in at the register, and answer the phone.  I worked in restaurant years ago and most cooks are ogres in my experience. That's definitely not the case here. 

The breakfasts are excellent and served all day on Saturday and Sunday. I came across another review that complained about the breakfasts and while I can't speak to the hash, the eggs, toast, and yes - the pancakes - are all superb. I'm a DD-only coffee drinker but have never heard any complaints about the coffee either.

As to the meals - here are some of my favorites:  the Chicken Curry (makes two meals easily), the Grilled Salmon served over salad (you feel virtuous eating it but I imagine the delicious creamy sauce adds some calories), the Turkey Burger, Middle Eastern Veggie Wrap, the Veal Parm, Chicken Alfredo, Tuna Melt, Corn Chowder (when it's on the menu), and the Omelettes which are served all day.  Unlike HiRise around the corner where I would quickly get bored with menu items, at Sarah's once you find a favorite you'll forever have cravings to have it again, and again.

Sarah's tables can fill up at lunch time but there are seats at a couple of counters for the overflow.  It's also an excellent option for take-out - call on your way and your food will be ready to bring home.

Sarah's Café is a hidden gem.  Don't just drive by - stop in!

Good food and good bar scene. Very busy at happy hour. Have tried the brisket sandwich, veggie burger and minestrone soup all which are very good. The sandwiches come with fresh baked french fries too which are a very nice addition!

based on the other yelps, this place is hit or miss.  I loved it though.  Six of us went, and ordered two or three dishes each.  Most were good to great, the muhummera (walnut, feta, red pepper dip) was excellent.  The shiraz and oregon pinot were fabulous.  The waitress either answered our questions, or asked the kitchen if she didn't know rather than fake it.  Cute space, a little tight.  If you go to a place that calls itself small plates and are disapointed by the size of the plates then...

I'm a fan of any place that spreads carbohydrates atop other carbohydrates. Damn, that Blue October'll knock your socks off!

We were one of the minions that hounded this small little place the day after purchasing a Groupon deal that essential gave us $10 off our order. It was a madhouse in there. Nevertheless, our pizza was done within 10 minutes of when they promised, and it was fresh, tasty, and not in the least soggy (which is often the fate of thin crust pizza). Among the four of us (two relatively thin and active women and two relatively meaty-meat-eating men), we stuffed ourselves with nearly two pies (only 2 slices leftover). We all thought they were fantastic.

My only criticisms... (1) It's VERY small. I would probably never plan on eating there unless during down-times. (2) It's a little pricey; although you are paying for tasty, organic, wholesome pizza (if "wholesome" is a word I can truthfully use next to "pizza") and in the end it's worth it, it will put a bigger dent in your wallet than the typical pizza/subs joint. Just be prepared... (3) The menu is small. While the choices are awesome and I don't foresee myself NOT enjoying any of their options, I'm one who enjoys being bombarded by selection. Their menu really only offers four different styles beyond a regular cheese pizza or pepperoni pizza. Beyond that they only have salads... I'd like to see what this place could do with some samiches. mmmmmmm

However, those criticisms are negligible and are nothing that should keep you from trying a pizza that has butternut squash on it. (Have I mentioned my carb obsession?)

The lamb ribs in the orange glaze are just delicious.  I still miss them after almost 2 years.  They are incredibly tender and slow-raosted to flavorful perfection.  If you eat here, they are a must-try.  The marrow bone app is also a good option.  The burger is just okay; Mr. Bartley's is only a few stops away on the T so I would much rather go there for my burger.  However, the small plates here are very good.  Highly advise just pairing 2 for a meal rather than getting an entree.

A cute little cafe serving Peet's coffee and tea within a Capital One location. They have a decent amount of seating with armchairs, regular tables, and a bar area. It wasn't too crowded when I wandered in, and they have some nice specials (50% off drinks with your Capital One card, and 50% off drinks with a student ID from 7-9pm every night).

The drinks are pretty standard, with a good selection of hot and iced coffees and hot teas. They didn't have any frappucinos or smoothies though, which are my favorite type of drink. I got a medium chai latte with whipped cream ($4.20 plus $0.35 for the whipped cream). The drink came out fast and the chai tea was creamy, smooth, and delicious. I do wish they didn't charge extra for the whipped cream, but overall I liked the service, the drink, and the atmosphere. I may come back here to study if I find myself in Harvard Square with my laptop!

Had my first meal here tonight and I was pleasantly impressed. I don't really get the whole small plates thing but I figured hey, why not give it a try.

The rabbit was extremely succulent, accented with two carrots and the cajun glaze was great - it was cajun style and just spicy enough to make me cringe a little if I took too much of a bite, but not bland enough that I didn't notice it. This was my favorite by FAR. Vegetarians beware, that litle bunny was TASTY.

Second, the Mussels I had were above average. I wasn't blown away by them specifically, but the sauce they were cooked in was to die for. I asked for the leftover sauce in the bowl to take home, I'm going to make a pasta dish with it. 

Next up was the semi-raw spicy tuna. Whatever it was glazed with to make it spicy was fantastic. I'm not a huge fan of raw items, so this was my least favorite, but I still ate the whole thing and was pleased by it.

Top that off with a nice selection of reasonably priced bottle of wine, and a perfect sized dessert of a scoop of vanilla ice cream garnished with fruit, I was a happy camper.

Other pros: Cool layout, you feel like you're just eating in someone's living room. Someone else posted about eating in the 'backyard', will have to try that in warmer weather.

Only cons: Hard to have a private conversation so be prepared for someone to hear your entire convo. Also, for whatever reason the lighting was really really bright to me. Maybe its just a pet peeve of mine but I enjoy a little bit more dimly lit space for privacy. Lastly like other people have mentioned it is pricey. If you want to be truly full at the end, and/or have a healthy appetite (both apply to me, admittedly), you'll have to dish the cash out. Dinner for two with wine and 20% tip was $120 for us.

Definitely will go back to try the other items on the menu.

So happy that Whole Heart is in Cambridge now. I've been to the one in Allston a ton of times and it's my favorite spot for a quick, cheap, and healthy meal that's also delicious. The new Cambridge spot is awesome. It is definitely my new go to lunch spot at work. My favorite things on the menu are either the Kaya or Seoul bowl but you really can't go wrong with anything on the menu. Would recommend this place to anyone.

I have to agree with Jen on this one - River Gods is trying to be both a restaurant and a funky bar/lounge at the same time, but just doesn't have the space to pull off both successfully, which is a shame because it does have great potential. 

The decor is certainly unique and the DJ spins well, which makes me inclined to think that going for the pure bar option would be the best route.

One of my favorite places in Cambridge.  Off the beaten path, locals hangout, funky atmosphere, excellent food, and good DJ's.  Bouncers are always friendly - waitstaff a little surly, but understandable since the place is so small and usually packed. 

Food is great... freshly prepared and delicious.  Frites are awesome defintely eat it Belgian style with mayonnaise aioli.  River god salad is tasty and filling.   Everything that I've eaten on the menu is good.  Check out the specials on the smaller cards at each table.  But watch out - dinner ends around 9-10PM - afterwards you only can get their chips and dip (but still good - red pepper hummus?).  Not good for late night eats.

Parking is hell for anyone who doesn't have a Cambridge resident sticker, but it's worth the walk from Central Square.  About 5 mins down River St.
We just loves their pizza, specially the Caprini...hummm the oignon marmalade is the best. We like the music in the restaurant also! And the brunch is as good as expected. The gravy is delicious.

The place was absolutely filthy especially where the food was prepared.  One customer in the entire restaurant. The staff was hanging out joking around. I was actually shocked after hearing so many great things about this place.  Maybe they're putting all their energy towards the grand opening in Harvard.

YES

One hundred thousand times, YES.

Egg sandwich was perfect.  Egg cooked better than any egg I've ever had.  I've had a lotta good eggs. I've also had a lotta bad eggs and those, my bagel loving friends, are life altering.  Bad eggs.  Who needs em?!  Good, fresh cooked eggs, hot off the grill on a bagel made with love by Ms. Mary Ting Hyatt and the other Saurus folk who make up the awesome team that is Bagelsaurus......what was I saying?  I was just salivating when I was remembering the awesome sandwich I just ate.  It was $6.38, which considering the quality that went into this masterpiece of a bagel sandwich, is quite fair.  

Grab a seat if you can snag one, if not, take it to go and devour in private, you might need a minute to yourself since it's so good.  You won't want to talk to anyone, except to exclaim, "Oh my god, this bagel!  Oh my god, it's so good!" You might say this to a stranger, you might say this in your own head, or you may tell the owner, who was sweeping up bagel crumbs how damn good her bagels are.  You may feel weird getting all verklempt over a bagel, but it's a freaking good bagel.  You'll even tell her, that your fiance, would drive on a Friday morning from Medford to Brookline, just to go to Bagelsaurus early in the morning, when the pop-up Bagelsaurus restaurant would show up inside Cutty's.  

You'll add, that you are so happy that Bagelsaurus is in Porter Sq now.  She will smile, genuinely at your little story and go back to sweeping the store.  You'll smile, knowing you now have a bagel shop for life.  Please, Bagelsaurus don't leave!  I hope you can feel the bagel love around you from your patrons, old and new, new and old.

I feel crazy writing this long review after eating one bagel sandwich, but I feel it's my solemn duty to let you all know about this!  Read on!

I heard some real Noo Yawkas in the small restaurant today proclaiming them AMAZING.

Yes, I still love Bruegger's down the street but Bagelsaurus has quickly moved into my carb loving heart.

I will be a regular since this is about 10 minutes from my work and I frigging love bagels!  WELL MADE BAGELS, that is.

Bagels made with heart, bagels made with love, bagels made for you and me.

Get thee to the source. 

Get thee to the Bagelsaurus!

Decent breakfast spot with a little more flair than a typical brunch spot. However, the size of the restaurant is incredibly small so the wait was over 45 minutes. While the food was pretty good, I think that the french toast wasn't really up my alley. The french bread seemed like it wasn't dipped in the batter/custard long enough so the middle was way to "bready" for french toast. The potatoes that came with the toast were tasty and cooked well as was the sausage link I got as a side.

I could easily upgrade this review to a 4 had I gotten a dish like my brunch companions, but for now I'm giving city girl a 3.

WOW this food sucked.  Maybe I went on an off day but my burrito was literally inedible.  I threw out almost all of it.  The tortilla was like rubber so I unwrapped it and tried to just eat the middle with a fork.  No good.  There was, I kid you not, half a cup each of sour cream and guacamole and a tablespoon of meat.  While I understand that vegetarians may be happy that there are more veggie options than Ana's, I would forgo burritos altogether if this were my only choice!

Delivery took nearly two hours and and the quality was inconsistent. 

The name of the restaurant turned out to be misleading, as our Pizza took way too long to arrive. The supreme-style pizza was really good. It was hot, flavorful, and loaded with tasty toppings. The margherita-style pizza was another story. It was dry, cold, and the bread seemed almost stale. 

Something clearly went wrong with our order behind the scenes... Not sure I want to find out what. 

With so many other options, I'll probably pass on Pronto next time I need pizza pronto.

MEH.

I realize that ABP is meant to be someplace to grab a quick bite, and is not fine dining.  The food is consistently OK (but never great...even for a quick bite).  The service is consistently bad.  

The last 3 times I've gone in, I've ordered one of the "standard" sandwiches,  but with some small change--different bread, no mayo.  For some reason, this caused the sandwich maker to call an all-company meeting behind the counter to figure out what to do with my sandwich.  I don't understand why not putting mayo on my sandwich is a life-altering decision for these people.  

If that wasn't enough, nobody ever speaks.  Unless the manager is nearby, its unlikely anyone says "Hello", "Have a nice day", or even "That will be $7.99".

The food gets 3 stars....but the people & service only get 1.

Zing's pizza (I haven't had the roti) is "good", but I certainly don't think it merits the many 4 and 5 star ratings on here.  I guess that's because for me, pizza is all about the crust.  No matter how creative and crazy the toppings, or how organic and local they are, if the crust isn't 5 star, the pizza isn't for me.  In a thin crust, I value the crisp factor, and Zing's just isn't quite up there.  It tastes fairly good and is somewhat crispy, but not really top notch.  I also hesitated to even order from here because of the lack of different sizes: you can get slices or one giant pie; and the fact that you can only choose from their featured pizzas.  I mean come on?  How hard is it to stock some basic ingredients like peppers, mushrooms, etc.  So while I didn't expressly dislike Zing, I'm not sure I'd go out of my way to order here again.

The delivery was super fast, however, I would not recommend the cheesesteak. I ordered extra cheese and I got a slab of congealed cheese on the top, none of the gooey melty goodness you want with a good cheesesteak. 
The chicken salad sandwich was decent, however it was about 80% bread...I had to combine the two halves worth of chicken salad to one half of the sandwich.

Love this place!  Everyone that works there is really friendly and the food is yummy.  I particularly like the fact that even though it's a small cafe, they make things to order.  And they do it graciously!  I know I'm a picky cry-baby about my food, but they never even bat an eyelash when I order.

They are quick too.  I don't always have a lot of time between clients to get something to eat, but I know I can always get something nutritious and yummy PDQ when I run up there from my spa.

Def check out the super fluffy pancakes!  YUM!

LOVE this place! The sushi is delicious and always fresh. Don't miss the midnight sun roll or hamachi tempura roll! The setting is casual and comfortable, small but I haven't had to wait for more than a few minutes. The meters on Mass Ave are free after 6pm so I've never had a problem parking. The staff is friendly and always eager to please.

Qdoba is great... if you want to vomit all over yourself and have explosive diarrhea to match!
I can't believe such a terrible excuse for a burrito shop dared to open within a few hundred feet of Anna's Taqueria. It's amazing Qdoba is still open.
The burritos weigh like 10 lbs and their employees put like 1 lb of guacamole inside that tastes like mold. The rest of the fillings taste like nothing. But you'll keep eating that massive burrito, praying you'll get to the place within that tortilla that has some flavor. It is a sad, sad day when you have to eat a meal at Qdoba.

We stayed at the Le Meridien hotel, which is about a block away. 
Delivery service was excellent; food was a 3 out of 5 because they try to be cutting edge. Portions were good, just be aware of the flavor combinations described, they may not be as appetizing as you're looking for.

They have great service, but the sushi was mediocre. We ordered the sushi and sashimi for two and a lot of the fish tasted like it was just pulled out of the ocean. It didn't taste clean. The salmon and tuna was delicious and the gyoza appetizer was very good. We may have ordered the wrong meal but seeing as it was a sample of a lot of fish I was slightly disappointed

Wanted a breakfast spot near Alewife but that didn't require getting in the thick of traffic. This fit the bill. There are tables in the deli area, and a range of breakfast choices (eggs and hash, egg white sandwich with avocado, omelettes, etc.)  There lunchtime deli sandwiches sound good too.

It's a Taco Bell. You know what you're getting. It's not healthy, but it's fast, tasty, and cheap.

I've been to this Taco Bell several times. It's always been very fast, even when it's super busy, and I've never had any complaints about what I get.

Tip: Taco Bell's nacho fries box is good!
Excellent ambiance and affable staff! My small soy latte was 0.34¢ cheaper than at the nearby Starbucks, not to mention the décor here is much more upscale and ornate than at Starbucks.

I wouldn't set out a plan to go to Sarah's market. It's merely one of those spots where you "are in the neighborhood" and pop in to grab a coffee, candybar, pack of gum, odwalla smoothie, etc...

It's really cute. People go there to actually sit down and eat. So try it if you're in the area.
The coffee is decent. The service is always cordial.

Probably my favorite restaurant in cambridge, extremely fast delivery, and great prices.  I LOVE their chicken fingers, fries, calzones and turkey subs.  I have nothing bad to say about this place!!!!

I can understand where a lot of the bad reviews are coming from--the guys who work there do come across as a little smug or hipster--but if you go often enough, they start to remember you, your name, your preferences, and they warm up. If you can get past that, the food is delicious. It's pricey, yes, but not compared to similar things in the square. 

Their sandwiches are fantastic: great fresh bread, lots of good cheese, plenty of avocado, lovely meats ... Their baked goods are excellent too: moist, dense brownies and cake-like goodies; cookies bursting with chocolate and nuts; creative scones. All that said, I'm not sure that the focus on the service is warranted. It's not like you have to eat the lunch with them.

Careful when calling or ordering online. They need to get their communication act together. I tried calling to place an order and nobody answered the phone, not even an answering machine. This was during their open hours, like, a Thursday at 6 pm. At the same time, the 3rd party ordering service they use wasn't working. You also couldn't view their menu online without actually starting to place an order, and with the ordering system down, tough luck. 

When we finally went there in person, I was allowed to order a sandwich and pay before finding out they didn't actually have the meat on hand. So I swapped it for something slightly more expensive but not really what I wanted. Not that they wouldn't have given a refund but my SO already ordered her breakfast, so there I was. The food is decent. I enjoyed my sandwich and my SO's homefries were top-notch.

This Qdoba: 

Why when there's so many other food options in Porter Sq? There's just no reason to eat here. Hit the Anna's in the Porter Exchange if you need this type of food and want to leave your car in the lot.

This applies to ALL Qdobas:

The service is usually nice and effective. The food is always flavorless and strangely gross. The ingredients all seem fresh enough but I can't help feeling like I just ate soft plastic afterwards. Saying this stuff is Mexican food is like saying white-bread with ketchup and a cheese single on it is Italian.

Fantastic seafood soup. Excellent service. Rotating seasonal menu. Ingredients were local and fresh. Highly recommended. I come here every time that I'm in the state. Good cocktail wine and rotating beer selection as well.
Walk in with only 3 people ahead of me. Wait 15 minutes just to be talked to and told it would be another 15 minute wait for chicken. Way too slow even when not crowded.

Not worth all the hype it gets. Slow service, noisy atmosphere, tiny portions for a lot more $ than its worth. Food quality-mediocre at best. Salad was wilted and burger bun tasted stale. I wouldn't go back here. Maybe drinks are better, but I was unimpressed with the veggie burger and the salad options.

The Tortellini Alfredo is really good in an artery clogging kind of way, the delivery is usually speedy, and the calzones and pizzas are good. Bonus points for being one of the few decent establishments open after 12am in Cambridge. And they'll deliver Tosci's ice cream with your order!

I recently came here with Emily and even though I had a pretty tasty, albeit expensive, slice of pizza, I won't be going back.  I got something with garlic and veggies on it and it was good, I don't usually like pizza that much but this had a really light, crispy crust and light cheese.

The service was atrocious, though.  I came here after getting my tattoo and my blood sugars were really low, my legs could barely support me.  I wanted to buy my slice of pizza, eat it, and go home and rest before going out.  The cashier for some reason would not ring me up, and 10 minutes later, I gave Emily some cash and peaced out so I could head home.  If you are going to sell pizza by the slice, it's probably going to be to people who also don't have a lot of time.  This is also a commuter location, I can't be the only person that's ever gone in there in a hurry.  Also, what kind of establishment won't take cash out of a customer's hand?
Very small restaurant and a pretty long wait but I'd say the food was worth it. Not much else I can say. I had the french toast and I would recommend it to anyone.

I waited to give my review on this place because I first went when it opened. I went there again and now I think I'm ready to give my assessment.

For the first time Me and the BF got the gnocchi lamb poutine, roasted oysters, fried fish tacos and the charred octopus. Our first visit was great I loved the food and even asked for bread to be brought out so I could dip it in the sauce from the poutine. For drinks I got to taste two white wines and picked one for my glass and the BF got a beer. We were impressed and new that we would be back again! 

We went last night, 18th to celebrate my birthday. The server was great. We ordered the same items mentioned above in addition to the smoked blue fish and well our taste buds didn't have the same experience as the first time. The poutine had just too much gnocchi and there was no sauce for dipping the bread-very unfortunate. Also the poutine looked like it was premade and not fresh like the last time. The charred octopus didn't melt in my mouth like the last time. Although we enjoyed the fried fish tacos it was missing a sauce to blend the flavors. I didn't enjoy the smoked fish but my bf did so I think that the flavor wasn't best for me.
For drinks I went with a sparkling rose which was served in a fancy glass and he got beer. The service was absolutely wonderful it was attentive but also he gave us time to ourselves which we always like.

Overall, I'm giving this place 3 stars because of their lack of consistency. Because this is a restaurant the food should be consistent throughout and well it wasn't. It's definitely a must try- when the kitchen is having a good day. The service is wonderful!

Wonderful café!

I always receive super nice service from this café, whether it's from the Peet's Coffee baristas or the Capital One ambassadors. 
I felt a bit awkward the first time I came in because I don't have an account with Capital One, but nobody judges or throws a sales pitch at you when they see your card (even the bank ambassadors), which I appreciate. (You do get 50% off drinks if you have a Capital One card though, which sounds lovely!)

The coffee is great and there is free wifi, a bunch of power outlets, and quite a bit of seating--although it is understandably pretty crowded most of the time. 
I love to grab a coffee and/or pastry and settle down and get work done here!

Convenient! with an awesome staff!
I went in not knowing what to expect because I'm not from around here. I didn't know if I wanted coffee or something frozen when I walked in. The girl at the counter had silver hair and was completely knowledgeable about the products! She offered me multiple options for coffee and tea! 

Prior to talking to her I had never heard of matcha powder before and I guess it's more like green tea than coffee. Can I just tell you the matcha javiva is my new favorite go to! They have multiple types of milk as well offering vegan options like soy and almond milk!
It reminded me of a frappe from Starbucks! But the texture was way better being less icy and more creamy. Happy I got to try something new! It was really clean and had a lot of room to sit to use the Wifi and charge my phone.

Quite a bit of a wait since it's a very small cafe, but it was so worth it. I'm actually not a huge fan of omelettes, but the one I ordered was so fluffy and light overall. The hubby got the goat cheese, caramelized onion and bacon egg sandwich, which he devoured!

I tend to be pretty thorough, so I read all of the previous reviews (yes, all of them).  I decided to order only universally acclaimed dishes and also keep an eye on some of the general criticisms of Small Plates so that I could give the closest thing to an authoritative review.

Ambiance: My wife loved it, and she's an ambiance junkie. Based on the "TOO BRIGHT" reviews, I tucked a pair of sunglasses in my pocket and braced myself for the supernova-strength lighting in Small Plates.  To my surprise, it was mostly lit by little shaded lamps, and was totally fine, which made me feel silly in the welder's mask.  People of Cambridge, you need to come out of your burrows a little more often if you consider that to be too bright.  I know it's the winter, but come on.  (It's possible that they've made it less bright over the years, but I prefer to think otherwise.)  
People had also said that the tables were too close together, so I was expecting Bartley's Burgers-style seating, but the spacing was just fine for a quiet dinner for two.

Food: I was wary of the small-portions problem which had driven some desperate Yelpers to late night pizza and Chinese food, so I planned ahead and had Chipotle for lunch.  By dinner time, I was a little hungry, but not too much.  We decided to order two dishes and a dessert and split (we're also fairly cheap and we don't drink often, so it came to only $25).  Based on my review-scouring, we ordered the braised Cajun rabbit and the mushrooms.  While we waited, we were served the homemade bread of the day, which was quite tasty.

Rabbit: I wasn't impressed.  It wasn't very Cajun (although my wife got one bite that was spicy, I searched in vain to replicate her experience), and it tasted quite a bit like chicken.  Not very flavorful, but decent.

Mushrooms: I was a little skeptical - I mean, $8 for mushrooms and some garlic? - but the reviews were more unrelentingly positive than a show for small children.  WOW, these were delicious.  Buttery, perfectly cooked, and flavorful.  We couldn't get enough, and it was a pretty big serving of mushrooms.  

Dessert: We ordered the Kahlua Creme Caramel, which was like a flan.  It wasn't incredibly special, but it was good.  My only regret was that there wasn't something to soak up the caramel sauce (flan is not very soakable, sadly).  I did tip the plate once to try to get more into the spoon but got a mock-disapproving glare from my lovely wife.  The rest of the caramel disappeared with the plate into the night.

Service: I kept a careful eye on the clock after having seen complaints about slow waitstaff, but our service was impeccable.  Helpful, friendly, and quick - we were in and out in an hour and we weren't in a hurry.  

Intangibles: The whole experience was a fun one - nice place, interesting food, and a fun concept of translating tapas to more everyday food.  

Come with a small appetite, order the mushrooms, and - whatever you do - don't ask how big something is.  My wife made that mistake and our server (as politely as possible) said, "It's not that big - that's kind of the theme around here."  Oops.

Thanks for reading my first review!

If you've never gotten the chance to go to Japan and try the best Sushi in the world, this is your chance, trust me. The Sushi at this Restaurant is always fresh. I have to mention the 2 brothers that own this establishment, they are really friendly and great at what they do. I went back yesterday and the Sushi was as it always is, superb. My husband had the Sushi Sashimi combo for 2 and I had an order of yellow tail, saba, and a shrimp roll, everything was great.

Are you kidding me?  This is a bank with cheap, lousy coffee.  Yes, you can get a tall mochalatteccino with extra corn syrup, but I've got news for you: that ain't coffee.  

Get a small regular coffee here and you'll taste it: old, acidic, burnt swill.  Great workspace?  Fine.  Friendly staff?  Check.  Good location?  Sure.  But don't go there if you want coffee.

Kika is named and decorated for a movie starlet featured in such Spanish films as "Kika" and ... oh interesting ... you should google it.  The decor is super crazy bright and there are patterns and ways to mesmerize you all over the place.  

The tapas menu is fairly short and priced high, but note that they offer skillets of Paella (rice based) or Fiduea (vermicelli based) in two different sizes/prices (party of 1-3, $32, or 4-6, $48) and three different flavors (tradicional, seafood, or veg).  Although I did not experience the dish last night, I will definitely return for a try.

Now, despite the short food menu, there is a nice list of cocktails, cava, rose, red, white wine, a selection of beer, and of course, sangria on tap.  I wouldn't agree with Paul R's opinion on the cost of drinks.  All of the cocktails were $10 or less and 2 for 2, were delicious (Basil Lime Gimlet and La Movida [woodford, sage, cherry preserves]).  I loved the drinks, very tasty.  So, sure, it adds up, but their prices are by no means the priciest I've seen or am willing to spend on a good drink.  They also serve their refreshing wine selection in those super fun shareable decanter/carafes that you can also get at Toro where you pour a tight stream of wine into your friends' mouths...

So, keeping the review low for now.  I'll be back for an update after I get food there.

Ok, this place is definitely different from other bars in Cambridge. It's unique in it's decorations and actually feels like a neighborhood bar, which is impossible to find in this part of Cambridge. The drinks won't blow you away, but the people are cool and it's a great spot to meet up with friends. Also, I've never had problems finding seating. Lastly, they have a big screen to play games which is cool and brings some life to the place.

They have to offer the healthiest wings served to date; they get their chicken three doors down at the poultry shop, hence the wings are unsurpassed in freshness and quality; straight from the butcher.

I have been here twice so far, both enjoyable experiences but I do agree with other posters in that this cafe could be way better. The great thing is that the wireless is free, there are many places to plug in, and there is a lot of room to sit. The downside is that it is a bit chilly in here, the selection of snacks/pastries could be better - I had a cookie that was OK, seemed like its been sitting there a little while. The tea was pretty inexpensive and came in a HUGE mug which was great. And they had no problem giving me more hot water. 
If they filled up the case / presented the snack options better, I think this place would be more successful. Until then, I will probably come back.

I've never had a negative experience here. Went here on a first date and came back for our anniversary. Been with friends for happy hour (5-7pm on weekdays, i think?) and dinner. The menu is always creative and different and delicious. the drinks are interesting and are a great complement to the food. good beer selection. AWESOME desserts. 
I would recommend this place to anyone looking for great service and great food in a lovely atmosphere. There is a patio out back that is just as cozy and intimate as the dining area inside.
Very convenient, friendly staff, great food, beer and wine... what more could you ask for??

I think I have eaten about 200 sandwiches from Hi-Rise by now.  My only wish is that they would come up with some new recipes.  If you're visiting Harvard Square in the summer, this is the place to eat lunch.

The Blue October pizza is delicious!  We had the pizza delivered and they were right on time.  They did not, however, come by bike.  No big deal.  Two things that does not make me give them a 5 - first, delivery was $2 extra dollars and that wasn't very clear from their website.  Secondly, the first time i called, the phone went to voicemail.  The message specifically stated - "don't leave a message."  Luckily, the second time I called someone picked up to take my order.  But, since the pizza was sooo good, I'll call twice again next time.

I really like the setup of this location, there's plenty of seating and open space. Lots of bar seating and a few couches as well. Use your capital one card for 50% off your purchase! Great price for awesome coffee. My favorite is the black tie - cold brew with condensed milk. They do put quite a lot of condensed milk in it which makes it too sweet in my opinion, so I ask for half the amount of condensed milk. 

I also tried the iced matcha green tea latte which was not good...definitely skip this, it just tasted like milk; I had to add a bunch of simple syrup but it didn't help much. Also skip the breakfast sandwiches. It tasted like like a microwave sandwich which had been sitting out.

Oy vey! I like another reviewer's appellation: bagel-tsuris.  Operational nightmares plus innovative, tasty takes on the pedestrian bagel. 

But as long as there is a line out the door... to paraphrase P.T. Barnum, we can write anything we want in this Yelp!...

Great place to get lunch near MIT. Also if you show your MIT student ID, you get a free drink!(: This is one of those vegetarian bowl places...you can either build your own bowl or pick one of the signature bowls. I picked the signature Seoul bowl with marinated kale and brown rice and quinoa pilaf base, and I'm soooooooooooo happy. It's so nice to finally get veggies (been deprived by student dining rip) which are really fresh. Very nice and refreshing, and the spiciness from the gochuchang sauce and the crispiness of the lentils really brings the dish to another level. Sooooooooooo happy right now. 10/10 would recommend.

These are my new favorite burritos and I have them for dinner every Tue & Thu night!  The ingredients seem very fresh, the portions are generous and the chicken burrito bowls taste great.  I think the atmosphere is a step above the competition - the seating area is clean and you can see enough of the food prep area to know it's kept clean as well.  The staff at this location are friendly and seem happy.  They also have a fairly generous 'frequent-eater' system that entitles me to free dinners every few weeks.

Cinderella's has become a lunch-time favorite for everyone at my office. The pasta is by far the best thing on the menu. Their pasta is always cooked to a perfect al dente and their sauce is flavorful and always the perfect quantity!

Although a little on the expensive side, I have never had a bad experience with Cinderella's!

I come here for drinks and snacks all the time! My favorite drink is the Persian Bellini- light, fresh, but definitely alcoholic. The service is good, and the portions are very good for snacking with a group! Love the lights and outside table setup when it's warm out, makes for a great night out ambience.

I've been here for lunch once, and it was fairly empty, but the food and service were still good.
Capital One deserves 5 stars. What great customer service & e atmospheres!! It's definitely the face of future banking.

i just paid $18 for a sandwich and fruit and granola.

AHAHHAHAHAHHAHA



i feel violated.


the bread was ok though.
also...
big ass bees flying around my sandwich.  watch out for that fools!

Whoa, surprised to see all the serious hating on Hi-Rise!

I came in here on a beautiful Sunday afternoon for a light lunch with a few friends and was very happy with it. We sat outside on the patio, which is exquisitely pleasant and just off-the-beaten-Harvard-path enough so that you aren't exactly all in the middle of things like the outdoor patio at Legal or Grendel's but you still get to do some decent Cambridge people-watching if that's your thing.

Try the pourover coffee, which is a cup brewed specially for you upon ordering in an elaborate-looking plastic apparatus that my coffee geek friend informs me is only for sale in Taiwan. Crazy! Also delicious is the iced coffee, which is actually (unadvertised) an iced americano. Strong and good.

I guess it's a pretty bad place if you're looking for a big, hearty sandwich, but a great place for a smaller bite. I had the "Saigon Sub," which was on a pretty small baguette but was highly delicious - pate, ham, daikon, and cilantro on a sandwich make for a great, novel sub and a great snacky meal. Mmm mm.

Visited last week for dinner. Really quiet--one gets the sense that this place is mainly takeout and delivery.  Very good sushi.  My only issue is that it is a little overpriced for the quality and presentation.  And sushi is all about the presentation, no?

I went here for my 25th birthday with my boyfriend. The place had a really laid back vibe for being rather trendy. We ordered a ton of food: gnocchi poutine(AMAZING), ramen(good for a non Japanese restaurant), the lobster roll(seafood was fresh and delicious, and the curry goat stew(OMG!)! 

The cocktails were about average, potentially a bit bland compared to other bars that have made similar drinks. Overall still great though!

It reminds me of a less busy Highland Kitchen in a way. I definitely recommend checking this place out.

Hana is my go to sushi spot.  I was afraid when they went all you can eat that the quality would slip but it certainly hasn't.  The staff is awesome and more importantly the sushi is to die for.  I will sadly die of mercury poisoning because I go here too much.

Scanning for brunch on my trip to Boston "City Girl" didn't appeal to me at first. From the name I thought it might be like NYC's Pret a Manger, industrial business brunch or something. Ohhhh how wrong I was!

City Girl is small and popular, so don't bring 80 of your closest friends and expect to be seated immediately. Like any popular brunch spot, if you squeeze in before the big rush (before 11am) you'll be handsomely rewarded with a table and a great meal. Service is swift and incredibly sweet while the decor really makes you feel cozy with just the right amount of kitchy, mix and match breakfast nook vibe. Everything we saw coming out of the tiny kitchen looked delicious and dining with a vegetarian was no problem, though I don't think the menu has the breadth to accommodate a vegan.

The eats:
-French Toast: the extra thick Italian-style bread makes it look like this might be a small portion, but believe me it's filling! Dip as you go for best results. Totally delicious.
-Eggs verde: Generous portion of eggs with pesto and potatoes. A little heavy on the olive oil but I finished every bite just the same.
-Coffee: Robust and consistently refilled, just the way I like it.

Pro tip: The bathroom sink is in the server's alley behind the register!

Sold on this place for lunch. Super friendly staff and yes you have to go through a store to get to them---no big deal, get over it.
The food is amazing-BLT is a solid sandwich, the Caesar wrap is great and the Corn beef/Pastrami sandwich is outstanding. I'm not a fan of Russian dressing so they ask you if you want something else. "Hell ya- thanks man"
I'll keep going back- in fact once I'm down with this review I am gonna head there now.

Cheers
