In terms of the issues around getting a place to sit - totally agree, and it's sort of absurd to be so stressed out over finding a table when talking about pub food, but there you go. Get there earlier, or head on over to something on Mass Ave., cause you ain't gonna get a seat!

Delicious bagels (my fav is getting a Salt bagel with butter and jam), great coffee (La Colombe), and friendly service. Only downsides are: wish they had decaf, it's a little pricey, the lines are intense. But, mainly I'm just thrilled to have them in the neighborhood.
PS: please open on Mondays! Starting the week without Bagelsaurus is just too cruel.

Decent food (I can only speaker for my burger and gouda fries, which were good but not exceptional) but the drinks I had were FANTASTIC.  I really, really like their drink menu and their bartender knows his way around a Boston shaker.

I like bagels, but dang... I had no idea people dreamt of them.  I mean who hasn't heard of the famous New York and Philadelphia bagels, but so what.  A bagels a bagel right?

Boy was I in for a surprise because according to bagel lovers, Bagelsaurus located near Porter Square in Cambridge has given the Boston area that glimmer of hope to contend with NYC and Philly... Why else would the line stretch almost around the block outside the establishment. That's right, a LINE for bagels.  "I come here everyday" said a regular patron and some NYC transplants admitted that they finally found a "worthy" bagel to travel for.  So we stood in line and tried them for ourselves and I must say, they were pretty good.  There were a variety of bagels to choose from along with various spreads, but since it was my first visit, I opted for the cinnamon raisin with plain cream cheese.  Call me boring, but if an establishment can't get this right than it's time to say, "bye bye."  Fortunately, the bagel was pretty good.  It was fluffy, crispy and chewy and there was the right amount of cinnamon and raisins that made it just right... ala Goldilock's way.

My friends in tow got the sea salt bagel with honey rosemary cream cheese and found the honey rosemary to be De-Li-Cious! The other got the Eggspanola sandwich (over-medium egg with feta, aioli, parsley-scallion gremolata and added avocado and roasted tomatoes) and gave it a thumbs up.  Pretty good for the first visit!  My only gripe is that it's not cheap, so you won't see me here all the time, but it definitely was worth the try.  Next time I go I might get the everything bagel with beet hummus.... sounds yummy!

Although, the bagel craze just might not be my thing, I was curious to see what other bagel creations were out in the universe and did a search on the net. What I  found were some pretty interesting and creative bagels like this.  Time to expand that culinary palate!

My favorite sushi place in Boston-area.. better the Oishii etc, for real. Tip: order the fancy rolls. Their sashimi and basic rolls are good but nothing special. The fish is fresh. Order the french kiss, fushion, and the chef's special (changes). It's more expensive but they are large and delicious. so creative!!

Had a Chicken Caesar Wrap today, which is my standard candle of quality. It was a bit on the heavy side, definitely more fried chicken than caesar salad compare to others in the city. Definitely tasty though, and after eating lunch I feel full, which is kinda the point, isn't it?

Will definitely be back - they have a long list of tasty-looking wraps on the menu. Cute little friendly cafe (and convenience store) in a cute neighborhood.

Decent little cafe with free wifi.  There a few comfy seats and a good number of tables.  The decor was a bit barren but they must've redecorated for a comic book party.  The service was ok and the banana-mango smoothie I got was very tasty.  At 20 oz for $4, I enjoyed it.
Had the chicken with garlic. It was a bit too sweet. Then the lamb meatballs were too fatty and heavy. The Tres leches dessert was just OK. Overall the food is fine but not worth the price.
I used to have a bank, now I have a coffee shop. I like to come here to grade papers and drink lattes.

Pretty good pizza! 

Definitely a overpriced for the quality, but the delivery is really quick and they're open late which is a plus!

Just received our pizza. Delivery took over an hour and it's pan pizza!!! :( I can taste and feel the semolina on the bottom of the pizza and it's so thin I can fit an entire slice in my mouth at one time! Not good pizza nor is it Italian

Quiet restaurant for dinner.

Excellent "tropical pasta" with prosciutto and pineapple.

Note: the table by the window next to the door has the window seats right under the air conditioning vent. This can get annoying.

Whole Heart is great not only for vegans but also for anyone who enjoys healthy, fresh food layered with wonderfully complex yet complimentary flavors. Definitely not your typical fast food. A destination every time we visit the area.

River Gods looks like the waiting area of an S&M dungeon, which I why I always use it to screen first dates. If they can't sit back (on their very own throne!) and appreciate the charm of this dimly-lit gem, no need to bother oiling the handcuffs. The holiday decorations are a personal favorite. Imagine if a whole kindergarten class got their hands on a fifth of Jager and some papier-mache. Pure magic.

The drinks and food won't blow anyone's mind but I am impressed with the range of their vegetarian and vegan selections. I'm neither, so perhaps it's not that impressive that the range is greater than 2 side dishes and not exclusively comprised of potatoes. But I feel that vegetarians and vegans are always getting shafted and it's nice that River Gods has their back.

Ordinarily this would merit about 3.6 stars, but I round up because this is the first delivery pizza we've had here that we'd actually order again.  The crust was tasty, the toppings were fresh and not too heavily applied, and it made it to our door on schedule.   The only downside was that the box didn't fit into our oven.

Overpriced and dirty--$5.19 for a cup of soup, toenail clippings on the floor, a band-aid wrapper and blood on a seat. (Also, this posting is not for the main Harvard Square ABP location by the subway stop. This is a location farther down Mass Ave toward Central Square.)

I think their food was quite alright, in fact very comparable to their competitor Chipotle. It's just a wee bit expensive than Chipotle and it tasted pretty good. Maybe because my last experience with a burrito was not great. I had a chicken burrito and added Guacamole which was freezing for some reason.

Where did this place come from?! Why does it rule so much? Great pizza with inventive toppings, friendly staff, and conveniently located next to a giant parking lot (that I'm pretty sure you're not allowed to park in). But my favorite thing about Zing is that they encourage you to slather your pizza in hot sauce and sell that shit on the side! You'll probably want to get your slice to go because the place is small and too well lit. Also just so you know the pizzas are rectangular, so your may find their nontraditional shape geometrically offensive.

Decent coffee, really good food, plenty of space to open your book/laptop and space out.

I had the opportunity to assess the service situation, and this is my take.  The tall skinny moustachio'd dude behind the counter appears to be a french-american hipster... certainly the most insidious species.  A rare find in the wild, they are both rude *and* judgmental.  After reading the reviews, I came prepared... I was hung over, wearing sunglasses and my locally-ironic cal poly pomona hoodie on.  I almost felt felt welcome, perhaps as much as a half-brother would be.  

On a more serious note, I think the hipsters behind the counter must be the owners, because the lack of courtesy extends beyond what they actually say.  Am I the only person who noticed that the nicest, brightest part of the entire space is the rightmost-part of the kitchen?
City girl cafe has a great vibe, stellar service, and amazing handmade pasta. I would recommend to anyone looking for a hearty meal in Inman.

This place is SO overrated... is what people say when they wake up on the wrong side of the bed before going to brunch. The wrong side of the bed is the left side. True story.

Allow me to contradict what I said earlier, if I may.  This place is SO underrated.  Well to be fair it's quite adequately rated.  I went here for Sunday brunch a few weeks ago and found that the other reviews had prepared me quite well for what was in store.  

--THE FOOD straddled the line dividing "tasty" and "delicious"  the same way a  bike-gang member straddles his Harley

--Speaking of bikers, THE STAFF is comprised of three women, at least two of whom have an array of tattoos.  If you look past their rough and tumble outward appearance, they're all very sweet, coming by every few minutes with coffee refills.  You should challenge them to an arm wrestling match... I would have, but I got scared. (I'm kidding please don't do this, it's too dangerous... oh, and they're also probably pretty busy)

--THE PLACE is small, as has been brought up by EVERYONE. Knowing that, keep in mind that it will be difficult to seat large parties.  I went with my girlfriend and we were seated immediately. Since we had a smaller party we were seated quickly: this lead us to have a small party (in celebration) (small balloons). 

--THE ATMOSPHERE is cute, quirky.  They're not one of those places that has 100 of the same mug on hand, so if you're drinking coffee, expect your mug to look different from your neighbors. There are various portraits of girls on the walls, many of them with eyes far too big for their faces (what, was the artist high??? I don't understand art. F**k culture.) The size of the cafe gives it a certain coziness that you don't get anywhere else.

Overall, 4 stars.  Would have been 4.5 if yelp would have adopted the decimal system like everybody else.

Came here the other weekend to study with a friend.  The space is great -- high ceilings and exposed brick -- but the owners haven't utilized it to its fullest potential.  The lighting is too dark, the tables too small and weirdly spaced.  The coffee is merely okay (I had a double capp), and the pastries are cold and unappetizing.  I had such high hopes for this cafe, which does have free wifi in its favor, but I left disappointed.  I hope that they at least bring in some better tables and chairs soon.

I went here with my mom and my moms friend and we enjoyed it. It was very good overall and not too pricy, each dish ranged from $20-$30 and was good quality. I ordered the steak frites (well done) which was served with fries and Some spinach thing. It was 5/5 stars excellent. My mom ordered the fish cakes and it also came with the spinach she said it was very tasty, My
Moms friend order a burger with fries and said it was "ok". The waitresses where nice but would on and off be caught slacking off. good overall, nice location. Loved outside portion of the restaurant.

I'm giving them 5* for their lunches (all I've tried), simply put, they are bringing it.  Not dirt cheap, but very high quality.  My fav's: Greek salad wrap with grilled chicken, chicken curry.  Much better than the attitude at the place across the street (not Armando's).
Usually going there for breakfast....finally went there for dinner. I had the butternut squash spinach pene, were amazing!! Definitely going back to try more stuff!

I can almost give this place 5 stars, but the atmosphere is a bit sparse. Anyway, I've been twice since my original review. Once I ate at the sushi bar by myself, and the staff was great--attentive, but not overbearing. The nigiri and rolls were awesome.

I got take out last weekend, and the same applied. The rolls and nigiri were very fresh and tasty. This is turning out to be my favorite place on the block.

The owners and employees here seem not to like their customers much. We have tried to study there a few times and are always getting sniped at for combining tables or for not bussing our dishes appropriately. Try Petsi Pies nearby for a great coffee shop where the owners act like they want your business.

Had an early dinner with a friend Friday night and it was perfect. The decor is over the top unique and makes the place special. Our server was friendly informed and flawless - really one of the best I have been served by in awhile. The tapas were great especially the scallops in saffron cream. Its great to have an alternative to Dali and Cuchi Cuchi.
The food is hit or miss. The service is always slow. Won't be going back.  Ordered a pork special. It seemed like it was old over cooked leftovers disguised as the special.

I've been to the one in the Fenway and loved it! Don't go to Cambridge. it was awful. The food looked like it had been sitting out to long, the service was slow and disorganized and the place did not look clean. I go to the fenway next them even though i live in Cambridge. Very disappointed.
Everything is fine, but nothing great.  Had the grilled octopus, roast chicken, and burger.  Very generic, service was a little slow, would probably choose other options in the area next time.

I need this in my everyday life! I was already having some tummy issues from travelling and was worried about lunch options being harsh on my tummy but I didn't need to worry about it after coming across this place. This place was delicious, healthy with very efficient service. Their flavors were solid and I was so happy with my miso bowl. The chickpeas were crunchy and I liked the broccoli texture (it reminded me of crispy kale). Really wished there is something like this back home. Highly recommended!!

City Girl never disappoints. Food is always great and the staff is always friendly.

I had recently moved back to Boston and saw that they no longer do dinners only brunch now. I was disappointed by this because whenever I feel for pasta I go to City Girl instead of the North End. Their Brunch is bomb-dot-com. 

My husband wanted to surprise me with date night and I was trying to figure out where we were headed and I started to guess but he wouldn't say. City Girl was one of my guesses but he didn't say until we walked up to their door. Apparently they do pop up dinners on a Thursday now (not all but 1ce or 2ce a month). We went to their pizza party and let me tell you... I'm not a pizza person, I have to be in the mood for it. Their pizzas are so good.

We started off with a bruschetta which is my favourite bruschetta I've ever had. It's not a refreshing summery taste but more of a comfort food to me with the whipped ricotta.

Then we ordered a pepperoni pizza and a prosciutto, fig and arugula pizza. Pepperoni is and will always be my go-to pizza. The PFA pizza I was a little nervous about it since I'm not really a prosciutto fan since it has such a strong flavour, at first we were thinking to swap the prosciutto with the pepperoni but our hostess wasn't so sure how it would pair with the fig so I said lets do it they way they have it on the menu I'm sure I'm gonna like the pizza since none of their dishes have disappointed me yet. And guess what I really liked it. I didn't even notice the prosciutto because how fresh the ingredients on the pizza were that it just tasted like a very refreshing pizza. I tasted each ingredient individually as well and I couldn't believe how strong each flavour is but they unify when eaten all together.

 
We had their tiramisu for dessert which was good.

Can't wait for the next pop up menu night.

Stopped in for a quick lunch,  but was quite annoyed by the customer service here.  The sandwich counter person acted like it was a major inconvenience to make a hot sandwich for the person in front of me and then again when I ordered. I assume they have a lunch rush here (although, given the reviews,  maybe not) so maybe they were trying to prep for that since we were a bit before noon.  Still,  it's a little counter intuitive to be well prepared for those other customers but then be borderline rude to the ones present. Their setup just seems inefficient and they don't deal with that by being extra friendly. On the bright side,  my turkey Cubano was good in the end.

I've been to this Cafe location a few times now. The staff is always friendly and eager to help and take your order. Drinks are always on point. the Aroma of the coffee just takes you in and you sip til the last drop. 

This case is very good size compared to a couple of others. very clean and provides free wifi as well as a clean lady's and man's bathroom which is also spacious. 

the only thing i don't particular like is the muffins they offer. i realize it's not made by the cafe themselves but wherever they're sourcing it from they suck. they're tiny and has no muffin top. yes, I am a muffin top kinda girl :) 

on the upside, really good croissants; i believe i eat them at least twice a week. 

If you're a Capitol One customer and use your CapitolOne Credit/Debit card, 50% off on all drinks. It's a GREAT deal :)
50% off handcrafted drinks if you use a Capital One credit or debit card. Where else can I get a almond milk latte for under $3??? Also, the space is nice and there are always seats. Love this place.

Shout out to Danielle, our server, who is caring,  patient and quick. And also to Shawna who exemplifies restaurant hospitality at a level that matches any great restaurant in nyc. The food was excellent and portioned well.  Our experience was great.  Get the baked chicken!

The decor is super cool and its not crammed like other coffee shops in the area - they also feature new art on the wall from local artist every month or so. It gives me something new to look at every time I come in and I think its cool that they give the local artist a chance to sell some of their pieces. The coffee is strong just the way I like it and they have a great selection of sandwiches at a good price. My favorite is the chicken avocado and I like how they serve it with chips and pineapple. But the best thing about this place is the GREEN MONSTER. If you have not tried this yet you really don't know how to live. Its pistachio muffin with chocolate chips. They make them fresh and its literally the best thing I have ever tasted in my entire life. The staff is cool and they always let me know whats new on the menu and they remember my coffee when I come in. This place rocks! If you haven't tried it yet you must next time your in town.
Hey guys. Thanks for taking 2 hours to deliver a pizza.

Had severe digestive system distress two hours after coming here (had the clam chowder and Thai beef salad). Seems likely that it was some problem with food preparation. 

Waitress did her best but was overloaded, and did not split the bill as requested. Mediocre compared to other Cambridge options.

Don't get me wrong. I love Taco Bell. The food here is really good. However, this location has problems. The lines tend to be pretty long, but that's to be expected because it's in a mall. But the employees are really unsatisfactory, being rude and unhelpful. For example, I'm trying to cut back on sugar, so I said I wanted water in the cups because I saw that it was an option on the machine. So the dude tells me they don't have it, and offers two bottles of water. I settled for that, but the guy proceeded to mess around with my order. He took everything from the meals and made them separate, which cost me more money. The water was expensive enough. I still go here because the location is convenient, but it's unpleasant.

So this place shows up as one of Boston's most Instagrammable restaurants when you Google it and when you walk in, you totally get why. It is literally glass everywhere, so a) that light hit different and b) you can snoop at what everyone else is eating and drinking. 

I came here for a work lunch with three others. We ordered the honey-sriracha hummus (very flavorful) and then each of us ordered our own sandwich. I had the mushroom melt, which was soooo incredibly truffle-y and cheesy. It came out piping hot with delicious well-seasoned fries and would totally have been Instagrammable had I not been at a work lunch..

The waitstaff is exceedingly friendly and efficient. This place is great!

Whole Heart Provisions is one of the best byob (build your own bowl) places in the Boston area. The service is quick, the servings are large, and the food is incredible. They even serve the bowls to you restaurant-style, which I haven't experienced at other salad bowl places I have eaten at. 

While there are many signature bowls you can get, I personally enjoy customizing my own bowl. They let you add up to two different bases which is really nice if you can't decide between a salad or a rice dish, and up to 4 toppings (additional toppings extra). 

They deliver via the Caviar app but I don't recommend it during regular meal times. I live in Boston, and while I have always gotten my food in a timely manner, the minimums and fees change frequently
depending on how busy the app is. But if you're down for a 3 pm lunch and don't want to travel delivery is the way to go and I haven't had a bad experience with it.

Also, if you are allergic or sensitive to gluten this is a good place to go. Everything on their menu is gluten free except for the buns they use for their falafel dogs and some of their Sunday brunch items.

Lastly, if you are an avocado fan, I cannot recommend their seared avocado enough! They pair smooth avocado with creamy tahini, and it's amazing!

Wow. Wildly disappointed with my experience at this Taco Bell. I placed my order online and waited patiently for the food to be prepared, they were quite busy. 45 MINUETS LATER and after placing my order again online, I asked the woman working there where my order was. Miraculously, she pulled out more order of now-cold-tacos and Mountain Dew. TF TACO BELL. They never called my name, leading me to believe that either someone took my order or it was never processed, thus I threw away 25 good dollars. My tacos were cold and not impressive. Overall a mind numbing extremely disappointing experience. Taco Bell, you lost a loyal customer today.

A cute place off of JFK, priced reasonably enough that students can pull off a dinner without killing the *entire* month's food budget (dinner + drinks for three came to about $75).

The mini-burgers are great, but at $8 for two were priced far too high.  The ricotta-filled crepe was my personal favorite, but the olive-laced bruschetta came in a close second.  Skip the warm plate (cheese, fruit, crackers), nothing special there.

Try to get the outdoor patio if you can swing a table.  It's "seat yourself," so you've got to find that nice "aggressive, but non-threatening" balance with folks finishing their meals.

Zing has thin-crust pizza (I remember it was ONLY thin crust?), covered in crazy combinations of toppings that change daily.  I had the butternut squash pizza and the one with carmelized onions.  They do the "unneeded quirky name thing" for their pizzas, and as I always feel awkward ordering using those, I've forgotten what the real names for the butternut squash pizza and the carmelized onion pizza were.  I bet it was something cute.  

Anyway, both pizzas tasted fantastic, and I'll probably order from here when I'm craving something strange.  The reason I'll order, though, and not go back to the physical location, is that when I went, the store was filled with smoke for the hour I was there.  Enough smoke that I could barely see what I was eating.  So, hopefully their ventilation has been fixed since February.  

Extra star for offering natural sodas and having friendly counter people who are willing to work in a smoke-filled environment (and crazy hours!).

Edit: I DID go back, and it was not as smoky as last time, still awesome pizza.

Just had the loveliest experience here with Dom and Jess. I had ordered a coffee and quickly realized I had lost my Capital One credit card. Jess gave me a free coffee and Dom graciously walked me through the process of locking my card and requesting a replacement. The two of them were so kind and put me immediately at ease. Thank you for the great service!

So so good!!! I can't recommend this place enough. Their fraises açaí bowl is my absolute favorite--yummy dragon fruit yogurt, strawberries, and jam on top--so good!! I've never had anything like it and I can't wait to go back and have more!

Every time I come to Galleria I make sure I get my box at Taco Bell. Today very disappointed. My Grande burrito. Was Extra small burrito it was tortilla and nothing else inside. I asked for the manager and they didn't have one. A girl from the kitchen came out to assist me but she was very rude. Instead of offering me a new one like I told her she offered me the money back. She said that's how the burrito is but it's not true I had here before and it's always fine. Maybe I have to come weekends when the manager is in and the food is perfect. Anyways here's another picture of my girlfriend food. You can tell it's only lettuce.

My wife and I have been reading in Capital One Cafe, 2 or 3 times per month for the last year.  The staff are exceedingly friendly and often give away free items.  The coffee is usually fresh and the price is unbeatable if you have a Capital One Credit Card (50% off all drinks).  Space is well constructed for studying with ample electrical outlets and spaces with no crocked furniture. I only wish every bank could convert to this operating model.

4 stars because the original location south Boston was incredible 5 stars but the Cambridge location was a 2. Out of several food items like rice and kale even. Terrible service. The original location was incredible in every way however.

If get food out for lunch it has to be healthy, that's my only compromise with myself if I'm going to spend the money. I recently started a new job in the area and i have to admit... I saw them online and thought "ehh no thanks" (I'm not vegan, sorry..) and last week a coworker recommended it and again I thought "ehh.. no thanks" -- then today ANOTHER coworker recommended it and my go-to place wasn't accepting orders to go so I thought you know what, I'll give it a shot.. given the reviews I'm like clearly it's good. 

WELL I HATE MYSELF FOR NOT GOING SOONER. It is literally so good, fresh, and healthy! I did a build my own bowl just because it was my first time and I was unsure what to buy, but with the newfound confidence I have in this place combined with peer recommendations I can't wait to branch out for lunch here again soon (aka.. tomorrow). I'm not even kidding it is wholeheartedly making me think about what I eat in terms of vegan vs not.. shoutout to the friendly staff member taking my order who recommend a dressing/sauce and put up with my pausing as I decided what to add. She was so patient and even made sure to ask if I wanted some other things when I forgot to mention them. This vegan food is delicious and this food proves all vegan cuisine can be finish your plate good.


Dear whole heart provisions, I am so sorry for doubting you.. sincerely, a believer.

P.s. Also props to them. When you pay extra for avocado they REALLY make it worth your while.

The craving for another one of these amazing bagels o love is still haunting me. A friend brought me here during my visit and without a doubt I'm dying, starving, craving another hint of that yummy bagel aroma. The shop was simple and sweet. And the ordering process runs like a well oiled machine
1. Wait in line
2. Happy dance when you get inside the doors
3. Don't slack, check out the order board and get your order set.
4. Front of the line, quick happy dance
5. Order, pay and drool
6. Wait for your name to be called and stay inside to savor the sights and smells
7. Pick up your order and dig in, don't wait, rip the paper away with your teeth and enjoy every warm bagel mouthful.

My order was the pretzel bagel with salmon; dill, onion, and avocado. Beyond amazing! I even grabed a half dozen to bring home with me, and sadly devoured them in a couple of days. Great place, great food, great service, little bit of a wait in line but all worth it! Bagelsaurus needs to open up shop in Texas now!!!

I've been here so many times since this place has opened! Amazing that everything is vegetarian! They have bowls with specific ingredients, or you can build your own. In general I find that their curries and sauces are pretty mild, nothing super spicy. They also put a crunchy topping on each of their signature bowls, which in general I am not a huge fan of, but you can ask for certain ingredients to be removed/added. 

They have a water fountain that can be used to fill up a reusable water bottle, which is really convenient. There is plenty of seating inside as well as outside.

Food is super great here, and everyone is very friendly. Love the Loukoumades so much and Jeffrey was super helpful, always ready with water and napkins and recommendations! Definitely one of my fav places in Harvard Square!!
Haven't eaten here...yet. Had a great night hanging out. Superb atmosphere. Cool staff. Cold drinks. Can't wait to go back!

Glass House is a great after-work spot. Their cocktails are consistently strong and delicious, and I've had quite a few food items here that have blown me away, like the Lamb Flatbread. Last time I went, the bar staff was not particularly helpful-- I had to wait a long time just to make eye contact to get some menus, but the vibe is good and the menu is delicious. Definitely my go-to for drinks in Kendall Square.

I've been coming here regularly because of the new breakfast menu that includes the delicious whole heart-ichoke toast (I love the name as well as the creamy delicious and crunchy flavor). The past two times I've come here they've been out of the whole heartichoke toast-- good for them, not great for me. I'm hoping they can meet the demand a bit better in the future as regularly being out of my favorite item by noon when it's supposed to be served til 2:30 could be reason enough for me not to return. I've been getting the avo smash instead: also delicious but not quite as mind blowing. Yesterday I had the Cocobrew smoothie (made with less banana specifically for me and my non-banana-friendly taste budsand ended up giving it to my friend as I was not interested in taking more than a couple sips.

The service I received today at the register was excellent-- the cashier was personable, friendly, enthusiastic about their new items, and made me feel welcome and more likely to come back. I will say that I usually receive similar, although usually more muted, service at WHP. I can only really recommend this place for their breakfast toasts and their friendly service as I haven't tried enough of their other items (don't sound appetizing or filling enough for me to fork over the big bucks).

Frequent spot for cocktails and apps after work. Ask for Jose at the bar! Never disappointed. Lychee or watermelon martinis ! Nice fire pit in the summer with lounge chairs. Good place for folks to congregate after work. Can get packed fast ! Nice expansive charcuterie board that's very meat heavy but delish ! Naan sandwich is good. Burger is solid - and fries has a good amount of crunch and petite! Hummus is also nice to try. Overall pretty consistent service and nice selection for meat eaters and veg options.

We had sangria and lots of different tapas. Everything was delicious. I wish that I didn't get so full because there were many more that I would like to try.  The service and atmosphere were excellent. I love Kika Tapas.
Classic Jumbo on an Everything Bagel should be an exhibit in the MFA it's that good! Best to go during the weekdays because weekends can have long lines down the street.

We came here on a Monday morning and the service was pretty quick.

We got the Classic jumbo on a pretzel bun and added roasted tomatoe and ham. We also got avocado and smoked salmon on a pretzel bun.

The classic jumbo with the added tomatoe and ham was out of this world. Tomatoe is a must to add.

Smoked salmon was ok, I was expecting lox and this is more of a cooked, smoked salmon.

These two bagels were a bit pricey at a $21 total.

Would come again to try some of he other menu options. Overall 4/5 Stars!

Love the concept of featuring globally inspired vegan veggie and grain bowls. I appreciated that each bowl was loaded with a wide array of ingredients. However, flavor was disappointing. We got te levant and the seoul. Both had a lot of good ingredients and generous portions. But the sauces they use are heavy and very creamy. There isn't enough zestiness or acidity to allow all those good ingredients to shine. We also got the shishito peppers and street corn. They were okay. Too much creaminess on the corn, not enough lime.

They deliver, and on top of that the delivery is relatively quick and cheap (it's cheaper on Foodler than most of the food options there).  On top of that, their food is decent for what it is, which is standard take-out fare.  The steak tips are tolerable (a little too chewy, and not great, but tolerable), but the other food seems to be decent enough.

This place is straight up delicious. Their hours and preorder schedule can get a little confusing but I'm writing that off to covid and just being grateful that this place is staying open and making it work. Truly amazing bagels (flavors are so well-executed, perfect chewy/crispy ratio) and super tasty shmears. Easily the best bagel in Cambridge.

Best/worst time to go: Saturday morning. When you're regretting all the toxins you had to intake last night or feeling refreshed after a morning run because you took the high road and stayed in on a Friday night...or just genuinely enjoy people watching and get a kick out of people's hungover misery. What to order: whatever your heart desires, it's a bagel shop. But I'd like to note that this isn't just any typical bagel. It's one of those bagels that's perfectly warm and toasted on the outside, with a flaky crust that's to die for..and a semi fluffy interior that you don't get sick of after 5 minutes of chewing stale bodega bagels (sorry Brooklyn). And better yet, slab on some of that homemade cream cheese and toss on some lettuce & tomato and call it a day. 

-1 star for the 45 minute wait...

Oh and did I mention that there's a stellar sock shop right next door? Picked up some matching corgi socks for myself and mi pa. Winning.

Saloniki is like the Chipotle of Greek food.  You choose a basic dish from the menu...plates, salads, kabobs, etc.   They then add feta, veggies, and other toppings at your request.   Hubby and I both chose salads.   They were large, fresh, and satisfying for a reasonable price.  Negatives for me were that my chicken was somewhat dry and there was a strange large-ish piece of tendon or something in there, about the size of the tine end of my fork.  Hubby took this back to the counter before we left to show an employee, but she either didn't understand or didn't really care, as there wasn't much response.  There are no gyros or lamb roasting on a spit here, but still plenty of Greek flavors available to satisfy your Greek fast food cravings

I came here hoping it would be similar to Life Alive but I ended up so disappointed. I ordered the Cassie per recommendation of the cashier. I also added the bbq tofu to it. It seemed like all of the vegetables were cooked in a different kind of flavor which did not go together. The dish was also drowning in a creamy, overly sweet sauce. I could only eat a few bites.
Sarah's market isn't bad. I've had there food before and it was not to shabby. But it was pretty delicious but I threw everything up.

I was pleasantly surprised by how much I enjoyed the food here -- I went in with low expectations. I had the Viet (8.85$+tax), and tried a few bites of the Cassie. The size of the bowl was basically what you'd expect from a rice bowl. The sauces were both on the heavy side, but it balanced well with the amount of vegetables in the dishes. The pilaf was filling enough, but nothing special. I think the particular toppings were hit or miss -- the roasted veggies were a bit bleh, but the green beans were amazing, as were the crispy chickpeas. I wish you could see the veggies to pick your own toppings though, as the way they look would give hints about how good they are; just reading them off the menu isn't really sufficient. 

This is a good instantiation of a "fast casual" rice bowl type restaurant. Worth the visit if you want a fast, filling lunch for a decent price.
The food is excellent, and the staff is patient and friendly . I appreciate the way the guided us through our first visit.

Like most places these days, this place is loud and it's difficult to have a conversation with your table guests. 
Service here is what this review is about. This place was far from being overly busy and the wait time to be greeted by a server was extraordinarily long. We typically like to have a cocktail before dinner but that opportunity was lost by the time we were finally approached.
We ordered our appertisers and entree's at the same time for at this point we were hungry and ready to eat. Our appertisers arrived, we took a few bites and then our entrees arrived before we even had a chance to get half way through our appertisers. This type of situation is telling about an operation that has no real sense of a dining experience. Just another restaurant, capitalizing on a booming real estate location!
All in all, food was bland as well as the decor. There are other great choices in this neighborhood.
This was a great spot to grab lunch! Very flavorful bowls! As a guilt ridden omnivore, it was great to find a vegan spot that was so enjoyable.

YO THIS IS MY BEST KEPT SECRET BUT I'LL LET YOU IN ON IT. 50% off handcrafted drinks if you use a Capital One credit or debit card. Where else can I get a almond milk matcha latte for under $3??? And on certain days (marked "Happy Hour" on the blackboard inside) once or twice a week it's FREE. Also, the space is nice and there are always seats. Love love love this place.

This is hands down, my favorite restaurant in Cambridge. 

I've been here so many times.. the bruchetta is AMAZING. Ricotta cheese - in the summer, with heirloom tomatoes, in the winter, with butternut squash (not as good as the tomato one!). The pizza is great... and the service has always been good for how busy this place gets (if you come on a Fri/Sat in the spring/summer/fall, you'll be waiting at least 30 minutes). There was one time where my brother-in-law ordered a pizza, and they brought all our food out but his. They explained how they had dropped the pizza in the back, and had to remake a new one. Understandable, as they were very polite about the situation. We see the waitress start to bring out his new, re-made pizza, and as she's walking out, she drops it. She looks up and it's obvious she's utterly embarrassed about the whole thing; meanwhile, we are just laughing our asses off. She comes over and states she doesn't really know what just happened and that they will obviously comp the pizza. It was pretty funny, and they were so nice about it, how could you not laugh? I've gotten multiple pasta dishes (I like the red sauce one the best), and they're all great. Last summer they had a mushroom barley soup... MMMM

The food is reasonably priced, the wine is decent, and the after dinner coffee is delicious! Highly recommended.

Not as good as it used to be (food quality).  And, generally, the guys behind the counter are OK, but there is one who is gratuitously unpleasant and snotty.  No need for that.  It was convenient to stop by there today, but for the diminished quality, increased prices and unnesessary attitude, forget it - it's not worth it.

I have a Capital One account and usually stop by for the ATM, but I've found that the Peet's Coffee inside is a convenient stop -- especially since account holders get 50% off drinks with their debit cards. It's a nice perk and a clever incentive.

I like that there is ample seating and fast WiFi, plus a couple private rooms that you can reserve. The atmosphere is surprisingly comfortable and not too institutional-feeling, given it's also a bank. I always see people working here. The people at the cafe are nice and service is quick. 

Occasionally the pacing security guards can get distracting, but otherwise it's a decent place to sit and work in the ever-crowded Harvard Square.

This is a great, overlooked cafe in Harvard Square. It serves Peet's coffee and, perhaps contrary to popular belief, there is a decent amount of seating. When you first walk in, there are only stools, but there is an adjacent room that offers lots of tables/chairs and even meeting rooms. Don't overlook this - i mistakenly assumed it was the bank, but it's open for the cafe.

The service was friendly and efficient. I visited during rush hour, but I received my drink within 5 minutes of entering the store. There was also plenty of available seating space - more than the standalone Peet's across the street. If you like Peet's coffee, you should check it out - don't be scared away by the Capital One logo.

Third I have came here. I always get the Niko. It is absolutely amazing and delicious. I love the chicken, and fries and the Nutella balls. Their sauce is also amazing. I will definitely keep coming here. LOVE IT LOVE IT LOVE IT!

A great setting, adequate food -- but ruined by the vile staff. Why, I wonder, do they hate their lives so much. And why do they have to take it out on the customers. It's a wonder this place can stay in business. I often wonder when I read reviews that mention unpleasant staff, whether the reviewer isn't just exaggerating a misunderstanding, or generalizing from one rare occurrence. But I have to agree this time with the many other reviewers who report nasty, surly (and barely competent) staff. Things are consistently so bad here that it's not worth bothering most days.

If you like the masochistic challenge of confronting unfriendly people, then make Hi-Rise your destination. Otherwise, stay away.
Horrible service, don't know what we did to deserve such terrible an attitude. Food was mediocre and definitely do not worth the bad experience. Will not go back again.

Nope, not a place I'd go to again. I went here because there were pretty good reviews on Yelp, and the description of where this place was located sounded great. We got there at about 7 on Sat. night. The place was dead inside. That should have been our clue to leave. I should have looked closer at the menu. It has upscale fancy dishes and few choices to pick from. I told my friends that is probably a good thing because that means the chef is probably well trained at cooking these few dishes. I settled on the steak frites. It was delicious, tender & cooked med. My friend ordered the lobster bisque soup. She didn't have any lunch, and at 2 a.m. she woke up very sick to her stomach. Sadly, she attributed it to food poisoning and I told her she should report it to the health department. I would just advise everyone to read the menu before you go here. There wasn't anything else on there that I would have eaten besides the steak and fries.

Capital one cafe should obviously be the Capital ONE Cafe, since ONE is supposed to be in Capitals. Silly.

What a strange and bizarre concept. It's a cafe named after a freaking credit card and the credit card is vital in this case because if you use it here then you get 50% off any drink - now that is a huge freaking discount. That's like better than an employee discount bro. It's like you have to work a full shift instead of a half shift just to get that as an employee bro.

And surprisingly some of the drinks are actually pretty good - for example I really like the black coffee with the condensed milk - so if you have a Capital One Card, do a Selena and Come and Get It - dirt cheap drinks and if no CC Card, it'd be overpriced but still enjoyabe at least.

Now let's allow OMC to finish the review, since Allison suggested it.

We've got kittens in the back
Sweet Barbie's in the front
Cruisin' down the Mass-Pike in the hot, hot sun
Suddenly red-blue lights flash us from behind
Loud voice booming
Please step out onto the line
Kittens purr mews of comfort
Barbie hides her shoes away
Policeman taps his shades
Did you review Capital One Cafe?

How Bizarre.
So fresh and good. The falafel dog = the most delicious and unhelpful item on the menu. Good make your own bowls options. Not redundant or too similar to sweetgreen

I've been to whole heart provisions a couple of times and every time I go I fall more in love.
 
Eating whole heart really makes me feel good inside. The food here is amazingly good. There is so many flavors in one plate and so many textures that you're in awe. I recommend this place. GO TRY IT NOW.

Ain't nothin' special about these bagels. 
Nothin' at all.  And the people- both customers and staff are snooty.

If you're considering stopping by Whole Heart Provisions, just do it. And when you go, order the Cassie Bowl (8.85) with miso bbq tofu (+2) because it's the best and none of the other ones (while still good) have compared. 

The Cassie Bowl comes with japanese eggplant (so freaking tasty, and this is coming from a once eggplant hater), chickpeas, pickled cauliflower (the perfect crunch and sour contrast), savory green beans, basil (yes yes yes), currants (I'm all about the hint of sweetness), crispy chickpeas, and creamy harissa sauce. The warm bbq tofu adds protein and extra sauciness to the already delicious bowl. 

I've had this combination at least 10 times since January, and I'm still constantly craving it. Do yourself a favor and check out Whole Heart Provisions.

On a side note, I've tried several other bowls including the Mission, the Viet, and Miso, the Levant, and the Kaya (okay, so almost every other option), and none were quite as delicious as the cassie. That said, the falafel is really good, and sometimes if I'm feeling wild I'll get The Cassie with falafel instead of bbq miso tofu.

The asshole on the phone called me a ni**er because I complained about my service.  Fuck this place, it's a disorganized shithole.  People that work there are assholes.  Someone keeps hanging up, they have zero customer service.  How is this place open still?  Racist, terrible service, and unorganized why ever order from here?
This place is awesome. Great staff, specifically Victoria and Katie. Rodney is awesome too!

Omg! You have to order Chicken Mozzarella Panini. It's delicious ever!!! The workers may not speak English well but they are nice and get your food done pretty fast. Every time I am hungry and need a quick food, I go Sarah's.

I can't speak of the bagels, due to the failed attempts of actually acquiring one, but I can speak of the business practice, and it's simple:
You do 2 things; you make bagels, you sell bagels. You should not be running out of bagels and closing an hour and a half early. You make bagels, you sell bagels. Make enough bagels to last through your posted hours of operation.

Everything everyone has said here is true.  But what I will add is that this ABP should not, I repeat, should not, have pastries out which can be handled by the general public.  I have routinely seen people approach pastries with their bare (dirty) hands, refusing to use those flimsy pieces of wax paper which are supposed to protect us from each other's germs.  Perhaps given the high foot traffic of this lousy ABP, pastries should be handled by staff only.

Dear Atomic Bean Cafe,

You are a *cafe* not a cafeteria!

I really wanted to like this place but there are a few major drawbacks:

- first, the environment is institutional... they have procured the world's most uncomfortable chairs  and tables, the place has no decor whatsoever, and they play terrible music. There's nothing to make you want to linger over a latte at this place.  People at ABC, please, please get some decent lighting, some plants or flowers, some better chairs, something...! Having two board games lying around does NOT count as decor.

-second, the pastry selection is horrendous.  Really, really bad. They have brownies, croissants, and some muffins sitting on cafeteria trays in a display case and then they have an entire row of bagels in store-bought packaging.  The only food they serve includes hummus plate, fruit plate, and cheese plate (none of which are particularly exciting).  

-third, they don't add the special touch to anything-- no cute mugs (think Dado Tea), no special teas (think Andala).

I want this place to thrive- it's a good location with amazing beautiful daylight streaming in.  Get some good cookies or scones or something, get some atmosphere going, and I promise I'll be back.

Was lucky enough to come here during their "soft opening" for friends and family and was so pumped by the warm vibe, good food, and the great service (shout to my girl, Fong!).  The place is really nice and located in the heart of Kendall where all the new spots are popping up.  The best things on the menu: the kale salad was really good, also liked the ghnocci with the lamb a lot, and thought the drinks (Palomas!) were great. A fun place to have good food and start the night off post-work before going out to some of the more intense cocktail bars in the area like Art Science. I am a huge fan and loved Marcy the fun hostess/wife of the general manager who goes around and talks to everyone... Yay for new addition to Cambridge!

-Tamara

my boyfriend and I were craving taco bell and this definitely hit the spot! they even made our nacho fries extra crispy. this time my favorite was the baja blast & the doritos locos taco. thank you taco bell

Being this close to work is dangerous. The food is phenomenal, although the actual dining space inside is a little tight. The cheese borak is by far my favorite, and you can't go wrong with any pastry from the case. The dips are amazing as well, my wife and I bought their cookbook to recreate some of them as well! 100% worth stopping by if you are in the area.

If you're tired of the same old curry, then you have to try out this place. They have food that hails from all parts of India unlike most Indian restaurants, so you'll likely be trying something new. It may be different or unusual at first to you, but it's definitely good.

Tanjore's has terrific food that handles spices deftly. The bread offerings are noteworthy. The place is warmly appointed and it doesn't feel cramped like Tamarind Bay (which also has great food). And the the service is warm and attentative (which I find a shortcoming at Tarmarind Bay). However, the daily buffet is smaller here than the other Indian places in the area. But the buffet food is at the right temperature (unlike Cafe of India which runs cool). Bombay Club has a larger weekly buffet but I find the food the least imaginative of the bunch. If I have out of town guests to entertain, Tanjore has never failed to please.

Seems like a fun place to live.  It's safe, has lots of little restaurants and hang outs, and is filled with college students.  Tons of stuff to do and several of the best places in Boston all within a few blocks.  On nice evenings in the summer there are tons of street performers doing everything from singing, henna, spray painting, fire juggling, etc.  There's also a few during the days, but not as many.

Note: my stars are based on category...so, this is 4* for cheap delivery places, but obviously I think something like Posto is way better pizza, it's just way pricier and they don't deliver.

That said, this may be my new favorite cheap delivery thin crust place.

I was feeling super lazy, so I ordered via Foodler for delivery on a Saturday night.  Pizza arrived in about 40min, which was actually faster than advertised, and pretty decent for a Saturday night.  Bonus: it was steaming when removed from the delivery bag, nice!  Waiting, I don't mind, but waiting and getting cold food, I do.

Toppings: pepperoni was top notch.  I find a lot of bland pepperoni around these parts, but Marco's pep is not!  yay!  Onions were sliced small, which I like, because you don't end up dragging half of them off the pizza with each bite, and were plentiful and not half raw.  My only fault was the broccoli, which appeared to be of the frozen variety.  Oh well, it didn't bother me too much.  Overall, I found the cheese, sauce, and toppings to be above average in terms of flavor, but I may order extra sauce next time.

The crust was a touch soggy in the middle, which can't really be avoided with delivery, but overall had a nice crispiness and was chewy without being too tough.  Definitely one of the better ones I've had around these parts.

Salad: not really worth $5 for the size compared to other pizza joints, but I appreciated the addition of sliced red peppers, and the Greek dressing was pretty good.

There was a $1 delivery fee, but I felt their prices were a bit lower than other comparable places that don't have a fee, so I don't mind paying it, plus the large pizza is ENORMOUS.

Overall a good experience, and although I'm moving in about 4 months, I'll likely order from Marco's again before leaving, especially during those last weeks when we're all out of food and don't want to stock the fridge :-).

i dream about the bacon cheeseburger. cut up pieces of bacon, giving the burger a perfect bite every time. 
i'm also a sucker for poutine and whether its fries or tater tots, you can't go wrong. get ready for some gravy/cheese curd heaven. 
need more of this in my life. 

staff was very friendly and accommodating.

Although the place is cash only, the prices are reasonable and the pastries are incredible. We had the Boston Cream Pie, Caramel Pecan Cannoli, Cheesecake, Red Velvet Cupcake, and few other items. Very yummy!!

I live next door to Marco's, and I think it's a great pizza place for the neighborhood. Sure, there are other places to go if you want atmosphere or gourmet food. But their pizza is yummy. I prefer the thin crust NY style pizza, so it's my favorite. My husband's more of a thick crust guy (he's from the Midwest) so he's less of a fan. I get the #4 Americano, which is good.

My in-laws like to order their pasta when they visit. They have enjoyed the gnocci with red sauce and the fettucini alfredo. Not for those counting calories, but tasty.

I'd get delivery or take-out. The inside looks like a Subway chain restaurant w/ the bucket benches.

They're always friendly when I go.

My first time at Mikes Pastry and I really wanted to go the original location in Little Italy but saw this one while we were visiting Harvard Square so decided to check it out.   

Actual Cannoli was good.  I had the hazelnut, lots of flavour nice the crunchy shell and sweet cream filling.  

The two girls that was working, wow talk about needed to learn how to smile or just have better costumer service, talk about attitude.  Ive never been to this bakery and really wanted to see what my options were.  The one girl just looked at me like you still don't know what you're having!??  And no one else was behind me or waiting to order.  Mikes have about 20 cannoli flavours and also other pastries.  I knew I wanted to try their cannoli just didn't know which one.   Maybe these girls had such attitude because they alway have to serve Harvard students all time who most likely will have a better future than them.

Not the best Portuguese I've had, but the best Portuguese I've had in Boston.

The best Portuguese I've had was in Portugal. Lisbon's a great place. The stuff at Casa Portugual is more Azores-type Portuguese. A little oilier, but still flavorful.

The littlenecks make a nice appetizer, as does the chourico (which came out flambe). The arroz e valencia was fantastic--squid and clams and fish cooked to perfection. The first time I went was with my Portuguese friend and my dad. Had the chocolate mousse that time. My friend said it was great but not as great as the mousse his mom made (on a visit two years later I was able to find out that he was right, but this is still really great). The second time I went with him and HIS dad. Had the littlenecks again and the chourico again, but had the bifa e casa (I think that's the name--grilled tuna), which was some of the best I'd had. Washed it all down both times with Sagres (which is one of the only good national macrobrews there is) and capped it off with an amarghuina. Excellent time both nights.

Simple atmosphere (which is a bit better after the recent renovation, but still not spectacular), good food and decent prices.  Its a nice alternative to Legal's when you're looking for a good seafood dinner in the Harvard Sq area.

Spice is truly some very good Thai food at reasonable prices.  The shrimp and vegetable tempura was lightly battered, delicious, and surprisingly big for the price.  The Siam duck was spicy, but in a good way.  Everyone at our table was happy with the food and the service.  With so many restaurants pricing themselves above the casual diners range, it's good to know that Harvard Square still has a good Thai option.

Marco's brings North-end quality pizza to North Cambridge. Big, cheap, slices at lunchtime, friendly staff, and delivery all day. This is the kind of pizza that's good enough to eat without any toppings.

What's with all this hate on Sebastian's?

This was one of the first places I tried when I came to MIT, based on the recommendation of a lot of friends.  Almost every time, I've left full and satisfied.  Where shall I begin?

[Note: I named my cousin Sebastian.  Then he changed his name to Kenya.  He's an Asian boy living in Hong Kong.  Does not compute.]

I like Sebastian's for three things: the sandwiches, the crepes, and the pasta bowls.  You can make your own sandwich, but I recommend getting one of the "Sebastian's Specialties," as they're cheaper and really good.  The only one I don't recommend is the San Italiano -- it's got a lot of olives and peppers in the spread, two things which I could care less for.  My favourite is the Rustico because I heart artichokes -- the Mediterraneo is good if you're a vegetarian and it is HUGE, and the SBLT has a really great brioche/bacon/spread combination.

The pasta bowls are pretty awesome too, if a little more expensive.  There's a good amount of pasta, mixed in with whatever vegetables you prefer.  Meat and artichokes are extra and usually ~$2 -- that's the real drawback.  There are 6 types of sauces and 6 types of pasta to choose between.  Yeah, it's expensive, but it's also delicious and very filling.  All of the sauces are delish.

Finally, the crepes are really filling too.  It doesn't look like much -- it's like the size of a pizza slice -- but you'd be surprised at how much they can pile in there.  There are two types: sweet (all-day) and savoury (lunchtime).  I don't particularly like the ratatouille crepe, because there are some hard seeds in there! -- but all of the other ones are good.  I haven't had the sweet ones in a while, but as I recall, they're less sweet than traditional European crepes.

A lot of people -- unlike this meatatarian -- like the salads.  They are GINORmous and really popular lol.  I don't recommend the Asian noodle bowls -- the noodles have a funny texture to them, the vegetables aren't inspired, and the sauces, in addition to not being very Asian to begin with, are somewhat watered down.

There's also a good selection of Ritter chocolate bars, yogurt and juice, premade (cold) sandwiches, and a nice bakery section, of which only leftovers remain by ~12 o'clock.  I really love Sebastian's, can you tell?

If you're going past 4 PM, everything's cheaper -- salads, sandwiches, and pasta bowls are only $5.  Most crepes are $5; the ones with meat are $6, which makes no sense to me -- there's so much more meat in the sandwiches anyways.  It's a really good deal, especially for the pasta bowls, which are usually over $7.  Again, meat is extra, but you'd never get anything that cheap from a decent Italian place anyways.

One last thing.  I brought my French professor and her son here when he was visiting over spring break.  They both loved the crepes.  And crepes are from ... that's right. =]  The woman behind the crepe station may be dour, but she sure can twirl a good crepe.  

I also brought my friends visiting from Calgary here.  They really wanted to try lobster, but on a student budget, we had to settle for Sebastian's.  They left Boston that afternoon very satisfied. =]

Go to Sebastian's, you won't regret it!

We went for a meal at 9.30pm (it closes at 10.30pm) and the waiter stood over us towards the end of our meal, and tried to take our food away before we'd finished. He then asked us to leave as he wanted to go home! It was still only just 10.30pm. 

The food was fine, but didn't taste very authentic at all (I've eaten a LOT of Indian food in my day, and travelled in India). The tikka masala just tasted like a tomato sauce - there didn't seem to be any Indian spices at all. For the price, I think this place was not worth it. 

Punjabi Dhaba in Inman is a lot better, and more authentic.

I went last Saturday and was very pleased!  What a gem.  First off, we called while on our way and spoke to the hostess who was helpful on suggesting where to find parking and also said she would put our name on the list.  How nice and helpful.  When we arrived we were greeted warmly and the place had a really cosy yet sophisticated vibe, with great music playing (at just the right volume, not too loud like many restaurants).  Our drinks were fab.  Loved the spicy margarita, but it does have some heat!  We were all really pleased with our meals, and the prices were extremely reasonable for what we got.  So many things sounded good, it was hard to decide.  I got the tuna appetizer which was really good and a decent size portion.  We tried the burger, chicken and salmon for our entrees and we were all very pleased.  One of our burgers came out cooked more rare than we wanted, and they took it back, cooked it right and didn't charge us for it.  It was really nice of them and the way more restaurants should handle things like that, but many don't.  The burger, when I got it was really outstanding.  We also tried the bread pudding which we loved, but found the cheesecake to be a little strong in the espresso flavor.  It took a little while to get our check, we had to wave the waitress down, and the drinks were a little slow to come after ordered, but we were not in a rush and so didn't mind much. 
Overall, we loved this place and will definitely be back.  Great food, great prices, great drinks and good staff.

Wow - whenever I come here, I almost feel bad that I'm making them make my sandwich.

They are the most unmotivated, inactive bunch of Subway employees I have ever encountered.  They can't muster up the energy to really talk to you - they mumble a lot.  They kind of just go with their own flow and the manager must not keep a close watch on inventory or customer satisfaction or anything because they ALWAYS run out of stuff.  Like bread.  Really?  We're not asking for much from a sandwich spot.  Just BREAD.

I don't understand where they get their employees from or why they don't seem to train them in any decent fashion in customer satisfaction.  

But, avoid if you can - I know the Subway brand is tempting, but be prepared to deal with unenthusiastic staff.

Went to Trata Saturday evening for my birthday and they were so accommodating first I called and made a reservation for 8 and they tried their hardest to make it work then when I called back an hour before  to change the guest number I was afraid they wouldnt be able to make it work or be annoyed, but the girl on the phone was so pleasant! I've worked in restaurants and I know how frustrating it can be when things like that change but she was great! It was my first time there and I totally dug the atmosphere much smaller than I imagined but it was still nice. We had a table right by the window perfect for people watching in Harvard square! Our waitress was sweet and got everything right ( even understood my boyfriend and half the table who have a thick irish accent and didnt get frustrated with us) The food was excellent and the perfect portions , I got the steak salad which was amazing and I totally recommend ordering the apple pie which took a little while but was definitely worth the wait. Cheap drinks, good vibe cute place I cant wait to go back. The only reason I didnt give it 4 stars was because of the beer tap selection. Get some Harp or Guinness! :) Thanks Trata for a great birthday experience!

So this is what Papa's became.  I am amazed that with Pinnochio's, Crazy Dough's, Cambridge 1, Oggi, and the other pizza joints spitting distance from this place, that another pizza place is warranted.

then again, it's right in Harvard Sq, so I guess there is a demand.

Ordered only a slice - and it was a weird looking one.  Pepperoni was very old school - like the size of a small drink coaster.  Cheese sprinkled on and melted.  And then a thin crust

Beware Barney B - thin crusts in this town disappoint.

But for $2.50, I won't feel guilty if I toss it if it lives up to normal standards.

The slice didn't disappoint.  It was greasy, but not too.  It was crispy, but not too.  It was tasty, but not too (so I deduct a single star).  But overall I am happy I tried it - and I welcome it to the neighborhood.

And like any good pizza shop - open until 2:30 on the weekends.  Not sure the last time I made it out that late, but it's nice to know it's there

I wish them luck

I've now been here for brunch and for drinks.  The brunch was very unimpressive (especially compared to Temple Bar brunch next door).  The coffee arrived cold, the orange juice was not-so-discretely poured directly from a minute maid carton, and the food was nothing to rave about.  

My experience with drinks was similar...my friend's diet coke arrived completely flat (actually undrinkable), and my beer took over 10 minutes to get.  Also, the decor in my opinion is weird...I'm not really sure what look they are going for but it is dark and has tacky ceiling tiles.

The food here is very good.  When school is in, many Harvard kids come here.  It's very nice inside, but still casual.  The chicken wing appetizer is tasty, as is the chicken pik-pow.  And definately try one of the ice creams, such as green tea or ginger.

I work nearby and used to pass by this place with coworkers on our way to lunch. It took me almost a year before I actually went in, but having done so has changed my work lunch experience forever. I go nearly every day now, and often twice a day (when I skip breakfast or need something extra in my stomach to help at the gym). I can't give an accurate review of the standard menu (other than the shawarma, which is the best I've had in Boston--go for the wrap or the plate with extra tahini), but the specials are unbeatable! Mon-Fri I'm treated to curries and stews from around the world. It's like you're getting a home cooked meal from Indian/African/Mediteranean step-parents every day for lunch. How can you beat that!? I'll tell you how: Tuesdays and Wednesdays is yassa, a delicious, traditional Senegalese chicken dish, made for you by the Senegalese owner and chef. The yassa is sweet and savory, with just a small, small kick of spice (which the owner can make spicy, if you prefer, with his handy bottle of custom made hot sauce at the ready!)

Everyone, do yourselves a favor and get the most inexpensive, delicious homecooked meal money can buy in this area!

I recently discovered Trata in the square. I'm always hesitant  with new restaurants but , thought I'd give it a try. We were a group of eight and all agreed that our experience there was excellent. Even though they seemed fairly busy the wait staff accommodated us quickly and were very polite. The menu was well rounded ,  I enjoyed a special seafood pasta dish. The vegetarians in our group were happy to see items like veggie lasagna and several veggie pizzas on the menu. All in all our experience was good, thanks to attentive service and delicious food, we wish Trata the best of luck and will surely be visiting again soon.

After laboring allll day long to finish my pset, I was excited to leave my windowless office in search of a hefty, healthy $5 footlong to satisfy my insatiable bottomless pit of a stomach.  I checked online, they close at 11pm, got there at 10:15.

"Sorry, we're closed. No bread! Want pizza instead?"

1 star for not having bread (You guys are Subway...a store that specializes in food that features bread?)  (Also, when did Subway start selling pizza?)

I'll be nice and give 2 stars because I DO like their subs when they do have bread.  Subway club with everything on it, extra pepperocini and jalapenos and spicy mustard.  YUM
Great cannolis - best I've ever had. Not 5 stars because way to sweet for my liking, but definately a great once a month treat for those on a tight meal plan.

the food here is tasty but pretty heavy. we started with the caldo verde- kale soup with pieces of linguica in it- and followed up with two entrees- bacalau with boiled potatoes and green peppers and also fried steak with mushrooms and fresh potato chips. both were good but greasy. there was a layer of oil lining each of the dishes. for dessert we had the pasteis de nata, which wasn't too different from what we had in portugal, except they sprinkled cocoa powder on it. oh, and we also ordered a bottle of wine, which wasn't spectacular. all in all, fun night, but we'll probably try portugalia next time.

I went to Tanjore with a group of friends for dinner and I found the food to be okay--passable if you're craving Indian food but don't have the time, patience or skill to cook some at home.

I ordered the paneer stuffed naan (paneer being a type of cottage cheese), Chicken Tikka Masala (note that is just written as Chicken Masala on the menu) and chai for dessert (drinking tea at the end of an Indian meal is highly recommended, not to mention authentic!).

The naan was soft and tasty but I wish it was stuffed with a more generous amount of paneer. The Chicken Masala was mediocre--I could tell that they had just stirred some pre-cooked chicken with a pre-made sauce because the flavors didn't infuse into the chicken. The chai was nice and definitely reminded me of the tea we have at home. Bear in mind that by chai, Tanjore doesn't mean the spicy variety you find at Starbucks etc--it's basically your regular black tea with lots of milk but nicely boiled so it's fully flavorsome.

I wouldn't recommend going here for dinner if you're picky, but I would go back for their lunch buffet, which at around $9 for buffet food, dessert and unlimited chai is quite the deal.

Harvard Square can be easily forgotten, overlooked or under-appreciated by Bostonians, especially folks who have lived here their whole lives. But, like Davis Square or Central or even Kendall and Porter, dense and exciting clusters of stores with a ton of foot traffic and interesting architecture are fairly rare once you leave the coasts.

There are a ton of cookie cutter towns out there, with only a couple of strip malls on a four lane road to entertain them. But we here in the Camberville have a wealth of squares that intrigue us, allow for walkable bar crawls, and let us spend money on all sorts of cool stuff without having to drive 45 minutes to do so.

Love your squares, Boston. Love them and realize how rare they are.

Love it. Nice, friendly, courteous owner and staff. Delicious pizza by the slice (amazing sauce). Meatballs a little dense for my taste but compensated for by the fragrant, fresh sauce. Salads were huge, fresh, tasty, and beautiful. 

Only has six or eight tables inside, so not a huge amount of room. Parking is plentiful, for Cambridge residents, on the streets nearby.

Great Salads, Great Crust, Great Pizza,Great Toppings, and Great Service. We need to come back and try some other toppings. Everything so fresh glad we stumbled across. Keep doing it right and see you soon

Ordered take out through yelp: chicken biryani and curry chicken. Both well done (and mild). Arrived within 40 minutes, which was the expected time. Overall, I liked the food - will be ordering from there again.
Cute little spot in Harvard square for a nice casual meal. The food is okay, but their beer list is what really caught my eye. Lots of interesting options and some nice imports.
kind of greasy and not very fresh tasting, all in all a poor meal. And they didn't honor various special request i put in the take-out order

We came here before seeing Cabaret across the street at Oberon and had some yummy seafood.
The calamari appetizer is a must! Every piece was cooked perfectly. After, we had the "Early Bird Specials" of the mussels and blackened catfish.  Both meals were cooked perfectly.

Pretty good restaurant in Harvard Square. The service is quick and the waiters/waitresses are very friendly. I always get the drunken noodles when I'm here. They're nice and thick and seasoned very well. It is a bit on the oily side so beware if you're trying to find a lighter option. If you're a group of around 3-4 people, this would be a good place.

I live not far from the place and this is my usual delivery place. Amazing pizza, amazing calzones, great greek cuisine items (love their Gyros!), great grilled chicken items. Great selection of special pizzas and pizza toppings. 
If you are in the area and you are hungry for some pizza or greek food - give this place a try. You won't be dissapointed.

I really loved this place. The staff was super friendly, extremely upbeat, attentive, and efficient. The food was amazing too. The dishes and cocktails were creative. The Oyster shooters were interesting and surprisingly good. I'd recommend the ones in Vodka. The waitress was knowledgeable about gluten-free options. Also, the lounge is very comfortable. The booths were cozy (but not small), the lightening was nice, and the music was at the right volume and the selection was perfect. Music was a cool mix of chill, a little R&B, retro and contemporary. I'm looking forward to going back for more Oyster shooters and plantains.

Not as quaint as some of the other squares, but  a nice place to hang out and grab a bite to eat or do some shopping. Harvard square has a little more going on and a lot more chain retail stores and food places if thats what you are looking for. Definitely a fun little place.

October '06-
I am so sad about this, but I'm modifying my review and taking away two whole stars from the dear old West Side.  They weathered the last chef change admirably, but recently Mark moved on, and the meal we had there last week was simply not good.  
The calamari, which has been light and crisp was chewy and tough.  The sauces were bland, as were two out of the three entrees we ordered.  The third was too salty- I wonder if they put all the salt from our meal in there by accident... nah!
I was particularly concerned when the house manager commented that we had ordered his three favorite entrees on the new menu.  Doesn't exactly bode well for the rest of them.
I'll go again, when the new chef has been there for a while and settled in a bit, but he's not without experience, so...
The bar, yes, the bar rocks.  Those stars up there are for them.


P.S.  this is the old review I gave them back when Mark was cooking there (3/06):

We love this place, we love it so much we go there whenever we can.  We used to go so often we called it "west side local", but recently it's been tougher to get babysitters...
The menu, at first glance, looks like just another run of the mill "American Fusion" thing, but do not be fooled.  The chef, who is pretty new, is finding his feet fast.  The food is excellent, always. For a light meal a few of the generous starters will do, or an entree between two. Not to be missed is the airy fried calamari which is battered in a spicy mix and served with a great dip. 
The drinks are also never less than stellar and usually (though not always) the music is actually listen-able.  
Last, but far from least, the wait staff are not to be bettered, anytime, anyplace

I've eaten here more times than I'd like to admit, but I plead having lived right next door.

The clientele usually includes some cops, some children wearing their Sunday dresses on a Wednesday at 11pm, some old dudes playing Keno, and other neighborhood denizens hanging out in the salmon-colored plastic booths.

The chicken parm and meatball subs are pretty good, although I hate to think about how many days worth of calories and fat each one contains. The pizza is amazingly greasy and not worth trying. The crust is disappointing.

Even though I lived next door for a year and now live 2 blocks away, I've never gone into the attached bar. There are some things not worth trying.
Affordable and delicious. A perfect low-key date spot too. I'd suggest getting one of their sugar cane sodas - super tasty.
Delicious, authentic Middle Eastern food! I bring everyone who visits Cambridge here because it is such a special and unique spot, and incredibly affordable for the quality of food. I love Sofra!

I love west side. Consistently good food, they always play CDs that I own (and therefore love), and the atmosphere is chill without being pretentious. Plus, they usually have the good olives (stuffed with blue cheese!)

Senegalese specialties and hearty, reliable American and Mediterranean cuisine, in a convenient spot for a reasonable price, by a caring and responsive family. What's not to like?

Bytes is a tiny but well established hole-in-the-wall eatery, presided over by the friendly and formidable Chef Mamadou Berry. A mom-and-pop shop, the family has produced excellent quality handmade breakfast and lunch for over a decade in this same location. Built into the University Park development, the location is easily accessible on foot for residents and workers in the area, and only a half-block walk from the street for others. (The location faces a private alley but opens onto a pleasant pocket park.)

Mamadou can produce an array of standard takeout American breakfast fare in the morning and tasty American and Mediterranean offerings at lunch. Since its all assembled before your eyes by Mamadou & crew  from the morning's prep work, you can customize as much as you want. (This made me a loyal breakfast customer for many years.) 

The glory of Bytes, though, is Mamadou's Senegalese specials. (Call ahead -- not everything is made every day!) The spicy peanut chicken stew is excellent, as are several other over-rice options. The crowning glory is his Yassa Stew, a delicious mustard-lemon-onion concoction spiced to perfection. I could eat this twice a week for a year and be happy!

Bytes is take out only, but numerous surrounding pocket parks provide the seating that isn't found in the store. Homemade teas are sometimes available, but otherwise canned and bottled waters, sodas, milks, and brewed coffee are your only beverages. You won't care though, as the entree will make your day!

Wow!

Since eating here for breakfast, I have learned how to make shakshuka.  I did not know that it even existed.

They actually cook their amazing food to order, and for Boston, I'd call it inexpensive and an excellent value.
