Great pizza and atmosphere. Today they messed up on our order and our last pizza came much later after we finished eating, after checking many times. We were so upset, but the manager came apologizing and gave us discount and free pizza so that made up for it :)

The pastries here are good but it doesn't live up to the hype. We waited in line for about 20 minutes, ordered, and then couldn't find a place to sit to eat. We go the chocolate cannoli - it has pistachios in it. My friend is allergic and the cannoli ingredients weren't labeled very well.

I stopped by Tanjore in Harvard Square for a Mango Lassi $3.95 ($4.23 after tax) to go.  They poured it for me at the bar right away, but my initial silent reaction was, gee, that's a small cup!

Yes, by the supersize American standards, it was a small cup.  But it tasted fine and the smooth creaminess was plenty for me.  Not the best Mango Lassi I've had, but it did the job and I found it quite acceptable.

*** 5/2016 ***

This time I wanted to try their Sweet Lassi $2.95.  I guess all their cold drinks are pre-prepared since they just poured this up for me.  Small portion again.  But it was thick stuff, so the portion was sufficient.

*** 3/2017 ***

Picked up a chicken dosa to go for $12.95 after tax.  It was okay.
It's really good veg food at a reasonable price! Wish there were more vegetables in the falafel wrap but this is still a great place

Haven't been able to find a better place for NY style pizza in the Boston area.  

It's not quite Joe's Pizza on Bleeker (but then again, nothing is), but it's damn close.

Really good hole in the wall Italian pizza joint. They also have good sandwiches pasta and risotto entrees. Oh, and the lasagna is huge , tasty, and reasonably priced. The staff has always been friendly, & I will be back again.

When I'm in Cambridge I am always drawn to this place.  It's so perfectly Cambridge in every sense.  Artsy, academic, dark velvet, young crowd.  The food is amazing and they have a great wine and drink list to match it.  
If you are in the area for brunch definitely stop by.  They have a really great menu with even better prices!
As far as the decor goes - dark velvet and cushy leather

Nice lunch spot. The specials are always good. I enjoy going here when I get tired of eating sandwiches every day. 
The rice can be inconsistent (sometimes under cooked), so I try to avoid having that and opt for extra salad instead.

Calzone was wonderful, cheesy without being greasy. Pizza crust also authentic. Prices very good, free delivery. Onion rings not so great, very greasy, had to reheat. Definitely will order from Skampa again.

Just went there for dinner and the food was amazing!! Great little restaurant in a quaint little area of Cambridge.  Highly recommend the Gnocchi Alla Sorrentina, garlic bread and calamari.  Would eat there every night if I could!

Oh boy... 

I'd see people in Harvard square carrying the boxes of cannoli from Mike's, and thought that they were lugging them all the way from the North End. 

I was so excited to try the cannoli here when I realized there is a Mike's right in Harvard Square.

Unfortunately, I was left underwhelmed. 

Don't get me wrong, the cannoli aren't at all bad, they just aren't spectacular, and don't live up to the hype.

I did like that the dough was not oily, but it was perhaps a little too brittle. There was quite a bit of ricotta in the cannoli, and biting into it made the dough crumble quite easily... either eat them at home, or pack the napkins.

The ricotta was good, the texture was nice, and it was barely sweet... while unlike that there was a hint of sweetness, I would have liked a larger hint.

Also, I feel that there was a slight balance between the amount of ricotta and the dough... either thicken the dough or make the tube a bit smaller.

I do remember they are. It the cheapest things in the world, given my experience... I question if they are a great value... not bad for sure, but not great.

$3.99 for pizza...can you get anything better than that?! and it tastes so good, you wouldn't know it cost $3.99! 
ANNNND the roast beef sandwiches are great, as advertised.

the interior has linoleum walls and floors, (not the best ambiance) so i've always had take-out from here.  

it's in a very convenient location, and is my go-to place for cheap fast yummy food!

To be honest, there wasn't much I didn't like about this restaurant.  It has a great atmosphere, good service, and delicious food.  The menu is moderately priced and the food is savory and presented well.  I have been a few times and each time the experience is pleasant and every meal I have gotten is yummy.

I should have updated this awhile ago. Sorry Marco's. Back up to 4. Gnocchi is still my "go-to" order here. Mozzarella sticks are also good, pizza is ok. Also like that they now have online ordering and a nice delivery guy that actually comes to my door instead of calling when he is 4 blocks away and saying he is outside my building so I will come downstairs and meet him outside, which seems to be the MO of most drivers these days.

Came here as our Cambridge stop in the Boston trip. I had a great time here walking through the neighborhood and Harvard campus. I wouldn't say its very busy and touristy but we came on a Monday.. Got to see a lot of students going and back from classes and as another poster have mentioned its a very great people watching spot.  Many shops and cafes along the main roads makes it feel very distinct. Definitely well worth a few hour stop if you're visiting Boston area.

Check out the lunch specials.  My wife, the fried "foodie," said the calamari and clams are wonderful.  I had a diet Pepsi, too bad it wasn't Coke zero, and a very tasty lobster roll on a soft bolillo. The clientele is eclectic, I hear German, English, and Spanish with hints of Irish accents here and there.   The age demographics also varied.  Table linens were being placed for dinner as we finished a leisurely lunch.

We were visiting my son at Harvard and wanted to find a restaurant not on Harvard Square. Looked at yelp, and it got very good reviews. It deserved it, very good service, nice atmosphere, good background music and most  of all, great food at reasonable prices. Highly recommended. If you get there before 6:30 on a Saturday, like we did, no waiting. Easy walk from Harvard Square to West Side lounge, or from Porter Square T station. Enjoy!

Roxy's was recommended to our group (13 youth and 3 adults) by the MIT Museum (across the street). We placed our order an hour in advance, and the staff at Roxy's was fantastic. Without us asking for this to happen, they placed everyone's order in their own individually-labeled bag which made distributing the orders so much easier. (Please note that I think this was possible because we ordered before their lunch rush. I don't think it would have been easy for them to do or even fair to ask them to do it if they had been slammed.) And the food was delicious! Their mushroom/lentil veggie burger is one of the best veggie burgers I've ever had. All of the kids enjoyed their meal. With great food and a really friendly staff, we'll definitely make Roxy's one of our regular lunch spots on our next visit to Boston.

Agree with previous posting, not a lounge. However, this place is a great restaurant and has some good bar offerings and interesting art. For food, highly recommend the burger and the pan seared cod. Fish tacos are ok. Fried calamari is recommended as an app as well. I definitely appreciated the flexibility the hostess showed our party by moving another group to accomodate a handicapped member of our group on short notice. 
Check out the pisco sidecar if you're a pisco fan.
Marco's has the best delivery calzones in our area - not too greasy, fantastic crust.

There's some pretty cool stuff to see/do for an afternoon. I probably wouldn't go here if I wasn't a student, but at least the restaurants are really good. Parking can be difficult, but it's very walkable.

Mouth-watering Mediterranean food with a Senegalese twist! I order the vegetable plate which consists of rice pilaf, green dal, hummus, Falafel, spicy chutney, green salad and chopped cucumbers and tomatoes. Sometimes they provide me with black beans instead of green dal. All in all, it is a healthy, vegan meal.

I ordered online and my order was ready in less than 10 minutes. I got the green muenster (who'd have thought guacamole would taste this good in a  grilled cheese sandwich!) and the truffle fries. Pro tip : get the truffle mayo dip. It's delicious. Overall, really quick service and great food. I'll be coming back !

Well I decided to give Marco's a try last week for delivery, as I 'm determined to find someone who offers good pizza and delivery in the Davis Square area. I've never been to their place, so I can't comment on that-just the delivery experience. 

So the menu had regular pizza and "gourmet pizza" listed as I recall, so I went straight for that and ordered their "gourmet" double cheese and pepperoni pizza. The order went well and the food came on time, BUT the pizza actually costed more than the price on their menu and the menu WAS their newest. Oh well no big deal.

So up the stairs and onto the table the pizza flew where we all gathered for the magical moment to see the result of our research through hundreds of bulk mail pizza shop flyers. The top opened and we stood there staring at...A pizza that looked like every other crappily assembled mainstream pizza...Sigh...For a pizza that was suppossed to have double pepperoni and cheese it actually seemed to have less of those two than the regular pizza I order from most places. OK, lets see how it tastes. Hmm, cheese seems to be of decent quality, but everything else is blah and typical-especially the crust. 

So in summary, this place claims to have gourmet pizza, but they define gourmet pizza as each pizza having a special name. Nothing wrong with this kind of mainstream blah pizza, but they charge a little more than I'm used to for it and it's a bit misleading. I can't comment on the place itself or their other food, but I'm guessing it won't be a radical departure. Until further investigation it's 3 stars for the night owl. 
 
P.S. Add a dollar or two to the pizza prices on their menus.

Tanjore was the fourth restaurant in our ongoing tour of Indian buffets in the Cambridge/Somerville area. This review is just based on the weekday lunch buffet.

I was a little disappointed in Tanjore. I realize that a lot of the reviews were written about the menu, which is probably a lot better, but the buffet was not all that great. In fact, it was my least favorite of the four places we've been to so far.

The biggest reason? Poor selection. With only 1 appetizer (vegetable pakora that was 100% cauliflower), 1 dessert (rice pudding) and 5 main dishes, Tanjore had the smallest selection of any of the buffets we've been to. They also had a small selection of salad choices and a couple sauces, but so does everywhere else, and usually more than that.

Instead of the typical saag/palak paneer, Tanjore had chicken saag, which was a nice change. But the chicken was a little dry and amazingly, there was too much of it. I wanted some more saag, to be honest. But it was just sort of a coating for what was mostly a chicken dish.

The quality of the food was fairly good, a solid 3 stars. I would put it on a level with India Palace in Union Sq., but not as good as Haveli and certainly not as good as Kebab Factory.

My roommate is the rice pudding expert and he said Tanjore's buffet version was runny and the rice kernels were a little firm for his taste. He said it tasted like "rice in sweet milk". It's true that Indian rice pudding is a different take, but this was thin even by that standard.

They had tableside naan service, which I like. But like Kebab Factory, they only give you 1/4 of a piece per person and you have to ask to get more. (at India Palace they give each person an entire naan to start out). The water refills were timely.

This was by far the most crowded buffet we've been to so far. No surprise since it's located in Harvard Sq. and not Union or Inman Sq. etc. But honestly, if this place wasn't in such a good location, it would go out of business. Those other locations have to try harder to attract as many customers. They couldn't get away with a lunch buffet with such a poor selection.

Price: $7.95 - this seems to be the standard weekday buffet price for Indian restaurants in the area. All but Haveli ($6.95) have been the same so far.

We had planned on going to Gandhi this week, but had a last-minute change in plan. That will be our next stop for sure.

As some of the other reviewers mentioned, this is definitely a neighborhood place and for that it's great.  If you are looking for a decent meal, this is not the place to go.  

We ordered the pasta and meatballs and the gyro plate.  The pasta was way over cooked and sat in a pile of water 1 inch deep.  The meatballs were clearly cooked either the day before or the day before that.  Unimpressed.  The gyro plate came with a salad which the dressing was tasty but the lettuce was all the hard, white parts from iceberg lettuce with a lone tomato slice as an accompaniment.  The fries were good but it's the gyro itself that was inedible.  The gyro meat was so dried out that when I held it in my fingers it stood out straight.  I pulled almost all the meat out of it and enjoyed the "gyro" with just the pita bread and vegetables.  The sauce that was on it was very good though.

I should have been suspicious when we were the only people in the entire place eating.  Others were only having drinks. Find another restaurant unless you only want drinks and to watch a game.

Love this place, everything is good! Really nice owners and great brownies if they have  them!

We usually get the tortellini with the bosciola sauce, Caeser with shrimp and a brownie!

Don't forget, it's cash only!

I know this is not NY pizza, but I didn't think it would be this bad. The pizza itself is very doughy and lacking flavor. If you are looking for bread dipped in a little sauce, you have found your spot, but if you are looking for a good slice... keep on walking.

I absolutely LOVE LOVE HArvard Sq. It's not exactly close to me but the 66 bus takes me straight there from the front of my house. There is so much going on between street performers, street merchants and artists to all the shops, local and chained. i always enjoy going into the smaller local shops and see what they have, new and old. i always pick up something, no matter what it is. There's something for everyone. 

I also end the day by getting froyo from one of the few places or tea from Peet's or Starbucks. Yea, i frequent here often!

Bytes is my favorite tiny take-out in Central Square!  It's a Senegalese food place that also makes standard breakfast and lunch stuff like wraps and subs (I don't say subs, they're all grinders to me but sometimes people look at me funny).  All the food here is tasty and I could live off the cucumber/tomato salad that comes as a side with all the dinners.  I really like the Senegambian dish "Yassa" which is out of this world.  In fact, this place is so cool I simply walk in and tell the owner to "gimme something good" and he always does, I suggest you try this.

Other things about Bytes: The staff is super friendly and polite, the owner likes to talk about soccer and they only accept poppy, cabbage, bread etc.
GREAT food, but a little pricy.  We had the green curry, lobster rolls, and red curry. Yum.

I love Cambridge, and I love walking around Harvard Square on any given day.  There are a bunch of awesome shops to browse around in and it's a great place to go for people-watching.  There's generally a bunch of artists, musicians, and actors doing gigs on the streets.  Harvard Square is home to, in my opinion, the famous Garage which encompasses my favorite tattoo and piercing place - The Chameleon.  There are a lot of awesome stores to check out in the garage, and the same goes for outside as you walk around Harvard Square.  It's definitely a great way to spend an afternoon.

I've been here a few times now and I feel like it keeps being just shy of the kind of place I'd give 5 stars.  It's better than Decent, not quite as good as Holy Fuck That Place Was Awesome What Do You Mean You Haven't Been There You Have To Go There NOW.

The lynchpin of WSL's success is their seasonal cocktail menu.  Last night for example I had a tasty autumny sangria, livened up with cranberry juice, ginger, and maybe something like cinnamon.  Other notables include the blood orange margarita and the blackberry mojito.  Not sure why they're still serving fall drinks in mid-January, though.

The decor and lighting are nice, but something about the actual materials of the fixtures feels a little cheapish to me, a little like furniture and lighting you might find in an airport lounge.  Other people who've been there haven't really noticed or been bothered by this, so maybe you won't be either.

The food is kind of hit or miss, but the hits are well worth discovering.  I wish they'd make an entire plate of the hot fried pickles that come with the vegetables frites.

So, yeah: awesome seasonal drinks, but why still fall?  Nice atmosphere, but slightly cheapy furniture. Good food, but occasionally not so much.  Oh, and the staff: I've only had very good experiences with the staff and service, but I have heard of someone that didn't.  See what I mean? The parabolic arc of its awesomeness keeps falling just shy of that 5-star ceiling.

I'm a fan and a returning customer, but the perfectionists out there may want to go somewhere else for a $10 cocktail with mood lighting.

Amazing.  It's a bit out of the way if you don't live nearby, but for us Huron Villagers (does anyone use that term?), it's an absolute gem.  

Pizza by the slice is $3 and a complete bargain for how huge and tasty it is.  I love that you can pick up a huge slice by the crust with one hand and not have the whole thing flop over.  Only problem is it might be slim pickings because you can only purchase slices of whatever they have out, which is usually 2-3 different kinds.  You have to order a whole pie if you don't like what they have out.  The sandwiches are also huge and can easily be split with someone if you're not completely starving.  Their antipasto items in the display case taste just as good as they look.  The calamari was delicious and a good change-up from the usual fried stuff.

The place is quite quaint.  There are a few small tables, but most people seem to take it to-go, so we've never had trouble getting a seat.  

My only gripe is that they are CASH ONLY.  I would come so much more often and buy more stuff if they would just take my card!  But I supposed that's probably a good cost control mechanism for a poor grad student.  

Oh, and all their meats are halal!
Small, cozy, super nice owners, and the food is fantastic. Real fresh food. The legit deal all around. Bring Cash! :)

This place is terrifying. The website made it look pretty nice but its essentially the Applebees of seafood. We walked in with a groupon, sat down, looked at the dirty blue carpet and lobsters coming out in large plastic salad bowls and decided we didn't want food poisoning. Do not buy the groupon!
I always get deliverey from here and never been dissapointed. I've only had pizza and appetizers, but so far so good. Deliverey always gets here in 20-30 min. Cheap and tasty.

I was looking forward to this one! He moment I walked in, I sniffed the Yassa, and while I liked it I can't digest the $12 tag! What was the downer was the shawarma- how can you make one without the meat shavings, fattoush, and tabouleh? I've eaten Omani & Emirati versions of it, not just the popularized Lebanese and Turkish ones, and NONE of those resembled their concoction. Our server was nice, but the food just doesn't cut it.

Eight months later and I'm still a daily visitor to this great lunch take-out restaurant. There's always something new to try, like today's steak teriyaki with vegetable stir fry. It was awesome, tasted fresh and clean, and not heavy or unhealthy like a lot of other places can be. I'll upload a pic. I've been on a paleo diet for a few months, and Bytes is always happy to accommodate. Don't want rice? Extra hot sauce? Just ask! Super friendly and great food. Try out a special. Ask what smells so good and try it!

Crowded.  Impossible to drive through.  OK To walk around and shop or eat but beware the area is FULL of freaks- either Harvard elite or punk rockers with nothing better to do.  I try never to go through this area.

Great food.  They brought warm bread and olives to the table to start.  Small and I would imagine quite a wait at peak times.  The staff was very good and friendly.  I had grilled shrimp that were the best I have ever had.. Cooked to perfection and fresh.
I love their pasta dishes!  My husband always gets the lamb kabob sandwich.  There isn't much of a dining room (just a few small tables) but the food is delicious.  Great for take out too.

I have probably walked past this restaurant a few times and never noticed it.

The food was just fine and the drinks were surprisingly not extremely expensive.  $4 for a beer..... Not too shabby.

We were there on one of the coldest nights of the year and in direct hit area of the door, which made it a little cold when we came in and out, but that wouldn't be an issue if you were seated somewhere else.

I would agree that the service was a little slow, but it wasn't to the point of not being able to get anything ordered in a timely manner.  Our food actually came out really quickly even as we ordered three separate times for our group members who came in at different times.

And lastly, I just remembered.... They could only spilt our check 4 ways.... I mean we had 6 people... Not a HUGE issue in my opinion, but whatever....Obnoxious

It's small and cozy, with decent service, but nothing so amazing I'd write home about it (though appearantely i'll Yelp it...). Yet, they have half off appetizers from 5-7 that make everything taste a lot better.

As an app, the Prosciutto Bruschetta is good. While I wasn't sure about it when a friend ordered it, the Pulled Pork & Blue Cheese Pizza was actually surprisingly tasty.
Food was good. Service was not. We were waited on by a manager who apparently could care less if we had drinks or to check on our table.

Harvard Square is basically a mall without a roof.  

There are some bright spots, like Cambridge 1, the Brattle, etc.

I've been eating lunch at Sebastian's every day since 2009. They have hands-down the best selection of really, really great lunch food near MIT -- salads, crepes, pastas, sandwiches, stir-fry. The portions are huge for the price and you can get it either fresh-made, customized in front of you or grab-and-go. I always get a salad - you can pick as many toppings as you want and I always go to town (except I leave off the dressing to keep it healthy!). By the time I make my way through the giant salad, I've eaten a ton of healthy veggies, fruit and protein, and I feel amazing!

Recently, they downsized their storefront, probably because the price of rent in Kendall must be exploding. Now they're tucked back on Ames and are a little harder to see. That's a shame, because I don't think any of the new lunch places on Main come close to Sebastian's quality, value, or tastiness. 

I've also seen some reviews hating on their staff. The staff has been exactly the same since I started going in 2009 and are all really nice people. No, they don't all speak English very fluently, but if you go in a few times and treat them kindly, they will all always greet you with a warm smile and a "Long time no see!" I would much rather patronize this business of hard-working, genuine people who have been a part of the Kendall square community for as long as I have lived here than one of the new bougie locations that charges you $12-15 for a tiny amount of just-ok lunch fare. 

I fully recommend Sebastian's!

They are always out of food by 6 pm. They never have sweet onion chicken teriyaki. They ran out of bacon, chicken, and onions at 2 pm today. Service is very slow. This is the worst Subway I ever been to. Unfortunately they are the only one on MIT campus.

Great authentic Italian fare at a classic hole-in-the-wall location. The owner and his small team of staff are all very friendly and welcoming. Every dish is made by hand and you get to see the entire process from start to finish. The flavor was excellent - I ordered the Linguine with Frutti di Mare. Calamari was extremely tender and cooked just right, same goes for the shrimp. The clams were also nice and juicy. Would definitely recommend this spot to anyone - it's a bit out of the way but well worth the trek.

Great experience! Great for birthdays. The whole restaurant sang and they gave us a delicious ice cream dish. Pad Thai is amazing and gluten free. There are a number of gluten free options. Service was unbelievably fast and a dinner for 6 was $82 with tip. Would recommend!!

I was very disappointed with last night's gnocchi! A greasy mess! I had to remove the grease with a turkey baster before it was even edible. Marco, I hope this was a fluke! I sincerely hope you haven't changed your delicious sauce.

Plus, it seems like their prices have gone up--$20 for gnocchi and mozzarella sticks is a bit steep.

Brought some out of town guests here for some fresh New England seafood and ended up having a down right nasty experience.  The prices are ok and the seafood was tasty if standard.  My fried seafood plate was surprisingly large, fresh, and varied.  But here are the negatives...and I mean the ruin-your-meal kind of negatives.  As a table of 9 they sat us in the long booth in the back by the kitchen.  Throughout the meal there were fruit flies everywhere.  Not a plague, but certainly enough to be unpleasant (and in your drink).  But the green wormy bug that one of us found coming out of a fried scallop was enough to ensure that we'd never return.  Oh, and they charge for soda refills.

My husband and I were in Cambridge yesterday, a beautiful sunny Saturday afternoon.  We were in search of a casual place for lunch.  Well I am so happy we came upon Trata.  The pizza was so delicious.  And you can't beat the prices!  I got an artichoke and mushroom pizza that was so flavorful.  The half size was plenty for me, and my husband easily polished off a full.  The decor is really nice and would love to come back for dinner sometime.  There was not one customer in there except for us so I truly hope they they get more people coming in.  The bar is small but cozy.  I would love to go back if possible.

Everything I've had from Sofra has been delicious!  It's expensive, though, and there is very little seating at the restaurant itself.  The best things I've had are the morning buns, the pistachio poptarts, every cookie, the hummus, and honestly everythign else.  It's truly delicious.

I am such a fan of the chef now.  Oleana is an amazing restaurant, but I rarely go out to eat at fancy restaurants.  Sofra has food that is just as delicious and innovative but in a casual bakery setting.  Like Oleana, it's also Middle Eastern style, with a strong bent toward Turkish food.  We got a bunch of little plates so we could try everything, and nothing disappointed - even the ful, which was not at all like ful I had in the Middle East, but it was delicious nevertheless.

My friend who grew up in Watertown introduced me to Sofra, and we go get breakfast there once in a while. It is amazing.

I have had the migas and the breakfast sandwich. Although both were good, the migas is absolutely delicious.

The earthquake cookies are a must. They will not disappoint.

Great roast beef and the french fries are the best in the area... 

Pizza is great also and the dinner plates come loaded with food... Great price for great food...

Love this place. I have eaten at almost every mom & pop pizza place in Cambridge, Boston & Brookline and this is my favorite one. 3 reasons why: freshest food, tons of food on my plate & friendly staff. Next best thing to being home. Oh yes and they have a bar too so I can have a beer and watch tv while I eat & chat
I am American Indian, and this is my go-to Indian restaurant in Harvard Square. The vegetarian appetizers are stand-outs.

Soooomewhat overpriced, but whatcha gonna do?

The MEDiTERRANEO SANDWiCH is super yum yum in my tum tum. 

and the cupcaKes are fun and delicious. 

soups are good too!
They make a great pizza. The owners are friendly and fast. I would definitely recommend if your in the area and want a good pie

I stopped by to pick up a quick lunch, and thought it was a great idea; only, minutes later, I walked out supremely upset, and no meal in hand. 
When I gave them the money to pay for my meal, they refused to take my change because they "don't accept pennies." Before you get the wrong idea, I wasn't paying my entire meal in change. Rather, I handed a ten dollar bill and 5 pennies. 
I've worked in customer service before, and I know this kind of behavior is ABSOLUTELY UNACCEPTABLE. I asked to talk to the manager and all of a sudden, after so eloquently explaining why she doesn't take pennies, the maître d' doesn't understand english. The manager was actually willing to lower the price of the meal, but the damage has been done. 
I will no longer eat at Spice Thai because of THEY ARE RUDE. Yes, the restaurant has the atmosphere, the food, but they utterly fail in delivering the most important element in a business: customer service.  Spice Thai cuisine's reputation has been irreparable stained.
I live next door to Marco's and they look to be permanently closed. I could be wrong, they might be closed for renovation, but their store looks abandoned. Anybody know the story?

The roast beef is so fresh, and they are so generous with it on your sandwich, wrap or sub. When you see how huge it is you won't believe it. You'll think, "oh, that's enough for three people ", and you'll put half in the fridge. Then after you devour the first half in five minutes, you'll unwrap the other half.  

Maybe next time I'll try one of their new salads. :)
This is exactly what every food fair is missing. Recommended 100% !! Delicious food, lovely concept, amazing quality.

I think this place has been responding to all of the criticism on yelp, because I went here today and it was actually an okay experience! I was served with a smile and my order was delivered accurately. Some of the employees weren't super attentive, the chip selection was low, and Subway isn't that delicious to begin with but overall it was fine. Not the worst choice if you're stuck in the food desert that is MIT.

Harvard Square, I give you 3 stars. I like how you're situated near the university and a multitude of shops, but I hate how sketchy people congregate near you. I was sitting near the Out of Town newsstand, waiting for a friend and reading to take my mind off the freezing cold, but I kept getting distracted by beggars and a group of "rebellious" ugly teens who hawked loogies, drank Irished-up Sprite, and harrassed the passersby. I'll try not to let this detract from the lovely shops and restaurants that surround you, though, as well as the short walk to the fabulous Charlie's Kitchen.

Ordered the Brie in puff pastry, veg lasagna, and half chicken for me and my wife.  The Brie was greasy and in need of more vinegrette, the lasagna noodles were over cooked, the sauce was Ala chef boride, and the chicken was dry and the skin was soggy. Sad ...

The roast beef sandwiches at Skampa need to come with the following warning label attached, as I was totally unprepared for them:

WARNING: The contents of this sandwich may be incredibly saucy. Side effects of consuming this sandwich include, but are not limited to, sticky fingers, BBQ-flavored burps, stained clothing, saucy grins, sudden feelings of contentment, and short-term food comas.  Do not consume this sandwich alone, as it may be necessary for someone to drag you back to your place of work after consumption.  Please consume in moderation.

I would imagine their other products will need similar warnings, but I have yet to find out.  Others have also mentioned it, but the prices are at depression-era levels here, they really can't be beat.  Add to this the adorable, cheery woman in a pink diner shirt taking orders, and you have yourself a real winner.

Food was ok at best. I ordered the "heavenly" salad. The greens were barely there, and were less than fresh. The "grilled" chicken was dripping in grease. The worst was the delivery guy. I had tipped $2 (on an $11 salad) when I ordered online and he insisted that I hadn't and demanded I give a tip. He glared at me angrily while handing over the food and made the entire experience uncomfortable. Won't be ordering again.

When I came here,  I realized I'd been here before for brunch.  While the brunch was not memorable, the dinner was one for the books.

I initially came for a drink, but the Tequila Stinger was quite potent.   They do not skimp on the tequila with this drink.  I ended up having the seafood paella.  It was quite savory with hearty portions of salmon, shirmp, cod, and mussels.  

The music was a an old school mix, ranging from the Chambers Brothers to Dennis Edwards.

I can't really give a lunch counter five stars -- it's just simple wraps and meal plates. You order, wait, and take out. But in this location this place stands out because it's convenient, fresh, tasty, and there's a nice variety. The West African flavor touches work for me, and the proprietor is friendly and quick. Also, he has real talent -- around Thanksgiving he was making a turkey, cranberry sauce, and stuffing wrap that was really something special. He cooks his beef on one of those vertical rotisseries, like the Mexican markets have for tacos -- some of the beef dishes, that broiled taste really works. For inexpensive, quick tasty lunch, this beats most places around.
Yikes! They don't accept coupons or gift cards?! And they are not sorry about it. Ended up paying $5 for a lame 6-inch sub with near-invisible meat. Stay away!

Delicious food! I often get it delivered and it arrives hot and fresh, the delivery driver is always friendly as well. Pro tip: Try anything you'd get as a sub, as a wrap such as steak and cheese, pastrami - much easier to eat that way and so good!

Is it a requirement to be rude and unfriendly to work here? Ugh. Come on. 

Cannoli are okay. Cookies just fine. The giant brownies are both fudgey and cakey which is nice. Modern Pastry has much better cannoli. And they're nice so...yeah I have my preference. 

All that said, this location is squeaky clean.
Normally I think Subway is okay, or pretty good.  Just not this one.  Food is worse than other Subways, service is terrible, and it's buried in the 2nd floor of the student center.

I really like Trata. I love places where you can walk in and sit where ever you want. The atmosphere is great, nice and dark with some candles lit just how I likes it. 

I usually get the avocado and asparagus salad with the lime dressing they have. It so good. It comes with grilled salmon on top but I'm vegetarian now so I usually just get the same thing sans salmon. 

This is a great place for a first date, I'm married and my husband and I love coming here for dinner and wine for a nice romantic but casual night out. I love love love the fact that they play the Gypsy Kings, it's the perfect music to play and adds a nice touch to the overall ambiance of the place. 

Good food, good drinks, great service..nice little space, nicely decorated and clean. Will definitely be going back very soon.

Eating dinner from Skampa is buying a ticket to Heartburn City. A Skampa dinner starts off with one of at least a dozen kinds of sandwich. Roast beef, which has been raved about in prior reviews, is as good (and tender and moist) as advertised. Seafood--which is what this eatery claims to specialize in--takes up as much of the menu as the grinders. I'll try it next time.

What puts the Skampa seal on the deal is the sides. With each dinner you get fries, onion rings, rice and pudding. Yes, that's AND--not OR. You're best off putting aside 4/5 of that for the next day. All this plus the main dish for $8. Inexplicable.

The interior is modest and clean, certainly not as grimy as other reviews would have you believe.

We went to Roxy's this evening and I was incredibly disappointed. We ordered our food to go because there was no table for four people at the restaurant. We waited far too long for our food - at least 15 minutes - and they forgot my dipping sauces, to boot. While the food was decent, I was frustrated that it took so long to get to us. I'd like to give it a second chance but the wait time was ridiculous. Not sure I'll end up coming back.

After seeing the reviews for Sofra here on yelp! I just had to try the Baklava. Let me expand on the "chocolate baklava" label.

I went on Sunday, it was intensely crowded, which by all accounts is a good thing. Had to send by buddy in with my card because we're all hefty fella's. Anywho, he picked up a dozen baklava for us to enjoy as desert after lunch.

This Baklava was simply fantastic! I've long been spoiled with home-made baklava from off-the-boat Greeks and honestly Sofra hold's it's own. There is chocolate mixed in with the nuts, (almond and haselnut) so the texture is flawless and it's soaked with a to die for cocoa honey.

I'll be back, Sofra, I promise..
Had a number of things here, and they were all very good. Including: pasta, rice/noodle bowls, and salad! This is one of most solid places in this area and i've been to them all!

Worse than hospital food. Pasta and rice bowls had no flavor. So bad. Plus stingy: only 4 shrimp in a $10 shrimp rice bowl. Do yourself a favor, walk over to Commonwealth on Third and have one of their sandwiches, which are awesome. About the same cost too. Sebastian's, never again.

I didn't come to this restaurant with high expectations, but even so, I was severely disappointed in the quality of the food and service. When we arrived at the restaurant the waitress came over to take our orders almost right after we received the menu. We asked for more time and then she left for over 10 minutes. Finally when she came back we placed our orders and happily waited for our food. Eventually we received some bread and I got my salad. My salad had disgusting old lettuce in it and tasted like soap. I am a salad lover, but even I couldn't finish it. It was the first time I have ever left almost a complete salad on a plate in a restaurant. Halfway through my salad, the waitress came over and said that there were no clams left, so I would have to modify my order. She offered no help but just gave me a menu. I was left trying to figure out what to do, while the waitress disappeared for another 10 minutes! Finally, when she returned I was the one to suggest to her that we replace the clams with something else (which ended up being calamari) and had to offer her another solution before she disappeared again. When the main food arrived it was soggy, greasy, and had absolutely no taste. No one at my table could finish the food, as it was just that gross. I left the restaurant feeling gross, slightly hungry, and $25 poorer then when I left.

Oh... and my water had something floating around in it too.

I have this bad habit of finding a cheap location that isn't technically "sit-down" but which makes delicious food, and from there on out comparing all similar cuisine to that location. Tanjore was totally fine, food-wise, but just not as good as Punjabi Dhaba. They were, however, very accommodating for a birthday dinner I was attending, and didn't get angry when some of the drunker folks at the table became downright overly loud.

Staying in Cambridge for the first time, I chose Tanjore over Tamarind Bay based on having a broader menu, especially in appetizers. The latter's menu is very hard to find on their website, so I went with Tanjore. 

I ordered take-out, starting with vegetable bhajia (plentiful mix of assorted vegetables with nice accompanying sauces) and followed that up with a chicken vindaloo and garlic naan. The naan was delicious, dripping with garlic. The vindaloo was interesting; definitely spicy, which it should be, but tasted almost like tomato soup, which I find unusual for a vindaloo. 

I would go again, but would probably order something else. There is also plenty of competition for Indian food around this block, so walk around and take a look.

The food here-DELISH. It's flavorful and I couldn't wait to go back! The options are vegan friendly but they also have amazing shawarma options. Staff is so sweet and friendly. The mall just got a whole lot better!

Food is very good, and that is the only reason I went back. The staff is pretty rude and ignored me both times for about 5 minutes while looking over the counter right at or should I say through me. The worst part though, was the uncomfortable atmosphere while the boss bickers with his employees.
 Two bad experiences in enough... I'm done with Marco's

Eh, whatever.  Pizza's RIDICULOUSLY greasy and the crust is stupidly tough.  I have to admit I found it almost inedible.

As weird as it might sound, I'd probably rather just stick with Cambridge House.
a-ok. would've been nice if the heat was turned up a little, but food was ok.

Played some fun games and was eating a delicious grilled cheese. With bacon & advocado!

Loud though, but ok! Nice place to be!

Overall, yes! 4 stars.

I stopped here for lunch after touring Harvard and was pleasantly surprised at how many different dishes they offered. Not long after being seated, a huge French tour group came in and filled up 90% of the restaurant, and that changed the ambiance of the place from tranquil to chaotic, quiet to obnoxiously loud. I ordered the green curry duck, and it came with a bowl of white rice. The dish was delicious, but the green curry flavor wasn't as pronounced as other green curry dishes I've tried elsewhere. While the duck meat was super tender, the skin fell more on the soggy/chewy side than a crispy finish. The dish also contained sliced carrots, bamboo shoots, peas, and green and red peppers. I managed to finish everything in one sitting, because it was just that tasty. I can see why this restaurant is so highly rated on another reviewing platform, but I just wished my experience was quieter and less stuffy. Service was still great though.

Second time fooled, so shame on me. But never again, never again. Any actual resemblance to menu items as pictured or described, or available at other (better) Subways, is purely coincidental. They may seemingly offer everything a normal Subway does, but trust me, the only thing on the menu here is a 5 Dollar Footlong (tm) of FAIL.

So yummy! Ordered the steak with Portuguese fries and rice. Topped with linguica and mushroom sauce. All were cooked to perfection. The sangria was delicious as well!

Great, friendly service. Complimentary olives and bread, which were fantastic! Will definitely be back.

CAN I PAHK DA CAH IN HAHVArD YAHD?

I met up with some locals when I was in Cambridge and I asked them how I could obtain this badass accent, and they kept repeating that phrase to me. HAHAHA.

Anyways, this place is beautiful even when it's covered in snow. The roads are basically frozen, and slippage occurs pretty frequently. There are a lot of great shops around the area, and it always seems hustling and bustling. Great setting to an obviously wonderful institute!
Very expensive and it was not even good. I was expecting much better pizza with a 4 star rating. In addition there was super loud rap music playing that really did not go with the ambiance.

Village Kitchen is the beacon of light (and by light I mean dinner) in Huron Village. We have Formaggio Kitchen, obviously, but as far as restaurants go, lunch/brunch is about all there is on the menu, unless you want to go over to Craigie Street Bistro for the prix-fixe and have your tastebuds lovingly caressed for two hours. There's not too much in walking distance around here and this gem is only a few minutes from my house.

I have eaten my way across Italy, and I think that the pizza here is approaching authenticity - thin crust, the most important part, fresh cheese that separates into melted ooze and a little cream over the fresh tomato puree (not pizza sauce as we think of it here). Their sausage smells of herbs and anise. It's GREAT pizza, very different from what we tend to get in the US (not that I don't love greasy pies and calzones). Go with the margherita if you've never eaten here before, this is the cheese/tomato/basil classic. Beyond this, the possibilities are endless - the vegetables are high quality, as are the meats. I also like the grape leaves (not sure if they are made on-site or if they come from Formaggio) and the fried cauliflower and eggplant. 
It's all yum yum and gorgeosity, as they say. (And at a reasonable price!)
I love hanging out at Harvard Square - there's so much activity and so much energy - full of students and all kinds of people. Super diverse area and just a great place to hang out and meet people.

Gnocchi Sorrentino, please!

I always get delivery from this place and, frankly, don't even know where it is. What I do know is that their gnocchi sorrentino is something that I crave and they bring it piping hot to my house. Deliciously prepared gnocchi with thinly sliced italian sweet sausage in a delectable sauce with just the right amount of cheese, this dish belongs in some restaurant in North End. Glad it comes with a huge amount of garlic bread because you need it to soak up every last bit of delicious sauce.

Pizza here is decent, but not fantastic. One of their special pizzas is discount-priced but they allow you to pick any 6 topping you like--nice way to give your customers a choice. I have also sampled their chicken fingers, which are real chicken breasts breaded with italian breadcrumbs and fried. Weird that they came with duck sauce (wtf?), but glad that I had the gnocchi sorrentino sauce to dip them in.

Staff is always friendly by phone and in person upon delivery. Great place to get some delicious gnocchi. Someday, I may try more things on the menu!

I know most pizza places re-heat slices.. but it's just a whole other animal to order a slice of pepperoni and then have the cashier take a sad looking cheese slice from the front display and apply the pepps. The weird thing is.. the pizza was actually pretty good! It just had a lot of flavor to it even without the pepperonis baking on the slice (which is still sacrilege to me). 

The prices are fair and they have some really awesome lunch deals if you happen to be in the area. Three stars for now.. I must do the calzone test! And to be honest I had no problem with the staff. I guess most of the places in this area are more personable (oh NoCa), but these guys weren't mean.. just kind of indifferent. I don't need to be chatted up while waiting for a slice of pizza.. but that's just me.

I've eaten here several times, from starving student (nearly) to not starving not at all student.  The meals if anything have stayed the same over 30 years.  Its wonderful location looking over Mass ave is what brings this restaurant from a 2 star to its current rating.  I go there whenever someone has 'found a place cheaper than legal', and each time it is indeed cheaper.  Some dishes are really good, slightly above average.  Most dishes are meh to OK.  I'm happy to say that it has not gone down hill in 20 years, and maintains a distant third to Legal Seafood (which has gone downhill in that time, on average, from it's humble 4 locations to now, 30?).  

TL;DNR  go if there if someone is paying, or if you have to, or if you can't afford good seafood

Nice environment inside, but the food is lacking in both originality and quality and the service is pretty lackluster. Have been here 3 times in the past 18 months (at the planning of friends) and not been impressed any time I've been. There are plenty of other options around here if you're looking for a relatively casual sit-down meal.

The waitress was very pleasant, my first visit was good because of her. 
I ordered the seafood salad and it was delicious.
Not a fan of chowder so my Hubby had to finish mine.
He had a seafood platter and that was alot he took home some and usually he finishes his dinner. So portions is a little over average, which is good....right?

I will return to try the stuffed mushrooms with crab meat.

Subway I love you - why do you hurt me so?

The food is Subway, pretty much the same food at every Subway right?  Well...that is if you get what you order.

I understand you are in a student union (at MIT). I understand that you are busy.  Do you understand that you are a business and if I am giving you money in exchange for food - I would like it to be what I ordered, how I ordered?

At least once a week I have to explain to someone, "No, I said cucumbers, those are green onions." Following by "No...no..I said, dohh..I said no sauce."  :(

And since I order my sandwich the way I want it and I have food allergies, could you please wipe off the knife before sticking it in my sandwich to fold it and then cut it...come on.  If I wanted the gooey stuff from that guys sandwich I would have ordered it for mine...aahh man.

This place will always be busy  because of the location and student population, so sadly, they will be able to plug along at 2 stars and nothing with change.  I hope that is not the case.  You have potential Subway...make MIT proud...or well, at least happily satiated.

Great local spot. Nice people. Reliable fare. The lamb kebab sandwich is a favorite. A neighborhood favorite in Huron Village.  In the summer there are a couple of tables set-up outside that add to the European cafe vibe.

I really dislike when a place does some things realllyyy well. And then some things are horrible. 

This is a shining example of that. I went here because its quick and I like the pizza and jalepeno poppers. I order the poppers and go out on a limb and try their meatball sub. 

Worst. Meatball. Sub. Ever. 

I get back and boyfriend and I eat the poppers first. I give him half his hero and he makes a face. "I dont like it" he plainly states. I figure it cant be that bad. But it can. 
Does Spam make meatballs? If so, I think they may use them here. 

The thing that gets me most mad here is that Riverside doesn't need to have a shitty meatball sub on their menu. Everything else is so good, that they just shouldn't waste menu space putting something on that they simply shouldn't offer if they are going to do it so half assed. I couldn't take more than two bites before I canned it. 

Subs=0 stars
Pizza=4 stars
Prices=5 stars 

Come for Pizza, and Apps, stay away from anything not requiring pizza oven or fryer.

Many VEGAN OPTIONS: I found the customizable dishes to be terrific.  Fresh, clean, many options.  Being vegan, i headed for the make your own salad counter.  Very similar to Pluto's in San Francisco!  I got some leaves, and proceeded to have half a dozen toppings added in, maybe 8.  Ended up with too much to eat, but still only $6.75.  A vegan bargan with a couple of rolls and some water, under $10 in Kendall Square.  I intend to go back and check out the pasta and tofu creations.

The food is fresh, there is a lot of choices and it all tastes good. But it sure is expensive! And sometimes the lines can be pretty long.

Still, for a create your own sandwich, salad, or pasta - it's a nice lunch place t stop by.

The staff can vary in friendliness and helpfulness though.

SERIOUSLY?!  The young man working the latter half of the sub assembly DOES NOT change his gloves between handling money and your sub.  Disgusting.  I wouldn't have made such a fuss about it if it was only his1st time offense but I've seen this consistently from week to week!

I was meeting a few friends for dinner and one of them made a strong push for Trata... OK, I thought - I've never tried it, looks nice from the outside, and I have almost walked in many times, thinking it was Daedalus (next door, but the door is very similar!) so why not? 

As is my usual MO, I immediately googled the restaurant to check out the menu... It was really hard to find the restaurant online (although there is a nice-looking Trata in NY) but eventually I did, and suddenly I got a whole lot less excited about dinner.  First item on the menu is Buffalo chicken tenders.  Now, I like chicken tenders as much as the next person, but at an Italian... really?  Doesn't necessarily speak volumes to the authenticity of the place.

Now, I'll just preface this by saying that it isn't that authentic... the pizza base is thick and the chicken tenders are just one example of some random non-Italian items on the menu.  BUT the food was *substantially* better than I expected.  

I ordered the risotto with duck (one of the specials) and it was excellent.  The rice was perfectly al dente and the meal was really flavourful.  I tried someone else's pizza and that was just so-so (although they were very generous with the toppings which is good).  I also tried the scallops, which were again generously portioned if not out of this world.  The Brie in puff pastry appetizer was a little light on Brie, but the prosciutto appetizer was very generous and really nice.

So all in all, there were highs and lows but I was impressed.  Trata's food had definitely far outshone my admittedly low expectations!  It's also a very nice cosy atmosphere and the prices are very reasonable.  What's not to like?

Note: make sure you bring your ID here if you are planning to drink... they are REALLY strict on checking ID considering it's a restaurant (and I am 27, I'm no young whippersnapper!)- not sure why this is, but I wonder if there's been an incident in the past??

I've been here two times.  Both times, the fish was cooked properly,  and tasted fresh.  It's nothing fancy but I believe that is what makes a fish taste like a fish-simplicity.
If you are looking for something like chain seafood restaurants- over-priced, over-seasoned fish, mediocre quality, too many choices, and fancy salads, don't go here.
If you like fresh fish, GO HERE!
Plus, the service was appropriately pleasant, timely, and responsive.
Incredible food! It may not look like much but the quality and flavor of ingredients is fantastic. I love coming here for the pizza and especially anything with roasted vegetables.

Marco's doesn't look like much, but it's a longstanding local business in a residential area (it outlasted Marino's Restaurant, which used to be next door). That should tell you something; either it's a front for the mafia, or it's pretty good.

Marco's is a pretty decent pizza joint, but I think their other offerings have gotten short shrift here on Yelp. Their fried chicken wings are crunchy on the outside, tender and juicy inside. I think they're on par with Olympic Pizza's wings; so good! Also, be sure to try their many subs. Their homemade chicken parm is excellent, as is the egg bomb and most of the other steak bomb variants. They also boast one of the best BLTs I've had in some time, if you're in the mood for something lighter.

As mentioned here, don't go expecting good service. You speak up when you need something, and don't expect extra politeness. Call ahead, the food takes a while to prepare (10-15 mins). There's not much seating to speak of, it's really more of a take-out place.

Marco's is closed on Sundays, which has caused me much consternation in the past. Still, well worth the money.

This subway has the worst service that I have ever experienced from any fast food restaurant. The staff offer absolutely horrendous service and blame the customers for their own mistakes, at this subway the customer is apparently never right.

I went in to the Subway to grab a bite to eat during an Indie Game Convention. Just when I had gotten in line I witnessed a shouting match between the store manager and a customer, I got there too late to see what started it but the customer was pissed, and the subway manager threatened to call security. I put this in the back of my mind thinking it was just some argument, and that as a calm guy my service would go uninterrupted, I was completely wrong.

After my sandwich was created it was put next to the register where it sat for owe 3 minutes before someone came to ring my order up, I got chips and a large coke, paid and when the cashier wrung me up I grabbed a large cup because she immediately left the cash register without giving me my cup. I went to fill up with coke when I only found carbonated clear water coming out. I wait for the person ordering to finish before talking to the cashier.

I said something was wrong with the soda machine. She glared at me and said in a rude manner that the only sodas available were Coke Zero and Sprite (she said this as if it were something I should have known even though I was not told and there were no signs). I then ask if I could pay the difference for a bottle of coke. She said snidely that it costs extra (of which I had already made clear I would PAY the difference so her remark was uncalled for). This is when it gets out of hand.

I say I want to upgrade from a large soda to a bottle and then she takes one look at the cup and said that I had ordered a small soda, and accused me of stealing by taking the large cup (i definitely asked for a large and also wow $.20 is worth that kind of accusation!). She then further said that she had charged me for a small soda and that I MUST pay the difference from that cost, which was $.50. I paid and then told her that she should really fix her soda machine (in an angry tone but not mean) she got really pissed at that, and I heard her complain about me to the next customer as I was leaving.

Overall this was the worst fast food experience I have ever had and judging by the other comments here people have had the same thing happen, if it were up to me this employee would be fired in a heartbeat, the service was so bad. Also I did the math later and based on how much I paid (I paid cash so I couldn't prove I got a large) I counted the change and I had paid for a large, so the entire thing was completely unwarranted. But I am never going to this place again and the food wasn't good anyways. This restaurant turned me off Subway for a long time, and I really don't recommend going there at all.

Tl;dr: Got accused of stealing $0.20 worth of soda (a large I had actually paid for) from a soda machine that didn't even work.

Ah how this place has changed. Once upon a time, hackey sacking, cigarette smoking, book reading and low key, Harvard Square is a tourist haven. Although many good restaurants have come to the area, the cool, sweet spots have mostly been forced out.

The garage houses a few remnants.

Meh, I really wanted to like you, Spice Thai... However, each of our dishes tasted a bit off. What we ordered: 1) drunken noodle with beef, 2) green curry with duck, 3) chicken pad thai.

1) drunken noodle with beef-- this was probably the best dish out of the three. Noodle was good, seasoning was okay, wish it had more flavor

2) green curry with duck -- I wanted more spice to it (kind of eh and bland) and more veggies with the curry 

3) chicken pad thai -- I was most disappointed with this dish.. it was missing vegetables! The dish was just noodles and chicken.. not a colorful dish at all, missing color (ie. veggies!) and way too sweet for my tasting.. I thought it was just me, but my friends thought it was way too sweet as well. 

Anyway, although the food wasn't the best... I have to give kudos to the restaurant for cooperating and collaborating with us. My friend and I had bought cupcake from Sweet for our other friend's bday. So, we gave the cupcakes to them ahead of time and they were able to bring it out by dimming the lights at the restaurant and lit candles on our cupcakes. Thank you, Spice Thai for making his bday special!
Great place - when I worked nearby, I had my breakfast there every day. Now I work close to Lechmere, but I still stop by time to time to enjoy perfect fresh cheese omelette....

I'll start with what I love:  The pastries.  Those chocolate chip cookies give my girlfriend's recipe a run for its money (hi sweetie!).  Every single baked item I've tried has been so good that I didn't want to finish eating it, because it was too terrible to think about never tasting it again.

The iced tea with apricot is absolutely wonderful.

The lunch items are also pretty good.  The first time I came in, my flatbread was way too greasy, and they've improved a lot.  The sampler plate of dips and salads was quite yummy, but didn't come with enough bread for 3 people to share.  Hm.

Here's what I don't like:  The space simply is not set up well.  It's still impossible to read the menu.  It's still tough to figure out what's what when you're ordering.  And the tables are so low that I thought I was going to strain my back while eating.  When I tried to take my flatbread to go one day, said greasiness almost got all over my car seat.  Being eco-friendly is great - but handing me a bag that prevents my lunch from melding with the upholstery is also nice.

If you're going to serve "gourmet" food, either make it easy to take home or make it easy to sit and enjoy in a leisurely way.  Sofra straddles the fence between take-out gourmet  and sit-down gourmet, and I really wish they'd just pick one and really nail it.

Best pizza I've had in Boston (lived here for 2 years).  The pizza has an amazingly delicious, seasoned crust and the sauce is killer.  I've never had a bad experience getting pizza from here.

You might want to order to-go, it's not the greatest atmosphere inside -- I'm just rating strictly on pizza quality.

Ok, consider, this is a mall food court. Now, this is by far the best option for food there. By far. No, it's not the best shwarma that I've had, but it's pretty good, and the meal is healthy, tasty, and reasonably priced; getting any of those three qualities is otherwise challenging around the area, let alone all three of them. So, thumbs up.

Are they trying to go for worst Subway ever? Because I can say without a second thought that this is literally the worst Subway I have ever been to, and I've been to a lot of Subways. I was at MIT this past January for their annual mystery hunt, and went out looking for food and found this Subway at about 7:30pm on a Saturday. Reasonable time to get dinner. When I showed up, there was one person in line, so I was thinking to myself "this should go quick!" Nope. The lone employee took about 10 minutes to put together this guy's sandwich, and he subsequently took the same amount of time to service my order. Most of his time was spent taking his sweet, precious time individually placing each pepperoni slice, each tomato slice, and each piece of cheese at a painstakingly slow pace onto the sandwich. Not to mention that they were out of stock on several ingredients, a fact about which the previous customer was visibly irritated. It was 7:30 -- prime dinner time! No excuse to be running out of ingredients. Neither was there any excuse to take 10 minutes to serve each customer.

I have nothing good to say about this establishment. It deserves a 1 star rating.

Been going there for many  years but the last 2-3 times we went the food has been pretty bad, finally the last time we went it went from bad to horrible.   Most of the table ordered shrimp with potatoes, the shrimp was over cooked tasteless and had most of the shell was still on it and took alot of effort to remove, and there was no spice at all. The potatoes were deep fried and tasted like state bland burnt potato chips, dry and hard to eat.  I asked for a steak rare, it came out well done and was lukewarm at best.  We kept saying lets give it another try but this time we all agreed this was the last time ever.
Perhaps the worst cocktails I've ever had. The pisco sidecar tasted like a baby threw up in my mouth
Great portions. Friendly service. The decor could be prettier, but I worry more about how pretty the food is. I am always very happy to come here.

We love this place.  We were regulars when we were in college.  Keno is always on deck.  The hot pastrami sub is the bomb.  They have great pizza and try the baklava.  The gyros are cooked to perfection.
The pizza was very yummy! We came here with a big group and service was impeccable, the food was made in under 5 minutes after ordering! The Staff was very friendly! Will definitely come again!

First off- thanks to my fellow Yelp'ers for posting reviews.  I never would have found this little gem if it weren't for you.  
Of course, I got the super roast beef on the onion roll.  It was rare, and truly superb.   The fries- a small feeds 2-3 were just as delicious.   
Outstanding job!

I used to spend a great deal of time here when I was younger. I grew up a Square away, in Central. I could usually be found here with all my friends running around, doing nothing much. 

As I matured, I then could be found here for the occasional meal, for work, or when I was enrolled at Harvard Extension School. It became a place that I would only go to for certain activities. From then on, it became a place that I didn't really want to go to all that often anymore, and now well over a decade since I used to frequent these streets on the daily, I drive through now and note the changes.

First, let me say that mid-afternoon is the best time for this lovely little joint because that's when you get to sit in one of the two bay tables and people watch on Huron Ave while sipping a jumbo sized cappuchino. I usually go here after getting my hair cut at Huron Salon across the street. The pizza is very good but when I'm there (again, mid-afternoon) and have tried a slice it has sometimes been sitting around for awhile and is a bit stale. The sandwiches are fine, but in my book nothing special. In short, I wanted to like this place more than I did; it's a cozy neighborhood place, the folks who run it are nice (if laconic) and who doesn't like homemade Italian. I'll go there if I'm in the neighborhood, but it's not a place I'll go out of my way for.

Terrible slow and arrogant service. My friend had a huge stomach ache after their oysters, which btw arrived at the very end after all the entrees. One of the ordered dishes was not available but it took the waiter 10 minutes to come and tell us about it and then 10 more minutes to come back to take a replacing order. The food was ok, too greasy, but eatable. But again the 4 ordered dishes arrived with a difference of more than 10 minutes between each.
Came in after a long day traveling.  Got set up with super fast service. Excellent fish and chips, steak sandwich, and outrageous onion rings. Very nice people.

There's not much I can say about this little mom-and-pop place that other reviewers haven't said. I went with a few out-of-towners and we all really enjoyed the food we got. It's very similar to Pita Inn (if you're from the Chicago suburbs) but with a more homemade taste to it. It was actually like comfort food for me which was much needed while I was traveling. The rice and shawarma had great flavors to it and went well with the salad. I would definitely recommend this place if you are looking for a quick place to grab lunch and you work/live close by because there is absolutely no place to eat in there.

I don't think I've ever went here and had the person pay attention to what's being ordered. The process is always the same; they ask you what kind of bread, then you tell them but they suddenly look arond and it's like their brain has just reset. Then they ask you again. You tell them. Cheese? Yes, cheddar. The employee is destracted seemingly by nothing. Cheese? I just told you, cheddar.. but they are already engaged in conversation with another employee all the sudden... seriously? And assuming people don't speak your language and don't understand what you're saying (always something inappropriate)... 

They never change gloves between handling money and food. Your order is never right. If you ask for no olives, the first thing they're putting on is olives. Want light mayo? Somehow they heard rasberry vinagarette or something absurd.

This place is the worst. Avoid at all costs (that's what they seem to want so that they don't have to do anything).
hung out there almost everyday for weeks the summer of 2001. great vibe. fun and interesting people around...!

Dude yeah right all you less-than-five-star-ers... this place rocks. 
Tanjore is such a go-to buffet joint in Harvard Square (you're only cheating yourself by ordering off of the menu). 

I will convince you using a three-pronged approach..

Prong 1: Real live indian people eat there all the time -- and they got Indian food street cred that proves me right. This ain't no taco bell trying to dish out mexican food... 

Prong B: The food was always delicious, but now they have a new and improved buffet selection! Granted they raised the price by a buck (I think to $10.95), but they added a new dessert, free chai tea, another couple of entree dishes, bought fancy new tableware and even moved the buffet line to the north side of the room instead of the south side!
...okay, fine, that last point is irrelevant. 

Prong III: Service and kindness are IRRELEVANT at a buffet. They give you extra napkins and water upon request and otherwise just stay out of your way. This is important when you're trying to eat enough food to be worth triple the buffet price (buffet etiquette rule-of-thumb). 

In conclusion, everyone else's opinions are wrong. Eat here.
Loved the salada mista, Presunto c/ queijo fresco, and Paelha á Marinheiro. Nice atmosphere, friendly staff. Lots of food for the price.

My friend and I were visiting from California and taking a self guided tour when we realized that we needed something to eat. We were really hot and tired... Yelp to the rescue! We found this restaurant and we were so happy! The food was amazing and they also serve cold drinks (boylan soda was unique to us, and delicious). Prices are reasonable but please note its cash only. Highly recommended!
Decent thai in Harvard, but not my go-to. tends to be a little on the greasy side, and tends to have less flavor than other thai places.

I came here with a couple friends this past Sunday night for a relaxing end of the weekend, ''goodbye you're going back to New Jersey/ Cape Cod" dinner. 

We walked in around 6 and there were only a couple other parties in the restaurant. I liked the vibe, classy but also casual and simple. Nice.  I was impressed with the cocktail list, but resisted the urge to get one and instead went with a beer ( I believe it was a Mayflower Porter). They only had a couple beers on tap, but the ones they did have were pretty unique, so points for that!

We were all in a seafood mood, so my 2 friends ordered fish and I got the lobster capellini. Everything was pretty good, but I wasn't blown away. Also, my dish was so small! My friends both got more food than me, which was puzzling. I mean I know I got lobster, but c'mon! I left feeling not as full as I should have  been, especially for the price. 

Service was slow, but it seemed like there was only one waitress in the place. Overall, West Side Lounge was pretty good, the only reason I'd return is to try a cocktail off their extensive and interesting list.

Who ever would have thought that cantaloupe, prosciutto, blue cheese galore, over spinach with a citrus vinaigrette would work????? 

Well, Trata did. And boy were they right! Every bit Matt J. and I took of this salad took us instantly to salad heaven. It was just one of those things that sounded kind of weird on the menu, we were bold enough to order it, and now I think I may just make this salad once a month at home, because I just could not get enough of it! I was actually almost sad that I had agreed to split it with Matt ha

After that we got their lobster ravioli paired with perfectly seared scallops, a white wine cream sauce, peas, and tons of freshly shaved parmesan. Mmmmmm. Taking a piece of one of those scallops and dragging it through that sauce so that you don't waste one single little drop just tasted so so good. And the ravioli's were pretty much perfect too!. It all just went so well together, and even the peas added a nice extra contrast of consistency to that dish. Man, we were just so so happy with this dinner choice.

Best of all we pretty much got the most expensive salad and the most expensive entree on the menu, and a beer and a glass of wine, all for $50. And when the food is that good, and you just feel so comfy sitting in this cozy bar restaurant, that means I will definitely be back. Until then....I better figure out how to make that salad!!!

This place can feel magical. There is something wonderful about the combination of youthful intelligence and energy, and the host of eclectic and sometimes charming stores. This is marred by the distressing sight of large numbers of people, sometimes families, living on the streets and begging to support themselves. It's a harsh reminder of the inequality in our society.

I'm a huge fan of this joint! So many tasty tasty drinks and the food i've gotten was mad good yo! I got the asian chicken bite things off the late-night menu, so get those. I also really like the atmosphere, it was great for the group of friends i was with.  We could hear one another talk and the other patrons were a good crowd.

The staff there is also pretty nice and welcoming.  They let my friends and I switch to a larger table as more people showed up and were quick to bring out our drinks and ask us if we needed more.  The one down side was the guy forgot my friends deviled eggs, but it wasn't on the bill so it wasn't a huge issue.  Also, they took awhile to get us our check. Oh! So when we got there it was before the late night menu came out and I asked if i could the chicken bites a bit early, he said they couldn't, BUT he remembered how much i wanted them so as soon as the menu came out he came over and asked if i still wanted them, and of course i did!

There are a ton of great shops and restaurants around, but this place can be WAY too crowded. Never EVER try driving around here. Definitely take the T and just walk around. Unfortunately, even still there is a ton of pedestrian traffic as well, but it's marginally better.

Worst Subway in the country. I came from California, where the Subways are plentiful, the vegetable are fresh, the bread is fluffy, and the avocados are cheap. Seriously, I loved Subway in California. The one in the MIT student center is literally the worst sandwich place I have ever been to. They don't have as good of a selection as most, the bread sucks, the meat's disgusting, they don't fold the meat or put enough vegetables on. They also don't understand English. I understand people with accents that have a slightly harder time understanding your order, but some of these people don't even know what mustard is. You need a basic vocabulary of the food items you're selling to work at a restaurant, no matter how shitty it is. Furthermore, they are complete assholes.

It really upsets me that this place only has 3 stars. Let me tell you that I am obsessed with Thai food; I've tried nearly every Thai restaurant on both the west coast and east coast, and I think that this is my favorite one. The spices are incredible, the tofu is cooked to perfection with a crisp outside and chewy inside, and the noodles are flavorful. 

I would eat here every single day if I didn't have a dining hall plan. Try any (or all) of the noodle dishes with tofu!

OK, I've been up and down the North Shore looking for the ultimate beef sandwich from Beverly to Lynn, Revere to Melrose and I was elated to have one of the BEST roast beef sandwiches I've had right in  my own backyard. The beef was as warm and tender as anything I've had outside of Nicks' Famous in Beverly. I also got a decent if not memorable slice of pizza. I noted that they have pizza specials every day after 3:30 that were ridiculously cheap but go for the roast beef. It was quiet when I was there but the staff was super efficient and friendly. I'll be back to try the fries next time!!

We were here for the barcade in the back. The two bouncers working in the front were extremely rude. When checking ids, they told my male friend that he looked drunk and asked if he could jerk off in a straight line while looking into their eyes. Like WTF.

Extremely unprofessional. I would suggest for the business to replace these two immediately before they make a complete fool of the business.

Never coming back.
Great place for a late night dinner or weekend brunch. Service is excellent, ambience is chill. Order the fried plantain chips with guacamole, or the homemade doughnuts with fresh raspberry jam.

Worst subway ever. Jerk let me stand on line for several minutes while he served someone and then told me and one other customer he was closing.., 10 minutes ahead of time. Most inconsiderately operated eating establishment I have ever been to. If someone is going to be the last customer, tell the people who get there BEFORE they waste their time

Horrible management. Waited 30min for pizza and throughout manager just said 5min more until realized that they were processing delivery orders. Really not an issue but should have let me know before they took order or at least realized they were doing online orders for 30min and not lie it's just going to be 5min. Used to be good - don't go!

I used to enjoy eating here. Ordered my usual falafel plate. I was in for a big surprise. They did not include the lettuce, hummos, and taboule. They told me they no longer serve taboule and if i want hummos i had to pay an extra charge! Thought this was temporary. But worse. In addition to the missing items they no longer serve bread. The rice tasted awful. The falafel were bitter. Had to throw away my plate.

CONS: Overpriced, slow and overcrowded at lunchtime. I have been outright ignored at the pasta station on occasion. Always playing Headline News. 

PROS: Yogurt parfaits are delicious and a good value. Soups are mostly good, but their chicken stew always looks disgusting. Sandwiches are good even though they are too expensive. Pasta bowls full of options.

THE WEIRD: The cash registers are in the middle of all the serving stations, which means that you need to pay for your food and quite possibly walk back past the station where you got it. I'm not sure how they control against people who don't pay. Many a time I have nearly picked up a bagel on my way out the door. Whoops!

Why write a normal review for a venue full of culinary sin
When unstructured words can't convey its delights held within? 

From the grilled bacalhau, stewed beans and piri piri pork steaks, 
To the rice pudding and chocolate mousse-you'll never make a mistake!

It's dining space is friendly- cozy (maybe cramped) and very complete
With tables and green wine aplenty to complement the decorative feats.

Though it doesn't seat many, and customer service is what waiters lack,
The food and authentic corn bread is what makes me always come back.
