Any other (like Atasca) to be called Portuguese is simply a joke..
Take it from an immigrant- for Casa Portugal I would gladly go broke!

OK, first off, I know what you're thinking.  Naming a seafood restaurant after a dolphin seems questionable. It is. 

That being said, the service is friendly, they have a late night raw bar that is inexpensive, they make good cocktails, and they have a fun Friday night karaoke! It's an intimate room that is cute and good for a date! 

What more do you need?

I love Mike's! I've always had good, friendly service when I've come here, and have tried a wide range of their cannolis. My personal favorite is the chocolate ricotta cannoli. I agree with other reviewers that their cannolis are pretty stuffed with filling, but I don't really mind that! They're crispy and yummy. 

Also -- this location is no longer cash only! I've walked past and seen it get really crowded, but if that happens the line usually still moves super quickly. :)

I've ordered catering from here many, many times.  Delivery is reliable, on-time, and extremely easy to order.  The food is alright for what it is, an upscale Subway.  If you're like me and don't care for stuffed olive leaves, stay far, far away from the Greek Salad. The Medeterrano sandwich is pretty good, but the tomato/basil/mozz lacks flavor.  I'm not sure why they decided not to add pesto or a vinaigrette to bring out some flavor.

I definitely recommend ordering Sebastian's for catering, but as an individual lunch spot the food is overpriced and unimaginative.

Loved it. Lived up to the hype and then some. I'll never forget the Cannolis or the Boston Creme Pie as long as I live. Oh and the staff was super friendly and helpful, even when dealing with a group of tourists from California who didn't know what we wanted.
great concept but the results are just ok. pizza was good, you get a personal pizza that will fill you for lunch. a good price for the area but not outstanding

This fairly new place in Kendall square isn't as good as it could be. Sure.. the square is dead aside from business hours where the local office workers on lunch keep it lively but honestly, that's not a reason to keep such crappy hours. They are pretty much only opened for breakfast and lunch. And that is a shame considering the way array of food that they have there. It's actually quite unique: they have stands: a salad one, a rice  bowl one, a pasta bowl one and a sandwich one. Oh and yeah, the crepe one. Depending on what you want to eat you wait in line in the stand where they custom make your food on to order and then you pay and enjoy it.

I've actually tried a few items there, from the pasta and rice bowls to the sandwiches and the crepes. They taste decent although I think that they are way too expensive for what they are. The whole place is a ripoff. I had lunch there with my girlfriend, and we each had a so-called entree and a drink and it cost me over $20. Sacristy I say!

The service is kind of slow, especially since they cook everything to order in their "stands" so if your in a hurry, I'd head over to Cosi next door. I had to wait like 25 minutes in line and then another 10 while my food was cooking to order. It's kind of annoying and the portions aren't the typical bang for the buck.

It's a fun place to try, one in a while I guess. The atmosphere is pretty cool and they have windows everywhere which helps cure winter depression!

REVIEW FOR DRINKS ONLY

The ginger cranberry caiprinha was ok, although the balance of flavors was a little off. I'd come back again to give this place another shot.

Decor is very nice, great place for a date or a drink with friends.

My first review on Yelp.  I've been visiting this site for some time now, it has become the site I visit when looking for a good eat.

Sofra, the exterior hearkens back to a shop from the 1950s.  I live about a half mile from it, but it took me a while to try it.  I've eaten there twice now and am glad I did.  The egg sandwich was excellent, one of the best I've had, the haloumi cheese perfectly compliments the flaky roll.  The Shakshuka a delicious combination of egg, curry and tomato, thick like a good tomato soup and just as tasty.  The bun they give you to sop it up so tasty I had to have several more.

If there's one or two things that prevent me from giving it five stars, I would have to say the tables are too low to eat comforably and it's always crowded on the weekends (what good breakfast place isn't?).  The line to order is a big discombobulated, and people getting fixins for their coffee are bumping into people clearing their plates.  

Overall, I would say this is an excellent place; I'm eager to try every item on the menu.  Walk there if you can, the parking is limited.

I'm use to the busy original location in the North End. Always packed, cash only,and big display cases of pastries to choose from. 
 I stopped by this location on my way home,and was shocked to see two display cases with not even half of what the original store has. I was "greeted" by two (what seemed to be) college students that were not happy to see me. Oh, I forgot to mention I was the only customer in there.
 I had to greet them,and no picked out 2 canolis and 2 pcs rainbow cake. They had to ask me if I wanted a box (how else would I carry this home?) And I said yes. I paid, and got my pastries,and even left them a tip in their tip jar. That got both ladies to say "thank you" 
 I was excited to hear that Mike's was in Harvard,but the store is really tiny. Maybe get friendlier staffers and maybe if they have more than 2 display cases,they would be more busy down here. I might just go to the North End, even if I have to wait in a crazy line, just to get better service.

This place is incredible! We've loved going here for years. As others have mentioned, it's a tiny neighborhood place and great for takeout, but a few tables to dine in. Everything I've had is delicious - pastas, pizzas, and especially the calzones! The bread and fillings are perfect - all fresh vegetables and quality cheeses, not second grade chewy mozzarella. Their antipasti plate is also a favorite. The owner is lovely and very hospitable. Highly recommended! 

Note - cash only!!!

Roxy's is a fun place that does a few spins on grilled cheese. There are limited tables inside but there are seats facing the window along the perimeter- I can see it getting really busy during lunch time. 

Grilled cheese menu isn't super extensive like other artisan grilled cheese places but we tried the mighty rib and the double fried (a chicken sandwich). 

The mighty rib was packed with meat (which I love) and the bread was perfectly buttery and toasty. I think it could've used some more cheese, considering this is a grilled cheese place. The double fried was perfectly crispy and flavorful- the sauce was a little too sweet, but we just added some sriracha to it and it was good. 

The two sandwiches combined were a little over $17. They came out really quickly- perfect, quick, lunch spot!
Delicious food, especially the soup. Awesome service, very kind people. Mailed me an item I lost there no problem, were very polite about it. Would recommend to everyone to try!

The food here is absolutely delicious! My personal favorites would be the chicken kabob and definitely the falafels. In fact, after doing "falafel hunting" in several places, i think Fava Bean has the best falafel I have ever tasted! The side sauces are tasty and nice choices of salads. Every time eating here or ordered a take out, i know and I can taste that everything is freshly made. It can be ADDICTIVE! Definitely would recommend to friends. Workers here are friendly and polite too!

Great Middle Eastern brunch place/bakery. This shop initially started out as a wine shop which was expanded into small cafe. The wine store can still be found in an adjoining room. 

If you go in on a weekend this place is sure to be packed and finding a place to sit will be difficult. They have a small parking lot, but more likely than not you'll have to find parking on the street. Thankfully parking in that area is free.

The food is good and definitely different from normal brunch fare. They serve middle eastern pastries and dishes like falafel and shakshuka instead of the usual pancakes and waffles.

You have to love this place for its charm. It has a real "neighborhood" type feel as most patrons are regulars. The inside looks like it hasn't been altered in 20 years. I love the Buffalo wings, and I try them everywhere I go. This place knows how to do them, real crispy skin and lots of hot sauce. Lip smacking good. Some people come here to catch up with old friends or play Keno. No beers on tap. The mixed drinks are strong. I like the Long Island Ice Tea. Everything I like about this place I can understand why someone else would hate it. It is way off the beaten path so don't go if you don't mind being around almost nothing else.
As far as Indian food goes, it's pretty good. Could be a bit more spicy. Would have given 3 stars, but the girlfriend liked the vegetarian options.

I met up with a bunch of Media Lab people here for a friend's birthday. I was curious about how Cambridge would do Portuguese food, since that's one cuisine that SE Massachusetts can do better than Cambridge.

I was pleasantly surprised that my fish, egg, and rice dish was tasty, spicy and very delicious. The thin fried potatoes were a good side dish, followed by a house red wine.

Decent.

I think that is the best way to describe it.  I just feel like you could find this same exact restaurant in any suburb across the nation.  Nothing really stands out about this place.

The food is good, but not great.

The atmosphere is nice, but not overly so.

The waitstaff is competent, but not engaging.

It's like this place is.... just.... there.

Having said that, I will most likely go back.

I like this place. They have good unique and flavorful thin crust pizzas by the pie or slice. They also have good sandwiches,  fresh salads and italian sides. I have not had the calzones but I have heard great things about them. Everyone I have taken here RAVES about it afterwards. 
The service is great, friendly and will make anything custom for you. 
They make you feel like family but cook better than mine do!

Great pizza! Unbeatable price!

I am not a roast beef fan, so I cannot comment on that. When I come here I stick to the pizza. Tried a salad and the chicken tenders once... eh, nothing special. 

But... the pizza... the pizza is the BEST around!! The small size is big enough for a few meals for me, its got a nice thick crust with those bubbles that poke through a thick layer of delicious red sauce and some crispy cheese. If you like green peppers, they cook them perfectly crisp on top... YUM!  Oh, and this size is always under 5 bucks with topping!

PS. Staff is super friendly and always chipper!

I hate to do this to you Tanjore, but it has to be done. 

The bf got food poisoning here from the Chicken Vindaloo. There is absolutely no question that it was the Chicken Vindaloo, from this restaurant, as it is the only thing the entire day that he ate that I did not eat, and I did not get sick and he did.

In addition, as an aside (I wasn't going to write this at first because it's one of those "it happens" things, but seeing as it might be related to the above incident, I will). I've been here twice in the past 10 or so day, and both times, there have been bugs (fruit fly size, slightly fatter) flying around that zeroed in our table when we got our food. Given that no part of this restaurant lies outdoors, and that the bugs were not an isolated incident, I thought it was worth mentioning -- particularly as many have mentioned the buffet (yikes).

Bye bye Tanjore.

I treated the SO to take-out last night to celebrate a new job. We ordered through my favorite menu aggregator, foodler.com . 

I found the selection to be excellent (all the Thai dishes I like in one place!), the prices were average, and the order showed up EXACTLY on time. Like, to the minute. That never happens!

We started with Tom Kha with chicken (SO's favorite. I am more of a Tom Yum gal), and SO was very enamored with it. Then we split the Massaman curry with tofu and the Spice Pad Thai. If you are a pad thai fan--and let's face it, every American who tries Thai food one time is-- try the Spice Pad Thai. It was a fun change-up to your typical pad thai; wide noodles like you'd have in Pad See Ew and fresh baby shrimp along with the chicken. I really enjoyed this dish, more than I usually enjoy pad thai.

Massaman (sometimes massamarn) curry is my favorite Thai dish in the entire world. I am definitely the type of person who orders the same thing every time I go to a particular restaurant. This isn't because I'm not adventurous, I am. I just love the things I love so hard. Massaman curry is that thing for me at Thai places, and this did not disappoint. If it had been a little thicker and had the potatoes been a little more cooked, I would not have complained though.

All in all, we were very pleased with our meal and will definitely be ordering from Spice Thai again.

The lobster ravoli was delish. The atmosphere was great - quiet but perfect for a Tuesday night date. We sat by the open window and it was perfect. 
Excellent bruscetta - the toast it was on was so buttery. I will definately go back - would like to check it out on a Friday/Saturday night for drinks and appetizers. 
And the music selection is alternative, which is just my style.

I love a good cannoli, and don't get me wrong, the flavors are delicious, but that is once you can actually order. This place is just a hot mess. I came here on a weekday evening and there were only 10 or so people in this small store front but the employees couldn't communicate if there was a line or whose turn it was to order. In addition, there were only 5 cannoli left in the case and they only told some customers that they could fill any cannoli shell to order. I ended up ordering a pistachio cannoli and as soon as I bit into it the girl after me was told she could order custom. I hate to say it, but the more hectic and much much busier north end location has a better process in place than this one

This place looks like it desperately wants to be something that it isn't. The prices were low enough that I convinced my friend to grab a small pizza here instead of walk to the square and get what we knew we would like elsewhere. 

We were two of 6 people in there tonight, and were told we could seat ourselves where ever it was we liked. We sat by the window and ordered waters which came quickly, we decided to get the pepperoni + black olive pizza which sounded just as good as the special pizza of the day, or any others listed. 

The service was lacking, I found. It seemed cold, apparently. It was not even close to something you could call busy, but we asked if there was any kind of bread or anything that we could have while waiting for our food to arrive. It never did, we reminded the waitress we had asked - only cause we were starving. We got more water and the pizza arrived shortly there after. 

The clientele is almost the polar opposite of whom I choose to surround myself with - from the scene we saw tonight, anyways. I was revolted by the conversations that were loud enough to stop my friend and I in mid conversation. This just may not be the place for me.

Oh... How was the pizza? It was OK. But not $11.50 for a small pizza OK. The jalapenos on the pizza took away from much of the taste. The crust was nice and thin and the sauce was ample enough to make the balance but I've definitely had better pizza on many occasions served by nicer people surrounded by less skeezy people. It's a damn shame you can't stumble by here late night and grab a slice anymore. What is this city becoming?!
Great food .   Great customer service.  A great variety of food.  Highly recommend if u are in mood for Mediterranean.!

You can do much better for Indian food in this neighborhood. (Like Cafe of India.) This place has little atmosphere and the food isn't anything to write home about. 

It's nice to have a South Indian option, but the dosa I had was nothing special. The sauces, both the sambar and the coconut chutney were especially lacking.

I enjoyed this place alright, but I certainly could have gotten more band for my buck somewhere else.

My sandwich was on rosemary foccaccia (pretty good) and featured roast beef (mediocre) provolone (A-OK), lettuce and tomato (average). The tomato aioli they used stole the show, and may be the single reason I come back again.

For a decent-sized sandwich I paid $7.86. Not bad, but there are better places around, so I don't think I'll be back soon unless there are few alternatives.

Small selection of grilled cheese spealities are great.   Had the Caprese with cheesy tots.  The sandwich was perfect combo or melted and crispy.   The tater tots were my favorite really crispy with decadent gouda cheese sauce.

I love this place. Not fussy. Friendly staff and great prices.  I loved this place so much for lunch that I went back again in the same week for dinner.  Check out specials on $1 raw bar on Wed. and Thurs.  And I love how they give you a little discount if you sit and eat lunch or dinner at the bar.  I tried the chowder, which was some of the best I've had.  I also had the fish and chips, which, like other reviewers have noted, is a heck of a bargain.  I could've eaten another piece of fish...For dinner I had the scrod and it was more than I could eat.  
The only downside to this little place is the bathroom!  You have to walk down a dirty hallway to get to the bathroom...if they spent a little time and money to spruce of this back end (yup, that pun was intended) this place would be a jewel from front to back.
Don't hesitate to bring out of town visitors here.  Get yourself here, too, if you are looking for a nice, reasonable lunch or dinner.
Terrible shwarma. Super salty chicken, stale bead, brown lettuce, bland sauces, overall fishy, nor fresh flavor.

Two words: old school.

That's what this place is, and in a good way, though. 

I've passed it numerous times and today I finally stopped by. Anytime I go to a new place I've never been to, I start with the basics. If they pass this tryout, then I branch out to other types of dishes. 

Anyway, today's tryout was a basic Italian sub with everything. I was pleasantly surprised to find they used different types of Italian cold cuts, while other places load their Italians with salami and mortadella, Marco's goes one more with a meat that is similar to proscuitto (or maybe it was proscuitto, not sure).

The sub was a good size, well loaded with fresh veggies and the bread was fresh. I asked for mayo and they also added oil and vin as well as oregano and that's always a nice touch.

So, this place has successfully passed my test and I'm willing to try out their other offerings, like pizza and maybe chicken fingers. 

Service was what made the place "old school" because of the OG Italian dudes working in the kitchen area and arguing/busting balls with both patrons and other employees. I can see how some people may be turned off by this but that's how it is for places like this that made a tuna wrap for your grandfather and will make a calzone for your grandkids.

Give it a shot, I like this place better over their competitors across the street, Cambridge House of Pizza.

This place defines the meaning of "hidden gem" when it comes to places to eat.

It's tucked away around some office buildings, literally a "hole in the wall", but that doesn't prevent the food from delivering on a big level.  If you're looking for a great lunch spot in Cambridge, then look no further.

While you probably haven't heard of Senegalese food, it's definitely something you should try.  Every day features a new special, like the yassa (a delicious dish of chicken and onion with a hard to describe mix of spices that you'll just have to try) and  a chicken peanut butter stew that has just the right amount of spiciness or items like the beef shawarma which are available every day.  On top of that, each of these plates is served up with rice and salad, so you can have a nice balanced meal that doesn't drag you down throughout the afternoon.  If you're looking for something more portable, you can always get a shawarma or felafel wrap, among other things, which are also quite good.

It may be hard to find, but when you do, I promise you'll be back.

I work in the area and frequent Bytes.  I've only ever had their daily curry special, and every time it has been delicious!  It can be super super spicy, so definitely ask for a taste if you are sensitive to spice.  The owners and help are very sweet and kind.  The only downside is they don't take credit cards.  It is hard to find, but not hard to get to once you know where it is.

I just love this place.  It's a bit of a drive for us, but we come often anyway.  I especially love the simple grilled seafood dishes, but have also enjoyed chicken and pork dishes.  The fried potatoes are delicious and I love the bread and little dishes of olives.  

I like the service, friendly but not long-lost brother friendly and well paced.  I also love the flan and the meringue and custard dessert, but don't always have room for them.   Portions are more than generous and it's a great (GREAT) place to go with a group and share.  Soups are yummy, too.  Good wine list.  Moderate prices.

Can be crowded on weekends, so make a reservation.

Went here for lunch on a day off and OH. MY. GOD.  Tastiest food ever!  I got the sesame, walnut, butternut squash and mozzarella stuffed flatbread, which sounds odd, but was AMAZING.  Somehow all the elements worked together perfectly, which is not surprising, given the chef (her main restaurant, Oleana, is known for quirky but awesome ingredient combos).  The bf got their spinach and 3 cheese flatbread, which was also delicious, but not quite as memorable.  We had a mezze (small side dish) of Armenian bean/walnut pate, which was great, though it was topped with some type of sweet syrup, which is different from how they do it at Oleana, and a bit odd.  The Earthquake cookie was delicious (if not messy as hell) and a huge hit of dense, chocolatey goodness.

The one major drawback is the seating setup.  I appreciate the low, round tables/benches and the ambience they create, but it seems like a very inefficient use of space (they could easily fit several more seats in there to help with crowd control), and the setup caused my poor, 6'3" boyfriend to have a very sore back by the time we left.  I think that next time, we'll take our food to go and enjoy it at home.

I thought that this place would be at least decent considering the reviews I had read but boy was I wrong.

I ordered a chicken broccoli ziti expecting the pasta dish to come in that traditional creamy Alfredo sauce. I even went as far as to ask the lady over the phone and she assured me that it did. Of course, it didn't. After taking way too long to arrive, the pasta had no sauce, unless you count the puddle of grease it was sitting in. Needless to say, I was highly disappointed.

I might give them a chance in the future but definitely not with anything pasta related.

Craving Mediterranean food, I came across the Fava Bean, which is innocuously located in the food court of a shopping mall. While the food court itself screams of 'fast food' with the likes of panda express, chipotle, and other commercial outposts, The Fava Bean was anything but! As I got in line, I witnessed food being freshly preparesd and professionally handled. I ordered the chicken and vegetables with yellow rice and was served a more than fair portion. The food, itself, tasted great. The chicken was well prepared and spiced appropriately. The veggies went well with the meal and the rice was flavored just right. The hummus I received on the side was some of the best I've tasted, on par if not better than some sitdown Mediterranean restaurants. If you're in Boston, give the Fava Bean a shot as you won't be disappointed.
This place is absolutely delicious and has such interesting menu items. Even before arriving to Boston, I made it a priority to make sure I got to come here, I am so glad I did.

When I am craving something healthy I come here for a nice big salad.  I really like that all of the ingredients are fresh and there is a wide assortment of what you can include in your salad whether it's protein like chicken or salmon or lots of different veggies.  I must admit that the prices here are a bit expensive so I don't come here very often but when I do come I am satisfied with all the fixins on my salad.

I have yet to venture into the pasta, sandwiches, crepes because I am such a fan of the salads but eventually I will!

I'm so happy to have found another location for Mike's Pastry that doesn't require a 40 minute train ride. Phew! Sometimes you just need to get your hands on a cannoli or my new favorite, the black & white cookie, and this small location fits the bill.

I stopped by on a weekday evening for 2 black and white cookies - which I believe were $3.50 a piece - and there was no wait even though a lot of customers were standing inside. I was acknowledged fairly quickly, the transaction was over before I knew it, and the cookies were consistent with those at main store at Hanover Street. The cashier wasn't overly friendly or a sourpuss (as others have described), but just  cordial. I don't have have any interest in anything but cannolis and these particular cookies, but the selection is noticeably more limited here. 

In all fairness, I've only ever had black & white cookies from Mike's, so I don't have anything to compare them to, but they were perfect as far as I'm concerned. They weren't overly heavy, never dry, and had that signature texture between a cookie and cake that everyone looks for. Overall, the price seems slightly high for a cookie but doesn't bother me much - this is a small family business and people have to make a living.  For the quality and size of the black & white cookies in particular (about 5 inches in diameter), the prices seem fair.

This location isn't even half the size as the original store in the North End and doesn't offer much space to enjoy your treats, and for me that's the biggest downside. On a rainy day, it'd be nice to pop in and enjoy something with a cup of coffee as that's kind of my weekend thing to do. Still, the location that's about as convenient as it gets, a few steps away from the Harvard Square T station.

All things considered, this is a nice new store for locals who want one of Mike's classic treats without heading over the water.

Remember CASH ONLY!

Now that I've moved out of the 'hood, I will definitely miss their $7 (with tax) large cheese pizza special... not the best pizza I've ever had, but you can't really beat that for a quick & cheap dinner! I hear their roast beef is pretty good too, though I'm not a fan in general.

Yummy and healthy. The best place where to get Fresh, healthy and Tasty food. I highly recommend this place If you are looking for food promoting maximum health.
...Also the prices are GREAT 
I hope to see you in different location

I want to start off by noting that I'm basing this review off Marco's pizza and service; as I've never had any of their other menu items. 

Marco's offers quality pizza. I mostly get their plain cheese because it's my favorite, but I have special ordered a tomato and basil pizza which they happily made (and tasted delicious).

The men who answer the phones, make the deliveries, and work the counter in the little North Cambridge establishment are all nice, friendly, make efforts to satisfy their customers. 

Case and point. One day after a snow storm, I cleared my car, called to order a pizza and walked down to pick it up in my snow gear. I didn't have any cash on me because I knew they took credit cards; well when I went tp pay for some reason their machine was having troubles. Rather than making me wait and my pizza getting cold, the gentleman asked if he could try to run the card through again later, took my contact information and sent me along my way. And indeed later, he was able to successfully run my card but I was able to enjoy hot pizza in the comfort of my home. 

With the pizza being so tasty, and the service being good, I imagine the rest of the food is up to par too and believe me I'll be working my way through their menu in the coming time.

This place is the bomb. I had the Italian Sausage Pasta plate and loved it. It is a great little nook in the wall and somewhat hard to find. The set up is fun and geared for both groups and individuals. I went with a group of about seven friends. One ordered the Margherita Pizza and another got the Pulled Pork Pizza. Both praised their orders. It is also situated in a nice spot on Mt. Auburn Street. Definitely go if you are ever in the area.

I walk by Sebastians every day, and waited a month or so after it opened before I tried the food.  It always seemed packed with people and I couldn't figure out the flow.

THANK GOD for Online ordering though!  I haven't ordered a single thing from inside the cafe.

I've used the online ordering system for salads about a half dozen times, and about 50% of the time something has gone wrong.  Either they forgot dressing, ignored my requests on the form, or the salad sat too long before it was scheduled to be picked up.

But when they do it right, it's great!  It is a little pricey, but I can justify it to myself if I haven't had a vegetable in a long time.  2-3 Servings of veggies in the one container.

The south Indian combo was not good enough. The medu vacation was small and looked like processed and out of the box. The idli was dry and chutney was cold. Very disappointed. Maybe their other dishes are good but I just had south Indian and it was not authentic.

I am generally a big subway fan, and because I usually enjoy the brand and appreciate the options they give me I am giving this place 2 stars instead of one. But, as many reviewers have said before, these people can't get an order right. And they go way too crazy on the condiments. You're much better off taking your own sandwich to the reading room.
Drinks/appetizers.  Drinks were 5-star, appetizers were 3-star.  For example: fried zucchini with dip - too much "fry" taste, too boring "dip" taste, and man does not want zucchini alone.

First of all, the name of this place alone was enough to keep me away. I know it wasn't their intention, but I immediately pictured a poor dolphin caught in a fish net, which unfortunately, happens much more often than people know. 

Putting the name aside, my friend invited me there for lunch the other day and I had a pretty nice meal. I had a standard ceasar salad which can sometimes be hit or miss, but this was a hit. But...an item on the menu named 'Dolphin Stirfry' had me cringing. (yes i know it was fish, but that poor dolphin flashed before my eyes)

I'm sure most people know that seafood and especially fish is not a very sustainable food source. But people love seafood! So I think that it's especially important for restaurants that serve a substantial amount of seafood to make their businesses sustainable in other ways. Energy use, composting, recycling, earth-friendly chemicals and products.. there are so many ways business can do this..and they can get certified as a Certified Green Restaurant. 
I think it's worth looking into!

I finally ate inside of Spice on a double date.  I know people knock them, but I really like menus with pictures of the food.  Sometimes it's not enough to tell me that I'm getting mango curry.  Just show me the damn food -- am I getting chunks of mango, or mango juice?  These are important questions.

Back to the review.  Spice is an average Thai restaurant.  I disagree with most of the reviews here, I didn't find any of the food particularly spicy, or seasoned relative to other Thai places in the area.  Portions, though, are large and prices reasonable.  Service was good despite the crowd.  We waited twenty minutes to get seated here, and I don't think the quality of the meal was worth the wait.

They are closed!! 
After trying to make a reservation for a couple of days, with no one picking up the phone...we decided to go ahead and go there and take our chances without a reservation.  At 6PM on August 4th (Saturday night) 2012...they were closed up tight and dark.

Great little very well hidden gem. Very tasty breakfast sandwiches and if your looking for unique cuisine this mom and pop serving food from Sengal is a great stop. Owner operators couldn't be nicer and customer focused.

Dude, you know what I'm craving right now for lunch?

Exactly. I live right next to this place and I never it until yesterday. This is straight up comfort food right here. I was having a shitty day, but the flavor in the beef shawarma made me feel so cozy. It was like a big ass teddy bear came up to me and gave me a hug. No homo.

There isn't any room to sit, but the idea is to just eat outside or take your food back home or work. Sure, I made a mess eating it on the bare carpet, but given the color of the carpet I don't think anyone will be able to tell. The succulent beef shawarma just melted in my mouth, and I must be crazy, but I'm thinking they used beef tenderloin for this. I wasn't aware that you could get shawarma spicy or mild, but I went with the spicy and was pleasantly suprised to find out they din't just put some sriracha sauce on it and wrapped it. It was spicy and a bucket of flavor. The veggies and greens were fresh as well, and you got the expected crunch from the crispy green lettuce. 

All around, I was highly impressed with what they could accomplish in such a small space. I'm disappointed that their hours don't extend a bit more into the afternoon/evening and that they are closed on the weekends. But I don't mind taking a 10 minute drive from work to go back home and enjoy some delicious food.

The owners are super friendly, and they genuinely care about your experience. Don't hesitate.
Cozy environment, pleasant staff, the food was deliciously flavorful. Well presented and seasoned meals.

Definitely a hidden gem! As soon as we walked in, the owner greeted us and gave us samples of the food. The food was flavorful and was seasoned very well. The deli itself is small and therefore easy to miss, and there is no inside seating, but there are plenty of seats outside.

A roach problem, that's what Spice has. Yes, I'm sure that other restaurants have roaches too, but those roaches aren't roaming around the restaurant.  I've eaten mediocre Thai food at Spice for years, but the last time I was there, I noticed something scurrying across the wall (I belive "scurry" is the only appropriate verb for roaches).  I called over the waiter and said "excuse me, I think there's a roach on the wall." He picked up a cloth napkin and scooped up the roach. No apology, no "please excuse the roach you just saw crawling down our wall," no nothing. I can't eat at Spice ever again.  That roach has killed the whole thing for me.

I am strangely a picky eater. I tend to be afraid of foreign and exotic flavors. Why is that so strange? I spent a decade growing up in Poland and Dubai. As a kid, I'd go on long-weekend trips all over Europe and the Middle East. You'd think that though all of this, I would have developed this sophisticated, worldly taste for new and exciting flavors. You'd think... but you'd be wrong.

When I started working in a lab in the MIT University Park area, I found myself walking all the way to Central or Kendall to eat lunch- dumb. I didn't even notice this little hole-in-the-wall called "Bytes@University Park." As the story goes, the man who owns this place opened it about 10 years ago, before that he used to sell sausage at Fenway. While he doesn't sell Fenway franks at this place, he does pay homage to his home- Senegal.

Bytes has a disproportionately large menu compared to its size, and they update their daily specials, well, daily.  They've got fare ranging from comfort food like subs and wraps, but they continue to move further and further from my comfort zone- on to Mexican, middle-eastern and eventually Senegalian (is that a word?) 

I usually only go there to grab a Diet Coke and take it back to work to eat my Lunchables, even then the staff was incredibly nice and friendly. They only got more friendly as I got up the courage to try some of their food (remember- picky eater here). I started with the meatball sub. It was a little on the bland side, and MESSSY. But I figured I should try something a little further from home there. So i took another baby step- chicken fajita. The fajita was pretty good once i removed the few things that i disliked. Apparently they put this chopped tomato and cucumber chutney in pretty much everything they serve. I'm sure some like it- but i really dislike both of those items. I gave them a shot, but eventually had to remove them. They were too crunchy, and the flavor of the cucumbers did not go along w/ the Mexican. The chicken was good, and the sauce was a little sweet, a little spicy, and a lot tasty.

I'll definitely go back, maybe next time i'll venture a little further and get a shawarma. My goal is to go all the way and try one of his signature Senegal selections.

I'll keep you posted.

Delicious fast-casual yet trendy pizza! Slightly pricey but worth it for the variety of pre-made or self-made topping options. I tried The Bella and The Walley and enjoyed them both, with a slight preference for the Walley. Based on past reviews, I was nervous about how thin or "cracker-like" the crust would be but to my surprise, it was pretty substantial and not as thin as I thought it would be! (So don't be scared off by those reviews.) Next time, I'll try making my own pizza - they have a lot of cool toppings that I haven't seen at other DIY pizza joints!

For mall food, this probably deserves five stars. I got the falafel wrap. All of the vegetables were very fresh, and the falafel was clearly homemade by the irregular shape. Good portion size, solid flavor, price consistent with the other shops in the area. Will visit again.

The server was very friendly, and gave me a big helping of kofta (like a spiced middle eastern hamburger). I chose crunchy fresh lettuce as a base- but you could also choose couscous or rice. I added red cabbage salad and diced cucumber tomato salad- both fresh and delicious, and added some spicy sauce, they called bisbas, and tahini on top.

Coming from New York its been hard finding these gems like SOFRA!! The turkish breakfast is delicious and perfectly portioned, my favorite is the labne that comes topped with a cherry jam. Expect to wait and bump elbows with people, but honestly that's just part of the charm to the place.

I just can't justify spending $11-$13 on a snack. The first time I went to Oath (Davis location), the pizza was good, but so thin-crusted that its more like flavored flatbread that you would receive for an appetizer or starter at a restaurant. I was definitely hungry for a real meal later in a few hours and I'm not interested in buying a meal for full price and then another full-priced meal an hour later.

My experience today was ordering through Snackpass so I can't really put all the blame on Oath-- it's partially the Snackpass app. But oath did decide to partner with Snackpass so they need to figure out their order terms with SP. When I ordered, the app told me my order would be ready in 15 minutes. It's going on 45 and I'm sitting here waiting for my pizza. I would leave except I paid a few bucks for it using my Snackpass rewards (otherwise I would not have shelled out $7 for the half pizza as it equals half a snack).

Big disappointment!  I tried this cafe for the first time expecting to love it based on Yelp reviews and was sorely disappointed.  I got the Topik and an Americano.  First, the coffee guy tripped and spilled one quarter of my Americano on the counter while barking out my name. Now the mug isn't big to begin with and down a quarter cup - really - with no offer to replace.  Second, the Topik - I think the cooks are allergic to lemon, garlic, olive oil, salt - any flavoring or spice  that would lift this dish to something tasty.  For $6 you get one ice cream scoop of chick pea/potato mush.  One or two pistachios, one piece of apricot, few almonds and spinach add up to a tasteless blob.  The only saving grace was the dollop of tahini on top which was good.  The small side of greens had not been picked over and I was given two brown creased wilted leaves out of eight or nine pieces.  The place may have atmosphere (although it strikes me as so many middle aged + yuppies trying for hipsterdom) but my meal did not inspire a return visit.

Spice is the only place where I wanted to eat thai food in college. It was actually almost always my pick for restaurants no matter how fancy the occasion... the pad thai is AMAZING! I don't think it could be more PERFECT! It's slightly juicy and a little lemony and SO great. I would go with my guy friend in college and we'd each order our own plate of pad thai and split an appetizer. At any other thai place, I'd recommend sharing all dishes to try lots of things... but here, I was all about the pad thai EVERY time!!! Our usual appetizer picks were the spring rolls or chicken satay which were also good but nothing compared to the pad thai. 

The tables are pretty close together, the lights are a bit bright, and there is often a wait for a table. I'd do just about anything for that pad thai though!!!

I never write reviews but I had to sign up for these guys.  Went there twice- first time I did not know any better.  The second time, well, shame on me for not learning the lesson the first time.

I asked for the $5 special of the month, which is currently the western egg and cheese.  The woman proceeds to begin making it, and then halfway through mentions "just so you know, you can only but bell peppers and onions on it."  Wait, what?  "You can't put other vegetables on it, you can have it just like it shows in the picture."  Amazing.  Apparently they were losing too much money on lettuce and tomatoes.  

The second time I went in, I ordered the $2 6 inch customer appreciation month cold cut combo.  I have never seen anyone skimp so much on the vegetables in my life.  I asked 3x for more olives.  At the end, there were about 6 olives on my sandwich.  Time to pay:  $3.75.  Oh, but every subway EVERYWHERE has this for two bucks.  Nope, not here.  

These are the cheapest, rudest subway owners I have ever met in my life.  Disgusting.  MIT students:  please please help put them out of business so we can have someone else that won't try to screw over us students.
Always out of white bread, often out of turkey. No care for customer service. They seem to not actually care about you enjoying the food or experience.
Quickly seated. Food ordered and brought out quickly. The best steak I've had in years. Shrimp was also tasty. Desert was on it. Service was quick, polite, and efficient. Great atmosphere.
Somehow still my FAVORITE Mediterranean spot. Call me crazy but I still prefer Fava over Aceituna or Tahaza. Their falafel...holy moly. All day.

A pizza place that has gluten free crust AND vegan cheese -- and both actually taste good! Oath is a winner in my book.

I've been here a number of times in the past few months, and it's been reliably good. You can choose from personal pizza options, build your own pizza, and a couple salads, if you're feeling veggie. But it's really about the pizza. My friends particularly enjoy the Spicy Mother Clucker, the David, and the Whole Hog (if you're in the mood for decadent meat on meat).

I usually do the "make a selfie." You can order a half pizza (3 medium-small slices), which is fine if I'm not too hungry, but it's more cost effective to get a whole one and save the rest (if there is any, that is). There's technically a limit of 5 toppings, but sometimes they'll let you slide and add more.

The Italian sausage, pickled red onions, roasted garlic, and balsamic drizzle are some of my favorite add ons. Because of the quick, high heat baking process, some toppings can dry out, like the chicken, white beans, and spinach.

GF pizza can taste like sawdust, but here the crust is thin with still a bit of a chew. Plus, whatever vegan cheese they use actually gets gooey -- as long as they put enough of it on the pizza. For $12-13 (no extra charges!), Oath is a much cheaper option than Otto or other places with GF options. I'm just not sure if the place is celiac safe, given the open kitchen, although each pizza is baked on its own pan.

This location has a good amount of seating, but each time I've been, it's either pretty packed or kinda empty. Usually the wait isn't too long (under 10 minutes), but when it's crowded you can wait up to 20-25 minutes for food. At least there's free WiFi (if you give your email address)!

There's Mike's Pastry in the North End and I was surprised to find this Mike's Pastry in Cambridge near Harvard Square. It's a small shop and not nearly crowded as the one across the river, but they do have a great selection of tasty treats. I got another canoli. It's still hard to say which is better (I'd had a Modern one the other day) because this one was crunchy and well filled with a rich cream and then dipped in nuts. Just delicious and only a million calories.

There's not much going on in Harvard Square that can't be found in any other mid-sized tourist town center across the country. Your choice of overpriced and underperforming bars, restaurants, galleries, trinket shops and the like, all vying for your dollars before you leave town or return to the dorm.  Little value. Less parking. 

That's not to say that the nearby Harvard Yard is not a terrific place to walk and contemplate American history and excellence in education. There, at least, you can get a big bang for your school tuition bucks. It's just that the same can't be said for the circus sideshow that inhabits the surrounding commercial zone. It all feels rather decrepit, even on sunny days. If franchised consumables are good enough, then you may feel right at home here. Then again, the same names are available with free parking out at the malls. 

Times Square and Piccadilly are far more entertaining, and do decadence, grime and glare so much better. Harvard Square is little more than a big disappointment with expensive parking and little honkytonk.

I had a great time here getting the Sweetie Pie special. This was like a thicker crust version of the original cookie dough pizza. They did a great job with the thicker crust as well; the texture was light and fluffy. This special is a must-have!

I feel like most of the negative reviews for Harvard Sq (most, not all) are from folks of a certain age for whom its hip to hate on gentrification, for whom only up-and-coming or down-and-out neighborhoods are cool.  Harvard Sq is less and less for hipsters and more and more for real students and thinking and working adults and families and visitors.  For my money, Harvard Sq is the best at what it is in the city.  

Compared to the other college-centric areas in Boston which other has this many bookstores?  This many coffeeshops and cafes?  This many avenues of public expression and live music?  This many nooks and crannies in so few square yards? The architecture is lovely and right on the river.

For those young and restless in society, please do stay away from Harvard Square. Its certainly not cool enough for you... and how could it be? This neighborhood houses one of the country's top schools; its probably not where you'd enjoy hanging out, so... Leave it alone to the Cantabrigian residents and families who enjoy it, as well as the tourists and serious students.

Sebastian's Cafe has a similar setup as an Au Bon Pain. They are a cafe that serve breakfast items, sandwiches, noodle / rice bowls, crepes, salads, and soups. Personally, haven't tried their food yet. For beverages, they have different types of coffees and teas (e.g., Republic of Tea brand). For the teas, they give you hot water but you have to use a tong to get the tea bags. My favorite teas are The People's Green and Ginger Peach.

It's a quiet place to study / work. They have nice, light music playing in the background. However, I feel that they don't have enough electrical outlet to use a laptop. There are about three sets of plugs, all of which are the most popular areas of the cafe and are near the trash and recycle bins.

Who knew grilled cheese could be so exciting? My family wolfed down this food! Our family of 5 had a variety of items and felt well satisfied. Unfortunately, the staff seemed tired - zero interaction - that's really the only thing keeping this from being a five.
The food was great! If i lived in this city i would be coming back. The only downfall was the service was slow even when it wasn't crowded. also the scent was a bit off but overall great!

Got shawarma. Cold rice cold chicken. What's the point of spit rotating in the back if you are making me a bowl with pre shaved cold and dry meat on cold rice. Show some interest while preparing. 

No wonder lines are at other stalls.  Wasted money!!
Wonderful food. The sandwiches were gigantic. Seemed like a pretty laid-back Saturday in Cambridge.
My favorite spot in Cambridgeport been going here since I was a boy. Local as local gets everyone knows everyone can't beat it
Worst subway ever.  As stingy as I have ever seen with vegetables.  They change the rules, change the pieces of meat you get.  They should be shut down.

Wow. Strong pastry game!!! I head stories about lobster tails in Boston but always figured Brooklyn could compete. Dang. These lobster tails blew my mind. They are Huuuuuge and full to the brim with cannoli cream!!! I got 2 cannolis and a lobster tail, I think the box was 20lbs. 

The cannoli cream is super smooth and is thick like whipped cream cheese. It was great in both the cannolis and the lobster tail. The lobster tail was definitely my favorite though, the crust is flaky and super hard/crunchy, but then soft and doughy in the inside- perfection.

The peanut butter cannoli was also pretty good, but too sweet for me. If you are a peanut butter fan this is definitely for you. It had a lot of powdered sugar so the cream was suuuuuppper thick. I would maybe stick with the originals- they are solid.

Either way I was satisfied and thought it was pretty good! The lobster tail is where it's at.

Went in today for the lamb kabob sandwich.  Amazing.  Enough for two people.  Delicious marinated sliced lamb with eggplant and veggies.  My new favorite place for sandwiches.  The pizza looked really great as well.  Reminds me of the tiny restaurants in the North End.  Super cozy.

Do yourself a favor and tell your worst enemy that this is the best Thai food in Cambridge. The only purpose of this "restaurant" as far as I see it is to punish any fool you know and the tourists of Harvard Square. This is the worst Thai food that I have ever had. Sickly sweet. Gloopy. Sloppy. No Spice. Place feels dirty. Coconut soup tastes like sweet baby formula. Wait staff is aloof. One hit my friend with the food she was delivering and didn't notice she stained her shirt with sauce. Fun time. Travel half a mile in literally any direction and you'll be better off. Thai Hut in Somerville and both Thai places in Porter run circles around this hole.

Best roast beef in the city.  I try to eat here at least once a month.  The hot super beef is freaking amazing.  Get a large order of fries to share and you are all set.  The rest of the food is great too, pizza has always been good.  I order the roast beef and my eyes never wander.

I have been saying this for two years at MIT. This is the "worst subway in America." This is obviously an exaggeration, but the sheer number of reasons why this is a below average subway is staggering.

Stale bread, stale cookies, broken ice machine, broken credit card machine (they'll tell you once you get to the register and its too late), disinterested employees, skimping on vegetables, not having the bread you want, and I could go on but the rest of the reviews here are pretty comprehensive (and have really validated what I always knew to be true).

I think the worst part is that I still come here a lot because there are so few good options @ MIT.

Excellent customer service and delish food! The staff was very friendly and highly accommodating. I unknowingly walked in here thinking I was in a neighboring restaurant, but I'm glad I tried these guys out. I will def be back!

Yum! I like this place for many reasons: the options, the fresh food, made to order,  the amount of seating, the crepes (You really can't beat a Chicken Cordon Bleu Crepe).
On the flip side, it is a bit pricey and sometimes you just want a set menu.   I do not want to have to decide what will be in my salad or in my pasta.  Quite honestly, if I wanted to make those types of decisions, I would go to the grocery store, buy the ingredients and make it myself.  I want a defined list of interesting options then I pick one.  The sandwich and crepe sections have set  combinations; this is what makes them great and the only sections that I visit when I get lunch here.  The lines in these sections might look long, but move fast.  

I would like to try some breakfast sometime- looks like it has potential for breakfast greatness.
When will the ice machine be repaired? It has been broken for a week. And the soda coming out of the machine is not chilled. Warm soda is gross.

Like many of the other reviews indicate, the service here leaves a lot to be desired.

I was in Boston for a work trip and really craving the Portuguese food of my childhood (grew up in CT and subsequently moved to the South were there are no Portuguese restaurants/grocery stores/anything).  After going through an ordeal on Boston Transit and walking what felt like several miles to get to the place, I walk in the door, just bubbling with excitement looking forward to sitting down, relaxing, and enjoying a pleasant meal. To my surprise, the lady servers look up at me like I'm some homeless guy off the street and offer me a seat at the bar (which is very sloppy, cramped, and uncomfortable... the bar was littered with junk and what looked like a phone book right in front of where I would be eating).  I look around and see at least 2 or 3 nice open tables and I'm like "Can't you just seat me at a small table?"  They said "We are busy and need to keep tables open for larger parties".  Literally NO ONE else was waiting for a table.  I say "How much do you want me to spend to get a table?"  And the lady rudely gestures to say "Ok go ahead and sit here".

I ordered caldo verde, pork and clams, and a glass of wine.  I will admit, the food was really quite good.

However, the servers were inattentive and rude.  Also, the food took forever to arrive.  The servers seem to be running around like chickens with their heads cut off.  They were clearly showing preference to the Portuguese-speaking local pork chops fawning allover them.

I didn't even feel like having to engage the server to ask her for the check and have to sit around waiting for her to bring it.  I just plunked down 80 bucks and walked out the door.  Will not be returning.

Not sure how this place gets such great reviews unless they are faked.  For $9 a large Italian sub with "everything" on it includes a couple slices of meat, onions, lettuce, and tomato.  No cheese, sauces or other typical toppings.  It was my first time eating here and I likely will not be going back.  The kitchen looked dirty and there was a bunch of old cardboard hidden behind signs and whatnot.
This pizza made me want to call my dad and call him my mom because my dad once told me about Marcus Pizza. 10/10 would put the Pepperoni's on my head

What makes Oath Pizza stand out among other build-your-own pizza places is the avocado oil they use in their dough. I highly recommend the David - the ricotta and balsamic vinegar pair perfectly with the sausage and mushroom. If you have a sweet tooth you can't go wrong with the cookie pizza. The melted chocolate chips oh my goodness... Shoutout to the store manager Anthony for the recommendations.

Located in Harvard Square, I'm glad I came to this location instead of the one in North End, because the lines were ridiculous.

Service was very quick and there was maybe two people in line before me. I got the classic Chocolate Chip Cannoli ($4), and was surprised at how hefty it was. I liked that the cream wasn't super heavy, but I feel it was a little tooooo light in flavor (but maybe I'm just uncultured). I had to search for the flavor intensely, and the overpowering taste of the chocolate chips didn't help either. 
I was glad the shell wasn't soggy like some reviews said - quite the contrary, mine was super crispy and tasted fresh, but along the middle, I feel like the shell to cream ratio was a little too close. 

All in all, I think it was very good, but definitely not amazing as some people have expressed. Definitely want to come back to try the other flavors!

This bakery is amazing. Their cannoli selection has such a great variety ranging from simple ricotta to flavors like pumpkin spice and Nutella. Although they are known for their famous, and quite large, cannolis, their other desserts do not disappoint. They offer lots of cookies, pastries, and even coffee. I'll definitely be stopping here again next time I'm in town.

One of my favorite go-to spots year-round (and during covid). Great sandwiches - I'm a fan of the chicken parm and buffalo chicken. Always ready really fast, usually about 10 mins for pickup. Friendly people behind the counter. What more can you ask for?

A solid Mediterranean option in the CambridgeSide mall and wider Cambridge area. Truthfully, I didn't have high expectations for Fava Bean, being Mediterranean mall food, but it was better than expected. They have a real shawarma that spins around, not just "chicken shawarma" that is made on a grill. They also offer kufta kebab (ground beef), falafel, and chicken kebab (grilled chicken) as protein options. 

It is somewhat like chipotle, where you can select a protein, toppings, and sauces, except with more limited options. You can also order either a bowl or a wrap with the bowls having a salad or rice option. The food tasted fresh and was on the larger side for portions. All the food is halal if that is something you might be concerned about. Overall, a solid option to try out if you're not into fast food at the mall!

Ngl, I'm definitely partial to this local grilled cheese chain. My go-to has consistently been the muenster club, with applewood smoked bacon, guacamole, peppered turkey, and chipotle mayo.

A side order of the truffle tots or tomato soup (slightly on the saltier side but goes great with the grilled cheeses) is a must, and my personal guilty pleasure (as if grilled cheese isn't enough by itself haha) is the rotating cereal shake! Love when they make it with fruit loops!

Overall, a must if you're craving a hearty meal!

If someone is visiting Cambridge and is near mit then this place is located at a central location right on the main road. The place looks nice with ample seating and modern interiors. The concept of the place is unique however the implementation is not good and hence the output pizza does not taste good. One can select a full or a half pie, I opted for half pie and ordered a chicken pizza. As a rule, the sauce is applied first on the base and is evenly spread however in this case, the person kept the cheese on the base then the chicken and finally with a spoon placed sauce in two places and it was not spread at all. I am sure if it was well made the pizza wld have tasted nice.

My boyfriend and I absolutely love it here. The vegan grilled cheeses are DELICIOUS!!

I usually get the Vegan Allston which is a grilled cheese (daiya cheddar) with fig jam, caramelized onions, and arugula. It's always so satisfying and yummy. I've also had the buffalo tofu melt in the past, but I find that one to be pretty variable in terms of taste. Sometimes it's great but other times it's incredibly vinegary or salty, making it nearly inedible, so I just steer clear of ordering that now.

My boyfriend also loves the vegan grilled cheeses as well as the beef burgers! He says they're very tasty. 

We recently tried a vanilla milkshake from here as well--OMG! Just like Wendy's LOL (that's a good thing!).
Yummy food, no line no wait. Not the same ambiance but food was awesome! Service was great and was right in Harvard's Square, nice place to walk and shop.

Somehow, despite all the intense/extreme reviews for this place, we had a really lovely dinner here.

I had purchased a Groupon deal for a lobster dinner for two--this included two lobsters, a shrimp cocktail, and four sides. The Groupon rules stipulated making a reservation, and since we decided to go on a Sunday night, I called half an hour ahead of time to make sure they could accommodate us. The manager was very warm and helpful, and when we arrived, we were greeted by name and seated at "the best table in the house".

Our shrimp cocktail was brought to us as soon as we sat down--six good-sized shrimp and some mild cocktail sauce (my companion thought it was pretty tangy, but he's from Chicago and doesn't know better). The shrimp were fresh and tasted like they'd never been frozen. Mmm!

If the shrimp were fresh, the lobster was practically crawling off the plate. I do love seafood, and vegetarianism is not my bag, but I still had a half a moment's pause when confronted with this possibly alive critter on my plate. Never one to be daunted by a gastronomical challenge, I broke through my emotional barrier and went to town on that delicious crustacean. Wearing a lobster bib was a must for this messy eater, and I came away content, full, and extolling the virtues of this Harvard faculty-rich eatery--seriously, I did feel underdressed in my sweatshirt and jeans next to all those vests and bowties.

The sides were underwhelming, but it's not like I was there to eat salad anyway. More lobster please!

I was in the need to grab a quick breakfast before heading to a meeting at MIT.  This place is AWESOME!  It's a cafeteria style place, wher eyou can order fresh salads, sandwhiches, and egg / omlettes.  They offer an assortment of fresh fruit, pasteries, yogurts, fruit juices, etc.

I had the fresh fruit yogurt parfait with granola ($4.00).  YUM!  I thoroughly enjoyed this item!  It was a generous serving of granola topped with yogurt and fresh strawberries and blueberries!

One minor complaint about this place (which stopped me from giving it 5 stars) was that the cafeteria staff weren't the sharpest kids on the block.  My colleague ordered an egg omlette and had to have the lady re-do her order two times.  Finally on the third attempt, the lady got it right.  I think the workers here haven't yet mastered the English language (but they work sooo hard!).

Went for their wings and wasn't disappointed - got the wing dinner with 6 wings, fries AND onion rings.  Great "homage" onion rings and breaded fries.  The fries are crispy on the outside and soft on the inside - perfect.  The quantity was generous and the price was right.  Friendly staff!!

Ordered a Pastrami wrap 30 minutes before a conference call from my hotel room about a mile away.  It came super fast and I didn't have to interrupt my meeting to fetch the delivery.  It was so good and prompt I ordered  couple pizzas (Hawaiian and Pepperoni and sausage)  for dinner.  Food came very fast and it was still too hot to eat when it arrived.  I will definitely be back.

Service was polite and quick. It's made to order. Minimal seating. There were a decent amount of menu options too. I went with the steak bomb. Meat portion was plentiful and tender. The bread was delicious too. It was super soft. I wish the meat was a bit more seasoned but, it did not disappoint
Hate to give this review because it will just attract more people to a great spot.  Absolutely love Sofra, i never leave disappointed. Great food & drink.
Great food, the choices are amazing and is made by order so the perfect touch is always applied

It's a grilled cheese place with a great vegetarian grilled cheese menu but I eat meat.  Luckily Roxy's serves meat and does it well.  My fav is the mushroom Swiss burger.  Cooked well, mushroom and swiss melt togther to make hamburger heaven.  And truffle tater tots to die for!   Prices are average for a mid-tier burger joint.  Service nice and very efficient.

If you're looking for some meat amongst all the vegan/vegetarian options near MIT try Roxy's.

Food, 5/5
Atmosphere 1/5

I came here and got a lamb shawarma and split a pistachio baclava with my fiance, the food was incredible. The shawarma was the best I think I've ever had. My fiance got a lentil durum that was amazing and the baclava was both excellent and beautiful. I would absolutely come back if I was getting takeout. The cashiers were slammed but our cashier still managed to be extremely warm and friendly. A+ on service and food! They have beautiful middle eastern desserts lining mismatched cake stands and the light in here is gorgeous. They have lots of middle eastern sodas too that I've never seen before, along with local baked goods and local food (like chocolate tahini, I didn't even know that was a thing). It's a great location because after you've just overeaten you can go stroll through Mt. Auburn cemetery and enjoy some foliage and views.

In terms of atmosphere though, YIKES. You go in and order your food and then go sit down. Easy enough right? Well not so easy when 500 other people have the same idea. We could barely even get in line it was so crowded. I went outside to grab a table because the thought of eating at one of the cramped tables inside (surrounded by people trying to wait in line) made me claustrophobic. So this next story isn't really Sofra's fault, but gives you an idea how competitive seating is. There was a line for a table outside (there are only 6) so I waited for one while my fiance got our food. When one opened up a woman tried to run in front of me to grab it and I had to go "excuse me there's a line" at which point she goes "you don't even have your food yet!!!" and a couple other people in line had to say "we're all in line!" The whole dining experience was so claustrophobic competitive and stressful I would only get takeout here in the future.

So I came here after my uncle who is an MIT alum told me he brought my aunt here on there dates when they first started dating.

I was in Boston for work and I was in desperate need of some chowder and a lobster roll... I mean... It's Boston. That's a requirement isn't it?

I sat at the bar since it was just me and the bartender was very attentive and helpful. She knew exactly what I wanted when I told her that I was just visiting. 

The chowder was very good. Lots of seafood in it, unlike another restaurant in town that I went to later. Just what I expected for the chowder. 

The lobster roll was even better. I didn't really even know what I was ordering, I was just told by everyone that I needed to order one while I was in town. But it was great. There was a generous amount of lobster in it, and the fries that came with it were great too. Nice and crispy like they should be. 

Overall. Great conversation. Great service. Great food. I will be back.

Call me crazy, but I like Harvard Square.  Yeah, yeah, there are chains, but there are also still some gems to be found: Crema Cafe's a new one; Hidden Sweets is fun for gifts, cards, and, well, sweets; the Abrano Collection/Baak Gallery for jewelry (depending on the size of your wallet); Boston Tea Stop for bubble tea (my newest obsession; Upstairs on the Square, Cardullo's, Schoenhof's ...I could go on since I've worked right outside the square for the past two years, but I'll spare everyone my rambling.  In any case, Harvard Square still has its share of good independent/non-chain shops and is particularly charming during the fall.  If you don't like students, well...you probably shouldn't be in Harvard Square.
Today is definitely my last time coming to this place. Workers at this place do not keep their hands clean while prepping food.

The food here is fast and delicious! If there was a place like this in my hometown, especially near my job, I'd be a regular for sure. 

As you add on things, it can get a little pricier for lunch. Such as the falafel, meat options, etc. But, gosh, so delicious and fresh and healthy.

Love that they have a bunch of options and sauces to choose from. 

I splurged on a baklava dessert, as well.
Noms!

Would go back here in a heartbeat. Probably the best food court mall food I've ever had!!

Tried their food truck near south station for lunch. Their grilled cheese sandwiches are really good. Tried their 3-cheese melt with fries. If ur into grilled cheese, its a must try! Prices are good too.

What a local gem this place is!  Recently stayed with friends in the charming Huron Village and ate here three times - which says something given the choices that abound. If you like eggplant parmigiana you are in luck. Both the meal and sandwiches are outstanding. I especially loved the crispy bread they serve. 
Everything is homemade. Did I forget to mention the pizza?  Thin crust that is hand spun with fresh toppings that run the gamut of your creativity. Enjoy!!!

subway's usually does a pretty good job of training their employees, but one managed to slip between the cracks and she happens to be employed at the mit location. i mean isnt "the works" common subway's parlance? they have a sticker on every freaking subway sandwich making station that you can ask for "the works" and they'll pile on all the veggie ingredients. also, when you order a 6 inch, aren't you supposed to get about 6 inches of sandwich rather than some pathetic leftover loaf edge that barely satisfies the hunger of a 2nd grader? i will not be going back here. as sad as it is, i'd rather patronize la verdes.

A friend had highly recommended this bakery. I decided to check it out in the evening, an hour before closing time. There was a lineup when I arrived. Due to covid, you aren't allowed to go into the store, but they have a booth where you can order. There is lots of outdoor space to dine in.

The line moved pretty fast. Taking one star off because pretty much everything (all the pastries) were sold out. I decided to try to the iced chai and the tahini chocolate tart. 

The chai was very sweet for my taste but after a few sips you get used to it. The tart - I was a little surprised at the small size however it tasted AMAZING.

Will likely be back in the morning to try some of their more popular items.

Everything I've tried here has been incredible. From their chai to their falafel to their incredible desserts, this place is an absolute treat. I recently tried some of their rose syrup in cold milk it was an amazing treat!

The pizza is good, tasty, no problem there. A bit pricey. The customer experience is lacking bigtime. ModPizza is much better. At Oath you order all your ingredients at the register from the overhead list, whereas at Mod you walk along and pick and choose what looks fresh and ask for more or less. When you are done, Mod puts it in the oven immediately. However at Oath they will print out the order and you hope they dont lose it. We ordered 2 pizzas to-go, each custom to our tastes, and one came out in 10 minutes. Fine. Then 3 other customers get their pizzas. Where is my other one? I go to the counter and the guy is justing starting to make my second pizza. His excuse was "I'm the only one making pizzas". But wouldnt you still make them in order? So ten+ minutes later we get our second one. We had started eating the first before it got too cold, was supposed to be To-Go. Both pizzas were cut in the middle but not out to the outer crust because the guy cutting has his arm in a cast.  Sheesh. No common sense. No apology. So, No return customer.

Oh my gosh, they're INCREDIBLE. The pizza, the sandwiches, the pasta, the salads - all amazing in house or for take out. Favorites: pesto gnocchi, all the pizzas, chicken parm, eggplant parm, lamb kebab - as a salad or sandwich! Amazing food, always ready fast! And their veggie sides are always a delicious add on!
Wow, this is incredible. The flavour is immaculate; never tasted anything like this before. The way it just melts in your mouth is really exquisite.10/10 will eat again.

I am ever seeking the best chicken parmigiana sub!

I usually go to the Cambridge House of Pizza but I wanted to try some new places! Hands down so far The Village Kitchen has the best tasting, hot chicken parmigiana sub! I also went to Mamagoo's! And I've been to Andrea's in Watertown.

First the bread  was crispy, good quality, crunchy and delicious. The chicken was real chicken (ie not like "fake" or frozen or rubbery) I mean it was crispy and hearty chicken. The marinara sauce was just enough and so was the good parmigiana cheese. I could always use more sauce but no complaints about this one. Lastly, it was a hot and every bite was good! 

I can't wait to order again. So thankful to have a great place that makes good chicken parmigiana sub! It's one of my comfort foods! One of my favs!!

Exactly what you would expect from an old-time diner. Lots of locals, friendly, looks like a hole-in-the-wall from the outside. Very good prices. 

Fast service, seems like fresh food. We will be back again and again.

The best espresso in Massachusetts.  They have tasty biscotti as well.  Importantly the place is clean and is not overwhelmed by smells of meats or cheese like many of the other cafés in the area. They have cold brew on tap;  source their milk from local farms (none of that Hood or Garrelick chemical cocktail that many of the other cafes use); play suave music at a volume that is not overwhelming; and provide glassed water to accompany hot drinks.  Makes me proud to be a Cantabridgian.

This place is overhyped. The food is mediocre at best. If this is considered "the best sub shop in Boston" then Boston has some terrible sub shops and some of the actual good sub shops from the burbs should set up shop. The reason for the three stars is the amount of food you get for a relatively low price compared to the rest of Cambridge where your easily spending 10$-$12 for one little sandwich.  Also, they are really fast at getting your sandwich to you.

This place is super efficient; they have their system down pat, and the women working behind the counter was very nice and helpful. The sandwiches are huge, though not overflowing, so not too messy to handle. We came in with a group of teenagers and they were more than happy with the size of the sandwiches; as a grown woman, I took home half of my small steak Bellino sub. Great deal for the money.

Me and my wife came to Moksa to have some sushi. Though it is rather diffucult to make something non-traditional in the sushi-world, I'd say Moksa have success in this direction. Their rolls are both original and delicious. But Moksa's cocktails are much-much more off the mainstream. I like the level of sofistication they are introduced, as well as their taste, of  coarse. Moksa has also very original appetizers and entrees, but we'll hold them until our next visit. Overall, it is very reasonably priced nice restaurant for people who got tired of traditional dishes. Very good addition to OM at Harvard square.

I really like the bacon cheese burger. Really really like it. I have to say the more familiar I became with this place it dictated where I sit. I sometimes want to sit with the boys at the bar, or if one of my favourite waitresses are there I will sit at a table with some friends and enjoy some pints and some dinner. 

Every night brings something new. Recently every few months brings a new employee. I really enjoy how laid back this place is, and that people aren't pretentious - or if they are, there are hardly enough of them to make an impact here. 

I love The Field. Big time.

If you're in the Central Square/MIT neighborhood and in the market for some quality breakfast, consider Sunny's Diner, hidden behind Sunoco across the street from Novartis (the old Necco building).  If you've got an appetite like mine, you'll do well with any of the omelettes or the Gentle Giant (save the pancakes for dessert!).  If not, craft your breakfast or brunch from the a la carte menu.  No surprises, no frills, but excellent potatoes and service.
Cheap and close by to Random Hall (MIT dorm).  Good place to go for breakfast with friends if you're still up tooling and the sun has already risen.
Strongly recommend 1369. Coffee is great and the vibe is excellent - laid back and comfortable it is a great place to sit down with a laptop and work. Check it out.

Friendly baristas, free wifi after 6 pm, and petsi pies' scones? What more could you want?

(Ok, maybe a bit more seating, but c'mon, it's inside a bookstore.)

Solid bar experience based on food, ambiance, beer, and waitstaff.

The menu is simple and straight forward, and the food itself is plentiful and tasty - significantly tastier than I have had at some more expensive brewpubs in the area.  I've been on a hunt for fried fish in this city, and the Field offers a strong contender.   

The ambiance is captivating, with a thick collection of trinkets on the walls, there is plenty to keep you visually interested while eating, hanging out, or just enjoying a quality beer.  

Speaking of beer - I am biased because of proximity - but I was exceptionally pleased to find out that they have Murphy's Irish stout on tap. Yum and delicious - almost a meal in itself.  The beer list is not huge, but it has a well curated range.  There are enough beers on the list that the waitstaff recite it fast enough that you have to concentrate to pick a beer out.
Best yogurt parfait ever!! Super friendly staff, great coffee, sodas, and lemonades!! Great sandwiches as well!

As someone who lives within 5 minutes of this place, I've been anxiously awaiting its arrival. The owner Israel is super nice and chatty and makes it feel like home when he's around. Also - the front windows open to the street when it's nice out, which gives it a nice airy feel to it.

The coffee is right on. I've been on several different occasions now (and so have had the opportunity to try a couple of different blends) and am very pleased with them all. On their non-coffee drinks, I recently had the chai with house-made almond milk. SO GOOD. It comes unsweetened, which is such a nice change from all of the super sweet pre-packaged chai's that you'll find in other cafes (not all, but some).

We also recently had a chance to try to food. I had the turkey club sin bacon. Nice and flavorful, nothing out of the ordinary, although the avocado aioli on it was quite good. The side of cabbage and pickles was a very nice touch - not too much as I first thought they might be. Pastries aren't really my thing, but I did try a flour-less brownie that was surprisingly rich and decadent. They always have different pastry offerings that seem to change throughout the day, so if you're there for a while, you're able to try a few new items as they come out.
