We could hardly eat our breakfast because it was cold. It was the last meal we were sharing with our parents before they left town and it was ruined. Will not be back to this place.

1369 is pretty much the best around.  I'm not a coffee drinker (although I do enjoy a mocha from time-to-time), but I am a tea drinker, and they also have a great loose tea selection, including White Peony (my favorite).  Food is yummy and reasonable priced. Atmosphere is warm and easy-going, with a good amount of seating, and open late.  VERY work/laptop friendly (i.e. free wifi, lots of outlets, good lighting).  But the staff really take the cake, always with a friendly smile and witty conversation.  It's my home office away from home.

cheap and a very neighborhood bar, everyone stares when you first walk in but eventually dont care, can be intimadating at first but no ones there to cause a problem.  For the area its pretty well known, walk around cambridge/somerville/charlestown and you'll see someone with a paddys 34 sweatshirt on eventually.  Theres a microwave but i dont think theres a kitchen anymore.

I'm originally from San Diego so my burrito standard are high. This place is one of the few shops in Boston that have a salsa bar with pickled carrots and radish!

The food here is probably one of the best (for Boston) in regards to quick Mexican food. I would say their only con is that there is always a line.

Note that sometimes they open both sides for food serving.
The coffee is just okay, seating is limited, and why the heck does any place keep the front doors wide open on a cold day in late November?

I walk past this place everyday and have been pining away for opening day. Finally, the "soft opening" happened a couple of weeks ago and boy was I excited. The space is beautiful! The setup is a bit confusing, but whatever. I've learned. During their soft opening, they had a limited menu (no macchiatos) so I had to go with the cappuccino. It was really disappointing -- tasted like I was drinking milk. And it was really sweet, so I think it was soy (not a fan). The staff was super nice though, so I chalked it up to opening day nerves and have been back a couple more times, but only ordering macchiatos after that. The macchiatos are great! They're not strong at all, but still very tasty, and not milky! I wouldn't drink one if I was in dire need of a caff rush, but for my 3pm macchiato fix, it does the trick. Also tried the banana cream puff this weekend and it was legit. Coffee is definitely overpriced ($3.40ish for single macchiato), but it's so close to my apartment that I have little choice :)

I love the stand up bar (perfect for espresso drinks). Wouldn't recommend this place for hanging out too long, only because it's super crowded (it's just plain rude to hold 1 of 6 tables for hours when it's so busy & people are sitting on the *floor*) and very limited table space (although they're working on that I think). I agree with the other reviewer about the electronic screen -- unnecessary, waste of energy (lists like 10 items that don't change daily. hello, chalkboard?)

I do recommend dwelltime bc of the great staff, good coffee (if not a tad weak and overpriced), and it's so damn pretty. Though if I didn't live around the block, I wouldn't make a special trip here bc there are lots of really good coffee shops in other parts of cambridge that are less expensive. However, dwelltime fills a gap for this neighborhood, and I couldn't be happier to have it so close to my house :) Will be here regularly and hope they don't raise the prices.

People have been raving about Al's and I gave it a shot today. The hot sandwich line was not bad for coming at 12:30...my colleague and I both orders the chicken Parmesan sandwich and were completely underwhelmed. Too much sauce, the consistency of the bread was very dough like and I was even willing to give the grilled chicken a try and it did not live up to expectations. Not my idea of a good sub.

Eating at the diner bar at Sunny's is like standing with your face smooshed to an aquarium tank with your entire 1st grade class behind you.  The wonder of it all!

The two-party staff works this joint like you wouldn't believe.  Never stopping.  One's waiting on patrons, pouring coffee, ringing you up, and cleaning the dishes, while the other is flippin eggs, pressing bacon and deep frying your browns (yep, your hash browns).  The cook, he's seemless and he cooks up perfect eggs.  As we waited to order, I witnessed a phenomenon -- cook cleans off the whole grill and spreads out the oils and proceeds to pour like a PINT of scrambled eggs onto the sizzling grill and scrambles them up.   I'm thinkin whoooaaa...who ordered that?!  Then a third individual behind the counter (let's call him, the mystery man) informs me that they're cooking up breakfast for a private school behind the diner.  AMAZING.

So the food.  Well, it's a diner, so you can expect diner-type foods.  Eggs, french toast, dense fluffy and LARGE pancakes (plain, blueberry, or chocolate chip), omelets, coffee (splenda upon request), and corned beef (usually my selection but I opted to be "healthy" and go for the 3 egg tomato olive n feta omelet ... mmmmm).  The prices are good, the music makes me move, the decor is simple (yet the Simpsons characters are a surprise) and it wasn't too packed (despite the tight space).  Quite possibly my second fave breakfast place in Central Square (and there aren't many that are open before 8 AM). 

Will need to try for lunch and will obviously return for breakfast.

This is our go-to place when we are looking for a healthier take out option! I take the chickpea fritter most of the time and it's really good! The rosemary fries are awesome as well!! The place is nice and they even serve beer now. Everyone I brought there was really impressed!
Great service and delicious food with good prices!  Will definitely be a regular customer here.
Great bar. You can watch EPL games on Saturday and Sunday mornings. The bartender is great even though he seems really aloof. Good drinks and good food in a nice atmosphere.
Visiting from Chicago and will definitely come back on every visit to Boston. Charming bartender, Irishman Seamus, made me feel right at home. Great pub!

I love this place! I've been there at least 6 times in the past 2 years.  I was a little upset when they changed the menu but the prices are still pretty reasonable and I'm always happy with the food.  Also, the staff is pretty friendly which is a big deal to me.

Dwelltime, your food is excellent and your coffee is good, but some of your staff have an inflated notion of their importance, and since I never know whether I will be condescended to, belittled, or just ignored, I have decided the odds are not good and I will just keep going to Lyndell's or 1369. Both are consistently welcoming, efficient, and easy going.

As they say on "Absolutely Fabulous," you just work in a shop, you know.

This is one of the finest bars in the city of Cambridge. I was rather intimidated by the awning...a bar that appeared as if it never left the 19th century (in a bad way.) 

Once I found my way inside, I realized that I'd never found a place that I felt I was more at home at while swilling newcastles and talking sports. I will forever support Paddy's and their hockey team.

I went there for a Sunday Brunch. It was great!! Truth is, that I was going to visit City Girl Cafe with 4 friends, but it ended up being so small and a long wait. We stumbled upon Ryles cafe and I was blown away!

This is a GREAT Sunday Brunch spot. There was live music, great lighting, nice ambiance, and friendly staff. The service is a tad slow, but the food was great. I had the Croissant French Toast and it was delicious!! My friends had the French Toast with Brie and fig and he said it was great. Another friend said their potatoes weren't the best; but overall presentatino and food was really good. The orange juice was phenomonal. 

Great spot for a small group. Great date spot.

And there's live music every day. I would definitely come back to check this place out at night, although the dance floor looks TINY.

I stopped in here on a Saturday night with a friend visiting from out of town, for "just a beer" as we were walking home from dinner. I really truly intended to only stay for a single beer, but somehow don't even quite know how many were had, whiling away the time chatting about the bar's history and the sports on tv with the bartender. 

Two regulars bought us a round as they were headed out, and the bartender joined us in our last round. At some point, when we were the only customers left in the place, I remember saying to him, "We're not keeping you here, right? I wouldn't want to..." and before I could finish, he replied, "Oh believe me, if I didn't want you here, I'd let you know it."

This place is awesome and unique, and the folks there really welcomed us newcomers, in true Yankee style.

Ok, I'm biased because my ex roommate used to work here...I like the ambiance though I've never been a huge coffee shop fan, the snacks are good, but for me the only reason to go is the cranberry cooler. If that item weren't seasonal, I'd get one every day. Pure heaven in a plastic cup.

It looks right, inside and out.  The clientle fits.  The menu is as it should be.  Yup, all the makings of a true greazy spoon are there.  But somehow the food turns out to be more expensive than is right, and not as satisfying.  The portions, at least when I've been there, aren't especially large and service isn't especially fast and there's a certain amount of grime and sourness to items that ought to be fresher that kind of unsettles.  I'm sure I'll go back some time, since it's close, and I'm due for a good experience, but so far it's been pretty mediocre.
It is a hidden gem! Small but not shabby. Their chicken soup is the best comfort food in the winter of Boston!

Good home-style diner food. The coffee kept coming and the food all hit the spot. The hash browns are more like home-style potatoes but still really good. The french toast was one of the best I had.

Only problem was it took forever for me to get served.

I play a lot of Social Boston Sports dodgeball. The sponsor bar is a well known over priced bar in Davis, which has been listed as a bar you can't step foot in over 30. ::insert blushing emoji here::

So I recently branched out into Monday night Kickball. I arrive after work, and my first question is "what is the sponsor bar". I am TOLD that it's Paddy's....now, this might be a wild and crazy RUMOR, but I said "I have been dying to go there, can I bring chipotle??" 

I walk into Paddy's with my Chipotle in hand and am WELCOMED by green plastic clover table cloths, and a bar seated full of locals. They were immediately friendly and wanted to talk Boston Bruins, despite being discreetly a Black Hawks bar (ballsy move, Cambridge). 

Our Fair City's finest not only welcomed us into their home base bar, but we're a lot of fun. I mean one of the clearly regulars may have been a little handsy, and definitely wouldn't have been friends with John Taffer, but completely harmless and a great dancer. 

The sound system seems to be a boom box with an aux cord and some big speakers, but we listened to maybe Temptations radio. It was so fun.

PBR IS ONLY $2.50!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! 
I got like 7 drinks for $20 (5 were PBR, 2 were a little crafty). Oh and they have a pretty extensive beer selection for a dive. 

In summary: the owner, super nice. The bartenders, super nice. The local crowd, super nice. All welcoming to the kickballers. If you're in the area, stop by for a cold, clean pint.  You won't regret it.

I was skeptical because of the iffy reviews, but my very critical fiance and I actually enjoyed our dinner.  We got the Korean seafood soup which was delicious and packed with seafood.  The wings and ribs were pretty good too.  It's a fun concept and I'd likely come back.

Just so everyone knows, as an update to al the previous reviews, wifi is now free with a purchase. I think it's for a limited time though: 45 minutes for each purchase (if done separately). Also a very good cappuccino, and they have free chess boards in the back to use!

Awesome! all the baristas are great and so much fun...and they all remember my name--not surprising i guess since i'm there every day, but it still makes me happy. 

I love the soft rolls. And the baked goods. 

It's true they have the downside of very little seating--but I usually go at 7am, when there is WAY more seating available.

Yes, its overpriced. But I like the flatbread/brie cheese/turkey sandwich and it keeps me coming back.

It gets pretty crowded and sometimes it can take 10 mins or so to get your food, so watch the clock if you need to be back in the office quickly.

Its a nice addition to the lame selection of lunch choices for those of us working in the Kendall area.

Terri was fantastic to deal with.  I was in a bind for my child's birthday and she was able to help me out with a beautiful cake that tasted as good as it looked.  When I realized the cake I'd ordered may be too small (the day before the party), though she was unable to make me a bigger cake she baked 12 additional cup cakes with a consistent design that were just as delicious as the cake
Fantastic no-nonsense breakfast place. Definitely the best value-for-money place in that part of Cambridge.

I can't for the life of me figure out why this place does not have higher ratings.  My husband and I have gone to moksa at least 12 times in the last six months and have never had anything less than a wonderful experience.  We've gone here for drinks, trivia, dinner out and big events.  The food is awesome (I love the spicy scallop rolls!) but what really sets this place a part is the cocktail list.  Most recently we went to their cocktail pairing dinner ($35 for four cocktails and a three course menu!) and had some of the best drinks we've ever had with an awesome chocolate theme.  There are so many cocktail bars in Boston that specialize in "artisan cocktails" but really never do anything terribly inventive.  Whenever I go out for drinks in this area, I feel like most bartenders make drinks that are either way too sugary or fake tasting, or just plain boring (despite whatever weird house-cured bitters or fermented banana leaf essence the hottest hipster spot is toting) Moksa's mixologist Noon Summers is a true artist.  The entire cocktail menu is phenomenal and is exceptionally balanced between sweet/spicy, interesting and downright tasty.  I have never had a bad drink here and I've probably tried every single cocktail on the menu.  Also, the staff is ALWAYS super nice, attentive and friendly.  In all capacities, Moksa rocks!  Keep up the great work, guys!
Their breakfast taters are great and the coffee is good too. Excellent and cheap, I go here all the time before work. My only complaint is they dont turn up the heat when it is cold out.

I grew up living in the house next to this place. The former owner, Bubby Ryan (R.I.P),  was practically a grandfather figure to me. I have fond memories of this place. I went through a phase one summer when I was like six or seven where I would try to hang out in there. Bubby would give me free chips and I'd play with his dog named Henry. Good times.
The music is great, they have good jazz performance. However, the food is just average. They serve very large portion, however I always prefer quality to quantity.

Sunday brunch review: This is the same as eating at S&S (isn't the menu virtually identical?) with the added benefit of nice music and a less diner-ish atmosphere.  Sunday brunch at Ryles is often a bit chaotic, and always bustling, and they serve enormous portions of just-above-mediocre eggs, bagels, fruit cups, quiches, etc.  Word to the wise- the quiche portions could serve at least two, and I'm certain contain all the nutrition one needs for three days, possibly more.

I stayed at the Marriott across the way and stopped in for a flatbread and a soda. It took a lot longer than I would have thought, but turned out quite tasty.

The staff were pleasant and helpful, and the dining room is pleasant, but otherwise not terribly notable.  There are plenty of other pleasant, polite places in the area, and not all of them are such large chains.

My very dear friend had a horrible experience here as a transgender person that frankly makes me want to tell everyone to never come here again for your own safety or for the safety of others. It is 2015 and it is completely outrageous to publicly humiliate and then kick someone out because they are not using the bathroom you think they should. I would give negative stars if I could.

Bull feathers, I've never written a review of the Field. 

The Field is a classic, simple, no frills, very likely no thrills, pub. There are no gimmicks. There are no fancy-tini lists. There are no knitting nights (at least I hope not). There are no open mic nights. There are no extensive lists of obscure beer. 

What the Field does have, makes up for what it is lacking. There are lot's of fun people at the Field (read that as freaks, geeks and people with all make and model of foreign sounding accents). There are cheap, quick, pub food items (burger, chicken sandwich, hot dogs, etc.). There are all your old time favorite beers and liquors. And, yes, people - yes, there are bathrooms. 

But, why am I a fan of the Field? Well. It's the least stressful bar to go to in Central. I don't feel bad that I look like a regular Joe when I walk in. I feel like nobody gives a shit that I am not wearing skinny jeans and a $75 dollar flannel shirt. And, well yeah....they have my favorite shit beer in a can.

the food at this place is awful.  i ordered ramen, the char siu bun, and the bok choy. i couldn't taste anything beyond saltiness in the char siu.  the ramen had a completely flavorless broth and somehow the berkshire pork had no flavor either.  the bok choy was overcooked and soggy.  i haven't been upset about paying for food in a very long time but i was actually mad that i paid money for this meal.

First time coming to the Saloniki location at Harvard Square! Ordered a Mykonos Salad - was very good and fresh. LOTS of seating/spaces so solo diners can enjoy and get some work done (outlets everywhere). Service was fast -- Jeff and Titanic were very helpful!
 
Will definitely be back again!

The staff is generally really polite and friendly. The kitchen staff are always laughing, singing, and talking while they work, which isn't a complaint. It's not disruptive and it's nice to see people enjoy their job. 

The food: a large selection of sandwiches, both cold and hot, and a daily soup. I wish they had more variety in their soups, but the quality is above average if a little pricey. I'll always choose Cosí over nearby ABP. 

Desserts: ABP has a wider selection nearby, so if you just want some sweets. I must say they probably beat Cosí. But there's cookies, muffins, and fruits if you're on your lunch break and you want to treat yourself a bit.

You gotta try the L.A. Galbi (beef ribs), they were perfect! They were juicy and the sauce they put on had just right amount of flavor. The meat was soft and easy to eat. The dish was so good it makes you want to order another plate. I'll definitely be coming back for this! 

My fiancee had the Japchae noodles and she said they tasted amazing! These clear noodles were sauteed in a delicious sauce. They were able to make this dish vegetarian and replaced the meat with mixed vegetables. As Californians who know good food, we highly recommend anybody try this place. 

-Frank & Diana

Clover is a vegetarian restaurant at the intersection of Cambridge and Elm Street, near Inman Square. Our review is for breakfast. 

The place: Clover has a very cool (in several senses of the word) decor.  It's also an iPhone-lover's dream:  they have creatively adopted computer monitors as displays and iPhones for service orders. (I imagine restaurants ten years from now will use these technologies regularly, but  it is neat to see them in action today.) The open floor plan and view of the kitchen is a nice addition - my breakfast companion, Alie, thinks it's better, in general, to see where your food is coming from...

The food: For starters, we're meat eaters, generally, but we don't mind a vegetarian-only restaurant once in a while.  Alie loves her muffin; my popover is good, but a little dry. The yogurt granola with apple and cranberry confit is very creamy and tasty, and the confit adds a great sweet flavor to the mix. The freshly squeezed OJ is incredible - both Alie and I are having seconds (they are small, but good). The banana muffin is very moist and tasty.  The coffee was just OK; but I'm not a big coffee drinker. 

The people: Service was good and helpful, but we didn't need much assistance.

Improper Bostonian ranked Paddy's Lunch as a Best of Boston 2012 Dive Bar. Should I take offense to that or should I embrace it? COME OVER HERE AND GIVE ME A BIG HUG I SAY!!! This place is as townie as it gets. Used to be that if you were not a local you didn't go there. That isn't quite the case anymore. Granted there is still a slight hint of xenophobia in the mildest sense of the word but no where near what it used to be. Once it becomes cool to hang out at a dive bar it's hard to keep them out. Cough Cough Sligo's... 

Went there for a Disco night. Assumed that it would be busy but we were wrong. Only about 12 people in the place but you know what? We still had a blast! Music was awesome. Bartender is a friend of mine. And they carry Heineken Light. WIN WIN WIN to me!

Paddy's Lunch DOES NOT SERVE FOOD! Ironic I know but deal with it...

Ladies bathroom is a little scary but again deal with it...

Too bright...

Best Chocolate Croissant I've had this side of Thomas Keller's Bouchon Bakery.

Amazingly delicious and served up by some very friendly young ladies.

I will be back.

Often!

Terri is a super sweet woman who has hooked me up with awesome cakes on the cheap!

She made my gross former boss a Celtic's-themed cake for his son's birthday with little notice, and just crafted the bud can cake above for a work birthday. It was a super hit, even though it wasn't actually made out of beer.

Terri is super flexible with her schedule and listens to exactly what you want, then crafts custom cakes that are very reasonably priced, and, I found out yesterday, taste good too! She even delivered yesterday's vanilla frosted madness to me at my office since she fell behind schedule. 

As long as I've got ridiculous ideas for themed birthday cakes, I'll keep going back to Terri to make 'em!

Terri made a cake for my wife's 30th birthday and it was absolutely amazing! She nailed the design and the cake was delicious. People kept commenting on how good the cake was. She was able to get the cake done for me with fairly short notice and it was at a reasonable price. If I need a cake in the future, I will absolutely be returning to Terri!

Great french toast and scrambled eggs, which come with home fries (a pleasant surprise). The guy who owns it introduced himself when I went for brunch on my birthday. He says his name isn't Sunny. And he offered to have the staff sing for me, but I told him it definitely wasn't necessary :).

Small, cozy, and affordable. Not too crowded on weekday mornings if you can make it!

I was a Chipotle guy before my friend introduced me to this place. (I still am, but I will go to Felipe's if I'm in Harvard Square.  Please note that I will be comparing the two as Iove both of them!) The price here is similar to Chipotle.  A comparable meal here will probably slightly less depending on what you get.  Here, they charge extra for corn.  If you get double meat, Felipe's is where you'll get the most bang for your buck compared to Chipotle.  Chipotle uses shredded cheese, but at Felipe's, they melt an entire slice onto the tortilla.  Felipe's also gives you options for burrito size and has options for whether you want cheese or not, so you can save a bit and not hate yourself after your meal.  I haven't tried the nachos although they look amazing (and they're huge!)

Taste-wise, it''s excellent and the freshness of the ingredients is on Chipotle's level.  The guys here are friendly and they seem to know their repeat customers' preferences like the back of their hand.  I'll definitely try the nachos next time!

Tip: If you get your burrito to go, grab extra napkins as it WILL pee on you!

I have eaten lunch here many times and the food is pretty good.

The service leaves something to be desired, though. I have been with friends who say that Cosi has gotten their order wrong more than once, but I have had no such thing happen.

The pizzas are pretty good, though that is a direct result of the flatbread they make them on being awesome. The sandwiches are alright. The tomato basil soup is really good though! Well, as good as tomato soup can be. It's really thick and mmm, good for dipping bread in!

It's a bit better than Au Bon Pain, the equivalent "trying to be fancy" restaurant in the area.

Let's be honest. The only times I've gone to Sunny's have been because Brookline Lunch was too packed. Noone beats that suckah cheapo place. However, I do like this little bastard. Portions are big, they have a good selection for breakfast and it's never been ridiculously packed....and, as a plus, you get to sniff a little gas on your way there (it's right next to the Sunoco).

Really enjoyed the food and the amazing prices but I did not like how they started closing soon before people were cruelly done with their food and even putting chairs on tables. But I would go again because I really like the food.

Went here last night as I was craving some scallion pancakes and crab rangoons.  Definitely some of the best rangoons I've had.  Overall, some solid greasy Chinese food.  I do wish they delivered on Seamless - that is my only request :)

Does it get any better than this. Paddys rounds off Cambridge perfectly. Tucked  5 min away from the square.real people,hard working folks, local people,most are here cause they are not all there.  Good bartender keep your empty glass full at all times. The beer is cold and fresh, the mixed drinks are stiff, overall a good bunch of local people!

People who are talking this place up haven't been there recently, or they'd be giving it negative stars. They're recently started playing a new genre of music. Or, more accurately, "music." I don't really know how to describe it----maybe "industrial noise." Lots of "experimental" crap that's really nothing more than feedback, high pitched tones and electonic screeching. It's literally excruciating to listen to--you can see people with their fingers in their ears, staring at each other in disbelief. Trust me, you won't be able to stand it. No one can.

e Al's. The bread is on point and I believe, made in house. The steak and cheese, pictured (half cos I was so hungry), is awesome and a large is massive. Somewhere in the ball park of 18 to 20 inches! I've also had their meatball which is just as good and a bit messy, which isn't a bad thing. The Tech Square cold sub is also good. Plenty of fresh  mozzarella and proscuto. One word of advice, know what you want before getting in line or risk getting sent to the back!

First of all, the food was delicious.  We had a special assortment -- only $30 for two appetizers, a main course and a greens.  We had excellent food that was served quickly and with terrific service. 

However, you don't go to Moksa for the food.  You go for Noon.  Noon is the magician of bartenders who should be consistently ranked #1 in the area.  Her creations are amazing -- including cocktails that are on the menu and those that she creates extemporaneously.  I always wind up wanting more... which means you should keep cab fare in your pocket...

Go see Noon... have dinner and prepare to be wowed!
The first time for me in Saoniki. It's really a nice place. The Service was fast and the stuff are so friendly specialty Jeffrey & Gayathri

A few friends and I went to Ryles on a Thursday night, the 18+ night (since not all of us are 21 yet). We had no idea what to expect. Right away my friends and I booked it to the unexpected free table of food, then those of us who were 21 ordered some well-priced, well-made drinks. The music was fitting for the type of club, and the DJ took requests.

My only experience dancing salsa is at college parties with my friends who grew up in Latin cultures where dancing was just something you learned to do, so I was amazed at seeing the clearly experienced dancers at Ryles who've taken lessons. It was a fun time because I was with my friends and we had finally found somewhere in Boston to go salsa dancing (not to mention for only $5 entrance fee!), but I'm not sure I would go with only girl friends and I definitely wouldn't go by myself. The crowd at Ryles was mostly 30+, and a few of the guys who asked my friend and I to dance looked old enough to be my dad. For the more mature crowd, however, this would be a great place to regularly go!

Note: It says they close at 1am, but the two times I've been here, they've stopped the music before 12:30am, and the crowd thins out around or before midnight.

The Field is a fun spot and a necessary stop on any pub crawl through Cambridge.  Bar staff is always friendly and there's darts and a pool table to keep you entertained.  Food is nothing special but this is a bar so you're not going for the grub.

I watched the Sox win the 2007 World Series here with friends and it was a great atmosphere.  I'd recommend this place if you're in the mood for a few drinks; conversation optional.

The blueberry pancakes here weren't bad, by any means, but I have had better, and I thought that they were a wee bit expensive (for diner food) at $6. Portions are gigantic, so I had barely worked up an appetite about 8 hours later (after going running). The pancakes are more on the heavier side than the fluffy side, which I don't mind, but some people prefer fluffier.

Bad coffee, especially for a diner. Excellent coffee should be expected at these types of places. The tap water that they served me (essential after a Friday night that went until 5 AM) was warm, despite a couple of sad ice cubes.

I was mighty relieved that there was a NYTimes in the front newspaper basket, as I had come alone, without reading material. I sat at the counter; the service had that pinch of attitude that diners are sort of expected to have- no harm there. However, the cook took kind of an absurd amount of time to get started on my pancakes, even though there was only one other couple there. Also, getting a coffee refill took more effort on my half than it should have. It's OK to have an attitude, but I've gotta have my coffee, lady!

I love the ambiance, however, and might consider coming back some day, but not before trying a few other local breakfast joints.

I went on a weekday afternoon to grab a drink and kill some time. It's exactly what you look for in a dive bar. Sticky tables, unclean bathrooms, and super cheap prices. I think the bartender, as well as the other regulars combing the bar, realized I wasn't necessarily from the area so they pretty much left me alone. Also, be warned that there is zero food there as they do not have a kitchen.
If I'm back in the area, I'll probably take a trip back for some cheap, quick drinks.

As evident throughout reviews, Moksa is not bad.  It's just not that good either.
We sat outside, eating lunch around 3pm so we were the only people in the whole place.  With all of the far-east Asian options in the greater Boston area, we probably won't go back to Moksa.  Nice fusion idea, a bit flat in the execution sadly.
We ordered:
pan seared bok choy - good taste but quite oily
chicken pad thai - pretty bland
Berkshire pork potstickers - OK, not too oily, nice vinger-y sauce
papaya/mango salad - quite good, nice flavors and texture
No drinks, so I can't review that aspect which has gotten reviews from a number of reviewers.  
For $50, I would have anticipated a bit more pop and pizzazz.

While the food is on the cheap end (plus), the crowd and the space exude that nice diner kitsch (also plus), the food is bland (not so plus).  Better diner food has been found, and can be, rather easily. Additionally, the service is pretty rude (that may be a plus or not, depending on how you take your old-school diners). I'd say, avoid this one.

I took my 4-year-old son today to 1369 for an afternoon treat.  The service was fantastic -- they really took good care of us and made us feel special.  However, a little bit into his brownie and hot chocolate and he said, "This is too sweet!"  When a preschool palate judges something that way, I think it deserves grown-up attention.

Homestyle korean food. I love how unfussy it is: there are only a handful of choices in a menu that still covers all the classics, free hot tea, super nice owners and servers, and casual easy atmosphere. Delicious soondubu and bulgogi and bibimbap, great prices.

Nice addition to the neighborhood. Ramen is solid. Better than Sapporo down the road, not as good as Santouka. Gyoza could be improved. For delivery, it would also be nice if the noodles did not stick together. That said, this place has good potential.

Placed an order online after getting a brochure in the mail and was excited to try it. After placing a pretty large order and waiting 20 minutes past when it was supposed to be delivered, I called them. The phone was picked up by a woman who claimed to never have gotten my order. After explaining that I had placed the order she found (while on the phone with me) that she had no paper in her printer so she hadn't received the order. she was apologizing profusely and I said I still wanted the order but asked if she could maybe discount me for next time for the trouble. I was offered a 5% discount- which was the standard online order discount. I asked politely if she'd cancel my order and refund me. Wanted to give this place a good neighborhood try, but this makes me feel not confident in how they run their business. I won't be ordering from here in the future.
Sad to see this place closed. Went here for brunch and enjoyed the atmosphere with the live band/jazz, good service, and good food.

Great place for sandwiches! I came here with a coworker and ordered a small pastrami from their hot sandwich menu. I was a bit confused when I saw my sandwich because it looked like a large but that was it! They are very generous with the size portion. Lastly, the sub was delicious.

Top option for Taiwanese food (with a Shanghainese twist) in Boston. As a vegetarian I can't speak to the XLB, but they custom made an order of san bei (three cup) tofu for me on my last visit, which was one of the best I've ever had. The veggie dumplings made with leek are also excellent, and you won't feel like you're missing out when your carnivorous friends are slurping soup dumplings.

Never, never again will I dine here.

We made a reservation yet were seated 30 minutes late. 
This seems to be the only viable option for decent Chinese food in harvard/central square area but we found so many reasons to not return again,

-- This place was unsanitary--tea stains on the spoon, dried up rice on the bowls, and green-colored food residue were stuck inside the tea cups.

-- Once we were seated at our table, we waited another 20 minutes to place our order. The soup dumplings were ok, but not any better than what you would get in Chinatown. We also ordered eggplant with basil but it was too starchy.

-- After we finished our dumplings, eggplant, and tea, we waited another half hour to get 3 other dishes we ordered and the soup.

-- We were charged $6.50 for rice as they do not come with entrees.

-- They missed one of our orders and we were short of the food we expected to have.

-- The entire time, they never apologized for the inconvenience or the dirty serving ware, missing our order, or for the 30 minute break in between our first dish and the rest of our food. The only thing they kept repeating was, we are busy!

-- And the most shady thing they offered us was to give us a 5% discount if we pay the entire bill in cash so that they can evade taxes on their gross income.

The dining experience was extremely unpleasant and uncomfortable. We were upset we had to pay to have such an experience.

My favorite Clover location, this is where they make their magical pita! Also they have a rotating beer on tap which is most welcome for a late lunch on a Saturday.

My go to is always the egg and eggplant plate, but their sandwiches are  awesome too. The impossible meatball sandwich is a treat, and I recently tried their seasonal zucchini one which was also yummy. 

Service is always fast and friendly and the seating area/tables are kept clean to a shine. 

I also have to point out their focus on local ingredients and on creating food that is environmentally sustainable as well as delicious. Can't beat that in my opinion!

Moksa is hit or miss, and slightly overpriced either way. However, if you can't get into the nearby Thelonious Monkfish, you may still have a pleasant dining experience. Their Chinese Zodiac cocktails, though expensive, are pretty good. Their run-of-the-mill sushi (spicy tuna, e.g.) is just that, and at about $8 a pop, maybe more than I'm willing to pay on a given night. However, I've had good luck with their specials (especially the shrimp and guava roll, delicious!). My boyfriend doesn't care so much for them, but I think they're ok, and better than Yoki, where we've tried both the Malden and Central locations and struck out several times. I would advise against Moksa's spicy edmame, though: not so much spicy, and very mayonnaisey.

1. The coffee is expensive.  Great is not cheap; deal with it.  $4/cup for drip coffee probably seems like a lot if you're used to paying that for what amounts to a hot milkshake but it's not when you consider the quality of the drip coffee.  (P.S. I like hot milkshake coffee too sometimes but this isn't it).
2. This place is dedicated to COFFEE.  If you need a half-caf, skinny, no whip - this is NOT the place for you.  Go here to taste DELICIOUS black coffee with nothing in it.  If you don't like the taste of coffee without dairy and/or sugar, don't bother.  I don't say that to be snotty - it's just that adding dairy/sugar to this coffee is like dipping caviar in nacho cheese sauce - you will not taste the full deliciousness if you drown it out.
3. The coffee is DELICIOUS.  Some of the best I've ever tasted.
4. Their method is laboriously overdone yet, delicious.  That is to say -- my realistic self knows that their method is too much work for me but I appreciate that someone else is willing to do it.
5. The baristas are REALLY NICE and know A LOT about coffee.  They seem so sad when I come in to buy a bag of beans and don't need to talk it over with anyone first.
6. I've never tried them but the pastries look really delicious and I appreciate that they have a decent selection of savory pastries.

This place is too expensive to buy all our coffee here but if we could, we would - it's well worth the high price tag.  We buy about a 12oz bag per month and cherish it in between good but not as great other coffees.

Their service is really bad. The people who takes orders are not clear. The menu is horrible as in they will cheat you. The menu have one price but they charge you something else. The menu look really cheap but be aware they will charge you extra for different meat and guacamole and it's not mentioned ones on the menu or by the ppl who are taking your order. Worst of all there was plastics or paper in my food that when I showed it to them all they said is OKAY as in it was nothing. I would not recommend this place to anyone
The cake we ordered was beautiful and delicious. Moist, rich and not overly sweet. Our corporate logo was perfect! You MUST use Cakes by Terri for your event.

Great beer selection, nice crowd, down-to-earth staff, cheap and tasty lunch specials. Probably dinner and appetizers deals, too, but I'm not sure. The grilled cheese and fries are awesome. It can certainly get crowded here on a weekend night, but there's always somewhere to stand...maybe not sit, however, unless you're lucky and happen to stake out a table. Night parking is not usually that tough on the side streets nearby.

Cash only. Don't conveniently  "forget" that fact and force your friends to buy you beers!
Really excellent organic coffee. At least it tastes organic. The wait staff is chill, like a coffee shop should be and they offer all the staples. Check them out coffee snobs.
Very good Kimpap, japchae and spicy Duk bokki would go back again for sure!!!! The store is bright clean and comfortable!

We had our wedding reception here for a very reasonable price.  The owners, Gary and Aimee, were very helpful on the night of the wedding and kept everything running smoothly.  The only problem is that we had to use the food from the S&S.  Now, I like their food, but it's not exactly what I was wanting for my wedding.  But it was fine enough.  And, the price was very reasonable!
We were able to bring in our own dj, but they would've helped us get one of their regular bands for a good price.
Anyway, it was a great experience and the vibe of the place was perfect for my wedding.

Checked out the new Al's spot today in Tech Sq after I had heard good things about the ones in downtown.

The subs are huge and are a great deal.  A small sub is $7.50 and is larger than what most places call a "large".  The bread is OK, I'm not sure why everyone complains, it's average bread.

I had the Italian.  I've been told to try the chicken salad, so that's next on the list.  The sub was mad within a few minutes and I walked out in less than 5 minutes.

Great price, big subs, decent taste, fast service and a good menu selection.

Dwelltime is one of those places I really want to like, but I just can't. Analog weekends, I get. But for a cafe with mediocre coffee and dull, undersized pastries to consistently disappoint with broken wifi on a Monday at 2pm? Unforgivable. That's the last time Dwelltime's slick decor and easy ambience fool me. Simon's, 1369, and myriad others are a stone's throw away and well worth the walk: better coffee, more pleasant baristas, and consistent wifi.

A place where the townies definitely go. Everyone there is very nice. Prices are very reasonable. Front section has bar seats and high top tables. Back part has darts. They have fun St Patty's Day events. Ideal neighbor place to walk to with friends and relax for the night. I feel very comfortable going there in sweats, jeans, or after play softball/kickball/etc. Bartenders have always been very very nice.

Ok.... I have always liked Al's - their Lady Cafe is my favorite but I have an allergic reaction to basil now so I can't eat it anymore :(

But... I was Craving a fresh sub so they are a solid go to.

I ordered through door dash and the restaurant had them call me to inform me of any extra charge for the sides that I chose - confirmed everything but apparently there was a miscommunication lol.  

They got the order wrong... BUT... It was still good... Albeit frustrating given the call to confirm everything.

The bread is my favorite!  And the sandwiches are huge!

My father is extremely hard to get an 'it was good' out of.  Usually, "it was ok" or "meh - it wasn't that great".

But this piqued his interest and asked the name of the place and location!  With a smile! Lol

Go there, they are great!  Crunchy chewy bread! Love.

BUT they can be super busy around lunch time.  I know the Harvard Square location takes the phone off the hook so you may just need to show up and order.

Enjoy!

I usually love everything about this place.  I usually get an iced chai, which I LOOOOOVE there, and have recently switched to the cheaper iced decaf Americanos which are also QUITE good and satisfying, one of my favoirted iced decaf americanos in town, very like the real thing. Their pastries are also quite satisfying.   Well it was only a matter of time before I said "today I'll get the iced coffee" wow am I glad they don't have an iced decaf and only make iced decaf americanos or I would have missed that good things.  Their iced plain coffee is really bad.  I don't know why, and I love them, so I don't want to hurt their feelings, but they really need to know, their iced coffee is watery and terrible.

Spectacular food.  And excellent recommendations made by the staff.  Every single thing we ordered came out looking identical to the professional photos that entice you to eat here.  The attention to detail, great service, and mouthwatering food make this my new favorite Greek place!
Terri was so great to work with and made a beautiful cake for our work baby shower!  We will definitely use her in the future!

Don't order the mini pork soup dumplings! The pork has lots of chewy pieces of fat and cartilage, and the dumplings don't have much flavor. Very disappointing. That said, I'd recommend ordering soup and other dishes that are not pork dumplings - some of them are quite delicious.

I can't believe the quality of the lamb souvlaki role that I had the lamb was cooked rare the way I wanted it it was no heavy sauce just a very light marinade They even included french fries ithat were salted and delicious

Just had an amazing experience here with my family. If you haven't tried their food, you are seriously missing out. The food was phenomenal. My wife had sushi and was delighted by the freshness of the fish. I had the Ramen and enjoyed every bit of it. Yumm.. Can't wait to come back.

Great eats, service and choices if you are vegan or just want to help the planet. I just had their great "vegan tuna melt" and also love the "southwestern nachos" and "greek salad with vegan feta". I've tried a lot of things here and am never disappointed. The Manager Manny is number One in customer service. Highly recommend!!!

Super rude they messed up my order and wasted my time going back and fourth about my missing items that they didn't pack. I had to go back to my car get my order and bring it to them . The location is already a hassle to find parking. Brought in the bag and they still were going back and fourth with me about my order. Until they finally re did my items with an attitude. Overall a horrible bad experience with this location and super rude employees.

The food was awesome! I'm so happy there's a new vegan place close to me ¡GO VEGAN! 
The staff was really friendly which is basically the one thing that determines if I'll keep going back someplace, I hope that keeps up because there is a lack of friendly food staff in the Boston area.

Best damn bar in Central. Good Guinness, good food... and a secret garden, an outdoor patio tucked away behind the bar, away from the street! 

Never a cover, always a good time.

Oh yeah, try the steak tips. You'll never believe that they cost under $10.

F*** anyone who gave this below 4 stars for not doing "cappuccino art" or having a good "caramel latte". Losers. This low key has the best breakfast in Cambridge. Mofo in the back whipping up the fire breakfast taquitos. Pastries on point with the complex flavors - had the rosemary/pear turnover this morning. Coffee hits hard with the fruity undertones, or go fuck your self and get a dank sparkling herbal tea. Congee bowl with dried figs be hittin' the mouthgasm button. 

Staff is cool.

Anyone who gave 3 stars, come see me in the streets(Broadway street to be exact).

Kimchi Kitchen definitely satisfied the Korean food cravings I'd been having for weeks! This small and cute cafe has all the popular k food dishes you could want. + friendly service, tons of small plates, and awesome service! The casual ambience and relatively quiet noise level makes this a great place to catch up with friends. 

I ordered the kimchi fried rice, which usually comes with pork, but they were nice enough to make it vegetarian for me. Even meatless, this dish was delish! There was just the right amount of kick from the kimchi, and the fried egg on top was cooked to perfection with a oozy yolk. The small apps were really good too, and they even gave us complimentary little sweet rice cakes in honor of Mid Autumn Festival! 

Kimchi Kitchen is a little out of the way from T stops, but that won't stop me from coming back for more!

Really delicious coffee! Sometimes there's a bit of a long line and it can be hard to move OUT once you've bought your coffee (not a great layout) but worth it for the malted cold brew. They also have an amazing Thai Iced Tea and London Fog.

This is a cute little space at Harvard square. I ordered the Mt. Olympus Salad ($12.50) that came with chicken and a lot of other salad things. The serving size is more than enough for a meal (I'm not complaining). I wish the salad was pre-mixed because it was difficult to mix in the container. 

The interior is absolutely beautiful! It was closed due to covid precautions but the patio outside is open. Would recommend going in for some quick fast food.
The food was amazing. We had the beyond burger which had great taste, the meat (less) was not burnt and cooked perfectly. The staff was very friendly, helpful and followed protocols

I've grabbed food from Saloniki and ordered catering from here many times, but never eaten in their Smith Campus Center location. But it's quickly become one of my favorite options to pick-up from in the area. The line is never too long and they're pretty quick with the food.

The pita sandwiches are yummy! Definitely not the healthiest, but good for a treat yourself kind of day. The salads are solid, though if you're going for salad I'm still a Sweetgreen loyalist. If you have room for dessert, the Nutella Greek Donuts are AMAZING! Maybe one day I'll dine in...

I am a vegan. The burren has more GF options then you, Flour, Tate, Clover, Veggie Galaxy, By Chloe all have more. Honestly get some GF pasta and bread. And trust me I am not GF by choice. Many people have trouble with gluten due to the growing techniques practiced in america. Please don't cut us out of this.

My husband and I walked in and asked the bartender to try some of the favorite dishes.  We LOVED everything that he suggested!  We had the grilled pita and spreads, fried cauliflower and moussaka.  They were all delicious!  The drinks were good and the bartender gave great service!!!

Good neighborhood spot with cheap drinks and friendly clientele. Mostly a beer and shots type of place. There isn't any food served here. This is a dive bar through and through. You can play darts here but that is the only game available. There are a few televisions inside as well to watch sporting events. This is a great spot to hit after work to relax, talk and have a few drinks.
Great vegan / vegetarian place with a slew of food stuffs. My personal favorite is the Mac Stack sandwich with Mac n cheese buns.
Great cake and very friendly service... Terri is able to handle specific and unique designs. 10/10 would do again

Delicious! We had nachos, shrimp tacos, and the tortilla salad. Super affordable paid less than $20 for the food and about $20 for 3 margaritas. The line was pretty long, but the staff were very quick so it didn't feel like we were waiting too long. Check out their rooftop during nice weather.

They make amazing sandwiches, and their staff are friendly and efficient. This is a great place to pickup a quick lunch in Kendall Square. Their sandwiches are huge with generous amounts of fillings. My favorite is the Sicilian Steak. The bread is soft and chewy - very fresh. And the portobello mushrooms and plum tomatoes complement the steak very well.

This place is almost always crowded but on those rare occasions when it is not, it is a great place to just sit and enjoy a great meal.  I have only tried the desserts here but everything else looks tasty.  The nutella baklava Loukoumades and lemonade is my go to comfort plate.  You definitely need to share this, the portion is huge but just downright sinfully tasty.  Its a good pick me up and a delicious treat.  I will definitely try their entrees one of these days.  For now, I recommend the Loukoumades!

Consider this a posthumous review, because I wrote it back when this place was still open. I'm really sad to hear of its demise. For three years I lived in the dorm next door, and now wish I had gone here more than a handful of times. This was always the place I would bring friends for breakfast or lunch.
---
Sunny's is the quintessential old-fashioned diner. You walk into what seems like an oversized trailer that hasn't been remodeled in 30 years, pick up the simple menu, and see it's just the usual basics. A few perks here and there, nothing fancy. How does that get five stars? It's the food that comes out. This will instantly become your favorite breakfast/brunch spot in the Boston area. P.S. Went back again this week. Pancakes were out of this world. They even have corned beef for non-pig eaters such as myself. Where's the sixth star?

I reallllly like this place and reallllly wish they'd open one in RI or even another in MA outside of the city. We drove an hour to get here and the food was amazing. 

We got the crispy green beans- those were good. The Mac and cheese, it lacked flavor but wasn't awful, just bland. The crispy cauliflower.. those were great/ flavorful and liked the orange dipping sauce. The Nashville chicken sandwich was great/ I ate the savory kale Cesar and found it to be the best salad I have ever had. The creamy dressing combo with the avocado was great. I would go here/ order from here ALL the time if it wasn't an hour away and incredibly hard to park etc. Hoping they open other ones! 

The other thing that I want to mention about this place is that I LOVE that you can order on a large touch pad. You select your item and can remove any thing it comes with if you don't like it! Like, the salad I got.. I don't like those fake bacons so I got it sans bacon. Makes it easy and fast to get exactly what you want.

Excuse me...The Simpsons figurines in front window display? The Simpsons cereal boxes framed on wall? Cook wearing The Simpsons hat? 

I don;t get it either but it's fantastic.

Counter service. One medium sized bench. Four 2 tops. Small place.

Breakfast till 2PM every....single....day. I suggest going after 12PM if you want to avoid chaos.

Everyone raves about the bacon strips here. I think the scallions in homefries does it for me. It's the crisp dark brown squared box potatoes!

Don't be a newb... if you want a cold drink.. you go to the fridges in the back and just bring it to your table. Also it's more like honor system here when it comes to paying. You go to the register and annouce what you had while they punch in prices. Don;t be a scumbag and remember to tip nicely.

One less star because the service can get way delayed with one tiny mistake since they only have one cook.. one server... and if you forget to specify which cheese.. they give you American and American with eggs I no like.

after waiting 23 minutes and getting a $25 parking ticket while waiting since the meter ran out, i was hoping this food would be as good as the reviews said.

wrong.

the reviews are most likely from people who arent really into seasonings and flavor. 

this was some of the blandest and overpriced food ive ever ate. $17 for a sweet potato and warm grains bowl with pesto and a pumpkin muffin/cake thing.. okay ridiculous.. anyway... the food needed salt.. and seasoning.. and everything else that makes food taste good.. the pumpkin thing didnt even have a pumpkin flavor. this is so disappointing after waiting this long. 

my best friend got a soup and we ordered at the same time, her soup came out as my food did.. it takes 23 minutes to put soup in a cup...? i understand being a new yorker we are used to fast paced living but sheesh.. no way

I really enjoyed trying this place out the other night - the menu is simple but everything looked really good and all of the entrees were super affordable ($8-12). I opted to try the ddukbokki and one of the specials on the board, kimchi tofu and pork over rice. The ddukbokki was definitely spicy and good, though I think prefer softer rice cakes and more fish cakes in the dish. The kimchi tofu pork dish was yummy and chock full of kimchi. There wasn't that much pork in it, but I didn't mind. We arrived around 8:30 on a Saturday night, and there was still a line and a wait to order. I can see it being difficult to dine in during the busy hours since there isn't that much seating. Overall I liked it, and I want to go back to try more of the dishes.

I've used Terri for two cakes, one for my daughter's HS graduation party, another for my son's fifth birthday. Both were exceptional -- great-looking, incredibly tasty, and ready on time with minimal hassle. I think Terri is just the nicest person to work with, too. I'm sad we only discovered her when we're on the verge of moving out of the area, but I'm planning to let all my friends know about her before we leave!

Very regrettable visit here. Came in for a late breakfast on a holiday and sat for 20 minutes without being once approached by a waitress. Several were working and visiting other tables. Literally not even a quick hello, they just left us sitting there. It was over an hour before they closed, so I can't imagine it was because the kitchen was closed, and even still wouldn't you at least acknowledge us? It was one of the most bizarre and disorienting dining experiences I've ever had.

The food is fresh, tasty and the whole crew is so upfront to help you. I loved my visit and will come back again for buffalo "chicken"
Shoutout to Hannah!! Who changed the whole burrito for my girlfriend.
Delicious! And appreciate their service (Jeff was my server). The Niko pita is always reliable.

I ordered a cake with less than a week lead time, and Terri turned it around on time and with a gorgeous design -- and best of all, a very tasty cake! My guests loved it, and the girls being honored (it was a graduation party) thought it was so gorgeous. It was very inexpensive -- about the same as a store-bought cake -- but so much better! I can't recommend her highly enough -- just a great cake.

This place is the best place to get espresso drinks immediately inside Porter Square. It really is a cute place inside of Porter Square books and the staff is always very friendly. 

The only drawbacks are that it is CASH ONLY and also kind of expensive. Otherwise, this place would definitely be 5 stars in my book.

THIS IS MY GO-TO PLACE IN THE SQUARE. it's open so late, tastes so yummy, quick service, always reliable, and there are always great perks with snackpass. i come here all the time especially when i end up missing dinner and always, always get the niko platter. if you're a student, you get free fries. you can sit and eat or take out, both are great options. its a great environment, and you can also do work in there! staff is friendly and quick to help. food is consistently great. ideal portion sizes.
By 7:00 pm they ran out of rice! Can you imagine? Someone's Greek grandma is rolling over in her grave.

This is a great place with a ton of ramen and sushi options. The ramen is excellent and the portions are large. We tried a sushi roll as an appetizer on our last visit and it was delicious as well. The staff is very friendly and the prices are very reasonable. Love having this in the neighborhood.
Will recommend Terri to all my friends. Was easy to order, very reasonable price, beautiful design.

This is a truly excellent diner. In an age when diners too often succumb to the traps of lower quality ingredients, inflated prices, cheap newfangled decor (often the result of tragic makeovers), and poorly treated employees, Andy's Diner is a breath of fresh air. Nothing fancy by any means, the decor is well worn, sturdy and carries history; the food is standard diner fare, and excellent, with ingredients that don't taste cheap; the prices are refreshingly reasonable for what you get; and the service is outstanding, friendly, attentive, precise. 
Diners are simple places, and they need just a few simple things to work, otherwise the customer feels not at home, and cheated of the experience. Andy's Diner has managed to preserve these simple elements, and that's why it's so good in spite of being nothing fancy or new. Let's hope it stays that way.

Dwelltime looks so nice from the outside that you just have to know more (at least compared to the surroundings). After driving past for months we finally decided to dive in and see what it was all about.

Ordering: The flow of traffic in Dwelltime was obviously in mind when they designed the space. However, even though the line stays out of the way of the seating area, it is pretty confusing. After you order, it is kind of a cluster to the register and then even after that there is a lot of people walking not knowing where to go. This of course was on a Saturday and generally people get that way on the weekend. 

Coffee: The coffee speaks for itself. It is pour-over, so each cup is brewed with care. The beans are high quality and very tasty. The price is high but that is the type of coffee you are buying. I was frustrated to not have more options on the menu. I wanted a double espresso and I got looked at like I had 3 heads. When my order came I only had one, :( 

Atmosphere: It is a cool space, high ceilings, active kitchen with a window in on the action. That being said, there are TONS of kids in here. The neighborhood is a very family oriented one. With kids comes tons of MESS. Nobody was really cleaning up during the Saturday rush hour and all of the tables and chairs were dirty. Maybe that was from the adults, who knows. Either way someone needed to be wiping up the mess. I also thought the space was under utilized. They could easily fit more tables in.

If you are lucky enough to get a window seat you can do some great people watching.
I placed a seamless order, it was accepted, 2 hours later it hadn't arrived. I called the restaurant and no one picked up the phone. Disappointing. Don't order from here.
Literally could NOT eat the bowl of ramen I ordered for delivery. It was inedible. I feel like I have a stomach ache from the little bit I did ingest. It was horrid.
Only the best fast food restaurant in Boston! Quick friendly service, creative & eclectic menu and great community & kid activities.

My sub, a steak and provolone with red peppers, was delicious. The bread was soft and fresh. However despite my request fort only red peppers, they used green peppers as well. The sub appears huge, but the steak did not fill the bread. My friend ordered a south street cafe chicken sub, but they confused it with the south st cafe special sub, which is prosciutto, which she could not eat.

This is a great fast casual place with lots of healthy options.  I love the masala bowl and the superfood salad.  Delivery has always been accurate and prompt.  A nice way to diversify the usual sweetgreen meal.

My friend and I came here for dinner and shared the most AMAZING soup dumplings as well as two other dishes. It was pretty busy for  a weeknight, but people know it's good!! We'll definitely be coming back here.

Ate in the patio and LOVED the food and ambience. My favorites were the Salmon with Sweet Potato Roll and Beef Shoyu Ramen. It was a busy night, but the staff took great care of us and went the extra mile to show they cared. :)

When a new place up in the area, you sure bet I'll be down to give it a try :-) 

As a meat-lover, I was curious to see what this place has to offer and if it could appeal to all sorts of people--vegans and non-vegans alike! I ordered the Bulogigi Beef bowl and found it to pretty good in terms of taste.  I was really surprised that my "beef"  tasted really similar to meat and if you had not known this was a vegan place, I think I would be pretty convinced that I was actually eating the real thing. 

My only complaints about this place are that I feel as if my serving size is not worth the money I am paying for a meal here. Maybe I was just super hungry the day I went, but I ate every last bite of my bowl and was still left a little unsatisfied. I think this is a big reason why I wasn't exactly wow'ed by this place. Don't  get me wrong, the food is good but serving size + price tag are enough to deter me from coming back here too often. However, I also understand that most vegan places tend to be on the pricier side, so I won't let these factors prevent me from coming back here every again!

Sometimes, all you want to do is sit down at a casual, slightly dirty, old school diner and eat some eggs and pancakes. Andy's Diner is that place. The food is simple but good. Be warned though, you get a very large amount of food in an order.

Fantastic, creative take on Greek carry-out food. 

Lamb meatballs are richly spiced on the George pita, and we loved the vegetarian Athena with halloumi and roasted veggies.

The "secret sauce" is a lemon-y acidic dressing that's great on the sandwiches and perfect for dipping fries in.

A real highlight of Harvard Square dining that also features a bar for those dining in.
All vegan menu!!! Southwest nachos are so yummy. One of the better vegan tempeh burgers in Boston too. Plus the staff is really friendly. Must try.

Delicious and excellent addition to the area. Staff was so nice and we really enjoyed everything we had.  Favorites were the Mac and cheese and the chick'n sandwich.  Drinks were also super tasty.  Parking is as expected for Harvard square.
I'm not a vegan or vegetarian but the food is really good and I can't tell the difference. Very good customer service 100/10 would recommend .)

The food is delicious! I always get the steak sub. I always order pick up because going in the store to order lunch is like going to a sold out musical festival for the first time.

Literally there's no organization of who is in charge or where does the line starts. If you're new to Cambridge MA, I would only go here if a friend brings you here. 

The only downside here is the customer service, the lack of energy and enthusiasm here. People look miserable working here, especially one of the ladies in the front. There's only one lady in the front that's looks nice and greets their customers!! Cheers

Hey management, can you feed some happiness to your team? I know you can feed the locals well, but what about your Ale family team? Let's get that going because small things goes long ways.

So I know the photo I attached looks half eaten but I didn't get a picture in the more beginning stages because I couldn't stop eating. This place is a casual corner with good prices and rich broth. The staff is friendly and I would consider this place to be less overwhelming and better than Hokkaido Santouka Ramen, and more reasonable portions than Yume Wo Katare. If you don't come here you're missing out and I'm saying that as someone that's eaten a lot of ramen in Cambridge/Somerville and in Osaka and Tokyo.

Delicious vegan food! Regular customer from southern CA. Great surprise enjoying the delicious green beans and cheesesteak fall special. 
The Mocha Horchata is a must in your visit

My family is a mix of vegan, vegetarian, and omnivore. My vegan daughter had been here last year and enjoyed it, so we decided to give it a shot for a family-wide meatless meal, even though Harvard Square is a bit farther than we typically like to go for takeout.

The food was really good, tasted fresh, and was definitely hot, as it was still very warm after we got it back home. We had a fairly large order, and one small side of fries was missing, but everything else was good enough (and the portions so generous) that I'm not going to knock off a star for that. Particular favorites were the Crispy Cauliflower appetizer, the Sweet Potato Fries, and the Far East Bowl with grilled chickin'.

We ordered curbside pickup, and or order was ready pretty much exactly on time, brought out to our car, and carefully placed in the trunk.

This place just sucks. Ordered a cheesesteak plain yesterday but they added tomatoes onions n Mayo. I bring it back and the front lady gives me an attitude as if it was my fault. I order a barbecue chicken "tender" sub plain w light sauce. I get heavy and sauce and grilled chicken. Even when they make the subs correctly they're still below average. My coworker found a insect in his salad a month ago
Excellent vegetarian sushi and great delivery radius. Recommend. More words...uhh..delivery is quick, packaging good, sushi fresh. Good place.

This location is on par with any other in the country! The food is always on point and deliveries/take out orders are always complete.

Shoutout to Manny and Jennifer! I called ahead to let them know I wouldn't be able to pick up my order for a while because I got in an accident on the way to get my food. They kept my food warm and offered to stay late so I could pick up my order! Thanks guys!
Jeff is the best, super friendly and great with helping us choose delicious options! Great vibes of the place and overall a really good experience! Make sure to bring your student id

Seems like a nice place but not my style. The humus should have been smoother imo. The pita bread better than average store bought but not better than Naf Naf which has very fluffy fresh pita. Poor logistical experience for takeout.

I tried this for the first time for dinner today! Came with a friend at around 7:30pm and it was busy (but not overcrowded). My friend really likes the place, but I was unfortunately a little disappointed; I got the Jimmy plate (pork sausage + some other things), and while it wasn't bad, I thought the food -- particularly the sausage -- was strangely over-seasoned. One pro: the service and ambiance were both quite good!
How to take an old standby and make it better - pulled pork burrito at Felipe's! Tender well seasoned meat, FRESH ingredients, yum!  And a lovely roof deck to enjoy it on with a well made margarita
Fantastic for the price!! Think chipotle but with fresh and delicious ingredients. I recommend a super quesadilla!

Food-wise: very tasty!
Service-wise: I believe it's very important to bring to party food at the same time so no one eats or wait for another person to eat.
All the times I've been there this rule was ignored and it feels very weird to eat when next to you is hungry person without food.
I and my boyfriend came to the restaurant and the main dish of my boyfriend was completely forgotten.
There is no way to call for a server because she is busy and trying to serve all guests by herself.
So by the time I was eating my course I asked where are the food and we figured out that it didn't even get to the kitchen.
As we already spent there a lot of time we decided to take it to go.
Therefore it was eaten from a plastic dish at home instead of a normal meal in an atmospheric place.
Place definitely needs to have one more server for a rush hour.
Chicken plate was delicious and huge! It was very filling for dinner, and the fries were delightfully crispy and salty (with some seasoning on it). The pita bread was warm and soft.

3.5/5

I had the cheesesteak with beyond meat and my friend had the beyond burger. They were okay. I wasn't a huge fan of the fake cheese they used on mine and I felt like the baguette was too fluffy and moist for my taste. I would prefer a crunchier baguette. But my lentil soup that I got on the side was fantastic. It was quite creamy, thick, and had a little kick to it. I felt like the portion sizes were small for the price. I liked the overall vibe and it was well decorated. Next time I think I want to try a bowl.
Dana loves this place but I don't get it. I was unimpressed with everything we had and not pleased that they got my order wrong. Largely forgettable food.

This is my favorite place to hang out in Harvard Square. Such friendly workers from owners to managers to waitstaff! Also the food is great! You will always leave there feeling fulfilled. My personal favorites are the niko and Yianni pitas. Also being a Harvard student I get free fries! Beautiful inside restaurant space. Definitely a great place to check out!

Some of the best soup dumplings around. In between Central and Harvard squares in Cambridge on Mass Ave, Dumpling House fills up quickly. You may expect a wait, but it is sure worth it. Several large family-style tables are available. The restaurant has a few separate areas and has the vibe of a Chinatown restaurant with the hustle and bustle of fast service and a loud atmosphere. Great for parties of two or ten. 

As one would expect (and hope), the dumplings are the star and the mini soup dumplings with pork are delightful with the chewy and slightly crusty dough of the dumpling oozing with a savory broth with a generous stuffing of ground pork. Pour the vinegar sauce slowly over the dumpling, bite a small hole, slurp, enjoy. 

The menu is quite large. Depending on the size of the party, would recommend one fish, one meat, and one vegetable dish. Option for spicy Szechuan style, Taiwanese, or even "Americanized" Chinese dishes. I'm sure the "Americanized" dishes are perfectly fine but do try to branch out. Take the Szechuan dried spicy chicken - its like a simple chicken nugget but with a flash of heat, served with a heaping portion of sliced dried peppers.

The sliced fish Szechuan style is lovely. The small portion (large also available) is enough for two to share with other dishes. What is more satisfying than a bowl of spicy red pepper broth with tender white fish, bok choy, and cabbage on a cool fall evening? You may even catch a Szechuan peppercorn or two.

Pros:
-Huge amount of vegetarian options that even meat eaters will like
-Order ahead
-Portions are large enough to split into two meals

Cons:
-Expensive
-Required to get a side with a sandwich. I'd much rather pay less for the sandwich and get no side!
-No parking

--
The Veggie Grill breaded "chicken" is pretty good, though it's not crispy and mostly tastes like soft bread. The carne asada is also good! I'm not a fan of the beyond burger so I stay away from that. 

Recommend: 
Santa Fe Crispy Chickin' ($10.95)
Buffalo Bomber ($11.25)
Carne Asada Burrito ($11.95)


Don't recommend: 
Gluten-Free Chocolate Brownie ($3.95)
Not bad but not great. Mostly crumbly. Has chocolate chips.

Crispy Fries ($4.50)
Not good. Unseasoned potatoes. Avoid!!

Great food, good service, outdoor dining

Recently our family of four were doing a 'socially distanced' tour of NE colleges and attracted by the vibe and outdoor seating we dropped in for lunch. 

The ramen was spectacular. Three of us had the Tonkotsu and one the Spicy Sesame - all were delicious. The Miso Cucumber salad was also quite tasty.

The sushi was just OK. I tried to dissuade my kids from ordering it - the restaurant clearly has the 'Ramen shop' vibe going - but they were not to be deterred. So we sampled from a platter of Shrimp Tempura, Spicy Tuna, and Salmon Avocado rolls. As I said...it was OK. Nothing wrong with it but nothing particularly right either...but for all I know it could well be the best sushi in the neighborhood. 

They had a variety of Calpicos which made my son happy and some tasty green tea for the rest of us. The service was good as well. 

But in these COVID times by far the most important thing a restaurant can offer is well ventilated and spaced outdoor seating. And so One Raman & Sushi was able to give us exactly what we wanted - a yummy sit down meal.

Ordered delivery and it was 35min as promised. Choose 3rolls combo (salmon avocado, spicy salmon avocado and eel avocado) and a sashimi dinner (18 pieces) with All were fresh and yummy. Came with 2 miso soups, 2 salads and a side of rice with the sashimi dinner. Food was packed perfectly and at the right temperature. This is a great neighborhood place and I hope folks are supporting this small business. They are terrific.

I'm updating my review due to the extremely poor experiences we have had for catering. When Veggie Grill first opened last year, we were offered a free catered lunch. What a deal! So, only a week after that deal was offered, decided to take them up on it. That was a bit of a mess- the communication on the free meal and pickup was a mess. We placed the order online, and when we arrived too pick it up, it wasn't ready. We got help, and ended up getting the food.

Fast forward months later, and we decided to give the catering another shot. Our office manager placed the order IN PERSON at the location 4 business days ahead of time to be sure everything was all set, and gave the business card number for payment.

I was sent to pick up the order.... only to be asked tons of questions. Did we order it with EZ Cater? I On the website? Phone? So, I ended up calling our office manager and she spoke directly to the manager on staff to sort it out, and our food was prepped and handed to us quite quickly- but sadly, it was half an hour later than our scheduled pick up time. Ever walk into a room full of starving coworkers who are now also half an hour late for a meeting? It's not the best. It's like a room full of wolves wearing business casual.   We did get a comped kale Caesar for the trouble, and some $5 off cards (but the cards expire on Friday, so not really useful)!

The food here IS great. We love it! We are just disappointed that not once, but twice, our catering orders were completely "lost in the system" and I doubt we will be able to confidently have office catered lunches from Veggie Grill again.

This place is definitely one of my favorite places to go for Chinese food. In our latest trip, my friends and I ordered a bunch of soup dumplings, scallion pancakes, pork and leek dumplings, as well as a couple entries: three treasures and a chicken dish. Overall, the food was very good; the soup dumplings were tasty as always and the three treasures was awesome. It contains eggplant, potato, and pepper and I would definitely recommend it as a vegetable dish. Overall, we had a fantastic experience and would definitely come back again.

I've only ordered delivery from this place, but it is definitely my go to for sushi in the Cambridge area.  Big fan.  

My favorite roll always has shrimp tempura and spicy tuna.  Here it is called something like "spicy crazy maki.'  Love the names at all sushi places but this one really takes it to the next level.  There is also one loaded with avocado and unagi that is delish, I think it is an Alligator role.

The menu has pictures too, clearly a plus.  I obviously haven't read them so I can't tell you about any other menu items.  I can just tell you that the sushi comes as advertised.  No bait and switch going on here.
Beef pad-see-ew was amazing! Delivery service is pretty average but I was not a fan of the thai ice tea. I will be a repeat customer!

After reading all the yelp reviews. I thought this would be a good place to get take out.  Boy was I wrong. Maybe the food tastes better dining in but I was not impressed at all. 

I would give this place a 1.5 stars.  I had one decent dish which was the Kra-Pow Rice dish but the rest of the order was terrible.  The egg-rolls were super oily and had no flavor.
