Then Pad Thai my boyfriend got was terrible.  There was no sauce, no flavor.. yuck... it tasted like they toss rice noodles with veggies and oil.  Completely disappointed.

Great sandwiches and coffee in a pleasantly cramped storefront.  Darwin's is actually pretty big and would presumably have plenty of seating, but seems to get so busy that you have to camp out to snag a table.  Something should come free by the time your food is ready though, so don't worry too much.  The coffee here is legitimately great.  I got a cortado and it's as good as anything in Harvard Square.  The sandwiches are not as good as the coffee, but still worth a try.  I'd skip the Leonard (veggies and hummus), at least in winter, the vegetables were out of season and thus quite lackluster.  The Ellsworth (Turkey, swiss, pesto mayo, and veg) was good and surprisingly huge.  Not an innovative sandwich by any means, but it hits the spot and is a decent value.  Whatever sandwich you decide on, get a pickle,  they're nearly perfect.
The ultimate "intimate" spot to catch a sweet local artist or a dinner show. Veggie planet is da bomb, whether you're vegan or not. Chill basement vibes, super reasonably priced.

WORST SERVICE EVER.

The servers are super rude, don't see you (probably intentional, given the number of times this has happened), and when dining here the feeling is that you owe them money, to use a Chinese proverb. It takes them around 10 minutes just to get you water and take the order, even after flagging a server and his/ her acknowledgement of having seen you. I have yet to see a server here smile - yikes. There are so many other eateries in Harvard Square, and Nine Spices would be at the end of my list.

I frequent Mother Juice very often, especially since I work over at TITLE down the street. Everything they have here is solid, from the smoothies, to the juices, to the food. They're great about substitutions as well! My go to items are: the chia pudding with mixed nuts subbed in for the granola and add in a scoop of sunflower seed butter, the acai bowl with the same substitution, a green juice with a fireball shot, and the coconut mocha amazeballs. One thing that makes it fun is that they always have new items on the menu, especially with the amazeballs. I've tried almost everything except the overnight oats and health bomb, which aren't paleo, but they have rave reviews among my friends, so I'd recommend those as well. If you're looking for toast, my favorite is the Cinnful and the Newbury, although the avocado is always a classic. 

The only downside is that they tend to run out of things quite often, as it can get really busy especially on weekends. I also wish they had unsweetened protein powder!

But otherwise, I definitely recommend. This is a great place for anyone who's vegan, gluten free, or health conscious.

with a 4 start average at 114 reviews i thought harvest of india was going to be packed during lunch time for their buffet. i was surprised of how empty it was but this was good as i did not want to fight scholars or erudites for chicken tikka masala or mango chutney. service was attentive, naan bread comes to your table. they had a nice variety of classic indian dishes and for the price it seems like a good deal to me.

I come here a few times per week for fresh pressed green juices.  The juice quality is just amazing.  So fresh and very tasty.  Staff is also very friendly and customer centric.  If you like green juices or smoothies of different varieties, definitely check out this place.

I was visiting Boston on a very quick trip and stayed with a friend who lives around the corner. We popped in for a late dinner, so we were the only ones in the restaurant. Even though it was so late, the staff was extremely attentive and friendly. Right when we sat down they put a basket of papadum on the table - yum! 

We ordered a chicken tikka masala (spicy level), masala dosa, garlic naan, and a mango lassi. The total came out to be around $30 with a 10% off coupon. The food was very good! Our first batch of naan was only okay, but they actually brought out a new basket that was on the house without us asking. Our waters were constantly refilled, too! 

I didn't realize there were indian/chinese fusion options on the menu so I didn't try it, but this type of food was popular when I visited India... seems to be not as common here in the states (or maybe... I just haven't noticed). 

The decor in the restaurant is nice, and the staff makes you feel welcome. I love that! Definitely give this place a try :)

pretty good thai food.
Good place for a larger gathering of people.
Lots of pad thai plate.
Price. good
I'd go there when I'm craving pad thai and some environment..
Hits the spot. From a fellow desi so you know it's authentic.

This is the best of all the MIT food trucks, and they definitely beat the quality of food at the Kendal Food Court.  

Nice mix of pan Asian food (Chinese, Thai, Indian).  All about $4, 50cents more for brown rice or basil fried rice.

Favs:
Cambodian Seafood (scallops, shrimp and fried fish with tasty sauce).
Bangkok Garpow (delicious ground chicken cooked with basil)
Ga Nuong (thai salad with grilled lemongrass chicken and rice noodles)

Perfect neighborhood cafe.
I used to always get Anthony's favorite breakfast sandwich. And the coffee is the bomb

Rude staff, horrible service. I'm not even rating the food. I couldn't get over how insanely rude and horrible the employees were to enjoy my food. The manager was sitting at a table and heard me say to my co-workers how horrible the guys in the salad bar were and he said nothing. He got up and walked over to the register while I was (over)paying for my salad and told me to get a cookie. I'll never be back.

This is a local favorite (at least for where I work), the food is great! They make great sandwiches. Their biggest assets is the quick-friendly staff, I think I heard some where they're all friends who decided to open up a business together. But either way they know your name by face, which is really cool and makes you feel important....lol. You should always call or check the website to see what the daily special is. Bon appetite!

I got the Pad Thai with Tofu, yummy. Not too greasy. My friend got the calamari, I took a bite and it was tasty.
Nice casual cheap asian food. No high expectations so I wasnt let down.

This is a terrific place to get some yummy vegetarian snacks and see live music.  

Tickets are available for food service at a table or just a seat along the wall.  The space is small and tables are tight.  They seat 4 to a table and if your party doesn't equal 4, you'll be dining with strangers.  Everyone there is quite friendly, so that's not such a big deal.  But if you want your chair(s) to face the stage, get there EARLY to claim your seats!  

The food I've had there is always very good.  I usually get one of the flatbread pizzas and a cup of organic chai with a splash of honey to wash it down.  I've often been tempted to try their mac-n-cheese made with Vermont cheddar, but am just so in love with the pizzas that I settle for the familiar.  

There are only one or two servers, even during the busiest events, but they move as quickly as they can and are always kind and patient, so you should be too!

Tickets are cheaper when you buy them in advance.  Many wonderful folk singers perform here on a regular basis.  Definitely check out Jess Klein whenever she's in town.

UPDATE:

I tried them again, just to give them another shot. I dined in this time and ordered the same plate of Drunken Noodle. Yup, still bad -- greasy and gross. My friends laughed at me for taking the same risk with the same dish. Well, joke's on YOU GUYS, for eating at the same restaurant with me! Your dishes weren't much better, either! Suckas.

Well, at least this time around, I got flat rice noodles.

There's usually a few trucks on Mass Ave and one in Harvard Square near the Science Building.  I lived off these bad boys during my summer at Harvard.  I still wake up in the middle of the night dreaming about those perfectly sized Styrofoam containers full of fluffy, cloud white rice topped with the General's Kung Pao, or the sweetest and sourest, sweet and sour chicken, or dare I say the orangiest, orange chicken?  A complete lunch for $4?  Nothing beats that...when are you guys going to pahk your trucks in the N. End or at least near the Commons for us working stiffs?

The food is pretty good! Having eaten here hundreds of times from 1996 onward, I can confidently say Goosebeary does not cause long term health problems. At least in 14 years. Great dishes include Thai peanut noodles, General Gao's (If you like strange gooey textures, like I do), and wonton noodle soup. Some people like the ma po tofu. It's a truck, and charges $4, so for the people who give it less than four stars, you clearly have miscalibrated expectations and some sort of bizarre disposition. PS, helpful note, many of the things on the menu are actually identical dishes, eg orange chicken and crispy chicken.

Extemely hipster place so if you appreciate fiscal responsibility you won't like it here. Joking aside, is a great local alternative to Starbucks, with the added bonus of having a larger selection. My favorite sandwiches are the Ellsworth, the Felton, and the Hovey.

Nice place but really small so a bit crowded,  food, menu is TOTALLY wrong for this kind of music and  great beer place - pricey gourmet?  Veggie planet was the right fit and a Clover menu would,be perfect.

Have been for buffet, dosas, vegetarian thali, and a few other times.  They made a gluten-free dining companion happy, make a vegetarian boyfriend happy, make me happy.  So far I've only seen this tiny place busy a few times, which is too bad, the food is delicious.  I hope to be back soon and often.
All of the sandwiches, coffees, and teas are delicious. They play good music and the staff is very friendly. This is a good place for breakfast, lunch, studying, or just chatting with some friends.

Came for the lunch buffet that everyone talks about. Unlimited naan and the ~$9 price makes up for the somewhat limited selection. There was rice, salad, yogurt, chai, and maybe a selection of like 5 curries and hot dishes.

The interior is quite cramped, and there really isn't much table space. Service is sluggishly slow. Naan is technically unlimited but it takes a good amount of pestering and a good amount of time before you get your second basket, especially if they are even remotely busy, and dirty dishes don't really get cleared which becomes a hassle with the small tables if you're feeling particularly gluttonous. The trays don't really get refilled so it might be a wait if something runs out.

But then again, the price is great - it really is unlimited/all-you-can eat and the food is pretty good. It's the same price on the weekends too, which is a nice plus.

I've been waiting for this place to open for a few weeks so was excited to check it out the other day. 

Ironically I went to a burger place and ordered a chicken sandwich. But it was soooo tasty. Perfectly crispy chicken with pickles and sauce on a nice soft roll.

We got their loaded waffle fries which come with chili, bacon, chives, and sauce. Very tasty. Fries came out hot and crispy despite all the toppings on them.

Topped it off with the "hummingbird." A frozen, alcoholic milk shake that resembled a pina colada. Out of this world but heavy - so share if you are worried about that. 

Service was quick, friendly, and helpful. Will be back to try more. Bar is also open until 11.

Probably one of the best meals you can get for that price in the area. Pay extra $0.50 to replace white with brow rice. The long line is deceiving since it typically moves faster than the lines at the other food trucks.

Very small place but has a very wide selection of flavors. I've tried their ice cream cakes yet, but for ice cream a kiddie size with sugar cone will be enough to cheer me up for the day. My favorite flavors are: butter crunch and strawberries! 1 scoop with waffle cone is also a good choice if you don't mind to gain some weight and be happy about it! Love to support local vendors and this place is a very cute store to hang out.

I've ordered only delivery from this restaurant ever.  Usually the food is reasonable quality and price (not the best food, and not the worst around), but delivery is quick if  you're hungry.

Tonight I ordered from them at 6:15.  At 7:15 we called since we still had no food, and they told us 30 minutes.  Then they told us an hour.  Then after two hours our food was still not here.  It was 9pm before we got food and the restaurant basically refused to compensate us at all, while being incredibly rude on the phone.  I understand people sometimes have car trouble, but it's unreasonable to not inform your customers of the problem and give them the option to purchase their food from somewhere else.  If you want to go to an honest restaurant I would not recommend this one.

This review goes out to all the people who have unreal expectations of old school food trucks.

Can't be beat.

Viet Curry Tofu w/ brown rice, I love you.


(In all honesty I used to have a slight preference for the Indochine truck. Six of one..).

Nice brewery in Cambridge. We stopped by to check it out with our son who lives nearby. Had a pint of their Lumen-a delicious Belgian Blonde Ale that got better as I drank it. Crisp, clean and comfortable tap room. Will be back.

We now call this the "Far Truck" as it takes ten minutes to walk from our office in its new location.  However, Far Truck is Far Worth the walk.  When on a diet, choose Mango Cabbage Salad.  When feeling indulgent, I strongly recommend the daily special (always with tofu).
I have to say, for five bucks, lunch doesn't come better, faster or with more flavor.

I have never been disappointed here!

I love their bread!  A fresh and perfect crunchy/chewy Combo!

Favorite sub hands down for me is the tomato,  mozzarella and basil!  Pretty sure it has a special name, it's been awhile since I have eaten it... formed an allergy to fresh basil... 

But product is fresh!!!

Place is jumping at lunch time!  So know what you want ahead of time to speed up the line.

Enjoy!  Everything is GREAT here!!!

Tiny in size, big in quality ice cream!  I was feeling good about finding another yummy flavor after trying the delicious Chocolate Orgy and Charles River Crunch at the Waltham branch...lo and behold, I did!  The ginger was refreshing and had that lovely zest and spice along with loads of ginger bits.

When I used to waitress at a very busy restaurant in Harvard Square this is where I would be on my lunch break.

Al's deli-style ordering is casual, quick, and easy.  The price for a small sub, bag of chips, AND a drink is only $6!  Not to mention the small is actually more like a large and not once have I finished the whole thing (and I can eat a lot...for a girl.)  I only ever order turkey subs from deli's (because I'm a creature of habit) and this one was of the most delicious I have had.  

Al's doesn't have seating in the actual cafe but it is located in the Holyoke Center and there is seating available right outside Al's doors.  In the warmer weather this is a great place to pick up subs for a bike ride, or to eat outside and people watch in the square.

This is a new place - just opened in August 2010. We went for the buffet lunch at $7.95 today - same price as the other place down the street. They had a good selection, but the food was really bland, the music was really loud, and overall none of the dishes jumped out as being particularly tasty in and of themselves. Was excited to try it, but will likley stick with the other Indian restaurants in the area.
yum! I got the special sandwich (roast beef with Boursin) and my boyfriend got the meatball sub. Everything was fresh and fast. The employees were SO nice too

The people who work here are complete assholes.  They're laughably aloof and have pretty poor taste in music - and they play the same music - The Rolling Stones seem to be a favorite - over, and over, and over.  The sandwich selection is decent, but nothing particularly special - it's just better than a lot of other sandwich places in the area.

The atmosphere is pretty obviously set up to maximize profit: They have rows of long tables that give the cafe a cafeteria-like feel.  There is one small couch in the back and one large leather chair, but if you aren't lucky enough to snag one of those, then you might as well be at a library.
Good breakfast sandwiches; great lunch sandwiches.  The line can be long, but it moves quickly.  Try the mama's meatball sub.  Keep in mind it's cash only.
I came with the family for a second chance still bad. Our ice cream was all ice! We constantly were picking out chunks of ice. On a positive side they have a bunch of flavors.

Well I guess I can do a review now that I no longer work there..I can't be accused of being partial anymore.. right? Here's a few tips/observations:

1) If you check out the website (specials and soups updated daily) and find yourself craving one of the specials, call it in! You're not the only one that wants mac n cheese with grilled chicken and spinach.. or salmon cakes.. or shepherd's pie.. or BBQ pulled pork. Call it in.. and then you can go ahead and skip the line when you get there! 
2) Cubanos and the grilled chicken sandwich with pesto, mozzarella, and tomato are probably the most popular.. I really like all the special sandwiches (best chicken salad I've ever had).. although I'd take a reuben on pumpernickel any day.  
3)Magical times to get food and avoid the line? Before 12 (lunch starts at 11) and after 1:15-1:30. But beware.. the grill closes at 2:30.. so get those cubano and reuben orders in before then. 
4) Taste the soup (like in Coming to America)! I never considered soup to be a meal until I started working there. Grab yourself some bread from the basket and have a some lamb and white bean, caribbean fish chowder, turkey chili, beef stew, or butternut squash etc (like I said, they change daily).. all filling and delicious! 
5) CASH ONLYYYYYYY.. the closest ATM is by the mall. 
6) If it's not on the menu.. and it's not too crazy.. they'll make it! BLT's, tuna melts (with spinach and tomato = yummy!), grilled cheeses, chicken caesar wraps.. toasting or grilling sandwiches... the staff is always willing to go the extra mile when they can. 
7) They do light catering. They have platters for sandwiches and can even do soups. For the most part, the day before would be best... earlier would be even better. No delivery.

Hmmm... I really wanted to list 10 things.. but I guess I'll come back to it later! haha
Quality sandwiches, had the chicken pesto and chicken melt. Service was quick. Cash only!

Just ate here last night. Had the chicken chettinad - very good. I could taste mint and cardamom in the dish. Also pleasant atmosphere. Now if only the place had a license. However, will definitely get carry out without a second thought.

Glad there's another lunch option in Kendall Square. 

Bailey & Sage reminds me of Sebastian's (the cafe next door to this new location), only with more reclaimed wood and Edison bulbs. Unfortunately, you pay for the trendiness with the price: their "specialty sandwiches" average $9.75 a pop, a solid $2 more than anything from Sebastian's or Flour... not to mention nearly $4 more than Clover.

For the Google crowd this probably matters less, but as a student, I was skeptical about paying $12 for some bread and corned beef. The Reuben panini was an okay sandwich (though nowhere as good as a deli like Sam Lagrassa's). Not worth the pricetag. I feel like this place may be spread a little thin. There are a million options, but I'm not sure the'll do anything well. 

It's definitely not a "destination" that you would have any reason to seek out beyond working here and walking by. There are only a handful of places to grab a quick lunch in Kendall, so I'm sure I'll be back soon, and I'll update the review. For now: if you're nearby, I would strongly suggest you detour to Flour.

Cool, newish brewery in a great location, but they have to do a better job shoe-ing out those working on laptops, taking up seats and tables. When we walked in there weren't available seats on a fairly quiet Sunday early afternoon because several of them were occupied by "non-socializing" coffee-sipping, laptop users... despite the signs at the tables. Otherwise, decent place!

Nice little place to get lunch. The sandwiches are very tasty, especially the Cubano and the Meatball Sub. They only except cash which is annoying. Otherwise, I'd definitely recommend! I would go back for "2nd's"!!!

I ordered a delicious chicken melt sandwich from here. It was my first time coming here. The line is literally wrapped around the tiny little place - and out the door. So I would recommend going on off hours - or not right at the lunch rush. All of their sandwiches and specials look incredible. Honestly - my biggest complaint is cash only.... why!!! It's 2015 - I know there are fees and it's a small business, but who carries cash in this day and age! I don't even think my grandmother carries cash anymore......actually she probably carries her checkbook still.

I really liked this place. I love sushi, but have a difficult time finding good raw fish in the Boston area. I'd recommend this place to anyone who likes contemporary rolls and a good bottle of saki.

The price was about right-my bill came to what I anticipated. 

The only critical comment I have is the edamame was on the dry side. It tasted like it was previously frozen. The sushi was very fresh however, so all in all 4 stars baby!!!!

Visiting Boston for the night and wanted to check out a brewery close to my hotel. I live in Charleston, SC and I am a restaurant owner. I'm here on business. I have to say, the beer was solid but the customer service was neutral at best. There is clearly a difference when it comes to how staff interact with customers here and its borderline snobby mixed with a little indifference. One of my biggest pet peeves is the "cool" "hipster" places failing to understand why we are in the business to begin with -- and that's to serve people and offer a great food and beverage experience. When I asked for my check, the bartender literally tossed the clip board on the bar and muttered an insincere "thank you" under her breath. Welcome to Boston I thought. The best meal I had was at the Gourmet Dumpling House in Chinatown. Straightforward, affordable, friendly, and not pretentious at all..

We accidentally discovered this place while Yelping on the go at the Cambridgeside Galleria. Briefly:

-The food is delicious. I had such a craving for a Reuben, and they really delivered.
-The sandwiches are really large. As in, no need for a bag of chips on the side. This sandwich will be your meal.
-They have that quirky, indie, hipstery, Cambridgey feel that I like (in moderation). The whole restaurant looks like somebody carved out a kitchen, dining room, and living room of a house to turn it into a small sandwich shop.

The bottom line is that nobody should ever need to go to the food court in the Galleria (unless they are cashless or it's a weekend). There really is no excuse when you've got places like 2nd Street Cafe around the corner.

The newest outpost of the original famous Lizzy's Ice Cream Parlor is located in Harvard Square now, so you no longer have to make the trek out to Waltham.  It appears to be takeout only since there are no tables for sitting.  I had the chocolate cookie dough and it was yummers, but I'm not convinced that this place is better than J.P. Licks (although the prices at Lizzy's seem cheaper to me).  I have no doubt I'll be back to try this place again though (maybe one of the fruit flavors next!) since I love the Harvard Sq. area :)
Wow, this has to be the worst pizza ever.  It is so gross... I'm shocked it's able to survive.  Greasy, bland.. I don't even think it's real mozzarella cheese!

I got a tuna sandwich on white Italian bread that happen to be the freshest bread I have had in quite some time. The tuna was very nice complemented with just a few cranberries, I never order tuna out of the house but this Cafe did a great job! Friendly staff! But the cost was a bit high.  That's the only reason it's not a five star for me!

I went to mother juice to get an acai bowl, one that I had been looking forward to all day. I got there at 7:40 and they close at 8. Obviously not ideal for them but for someone that works all day that was the only time I could go. I asked for an acai bowl and was rung up accordingly when someone from the back came out and was annoyed that I had ordered the bowl. I apologized for coming in so late and she said it was fine and went back to make the bowl. When she came back out I apologized again and she said "it's fine I had just thrown something away that I shouldn't have."  I'm assuming that something was the almond butter or peanut butter ingredient because it was missing from the bowl and it is literally one of the key parts of the bowl! Even with that addition the bowl would have sucked. It was not good at all and it was SO SMALL. Seriously tiny for the $11 I paid. I get acai bowls all the time because they're amazing but I will never get one from mother juice again. Absolutely disappointing.

I highly recommend this as a healthy, fresh, and *affordable* lunch option in Kendall. I can see and pronounce all ingredients, get a ton of vegetables - and flavor - in my meal, and get out the door for under $10 (more than I can say for Tatte, Commonwealth, Bailey Sage, etc.). Yes, the juices seem a bit pricey (perhaps that's because I see juices as a meal addition, not replacement), but breakfast bowls and salad/entrees are all under $10.

I had the Keen What salad - it was flavorful, fresh, and felt like a solid amount of food for $8. I went in just before noon and got my food in under 5 minutes.

Staff was very friendly but not overbearing, helpful in answering questions, and efficient in serving customers. I'm happy to have Mother Juice in the neighborhood and look forward to trying their other options!

I wasn't a huge fan. Their ice cream wasn't awful, but it wasn't as creamy as I would have liked and there was nothing really impressive about it. I wouldn't mind getting ice cream here, but with so many other awesome ice cream parlors in Boston I'm not sure if I'll be coming again.  If you like something very light, this place might be the place to go!

Seriously man - smile.  You are in the hospitality business.  I made a mistake by not going to Clover and coming here.  Poor service, poor menu.  What's promising - the menu and the name.  All else is - zilch.

What I ordered - "Korean Quinoa Bowl" with quinoa, bean sprouts, carrots, sesame seeds, zucchini, scallions, fried egg, and soy sauce with spicy miso

What I received - "Whatever B&S Has on Hand Bowl" with quinoa, kale, carrots, zucchini, soy sauce and miso

I really like the idea of a quinoa bowl - something a bit different than a typical sandwich or salad. However, they were out of bean sprouts, fried egg, sesame seeds and didn't tell me. They subbed in kale without asking me along with hard uncooked stems. I added chicken for $2.50 and received ~maybe~ a scant 1/4 cup of diced chicken. 

$11.50 for something that doesn't even close to resemble what I ordered. Flavor was OK but for the money and lack of service I'd prefer to get what I asked for. 2 stars.

The sandwiches here are huge! Great spot if you're feeling pretty hungry and want something fast and cheap! My favorite sandwiches are the lady cafe sandwich (mozzarella, tomatoes, and basil with olive oil and balsamic vinegar) and the chicken pesto (grilled chicken with homemade pesto). The small sandwich is definitely big enough to share between two people and it's only $6.50!
By far the best sushi restaurant around the boston area. I first found this hidden gem in 2006 and have been a frequent customer since. I  have left each time feeling even more satisfied than before!

This place is overpriced and the quality is poor. My $9 salad was mostly freezing, waterlogged lettuce. They were clearly keeping the salad items too cold. This was the second time I've had something off temperature there. With so many other, cheaper options in Kendall there is no point in going here. It's too bad because with some very basic changes it could be way better.

I've actually never been inside. We order from here for lunch all the time. Pizza is good nothing special ,but I love their Steak-Tip salads. I've also had a few of their subs all of them were good. Delivery is typically very prompt and they even throw in a free 2 liter of Soda!

Ordered online takeout:

Vegetable Samosa  $2.95
Meat Samosa  $3.95 
Chicken Korma  $11.95
Peshawari Naan  $3.95
Tandoori Roti  $2.50
Kheer (Rice Pudding)  $2.95
Tandoori Seafood Masala $14.95

Everything was made fresh to order and was delicious.  Portions are right on and veg samosas were large!  The waiter was super nice and friendly and order was ready in 20 minutes just as they said.  This is not the closest India place to me but I am definitely coming back since they have quite a varied menu.  Loved it.

The service isn't friendly but it won't matter since you'll be spending a total of only about 15 seconds at the window, just long enough to pay while they prepare your styrofoam meal-on-the-go. 

Their menu is extensive, especially considering its from a truck and it is actually good. Better than some Chinese restaurants, but hey, they need credit considering that they are in a truck. It's a mix of Thai, Vietnamese and Chinese food and it is really, really cheap.

A strong competitor with Clover within the army of lunch trucks. I like it.

I have just gotten a quinoa bowl here and it's delicious! It was hectic at lunch but I thought they handled the crowd well. My only complaint about the quinoa bowl is that I wish there was more of the toppings besides quinoa in the bowl. I got the Jamaican jerk one and it could use some more fried plantains and sweet potato. The chicken in it is delicious!

Will be back to try more things!

great tasting food.  had the pad see ew beef, something with walnut and chicken, and a curry.

professional service.

super busy on a friday night so that's a good sign.

I'm just sitting here on a Tuesday night, watching re-runs of Lost and remarking at how Mona Lisa has outdone themselves AGAIN with their perfect pizza. My bf (who is a chef, if that says anything) and I eat it pretty much at least 3X a week. The guys at Mona Lisa call him "boss" and always smile at me as I'm walking by to catch my bus in the afternoon, even if I'm not getting a pizza that day.

It's the kind of pizza that's like the perfect chocolate chip cookie-- crunchy on the outside, soft on the inside. While with other pizzas I can be a toppings girl, with Mona Lisa I like plain cheese-- it's DELICIOUS and so pizza-y. mmMM.

You can't go wrong. You can get plain cheese pizza for an authentic, yummy sauce, crispy crust, soft inside, perfect crunch experience, or you can get a gourmet pizza with ricotta cheese, peppers and hamburger... however you do it, it will be delicious, and it will soon be your go-to place for take out or delivery!!!!!

Great new restaurant ! I've been trying to find a place serving 'decent' Indo-Chinese food... Although the range of Indo-Chinese cuisine is somewhat small, I really enjoyed their Shechuan Chicken Noodles ... Their service is great too... And affordable. Will likely go back.

I ordered the taco bowl and absolutely loved it!! It was incredibly filling, tasty and I will definitely be going back. The staff was very nice and accommodating. Overall, I had a spectacular experience at Mother Juice!

I came here with my lao peng you, Evelyn, and her jie jie after dinner for ice cream. We all agreed that the ice cream was significantly too cold. I would even go as far to say that it was unpleasantly so. Also, the almond toppings were below average. I've certainly had better almonds elsewhere.

3.45 for a kiddie cup of pistachio ice cream.  THREE. FORTY. FIVE.  I can get a pint of Ben and Jerry's at the grocery store for that!  Oh, and it was freezer burned.

Never again.

This place opened a few months ago and is absolutely amazing. Their veggie burger is the best I've had all over Boston and their milkshakes are downright delicious as are their waffle fries. The service is warm, friendly and very welcoming. I would highly recommend this place!

I loves me a GOOD REUBEN!

The little hole in the wall is within walking distance of where I work, and the sandwiches are great.  You REMEMBER the sandwiches you eat here.

I've been greeted each time I've walked in the door here, which isn't mandatory, but something else I remember, and it was nice.

I'd probably eat here more if they accepted cards, but that's NOT a reason to shun the 2nd Street Cafe.  If anything I should plan ahead to have a good lunch here.

Goosebeary's Food Truck is found parked outside of MIT Medical Center right off of Kendall Square's T stop. It's one of the oldest food trucks in the area, and their cheap food, served within seconds of ordering, is legendary. Unfortunately, their reputation was built up mostly in the past, and now it's just not a great experience anymore.

You can usually locate this truck, because it has the longest line (by far) at lunch time. Even so, this truck will probably take you the least amount of time, because once you step up to the window and order, it'll take less than 30 seconds for you to receive your dish. It's almost magical how they do everything in that tiny little truck.

Their menu is wide and varied, but most of the items are not really worth getting, IMHO. The best items include the General Gau chicken, the fresh spring rolls and the dumplings (Peking Ravioli). The Indian curry is not terrible, though the quality varies. They also serve rice congee, which can be good on a cold day. I'd stay away from the Pad Thai and the most of the dry chicken dishes.

As an undergrad, I remember paying my daily $2.50 for a piping hot styrofoam full of crispy General Gau. Maybe part of it is that I got older and don't appreciate really cheap food as much anymore, but today's truck doesn't live up to the halcyon days of my youth (I still work at MIT, so I have the regular opportunity to go, but I generally don't). I heard maybe 10 years ago that they did more than $1 million in revenue per year, and I'm sure they do much more now that prices are up to $4.50 for most dishes. We've seen the original food truck lady driving around in a very nice Mercedes and sitting at Cafe Armani in town, so I fully believe that. Hard work pays off, and they deserve to have done well.

BEST salads in Cambridge. The produce is always perfectly fresh. Also, the frozen yogurt is incredible! They blend the fruit right in front of you. The owners are always around and are very friendly. I highly recommend this place!

Juices, Acai Bowls, Smoothies, this is my morning paradise a couple of times per week for my breakfasts. This is as good as it gets for a Juice Bar.

The staff is always friendly, and the orders are served very fast, which is always a plus for a morning spot! This is my go-to destination now!
Great job creating this space on Newbury, this was a much-needed addition to the neighborhood!

Wow..... I can't stop raving about Mona Lisa to family and friends. The owner was friendly and very helpful. My mother is at Spaulding Rehab, which is walking distance from the sub/sandwich shop. I had an awesome grilled chicken salad with delicious garlic bread tonight.  The menu is large with a great variety to choose from. I look forward to trying their homemade pasta. I'm so glad to have a great choice for a hot delicious homemade meal.
THE small club for live music in Harvard Square. They get the best acts, consistently.

The food is incredible, try the dynamite sushi, salmon avocado sushi, steamed pork dumplings, popcorn sushi, and the crispy creme sushi
Definitely coming back
P.S. Fast and good service

Awesome, I love this place. I usually order a large chicken salad sub or a large pepper steak & cheese and each of them are my favorite sandwiches. A large sub, chips, and soda are only $9.50. Not bad at all. I used to go to the one in Boston during lunch but now I can go here because of their hours. They are open until 10pm every night and 8pm on Sunday. What a great deal. The one qualifier that I have for this location is that the radio is too loud. Another review also mentioned this too. The times I have been here, I feel like I needed to shout my order so they hear me to get it right. Other than that the food is excellent, fresh bread, large portions, and great prices. Can't go wrong, highly recommended.

the burger was moist and juicy. great flavor sitting upon a soft potato bun. waffle fries had the right amount of seasoning on it. good assortment of beers available. great new restaurant in the inman square area!

I refuse to discuss the merits or lack there of, from ordering food from a truck, but like all people in the Kendall area that suffer from the lack of choice for food options on a daily basis, sooner or later you go to the trucks. In this case out of all the Asian cuisine trucks in the area this one seemed to be the best to me. I've tried numerous dishes, like lo mien, General Gao's and graprao to name a few. The lo mien always seemed to be the best and its only $4 and you can spend an extra $1 for a spring roll. I wasn't a big fan of the General Gao's Chicken, the meat didn't seem right to me. Anyway, this food isn't going to knock your socks off, it's just going to be really cheap and the portion is enough to fill you up. Another good option is just getting a cup of hot and sour soup which is actually one of the better items.

Food is decent. The waffle fries were great, and the burgers are pretty good.

However, the ambiance is terrible. Music is way too loud. We ate quickly and left. I recommend takeout.

I was very excited to try Al's Harvard Square Cafe, particularly when I saw "Boston's Best Chicken Salad" on the menu (I really love chicken salad). 

For the first time since I've started eating chicken salad (somewhere around the same time tuna stopped repulsing me), I couldn't finish the sandwich. It was terrible! The menu said something about "the perfect amount of chicken, mayo, and seasonings" on the sandwich. By seasonings, they meant that they poured an entire box of Morton's salt into the chicken salad mix. Otherwise, I couldn't taste much seasoning. Between the bottle of soda I bought and my water bottle, I still couldn't power through that much salt to finish the sandwich.

I'd like to think that the other sandwiches might be better, but I just can't bring myself to try them. After all, the lunch box deal is a good one (a little over $6 for a small sub, bag of chips and a soda), except the selection of chips is pitiful and my soda wasn't even close to cold. 

I suppose you get what you pay for, but from now on I'll pay a bit more for food that is actually edible rather than spend less to just throw my food away.

Ladies and gentleman, we have a winner!! I went last night. It was crowded but the service was excellent. There were enough people running around to help fill your glass, refill your popcorn, or grab your check. Very cool people here. The beer was great and the variety was excellent. I was pleasantly surprised with the sour beer I tried (not usually a fan, but this was fantastic.) I went during cheese night, and the cheese was to die for and the two spreads were amazing!! I would recommend the chefs put more bread on the board next time, because I was basically throwing chunks of cheese in my mouth at the end. So excited for this place--check it out!! Great beer! Great service! Tasty snacks!

I wish I could put zero stars....

Every experience me and my coworkers have had ordering from this company has not been the best but after today... WE WILL NEVER ORDER FROM HERE AGAIN. We've had repeated issues with our orders not being filled correctly and when we contact them about these issues they REFUSE to take the blame for their poor service at the time. It's fine if it's not your fault... but don't tell the customer is not your issues. You never say that or that it's the customer's issue. You're providing a service. If you can't fulfill that service its your issue too. 

Back to the issue at hand. When we complained again today, the owner came to our place of business personally.  When she expressed her frustration to him with the lack of customer service, my coworker who expressed her concerns was lectured about her attitude (where she was professionally expressing her displeasure with the experience). She is not one to lose her cool. She's honestly one of the sweetest people I know. The owner of this "fine" establishment was demeaning to her and rejected to hear her concerns. After receiving a lecture from the owner about how their business works and its not his fault that we didn't get our correct order, we told him we didn't like how he was talking to our coworker. As he was leaving.  We told him to have a nice day and he said, "Don't worry I WILL. Never order from us again!" ..... Well ,gladly, buddy. I've never dealt with someone that unprofessional in my LIFE. 

This is a business that deals with customers. No matter how angry your customer is or may be you need to keep your cool and still provide the best possible service. Bailey and Sage doesn't know how to do this no matter who or how the customer is. It's disgusting and appalling that someone thinks they can treat a customer like that. If you want to get treated like dirt, then order from here. Really though avoid the experience we had and don't order from Bailey and Sage. AWFUL SERVICE, AWFUL MANAGER, even WORSE OWNER.
There is nothing to dislike. Cozy, Cambridge lunch with creative sandwiches and competent staff. Really welcoming atmosphere. Vegan options.

One of the few things i crave in cambridge, and always a first stop when i'n back!  The fresh baked bread makes it.  I highly recommend the meatball, steak piazzoli, chicken parm, and the new burger sib.  It is a 10 of 10!

The other reviews that refer to 9 Tastes with various synonyms for "mediocre" are right.  But sometimes, "mediocre" can be a superlative.  That's exactly the case here with 9 Tastes.  The food is nothing spectacular, but they do all of the staple items well -- pad thai, curries, and other noodle dishes leave you satisfied.

Perhaps more importantly, this is one of the best options for Asian cuisine in Harvard Square.  Just face it: everything in this part of town is a little overpriced and fairly unimpressive.  You should go in knowing what you'll get out of 9 Tastes: tasty food at reasonable prices (in kind of a strange basement layout).  It won't blow your socks off, but it's a solid option if you're looking for something to satisfy your craving for spicy food and/or concentrated umami, and you don't feel like venturing too far.

After all of the hype, I finally stopped by this joint for Lunch one sunny afternoon.  I ordered the WuBurger (which was essentially a double cheeseburger) which came with waffle fries and ultimately left satisfied.  Both the burger and the fries were cooked fresh and served still steaming (which I certainly appreciated) and the service was prompt and polite (the order only took about 5 minutes to come out).  

The burger was satisfactory and very filling if maybe a little under-seasoned.  I think they must smash the patties on the griddle since both patties were well-crusted but still cooked to medium (they didn't ask for a doneness preference).  The waffle fries though were another story.  Even though they were well-seasoned and served fresh, they still felt heavy and oily.  They could have possibly used some more time to drain, but then again maybe I'm just not a waffle fry kind of guy.

The layout has largely been preserved from back when this building was the location of North by Northeast, so while it's still kind of cozy, WuBurger has made pretty good use of the space (somehow managing to fit in a pretty respectable bar with four taps).  If you order take-out, I would recommend ordering over the phone ahead of time because there doesn't really seem to be a convenient place to stand and wait for your order.

On the subject of value, I paid $11 + tax + tip for my double cheeseburger and fries.  While some may scoff at that price, consider that most pub burgers in Inman (such as those found at The Rising, Atwoods, and The Druid) start at around $12-13.  Now, those joints also each have more space, a larger tap list, and more variety when it comes to the rest of the menu, but WuBurger's pricing is at least comparable if not competitive.  My one request would be that the owners consider adding a single patty version of the burger to the menu for $2 less (like they do at the Woburn location).   The double patty version is just a little much for lunch if I don't want to be in a food coma for the rest of my work day.

Although this place delivered a solid product, I think they have a lot of competition in the immediate area.  If you're local to the neighborhood, it's definitely worth stopping by to see if you're a fan - but I don't see people flocking from other neighborhoods with similar burger joints (such as Harvard Square or Central).  If I could, I would give this place 3.5/5 stars, but I'll gladly round up because of the great service.

Ice cream is just good. There are lots of places to get good homemade ice cream. But the other day I stopped in with my daughter and had a kiddie scoop of Charles River Crunch. Dark chocolate ice cream with almonds and toffee. It was so good I felt that I must give them an electronic pat on the back. Well done Lizzy's.
I went here for a bluegrass festival. The bands were great and they have $5 Narragansett beers. It's a small venue so you're right up in the action.
Super Healthy Super Tasty Food, Smoothies and Juices ! Staff is kool too!

Ugh. The Tom Yum soup was sickeningly sweet, there was tons of added sugar in it which just ruined the dish, I couldn't even finish the soup. The Jungle Curry I ordered featured watery curry with raw vegetables, mushy bananas (didn't go well with the dish at all), and dried stringy chicken. It was bad. I'm really surprised Zagat deemed this a "Very Good/Excellent" restaurant. Another tourist trap in Harvard Square.
We went there two times for the lunch buffet (7.95$ incl. weekends). The food was delicious (especially the warm Naan bread), and the staff is extremely friendly. We'll come back soon for sure!
Two words. Cambodian Seafood. Chock full of shrimp and scallops. Best seafood rice plate in Boston. (That's right, I'm putting the food truck fare up against the restaurants.) Four bucks. Recognize.

I visited Takemura with a couple friends on Saturday night and enjoyed it. It isn't the best or worst sushi that I have ever had, but definitely palatable. 

Portion size was nice, fish was fresh, prices were fair. There is a HUGE selection of specialty rolls, but almost all have something fried inside and a gallon of some sort of sauce on top. I know a lot of people like that, but when I do sushi I am trying to keep things light durnit!

Be aware the sake by the glass size is only about 2 ounces- other places like Yoki do about 5. They do have the giant Kirin cans though!

The service was a little harried but pleasant. The restaurant is very small so I think they are keen to move people in and out quickly. I would come back again, but maybe only if I was in a rush.

EDIT: Now in my fourth year of graduate school at MIT, really bored with the food options around here, but still WILL NOT eat at Goosebeary's after the three times I gave it a go. It's that bad. Go to the food court behind the Co-op instead -- even that is better!

Personally, I am all for eating food from a truck -- don't get me started on west coast taco trucks -- but I'm giving Goosebeary's one star because while I don't care where my food comes from, I do expect it to be good. And Goosebeary's is not. Vegetables are mushy, sauces are generic/not fresh tasting (fresh herbs would help), cuts of meat are kind of weird and sometimes tough. I go here after I've eaten from Anna's 17 times in a row and I just/can't/take it anymore. And then regret it and wished I had just gone to Anna's.

I should say that I am probably a particularly critical reviewer after having eaten my lunch in NYC Chinatown for years, where you can get food for half this price (no exaggeration) that is 1,000 times better. But still, I see no reason not to maintain a high bar for places in Boston. Don't give into food mediocrity!

My go to place for a quick bite for lunch. I usually get a steak and cheese with mushrooms and onions or the italian sub. It takes about 5-10 minutes for the order and it costs less than $7 for a small sub. Their small sub can easily fill you up as it compares to large subs at other sandwich shops.

This place is a fantiastic addition to the North Cambridge neighborhood. I'm a big fan of Indo Chinese cuisine, and I was not disappointed with Curry N Wok. I've been here twice now, and each time the food was consistently good, with an authentic Indo Chinese taste. My favorite dishes so far are the chilly fried fish (nice and spicy and delicious!) and the veg hakka noodles. There are still a lot more dishes I need to try- I saw someone eating what looked like chilly paneer and it looked great as well. 

This place is right on Mass Ave, but I've never had trouble finding parking within 1-2 blocks given its north Cambridge location. You could also take a bus straight up Mass Ave from Porter T stop.
Steak Bellino with provolone is better than anything I ever ate in Philly. I miss working in Harvard Square only because I'm so far from Al's and Clover.
I went to this place yesterday and I have been sick since. I think I got food poisoning from this place. I was so sick I didn't go to work.

I got their acai bowl and it is actually filling, i feel like some places acai bowls aren't very filling at all but here they actually put stuff in like pumpkin seeds and raisins and cashews as well as fruits on top so that sets it apart from their competitors in my opinion. it's a great place and prices are on par with its competitors, just personal preference i didn't love all of those things inside my bowl but it definitely did make it have more sustenance making my belly feel full! i'm sure you can also ask to take stuff out as well but i haven't tried.

What a good time!

Came here after my daughter's baseball scrimmage.  Gent who greeted us was friendly and specific about the menu and taking orders.  

Everything from Jack's Abby Smoke and Dagger to PBR available.  Great selection of brews.

My husband and daughter enjoyed their burgers.  I had the Coney Island Dogs and could barely finish one.  Please note that they are Pearl dogs with skin.  If my father were visiting for a quick lunch, he'd love this place.

TL;DR:  Good food, good service.  Go.

I was only disappointed here once, because it was closed. Well that and I once ripped my pants on the step. long story. anyway...
A coworker recommended this place to me. what a great suggestion. i frequent often. 
I love the people that work here, they are so friendly!! their favorite bread is  pumpernickel (with the roast beef sandwich). so i always order that. the portions are huge, so I only order half. the Asian mushroom soup is DELICIOUS.  if you are lucky enough, it will be on the daily changing soup menu.
tip: bring cash, the pumpernickel encased sandwich cannot be purchased with plastic.

Good for pad thai (dining in).  The takeout gets really clumpy, so I'd recommend going here to dine.  The service is typical for this cuisine.  They are quick/efficient/but may not speak English too well.  But, I go for the food not the conversation.
Loved the food! quantity is a lot! Finally a good indian restaurant in Boston.
Awesome music venue.  Great food (and now that they have their liquor license, a nice glass of wine as well) with a fun atmosphere!  A hidden gem in Harvard Square.

The large subs ($8.25) are good for a meal and a half. The workers are friendly. There's never a long wait. Just remember to order on the LEFT if you want a cold sub and on the RIGHT if you want a hot sub. Ignore any apparent line - there is no line here, just a disorganized mass of very hungry people.

3.5-4 stars.

The place was packed on a Saturday afternoon - standing room only even with the new back room that is open. The flights were $8-$9 and they pre-pick the beer for you which I am not the biggest fan of. I liked most of their IPAs and the free bar snacks were a nice perk!

Months ago I got an insider tip that Al's was expanding into Harvard Square, which I can get to on my lunch break. I was ecstatic. Al's State Street Cafe has been one of my favorite local lunch venues for a very long time, and not having one nearby has been one of the few frustrating things about working in Cambridge. After checking time and again, I finally saw it today. Al's Harvard Square Cafe has opened. 

If you've ever been to Al's State Street, you already know what to expect, but for those of you who haven't, here you go: 

Expect some chaos. Al's is loud. There is a constant din of customers and employees loudly calling orders back and forth. When it is busy, it's hard to tell which customers are waiting to order, which have ordered and are waiting to pay, and which have paid and are waiting for their sandwiches. There aren't lines so much as there are nebulae of people slowly weaving their way through each other. The chaotic nature is the only thing that takes a star off for me, and even then just barely. 

The sandwich menu is simultaneous elaborate and simple. While there is a large selection of cold and hot subs, with many options for mixing and matching ingredients, you don't have to worry at all about price. Larges (which live up to their name) cost $7.50, and smalls $5.50, made with fresh ingredients and bread baked in-house. The prices are the same no matter what you order or how many (or few) ingredients you ask them to stuff into that delicious pocket of crusty bread. 

As for the sandwiches, there isn't anything I've ever tried at an Al's that I didn't like. The eggplant parm, the chicken pesto, the cafe special (prosciutto, mozzarella, tomatoes and basil) and the lady cafe special (the cafe special without the prosciutto) have always been standouts for me. The lady I got for lunch today was as good, if not better, than ones I've had on State Street, and the scent wafting from eggplant parm I got for dinner had my mouth watering, too. 

One thing that may be confusing and add to the chaos early on is the layout. They take orders for cold subs and assemble them on the left side, while taking orders for the hot subs on the left. However, the giant overhead menu has the hot subs on the left and the cold on the right. In the short time I was inside, I saw several customers confused by that, and I hope they switch them around. 

The new location is in the Holyoke Center in Harvard Square. If you aren't looking for it, you may not find it, as it isn't visible from the street. I'm hoping this doesn't hurt the Cafe's ability to survive in Cambridge, because I plan to go here all the time. Thank you Al, for bringing your delicious subs up closer to my neck of the woods!
Good Indian Chinese food! It was too spicy, even when I asked for 3/5 on the spiciness scale, it was more of a 5/5. However, quite flavorful. Trying their delivery service next.

The mango salad with chicken is so freaking good, I could eat it every day (and for $4, sometimes I do).  They also have a rather delicious seafood curry thing which claims to be a special, but they seem to have it daily, so I don't know how special it could possibly be.  In any case, it's tasty.  Also, props for offering brown rice for us refined carb watchers.

I totally love Monalisa. Their Veggie Sub is amazing! The hamburgers are fairly big and the pizza is good! The food is very cheap and tasty!

Most of people that work there are very nice, but the bosses are awful! They have no patience, will probably mistreat you, ignore and tell you to wait (and never come back).

Best acai bowls ever!! So yummy, big, filling, and they give you a lot for your money. Pretty presentation and just go good! The people who work there are really nice, too. Ask for extra agave and almond butter!

I walked past this restaurant several times while taking my dog out for her daily stroll, and finally decided to give it a shot one day.  I ordered take out, and even though the girl on the phone said it was her first day, she was very pleasant and I have no complaints.  My food arrived in a fair amount of time and everything was hot and fresh.  The chef even went out of his way to make me chicken korma, even though it wasn't on the menu (veg korma is, so I knew they could make the sauce).  Weirdly enough, they don't have naan here, only paratha.  The flavors were average, and while I'll be back, I am not in love.  I wish there was a little more kick to the sauce though.

I ordered a DIY sundae party for an orientation event. When I called to put in my order, they were great, letting me know that I could do 1 more flavor than I thought, and helping me figure out how many gallons of each and what might be popular. 

At the actual event, they delivered a bit early (better than late!), and everything ran really smoothly. Everything was absolutely delicious, including the dairy-free sorbet and Tofutti. The only downside was that we had a TON of toppings left over, but my guess is they decide how much to send based on kids; parties, and the grad students we were serving were a little more sparse with toppings.

The ice cream was great, the toppings were great, the service was great. I would definitely order again!

Fun atmosphere! Waffle fries were pretty great here. Burgers were tasty! I like how they serve alcohol here too. Kind of a small place so it can get crowded here pretty quick. Not a great wait area for take-out. I will most likely be back!

I've eaten here a few times in the month I've been working in Kendall. I'm vegan, so anyplace that has food I can eat automatically gets a couple of stars. So far I've had the red curry tofu and mango curry tofu, and both were very good. It is astonishing both how quickly they serve the food, and how they manage to provide that volume of food out of a truck. I can't imagine how big the vat is inside that they're scooping it all out of.

Lizzy's has really solid ice cream for sure! The store is tiny and can get really crowded easily and a little overwhelming. The Chocolate Orgy flavor is decadent, banana flavor is super good and their seasonal Red Velvet Cake flavor is pretty tasty. Service was kind and timely and portions were generous.

Overall I would definitely recommend going to Lizzy's instead of JP Licks if you're in Harvard Square. If you're in Cambridge in general, Christina's Ice Cream in Inman Sq is my favorite but Lizzy's is a good runner up!
Man this was an EXCELLENT sandwich.  Good ingredients and the sourdough was great.  Loved that they accepted LevelUp, too.

I see a lot of potential in this place, and I know it's new, so I think if they change a couple things, it could be great. The location is perfect and enticing. You can order your salad/bowl and go eat it on top of the Kendall Square rooftop garden. Kendall Square doesn't have very many healthy food options, so this is a very nice addition. The staff was very friendly, and I like that you can build your own salad. 

I ordered a salad with romaine, beets, artichokes, cucumbers, and apples, with a lime vinaigrette. I was expecting it to be amazing, especially with all the delicious toppings. Unfortunately, all I could taste was the lettuce. The toppings were very sparse; just a tiny scoop of each one of them. I maybe got 5 or 6 tiny pieces of granny smith apple in the entire salad, and could barely taste the artichokes or beets. It would be great if they gave you more toppings. 

The cannolis looked amazing. I had to stop myself from ordering one. Maybe next time.

Gooseberry's is the best of the Styrofoam Warriors of Kendall Square (so the Trucks, plus the Food Court options), in that the food does not leave you disgustingly full.  If you go with the right sauce (in my case, teriyaki), you can survive the experience better than with, say, most stuff from the Food Court.

Working on the East side of MIT (EAST SIDE!), we don't have many options.  ABP is there.  Cosi's line is too long, Rebecca's was never that appealing, and Delicious Lunch Box stopped parking in front of E40 (best truck name ever, by the way).  While I'm usually at ABP, if I need some variety, Gooseberry's has it.

Note: The hours Yelp lists for Bailey & Sage are off; my understanding is that they're open til 8pm... or I was told by a manager they were last Friday, at least.

But, really, I'm here to give these folks due props: 

I grew up on the east coast, and then spent six years in California before moving back east and further north to Boston this year. I also used to think I hated vegetables... that is, before the land of fruits, nuts, crazy granolas and baby-spinach-picked-that-morning local farmers markets taught me what fresh vegetables really were. 

And in the middle of my first winter here, I miss them terribly.

When I first walked into this place, I suddenly felt like I'd left the land of tiredly unsmiling black and navy, pearls and colonial lace collars. Hip music, a modern yet not in-your-face vibe, casual yet competent staff expertly chopping vegetables next to the stir-fry bar. I was noodling over which quinoa bowl I wanted to try, and the chopper asks if he can help. He says the big difference between the two bowls is really whether I prefer water chestnuts over zucchini. And I suddenly feel like it's ok to ask... "I don't want to sound like a snob, but are the water chestnuts.... fresh...?"

"EVERYTHING here is cut and made fresh daily. Including the water chestnuts. No soggy canned chestnuts here. And we don't feed you leftovers."

I'm not even sure if it's 100% true. But the quinoa bowls do have FRESH VEGETABLES that ACTUALLY TASTE GOOD. And you can get tasty steak or shrimp or chicken or tofu if you want, too.

...Fast forward six months: My best work-friend and I love, love, love this place. We eat here at least weekly, usually more, and we cannot get enough of those bowls (particularly the Korean and Aztec bowls). I usually ask for 'mostly vegetables' vs 'more quinoa' and they always accommodate me. My friend also loves their rosemary focaccia that comes with the lunch bowls and soup. I love that I'm allergic to wheat and gluten-free is actually listed on the soups.

I was honestly a little confused and sad about their low yelp stars, until I realized they might be for a) things I've still never tried - salads and sandwiches, b) the place is a bit pricey. Yes, I am one of those people willing to pay a slight premium for fresh and tasty quality. (No, I am not a Googler.) I love their smoothies, too, but the fact that I can get a similar fruit/juice smoothie at Whole Paycheck for half the price puts it in a bit of perspective. 

Also, you guys have neglected to give us eggs the last four times we've come for quinoa bowls. When we asked, you said you were out? We love you, but if it's on the menu, it should be in the bowl, or at least let us know! 

Regardless, thank you for your fresh awesome. You make me not hate vegetables in wintery Kendall Square.

Was going there nearly everyday since they opened. Fell in love with their Seitan Reuben sandwich the first time there, and had it every day for a while. However, I noticed as the week went on the portion was getting smaller, and I thought maybe it was because I started getting it as a bowl rather than a sandwich. Then today I ordered the sandwich around 1pm. It was not very crowded, but there was a line at the sandwich station. Only one person attending. It took 20 minutes to get my sandwich. I decided to pay ahead as I was waiting so long. Was talking to my friend in the salad line and watched as they made the chopped salad, a great deal of which was ending up on the floor as they were frenetically cutting up  the salads up with a pizza cutter. 

Then I got my sandwich. Opened up the foil, and it was even smaller than the first time, and it was soggy, was not cut through, and had pastrami pieces in it (not appetizing for a vegetarian to see). Clearly, they did not clean out the pan before heating the seitan. I went back and returned the sandwich, and asked for my money back, the cash register person was great and got the person who was the on duty manager. I asked her to refund my card, and she went to the register, and I thought would ask for my card. Instead she started waiting on customers who had formed a line at the register. I waited another minutes, and couldn't wait any longer, it had already been 30 minutes there, and said I needed to leave, figuring I would be out the nearly $10 for the sandwich I couldn't/didn't eat. She asked me to write my number on a lunch bag, and said the manager would call me. This place seems great and has great ideas; however the execution and consistency are not there. Pretty understaffed for the size of their menu. Plus given that it has just opened, there should be an owner/manger there at all times. Somewhat disappointing. We'll see if I get a call?

Update: Went back to get refund, they said had to wait for manager. Left my CC info. Got call back from manager, said he would talk to his staff, and would refund. I'd say worth trying out, but if you find inconsistent service or food, might want to go to Clover. Went there got food in 3 minutes, no waiting, and was spot on.
What a great little place! Artsy with good beer.  Can't be beat for small music venue with different kinds of music.

Enter Al's in the Holyoke Center with a bit of patience if you roll up at lunchtime. If your blood sugar is crashing and you are getting cranky pants, you might want to get there before the lunch crowds M-F. Seriously. Happens to me. I get it. 

That being said. Al's is a sandwich shop. A to-go sandwich shop. Hit up Al's for your standard lunch time turkey sando or maybe something fancy pants like a bbq chicken sub. Go bananas. Get spicy stuff on there. Let your hair down. Throw caution to the wind. Leave your man. Get mayo on that ham and cheese. Live a little. 

Get the lunch special. Bag lunch. Small sando. Chips. Soda. Be American. Embrace it.

Ice cream, ice cream, how I love thee...

Now that I've discovered Lizzy's in HSquare, HSquare just gained about 10 brownie points.  The ice cream is creamy, milky-delicious; there are loads of flavors; and get this, they have mini-cone options! So for those of you (like me) who don't like having to choose 1 (or 2) flavors, you can get mini-cones for $1.50.

Admittedly, I've never gone the mini-cone way, preferring to get 2 giant scoops instead :) Coffee is tasty, but the oreo and mint oreo are delicious^2.

It's a tiny shop with only 3 stools but I usually go at random times in the late afternoon so I've never had to wait in line. PLUS, they're open until 11 PM - read the article on the wall that details the owner's love of ice cream late at night, man after my own heart.

If you would have told me I would have liked a Thai food place I would have called you a liar.  I would have found a glove to smack you with so I could challenge you to a duel!

Well, the glove is in your hand, my friend, and it is your turn to smack away...because this place is damn good.

The menu has a ton of options - even for the food allergy weary such as myself.  

The staff was great - very attentive and polite.

The prices were good - considering the portions and that it is in Harvard Sq.

Our dinners came out insanely fast even though the the restaurant was somewhat busy.

All in all I will definitely be back to this place - which again, had you asked me prior to my visit I would have laughed in your face had you told me that.

Just goes to show you, try new things and be pleasantly surprised!

1 taste down..8 more to go...

The sandwich I had was delicious! My friend also thoroughly enjoyed her sandwich. We also ordered the peanut butter brownie and blueberry lemon tart. Both were great! The only criticism I have is about the service. My friend and I were ordering our desserts and I kid you not the worker tells us to hang on and puts up his finger to us so he can chat up his buddies who walked through the door. Seriously?

Honestly, there aren't many great lunch choices around the Kendall area.  This place has pretty good sandwiches and tasty salads.  Be prepared for the crowds, though; everyone else has also found this little gem.  It sure beats Au Bon Pain or Galleria sushi!

Some of the soups are great as well, but the gazpacho was pretty unimpressive.
Wow what a great space for creativity.  The music here has  resonance that you can feel.  I'd you are looking for good food and drinks and world class music well this place is for you.

I was fooled by its name...thought this place could be a decent sushi place in Cambridge...should have read other reviews a little more carefully.
Good sushi place should not smell "fishy" inside restaurant to begin with...as soon as I walked in, the fishy smell was there...at this point, I should have changed my mind and left without eating there. But I was hungry for sushi...so, I tried.
Tuna roll was smashed and squeezed rather than cut...rice was too soft and too sweet for my taste. Hardly with vinegar flavored...I wonder if this place uses real rice vinegar for sushi rice.
Then, sushi pieces - Tuna was very thin and no flavor on; Salmon was probably best out of all but had a little fishy smell; Hirame was totally out of question, mishandled; Unagi was covered by heavy sweet sauce; Izumidai (tilapia) was too fishy...and price...may be because of Cambridge, but it was more than expected for sure.
Last word - I will not go back to this place.
Good place to do social networking, no more tables but standing way. Beer is nice, don't drink too much.
Had the chicken salad sandwich. Not nearly as good as other locations. The ordering process was also wacky.

Major props to my friend Molly for inviting me to catch some music here -- somehow, despite three years in Boston and many, many shows I had never heard of this venue. 

Club Passim is small and intimate (I mean this in the "cozy" real estate sense of the word; seating was actually pretty crowded). Since CP offers food and beverage service through Veggie Planet during performances, servers have a tough time making deliveries. 

Despite the cramped drawbacks, though, this means you're likely close to the stage. Even people in the back had great seats because the acoustics were excellent, with sharp & clear sound. The acts we caught were one and two-person bands, which makes audibility especially important. 

I was surprised and happy to hear that Club Passim is a nonprofit, handing out artist grants and running outreach programs in the community. They also host a music school. After being burned so often by Ticketmaster "convenience" fees it's really nice to know my admission money's going toward the greater good. 

After a lovely evening of roasted squash and goat cheese pizza, folksy harmonies and craft beer, I'm definitely planning on a return trip.

When one thinks of live acoustic music in Boston this is where one pictures it.  Forget the historical reasoning (by which I mean Bob Dylan, Bruce Springsteen, Tracy Chapman, Susan Vega, etc. have all played here), instead, focus on what it is.  What this place is is a cozy little venue where people go to listen to talented people make music and they get to do so for less than $20 bucks almost every night!

I've been coming here since I was 8 and it is the model I use to compare other venues to.

As a warning, this is a listening room.  Do not come here to take a load off and chat with your friends, you will be scowled at most fiercely at best and told off by the performer at worse.  This doesn't sit well with some of the crowd that loves gum flapping, but its because of this that for everyone that's come to listen, there is an intimate availability between yourself and the performer available on a nightly basis.

Great place to dine if you want to ...
 - Wait in long lines
 - Have the cooks treat you rudely
 - Have the cooks forget your order
 - Have the cooks not apologize for serving the 6 people behind you in line first (b/c of forgot order)
 - Pay more than the food is worth

Keep doing a great, 2-star job, Bailey Sage!

Reposting under the correct address:

So by now I have tried every juice they make, breakfast bowls, two different smoothies and now the curried sweet potato lunch as well as the quinoa what dish. 
All I have to say is YES! The curry was awesome and SO FILLING yesterday. I felt great all afternoon and only wanted a small dinner last night. Today I am having the quin-what - a bowl of quinoa and beets with kale, avocado, carrots, and a tahini lime dressing i believe. SO YUM! 

the people that work there are wonderful, and it has been a saving grace for myself to eat better during work hours when I get busy and don't prepare food at home. 

The place is small and everyone works really hard. They are busy from open to close it seems, so people should ease up on giving them bad service reviews - they are new and flooded during lunch time, and trying not to mess up orders. They're extremely helpful and had explained to me that weekends they are only open Saturday to give juices and smoothies since they fill orders, themselves, for cleanses. I think for a new place and the same people each day, they deserve a break! How else does a growing business grow?

I think $9 on a juice that does not have added water is well worth the price also, especially considering most people spend $6-10 on crappy processed breakfast every morning anyways - as a busy culture.

This Mother Juice is at a convenient location and is easy to find. My favorites are the overnight oats, chocolate-coated stuffed dates, and The Cure. I have tried the Almond Love smoothie with blueberries but I found it to be a pretty heavy smoothie. I think their items are pricey for what they are but they are very good!

I grab lunch here usually once a week.   Best local lunch place close to my work.   Delicious quality food my fav is chicken Caesar salad but they have a lot of great sandwiches too.   Daily soups are yummy but I think they are all puréed so gotta be in the mood for that.   Have cash when u go they only take cash or its $3 for the ATM in there.  Second street always ends up being my go to when I can't decide what to get for lunch.   Pretty consistent.

First time coming here and this place was awesome. I had me a slice of buffalo chicken and a cheese burger sub, the food was delicious! And the price was also amazing! And on top of that their service and friendliness was top notch. I would have to make the journey back here for sure.

Obsessed with this place! Great, hip, trendy joint with amazing health foods! Amazing for vegans but also for anyone looking to eat healthily and clean! I had the berries smoothie and the sun butter toast. My friend had the avocado toast and the almond love smoothie. All were great and would highly recommend! Cannot wait to go back and try more of the menu!

3/5 may seem low for a place that just opened but I'm going to revisit at least once more and then will update.  My Lamb Rogenjosh was....... confusing.  This is one of the few places that trusted me when I said 'extra spicy' so it gets a big thumbs up there. But the lamb....the poor, poor overcooked lamb.  The sauce was top notch, some of the most intense flavors I'd had in the area.  If only the lamb was edible :(  I ended up throwing more than half away.  Sadness quickly ensued.

I'm giving it's 3/5 because I hope the lamb was a one-time thing. If all of the recipes are as flavorful, then it might just need some tinkering with cooking times.

Terrible customer service. They close at 8 but stopped serving quinoa at 7 with no signage because the staff wanted to clean it up. That's just pure laziness. If you don't want to serve food until 8, close earlier.
Cute place, great Harvard Sq location, great prices. Delicious food. If you want Thai that wont kill your budget, go here!

Great little taste of Japan. It really reminds me of cute little basement places in Kyoto and Okayama. Quick service, awesome location, nice sushi. The atmosphere is so comfortable to me. Oh, and they give you a nice hot towel for your hands when you sit down.

I took a look at the seating chart before heading over to my first concert at Club Passim - it is a lot tighter in person!  The square tables were lined with metal folding chairs placed in close proximity to one another.  If all the seats were filled, it would be very crowded.  But fortunately for us (if not for the entertainers) the seats behind us were empty.  To sit at these tables, each person is supposed to order at least $5 worth of food.

The stage is also a lot smaller than I had presumed.  But  it made for a much cozier experience.  And I had a great time cause both the opening act (Seth Glier) and the main show (Kevin So) were fun and funny.

Our dishes arrived just after the lights went off as the show was about to start, so we couldn't really see what we ate and the pictures turned out fuzzy.  There were large florets of broccoli in the mac & cheese which was of a decent portion.  The large half and half pizza ($11.75) of Safe'n Sound with spinach and Dinner for Henry arrived sliced in four quarters and was pretty good, but slightly too crispily burned in spots.  I preferred Henry's side, especially the part covered with the fresh mozzarella.

The waitress was friendly and checked up on us often enough, while still allowing us to enjoy the concert.  She also offered to split our check or consolidate based on our preference when we placed our orders.

Bathrooms, there are two, and they are for either gender!  That I like!  The men can wait along with us women. ;)

They validate for parking at University Parking ($6), but make sure you get your white ticket back along with the yellow validation ticket.  Sometimes they have volunteers helping out who may or may not know how this validation thing is handled.

Shady online ordering, very disappointed. 

Walked the whole way over to the restaurant after ordering take-out online. When I get there they say they cant make the order for this price (the one listed on their website) because their website is wrong. I told them they should probably update their website. They said they were trying but as for now, the price was wrong. 

Very disappointed that they did not honor the sale they made to a long-time customer. I will not be ordering from them anymore.
I've been coming here regularly for about a year and the food has been consistently delicious! The servers and owner are friendly as well.
