People run after this truck with tongues lolling wildly -- and for eats that can be bought with couch cushion change it's no wonder. Expect Vietnamese, Thai and Cambodian slopped into styrofoam, carelessly thrown together food, warm drinks, gruff service. The fresh springs rolls are good in the mouth, in the gut, and on the pocket. Stock up for a yummy afternoon snackerooo.

Pretty solid food, but they have a tendency to run out of things around closing time.

Closes at 3pm so not for folks who like to have their lunch later in the day.

A bit of serendipity landed us @ Harvest this past weekend and we are total converts! It's easy to find mediocre Indian food in Boston - so we had zero expectations. From the first minute, we were amazed by the positivity and friendliness of the staff, not to mention the clean and charming dining area and kitchen. The food was amazing! We are no Indian cuisine experts, but compared to most places - we got a sense that Harvest uses the best ingredients possible. The food felt lighter and healthier. Also, they were keen on meeting our every need - whether it was making sure we had just the right spice level, or concocting a lactose-free mango lassi.  Harvest of India has become our favorite Indian restaurant in the city!

Wow this sandwich shop is the best I have seen in long time. My family had 2 chicken Parms, 1 ham sandwich and a natural burger.

While the ordering process can be confusing, we are at Harvard, so I am sure people are smart enough to figure it out. Once you order the food, it came out fast and it was Delicious. My sons natural burger was a hamburger on sub bread and was tasty also. The chicken parms were the best I have ever had.

Seating is very limited in the Holyoke Center,  but it would be great to get on the go.

Quality of the fish in terms of how it looked was disappointing-it didn't look very fresh.  However, it tasted fine and neither I nor my date felt ill afterward.  Prices are okay and the decor of the place is acceptable.  I'm still searching for a dependably good sushi place in Cambridge...

Meh. Paid almost $9 for a strawberry banana smoothie. Space is small, menu is pretty simple, I just don't see how the overhead costs are enough to justify charging NYC prices for something that doesn't even require an entire kitchen setup.
Fantastic Sandwich shop.  They do not skimp on the fillings. Very crowded during the lunch rush, but well worth the short wait just to take your sandwich to go.

I had lunch there 5 times. Every time they served me with dirty dish and silverwares. Dish still had fat and oil on the surface, silverwares with rice sticking to them.

Food taste okay. But 1* for uncleanness.
Best sandwiches in town. Nice seating area. I think I like this Darwin's better than the one on Mt. Auburn...

This is a new burger joint in Inman, and in all aspects, it was a great experience. I would most likely give it 4.5 stars if I could. As a note, I reserve five stars for places where I am seriously impressed or surprised by the food, service, or ambience.

I first expected the restaurant to be organized more like other fast casual restaurants like Chipotle or Shake Shack, where you order at the counter and then pick up food yourself. But it is a sit down restaurant with a small bar; you can also order take out. People ordering take out did not seem to have anywhere to stand, and I can see it being chaotic if the place gets busy. The restaurant is clean and nicely decorated. 

Service was friendly, attentive, and extremely fast - I was pretty impressed how quickly I received my burger. Definitely at fast food speeds in a nice sit down environment. 

Food wise, I love waffle fries in general. Just for that, this place has a slight edge over other burger spots. Their waffle fries are piping hot and crispy - fresher than Chickfila's sometimes limp waffle fries. The burgers here are 'simple diner style' rather than zany combinations like Boston burger company(?) There are a lot of topping options, but I stuck with a simple combination. The burger was flavorful, moist, and not overly greasy with a good 'crust'. I still find shake shack's burger to have that best slightly crunchy exterior that I love in a burger. Potato bun was soft and excellent too; the burger did not fall apart at the slightest. These burgers come with two patties, which is odd, but really quite good and very filling. Out of curiosity, I looked at their other location's menu and it was structured slightly differently, where you can customize the number of patties. Value wise, it seems pricey at 11$ for a double cheeseburger, but given the burger is quite big and comes with fries, I think it's a good price. 

I highly suggest checking the place out if you live nearby and have a burger craving. And if you do not want a thick pub style burger from the Druid. Wu burger is also worth eating in, since the atmosphere is pretty nice and the service is friendly/fast. The food is fresher that way!
Had the lunch buffet.  It was okay.  Pickles were the highlight.  Probably wouldn't go back for buffet other than the price.

I think Thai and/or Laotian people who go here would be insulted by the 4 &5 star reviews for 9 Tastes--my girlfriend certainly is. 

This is a place to go when you want somewhat affordable, decently cooked dishes that have a Western twist to it. But in no way is some of this food remotely authentic.

The Indonesian fried rice is very filling to me BUT OK in terms of taste. The dish had tasty chicken satay enhanced by the smooth peanut sauce and a plentiful plate full of rice. But you could tell that the dish was not made fresh at all. How hard is it to make fresh fried rice, really? Not hard at all. 

I ordered the crispy duck on another occasion when some friends wanted to come here. The duck was okay, very crispy and goes very well with the sweet plum sauce that accompanies the dish. But I've had better since I've gone to Asia and had duck prepped there. Furthermore, the greasiness of this duck left me bloated and with a stomach ache. Ouch.

Overall, this place is good but not great. Probably great to Westerners and novices of Asian cuisine, but insulting to Asians and foodies who actually have gone to Asia and/or had home-cooked Thai, Indonesian, and Laotian cuisine.

Spend your dollars here if you have a fix for Thai food and have nowhere else/ better to go to in your time constraints.

This place was nothing special, came here with my friend who was visiting from NY  , her food was really good, mine on the other hand was bland and tasteless! I ordered the veggies with rice, veggies had no sauce or salt or any flavor at all! Had to use the peanut sauce they gave us for the salad to flavor the veggies. I wouldn't recommend this place if you are familiar with Thai food as I am, I've had ALOT better Thai than this. But I have to say that their Thai iced tea was good.
Good service. Good food. Fast delivery. Friendly staff. Close to work. Can't go wrong with this place! My good to place!

Great imitate setting for concert watching. Waiter service before and during the show which is very convenient - both drinks and food from Veggie Planet Restaurant. A few poles block views to the stage and tables/chairs are crammed in to allow as many concert views as possible so it's pretty crowded. They bring in up and coming artists and some come back multiple times if you want to see them again!

The Good: 

large slices of pizza - wicked cheap!
good salads - on a real plate with a real metal fork!
the guys are really nice and have funny musical ringtones on their phones...
the place is pretty big, and there is seating for about 12-15 people
good chicken tenders and jalapeno poppers

The Bad:

if you accidentally show up at lunch A or B for the high school, you could inadvertently get trampled to death
sometimes if you show up after said lunch breaks, they don't have slices and you have to wait
sometimes they run out of salad base (mixed greens for some salads) and you have to get something else

The Ugly:

sometimes parking is a bitch, but... that should be taken for granted. it's really not that ugly, I just thought I should finish the trio...

I think this place is pretty good. Will be trying it again for breakfast. While I was there with a friend we had both samplers. Some were good and some I didn't like, which is okay. They have a good cross section of beer and make a decent IPA. It was really crowded after work, so we didn't stay too long. Cool environment though.
One of the best breweries in Cambridge/ Boston area. Great variety of beers and they also offer delicious kombucha:)

4/5 stars for the food - 1/5 for the delivery service.

Really wanted to give this place a better review, as we ordered regularly from them in the past. However, we have since learned our lesson: they consistently quote delivery times that are 40 minutes to an hour earlier than your food will arrive. Typically, I call and check on the order status, they make some loud excuse, and the order will take an additional 20 minutes. 

That just won't fly when you're craving your tikka.

Harvest has some of the better Indian food in the area, but I wouldn't recommend ordering in from them anytime soon, unless they get their s*** together.
I am standing in line. Staff ran in from outdoor, put gloves on and started making salads. No hand washing.  Don't think I will eat here as often.

I've loved this place since I first visited, and many trips have been taken since.

The buffet is an excellent choice too if you have time in the afternoon for lunch and boasts a freshly-made and wide selection. 

Not expensive at all, but the food's quality is very good. Recommended.

The reuben at this place is so delicious that it has inspired "Reuben Tuesday" at my work. Depending on the circumstances of the week, anywhere from 5-10 of us place our reuben order with our Reuben Tuesday sponsor and await the arrival of that delicious sandwich. I'm a big fan of this place for lunch. The reuben does stand out, but all of the other sandwiches, salads, and entrees I have had here have been fantastic! Super nice staff just adds to the awesomeness of this place. Go here for lunch, soon! If you're up for a reuben, I'll see you on Tuesday!

P.S. They now deliver larger orders for lunch! ($45 or more, you have to order the day before.)

I've eaten lunch here on a few occasions and it has always been a positive experience. The food is good, the service is polite and fast, and the price and location are right.

Personal favorites are the meatball sub and the grilled chicken sandwich with pesto and mozzarella.

I was looking for a brewery during my stay in Cambridge and came across Lamplighter.  It was a little walk from where we were, but proved to be worth it.  They have a nice selection of beers with the choice of getting a couple of different flights.  I went with the bring on the funk, which if you like funky beers this is for you.  The space was a nice atmosphere.  It was pretty crowded when we were there, but we were able to snag a seat by the window and enjoyed our beers.

I was excited to find a new lunch place when strolling through the Holyoke Center in Harvard Square. Al's didn't disappoint. The eggplant parm sub I ordered was delicious and my husband said that his meatball sub was also yummy. The price is right too -- $5.50 for a sub and $6.75ish for a sub, chips, and drink. The only downside is that there isn't a place to sit, so it's a lunch on the go kinda place. This will definitely become one of our regular spots!

I'm not a fan of curry, but I am a huge fan of pad thai.  I think 9 tastes has the best pad thai I've had in the Boston area.  It's slightly sweeter than most places.  I also think they have the best crab rangoons.  The price though for them is expensive for the quantity you receive, but still worth trying.

Rather flavourless and unexciting Indian food. Went with a group and ordered a bunch of dishes to share, and none of them stood out as worth having again. Chicken was dry, lamb was gamey, medium spicy had no heat. The dishes were also small and had very little meat for the cost. Maybe the buffet is better, but I wouldn't recommend this place for ordering off the menu.

Worth seeing a show at just for the unique lounge atmosphere.  When you arrive, everyone is seated at a table, where you seem to remain throughout the show.  We saw a sort of jazzy act, so maybe that explains it, but I think this still a place to sit and enjoy the music rather than a raucous concert hall.  The drinks are reasonably priced for Harvard Square too.
Phad Thai and 9 Tastes Phad Thai are tasty. 9 Tastes Phad Thai's house sauce gives it a saliva-inducing, tangy vinegar taste. Delicious!
Food ranges between 3.5 and 5*, price is 5, and the subs are huge.  Solid 5* overall.

Decent food - I ordered the khao soi which is rare to find in a Thai restaurant as a dish of Burmese origin, I believe. The dish was quite good - my only complaint would be the shortcut they used for the fried noodles that typically go in the dish. They used some sort of prefried snack material you get in most Asian stores and I was really hoping for real fried noodles. Oh well. (Pro tip if anyone else is a huge fan of khao soi - I really like Similan's.)

The service was really fast in getting our food to the table but it also kind of stunk that they were so busy that they asked us to leave a few min after we paid. I understand that though, but fair warning it's not a place to have a long, casual evening meal on the weekends if you're looking for that.

A wonderful little joint- quaint interior & friendly staff. The burger was excellent- the double patty was juicy and sizzling, the sauce was amazing and yet didn't drown out the rest of the burger... all encapsulated in a toasty and delicious potato bun. The waffle fries were also excellent (though I wish they gave more fries!)

4 stars and not 5 because of the price. I guess it's 'gourmet' for a reason

I decided to check out Bailey and Sage for lunch today.  

When I saw the huge signs hanging that gave me all these options of my sandwich, I said, meh, I don't have the time to pick and choose this and that and found the specialty sandwich area where...

I had the special Grilled Portobello and Goat Cheese sandwich for around $10 (ouch).  The sandwich ingredients were very good (hopefully for $10 it better be!) and the roll was nice.  I am not sure what was heated up on the panini press, though, since the sandwich was still cold when I received it back.  I think the bread was way to thick for the heat to penetrate or something.  And, oh, the sandwich was a decent size.

While the sandwich was good, their prices are way too high for me to eat there on a regular basis.  It is still only mushrooms, some peppers and goat cheese folks.  The décor was pleasant but not too many seating options.  It is basically an up scale take on food to go.

If I can offer one constructive criticism, it is this:  those who, like me, were visiting for the first time, did not know where to stand to order specialty sandwiches.  Very chaotic.  The restaurant set up is confusing and the general flow could be better. Either a sign is needed (line forms here) and the folks behind the counter need to direct the flow (or have someone on the floor directing).

Authentic Indian food. Had podi masala dosa and chicken chettinad.. Both of them were amazing and well prepared. Service was also very good. Will visit again and this time try their Indo Chinese food.

My girlfriend is from India. I grew up in the Boston area. We both now live in Cambridge, MA. And, WE both love this place! Dishes at "Harvest of India" are authentic and very flavorful. Also, the food is not weighed down with excess oil and cream. So when it's time to leave the restaurant, you can actually walk out of the place feeling upbeat and healthy.

Lizzy, how I was quick to judge.

You've totally redeemed yourself with your ridiculously large kids scoop, and decently tasting "special" flavors. I'm still a sucker for vanilla, but I admit the cinnamon was quite delicious. I can't really say either for JP Licks, so I rescind my previous threat to jump ship for greener pastures.

Your employees still look like they'd rather hang themselves than to serve me ice cream, but there was an Asian guy in glasses who was surprisingly gracious and perky. So thank you Asian guy, for totally restoring my faith in the dairy industry. 

Slow. Clapping.

Great atmosphere, great lineup, not much to add to previous reviews. I'm sad that I missed Marco Benevento this week (there was one ticket left when I checked online, but it was snapped up when I went to buy). Never tried the desserts because I enjoy the ginger beer too much.
Decent eel rolls and tasty noodles... would like to try it another time! Only problem is hard to find parking place.

I love this place.  Finally, the right kind of delicious Italian sub and the price is terrific!  My mouth waters when I think of the Italian's Italian.  The right onions cut up just right, the hots, the dressing, the tasty plum tomatoes...makes me want to run back through the rain and get another 1/2 sub (which are quite generously sized).

As I stood there in front of the menu today, pondering if I should consider trying something new, I may have even had the pleasure of meeting Al himself--if he's a big guy with a mustache.  He was very friendly in greeting me and sending me to the front.

I've gotten the Italian's Italian at least 4 times and my husband and I like.  And they carry Utz chips.  As a native Pennsylvania from Utz country, I salute you!

best sandwiches in boston.  i know at least a half-dozen people who became addicted to the mt. auburn.  if you care about price and/or service go somewhere else.  if you want a sandwich that never disappoints, darwin's has no peers.

I'm from out of town - state - and country. I've been to Club Passim a few times but only for specific shows so I haven't been able to explore the venue to its full extent. However, I've been to it enough that I can understand its seeming legendary status.

It has a great community vibe. Of the few times I've been, there are people I recognize from previous visits. Something like that is important for a place like this and also likely hard to attain so that's definitely a plus. It's also a great performing venue - at least from a spectator perspective. There have been shows there that I've wanted to go to but couldn't (due to my being from so far away) but the shows I have seen have always been excellent.
The downsides? It's cramped and tight. Good when it comes to watching performers but it's easy to get interrupted when drinks are being ordered and delivered. It's a tight squeeze. Other than that, I do look forward to coming here any time I'm in town.
I thought the Indian food at the lunch buffet was average - some dishes were good, others were just so-so. I liked the saag paneer and the vegetable fritters. I wasn't a big fan of the chutneys.

I would almost give them 5-stars but seeing I live across the street I'm geographically inclined to like them right? 

FIrst, the pizza, succulent, amazing sauce, a great slice! Let me say the Italian food is nothing short of spectacular. These guys aren't Italian I know but they were taught by the previous owners who were from the old country and they get it right. Amazing Chicken Parmesan. 

The Grinders are very good. They're bread is very soft, fresh and chewy. The Italian grinder is really good. My new favorite sandwich is the Chicken Kabob Sandwich. Perfectly seasoned tender juicy chicken, grilled onions, peppers, and mushrooms. It's a recurring religious experience. 

I'm not a huge fan of their wings. These guys do it okay but just Deep fried chicken wings, they do have a few good sauces for them though. Their Falafel is of course the real deal. Gyro is really good too. 

Some people may complain about the service and I've had some long wait times early on and occasionally but I usually do take-out and it's generally all done in 15-20 mins. No real upsets there. 

These guys are generally very professional, courteous, and they want to please. I always feel respected as a customer and am lucky to have such a great place close by. And yes, stay away during School lunch hours and right after school. It get's really annoying.

My worst ever experience. there's no -5 star. I live not too far from this place. I ordered few times online too. I took my friend to this place today (6/21/14). We were discriminated. We were really polite to not fight in front of everyone. All other tables got papadam that they serve at the begining. They didn't give us. I ordered masala fry fish and my friend ordered Alleppey Fish Curry. Both go with rice. I have ordered them online before. they even gave me options whether I want brown rice or white rice online when I ordered earlier. But this server knew nothing about the food. He gave us just one small rice. I asked him for second he said Malsala fried fish doesnt go with rice. He may be new but I want him to know I am not new to this restaurant. It takes forever to be seated first of all. Then they take another 1 hr to get the food you ordered. We didnt dare to order something to eat the fish with as it would take our rest of the night. It left such a bad experience in me that I will never go nor recommend anyone to go. Its impossible to get server's attention. They just ignore even if they see you raise your hand if you want something.  I strongly objected my friend leaving tip. After grand dinenr and spending over $35 there we went home and ordered pizza.

So how can you make "Pad Thai" with no tamarind, no fish sauce, no  peanuts or lime? Apparently cantonese-like glop on a brick of congealed rice noodles counts at this place. Cheap calories, perhaps; good food, no.

The top five reasons why I love Darwin's:

1. The Gerry
2. The Hilliard
3. The Story
4. The wide variety of yummy chips and desserts
5. Other fun random items (from fresh flowers to sushi)

Darwin's is defintiely my number one go-to place when I'm in the mood for fat, filling, tasty sandwiches.  It's always crowded but you can always eat on one of the benches outside, or walk to the Charles and have an insta-picnic.  Or have your food to go and make your own list of why you love Darwin's while enjoying your mouth-watering, happiness-inducing meal....

I had the South End chicken sandwich, my friend had the veggie burger.

My friend wouldn't talk to me for 15 minutes straight because she was so busy enjoying what she called "the best veggie burger I've had in my life".

South End chicken sandwich was also delicious.

Waffle fries were amazing! I will be back to try 1) burger and 2) Nashville hot chicken on Sunday!

My favorite ice cream place in Cambridge -- much better than Toscanini's, imo. I've tried coconut and ginger and coconut is the flavor that makes my dreams come true. I'm usually used to the sorbet version and not many places have coconut, but their's is divine. It's just the right amount between creamy and sorbet-y and has flakes of coconut in it. The ginger didn't make me a fan - the ginger flavor was too weak and it didn't have that amazing creamy balance of coconut. The boyfriend has tried every chocolate flavor on the menu and overall, he loves it but leans towards chocolate cookie dough for its intense chocolate flavor.

The line is always manageable, the prices are less than $4 for a single scoop (gigantic scoop) and you can grab a pint to go of your favorite flavor. I hear they have the best frappe, as well. And while you're waiting in line, check out the interesting Cambridge-esque posters! There are a few bar stools but not a great place to hang and enjoy -- just head outside to the square.

One must enjoy Takemura for what it is: inexpensive sushi that's fresh and tasty, an impressive menu with a great selection of rolls, and unostentatious surrounds.  Who goes here?  Undergrads on sushi dates, grads on low budgets, and locals (myself included) who are looking for good and inexpensive sushi.  I've been coming here for years and have never been disappointed, but I've also not expected heroic sushi with avant garde displays.   Takemura fills a gap in H-Sq area: there are places like Dolphin that bizarrely have sushi.  There are places like O Sushi which beg for tight pants and a little bit of commercial snazzy.  Then there's Takemura: low budget, tasty, appropriate, in an "English Garden" setting, with a lot of different kinds of foods for a lot of different kinds of customers.  Some exceptional bits: the tuna belly (yum!).  I've had extraordinary Hamachi Kama (tuna collar), and their Japanese squash and rice dishes are really delicious.  The udon is rich and filling.
This is the place to go to for a sushi lunch or to come and hang out with friends and get stuffed.  
The rating above is considering what Takemura is, not what it isn't.  It might not be the most authentic place on the planet, but it serves a playful variety of rolls, it's got a great college feel, and is always pleasing.
Great frozen yoghurt! Coffee Oreo flavor is amazing!! Chocolate peanut crisp let me down:(

This place is a cafeteria disguising itself as a high end lunch place by charging exorbitant prices.

Was here right after it opened in the summer, really wanted to like it as a new option, got a salad...  and dollar for dollar (at almost $12!!), definitely the worst salad for the money.  Made with the wrong ingredients, drowning in dressing and wilted greens.

So I decided I would give them another try today, opting for a sandwich, the prosciutto and mozzerella.  Was supposed to be heated up panini style, but in the end everything was cold except for the burnt top and bottom of the bread.  Sandwich was $12.  Why anyone would go here and not just next door to Clover for a higher quality lunch for half the price is beyond me.

Disturbingly good food from a truck. I'm still surprised at how many menu items they cram into the cubed footage of this tiny vehicle. Don't miss the goi cuon (spring rolls) or sesame salad, two of their best vegetarian options. I do miss Pop's Way tofu -- formerly a menu standard -- which is now only occasionally featured as a special.

For the eco-conscious, bring your own bowl, and they will fill that instead of giving you styrofoam.

My favorite Indian in Cambridge; one of my favorite go-to restaurants overall. Service is very good - friendly, personal, attentive. Food is excellent, priced reasonably. Gets less of a crowd than it deserves because of the out-of-the-way location halfway between harvard and central squares, and because it looks like a hole in the wall - but its worth the walk. Order from here all the time, delivery is usually pretty fast. Lunch buffet is a very good value.

Meh, I've experienced better in the area. The options were limited and it just wasn't as good as some other area indian buffets. The service was good and the price was very good, but otherwise I would probably spend a few extra bucks and go to a different buffet next time.

Went in at 2:00 p.m. on a Friday to build my first salad, but the guy was so pushy about rushing me as though I were taking too long. He started waving his hand as in hurry up.  I turned around to see that I was the only one in line, and thought, "what's the rush?"  The other man was making my friend's salad and not rushing her along.  I opted not to build a salad, and simply ordered a Caesar, which was not at all bad; however, I am not going back for the cat-herding experience.

The only stand out thing is the quinoa bowls, which are quite substantial for a lunch in Kendall. The toppings aren't super exceptional, but it's warm, tasty, and filling. It costs 8.51$ (inc tax). Although MIT students are supposed to get a 10% discount, it's a tossup if the discount actually gets applied at the register -- approximately half the time, I end up being charged the full amount even after showing my ID. In turn, this makes me not want to go here, because I hate being overcharged.

Lizzy's probably gets by on location. The ice cream just isn't very special. Sure, they have lots of flavors with lots of sugary things thrown in, but it doesn't taste very good. I mean, it's not BAD but if I'm going to delay my dream of having a 22 inch waist by eating something, it should be worth the sacrifice.

They put a little too much emphasis on the "ice" and not enough on the "cream" as the ice cream usually tastes a little freezer burned. Sometimes you can feel little ice crystals crunch between your teeth. I thought this might've been a one-time problem with the freezer, but it's been that way every time I've gone back. The flavors also aren't strong enough. More accurate menu descriptions might be: "coffee-ish", "Chocolate-ish", "We put some walnuts and fudge near this once"

They tend to go the "huge portions, huge prices" route, so it might be a good idea to size down your usual order if you go. The servers are fairly friendly, but the space is small. You'll probably have to take your ice cream to go.

I stopped in Lamplighter for the first time yesterday, and I gotta say that this place is pretty cool! It's a converted garage space, so it also feels borderline annoyingly hip, but we had a great experience. 

When we got there around 1:00, it was crowded with people doing work, hanging out, and playing games. We were able to get two seats at the bar, and I started with the "funky" flight of beers. I really liked the So Much for Subtlety, which is a fruity, hoppy double IPA. The Easy Tiger Brett IPA was slightly sour and hoppy, but the Astral Weeks was a little too funky for me. After the flight, I ordered a pint of the Werewolves of Cambridge, which is a great toasty, light Porter. 

Other great things about this place--free bar snacks! We snacked on some Cheez-it's with our beer, but they also had pretzels. If you want more to eat, you can order food from a few nearby places or bring your own. The staff is really friendly, and it has a great community/neighborhood vibe. We didn't play any of the games, but I love that they provide them. 

Some people were clearly camping out for hours doing work (and not really drinking coffee or beer) which I think is pretty annoying. We were there for almost two hours and some people at the bar hadn't make any movements to leave or order more. I know the struggle is real to find somewhere to work from home/do homework, but seats were at a premium on Sunday afternoon.

Next time I go, I definitely want to try some of their coffee offerings and breakfast sandwiches.

This place serves among the best sandwiches in the Cambridge area, although they're a bit pricey.  Really fresh ingredients and ample portions!   I get a kick out of the fact that they name their sandwiches after the local street names (i.e. the Cambridge, the Trowbridge, etc.).  They also have an excellent selection of pastries to satisfy your sweet tooth...

It's one of my favorite cafes in the area because it's simultaneously a neighborhood hot spot while maintaining a very cozy vibe.  If you go to the back there are some extremely comfortable leather chairs/couches for working/eating/napping/etc.  The employees are always really friendly and helpful too.  If you're new to the area definitely check this place out!

Al's is my new favorite place to eat in Harvard Square. The food is a great deal--a small sandwich is still 10 inches long!--and even though the sandwiches aren't overstuffed or anything the bread is so good it makes the sandwiches taste great. All the ingredients seem pretty fresh. Seriously, I know Subway is a little cheaper, but the quality of the ingredients just does not compare. I especially like the tomatoes they use. The staff is very efficient and I have always gotten my sandwich quickly and they have always gotten my order exactly right. I do wish they stayed open just a little later.

We had take-out on the second day Curry N Wok was opened.  All in all, it was an excellent first outing.

True to their name, their menu features a mix of Indian and Chinese influences, although the Indian influence seems a little more prominent.  Despite a relatively small menu, all the highlights seem to be covered:  proteins include chicken, lamb, goat, shrimp, and fish.  Naturally, there is a good selection of vegetarian options.  (Although regrettably, there is no tandoori on the menu.)

We tried the chicken tikka masala, the shrimp balchao ("Shrimp mellowed with coconut milk tempered with mustard seeds"), and the baingan bharta ("skinned and grilled eggplant with a smoky sauce.")  They were all fantastic.

Chicken tikka masala is one of the omnipresent dishes that lets one compare Indian restaurants.  At Curry N Wok, they offer a "dry" version or a version with "gravy."  We were interested in comparing this dish to other Indian restaurants, so we went with the gravy version.  It held no surprises here (... which is a good thing), but it was executed very nicely.  The chicken wasn't overcooked -- it was still moist and very flavorful.  The sauce was properly balanced.  

The shrimp and the eggplant were also quite good.  Echoing the theme, they were very well put together and balanced, and nothing was over- or under-cooked.

I must, unfortunately, take a star off for the rice.  I think the rice is Indian-influenced... it's fluffy basmati rice, not slightly stickier jasmine rice.  But there was absolutely no seasoning in it.  Certainly no cardamom, cinnamon, cumin, or the like.  It didn't even seem like there was even any salt.  I hope this was just an oversight (... this IS their second day open, after all!)

Beyond the food, the restaurant seems well put together.  The space has previously hosted two pizza shops, but they have definitely upped the decor.  Despite being a little small, the space is warm and inviting.

The take-out ordering went smoothly on the phone.  The girl who handled our call was clearly still learning the menu, but ultimately there were no mistakes and she was friendly and had a good attitude.

We'll be back.

I went in here specifically because they offer a tiny cone for $1.50, which is exactly the right amount of money for the right amount of ice cream. Everywhere else you go in the U.S.A., the whole more=better equation is in effect, and even the smallest portion sizes available seem to be based on the idea that you want to have ice cream as A MEAL. The small sizes at most places are basically still a small MEAL of ice cream. Lizzy's understands that some customers don't want to be Full of Ice Cream. I, for example, just want to experience the taste of ice cream in my mouth for about 15-20 minutes, and then be done. Thankyou, Lizzy's for understanding me.

Two words. The Magnolia. 

I recently started eating veggie (pescatarian, really) and I really thought I wouldn't be able to resist a delicious meaty sammich. In comes Darwin's and their Magnolia sandwich. Carrots, sprouts, tomato, avocado, cheddar cheese on a chewy, fresh French baguette! Bitchin'.

Only downside, kind of expensive, but its totally worth the splurge. I haven't had too many issues with the staff, but I've typically just ordered to-go during non-busy hours. It'll be my dream one day to get up early enough to make it to their breakfast sammich menu.

I ordered the double cheese burger it was very tasty. Slightly greasy with a classic burger taste. The ground meat was good quality and bun perfect. The staff was super nice. They serve booze so I will hit them up for a Friday luncheon soon.  My co-worker got the fried chicken "south end" and he thought the bun was under toasted.

I used to LOVE Darwin's when I lived in Cambridge 3 years ago.  It's changed since I moved back - the service is so unbelievably bad.  I can't believe how rude the employees were, and on several different occasions.  I'm not going to go there again  - in addition to condescending answers to my questions and making me wait longer than I would generally make someone wait when I have been the person behind the counter, the last time I went there, the employee began stacking up the chairs at my table with 30 minutes to go before they closed.  Despite the fact that there were chairs at other tables he could have stacked first.  It's disappointing since this used to be a great place to eat, hang out, and study.  Fortunately, there are several coffee shops in Cambridge which are much better.

Wu focuses on burgers - there's little else on the menu - and nails it. Juicy, maillard-heavy patties, fresh toppings, legit bun. It stands against any burger in Cambridge.

The fries and sides are solid, service prompt and friendly, beer list small but covers anything you'd want to drink with a burger.  Array of cocktails and boozy milkshakes that I've not tried but looks promising

Sundays are Nashville fried chicken day - definitely worth a try if you like fried chicken at all. 

Before they moved in, I had never considered that Inman needed a burger joint. Now I know better

Awesome veggie sandwich special today (2013-10-2). Roasted eggplant with mozzarella on some whole grain bread. Most importantly, I wanted to light and appreciated that they offered me a half sandwich. Always tasty. Great for a quick lunch. 

Watch your seat though. When they called me that my sandwich was ready I stepped to the counter and about 15 seconds later upon my return to the table - which was three steps away - a guy is trying to walk of with my chair.

I think a 3.5 would be apt here. 

The juice I tried, the OH.EM.GEE, was really delicious--I think that's where they excel. They had freshly made fruit in mason jars in the fridge and poured your juice to order. It was fresh, clean, a really nice combo of Tart sweet and refreshing.

My coworker and I also got lunch here and we're up for something heartier than than juice. I got the forbidden rice bowl and she got the Keen What! I enjoyed mine--the sauce was great--but it isn't something I'm still thinking about the next week. I'd order it again but I'm not rushing back to grab it. Hers was basically a lump of kale and quinoa and wasn't that appetizing--the shredded kale was a huge mass. It was hard to eat and I wouldn't order it for myself after seeing it.

I'll come back, but probably for a juice to start my day rather than for lunch.

I got there at noon and there was no line. The staff was really nice and helpful. 

Also, parking was impossible. What's up with Kendall and the terrible 15 min parking?! There isn't any!! Not their fault, obviously, just an observation.

This space is a cafe in the morning and microbrewery by early afternoon. Lamplighter serves free snacks, and hosts pop ups with local restaurants. I'm not an IPA fan but enjoy the sours and pale ales. The menu frequently rotates and my latest favorite is Rhapsody. There are also trivia nights and lots of board games for you and your friends to enjoy!  The back opens when it gets lit so feel free to take a snap with the neon sign. Lamplighter is my go-to hipster microbrewery.
Goosebeary's used to be my favorite food truck around MIT. Today I could not find it on either Ames or Carleton. Are they on vacation for the summer?

Oh Darwin's, for every positive thing you do, the woman in the thick framed glasses always manages to  mangle it with her overly aggressive hipster attitude. Even when she's being nice, I almost always have the need to feel guilty of my existence as a customer. 

Sorry for liking your delicious coffee. 
I'll be sure to go to Starbucks next time so that I won't be such a burden.

#almostthere

I waited patiently all summer for this place to open and they have not disappointed! Reminds me of Backyard Bowl in Santa Barbara. Delicious! 

Only reason this isn't a 5 star review is the wait time... Every time I've gone has been at weird hours when they are not busy, and it is still a 15-20 minute wait for my acai bowl. 

Of course it is because everything they made is made to order, so the wait is totally worth it.  But would it be too much to ask for there to be one person taking the order and another making the food?

Lunch Buffet is delicious and cheaper 7.95 with 5 % discount for students,but there are so many items 3 vegetarian dishes,3 non vegetarian dishes,masala tea,fresh salad,rice podi,garlic naan.Really enjoyed the food and service

Pretty damn impeccable edibles.

I'm a glutton for flavor and 2nd St delivers on my dream for complex sandwiches no one in their right mind would know how to ask for. Avocado, mustard and excellent cheeses are bound for your sandwich. 

If choice is available, go for the sourdough. Perfect bread. 

One more heads up -- they're cash only unless you use the LevelUp app. Stick to the latter and get some points toward your meal.

What a brilliant idea for a venue. I love the kind of artists they pull in, but I hadn't had the opportunity to see anything here yet. The space is incredibly intimate, good acoustics, loud enough to get your heart fluttering occasionally but typically nothing close to earplug fare (and that's a major plus, in my book). Our beers took a full half hour to arrive, which was not so great, but at least they didn't forget. Ordered a white pizza, which was all right; the tofu actually detracted significantly from the deliciousness of it overall, though it seemed like a good idea to protein-ify. Other negative was the fact that because we were all packed so close, the waitresses had to navigate over people's feet and bags and the like constantly to get to their tables (not an easy job). But overall, the music was so good and the experience so pleasant that I think this deserves 4 stars. The rest of the food smelled intoxicatingly good, too - I'll have to go back to Veggie Planet just for eats.

Great, centrally locates spot for American-style Thai. And they will spice it up if you like it that way- I went twice ona 2-week trip- once for Penang Curry, and another time for crispy chili chicken. Friends had pad Thai, seafood, and calamari. All good. Portions are huge (2 people or take leftovers). Not 5-star not because they are too liberal with their onions and peppers, and I prefer more of the protein, and smaller bites. Quick service. Lively environment.

This was my go-to at Holyoke: 10" grilled chicken pesto filled with marinated, seasoned tenders, LTO, sharp provolone, EVOO, and a yummy pesto aioli, a bag of chips, and a soda.  All for under $8.  The sandwiches are big enough for 2 meals, too.  The chicken cacciatore is great, as well.  I love the hot options, but the standard deli hoagies are very solid, too.

The food/smoothies here are amazing- natural ingredients and gluten free. The staff are very friendly but be prepared to wait. Last time I went I waited 20 minutes for a smoothie-it's frustrating to pay premium prices ($8 for a smoothie) and wait that long with no explanation or apology.

I recently started working in East Cambridge near the Galleria Mall and noticed 2nd Street Cafe on my walk to the "T" one day.  I made a mental note because normally I like to eat out once or twice a week on my lunch break.  2nd Street Cafe is a very small nook in the wall but they have a great little sandwich menu.  I ordered a delicious chicken sandwich on wheat bread with fresh mozzarella, tomatoes and pesto.  

Overall it was a very satisfying sandwich and a welcome alternative to the mall food court!  My one complaint was that there was a bit too much pesto.  It ended up making the perfectly toasted bread soggier than I would have liked.  They'll definitely get a second look from me, however.  Check it out if you're in the area and want a quick bite that will fill you up!

I found out about this place because I'd just started listening to The Unthanks (lovely little folk band from Northumberland, England) and they happened to be putting on a show right here. Honestly, I don't think I'd ever have gone otherwise, which would've been quite a shame. 

Sitting in that basement area (along with Veggie Planet), the room is small, but perfect for the shows they have going. We were offered slightly more expensive tables if we wanted to eat, and those really had the best view of the show; everything else was around the edge of the room. 

I really liked the close, cozy atmosphere, and the acoustics of the room, while not stellar, were perfect for the small show going. The Veggie Planet/Club Passim staff were all pretty friendly and made themselves available if we needed anything. Great vibe, great people.
 
The only thing that keeps me from giving it a full five stars is the fact that we couldn't see the actual stage floor (I'm not sure that even the people in the middle could), which is kind of a problem when a good portion of the performance is clog-dancing...

Soooo yum!  Two nights in a row i went for two scoops with hot fudge.  The flavors were chocolate chip and black raspberry.  So good!  I have to say that my companionv had plain vanilla with hot fudge last nudge night and he preferred to my suggestion of getting what I ordered? A purist!  Don't understand but to each his own!
    So the black raspberry was very delicious and a winning combination with hot fudge.   The chocolate chip had big, flat chucks of chocolate...another winner!
      They are not skimpy on the hot fudge or the ice cream.  But you do have to pay extra for nuts.  It was already about $12 for the 2 sundaes so as good as they were,  I only go for a splurge!  (Should have been rated with at least 2 dollar signs!)
         One suggestion for Lizzy...the bowls for the sundaes have vertical ridges in them.   The fudge gets stuck in them and you don't want to miss a drop!  Oh, second suggestion...put a couple of tables outside so people can sit and enjoy their ice cream. Seating inside is too limited.

Stopped by here for brunch today to taste a new restaurant nearby! And it was delicious! Staff was extremely friendly, nice atmosphere. Coffee delicious, really no complaints at all. Just excited to come back for dinner and for brunch again!

So glad they are open. Always looking in greater Boston for a good Mexican food restaurant and I think we have it! Super friendly, Franky is great, and very good food. Margaritas are excellent and great atmosphere!

I love Corazon de Frida!

Went there for brunch for the first time and wasn't disappointed. The  in the ranchero omelette were tender and nicely flavored with parmesan cheese. The omelette was great and the pork in it was tender and juicy. Just look at the pictures!  The Huevos rancheros were to die for. The chips came with three flavorful dips: Chipotle yum, tomatilla yummy, and salsa. 

I think this is going to be our regular hangout. You've got to try it.

This was my first time ordering from Corazon and was excited because the menu looked delicious.   I ordered the Maui tacos (Achiote chicken, queso Oaxaca, caramelized onions, pineapple pico, chipotle crema and queso fresco) with no caramelized onions.  Instead what I got was a taco with chicken and chopped tomatoes.  Nothing else on the taco that I just paid $12 for.  I understand maybe forgetting the fact that I did not want caramelized onions, but the only thing they actually got correct with this was the chicken part.  First and last time here.

Yes, the sandwiches are great -- but here are three more reasons to go to Darwin's you may not have heard of:

(1) The daily lunch specials offer great variety on weekdays -- and if you come later in the afternoon, leftover lunch special sandwiches sell at half price, which is a great deal.

(2) On weekends, breakfast sandwiches are offered until 3 pm -- I recommend the Hancock for a late breakfast/early lunch.

(3) The espresso drinks served at Darwin's are the best you can get in this area -- and definitely better than at Starbucks. I usually go for a latte, and I have never been disappointed.

It's a nice new restaurant in Inman Sq! I love their food. They make guacamole in front of you which is kinda cute. Food is great and if you like spicy, but not too hot, the food here is perfect. Service is good, and servers are friendly.

Highly recommend!
Great vibes! Good amount of choices. I have only 4 stars versus 5 cause I think the margaritas could improve, tasted anoitece too much of margarita mix. Tacos were good!!

We have now done takeout twice now, and everything was extraordinarily good.  Highlights so far were the malai kafta, masala dosa, and chicken tika masala. 

Can't speak to the table service, but the woman at the register was bright and pleasant.  Also, our takeout was thoughtfully packed so that nothing got smushed or soggy.

I think highly enough of this place that I was motivated to make my first ever Yelp post.

I love this place. I think the beer is mellow and not overly hoppy just for the street cred. I love that the space is multi-use. You'll find all sorts of people here, doing everything from working to after-work drinks to that awkward first bumble date. 

Service is always friendly and its very clean. They also give out snacks. I tend to go darker with my beers and haven't found one I haven't liked yet. Keep it up Lamplighter- you're going places kiddo!

TLDR - great sandwiches, nice study space, coffee's a little funky

I like coming to Darwin's if I need to get some work done. They have everything you would need from a cafe from snacks, to coffee, to full meals. You can't go wrong with any of their sandwiches, especially the breakfast ones. If a hearty breakfast is what you're looking for, they've got you covered. Prices are a little steep in my opinion, but the amount of food is more than compensating. Only setback is their coffee. I've had it a couple times, but I can't get over the aroma and aftertaste (its finish, as I think the pinky-raisers call it). Maybe I'm just not fancy enough for their coffee, or maybe I've just had bad luck with it.
Solid choice if JP Lick's line is crazy long. I came here just once, but it was a nice satisfying homemade concoction that I tried.

The waffle fries here are warm, fresh, and delicious, 

The burgers here are also warm, fresh, and delicious. 

Seriously though.. best waffle fries I've ever had.

The buns of the burgers are soft and buttery. The patties are juicy and cooked all the way through. 

The staff here are really friendly and outgoing! I came here for lunch but I can see this being a cool place to hang out at night

Unfortunately I think it's pretty pricey for what you get. Also, I would have liked my dine-in burger to be served on a plate because I like eating burgers with a knife and fork. I can see they're trying to go for low maintenance and not have to wash as many dishes. However, the iron basket and cookie sheet tray thing is kind of awkward for me. It was just strange because I asked them for a knife and fork which they gladly gave me but I wasn't even able to eat my burger with a knife and fork..

PLEASE READ.

I went here to catch up with two of my colleagues. After a nice meal, we pay our check and get up to leave. The nightmare soon began.

The woman at the register physically blocks our path to the door, saying that we did not pay enough tip. We were absolutely shocked. She goes on to say that people usually pay 20 to 30% in tips and that we didn't.

My street smart friend from NYC tries to usher us out, but this lady is not going to let us leave without a few more dollars. She grabs a hold of my arm. I could not believe this was happening. As I'm writing this, it actually sounds made up. 

I am livid that she thinks she could put her hands on customers, because she is unhappy that the tip is not what she wants. I rip my arm from her grip and the three of us hurry out. Will not ever return. Please do not support this abuse.

I used to go to the public high school in Cambridge so that's how I initially started going to Mona Lisa's. I graduated almost 7 years ago and whenever I come back to the area I always stop by to get a grilled chicken sandwich or buffalo chicken wrap or even a slice of buffalo chicken pizza. Everything is amazing here I love the food so much! And the customer service is excellent!

I am a fan of Lamplighter mostly because of the unique space and some of the tasty beers they create.  Great if you are looking for a fun place to get a coffee or a beer on a Saturday afternoon.  Also check out the back room which can be accessed through a hallway in the back which opens to a space with a more living room feel.  One downside is that this place on occasion can get pretty crowded so plan accordingly.

Loooove this cute little local brewery, it's definitely my favorite in the area. From the beer to the atmosphere to the funky graphic designs on their cans, everything about this place is so well done. My office is right down the street and I swing by regularly for a post-work beer or a mid day cold brew/kombucha. 

Their beer list changes from time to time but some of my all time favorites here include the Rhapsody in Red (perfect summer sour full of fresh flavors) and the Rabbit Rabbit (one of their most popular beers, a double IPA). 

The taproom itself is not dog friendly, but you can regularly spot a couple of the staff's adorable pups on the other side of the building. They're always bringing in food trucks, hosting special events, selling fun artwork and swag, and much more. Basically this place is always up to something creative and fun, so if you stop by frequently like I do you'll never be bored.

Super trendy. Super tasty beer. You won't be disappointed.

Seriously amazing! Best Mexican food we've had since moving to Cambridge from San Diego. We've been 3 times and everything we've had to eat and drink was delicious. Can't wait to try more items from the menu. The staff is always very friendly.

Love this place! I'm often in the area so I've already eaten there 3 times.

Best veggie burger I've ever had. Wormtown Be Hoppy IPA on tap!! Small place but the service has been excellent and fast, the lighting and decor made it seems spacious and comfortable. 

I don't eat meat very often but tried a WuBurger -- tasty! Potato bun, "Wu-sauce" and homemade pickles really brings it together.  On one visit, my friends had cocktails with their meals and someone got a boozy milkshake! Everyone really enjoyed their experience.

My only complaint was having to order a kids burger when I wanted a single patty burger. FYI, server said it was not an issue but I can see how that's confusing for new customers.

I've been wanting to try this restaurant since when they opened and finally tried it this evening. First, they have tons of outdoor seating due to one side of the road being closed. The tables are spaced out way more then 6ft each and the staff and servers are COVID-19 compliant, all wearing masks. I do wish that they gave everyone hand sanitizer upon sitting down (but they did have some on the main table where you check in with the host) and I wish took down names/numbers for contact tracing like some other restaurants are doing. But overall, I felt safe and comfortable dining outdoors at Frida! 

Now for the food. Wow! The food was excellent and far exceeded my expectations (Boston isn't generally known for its Mexican food, let alone authentic Mexican food). The menu does have some more authentic dishes and then some other dishes more catered to the American palate. We ordered the shrimp ceviche, mole enchiladas, and poblanos enchiladas. I was blown away by the enchiladas!! The sauces on both were SO DELICIOUS. The mole was perfect - not too sweet, not too spicy, not too cocoa-y and the poblano sauce was creamy and tangy. Next time I'll have to ask if they can do half mole/half poblano!! 

They forgot to bring out the shrimp ceviche appetizer before our entrees came out and were so kind to comp it from our bill, we left a really nice tip because that was so kind! They also provided chips and a trio of salsas on the house for all tables which was generous! For dessert, we got the fried ice cream - it was very good, but very heavy and too much for 2 ppl! 

Overall, I'll definitely be coming back! Looking forward to trying more items on the menu (and the margs) and getting the enchiladas again!!Enchiladas PoblanasMole Enchiladas
Delicious food. The decor is  beautiful. Service is friendly and attentive. Had the enchiladas poblanas with creamy poblano sauce. Will definitely be back.

Ordered online. Was supposed to get 10% off first online order, and did not. Also tried to apply one of their coupons. Did not accept that either. I ordered anyway for delivery. Included a $5 tip too. The website charged my credit card and said half hour delivery. I even received a confirmation email of my order. The food never came! I tried calling 5 times. No answer and no answering machine. I would not even give this place one star. Now, I need to go there tomorrow and get them to credit me for food I ordered and never received. Bad! Very Bad!

The food here isn't bad necessarily, but if BuzzFeed had a show about the least "worth it" food, they'd have Takemura's Tekka Don and Sake Don because it's like paying $15 for each bowl of rice and a few extra dollars for thin slices of fish. It's almost like ordering 1 salmon/tuna roll and 3 rolls of plain sushi rice. Just get the regular salmon/tuna roll.

Oh man this is tough... I really hate to knock a local brewery but... They were nice enough to pour free 2oz samples of all five beers they had available, points for that. But not one beer was anything I would rush to show my beer friends. One IPA was decent but that was as good as it got... the other IPA had a messy hop profile and there was a sour beer was trying wayyyyy to hard to be funky and unique. It reminded me of some early-aughts Dogfish Head beers (Au Courant, especially) that seemed to be made deliberately unpalatable just for the hipster appeal. No thanks.

Here's the kicker: when I asked what kind of hops were in the decent IPA, I was told "We are not allowed to tell you that". That answer is a death knell for this place. A company policy of *not* talking about the beer with eager customers (your FUTURE PROMOTERS!) is so far out of touch with the craft beer movement that I wonder if these guys really have any clue at all. Every brewery I go to, I talk about the beers with the staff, and they are always eager to engage an excited customer who is obviously looking to spread the good word about their beers. Earth to Lamplighter: nobody is going to plagiarize your mediocre IPA recipe because you told them what hops are in it. Get off your high horse and engage people and create promoters if you want to grow!

Great spot for a covid brunch or dinner. They have lots of outdoor space and are following the rules. The chips and dip (complimentary) with brunch were fresh and delicious. All the items have a similar combination of onions potatoes eggs and beans but are still different enough and delicious. Highly recommend the french toast. Only tip is that the breakfast potatoes are not crispy nor particularly flavorful. The actual dishes they came with were so large that no one ate them. I would either improve them a bit or stop offering as a side.

Loved the outdoor vibes at Corazon De Frida and happy to see it join the neighborhood. After reading the other raving reviews online I knew I had to stop by Frida's and see what the hype was all about. 

Upon arriving they had pretty large outdoor seating area due to half of Cambridge Street being blocked off to pedestrians only. Our party arrived pretty early around 5:30 and we were seated right away. The wait staff all wore masks and followed COVID procedures. 

Now, let's get into the food. It was really good and the menu was HUGE. The one downside to the menu was a lot of the dishes were listed as MP and upon asking the waitress she didn't even really know what all the prices were because it was just so many to remember. This is my only real complaint. Everything else was spectacular. The portion sizes were large and we all left feeling very full. We ordered the Chorizo Croquettes, Poblanos, Baja Fish Tacos and Chile Rellenos. With each item coming with raving reviews from everyone at the table. We all ordered a cocktail with our meals and the waitress did a great job hearing what we liked for flavors and helped us pick out a set of great drinks. 

I highly recommend checking out this restaurant and I will be back!

I ordered a sandwich on gluten free bread and the cashier asked me if it could be prepped and toasted on the same surface. I said no and received immediate attitude for it. Why offer gluten free bread if you'll have an attitude about ensuring the sandwich actually stays gluten free? I won't be returning, even though the sandwich was good.

Lovely patio dining experience. The music is fire. Reggaeton, Celia Cruz, and Selena- how could you go wrong? They bring out fresh made salsas and chips to start. I had an horchata- a bit too vanilla-y for my taste, but very refreshing, and lovely aroma and taste of Mexican cinnamon. My friend had a special quesadilla (can't remember which, but great presentation), and I had the ceviche. Interestingly, you can also opt to get the ceviche with tomato juice and chile, which reminds me of coctel de camaron in MX. They also have a huge drink menu- about as big as the food menu.

WUBurger Gourmet joins Hops Test Kitchen in the list of Inman Square "upscale version of" restaurants. I'd say it hits a sweet spot. Just not-fast-food-y enough to seem like a proper sit-down meal; it doesn't quite have the Tasty Burger vibe of a dive bar strapped to a McDonalds (not to say that doesn't have a certain charm when you're in a certain state of mind). But you can still get a beer with your classic burger and a good portion of fried potato, just the right amount to compliment the sandwich.

The burger is good, and the waffle fries are very tasty. The "South End" chicken sandwich was also a winner taste-wise, but the portion of chicken in the sandwich seemed a bit skimpy. Spiked milkshakes and hot fried chicken on Sundays (among other options) round out the menu.

Overall, a good addition to Inman Square!

Consistently solid sushi, and VERY good teriyaki dishes.  I come here frequently, and have never had a bad meal.  For cost and location, this place is unbeatable.  The service is lovely - the hostess and wait staff are very kind and friendly.
Mediocre food, boring vibe.  Move on. Ate nearly half the menu over 3 visits.  Not sure why anyone likes it.  Puketown.

This place was vewy vewy quiet when my friend and I went one Saturday afternoon. Is it because the kiddies are still away? We were seated right away, obviously, in the center of the room. Greeeaatttt...

While we were waiting for our food the place started to fill up (well 3 tables worth of people came in) so I began to speak louder than an inside voice. I had ordered a mango smoothie and edamame to start. The mango smoothie was quite good and I slurped that right down. A good smoothie never makes it to the main course. The edamame seemed slightly undercooked, but I don't know. It was just a bit crisper than I'm used to, not bad, but I prefer my beans on the verge of soggyville. 

We ordered 4 rolls and some nigiri. It seemed $1+ more expensive than other places I've been to lately (like the extra charge for sashimi)... but all the fish tasted super fresh. The salmon/avocado roll was a bit on the small side. The toppings looked like they were about to jump off the roll. We also ordered another roll, and for the life of me I can't remember the name, that had scallops and a slew of other sea creatures.. and it came out SPICY (I'll find out). That was not noted on the menu and it was a bit of a disappointment. I don't eat spicy and even if I could, I'm not supposed to with my current vocal chord condition.  My friend and I were starving though, so we didn't send it back. We kind of shoved off the spicy topping and ate the roll underneath it. 

I liked the way the restaurant was set up.. the unique dry erase menu you're given.. the great service.. and the cute little med students on a first date in the corner*. Aww.. they just decided it'd be so cool if they opened  hospital together.. awww.. lmao

3.5 stars for now.. 

*Med students and paper barf bag not necessarily included.
What a fantastic experience. We are here from Texas and this Mexican Restaurant did NOT disappoint. The ceviche was delicious, best margarita I've had in some time. Will be back when back in town.

This place exceed my expectations. I'm always weary of how trendier Mexican food is going to taste, but their food was incredibly flavorful and unique! The chef really knows what they're doing. I sat outside a few times I went and they seemed to have ample space but the place is always poppin so you'll definitely need to reserve in advance. The inside is very aesthetic. They've placed dividers for all tables to keep up with covid safety. Highly recommend this place! Get the Wicked margarita and the loaded nachos! So good!
Great food, great atmosphere, ice cold drafts are the best served in town.  The fresh natural made guacamole that is presented and made in front u you!!!! 5 star place to be

Already visited this place twice. First time was during early November, where we dined in for weekend brunch (now not offering anymore); second time we ordered the dinner take outZ. Both time I had a very nice experience. Food there was really delicious and they offer super big portion with low prices. Their brunch menu is very special: there are a lot of traditional American brunch dishes and they put a lot of Mexican twist in it. 

Some of my must get:
Dos ehuis xx taco
Del mar
Animal style fajitas 
Breakfast taco
Ranchero omelet

We saw this open up and decided to give it a try and were not disappointed! It might be one of our new favorite places! Arriving on a Saturday around noon we were offered the brunch menu - the passion fruit mimosa is a must. The chips they give you are warm and fresh and the 3 dips (salsa, salsa verde and chipotle crema) are all amazing too, salsa verde being a favorite. The service was awesome and the food was too. The torta was a bit messy but very worth it, home fries cooked perfectly. 11/10 would recommend and will be back soon!!

Such a great restaurant! We came for brunch and the food was amazing and the wait staff were so friendly. There are tables outside and all the servers were wearing masks. I recommend the chilaquiles and the ranchero omelette!

I so enjoyed having dinner at Corazon De Frida. The drinks were some of the best I've had in the Boston area (and I've had QUITE a few drinks in the Boston area if I do say so myself) and the food was absolutely delicious. I could only finish half of the burrito I ordered and just one of the chorizo croquettes was practically the size of my head. Definitely check this place out if you're in the Cambridge area, their outdoor seating is wonderful and their salsa selection is even better.

All about the ambiance at Lamplighter. Longfellows Cafe space by day, chill zone with $8 flights, free bar snacks (pretzels and popcorn and goldfish, oh my!), board games and mod vibes at night.

Still pouring the LF cold brew, alongside their beer, in the evening hours? Oh, okay.

Longfellows Cafe space by day, chill zone with $8 flights, free bar snacks (pretzels and popcorn and goldfish, oh my!), board games, mod vibes, and Longfellows Cafe lite at night.

I went with the Funk It Up flight by name alone, and found the funk to be more brett-like-the-sweat-socks driven than tart or acidic sours. I respect that, and blame myself for being unprepared; the Easy Tiger may be a divisive brew that strikes the nose with a stanky cheese fragrance before drinking smoother than you'd expect. Same brew gets barrel aged, and mellows out the ripe scent in Astral Weeks (only avail in the taproom).

Thrilled as a gose fan to see Metric Systems, and while the mild salinity and lactic pungency made for easy drinking, I didn't catch the coriander or other herbal flavors you'd associate with this style du jour. Heavier than a session but just as sippable, it's a perfectly pleasing brew that serves as a great entree to those hesitant on goses.

I'll definitely be a regular here, day to night, and love their event calendar (http://www.lamplighterbrewing.com/events/), which regularly features trivia, food truck pop-ups, cheese night and... brewery yoga. Because Cambridge.

Total waste of $20. Sushi was awful- the tuna was a murky brown/purple that almost looked like a plum. The yellowtail was an offputting color as well. 2 stars instead of one because the delivery was fast and the gyoza was passable. Seriously never worth ordering from again.

Outdoor seating available! And you will be welcomed with chips and 3 very tasty salsas commentary. We came here for brunch today (Saturday) and didn't have to make a reservation. For drinks, we ordered cocorita and a drink that comes with corona beer (check the pictures). Cocorita was too good! The drink was sour(perfect if you like sweet and sour) ,  very different,  my husband and I loved it. The service was too good! Food- BOMB! We ordered from. Their brunch menu- Breakfast burrito (crispy taco shell with a patty inside and egg on top with amazing sauce) and Torta(sandwich with great bread filled with beans onions peppers and eggs) both of them were very delicious and different! Overall, we had a great experience at this place and I would definitely come back here for may be dinner(to try out their croquettes )
